and statesman.
Religion
Broken Europe hosts various religions. Catholicism is 
present in Spain, France, Italy, Austria, Poland, Ireland 
and in some Germanic areas. Protestantism is widespread 
in the Netherlands, in many areas of the Holy Roman 
Empire (Prussia, Palatinate, Saxony), Denmark, Sweden 
and Norway. Anglicanism is in England, Calvinism in 
Switzerland, Orthodox Christianity in the whole Balkan 
area and especially in Russia. Islamism can be found in 
many parts of the Balkans, in Asia Minor, in the Middle 
East, and throughout Mediterranean Africa. Many 
religious minorities, such as Judaism, are widespread, but 
have always kept a low profile out of fear of retaliation 
from the powers-that-be due to the great solidarity of their 
communities.
Churchmen play a very important role in society, both the 
high clergy (representing a branch of the aristocracy) and 
those who take care of the many villages.
Although the Enlightenment has begun to undermine the 
dominance of religious beliefs, few dare to call themselves 
atheists. Almost everyone else, in any European or 
Mediterranean area, are fervent supporters of their faith. 
It is customary to regularly attend religious services and 
receive the sacraments, and this applies to the nobles, the 
rich, the poor, and the peasants, without distinction.

111
Rich people’s belongings
The best way to show off wealth is to wear it, including 
elegant clothes, made by professional tailors, but also shoes 
and boots. Even weapons can be a subject to fashion, such 
as dueling pistols or very elaborate and engraved shotguns. 
Of course, jewels like rings (also used by men) and 
necklaces (especially for women) can range from tasteful 
to opulent. Ladies desire expensive perfumes and highly 
coveted dressing tables. In wealthy homes works of art, fine 
furniture, tapestries, and fine silverware are common.
Sickness
Seasonal ailments such as flu and cold are omnipresent, 
to the point of being deadly for those who cannot afford 
treatment.
Smallpox epidemics break out continuously and syphilis, 
tuberculosis, and cholera are more or less widespread. 
Among the nobility, due to a diet featuring a great deal of 
meat, gout is widespread, while scurvy is rampant among 
sailors. Although not really a disease, septicaemia and 
gangrene are a constant presence among the wounded on 
the battlefield, often with lethal consequences. Tetanus is 
also a common infection. 
In most cases, the ill are treated with home remedies and 
according to popular wisdom. Only the affluent can afford 
the expensive care of a doctor. If a sick person can continue 
to work, they will be grateful to avoid ending up in a 
hospital or, worse, abandoned on the street... 
Superstition
The Enlightenment is undermining many popular beliefs 
and superstitions, but only among the upper social classes, 
meaning those who can afford adequate education and are 
genuinely literate. Among the common people, however, 
popular belief in legends and the existence of supernatural 
forces are well rooted, and often “Christianized” as being in 
service to the Devil.
Every region, if not every single village, has its own folk 
remedies. There are literally hundreds of different ways to 
interpret a situation through superstition. The great witch 
hunts have been over for years now, but in the countryside, 
it is still easy to believe in dark forces and question any 
person who deviates even slightly from what is considered 
“normal” . Regarding the Church, the Order secretly deals 
with every supernatural phenomenon, leaving the village 
priests with the task of maintaining a fear of the unknown. 
For the common people, it is natural to wait for the 
intervention of the Church to resolve a mystery. For the 
nobles and rulers of the cities, the Order is an established 
truth not to be named in public, but on which one must 
blindly rely in case of need against the forces of darkness.
Traveling
Traveling is not easy. For farmers, survival is anchored to 
the land they cultivate. It is difficult to move around except 
for a few rare exceptions, such as fairs and markets. Those 
who travel often are nobles, scholars, merchants, and of 
course ambassadors and political officials. 
The horse is the most popular way to travel. The nobility 
have their own horses, but scholars, notaries, or those who

112
live in the city must necessarily pay for a ride in a horse-drawn 
carriage or coach. Traveling by ship is a good way to move along 
the coasts, though there are no passenger ships, but only merchant 
ships which offer passage for a few coins. 
The borders between the various states of Broken Europe are not 
well defined and border controls are focused almost exclusively 
on the main roads. It is also true that safe roads are few and very 
busy, and moving a heavy cart through inaccessible streets is not 
the easiest of operations. 
For Hunters, traveling is what connects one investigation to the 
next and accounts for a good portion of the time they spend on a 
mission each week. The Order enjoys respect and esteem almost 
everywhere, and it is common practice to accommodate Hunters 
within the limits of one’s ability. Even the humblest of peasants will 
open their home to them.
Work
The industrial revolution is upsetting Broken Europe. Talking about 
industries means talking about extreme manpower requirements, 
where the power of rivers and coal play an important role. Except for 
the nobles, who do not work (because it is considered humiliating 
to perform hard work), all other social classes start working during 
childhood. Especially in the countryside, children regularly help 
their parents with every task. Even in the cities, artisans and 
merchants are helped by their children at least in the simplest jobs, 
even if only to pass on the family profession. 
Work is often a means of redemption for Broken Tales that yearn 
for a normal life. Although, as you can imagine, a lifetime of 
restraint can be ruined by a single moment in which they manifest 
their special abilities.

113
Spoiler
! Warning !
Go no further if you don’t want to lose your way... 
This part of the book presents the complete rules for playing a Broken Tales Campaign, all the 
Scenarios and the truth behind the tale of the Child Savior.
If you want to experience Broken Tales as a player, stop here. After all... would you peek at the 
last page of a novel you are reading?

114
The Fairytale Of 
The Child Savior  
Once upon a time... the world of fairytales was on the verge of ending, forsaken by children who grew up too fast and forgot 
the value of old stories. Within the void left by failing stories, darkness and oblivion blossomed. All the inhabitants of fairytales 
wept with fear at the long-dreaded end. “ All it would take is one last reader. One single young heart willing to believe in us still, ” 
sighed the weary Fairytale King.
Once upon a time... A writer labored in obscurity, desperately trying to create new 
fairytales that could find a place in the hearts of children. But every time his stories were 
rejected. Too dark. Too dangerous. Drawn from old tales no one remembered anymore. 
“People don’t want to hear about fairytales, ” people teased him.
Reprise

115
The Writer drove himself harder and harder, giving birth to even darker and more painful 
stories, resigned to the fact that nobody would appreciate them. Perhaps the fairytales were to 
blame, stubborn things unwilling to change? After having devoted his entire life to writing, the 
fairytales were refusing to help him, stubbornly unchanging for hundreds of years. 
Weren’t the fairytales the first to bask in undeserved immortality?
The Fairytale King noticed the little 
dreamer, for if even a single reader was 
ready to believe in fairytales, the whole 
world would continue to do so. Thus the 
Child became the Savior. From his dreams, 
fairytales drew new life. As a reward, the 
Fairytale King granted the Child Savior a 
wish. “Thanks to you we are remembered. I 
trust you will find in your heart the proper 
reward to ask for. ”
In the darkest moment of his life, the Writer no one believed in was 
lost in the memories of a forgotten 
childhood, when the magic of fairytales 
ignited the passion in his heart. 
Then he remembered everything: 
he remembered a distant wish, he 
remembered what he saved, and saw 
how ungrateful the fairytales were.
But there was a Child who still believed in fairytales. Alone and abandoned by everyone, the Child found shelter in those old stories. 
Within was protection from the world outside, too real and too cruel. Lost among myths and tales, he forgot the pain of a family 
unable to give him the childhood he deserved. “I love all fairytales full of heroes and villains to defeat, ” said the Child. “Why doesn’t 
anyone write new fairytales? If only I knew how, I would do it, ” the little dreamer kept repeating.

116
So it was that the villains 
became good, the heroes 
became evil. 
And all fairytales 
shattered.
For a long time, the Child searched his heart for the most beautiful wish he could think of. However, hidden in 
his heart’s innermost corner, he found his own shadow. The One Who 
Plots in the Shadows emerged from the future, driven by rejection, whispering in the Child Savior’s mind a malicious intention. 
The Child Savior truly believed that it was a good idea. 
Innocent, he said, “I would give new life to the rogues, a chance at redemption for every character who has been relegated to the 
role of villain for centuries. ”
But every light needs its shadow.
Hunched over in his studio, into the darkness of his soul, The 
Writer no one believed in died, to rise again as 
 The One Who Plots in the Shadows.

117
Scenarios

118
Red-hood 
Iskra Once upon a time…
The prosperous village of Durfort in 
Gévaudan, France is being terrorized by 
ferocious beasts. Gerard Dubois, the local 
lord of the manor, is recently married to the 
beautiful Elizaveta of Volhynia but finds 
he cannot enjoy his honeymoon when his 
desperate subjects are constantly pestering 
him for help. Finding that the cause of 
their fears are beyond his meager ability to 
manage, Gerard has written a letter to the 
Papacy, quietly begging for help. The letter, 
written in Lord Gerard’s trembling hand, 
reads, “The beasts that menace the Gévaudan 
are unnatural, fashioned by something other 
than God. ”
A group of the Order’s Hunters is sent to the 
village of Durfort to find out what is really 
going on...
A Scenario inspired by 
Little Red Riding Hood 
(Charles Perrault)
Safety Keywords:
  
Child neglect / Revenge

Play the official Red-Hood Iskra 
soundtrack during your game 
session!
119
The story that led us here…
Elizaveta of Volhynia has always been an attractive 
woman, accustomed to the rich salons of the gentry, and 
the pomp of nobles and courtiers. Becoming pregnant with 
Iskra, her eldest daughter, was a curse for her.
Abandoned by her wealthy lover, Elizaveta was  
impoverished for years, with few skills, little work, and only 
the help of her old mother Anastazia. A chance encounter 
with Gerard, who is ignorant of her past, rekindled 
Elizaveta’s ambition for a life of comfort and decadence as 
the future wife of a nobleman. Elizaveta, determined not to 
miss such an opportunity, sent her daughter Iskra alone to 
visit her grandmother. Elizaveta knew that she was exposing 
her to the dangers of the dense forest of Białowieża, a place 
full of beasts and bandits, and deadly to the unwary. Ten-
year-old Iskra trusted her mother and disappeared forever 
into the darkness of the forest. Anastazia had always treated 
Iskra with love and kindness and, overwhelmed by the 
grief of her granddaughter’s sad end, died soon after. Free 
and unattached, Elizaveta fled to Gévaudan with her new 
husband, unaware of her daughter’s real fate. In fact, Iskra 
did not die, but joined Greskar’s wolf pack, becoming a wolf 
herself...
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Iskra didn’t die in the forest of Białowieża. Instead, she 
faced every trial with fierce tenacity and eventually 
prevailed, defeating Greskar. A huge, ferocious werewolf, 
Greskar infected Iskra with the wolf ’s curse. Becoming a 
member of the pack and the favorite of the alpha Greskar, 
Iskra set off for the Gévaudan, following the scent of her 
mother, determined to get her revenge. Thanks to the fury 
of the wolf pack, Iskra has thrown the village of Durfort 
into disarray. Now, she waits in the shadows for the right 
opportunity to enter the palace and devour her unworthy 
mother. 
Elizaveta has realized what is happening, for Iskra left  
a pendant she had once made for her on the windowsill, 
a memento of a rare moment of happiness. Elizaveta, a 
cunning manipulator, got rid of the token and did not panic 
or lose her temper. After convincing Gerard to turn to the 
Order, she will seek to move the pieces on the chessboard, 
using them to rid herself of her unwanted offspring once 
and for all...

1
120
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Our story opens as you are 
being interviewed by a nervous Gerard Dubois, who, anxious 
to get rid of the monsters infesting his lands, has organized a 
dinner for his long-anticipated guests. A room has been booked 
for you at the town’s finest inn, but even though it’s been a long 
day’s journey, the urgency of the matter at hand demands that 
no time be wasted. Next to the fireplace, a beautiful woman 
is pouring wine from a goblet and smiling at you. She is Lady 
Elizaveta, Gerard’s wife.  
Place: The ceremonial hall of the Dubois family’s grand 
palace. A richly furnished room with an enormous painting of 
Patrick Dubois, Gerard’s late father. Stern and sullen-looking, 
Patrick seems to judge his son’s incompetence from beyond 
the grave with ill-concealed contempt.
Time: The sun set more than two hours ago and after a long 
journey, it is now dinnertime.
Initial situation: Gerard is anxious for the Hunters to get to 
work. Elizaveta studies the situation in silence, trying to figure 
out how she can turn the Hunters’ arrival to her advantage, 
using them as tools for her own purposes. Choose one of the 
villagers to be her dinner guest, or create one of your own. 
Vincent, her trusted head of the guards, will also be present, 
of course.
Information: The following information/clues can be 
obtained from the interview with Gerard.
 ◆ The monsters that scourge the Gévaudan are ferocious 
beasts, as can be seen from the state of the victims’ bodies.
 ◆ The first two victims were Guy and Ivette, two young 
lovers who used to meet secretly in the old cemetery 
just outside the city. It is there that what remains of their 
bodies are buried.
 ◆ It is strictly forbidden to leave the city after sunset. The fall 
of night now brings with it the mournful song of howling 
wolves.
The Dubois Palace
Descriptor: A great palace with more than a hundred rooms, 
rich and opulent.
When the Hunters visit the palace, roll 1 die to determine a 
special feature.
1-2: The palace cellars are filled with excellent wine and fine 
game meat.
3-4: A secret passageway connects the large dining room to 
the inner courtyard.
5-6: Every room in the palace is filled with flattering paintings 
of Patrick Dubois, Gerard’s father.

121
Gerard Dubois the Incapable Noble
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the noble baron of Durfort and my 
word is law. I live in the shadow of my late father’s glory.
Agenda: Put an end to the menacing werewolves and 
cover up any possible mischief.
Elizaveta – The cruel bride
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am skillful and charming. I’ll do whatever 
is needed to get what I want.
Agenda: To maintain the comforts and luxuries I have 
earned. That means ridding myself of my annoying daughter 
Iskra.
Vincent Arn – Former Huntsman and Chief of 
the Guards
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled hunter. I have no qualms 
about doing whatever is needed. I want to enjoy a good life 
without working so hard.
Agenda: I understand how the world works and who 
really runs the Dubois household. Pleasing Elizaveta is the 
best way to maintain my status.
Gift - Veteran:  Vincent is skilled in taking advantage 
of any situation. When he has the support of at least two 
of his men, every action against him receives 1 Penalty 
and Vincent can inflict 1 additional Wound.
The Guards of Gerard Dubois
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are duty-bound    -    as long as we get 
paid.
Agenda: Vincent is the boss. We’ll follow his lead.

122
Durfort Village
Descriptor: A rich and prosperous place thanks largely to the 
previous baron, Patrick Dubois.
When the Hunters roam Durfort, roll 1 die in each Scene to see 
who they will meet:
1 Anne the Little Chatterer
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: Lady Elizaveta has instructed me to keep her 
informed about what is being said in town. I do my job as a spy 
because I don’t want her to stop giving me gifts.
2 Nicholas the Huntsman
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I know the ferocity of wolves and I can’t wait to 
show off in front of Baron Gerard.
3 Genevieve the Chemist
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I escaped from a past full of witchcraft and deceit. 
I know how to summon spirits and talk with them.
4 Marcel the Time-Waster
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I frequently wander the town and may be found 
anywhere. Once, I saw a young girl at the Oak Glade, but no 
one believed my story because I am too young.
5 Corinne the Housekeeper
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I know all rumors in the village, partly because 
I start most of them myself. I’m easily offended and I love 
receiving gifts.
6 Didier the Grumpy Old Man 
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I hate all foreigners and I miss the old baron. He 
knew how to stand up for himself. Not like that lax Gerard who 
even married a foreigner!

123
The Great Gévaudan Forest
Descriptor: A huge expanse of trees and natural ravines. Home to animals and fairy creatures.
The Old Cemetery
Descriptor: A small cemetery with rusty gates and stone tombs. A 
small, abandoned church lends a gloomy aura to the place.
When Hunters wander through the cemetery, roll a die:
1-2: A small hidden path leads to an abandoned well, where 
Elizaveta has thrown an old earthenware pendant that Iskra once 
made for her as a gift.
3-4: Armand, an old, rich wine and spice merchant, arrives in a 
hurry at the cemetery. The wolves are following him. Roll a die: 
1-3: 8 Black wolves.
4-5: 8 Black wolves + Greskar. 
6: 8 Black wolves + Iskra.
5-6: A group of bandits is traveling through Gévaudan, carrying 
illegal game. Jozef, their leader, is a former lover of Elizaveta and 
knows the woman’s past. The bandits want to stop in town to rest, 
but without attracting attention from the authorities.
Descriptor: A small green area just over an hour from 
Durfort. The glade is a place of arcane power in the middle 
of the great Gévaudan forest.
Gift - Fairyland: Any action that has to do with magic 
receives 1 extra Success within the small glade. This place 
absorbs energy from living things. For every hour you stay 
there you must spend 1 Soma or receive 1 fatigue Wound.
When the Hunters arrive at the glade, roll a die to see who 
they will meet:
1-3: The glade is empty, but there are traces of wolves and 
the bones of devoured prey. After ten minutes, 8 Black 
Wolves arrive and ambush the Hunters.
4-5: A pack is at the glade: Greskar + 8 Black Wolves.
6: Iskra is at the glade, alone if it is daytime or with the 
pack (4-5 result, but without Greskar) if it is nighttime.
As a modifier: if it is night, add 2 to the roll.
The Oaks Glade

IskraIskra
124
The Wolves’ Lair
Descriptor: A large, natural cave full of tunnels and 
small chambers, less than three hours from Durfort. 
During the day the den is full of resting wolves, but at 
night it is almost always empty.
Gift - Defense Lair:  Moving into the lair without 
masking your scent will cause the beasts to quickly 
become alert. At the start of each Scene spent in this 
place, you must pass a Medium OL Defense roll. If you 
fail, 1d6 (2d6 if it is daytime) black wolves arrive.
As the Hunters explore the lair, roll a die at each Scene 
to see what they will encounter, or present the rooms in 
ascending order:
1-2: Iskra’s personal den. It is clear that this space is 
occupied by a little girl. Drawings on the walls depict 
a mother and daughter holding hands. They are drawn 
in blood.
3-4: A prisoner that is too badly injured to move. 
Depending on what has happened thus far, he may be 
one of the inhabitants of Durfort (see the Village table) 
or one of the encounters at the Old Cemetery that hasn’t 
already been triggered.
5: A large cave full of bones and human corpses.
6: A long underground tunnel leading from the Den to 
the old well in Dufort’s main square.
Anastazia the 
Sorrowful Spirit
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a restless spirit, bound to the memory of 
my granddaughter. My condition as a spirit does not allow 
me to communicate with the living except through dreams, 
whispers, and visions.
Agenda: Preventing further harm to Iskra and find a way to 
save my granddaughter’s soul.
Gift - Spirit: Anastazia is a spirit and can only be damaged 
by magic. She may appear in a dream to a target, who may 
resist with a Defense Check or accept contact voluntarily. 
The target suffers 1 Wound from stress but will receive from 
Anastazia a vision of a present or past event with a barely 
whispered phrase.
Gift - Loving Grandmother:  Anastazia always follows 
Iskra and protects her from danger. Thanks to her strong 
will, she can interact with the material world by moving 
objects and manipulating the environment, even inflicting 
a Wound on a target. If a subject is likely to help save her 
granddaughter, Anastazia will simply fill their mind with 
memories of her.

IskraIskra
125
Greskar the 
Big Black 
Wolf
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC  
(4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a huge, mighty 
werewolf. I have to constantly prove my 
strength to stay the alpha of the pack.
Agenda: Protect Iskra and devour as 
many humans as possible.
Gift - Big Wolf: Greskar is an enormous 
black wolf, as big as a horse. When he 
grabs someone with his jaws, in addition 
to inflicting a Wound, he can drag or 
hurl them away as if they were a rag doll. 
Once per Scene, Geskar’s bite can inflict 
1 additional Wound.
Gift - The Wolf’s Smell:  Greskar’s 
sense of smell is infallible. The big wolf 
will always be able to identify someone if 
he knows their scent, ignoring all kinds 
of disguises and even magic.
Red-Hood Iskra
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I possess the strength and agility of a wolf. I 
hide my face with a rudimentary wooden mask that I made 
with my own hands. I wear a threadbare red cape, the 
last gift from my mother.
Agenda: To unleash her animalistic fury 
on Elizaveta and anyone who defends 
her. Taking revenge on my mother has 
become an obsession for me.
Gift - Wolf’s Blood:  Iskra is 
moved by an animalistic fury 
that allows her to act twice in 
the course of an Exchange.
Gift - The Senses of a 
Wolf: Iskra’s sense of smell 
has become infallible. The 
little girl will always be able to 
identify someone if she knows 
their scent, ignoring all kinds 
of disguises and even magic.

126
Black Wolves of 
Greskar’s Pack
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are fierce black wolves, we hate 
humans and obey only our pack leader.
Agenda: Follow without hesitation every order 
from the pack’s alpha.
Gift - The Power of the Pack:  Any action 
taken while being engaged by two or more Wolves 
receives 1 Penalty.
Story Events
In this Scenario, there are six main Events. As Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.
If the raids of the wolves are not stopped in one day:  
many of Gerard’s soldiers will be eliminated, victims of a much 
more intense attack than those that have occurred so far, taking 
place at the gates of the palace. After the furious assault, the 
city’s defenses will be reduced to a minimum and it will be very 
easy for Iskra to break into the Dubois palace under the cover of 
darkness.
If a Hunter finds Iskra’s pendant in the well:  Anastazia 
will contact them, showing them a vision of Iskra as a child 
drinking at the Oaks Glade. The spirit of Iskra’s grandmother, 
while unable to communicate directly, will try to make the 
Hunters understand that Iskra is not a monster.
If Elizaveta goes out of town for any reason:  Iskra will 
immediately smell her scent and rush to eliminate her regardless 
of any risk. Confronted with her mother, the little girl will lose all 
restraint and refuse to listen to reason.
If Iskra is captured: the wolf pack led by a furious Greskar 
will launch a tremendous attack on Durfort. Elizaveta will try to 
eliminate her daughter with a powerful poison.
If Iskra is killed: Anastazia’s spirit will turn on her murderer, 
sending visions of Elizaveta trying to wash the blood of a dead 
child from her hands.
If someone accuses Elizaveta: Gerard will deny everything 
out of fear and shame, invoking concrete and irrefutable 
evidence. Elizaveta, for her part, will play the role of the victim, 
always trying to highlight the danger posed by the wolves.

1
2
127
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Red-Hood Iskra Scenario and can be acquired once the 
conditions are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario 
Gift. If they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will 
have to forgo the previous one.
Voice of the Spirits
Requirement:    You have come into contact with the Gift  
Spirit.
By calming your mind and concentrating, you can open 
your mind to the spiritual world and communicate with 
restless souls. Spend 1 Soma to contact a creature with the 
Spirit Gift you’ve previously encountered. Y ou do not suffer 
the Wound caused by the stress of spiritual connection.
Wolf-killer
Requirement: Having faced the wolves in a fight at least 
once.
Y ou know how to deal with the fiercest of wolves and are 
careful not to be surrounded. When you obtain at least a 
Standard Outcome on a Position or Defense Check against 
a pack of beasts, you can immediately eliminate 1 Minor 
animal NPC with 1 Wound.
Where are you going all alone, 
sweetie? (Special Gift for Garou the 
Old Wolf)
Requirement: To meet Iskra alone in the forest.
Y ou are attracted to the little girl who pretends to be a wolf 
and you dream of devouring her. Once per Scene, you can 
spend 1 Soma to know exactly where she is. If you anticipate 
her and arrive somewhere before her, you get 1 Additional 
Success on every Check you make to hurt or devour that 
little morsel. Y ou can’t wait to swallow that tidbit and it 
makes you let your guard down. As long as she is present 
in the Scene, you receive 1 Penalty in all Defense Checks.
The Scenario Red-Hood 
Iskra in Scenes
FIRST SCENE – Dinner with 
Gerard Dubois
It’s the same one you find on page 120.
End of Scene: Gerard asks the Hunters to look for traces 
of the Wolves by sending them to the Old Cemetery, 
where the first massacre took place. Continue to SECOND 
SCENE - The old cemetery.
SECOND SCENE – The old 
cemetery
Read aloud to the Hunters: The old cemetery of Durfort 
lies just over ten minutes’ walk from the town. With the 
constant threat of wolves, it is now totally abandoned. Beyond 
the old rusty gate, you can see many weathered gravestones, 
a small church, and an old well whose wooden planks have 
recently been dismantled.
Place: The old cemetery of Durfort in the middle of the 
large Gévaudan forest. It is reached by a narrow, dirt path

3a
3b
128
that winds its way through the woods from the main road.
Time: It is early morning, the sun is high on a clear winter 
sky.
Information: In the cemetery, there are many wolf 
tracks. In the middle of them are the prints of two small 
bare feet, almost certainly a little girl. At the bottom of 
the old well can be found the pendant that Elizaveta tried 
to make disappear. The wolves’ tracks lead deep into the 
forest, to their den. Anastazia will contact the Hunter who 
recovered the pendant, sending them a vision of Elizaveta 
trying in vain to wash her bloodstained hands.
End of Scene:  Hunters can decide whether to return 
to the town to report (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - 
Reporting) or follow the trail to the wolves’ den (THIRD 
SCENE OPTION B - On the Wolves’ Trail).
THIRD SCENE OPTION A – 
Reporting
Read aloud to the Hunters: Now that you see Durfort in 
broad daylight you realize how large the village is and how it 
is slowly expanding, so much so that it could almost be called 
a city. Trade flourished in Dufort under the old baron’s rule, 
but now all that is threatened by the savage beasts lurking in 
the nearby forest. 
Place: Along the streets of the village, men and women go 
about their daily business. Their somber glances betray the 
fear that runs through the population.
Time: It is late morning, just over an hour until noon, and 
the whole village will stop to have a frugal lunch.
Information: Along the streets of the village, two of the 
inhabitants (see Durfort Village table) will approach the 
Hunters to interact. At the palace, Vincent will greet the 
characters by asking them how it went at the cemetery, 
gathering information for a report to Elizaveta. Gerard, 
once he hears the Hunters’ report, will hesitantly ask about 
their next move. Elizaveta will try to learn what the Hunters 
have discovered, showing excessive interest if the pendant 
is shown.
End of Scene:  Hunters can either look for the wolves’ 
lair (THIRD SCENE OPTION B - On the Wolves’ Trail) 
or ambush Iskra while remaining in the city (FOURTH 
SCENE - Surprise Attack).
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – 
On the Wolves’ Trail
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Following the tracks 
in the forest is not easy and several times you risk getting 
lost. Eventually, the tracks lead to a large natural cave that 
disappears into the darkness underground.
Place: The lair of the Black Wolves. Traces of the pack are 
clearly visible outside.
Time: Crossing the forest takes more than half a day.
Information: Depending on whether it is day or night, 
consult The Wolves’ Lair to see who the Hunters will meet. 
In the lair, it will be possible to discover traces of Iskra and 
information about her. If freed, the village prisoner will tell 
the Hunters, in great detail, of a little girl, masked and in 
a threadbare red cloak, that is the leader of the wolf pack.
End of Scene:  While the Hunters are in the lair, Iskra, 
Greskar, and the remaining wolves of the pack launch an

4 5
129
attack on Durfort, decimating Vincent’s men. The Hunters 
may arrive in the middle of the battle or when the fight 
is almost over. Continue to FOURTH SCENE - Surprise 
Attack.
FOURTH SCENE – Surprise 
Attack
Read aloud to the Hunters: The cries of the townspeople 
mingle with the howls of the wolves, while large feral figures 
dart through the alleyways in the moonlight. Using a secret 
passageway, the pack launched a furious attack on the 
Dubois mansion. 
Place: The village of Durfort, doors barred and terrified 
villagers fleeing through the streets.
Time: It is night, but a full moon high in the sky illuminates 
the village with a gloomy white light.
Information: There’s no mercy in the wolves’ fury and 
many villagers will be devoured alive if the Hunters don’t 
save them. At the head of his men, Vincent tries to repel 
Greskar and his wolves, but he will not fight to the death. 
Iskra, meanwhile, has snuck into the palace in search of her 
mother.
End of Scene: However the battle goes, the Hunters arrive 
at the palace to find a terrified Gerard fleeing the scene. 
The nobleman tells them that a small, furious creature 
has broken into the palace and is now hunting down his 
beloved bride. It’s time for a showdown. Go to FIFTH 
SCENE - Which Side Are You On?
FIFTH SCENE – Which Side 
Are You On?
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The Dubois palace is a 
veritable slaughterhouse. It seems that a whole pack of wolves 
has run rampant, destroying as they went. When you hear 
Elizaveta’s cry, you rush into the great hall where you had 
dinner when you first arrived. In front of you, a terrified 
Elizaveta is confronting a little girl covered in mud and 
blood. The little girl bares her teeth like a wolf, but she has the 
crystal blue eyes of her mother.
Place: The ceremonial hall of the Dubois family’s grand 
palace.
Time: Time for a showdown.
End of Scene:  Elizaveta will ask the Hunters for help. 
Iskra, on the other hand, after a moment of surprise at the 
newcomers, is ready to die in order to have her revenge. If 
he is still alive, Greskar’s howling will hint that the big wolf 
will arrive any moment. If someone has made a connection 
with her, Anastazia will intervene with a vision to help the 
Hunters realize who in the room is truly the monster.

130
Once upon a 
time…
The Hunters arrive in Sinalunga, a small 
town in the Grand Duchy of Tuscany where 
a gruesome discovery has been made. Not 
far from the town, the mutilated corpses of 
ten people were found in an old well,  solving 
almost all of the many disappearances in 
recent months that have made the area 
the epicenter of macabre rumors and tales. 
Of Flesh 
and Wood
A Scenario inspired by 
Pinocchio (Carlo Collodi)
Safety Keywords:  
Blood sacrifices / Mutilation / Body Horror
The Papacy, determined to clarify what 
happened, fears that there is an ancient 
esoteric practice behind the killings, one 
that involves the sacrifice of innocent 
souls. Once before Sinalunga had been 
the setting of a terrible story: the noble 
Azzurra De Baciocchi was accused of 
witchcraft for sacrificing young women 
in search of eternal youth, and burned at 
the stake in 1513. The task of the Hunters 
will therefore be to understand what lies 
behind the many disappearances now 
afflicting Sinalunga.

131
The story that led us here…
Two old alchemists, Antonio and Geppetto, have 
worked on a magic oak tree, hoping to obtain the fantastic 
ingredients required to uncover the secret of eternal life. 
They were led to the supernatural discovery by an ancient 
text in their possession, which revealed the alchemical 
formula for unending life, the key component being a body 
made of enchanted wood. 
The mystical material would bestow immortality, but to live 
among men without frightening them it must be covered in 
flesh. In the course of their experiments, the two alchemists 
were astonished to discover that the magical oak had a life 
of its own. Animated by the power of fairy magic, whose 
only purpose is to bedevil humans, this piece of wood 
could bring lies to life.
 
To prove it, the enchanted oak turned into a living, wooden 
puppet named Carlo, who by his nature could not help but 
deceive Geppetto and Antonio about the veracity of their 
theories. Carlo, unable to speak even a single word of truth 
to the two alchemists, convinced them that the rite they 
searched for required harnessing a person’s soul inside a 
wooden body. Antonio and Geppetto then began to kidnap 
the poor and desperate around Sinalunga, torturing and 
flaying them alive according to Carlo’s instructions. After a 
few weeks, horrified by the situation he unwillingly created, 
Carlo escaped to the city. 
After the discovery of the well, Antonio and Geppetto fled 
the old carpenter’s workshop they used as a laboratory and 
took refuge in Villa Cilesterino, an old abandoned manor 
house not far from Sinalunga. Here the two alchemists met 
the spirit of Azzurra, the ancient owner of the villa, who 
wanders in this world longing for past glories. The gullible 
pair did not hesitate to tell her the whole story. Azzurra 
deceived them in turn, animating the mannequins with her 
powers and convincing Geppetto and Antonio that their 
research was heading in the right direction. Azzura’s spirit 
now needs to find Carlo and take advantage of the fairy 
magic that animates him, using it to bring herself back to 
life.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Carlo escaped from the old carpenter’s shop and is now in 
Sinalunga. Every morning, thanks to the power of his lies, 
he disguises himself as a real child. Carlo has even begun 
attending school, where he met Lucino, a loyal and studious 
child who took a liking to the living puppet. At Villa 
Cilesterino, Antonio continues his experiments, animating 
more and more mannequins that he wraps in human flesh 
to make them look like ordinary people. Azzurra’s magic 
completes the “miracle” by combining flesh and wood, 
thereby creating servants to do her will. Geppetto, tricked 
by Azzurra, has instead returned to Sinalunga, where for 
days he has been desperately trying to track down Carlo 
and take him back to his mistress.

1
132
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You have recently arrived 
in Sinalunga. The small town is bustling with activity and 
no one seems to have noticed your presence. However, in 
the eyes of the city’s inhabitants you clearly see a darkness, 
reflecting the horror that has recently been discovered... 
You notice a man in military uniform, intent on talking to 
a couple of peasants who are dragging a large pig with an 
injured leg. The two townspeople look puzzled at the officer, 
and from their disinterested expressions, you understand that 
they can’t wait to get back on the road and leave the insistent 
investigator behind. The Order is in close contact with the 
Grand Duchy of Tuscany, so the officer should be aware that 
a group of Hunters is arriving in town. Could he be an ally in 
your investigation?
Place: The road that crosses the whole town of Sinalunga. 
The townspeople are hard at work, trying to finish all their 
chores before dinner.
Time: It is less than an hour before sunset and the low, 
intensely red sun illuminates the surrounding hills, full of 
vineyards.
Initial situation:  The Hunters have arrived in the 
town of Sinalunga. They already have various pieces of 
information regarding the incident and they know where 
the old well, and makeshift morgue, is located. In front of 
them, Vincenzo Carabiniere, the military officer, is also at 
work to unravel the murder mystery.
Information: What the Hunters already know.
 ◆ The well is a ten-minute walk from the town and was 
part of an old manor estate called Villa Cilesterino.
 ◆ The bodies found had their skin flayed off. It seems that 
the killer wanted only the skin and muscles of their 
victims, but not the organs and entrails.
Speaking with Vincenzo, the Hunters will discover that the 
two farmers he was questioning are Lapo and Cino, the 
ones who found the well. The two were reluctant to talk 
because at the time of their discovery they were hiding at 
the well to eat some bread they had stolen from the bakery. 
The two know very little and Vincenzo will make it clear 
that they are not the sharpest knives in the drawer.
Vincenzo Carabiniere
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a loyal soldier and I always try to do my 
best to help others. Given my integrity, my superiors keep me 
away from battlefields.
Agenda: Find out what lies behind the disappearances in 
Sinalunga.
Sinalunga
Descriptor: A small town in the Tuscan hills. Thanks to its 
famously delicious wine, many in the town manage to have a 
good standard of living.
By wandering around town and talking to the inhabitants, 
the Hunters can get some information. The fear that 
an intervention by the Grand Duchy could upset life in 
Sinalunga means the inhabitants are reluctant to speak to 
anyone other than the authorities. Only Lapo and Cino, 
who are not particularly bright, won’t hesitate to talk with 
the Hunters, though only with the promise of a reward.

133
Threat - Distrustful Inhabitants
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: We don’t want to attract too much attention 
to Sinalunga, so we’re reluctant to talk to foreigners. Nobody 
here wants to lose what they have gained through years of 
hard work.
Information from the townspeople: 
Roll a die or pick from the list.
 ◆ 1-2: 10 people have been found, but 11 went missing. 
Nino’s body is still missing. He is Mastro Antonio’s 
apprentice carpenter. Antonio is known as Mastro 
“Cherry” Ciliegia because of his squat and red nose. 
 ◆ 3-4: Villa Cilesterino is haunted by the ghost of the 
old owner, Azzurra. She was burned at the stake for 
witchcraft.
 ◆ 5-6: The high standard of living in Sinalunga led to the 
opening of Villa Balocchi, a tavern on the edge of town 
where one can find company as well as a comfortable 
bed. The tavern is managed by Amabile Burromo and 
is in open competition with the historic town inn, the 
Gambero Rosso (meaning “the Red Prawn”).
The Old Well
Descriptor: An old abandoned well in the middle of a 
dilapidated vineyard. A nauseating smell from the bottom of 
the well reminds anyone who approaches of the horror that 
it hides.
Information: 
 ◆ The bodies have already been removed and burned to 
avoid infection and disease. 
 ◆ When going down into the well, however, there are still 
some remains and traces of blood. 
 ◆ On the bottom, it is possible to find a carpenter’s chisel 
that Geppetto lost while moving the corpses. 
The Gambero Rosso Inn
Descriptor: A small and cozy tavern, full of bottles of good 
wine.
Information: 
 ◆ Speaking with the innkeeper, a big fat man named 
Duccio, the Hunters will learn that Villa Balocchi is a 
place steeped in sin, where men and women gamble 
and entertain themselves in shady encounters. 
During the evening, Geppetto is at the Gambero Rosso and 
will not hesitate to play out his little rehearsed spectacle of 
the worried father for whoever shows up. Geppetto will 
describe Carlo as an annoying child with a pair of old, 
elegant shoes that Geppetto gave him for his first day of 
school.

134
Geppetto Gullible Alchemist 
Apprentice
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a poor man who has always been looking 
for a way to make a living. I learned the basics of alchemy 
from master Antonio.
Agenda: Pretend to be Carlo’s father and find him / Obtain 
the secret of eternal life, thanks to alchemy.
Gift - Improvised Alchemist:  Geppetto can rely on 
a series of alchemical compounds contained in vials that 
he hides under a tacky yellow wig. When he throws a vial, 
roll a die: 1-2 Explosive: The liquid explodes on contact 
with the air, causing 1 Wound to everyone within thirty 
feet. Thick black smoke will engulf the whole area; 3-4 
Stench: A nauseating odor fills the air giving 1 Penalty to 
all actions within the area for the rest of the Scene; 5-6 Air 
Bomb: All subjects in an area of thirty feet are knocked 
back and fall to the ground. If they hit an obstacle they can 
suffer 1 Wound. Geppetto is immune to the effects of his 
alchemical concoctions.
The Old Carpenter’s Shop
Descriptor: Once the alchemical laboratory of Mastro 
Antonio, the carpenter’s shop was abandoned after the 
discovery of the corpses. 
Information: 
 ◆ The place is a normal carpenter’s shop, abandoned for 
some time. The only element that catches the Hunters’ 
attention is a series of chisels on the wall, with one

135
missing from the set. If the Hunters have recovered the 
chisel in the well they will see that it is the missing tool. 
 ◆ Among other things of interest, the Hunters can find 
an old leather bag with some books for the local school.
At the back of the carpenter’s shop are the remains of a 
hastily packed alchemical laboratory. On the wall, there 
are two human-sized wooden mannequins, skillfully 
made but stained with blood and decomposing skin. These 
are Marionettes, the first two produced by Antonio and 
Geppetto’s experiments, later animated by Azzurra’s magic. 
The Marionettes will spring on the Hunters as soon as they 
get close.
Marionette 
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are souls cursed by Azzurra in the wooden 
bodies of marionettes. We hate all living beings and want to 
be destroyed to be free again.
Agenda: Attacking every human we meet.
Gift - Boundless Rage:  Azzurra’s power animates and 
forces the Marionettes to do evil acts. When a Marionette 
suffers the last Wound it will immediately spring on the 
enemy with incredible momentum as Azzurra’s power 
compels them one last time. The Marionette can take a 
final action with OL Hard 7. This action must be aimed at 
harming a living being.
The School
Descriptor: A small school for the studious children of 
Sinalunga. Jolly voices and shouts cover the lamentation of 
a dispirited teacher.
Information: 
 ◆ The school is open in the morning and brings together 
all the town’s children who want to learn something. 
The situation is clearly out of control and the teacher, 
a young academic from Florence, does not know what 
to do. 
 ◆ The school is attended by Carlo who, however, appears 
as an ordinary child. In fact, he does not correspond 
to the description that Geppetto may have given. The 
old shoes on his feet are the only clue that the Hunters 
may notice.

136
Carlo The Living 
Puppet
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a puppet animated by fairy magic. The 
lies I tell become reality for a short time.
Agenda: Living a carefree life without responsibility / I don’t 
want to abandon Sinalunga and my new friend Lucino, but 
I’m always afraid that Geppetto and Antonio will catch me. 
Gift - Lies: Carlo can bring the lies he tells to life 
by creating a Descriptor that allows him to 
perform incredible feats. All lies must be 
centered on him. For example: I can walk 
through walls or I am invisible to the human 
eye. The effects of the lies’ power are literal. 
For this reason, Carlo would not be able to 
go through a closed door and he would be 
seen by animals. The effect of Lies lasts for 1 
Scene or until a new lie is told.
Lucino - The Good Friend
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a dutiful and studious child 
who can’t wait to learn a trade to help my family.
Agenda: Help Carlo become a responsible 
child / Work to help adults who behave well 
and follow the law.
Villa Balocchi
Descriptor: A beautiful and sumptuous inn, recently 
refurbished.
Information: 
 ◆ Amabile hosts little Carlo, making him sleep in a small 
back room. 
 ◆ The Puppet pays the greedy innkeeper with stones 
which he temporarily turns into gold coins with his 
powers. Throwing the coins into a safe in his room, the 
innkeeper hasn’t yet noticed the Puppet’s trick. 
 ◆ During the afternoon and evening, Carlo is at Villa 
Balocchi and can be found playing with Lucino or 
pretending to study.
Amabile Burromo
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a skilled business owner 
and I manage my inn without delegating 
anything to anyone. I trust myself only.
Agenda: To make profitable deals, 
without worrying about scruples.

137
Villa 
Cilesterino
Descriptor: An old abandoned manor house. Weeds and 
plants have covered everything, while the large garden is 
abandoned to the passing of time.
Information: 
 ◆ Villa Cilesterino is divided into 3 environments: the 
Garden, the Villa, and the Secret Laboratory. 
 ◆ Azzurra is connected to the whole place and will always 
be aware of anyone who ventures onto her property.
The Garden
Descriptor: A large abandoned garden where flowers and 
plants of all kinds grow. 
Information: 
 ◆ The Garden is guarded by 4 Marionettes and by 
old Medoro, a sick bear that Azzurra enslaved with 
her powers. Wandering around the Garden will 
immediately alert the guardians.
Medoro The Old Bear
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a deformed old bear that has lost all its 
fur. Azzurra treats me like her faithful dog and I am happy to 
serve her. I have retained most of my bestial strength, despite 
my age.
The Villa
Descriptor: A large manor house, once flamboyant, but 
now covered in dust and mold. 
Information: 
 ◆ Azzurra is wandering around the Villa and will not 
hesitate to appear and confront intruders.
 ◆ In the dining room, there is a huge painting on the 
north wall depicting a whale swallowing an entire 
ship. By looking behind the painting, the Hunters can 
activate the mechanism which opens the secret door to 
access the Secret Laboratory.
 ◆ The remaining 4 Marionettes covered in human skin 
roam around the Villa, but they only act on Azzurra’s 
orders.

138
Azzurra The Spirit of the Witch who 
Spurns Death
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the spirit of an old and powerful witch. My powers allow 
me to summon the souls of the dead and bind them to my will. 
Agenda: To come back to life thanks to Carlo’s power.
Gift - Ancient Spirit:  Azzurra’s body is immaterial and can only be 
damaged by magic. Azzurra can interact with the world by evoking the souls 
of the dead, choosing between various effects. 1. Poltergeist: Throw up to 
3 objects at once. The force with which the objects are thrown is such that 
they can cause a Wound. 2. Cacophony of the Dead: Frighten everyone 
with the agonized voices of the damned. Anyone who hears the cacophony 
must pass a Defense Check or lose 2 Soma points. 3. Possession: Take 
possession of a target’s body and cause them to perform a single action, 
albeit not a self-destructive one. Azzurra must change the spirits’ effect with 
each action and must use all three before she can use an effect again.
The Secret Laboratory
Descriptor: The cellar of Villa Cilesterino, converted into an alchemical 
laboratory by Antonio. The smell of wine and mold is unbearable.
Information: 
 ◆ Antonio continues his research under Azzurra’s guidance. This led him 
to create a magical brazier in which Carlo is to be burned.
 ◆ Nino, the carpenter’s apprentice, is tied to a table, alive but with his legs 
now ruined by Antonio’s slow flaying. Azzurra wants him kept alive, as 
she needs innocent blood for the rite.

139
Antonio Ciliegia 
Gullible Alchemist
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an old alchemist who has never discovered 
anything concrete. Despite my skills and my experience, luck 
is never on my side.
Agenda: Obtaining the secret of eternal life thanks to 
alchemy. 
Gift - Unlucky Alchemist: Antonio can rely on a series 
of alchemical compounds contained in vials that he hides 
in his tunic. When using a vial he rolls 1 die. On a 5 or 
6 something goes wrong and Antonio suffers 1 Wound 
due to his proverbial bad luck. 1. Fire: The target and the 
surrounding area catch fire. All targets involved suffer 1 
Wound. If the fire is not extinguished, burning targets 
suffer 1 Wound per action. 2. Thunder:  The target 
is knocked over thirty feet away and ends up on the 
ground. If they hit something they will suffer 1 
Wound. 3. Glue: an adhesive compound that 
quickly solidifies in the air. The target will be 
paralyzed until the compound is removed. 
The Glue can take 2 Wounds.

140
Events of the Scenario
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters put pressure on Carlo:  Lucino will 
intervene to help his friend by giving him time to escape.
If the Hunters reveal their identity to Vincenzo:   
the officer will be at their disposal, trying to help them 
whenever possible. 
If the Hunters meet Geppetto: the man will attempt to 
play on their sympathies with the made-up story of the lost 
son. Geppetto is not a genius, but neither is he stupid. If 
he understands who the Hunters really are, he will try to 
follow them to see if they find Carlo.
If the Hunters gain Carlo’s trust:  the Puppet will 
reveal the truth. Geppetto and Antonio are to blame for 
everything. The Puppet ignores that it is now Azzurra who 
runs things.
If Geppetto is captured by the Hunters:  he will lead 
them to Villa Cilesterino, where his companion is. Geppetto 
will pretend to know nothing, aware that once they arrive, 
Azzurra will get rid of the Hunters.
When Azzurra meets the Hunters:  she will try to 
deceive them by pretending to be a good spirit who tries 
to watch over the inhabitants of Sinalunga. Azzurra will 
ask the Hunters to help her recover a magical Puppet, 
animated by a dark force that is devouring the souls of the 
poor townsfolk.
Exclusive Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Flesh and 
Wood Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario Gift. If 
they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will have to 
forgo the previous one.
Marionettes’ Destroyer
Requirement: Defeat any Marionette or understand that 
they are souls imprisoned against their will.
Y ou know how to calm restless spirits by giving them 
a proper greeting when their soul is freed. Y ou are not 
affected by the Boundless Rage Gift. Receive 1 Additional 
Success when defending against beings that are animated 
or controlled by spirits or magic.
A True Friend 
Requirement: Gain Carlo’s trust despite all his lies.
Carlo understands that he can trust you and will run to 
your aid if you need it. Y ou can spend 1 Soma to have Carlo 
appear in the Scene as your NPC ally. Carlo will use the 
power of his lies to help you to the best of his ability.
The Field of Miracles (Special Gift for The Fox 
and the Cat)
Requirement: Arrive in Sinalunga.
Y ou know Sinalunga well because it is where one of your 
secret shelters is located. Once per Scene you can spend 1 
Soma so you or another subject can leave the Scene and 
hide in the Field of Miracles. No one can find the exact 
location of the shelter. When you are at the Field, once per 
Session you can recover 1 Wound you have suffered.
