3a
141
The Scenario of 
Flesh and Wood in 
Scenes 
FIRST SCENE - Arrival In 
Sinalunga 
It’s the same one you find on page 132.
Information: 
 ◆ Speaking with Vincenzo or the citizens, the Hunters 
will discover that Villa Cilesterino is a place considered 
cursed. 
 ◆ They’ll also discover that the well where the corpses 
were found is located on the edge of the same estate as 
the villa.
End of Scene:  The Hunters leave in the direction of the 
old well to better understand what happened. If they want, 
Vincenzo will accompany them. Go to SECOND SCENE - 
The Old Well.
SECOND SCENE - The Old 
Well
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You find the old well after a 
ten-minute walk from the town. On the nearby hill, the old, 
shabby silhouette of Villa Cilesterino looms over you like a 
dark shadow. The boards that closed the well are uprooted and 
are now just laying across the top. The stench of death still 
lingers in the air, still strong enough that it cannot be ignored.
Location: The old well on the edge of the Cilesterino estate. 
Time: The sun is now setting and it won’t be light for much 
longer.
Information: 
 ◆ By descending into the well, the Hunters will find 
Geppetto’s chisel. 
 ◆ Around the well there are traces of people which then 
lead towards the abandoned villa.
End of Scene:  The Hunters can decide to investigate the 
Old Carpenter’s Shop from which the chisel surely comes 
(THIRD SCENE OPTION A - At The Old Carpenter’s Shop) 
or follow the tracks to Villa Cilesterino (THIRD SCENE 
OPTION B - Villa Cilesterino).
THIRD SCENE OPTION A - 
At The Old Carpenter’s Shop
Read aloud to the Hunters:  From the outside, the 
workshop seems abandoned. A large sign outside sways, blown 
by the wind. It reads “Mastro Antonio Ciliegia, Carpenter. ” 
Apparently what they told you in the town is true. It seems 
that Antonio has left and closed up his business. 
Place: Mastro Antonio’s carpenter’s shop, a well-kept place 
that, on the inside, does not seem abandoned at all.
Time: It is night now and no inhabitants of Sinalunga 
wander the streets. The shutters in the windows flutter as 
prying eyes stare at the Hunters.
Information: 
 ◆ Upon entering the Old Carpenter’s Shop, the Hunters 
will come across the Marionettes on guard. Once the

43b
142
clash is resolved, the Hunters will be able to verify that 
the chisel found in the well is just what is missing from 
the wall rack. 
 ◆ A school bag full of old books attracts their attention. 
Among other things, they find an unsent letter 
requesting enrollment for a boy named Carlo.
End of Scene:  Hunters can go to Villa Cilesterino 
(THIRD SCENE OPTION B - Villa Cilesterino) or wait for 
the morning to go to FOURTH SCENE - The School.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B - 
Villa Cilesterino
Read aloud to the Hunters: As soon as you step into the 
large garden of the abandoned villa you realize that what was 
once a magnificent garden is now nothing more than a thick 
mass of scrub and weathered stone statues. Your attention, 
however, is attracted by a bestial verse, which materializes in 
the grotesque figure of a bear, but one without fur, the hairless 
skin pocked by who knows what disease. The creature sniffs 
the air. After spotting you, it starts charging. 
Time: It is night and a cloud-veiled moon illuminates the 
old marble statues in the garden with a gloomy glow.
Information: 
 ◆ Medoro lashes out at the Hunters. Azzurra will 
intervene at the end of the battle to ask the Hunters to 
spare the unfortunate creature she uses as a guardian. 
Then, she will show them the Marionettes in the 
garden, saying that in the town someone is carrying out 
atrocious experiments on the townsfolk. Azzurra will 
tell the Hunters that everything started with a small 
puppet animated by dark magic. Azzurra will ask for 
the Hunters’ help. They should bring back the puppet 
who hides among the townspeople pretending to be a 
real child named Carlo. Once captured and brought 
before her, Azzurra will use her power to banish the 
evil spirit that animates him.
 ◆ Azzurra’s intention is to deceive the Hunters, in the 
hope that they will bring her little Carlo.
End of Scene: Hunters can also go to the Old Carpenter’s 
Shop in search of clues (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - At 
The Old Carpenter’s Shop) or wait for the morning to go to 
FOURTH SCENE - The School if they have already obtained 
the clues from the carpentry. 
FOURTH SCENE - The School
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The small school of 
Sinalunga welcomes no more than thirty students. A young 
teacher with the typical Florentine accent yells and gestures 
in a vain attempt to get the unruly young scholars to settle 
down. But children run everywhere like ferrets, ignoring the 
disconsolate man’s calls. 
Information: 
 ◆ Watching the children play, the Hunters will notice 
that one of them has no school bag or books with him. 
If asked about it, the teacher will say that the pupil is 
called Carlo and that he is an orphan who comes to 
school but has never been officially enrolled. The man 
is not sure where he lives and what he does outside of 
school.
 ◆ If a Hunter knows magic, they will sense that there is 
something strange about the child.
 ◆ Carlo, sure of his disguise, plays with his friend Lucino, 
but as soon as the Hunters corner him he will try to 
escape using the power of his lies.

5
143
 ◆ Once captured, Carlo will reveal the whole truth to 
the Hunters. The Puppet doesn’t know about Azzurra 
but will speak of Geppetto and Antonio as two 
unscrupulous killers.
End of Scene:  The Hunters have managed to capture 
Carlo. Whether they decide to believe him or not, it is clear 
that Villa Cilesterino is hiding a secret. Go to the FIFTH 
SCENE - Return to the Villa.
FIFTH SCENE - Return to the 
Villa
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you arrive at the 
Villa you perceive a strange tension in the air. The weeds that 
had swallowed the garden are uprooted as if displaced by a 
supernatural force that has created a path for you. The door 
of the mansion is wide open, while from the broken windows, 
the dim light of the candelabra suggests that some kind of 
welcome is awaiting you.
Location: Villa Cilesterino and the Secret Laboratory in 
the basement, where Antonio and Geppetto carry out their 
grim experiments.
Time: It is just past noon, but the sky is full of clouds, 
giving a gloomy atmosphere to the abandoned villa.
Information: At this point, the story can go in two 
distinct directions.
 ◆ If the Hunters trust Azzurra, the spirit will hand over 
Carlo to Antonio and Geppetto. The two will take 
refuge in the basement dragging the weeping puppet 
with them. At this point, Azzurra will order the 
Hunters to leave. If not listened to, she will try to get rid 
of them with the help of the Marionettes and Medoro, 
if it’s still alive.
 ◆ If the Hunters believe Carlo, Azzurra will try to kill 
them in order to capture the puppet but will not 
hesitate to flee if the situation turns for the worse. 
Antonio and Geppetto, abandoned by their mistress, 
will take refuge in the Secret Laboratory, trying to put 
up a last resistance to the Hunters.
End of Scene:  However it goes, the Hunters will have 
Carlo’s fate in their hands and somehow, they will shed light 
on the disappearances that took place in Sinalunga.

144
The Smile in 
the Darkness
Once upon a 
time…
The Hunters are sent to Paris, where someone 
has been setting fires in the city’s slums for 
weeks. At first, some assumed the radicals and 
agitators opposing the monarchy were to blame. 
However, many of the fires’ victims have been 
the poor and desperate living beneath the city 
in the catacombs, suggesting a different cause 
and drawing the interest of the Order. Father 
Adrien, a humble servant of the Church in the 
city, has reported rumors that speak of the eerie 
laughter of children and glimpses of strange, 
dark presences. Hidden in the shadows, these 
entities grab all those who have a soul to lose...
A Scenario inspired by The Little Match Girl (Hans Christian Andersen)
Safety Keywords: Murder / Revenge / Corpses / Religion / Burning Flesh

145
The story that led us here…
Yvonne was a ragged, little orphan who sold 
candles as a way of surviving on the streets. The girl was 
scorned by all, even by the poor of the city who had always 
considered her only to be a burden. Little Yvonne found 
compassion only in Father Adrien, a young priest who 
had always offered her a helping hand and a warm shelter 
from the cold Parisian winter. Unfortunately, one evening 
Yvonne discovered the laboratory where Adrien distills 
absinthe. The man, his sin discovered, strangled the girl 
into unconsciousness. To hide his deed, Adrien set the little 
girl on fire and threw her remains in the catacombs. What 
was found the following day was a small, charred body with 
only one part left intact: the teeth, shining white and locked 
in a deathly grin. Many poor people in the city who depend 
on Adrien for a few pennies worth of work witnessed the 
scene, but they kept quiet so as not to lose the priest’s favor. 
Thrown into a mass grave without ceremony, Yvonne was 
reborn the following night as a spirit. Her vengeful ghost 
now wanders in the darkness of The Catacombs of Paris, 
where she torments those who were silent at her murder 
with maddening laughter and vicious whispers. When 
her quarry sparks a light to discover who is hiding in the 
darkness, the spirit feeds the flame to set the victim afire. 
Yvonne is slowly burning alive all those who know her 
murderer and yet say nothing, one by one, saving Adrien 
for her grand finale.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Yvonne is carrying out her revenge with methodical 
determination, killing one person every night. Only a 
young man named Olivier has escaped her attention and 
then she can pour out all her hatred on Adrien. If left  
unchecked, Yvonne plans to burn all of Paris, igniting the 
Cemetery of the Innocents, full of rotting flesh and slime 
from the corpses crammed inside. With the wisdom of the 
dead, Yvonne knows she will only be able to satisfy her need 
for revenge during the passage of a comet called Morituris. 
With the comet’s passage, the souls of the unquiet dead 
will fill with mystical energy, which Yvonne will be able to 
channel into an inferno. The passage of the long-tailed star 
is scheduled for December 31, coincidentally the day the 
Hunters arrive in Paris. Adrien understands that something 
fearsome and dark is pursuing his flock of followers, which 
is why he wrote to the Order. The priest is sure that it all 
has to do with Yvonne’s death, as every night he dreams 
and relives the moment he set the child on fire, before the 
vision widens to reveal the whole of Paris burning. Olivier, 
a barely eighteen-year-old boy in Adrien’s service, saw his 
companion Thierry be burnt alive after Yvonne’s spirit 
began to seek revenge. The young man confided in Adrien 
but quickly realized that the priest would do nothing for 
him. He is now desperately trying to leave the city in a 
hurry. Olivier doesn’t know that he is the next one on 
Yvonne’s list…
A historical note: The Catacombs of Paris were born as 
gypsum and clay quarries and only began to be used as a 
sepulcher starting in 1788. In our story, the government of 
Paris has already begun to work out future plans for the 
catacombs and this name has already spread among the 
citizens of Paris. It is worth noting also that, in reality, the 
Cemetery of the Innocents was closed in 1780 and emptied 
in 1786.

1
146
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: Our story begins in the small 
church of Saint Pierre, where Father Adrien welcomes you 
tiredly. The young priest has been in the service of the city 
of Paris for just over a year, but already wrinkles caused by 
worry and stress are starting to show. Adrien has the swollen 
eyes of someone who hasn’t slept for days and his fatigue is 
obvious as he guides you into the small sacristy. “You have 
finally arrived, Hunters. A hidden presence lurks beneath the 
whole city and I fear there is no hope of salvation without the 
intervention of the Order... ”
Place: The small church of Saint Pierre, one of the oldest 
in the city.
Time: It is late afternoon and the Hunters have arrived 
after a long journey.
Initial Situation:  Adrien will tell the Hunters that he 
fears an evil spirit is haunting the catacombs. Many beggars 
have been found burned alive. And, while in the wealthier 
parts of the city not a finger is raised for the lowest of society, 
Adrien has always welcomed and cared for them, and he 
cannot allow this to go on. From interviewing the young 
priest, the Hunters will learn the following information:
 ◆ The denizens of the catacombs insist a spirit wanders 
beneath the city, where many of the poor and desperate 
sleep in the absence of a roof over their heads. The 
presence manifests itself only at night, first with a 
childish laugh and then by whispering in the shadows.
 ◆ A certain Lorenzo Scannapecore settled in the city 
cemetery. Lorenzo is an Italian doctor very famous for 
his unorthodox practices on corpses and who is said 
to be able to speak with the dead. Lorenzo was hired 
by the cemetery manager to investigate strange noises 
coming from the mass graves.
 ◆ Adrien will be careful not to mention little Yvonne and 
the fact that she was the first victim to be found.
The Church 
Descriptor: One of the oldest churches in Paris. An old 
passage directly connects the cellars where Adrien has his 
workshop to the catacombs.
Information: The church is not well maintained, but 
welcoming. In the basement, there is a large cellar that 
Adrien has adapted into his laboratory. The smell of 
absinthe is clearly recognizable to anyone who has the 
slightest knowledge of distillation as soon as they set foot 
in the laboratory. A heavy trap door connects the cellars to 
the catacombs and from there Adrien passes his loads of 
absinthe to his henchmen.
Father Adrien
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds) 
Descriptor: I am a young priest who chose to take holy 
vows to avoid a life of struggle. I am a scholar of alchemy and 
distillation, but I do not shy away from the pleasures of life. 
Agenda: Accumulate wealth through absinthe smuggling 
and then flee across the ocean to rebuild my life in the new 
world.

147
The Catacombs
Descriptor: A series of tunnels and galleries that extends 
under most of Paris. Over time, they have become a refuge 
for the poor and homeless.
Gift - A Tunnel for Everywhere:  Using the 
catacombs, it is possible to reach many places in 
the city through hidden openings or old buildings. 
A Hunter who has a minimum of familiarity with 
the place (that is, who has visited at least 1 of the 
6 places presented below) can make a Position 
Check against an OL Medium 5 to arrive at a 
specific place in the city.
The catacombs are divided into environ-
ments that the Hunters can explore. Y ou 
can roll a die to see where they arrive or 
simply decide the place when needed.
1 - The Dormitories:  A 
series of tunnels and galleries that make 
up an old gypsum and clay quarry. 
Connected to one of the city’s squares, 
poor and homeless people take refuge 
here to have a roof over their heads. By 
exploring the dormitories it is possible 
to gather some information:
 ◆ In the catacombs, there are 
many scoundrels who engage in 
smuggling and illicit commerce. It 
is quite easy, especially at night, to 
come across one of these groups.
 ◆ Spirits roam the catacombs, though 
by the grace of God and with a 
little luck, they rarely trouble good 
folks. Recently, several burned bodies have been found 
in the catacombs and for this reason, those who feel 
they are being followed by a strange presence prefer to 
douse torches and other flames.
The poor people of Paris
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the poor and desperate who have 
no other home but the catacombs.
When the Hunters interact with the poor, you can 
decide or roll a die to learn their history and what 
information they can give.
1. Marie lost all her children and her husband to 
the war. She had grown fond of little Yvonne and 
weeps for the end she met.
2. Claude is a soldier who lost a leg in the war. 
He wanted to go into business with the Ravens 
who live in the east side of the catacombs, but 
they are all young and arrogant and have 
turned down his offer.
3. Brigitte is a little girl who lost her parents 
during the most recent plague. She knew 
Yvonne and occasionally went to sell candles 
with her. She knows that Adrien had taken 
Yvonne into his service to do small jobs in the 
sacristy.
4. Jerome is an old man who has almost 
completely lost his sight. In the past, he was 
a skilled goldsmith, until his blindness left 
him without a job. He will say that he heard

148
the Ravens boast about the profits they made thanks 
to an important customer. Their mysterious employer 
is someone who will never be suspected and who can 
therefore provide goods in defiance of any authority.
5. Bernadette is a youth who witnessed a murder and 
is now wanted. She fears that the killer wants to make 
her disappear and shut her mouth for good. The young 
woman will tell of having encountered a strange 
presence in the catacombs. In terror, Bernadette threw 
her torch to the ground and fled into the darkness.
6. Bruno is an arrogant street boy, but he knows the 
catacombs like the back of his hand. He’ll boast that he 
can get anywhere, but he wants to get paid for it.
2 - Yvonne’s Alcove: A small stone alcove with 
a bed of straw and rags. An old vase of withered flowers 
indicates that no one has been using it for some time. If the 
Hunters ask around, a poor old woman will tell them that 
a foundling named Yvonne slept there. The girl hasn’t been 
back for more than a week.
3 - The Tunnels: It’s easy to get lost in 
the maze of intricate tunnels.
Threat OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A dense network of tunnels 
that all look alike.
Getting lost in the Tunnels will force 
the Hunters to waste several hours 
before finding the exit.
4 - The Ravens’ Hideout:  This part of 
the catacombs is only frequented by scoundrels who had 
business with Adrien. A couple of broken bottles of absinthe 
are scattered among some makeshift beds. To those with a 
sensitive sense of smell, the scent of alcohol is still strong. 
An old dagger and a wooden crucifix are the only other 
noteworthy details.
Information: If the Hunters arrive here before sunset, they 
will find Olivier preparing his escape. When night falls, they 
will find his charred corpse.
Olivier the Raven on the run
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I’m a thug always looking to fill my pockets with 
coin, no matter how dirty the job.
Agenda: Collect the money I have hidden in the catacombs 
and flee far from Paris before the fire spirit finds me.
5 - The Burns: Scattered through various tunnels, 
large patches of ash indicate where the bodies of the burned-
alive victims were found. Under the ashes of Yvonne’s, it is 
possible to find a small candle, miraculously untouched by 
the flames. During the day, Yvonne remains near the place 
where she burned, sadly absorbed in the memory of her 
death.

149
Yvonne the Spirit who Longs for 
Revenge
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a restless spirit who can manipulate fire.
Agenda: Get revenge on Adrien and all those who ignored 
my death / Set Paris ablaze with the fires of the dead.
Gift - Spirit:  Yvonne is in a spirit form and can only 
truly be damaged by magic. The touch of a Spirit absorbs 
life energy and inflicts 1 Wound. Physical protection 
and barriers cannot help against this Gift. If the Spirit 
is harmed by non-magical means, she still will suffer a 
Wound. However, when Yvonne has no Wounds left, she 
disappears and rematerializes immediately. In doing so, all 
her Wounds are healed, except those inflicted by magical 
means. Once per Scene, Yvonne can call 3 Restless Spirits 
to her who will obey her will.
Gift - Soul of Fire:  Yvonne can manipulate fire at will. 
This Gift only affects a fire that is already lit. Manipulating 
fire, for Yvonne, is considered an additional action. The fire 
inflicts 1 Wound and if the Defense Check is an Outcome 
with a Cost, the target will continue to burn. During the 
following Exchange, if not put out, the fire will inflict 
1 additional Wound. Thanks to her power, Yvonne can 
multiply the width and size of a flame by up to ten times.

150
6 - The Halls of the Spirits: In the most 
central part of the catacombs there is a great concentration 
of restless souls, who will not hesitate to target the living.
Restless spirits
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are restless souls and yearn for the essence 
of the living.
Agenda: We sense Yvonne’s wrath, driving us to prey on the 
living.
Gift - Spirit: This NPC is in a spirit form and can only be 
damaged by magic. The touch of a Spirit absorbs life energy 
and inflicts 1 Wound. Physical protection and barriers 
cannot help against this Gift. If the Spirit is harmed by 
non-magical means, it still will suffer a Wound. However, 
when the Spirit has no Wounds left, it disappears and 
rematerializes immediately. In doing so, all its Wounds are 
healed, except those inflicted by magical means. 
Information: If a Hunter communicates with Spirits 
through the Gift Voice of the Spirits, they will be able to 
learn of Yvonne’s presence.
The Cemetery
Descriptor: The great cemetery of Paris, protected by 
high walls and well-maintained thanks to the work of many 
custodians. Strange noises and a strong smell of death come 
from the mass graves.
Information: The cemetery is currently under the control 
of Lorenzo Scannapecore, an Italian doctor hired by the city 
of Paris to manage the excessive number of corpses inside 
the mass graves. Lorenzo has sent the cemetery keepers 
away with the excuse of requiring quiet for his studies. 
However, that was only a ruse to secretly host Alizée, a nun 
he has fallen in love with and whom he keeps safe from the 
severe judgment of the Church. Lorenzo’s intent is to wait 
until the new year to embark with his beloved towards the 
New World.
 ◆ Speaking with Lorenzo, the Hunters can learn about 
the dire state of the mass graves of the cemetery, which 
in fact is about to explode due to the excessive number 
of decaying corpses. Lorenzo will appear strangely 
nervous, looking wary. The man fears that the Hunters 
may discover Alizée hidden among the tombstones.
 ◆ Lorenzo is also a good astrologer and will be able to tell 
the Hunters about the passage of Morituris and how, 
according to the legend, this gives power to the spirits.
 ◆ Alizée will observe the Hunters from behind some 
tombstones, trying not to be discovered. However, she 
will always remain nearby, ready to intervene to help 
her beloved.
 ◆ Alizée will try not to reveal her abilities. When cornered, 
she may report that the spirits are very agitated, both 
due to the presence of a very powerful and hateful 
spirit among them, and due to the imminent passage 
of the comet.
 ◆ Strange smells and noises come from the mass graves. 
Lorenzo will say that it is the dead bodies generating

151
gas during decomposition. By opening one of the large 
hatches, it is possible to access the underground pit.
 ◆ Lorenzo has all the keys to open the hatches to access the 
underground pit.
Lorenzo Scannapecore the 
Plague Doctor
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a skilled plague doctor. My attention to 
detail is obsessive.
Agenda: Pretend to work in the cemetery to protect Alizée 
from prying eyes.
Alizée the Fugitive
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a young nun who repudiated her vows out 
of love. I have always been able to see spirits, though my ability 
sometimes scares me.
Agenda: Escape with Lorenzo to a place where we don’t need 
to fear the Church’s punishment.
Gift - Spiritualist:  Alizée can see and communicate with 
spirits. If anyone has been in contact with spirits in the past 
twenty-four hours, Alizée will sense the remaining spirit 
energy. With each use of this Gift, the young woman can 
obtain a single piece of information about the spirit aura she 
has perceived. The Spiritualist Gift can also be used to remove 
a Grudge Token from the Gift Mass of Restless Spirits.
The Underground Pit
Descriptor: A huge pit where the corpses of all those who 
cannot afford a burial are thrown. Crowds of restless spirits 
throng among the inert bodies, invisible to mundane eyes.
Gift - Mass of Restless Spirits:  The pit accumulates 
spiritual energy thanks to the passage of the comet Morituris. 
At the beginning of each Scene, there are 2 Grudge Tokens 
in the pit. An NPC with the Gift Spirit, Alizée with the Gift  
Spiritualist, or a Hunter with a Descriptor associated with 
magic or spirits, can use their action to add or remove 1 
Token from the mass. If at midnight on December 31st there 
are 3 Tokens in the pit, all the accumulated energy will cause 
a powerful explosion capable of wiping out half of Paris. 
The passage of the comet will add 1 Token at the stroke of 
midnight. There can never be more than 4 Grudge Tokens on 
this Gift.
Threat - Grudge of the Dead
Descriptor: We are the spirits of those who are dead and 
forgotten by all. We hate the living and their life energy, and we 
attack anyone who dares to approach the underground pit.
OL Hard 7
Gift - Chorus of the Dead: A powerful spirit cacophony that 
only those who perceive spirits or understand magic can hear. 
For everyone else, the chorus is nothing more than a terrible, 
overwhelming feeling that prompts you to walk away. Anyone 
wishing to act in the area where the choir can be heard will 
lose 1 Soma for each minute staying under its influence. If 
they do not have Soma, a subject will suffer a Wound until 
they faint. A Defense Check can shelter a subject for 1 minute 
from the effect of this Gift.

152
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Smile 
in the Darkness Scenario and can be acquired once the 
requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario 
Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Voice of the Spirits
Requirement: You have been in contact with either the 
Spirit or Spiritualist Gifts.
With the right peace and focus, you can open your mind 
to the spirit world and communicate with restless souls. 
Spend 1 Soma to contact any creature with the Spirit Gift  
that you’ve previously encountered. Y ou can communicate 
with the Spirit and understand what it says.
Exorcist
Requirement: Have a Church or Magic-related Descriptor. 
Talk to Adrien or Alizée about practices used against Spirits.
Y ou have learned how to use your skills against the Spirits. 
All your actions can affect a target who has the Spirit Gift  
and all Wounds you inflict cannot be ignored by the Spirit 
Gift.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters encounter Olivier in the catacombs 
before night falls:  Yvonne will arrive before the 
scoundrel opens his mouth to try and silence him by taking 
her revenge.
If the Hunters talk to more than two poor people 
in the catacombs: Yvonne will notice their presence and 
will come to spy on them, trying to stay hidden.
If Olivier is not found before sunset:  news of a new 
charred body will spread in the catacombs shortly after 10 
pm.
If Olivier is saved by the Hunters:   he will tell of a 
strange presence that has killed his companions. The 
scoundrel won’t make mention of his connections with 
Adrien and Yvonne unless put under pressure in some way.
If Adrien is confronted with his faults:   he will 
collapse in tears and ask for forgiveness. At this point, 
he will reveal the recurring dream in which he sees Paris 
burning on New Y ear’s Eve.
If the Hunters attempt to mediate with Yvonne:  the 
spirit will be willing to give up blowing up the graveyard, 
but not her revenge on Adrien.

1
2 3
153
The Scenario 
The Smile in the 
Darkness in Scenes
FIRST SCENE – Father Adrien
It’s the same one you find on page 146.
End of Scene: Adrien will suggest to the Hunters to speak 
to Lorenzo Scannapecore, as he is an expert on the dead 
and ghosts. The doctor lives in the cemetery in Paris. Go to 
SECOND SCENE - The Cemetery.
SECOND SCENE – The 
Cemetery
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The Cemetery of the 
Innocents stretches as far as the eyes can see and is probably 
one of the largest you have ever seen. As you wander through 
tombstones and family mausoleums, a black-cloaked figure 
is busy fumbling among the tombs. The typical beak mask 
identifies him as a plague doctor. The figure had been 
occupied exhuming a grave, but now he puts down his shovel 
and approaches you.
Place: The great cemetery of Paris.
Time: It is late afternoon and the winter wind whistles 
through the corridors of the cemetery.
Information: Lorenzo will prove helpful to the Hunters, 
while Alizée will keep watch from behind a noble’s large 
tomb. Lorenzo will be visibly nervous, however, often 
looking over his shoulder at her. It’s simple to deduce that 
someone is hiding nearby.
 ◆ Having discovered Alizée, the Hunters will know 
from her that the spirits are restless, due partly to the 
passage of the comet, but above all from the presence 
of a particularly angry spirit in the catacombs.
 ◆ Lorenzo will confirm the passage of the comet 
Morituris and how it charges the spiritual world with 
energy.
 ◆ Towards the end of the interview, Father Adrien will 
arrive to warn the Hunters that another charred corpse 
has been found in the catacombs.
End of Scene:  The Hunters rush to the 
catacombs to find out what happened. Go to 
THIRD SCENE - The Catacombs.
THIRD SCENE – The 
Catacombs
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You go 
down in The Catacombs of Paris and what 
you see is a maze of narrow tunnels that 
open onto small chambers and dark alcoves. 
The stale smell mixes with the stench of 
unwashed, desperate people crowded into 
the narrow spaces. Apparently, those who 
do not have a roof over their head can find a 
shadow of comfort under the city, effectively 
disappearing from the sight and conscience of 
“respectable” people.

4a
154
Place: The Catacombs of Paris.
Time: The sun has set and in the streets, the last workers 
hurry to return to their homes. 
Information: The Hunters explore the catacombs until 
reaching the place where Olivier was found. The body is still 
there, along with an intense burning smell. The expression 
of sheer terror is still discernible on his charred features. If 
the Hunters have Alizée with them, the woman will sense 
Yvonne’s presence. Either way, the spirit will attack the 
Hunters with the support of 3 Restless Spirits. Yvonne’s goal 
is to drive the Hunters out of the catacombs.
 ◆ Regardless of how the fight ends, the Hunters will be 
approached by Marie, who witnessed the scene. The 
woman will reveal that the spirit is little Yvonne, a 
foundling who lived by selling candles Father Adrien 
had given her. The little girl has been missing for more 
than a week and Marie was convinced she had gone to 
stay with Adrien, given the charming, almost paternal 
relationship that was forged between the two. It will 
be clear to the Hunters that Adrien has not told the 
whole truth, but at the same moment, a roar from the 
graveyard will cause the earth to shake.  
End of Scene:  The Hunters can go find Adrien at the 
church (FOURTH SCENE OPTION A - Confession ) 
or head to the cemetery to find the source of the roar 
(FOURTH SCENE OPTION B - Return to the Cemetery).
Note: Given the obvious emergency of both situations, 
advise the Hunters to split up to deal with both Scenes.
 
FOURTH SCENE OPTION 
A – The Confession
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at the church 
looking for answers when Adrien’s cry breaches the 
stillness of the night. When you open the door, the priest is 
kneeling in the center of the aisle, his face covered in tears 
as he whispers, “Forgive me, please forgive me for what I 
have done. ” In front of him, the spirit of the little girl you 
faced in the catacombs floats ominously, while behind her, 
the flames of the offering candles swell until they become 
tongues of fire.
Place: Father Adrien’s church.
Time: It is almost midnight on December 31st and it is 
possible to observe the passing comet, Morituris, in the 
sky outside the church.
Information: Yvonne intends to kill Adrien. If 
stopped by the Hunters, she will inform them that in 
less than an hour the cemetery will explode thanks to 
the anger of the spirits she has stirred up.
 ◆ Cornered, Adrien will confess to what he did to 
Yvonne and to his involvement in the absinthe 
trade. The priest, however, will try to distract the 
Hunters with the knowledge that now the little girl 
is a spirit full of anger and, in fact, a hostile creature 
of the sort that the Order would hunt down.

4b
5
155
 ◆ Adrien will try to escape through the secret passage 
in the cellar if he has the opportunity. The man is 
absolutely not sorry for what he did.
 ◆ Yvonne will be willing to help the Hunters if they don’t 
interfere with her revenge and allow her to deal with 
Adrien.
End of Scene: The Hunters must run to the graveyard to 
avert the explosion. Go to the FIFTH SCENE - The Fire of 
Revenge.
FOURTH SCENE OPTION B 
– Return to the Cemetery
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You hurry back to the 
cemetery and find Lorenzo passed out on the ground. When 
he wakes, the doctor tells you that the reinforced trap doors of 
the mass graves all exploded in unison, and now Alizée feels a 
spirit force beyond comprehension. If something is not done, 
the consequences for the city will be catastrophic.
Place: The Cemetery of the Innocents.
Time: It is almost midnight on December 31st and it is 
possible to observe the passing comet Morituris in the sky.
Information: Hunters will have to approach the pit to 
quell the Mass of Restless Spirits. After having retrieved 
at least 1 Token (with Alizée’s help, if they have no other 
resources), the Hunters will be aware that, until the comet’s 
passage is complete, the graveyard is a potential bomb. 
End of Scene:  When the Hunters reduce the Grudge 
Tokens to zero, go to the FIFTH SCENE - The Fire of 
Revenge.
FIFTH SCENE –  The Fire of 
Revenge
Read aloud to the Hunters:  A long and heart-breaking 
lament rises from the mass graves, deep enough to make your 
skin crawl. Apparently, the dead have no intention of resting 
forever.
Place: The Cemetery of Paris.
Time: The stroke of midnight on December 31st. In the 
sky, the comet Morituris is as bright as a small sun.
Information: This Scene has two possible developments.
 ◆ If the Hunters haven’t faced the FOURTH SCENE 
OPTION A - Confession , Yvonne will appear in the 
center of the mass grave with Adrien. The spirit intends 
to throw the priest to the spirits and thus activate 
the explosion of the cemetery. Yvonne will face the 
Hunters backed by 4 Restless Spirits. If during the fight 
the Grudge Tokens reach 3, there will be an explosion. 
 ◆ If the Hunters have Yvonne’s support, the pit spirits will 
rebel against their own mistress. 9 Restless Spirits will 
appear in the graveyard to face off against the Hunters 
and Yvonne. During each Exchange, 1 Spirit will use its 
action to add 1 Grudge Token.
End of Scene:  It will be time for the Hunters to judge 
Adrien (if still alive) and decide what to do with little 
Yvonne, condemned to eternal unlife.

156
A Soldier’s Duty
Once upon a 
time…
The Hunters arrive in Borgholm, on the 
beautiful island of Oland. The Hunters’ 
intervention is ordered by the Papacy after 
a request from the Riksråd, the great council 
of the kingdom of Sweden. A former soldier 
and war hero named Nils, also called the 
Poor Devil, has sent a letter to the council, 
imploring them to help him and his former 
comrades. Six valiant soldiers were all that 
remained of their company after the bloody 
war between Sweden and Russia. Now, they 
are being killed one by one by a monster that 
seems able to take a bullet without batting an 
eye. Two of the comrades have already fallen 
to the monster’s fury. Those that still live 
have barricaded themselves in the mansion 
of their one-time commander, Andersen, who 
now leads them in defense of their own lives 
and those of his family.
A Scenario inspired by The Steadfast Tin Soldier 
(Hans Christian Andersen)
Safety Keywords:
  
Rape / Revenge / Murder

157
The story that led us here…
Six years earlier, a company of Carolean soldiers was 
stationed in Vaasa, a small town on the Finnish coast. Here 
Private Enar fell in love with Satu, a young and beautiful 
dancer. Enar deserted to remain near his beloved but was 
discovered by his comrades. They judged him a sinner 
according to the strict religious discipline of the Caroleans.
Led by Captain Andersen, the soldiers punished Enar by 
cutting off his leg and throwing him into the sea. But in 
reality, the true reason for Andersen’s ferocity arose from 
the fact that the officer too had fallen in love with Satu and, 
mad with jealousy, had punished Enar far beyond what the 
rules dictated. After Enar’s execution, unknown even to his 
own men, the Captain tried to romance Satu, who rejected 
him. Unable to accept that refusal, Andersen raped her and 
as a result of that terrible act, the girl became pregnant. 
Six years later, the company disbanded and Andersen, a 
wealthy war hero, paid his underlings handsomely to keep 
the secret of Enar’s death. Having retired to private life, over 
time, Andersen grew to regret his deplorable behavior. He 
sought out the dancer and, after regaining contact with her, 
discovered with great shame the existence of his son, Erik. 
Ravaged by guilt for what he had done, Andersen managed 
to earn Satu’s grudging forgiveness after months of effort. 
He freed her from a life of hardship as an assistant cook and 
took her with him to his mansion, together with his son. 
But Enar’s fate did not end beneath the sea waves. Love for 
Satu soon turned into blind hatred for Andersen and his 
former comrades. Below the waves, Enar remained alive for 
a long time while the fish slowly devoured his flesh. Of the 
soldier’s body, only the heart was spared and, as it fell into 
the abyss, it attracted the attention of a Sea Fairy. The Fairy 
took pity on him and, seeing that heart so wounded, infused 
it with new life. Enar’s body slowly began to regenerate, but 
the young soldier’s mind was now lost. All the accumulated 
hatred and resentment had driven him insane. Worse, in 
the process of his rebirth, the soldier’s body began to absorb 
everything it found. Algae, shells, and timbers from an old 
wreck fused to his limbs, making him an immortal monster 
bound to the world only by an infinite grudge...
Six years have passed, and Andersen managed to get closer 
to Satu, who is now at least accepting him. At the same 
time, Enar’s heart has started beating again. Now that his 
greatest enemy, Andersen, has found something he truly 
loves and would not want to lose, Enar is ready to return to 
take it away from him.
Appearing every day an hour after sunset, Enar began his 
personal war by killing his fellow soldiers, one by one, in a 
heinous fashion. Six survived the war, and two have already 
been murdered, with one gutted and another burned alive 
after being flayed. Andersen soon understood the nature of 
the threat and brought Hans and Christian, his remaining 
former companions, to his mansion. Only Nils was not 
called, being unable to move due to his war wounds. The 
Poor Devil has many friends, however, and has gathered 
information from his home in Sundsvall.

1
158
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Enar is completely insane and fully intent on killing all his 
former comrades, only to finally “merge” into one being 
with his beloved Satu. Nils in his heart sensed that the 
killer is Enar and, seized by a sense of guilt, he summoned 
the Order to try and stop his former comrade in arms. 
Andersen has found happiness with Satu and Erik, and 
despite the shame he feels for what he has done in the past, 
he is absolutely determined to destroy the revived Enar to 
secure a future with his new family.
Satu has guessed who the monstrous being that appears 
every night really is, but fear makes her hide from Enar’s 
gaze. The woman now wants nothing more than the best 
for her child, even to the detriment of her own happiness, 
even if it means accepting a life alongside the one who took 
away her true love six years earlier.
On this sad stage, all the actors have something to hide 
while Enar represents the truth that, inescapably, will 
destroy the wall of lies built with care by Andersen and his 
men.
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: It is late afternoon when you 
finally see the mansion of Andersen Olsson, the war veteran 
who, according to the letters they’ve received, is the victim of 
an attack by a monstrous creature. You notice immediately 
that the mansion has been fortified. A wrought iron fence 
and main gate, reinforced and strengthened by barricades, 
form an improvised wall around the grounds. A smaller gate 
that allows access to the garden has been bent in a way that 
suggests some very violent force has acted there, knocking it 
down and warping its hinges. A rudimentary watchtower 
has been built in a large oak that rises beyond the makeshift  
palisades and, from the top, a figure beckons to you with his 
hand before descending to the ground and running to meet 
you. He wears a soldier’s uniform, and with every step, a 
saber clatters in the scabbard at his side. The man arrives 
at the gate with a grim and serious expression, asking who 
goes there.
Place: Andersen’s large mansion just outside the town of 
Borgholm.
Time: It is late afternoon and the sun begins its slow 
descent. The salty air that comes from the sea teases the 
nostrils.
Initial Situation: The soldier who welcomes the Hunters 
is Christian, who will not allow the Hunters to enter the 
estate without first understanding who they are. As soon 
as it becomes clear they belong to the Order, Christian will 
call Andersen to let him handle the situation.
Information: During the Scene, Andersen will make 
no secret of his disappointment at seeing the Hunters. By 
talking to him you can learn the following information.
 ◆ Andersen disapproves of the fact that Nils sent a letter 
to the Order. The creature attacking his property yearns 
to take revenge on his men. The identities of the first 
two victims, his former comrades-in-arms Jacob and 
Wilhelm, were enough to convince him of that.
 ◆ Andersen has called the survivors to him to deal with 
this new threat, which he is sure is connected to the 
many deaths his company caused during the war. 
Andersen will make no secret of the fact that he and 
his men had their souls soaked in so much blood they 
were stained crimson. The monster is able to travel

159
great distances since the first two victims lived many 
days’ travel from each other. That’s why Andersen has 
gathered everyone on his estate to join forces.
 ◆ Although Andersen despises Nils because he has 
always been a coward, he has sent Hans to keep him 
safe, as the Poor Devil has lost his legs in battle and is 
unable to travel. Nils lives a day’s drive from the estate 
in the village of Sundsvall and Hans left a few hours 
before the Hunters arrived.
 ◆ Andersen is a soldier and if the Hunters insist, he will 
reluctantly welcome them into his home, respecting 
the authority of the Order.
Note: This Scenario uses Events as timers for story 
development. For this reason, the Storyteller must keep in 
mind some factors.
 ◆ There are only 3 places (the Andersen Estate, the town 
of Sundsvall, and the cliff) that make up an equilateral 
“triangle” . Moving from one place to another always 
takes 1 day of travel on foot. 
 ◆ Some Events have an indicative day number in which 
they occur: 1st day, 2nd day, and so on. In this way, 
the Storyteller will always know where Enar will be and 
who he will try to strike.
 ◆ Since the action always occurs in two places at the same 
time, the Storyteller must point out that all the former 
soldiers are in danger, and that this mission requires 
action on multiple fronts. For this reason, splitting 
the Hunters into two groups is encouraged. However, 
it is not an essential condition and the final decision 
regarding what to do will always be up to the Players.
 ◆ For better management of Events, given their 
importance, they are shown below instead of at the end 
of the Scenario as usual.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As Storyteller feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
Day 1:  Enar will attack Andersen’s estate shortly after 
sunset. If the Hunters are not present, Christian will be 
killed.
Day 1: Hans arrives in Sundsvall to interrogate Nils, who 
will no longer be willing to keep quiet about Enar’s murder. 
If the Hunters are not present, the soldier will kill the Poor 
Devil.
Day 2: Enar will arrive after sunset in Sundsvall to punish 
Nils. If the latter is already dead, then he will find Hans 
ready to ambush him. If the Hunters are not present, 
Hans will die. If Nils is alive, however, Enar will attempt to 
eliminate him unless the Hunters do something to prevent 
it.
Day 2: Erik will recognize the shells that have fallen from 
Enar’s body as those present on the cliff. If the Hunters 
are present, Andersen will invite them to investigate. 
Otherwise he will send Christian, who will be eliminated 
by the Sea Fairy. 
Day 3: Enar will arrive at the estate after sunset to eliminate 
Andersen and possibly Christian. Once this is done, he will 
try to reach Satu.
If Erik is put in danger:  Both Andersen and Satu will 
put their lives at risk for him. Both are willing to confess if 
this can save the child from danger.
If Enar discovers that Erik is Andersen’s son: The 
child will become his new target, as killing him is the best 
way to make Andersen feel the pain he has inflicted.

160
If the Hunters convince the Sea Fairy of their good 
intentions: The Fairy will lead one of them to the bottom 
of the sea where Enar’s heart is.
Andersen’s Estate
Descriptor: A large, lush estate ten minutes from the city 
center.
Wandering around the estate, the Hunters may encounter 
one or more of these NPCs. Roll a die:
1-2 Satu: With a sad and depressed air, the woman will try 
to avoid any interaction with the Hunters. Her shy demeanor 
will make it clear that something is bothering her.
3-4 Erik: Intrigued by the Hunters, he will approach them 
brimming with questions. He is a lively child, and it will soon 
become clear to the Hunters that he is not fully aware of the 
danger he is in.
5-6 Adam the Chatty Servant:  A young man in his 20s 
who can’t escape to the city, as he works on the estate to 
pay off a debt to Andersen. Adam will speak without much 
hesitation about the fact that there is a complex relationship 
between his master and Satu, and that the woman comes 
from the past of the former commander. Adam will add that 
Andersen is also very considerate of Erik and it becomes 
clear that he regards the boy as a son.
Information: Over twenty servants work on the estate, 
but few are capable of wielding a weapon. The whole defense 
rests on the shoulders of Andersen and Christian. It will be 
clear to the Hunters, however, that Andersen’s abilities as a 
commander allow him to keep everything under control and 
to bring out the best even from inexperienced servants.
After the first encounter with Enar, Erik will recognize the 
shells he collected when he lived with his mother in a small 
cliffside village. According to some old stories, under the cliff 
there is a dense network of caves where a Sea Fairy is said to 
reside.
Andersen
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a war veteran with strong leadership skills. 
In addition to being a good leader, I am also a capable fighter.
Agenda: Defend my new family from the danger that Enar 
represents / Redeem myself in the eyes of Satu and make her 
understand that I am now a different man / Prevent anyone 
from discovering what I have done.
Gift - Commander:  Andersen is skilled at giving orders 
and managing a battle. Up to 3 times per Scene, Andersen 
can issue an order to an ally who will receive 1 Additional 
Success (+1 to OL in the case of an NPC) while executing it.

161
Satu
OL Easy 3 – Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a former dancer who has lost everything. 
I am but a shadow of my former self.
Agenda: Defend my son Erik, the only reason that I keep 
going.
Erik
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a lively child who can never sit still. 
Agenda: Understand who Andersen really is, and why he 
took my mom and me out of the misery we lived in.
Christian
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a former soldier, called the Relentless 
by all because of my cold determination. I act without any 
qualms when it comes to executing an order.
Agenda: Help Andersen in memory of the old days and a 
military life I can’t forget / Eliminate Enar to prove I am the 
better man.
Gift - The Relentless: When he is carrying out an order, 
Christian is immune to any effect that affects his behavior, 
such as fear or charm. The soldier increases his Opposition 
Level by 1 when he tries to eliminate an obstacle that stands 
between him and the subject of an order he must carry out.
Enar
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an immortal being, the result of all the 
hatred I harbored in my exile into the abyss. I have the 
strength of five men and my body feels no pain.
Agenda: Take revenge on Andersen, but not before I have 
eliminated all my former companions / After eliminating 
Andersen and his people, I want to meld with Satu so that 
our bodies and souls are united for eternity.
Gift - The Eternal Soldier:  Enar’s body is the result of 
powerful magic tainted by hatred. When Enar suffers his 
final Wound, his body becomes seawater and soaks into 
the ground. The following day, Enar completely regenerates 
himself at the bottom of the sea, where his heart is, the very 
focus of his existence. Only by recovering the heart and 
destroying it can Enar be permanently eliminated. 
Gift - Rage of the Sea:  Thanks to the magic granted by 
the Sea Fairy, Enar has gained powers. When he acts, he 
can always choose an additional effect. 1. Algae Whip: A 
string of algae stretches from Enar’s body and can perform 
a second independent action. 2. Wave:  Enar’s body 
becomes seawater for a few moments, allowing him to slip 
away and cover a distance of 30 feet before regrouping. 
3. Shells: 3 large seashells are shot from Enar’s body in 
different directions, which should be considered 3 attacks 
with OL Medium 5. The shells must be aimed at 3 different 
targets.

162
The Cliff
Descriptor: A steep cliff overlooking 
the sea, riddled with many inlets. In 
the most inaccessible part, there are 
entrances that lead to underground 
caves.
Going down the cliff, it is possible to 
arrive at the caves’ entrance. In the 
center of the tunnel network, there is a 
pool where the Sea Fairy lives. Getting 
to the pool may require you to deal 
with one or more of these Threats. 
Roll a die:
1-2 Landslide:  As they descend 
along the cliffs, part of the rock path 
collapses under the Hunters’ feet. 
Descriptor: A sudden landslide. OL 
Hard 7. 
In the landslide, a Hunter can suffer 
1 Wound or lose some of their 
equipment to the sea.
3-4 Submerged tunnel:  To get 
to the central pool you need to swim 
through a narrow tunnel. 
Descriptor: A long, narrow 
submerged tunnel. OL Medium 5. 
Failing the Check means not being 
able to make it through the tunnel 
successfully and returning to the 
entrance.
5-6 Hook’s Crew: A dozen pirates 
died here after a shipwreck and now 
their souls are demanding vengeance 
on the living.
Dead Pirates
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a lost soul who only 
wishes for revenge on those who are still 
alive. My body is a heap of swollen and 
rotten flesh that moves slowly.
Special agenda (James the 
Swordsman): We recognize James 
as the one who killed our captain in 
a duel! It is his fault that our ship was 
wrecked, and we will taste his flesh 
first!
Gift - Walking Dead:  The body 
of the Dead Pirate is rotten and 
putrescent, but it does not stop even if 
dismembered. After suffering his only 
Wound, a Dead Pirate can still take his 
action before being eliminated, if he 
has not already done so in the course 
of the Exchange. If a Dead Pirate acts 
against an outnumbered enemy, his 
Opposition Level is increased by 1.

163
Sea Fairy
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fairy of the sea and my magic allows me to 
perform true miracles. My entire existence is tied to the sea and 
leaving it would destroy me.
Agenda: I am sad because Enar has forsaken the love that kept 
him alive in favor of blind hatred. I would like to do something 
for him, but I cannot go to the mainland to stop his fury or find 
his beloved.
Special Agenda (Marina the Sea Explorer):  I have 
heard of Marina and know her reputation. She uses her powers 
only to enrich herself at the expense of others. I will never agree 
to help her unless she first shows me that she is also capable of 
good deeds.
Gift – Sea Magic:  The Sea Fairy can always use one of 
the following effects.  1. Water of Life:  A target recovers 1 
Wound and, if they have been dead for no more than a day, 
they can even come back to life.  2. The Fury of the Sea:  
The water of the sea engulfs everything, sweeping away all 
threats. Those who are within 30 feet must pass a Defense 
Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid being overwhelmed by 
the waves and being washed away, suffering 1 Wound. The 
Sea Fairy can deliberately avoid hitting anyone in the area of 
effect.  3. The Waves of the Sea: The Sea Fairy can travel to 
any point of the sea within 6 miles. This shift is instantaneous, 
and if she wants, the Fairy can take up to 2 subjects with her.
Sundsvall
Descriptor: A small coastal town, where the tranquillity of the 
sea provides peace and serenity.
Information: It’s easy to find old Nils just by wandering 
around the town. The Poor Devil has invested himself in 
the community by donating part of his wealth to the 
neediest, and he is well regarded by all in town. In Nils’ 
house rests a diary where the former soldier wrote 
down everything that happened six years earlier. From 
the Poor Devil’s memories, it is possible to reconstruct 
Enar’s identity and his relationship with Satu, but not 
that Erik is Andersen’s son. The abuse perpetrated by 
the officer was never discovered by his comrades in 
arms. However, it will be clear from the writings that 
the punishment reserved for Enar was beyond reason.
Nils the Poor Devil
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an old soldier who lost his legs 
in the war.
Agenda: The guilt for what we did to Enar 
haunts me, I want to find a way to bring him 
peace.
Hans
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC  
(4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a former soldier, once one 
of the company’s best marksmen. I never miss 
my mark and always have a loaded weapon 
hidden under my heavy overcoat.
Agenda: Convince Nils to keep our 
secret or remove him.
Gift - Gunslinger:  Hans owns a 
pair of prized experimental pistols, 
which he bought from an inventor

164
for more than half of his war earnings. 
Hans’s guns can fire multiple shots 
at the same time and with one attack, 
the soldier can aim at 3 targets that are 
within an area of 30 feet square. While 
firing one pistol, Hans can reload the 
other as an additional action. When 
he draws a pistol from under his coat, 
Hans can make an attack with OL Hard 
7.  
Gift - Smoke Bombs:  Hans has 
some smoke bombs, which he uses 
to surprise the enemy. While firing 
a pistol, Hans can drop a bomb as an 
additional action. The thick smoke 
will cover an area of 30 feet square and 
inside it, everyone receives 1 Penalty 
for every action that requires the use of 
sight, except for Hans, who is immune 
to the effect. Additionally, he can always 
get away from opponents who engage 
him without anyone preventing him.
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities 
related to the A Soldier’s Duty 
Scenario and can be acquired once the 
requirements are met. A Hunter can 
only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to 
forgo the previous one.
Blessing of the Sea
Requirement: Prove to the Sea Fairy 
your intention to do something for poor 
Enar.
Y ou are blessed by the mighty Sea 
Fairy and therefore sea-related abilities 
have no effect on you. Y ou receive 2 
Additional Successes on each Defense 
Check you make to defend yourself 
against a Gift, spell, or action made 
using a Descriptor that includes the 
word sea. 
Art of Command
Requirement: Withstand an on -
slaught from Enar by fighting alongside 
Andersen.
Y ou’ve observed a good leader and 
figured out how to hone your strategies. 
Once per Scene, you can give an 
order to one of your allies and if they 
try to carry it out, they will receive 2 
Additional Successes to any Check.
Coral of the Sea Fairy 
(Special gift for Marina 
the Sea Explorer)
Requirement: To kill the Sea Fairy.
Y ou dispatched that hateful fairy and 
harnessed her soul in a lump of coral. 
Now her power is yours. Y ou can use 1 
Treasure Token to unlock this Gift for 
the duration of the Scenario. Spend 
1 Soma to tap into the Coral’s power 
and choose one of the following effects.   
1. Water of Life:  A target recovers 
1 Wound.  2. The Fury of the Sea:  
The water of the sea engulfs everything, 
sweeping away all threats. Those who 
are within 30 feet must pass a Defense 
Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid be-
ing overwhelmed by the waves and be-
ing thrown away, suffering 1 Wound.   
3. The Waves of the Sea:  Y ou can 
travel to any point of the sea within 6 
miles. This shift is instantaneous and 
you can take up to 2 subjects with you.
Sea creatures sense what you have done 
and will attack you without hesitation 
to try to free the soul of the Fairy.
