2a
165
The Scenario 
A Soldier’s 
Duty in Scenes
FIRST SCENE – The Arrival at 
the Estate
It’s the same one you find on page 158.
End of Scene:  After talking to Andersen, the Hunters 
have a rough understanding of the situation. Given the 
danger on several fronts, Andersen will ask half of the 
Hunters to stay at the estate (SECOND SCENE OPTION 
A - The Defense) and the other half to travel to Sundsvall to 
help Hans protect Nils (SECOND SCENE OPTION B - The 
Poor Devil).
SECOND SCENE OPTION A 
– The Defense
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The sun has just set, while 
servants are nailing a last few planks across the windows 
on the first floor. What should be a luxurious mansion 
increasingly resembles a fortress under siege. Andersen and 
Christian take the last few puffs on a pipe while setting up 
their rifles, a ritual that probably formed during the days 
they fought side-by-side on real battlefields. Then the pungent 
smell of burnt tobacco fades, covered by that of salt and rotten 
fish. A figure stands at the entrance to the garden, huge and 
ungainly. It is a colossus of pale flesh, driftwood, rocks, shells, 
and other debris from the sea. The being stares at the large 
mansion for a moment, before roaring out its rage.
Place: Andersen’s fortified mansion.
Time: It is night, but a high moon and a cloudless sky 
allow for good visibility.
Information: 
 ◆ Enar will attack without hesitation, ignoring any 
mediation attempts by the Hunters. However, it will be 
clear that Enar understands what he is told but simply 
ignores it.
 ◆ Andersen will give his best as a leader, using his 
Commander Gift to lend a hand to the Hunters.
 ◆ When Enar is left with 2 Wounds, little Erik will enter 
the scene. Sneaking out of the mansion to get a better 
look, he will find himself in the middle of the battle. 
Satu will arrive soon after and the Hunters will be able 
to observe the reactions of Enar and Andersen.
 ◆ Enar will understand from their actions that Erik is the 
couple’s son. When he does, he will roar in anger and 
try to unleash his rage on the child.
 ◆ When Enar is defeated, his body will fall apart, and 
Erik will recognize some particular shells he has always 
seen at the cliff.
End of Scene: Erik recognizes the cliff shells, which could 
be a good clue to find out where Enar resides. Go to the 
THIRD SCENE OPTION A - The Cliff.

2b
3a
166
SECOND SCENE OPTION B – 
The Poor Devil
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Sundsvall is a charming 
seaside town, with colorful houses and smiling people. Here, 
it really seems that war and suffering have never happened. It 
is easy to find Nils’ house. Apparently, the former soldier has 
donated a lot of money to the poor of the city and these, given 
his handicap, often give him a hand to thank him. When 
you arrive at the small red brick house, you’re surprised to 
find the front door open. Did Hans get there before you? The 
sudden sound of a gunshot does not bode well.
Place: Nils the Poor Devil’s little house.
Time: It is late afternoon.
Information: When the Hunters arrive, Hans is trying 
to execute Nils after the Poor Devil has expressed his 
resolution to confess what happened six years ago. Hans 
is ready to fight to the death against the Hunters while 
keeping the elimination of Nils as his main goal.
 ◆ If the Hunters kill Hans and Nils survives, the Poor 
Devil will tell them everything he knows. From the 
memories of the Poor Devil, it is possible to reconstruct 
the identity of Enar and his relationship with Satu, 
but not that Erik is the son of Andersen. The abuse 
perpetrated by the officer was never discovered by his 
comrades in arms. However, it will be clear that the 
punishment reserved for Enar was beyond measure.
 ◆ If Nils dies, it will be possible to find out the truth 
regarding Enar’s punishment by reading his diary.
 ◆ If Hans is captured, the soldier will not speak even 
under torture and will try to give the Hunters a hard 
time.
End of Scene:  It is now clear that Andersen is hiding a 
secret linked to Enar’s revenge. The Hunters must return 
to the estate, go to SCENE THREE OPTION B - Return to 
the Estate.
THIRD SCENE OPTION A – 
The Cliff
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Following the directions 
of little Erik, you finally reach the coast. The sea is stormy 
and crashes against a long cliff that acts as a promontory. 
It doesn’t take long to find what looks like a natural path 
between the rocks, exactly as Erik described to you.
Place: The cliff on the stretch of sea from which Enar 
emerges.
Time: The setting sun is covered with rain clouds.
Information: Descending along the cliff will put the 
Hunters to the test. Select or present in order the Threats on 
page 162. Once on the bottom, they will be able to meet 
the Sea Fairy and convince her to stop Enar. The Fairy will 
reveal that Enar’s heart is darkened by great pain for the 
injustice he has suffered and that it is safely laying on the 
bottom of the sea. The Fairy can bring it to the Hunters, but 
only if they first do justice to the memory of Enar, bringing 
her Satu so that she can say a last goodbye to Enar’s heart. 
The woman is Enar’s lost love and it is right that the 
unfortunate soldier should have a chance to rest in peace.
End of Scene:  The Hunters must return to the estate 
to retrieve Satu and join their other companions. Go to 
SCENE FOUR - Destinies.

3b
4
167
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – 
Return to the Estate
Read aloud to the Hunters: Tired from the long journey, 
you arrive at Andersen’s estate as the sun goes down. You 
are determined to untangle this story, but all your intentions 
collide with a monster’s insane howl. Like every night, the 
fearsome brute has come to carry out a massacre.
Place: Andersen’s Estate, marred by the previous night’s 
clash.
Time: The sun is slowly setting, but the estate remains lit 
thanks to lanterns expertly positioned by Andersen.
Information: As with the previous night, the clash with 
Enar will be inevitable. With the monster down, the Hunters 
will be able to ask Andersen why he tried to eliminate Nils. 
The man will fall to his knees as he tells his entire story, 
including his abuse of Satu. Wanting Erik to keep both a 
father and mother, Andersen will ask the Hunters to end 
Enar’s persecution.
 ◆ As the Hunters sent to the cliff arrive, Erik will come 
out of the barricaded mansion crying. Satu fled during 
the fight, to head to the cliff. The woman reassured her 
son that the monster will not harm her and that only 
she can stop his fury.
End of Scene: It will be clear to the Hunters that Satu will 
try to meet Enar at the cliff. Andersen will want to leave 
immediately and nothing can stop him. Go to SCENE 
FOUR - Destinies.
FOURTH SCENE –  Destinies
Read aloud to the Hunters:  By the time you get to the 
cliff, night has fallen. The storm has passed, and a clear and 
gloomy moon illuminates two figures on the edge of the rocks. 
One is the graceful silhouette of Satu, the other the huge, 
grotesque mass of Enar. The two observe each other for a 
few moments. Then tentacles of seaweed emerge from Enar’s 
body, reaching out to seize his lost love.
Place: The Cliff.
Time: It is night, but a clear moonlight illuminates the 
epilogue of this sad story.
Information: Stopping Enar is a priority or he will 
assimilate Satu by merging with her within the course of 
one Exchange. The Hunters know that the only way to 
permanently kill him is to call the Sea Fairy to bring the 
monster’s heart.
End of Scene:  Enar, Satu, and Andersen’s fate will be in 
the hands of the Hunters

168
Wonderbedlam
Once upon a time…
No less than the Duke of Norfolk himself has approached 
the Order with a request which must remain secret. Five 
months ago, his daughter Alissa was taken to Wonder, 
the bedlam of Y ork, the most renowned asylum in all of 
England. Under the guidance of a mysterious Director, 
the Duke hoped for the quick recovery of his daughter, 
who possessed an attitude that was too rebellious and 
unseemly for a young noblewoman. After the first 
month, Alissa’s letters to her family became more and 
more terrified and delusional, until they abruptly 
ceased. According to Alissa’s words, Wonder is hell on 
earth, where strange presences move in the dark rooms, 
animated by an evil will. The Duke’s plea to the Hunters is 
simple: save Alissa and shed a light on the dark dealings 
happening in the asylum of Wonder.
A Scenario inspired by Alice’s Adventures in Wonderland (Lewis Carroll)
Safety Keywords: Mental illness / Imprisonment / Abuse / Cannibalism

169
The story that led us here…
Alice Liddell, daughter of an English aristocrat 
in great favor with the Crown, developed a complex 
mental illness as a child. From an early age, she suffered 
from visions and delusions about a fantasy world hidden 
in her dreams.
“Treated” in great secrecy by the family with drugs and 
experimental therapies, Alice has learned to control 
her madness enough to appear to be as sane as the next 
person. At the age of twenty-seven, she graduated with 
a degree in medicine, dedicating herself body and soul 
to the treatment of mental illness. The culmination of 
this dazzling career is Wonder, a “hospital” meant to 
welcome children of wealthy families who want to avoid 
patronizing one of the infamous asylums of England. 
Little do they suspect that inside Wonder, Alice has 
created a world of sheer madness, where unquestioning 
patients must follow her every perverse idea to the letter, 
in a physical and mental ordeal that she believes will bring 
them back to sanity. Alice’s lucid madness has wrung 
more and more strength from the patients’ suffering, 
allowing her to shape reality itself. Wonder has become 
a building out of space and time, where a door might 
lead you anywhere unless you know the right words. To 
control Wonder and not fall victim to her own creation, 
Alice has hired the most skilled inventor in all of England, 
Hatta Carter, famous for his theories on automatons. The 
man has thoroughly researched Wonder in an attempt to 
make sense of the physics-defying nature of the asylum. 
Thanks to Hatta’s hard work, the structure now responds 
to Alice’s will, and it has become a place from which it is 
impossible to escape without the consent of its Director. 
Thanks to her power, Alice controls a large, trained lynx 
named Cheshire. By moving through the narrow conduits 
of the asylum, she acts as the eyes of her mistress.
 
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The young Alissa of Norfolk proved incredibly resistant to 
Alice’s “cures, ” and for this reason the Director of Wonder 
imprisoned the girl in the Hall of the Mind, disregarding 
her normal procedure. Day after day, Alice invades 
Alissa’s mind with nightmares in an attempt to quell her 
rebellious spirit. Alice manages Wonder completely by 
herself, taking advantage of her control over the building 
and rooms, which can only be accessed with the right 
passwords. Hatta Carter is the only other person able 
to solve the Wonder puzzle. For this reason, Alice has 
imprisoned the mad inventor in the bedlam’s Basement, 
intending to keep him safe for use in future projects. 
Meanwhile, Alicia, Alissa’s young lover, has infiltrated 
Wonder in an attempt to free her imprisoned partner. 
In addition to their romantic bond, Alicia is driven by 
guilt, convinced that Alissa was sent to Wonder due to 
the two girls’ relationship. Alissa had even refused several 
promising marriage proposals to remain with Alicia, 
indifferent to her duty as the Duke of Norfolk’s heir. 
Despite her courage and tenacity, Alicia soon became a 
prisoner of the deranged rooms of Wonder.

1
170
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: You have finally arrived 
at Wonder, the most famous and renowned bedlam in all 
of England. What you see, however, is certainly not what 
one might expect from a place where people are being cured 
of mental illness. After crossing an immense garden full of 
dead plants, you find yourself in the entrance hall before an 
immense, mirrored wall that reflects your perplexed faces. 
A large, tree-shaped fountain gushes water into a pool full 
of colorful fish. Three doors on the east wall attract your 
attention. The central one is of normal size, while the one 
on the right is twice as large, and the one on the left is 
barely half the normal size. On the west wall, a painted 
inscription dominates in large letters: “In this place, 
madness becomes reason, so that the sick can become 
healthy again”. Finally, you notice a fourth door that 
initially escaped your gaze, painted on the floor with 
such skill as to seem real. While you are wondering 
who will come to welcome you, a small white rabbit 
peeps out from behind the large fountain. Around his 
neck, he wears a small message holder, in which you 
can see a rolled-up note.
Place: The Entrance Hall of Wonder. Hunters 
will have the strange feeling of not being able to 
estimate the exact size of the place. 
Time: It is early in the morning and sunlight 
filters through the large windows on the west 
wall, the only ones in the room. Hunters 
will feel like they can’t tell the exact time. 
Initial Situation: Thirty minutes have passed since the 
Hunters arrived and it is becoming clear that no one will 
come to welcome them. 
Information: Exploring the Entrance Hall and the 
exterior of Wonder will yield the following information.
 ◆ Catching the rabbit will be easy. The animal seems 
accustomed to the presence of humans and for this 
reason, it will get close. The message it brings with it is 
clearly addressed to the Hunters: “You are not welcome 
at Wonder, as you are not here seeking something 
but someone. This place welcomes only those who 
need healing of the mind.” The paper is signed, “The 
Director” .
 ◆ The exterior of the asylum looks quite bizarre. 
From the outside, all the windows on the walls, the 
doors except the entrance door, and in general every 
possible entrance to the building, seem real but are 
just painted. There are therefore no real access points 
to the building, except the main door leading to the 
Entrance Hall.
 ◆ To a Hunter who understands magic through 
their Descriptors, it will be clear that all of the bedlam 
is under the influence of a powerful arcane force (see 
the Place Wonderbedlam on page 171).
 ◆ The three doors in the east wall open smoothly, 
all three leading onto a long corridor that ends with 
another identical door.

171
The Entrance Hall of Wonder
Gift - Beyond the Mirror:  The large wall mirror hides 
the passage leading to the Hall of the Mind. Only by saying 
the formula, “Beyond madness, beyond the basement, 
beyond the mirror, ” is it possible to see the passage. The 
complete formula is known by Hatta Carter, detained in the 
Basement.
Gift - The Door to The Below:  The door painted on 
the floor hides the passage to get to the Basement. Just 
by tapping a foot on the painting and saying the formula, 
“Is anyone under the house?” or something in that vein 
you can see the passage. The instructions to open it are 
contained in the message holders of the White Rabbits all 
across Wonder.
Gift - The Doors to Elsewhere:  The three wall doors 
each lead to one of the Cure Rooms. When traversing the 
connecting corridor, the senses are confused and clouded 
by the magic of Wonder. When you finally open the door 
at the end of the corridor, you realize you are in another 
place. Attempting to take something out of the Cure Rooms 
activates the Gift Place of Pure Madness (see this same 
page).
Cheshire The Faithful Lynx
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a snappy and aggressive lynx. I roam the 
halls of Wonder guided by the will of my mistress.
Gift - Elusive:  Thanks to the power infused in Wonder, 
Cheshire can disappear and reappear a short distance away, 
and move through doors and walls. Cheshire can always 
move without anyone being able to stop her. All actions to 
attack or capture Cheshire receive 1 Penalty because of the 
constant appearance and disappearance of the lynx. This 
Gift is not affected by the Place of Pure Madness Gift.
WonderBedlam
Descriptor: I am a building animated by sheer madness 
and no one can attempt to alter me without Alice Liddell’s 
permission.
Gift - Place of Pure Madness:  When a subject 
attempts an action or uses a Gift to change Wonder’s insane 
structure (such as breaking through walls or circumventing 
passageways), the building’s inherent magic activates. 
Before the action taken is resolved, roll a die to see where 
the subject will end up being teleported.
1-2: The Rabbit Hole. 
3-4: The Labyrinth of Doors. 
5-6: The Caterpillars’ Greenhouse.
This magical shift causes a side effect that lasts throughout 
the Scene; roll a die to determine what happens.
1-2 Shrunk: The subject receives the Descriptor I am half 
the size I was, all my physical abilities are diminished as well.

172
3-4 Enlarged: The subject receives the Descriptor I am 
twice as big as I was and I move awkwardly and slowly.
5-6 Confused:  The subject receives the Descriptor My 
mind is confused, I can’t concentrate, and I misunderstand 
words when spoken to.
The three Cure Rooms
Descriptor: Magical places, out of space and time, where 
patients are thrown to face whatever is troubling their mind.
Information: The Rabbit Hole, the Labyrinth of Doors, 
and the Greenhouse are three places connected by magical 
corridors to the Entrance Hall of Wonder. Within the various 
sections of the asylum, the pitiable patients are left to fend for 
themselves. The main purpose of the rooms is to encourage 
patients to see “beyond” their madness and understand, 
through the clues of the White Rabbits, how to access the 
Basement. Once there, Alice Liddell will pick them up to 
take them to the Hall of the Mind. Every room will feature 
the Threat The White Rabbit.
Alicia The Young Girl in Love
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young rebel from the slums who knows 
how to get by on her own. Thanks to my tenacity, I can 
overcome every obstacle.
Agenda: I want to save Alissa from this cursed place and run 
away with her to live out our love in peace.
Threat - The White Rabbit
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: I’m a little white rabbit wandering around the 
Cure Rooms and I have a message holder tied around my neck. 
I fear those I do not know.
Gift - Messenger:  By capturing a White Rabbit it is 
possible to take the message it carries. Roll a die.
1-2: It’s not always convenient to knock on a door with your 
hand. 
3-4: Knocking on a door is the bare minimum to be 
expected of a respectable person. 
5-6: When you arrive at a door, it is polite to ask if anyone 
is home.

173
The Patients of Wonder
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are mad and desperate, driven to Wonder 
in the hope of curing our ailments. Madness guides our 
actions and we no longer understand that there is a world 
outside the bedlam.
Gift - Why I Am Here: Roll a die to see what problem led 
them to Wonder.
1 Violence: The Patient struggles to contain their anger. 
When they act violently increase their OL by 1. 
2 Kleptomania: The Patient yearns for any object they 
deem interesting and will do everything to have it. Once 
per Scene, they can act 2 times consecutively, though the 
additional Action must be used to attempt to escape and 
hide with the stolen goods.
3 Abandoned:  The Patient was interned at Wonder to 
be forgotten. Hardened by loneliness, they gain 1 extra 
Wound.
4 Split personality: The Patient changes personality in 
the face of a specific event, such as a threat. When the new 
personality acts, its OL becomes Hard 7.
5 Dementia:  The Patient is not fully aware of what is 
happening around them. They are immune to all attempts 
at intimidation. Increase their OL by 1 when defending 
against a violent action.
6 Illness: The Patient is ill or horribly disfigured. Each 
action against them receives 1 Penalty.

174
Room 1 – The Rabbit Hole
Descriptor: A series of burrows and tunnels full of dirt 
and vegetation. In the small rooms and crevices, naked and 
emaciated patients move like animals, always looking for 
something to eat.
When the Hunters roam the Rabbit Hole, roll a die.
1-2: A group of 1d6 + 3 Patients will approach the Hunters 
in search of food. 
3-4: Alicia is busy fending off a group of aggressive 
Patients. 
5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.
Room 2 – The Labyrinth of 
Doors
Descriptor: An infinite series of rooms, all with doors 
leading into a long corridor. Each room is decorated in a 
different and macabre way, recreating situations of torture 
and stress.
When the Hunters roam the Labyrinth of Doors, roll a die. 
1-2: A group of 1d6 + 3 Patients will approach the Hunters 
to push them into a room shouting, “We must see what’s 
beyond!”
3-4: Alicia is busy fending off a group of aggressive 
Patients. 
5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.
Room 3 – The Caterpillars’ 
Greenhouse
Descriptor: A huge greenhouse full of alien and unknown 
plants. A strong smell of pollen saturates the air, making 
it difficult to breathe. A dense yellow fog sharply reduces 
visibility.
When the Hunters are roaming the Caterpillars’ 
Greenhouse, roll a die.
1-2: Two Patients are escaping from a Caterpillar of the 
Wonderful Lands.
3-4: Alicia is busy fending off 2 Caterpillars of the 
Wonderful Lands.
5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.
Caterpillar of the Wonderful 
Lands
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a huge carnivorous caterpillar over two 
meters long. I feed on fresh meat and then lay my eggs in the 
carcasses of creatures I’ve devoured.
Gift - Hunter Caterpillar: When a Caterpillar moves, any 
human-sized subject in its path risks being overwhelmed. 
Whoever is in the Caterpillar’s way must perform a 
Defense Check in order not to end up knocked down. The 
Caterpillar inflicts 1 Additional Wound against people 
prone on the ground.

175
The Basement
Descriptor: A huge underground hall filled with food and 
comfortable cushions to sit on. Unnatural darkness surrounds 
the room, making it difficult to distinguish the walls.
Information: The Basement is the place where Patients 
arrive when they have figured out how to open the door on 
the floor of the Entrance Hall. In Alice’s crazy theory, those 
who arrive underground have understood how to leave 
behind their issues and psychoses, represented in the Cure 
Rooms. In the Basement, a Patient can refresh themselves and 
then be picked up in their sleep and taken to the Hall of the 
Mind. Hatta Carter is a prisoner in the Basement, kept alive by 
Alice to be used for future bedlam expansion projects. Hatta 
has gone mad with loneliness and has built two Automata as 
pretend tea companions.
Hatta Carter The 
Mad Inventor
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled inventor, the most brilliant of my 
time. My emotional fragility leads me to frequent mood swings 
and aggressiveness.
Agenda: I want the company of witty people and not cold 
automata. I long for the chats I had with Alice.
Gift - Time Clocks: Hatta can manipulate one of his clocks 
to influence time.  1. Slow: Once per Scene, Hatta increases 
his OL by 2 when defending against a threat.  2. Speed 
Up: Once per Scene, Hatta and one of his Automata can 
act, immediately stopping anyone’s action regardless of the 
situation in the narrative. If an Automaton has already acted, 
it can act again thanks to this Gift.
Rabbit Automaton
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC  
(2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an automaton in the service of Hatta 
Carter. Thanks to my strong legs I can make incredible 
jumps and crush everything when landing.
Gift - Leap:  When it acts, the Automaton can free 
itself from any engagement with ease by jumping away. 
If it lands on a victim, it increases its OL by 1 for the 
attack and if it inflicts 1 Wound, the target will also be 
knocked to the ground.

176
Lizard Automaton
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an automaton in the service of Hatta 
Carter. Thanks to my large tail I can perform several actions 
at the same time.
Gift - Tail: The Automaton always acts twice, once with its 
front legs and once with its long tail, but cannot focus both 
its actions on the same target.
Hall of the Mind
Descriptor: A series of rooms painted entirely in white. The 
doors themselves do not have handles and it takes a careful 
look to distinguish them from the walls. A large central hall 
gathers all the Patients, lying on rows of beds, asleep in a state 
of trance. The distant ticking of a clock breaks an otherwise 
unnatural silence, creating a hypnotic echo.
Information: In the Hall of the Mind, it is possible to 
find several Patients in a catatonic state, including Alissa 
of Norfolk. Alice Liddell will not seek a confrontation with 
any intruders but will be friendly and willing to listen to the 
unexpected guests. Alice will offer a good tea in which 
a powerful sleep drug is mixed.
Threat - Alice’s special tea
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A delicious tea mixed with a powerful 
soporific.
Gift - Sleep: Those who drink the tea and fail their Defense 
Check will fall into a deep sleep for over eight hours. Only 
suffering severe pain (equivalent to receiving 1 Wound) or 
the use of magic can awaken a sleeping subject.
Alice Liddell The 
Director
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the director of Wonder and, thanks to the 
power of madness, I can enter people’s minds to heal them.
Agenda: Safeguard what I have built within Wonder and 
all the incredible discoveries that the magic of madness can 
give to mankind / Solve every problem by transforming it into 
a Patient to be treated.
Gift - Alice from Other Worlds:  As an action, Alice 
can attempt to possess the body of a person or animal by 
looking into their eyes. Hunters can make a Defense Check 
to prevent this. Alice can both act through the body she 
controls and continue to use her Gifts and Descriptors. 
The possession ends when Alice’s body suffers 1 Wound. 
Alice cannot control more than 3 common subjects or a 
single Hunter at the same time.

177
Alissa of Norfolk 
The Rebel
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I have been physically tested by my stay in 
Wonder, but my mind is still strong. My love for Alicia kept 
me in the real world.
Agenda: Escape from Wonder and my old life and make a 
future with Alicia.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
When the Hunters visit the third Cure Room 
without first meeting Alicia:  they will encounter 
Alicia, who is lost. The young woman will be reluctant to 
reveal her true identity but will give her name and introduce 
herself as a patient.
On a regular basis, if the Hunters waste time in the 
Entrance Hall of Wonder:  one of the bedlam Patients 
will walk out of one of the three doors into another. The 
Patient will mumble disjointed words such as “see beyond” 
or “go under” .
If the Hunters cause too much havoc in a Cure 
Room: Alice will take control of Cheshire and order the 
Hunters to leave, or try to surprise them by making it clear 
that they are being watched.

178
If the Hunters try to leave the Basement: a desperate 
Hatta Carter will attack them with the help of his Automata 
to keep his new guests with him.
If the Hunters corner Alice Liddell:  the Director will 
be ready to make deals, such as freeing Alissa or whatever 
the Hunters ask. Alice’s real worry is that Wonder might be 
destroyed.
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Wonderbedlam Scenario and can be acquired once the 
conditions are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario 
Gift. If they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will 
have to forgo the previous one.
Friend of the Rabbits
Requirement: To have captured at least one White Rabbit.
Y ou are able to approach the little white rabbits without 
frightening them. Receive 2 Additional Successes when you 
face the Threat The White Rabbit. Once picked up, your 
new little friends will be able to deliver a message for you to 
any room in Wonder. This Gift allows you to send a White 
Rabbit even through seemingly closed doors.
Lucid Madness
Requirement: Having suffered the Gift Place of Pure 
Madness at least once.
Y ou are slowly figuring out how to make the weirdest ideas 
real. Y ou can spend 1 Soma to try to activate the mad magic 
of Wonder. State something crazy that you are capable of, 
such as I want to walk on walls or I want my arm to stretch 
out of proportion. The Storyteller will ask you for a Position 
Check against an OL proportional to the madness of your 
request. If you succeed, you get the Descriptor you declared 
for the rest of the Scene; if you fail, you have spent your 
Soma in vain. The effect of this Gift also ends when someone 
makes clear that what you are doing is utter madness.
The Forgotten Queen (Special Gift for Regina 
the Thief of Hearts)
Requirement: Visit a Cure Room.
Confusing flashbacks and painful emotions grip your 
mind. Y ou’ve been here as a patient before, but maybe 
you’ve forgotten why. Are you sure you want to remember? 
Once per Scene, you can try to remember something of 
your experience in Wonder. Choose a place or a subject 
from the bedlam, and the Storyteller will give you useful 
information about a Descriptor, a Gift, or their Agenda. 
Remembering is painful. For the rest of the Scene, when 
you use one of your Gifts, roll a die: on a 5 or 
higher you lose 1 Soma and you 
are no longer so sure that you are 
the Queen.

179
FIRST SCENE – Arrival in Wonder
It’s the same one you find on page 170.
End of Scene:  For the Hunters, it will be clear that the 
only way forward is through one of the three doors to the 
Cure Rooms.
SECOND SCENE – The First Room
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You walk through a long 
corridor that leads you in front of a door exactly the same 
as the one you just went through. As in the Entrance Hall, 
time seems to flow differently in this place. You have the 
impression that the few steps necessary to cross between one 
door and the other takes hours rather than moments. You 
open the door and what is shown to you...
Note: As the Storyteller, continue the description based on 
the room that was chosen by the Hunters.
Place: One of the Cure Rooms.
Time: It is impossible to establish the exact time and, 
from one room to another, day changes to night in a totally 
random way.
Information: The Hunters encounter the result 1-2 of 
the relevant room. Once the encounter is resolved they will 
notice a White Rabbit and, by capturing it, they will get the 
first part of the hint to enter the Basement.
End of Scene: It will be clear that each Cure Room holds 
a clue to progress through Wonder. The Hunters must 
move to another room. Go to THIRD SCENE – The Second 
Room, the Encounter with Alicia.
THIRD SCENE – The Second 
Room, the Encounter with Alicia
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You walk through a long 
corridor that leads you in front of a door exactly the same 
as the one you just went through. As in the Entrance Hall, 
time seems to flow differently in this place and you have the 
impression that the few steps necessary to cross between one 
door and the other takes hours rather than moments. You 
open the door and immediately you are alerted by the cry of 
a girl.
Note: As the Storyteller, continue the description based on 
the room that was chosen by the Hunters and highlight the 
presence of Alicia engaged in an encounter, as a result of 
3-4 of the relevant room.
Place: One of the Cure Rooms.
Time: It is impossible to establish the exact time and, 
from one room to another, day changes to night in a totally 
random way.
Information: The Hunters are aware that without their 
intervention the young woman will succumb. 
 ◆ Once safe, Alicia will introduce herself, saying that 
she is in Wonder to save a friend from this place of 
madness. Alicia has two clues with her, one from the 
The Scenario Wonderbedlam in Scenes

4
180
White Rabbit in this room and one from the one the 
Hunters have not yet visited. 
 ◆ During the discussion with Alicia, Alice will 
attack the girl by surprise via Cheshire, and then 
make the lynx flee through a wall.
End of Scene:  It should now be clear to the 
Hunters how to access the Basement (Alicia can 
help them if they haven’t quite figured out how to

FOURTH SCENE –  
The Basement
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When the door 
opens, you see a large flight of stairs that descend 
underground. A few minutes later you find yourself in 
a large room, richly furnished with colored cushions 
and drapes. The Basement is hard to see across, so 
much so that you can’t make out the walls. Suddenly, 
a shrill voice calls for your attention. In front of you, 
a gentleman with a strange top hat on his head 
converses amiably with two Automata, one in the 
form of a large rabbit, the other of a lizard. In a 
grotesque imitation of teatime, the man with the 
top hat conducts a one-way conversation, where his 
words are left unanswered by the cold, unintelligent 
gazes of the Automata.
Place: The Basement of Wonder, where those 
who have passed the Cure Rooms can rest while 
waiting to be picked up.
Time: It is not possible to distinguish between day 
and night, but the atmosphere and the soft lighting 
recall the time for evening tea.
Information: Hatta Carter will be delighted to have new 
guests and will invite the Hunters to join him for tea. By 
talking to him you can get the following information.
 ◆ Those who pass through the cycle of therapies in the 
Cure Rooms can access the Basement where they 
can refresh themselves, dress again, and wait for the 
transfer to the Hall of the Mind. The Director keeps 
Hatta tucked away for future expansions.
 ◆ Hatta is a prisoner of the Director. He built Wonder 
and knows the formula for going beyond the mirror, 
accessing the part of Wonder that is exclusive to the 
Director. 
 ◆ If the Hunters point out to Hatta that if he knows every 
secret, he can escape whenever he wishes, the man will 
nod in amazement, revealing the formula for going 
beyond the mirror to the Director’s sanctum. Once 
this is done, however, he will return to his madness, 
complaining once more about his imprisonment.
 ◆ Hatta will say that he doesn’t know the Director’s real 
name, but he knows that she is a renowned doctor and 
that she is not really a bad person. As a scientist, he 
understands well that indelicate acts must sometimes 
be done in the name of scientific progress.
 ◆ The tablecloth on the table is actually the blueprint of 
Wonder drawn by Hatta during construction. It reveals 
the formula for going beyond the mirror. The mad 
inventor, however, will strongly object if the Hunters 
try to take the map, as this would ruin the table’s setting 
for tea.
When the Hunters attempt to leave the Basement, Hatta 
will beg them to stay with him, insisting until he becomes 
aggressive and unleashes the Automata against the Hunters.

5
181
End of Scene:  After Hatta and his Automata are dealt 
with, the Hunters know the formula to cross the mirror 
and finally discover who is behind Wonder. Go to FIFTH 
SCENE - The Director.
FIFTH SCENE –  
The Director
Read aloud to the Hunters: In front of the large mirror 
on the north wall, you say the formula “Beyond the madness, 
beyond the basement, beyond the mirror, ” and you see a 
small door appear. Crossing through it, you find yourself in 
a bare and aseptic environment with walls, furniture, and 
doors, all in white. The doors themselves do not 
have handles and only with a careful inspection is 
it possible to distinguish them from the surrounding 
walls. Finally, you arrive in a huge room, furnished 
solely with an endless series of beds on which placid 
patients are sleeping. The distant ticking of a clock 
breaks an otherwise unnatural silence. Then, a door 
opens and a distinguished-looking woman with 
a cold gaze appears in front of you. “Welcome to 
Wonder. I’m the Director.”
Place: The aseptic Hall of the Mind of Wonder.
Time: It is not possible to distinguish between day and 
night, but the atmosphere and the white lights recall a visit 
to a hospital.
Information: Alice will offer her guests a fine cup of tea, 
trying to put them to sleep. Alternatively, she will try to take 
control of Alicia and pass her off as Alissa. The Director will 
claim that Alicia is a second personality Alissa has created 
inside Wonder to escape from herself. Alice will try not to 
give the real Alissa to the Hunters, but she won’t be willing 
to lose Wonder to keep her.

182
Tuvstarr’s 
Reflection
A Scenario inspired by Tuvstarr 
(Helge Kjellin) by Petter Nallo
Safety Keywords: Decay / Corruption of body and 
mind / Folk Horror
Tuvstarr’s Reflection is a special Scenario built as a Scene Tree (see page 100) and 
is great for introductory Sessions, one-shots, or to introduce Sherazade the Weaver 
of Tales to a Campaign. 
This Scenario takes place within a story told by Sherazade, an emissary of the One 
who Plots in the Shadows (page 196). The Hunters will awaken in the world of 
Princess Tuvstarr and must solve the mystery of her disappearance in order to 
awaken.

183
Once upon a 
time…
Good evening my dear hosts, my name is 
Sherazade and I am a weaver of tales. My 
words will take you to incredible worlds 
and follow the feats of brave Hunters… 
however this story is not like the others 
and these feats are not just imaginary. Will 
our heroes succeed in their quest? Or will 
they perish, disappearing forever from the 
memory of time? 
The story that led us here…
The original fairy tale was written by Helge Kjellin 
in 1913 and tells how Princess Tuvstarr left her home to 
follow Skutt the moose into the forest.
Tuvstarr does not listen to Skutt’s advice, and so the 
Fairies steal her crown, the Hulder (a forest spirit) steals 
her dress and, finally, she loses a golden heart-shaped 
necklace in a black pond in the depths of the woods. The 
Princess will stay there staring at the black pond until she 
turns into a blade of grass: Tuvstarr (Carex Cespitosa).
But stories don’t always go as they were originally 
written... Appalled by the wickedness of the world, 
Princess Tuvstarr learns from rumors around the court 
of the existence of a pond, deep in the woods, where 
wishes can come true. Tuvstarr asks the trusty moose 
Skutt to lead her through the woods. There, the Princess 
trades her crown with the Fairies, to gain free access to 
their lands. 
Tuvstarr then gives the Hulder her dress to continue 
her search, and finally arrives at the heart of the forest. 
There, in the black Pond that no human being has ever 
seen, she plunges her heart and soul. Tuvstarr desires a 
perfect world, where everything is bright and good. And 
that’s what she finds on the other side. A perfect dream 
world. There she is happy, the fields are always green, and 
idealized constructs that resemble her mother and father 
smile at her. But the Dream World needs to balance itself 
out. And so reality, its mirror, becomes dark and twisted. 
The sun sets in eternal darkness, the forest is corrupted, 
animals get sick, and the soil rots.
Tuvstarr’s father and mother, the King and Queen, send 
their knights to bring her back. None of them returns. The 
lands are becoming increasingly corrupt and the people 
are desperate. Eventually, the rulers turn to anyone who 
can help them. This is where our story begins.

1
184
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The land is bewitched. The fields are sterile, the church 
bells are silent, mothers forget to feed their children, and all 
dreams are dark and full of pain. The sun now rests below 
the horizon and the lands are covered by eternal twilight. 
Tuvstarr the Child Princess is gone, wandering into the 
forest and the curse is believed to have something to do 
with her disappearance. The knights were sent to the forest, 
the wetlands, and the wilderness to find her, but none 
have returned. The King and Queen now seek out new 
champions to retrieve their daughter from the wilds and 
end this curse.
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: Sherazade clears her throat, 
then smiles at her guests before speaking. “Our story opens 
with a rude awakening. You are in a strange inn which is 
certainly not the place where you fell asleep last night. The 
bedroom door is thrown open violently and a tall, thin man 
in a soldier’s uniform asks you to follow him out of the room. 
Apparently, the King and Queen of this strange place await 
you to give you an audience.  In the midst of a field of withered 
flowers and rotting crops stands a mighty castle. The walls are 
covered with ivy and the roofs, once gilded, seem blackened 
and burned by the rays of a dying, red and menacing sun. ”
Place: The royal castle, in the center of a large city. The air 
is gloomy, and the faces of the people are sad and dejected. 
A black sky illuminated by a faint crimson light hangs over 
everything like an eternal night.
Time: It is early morning on a gloomy autumn day.
Initial Situation:  The Hunters are summoned by the 
King and Queen for an important mission. They are aware 
that they are in another world or, perhaps, within a dream.
Information:
 ◆ The King and Queen sit on their thrones staring sternly 
at the Hunters. They do not speak, but their butler, a 
lean and hunched old man, will offer the Hunters gold 
and honors to bring back Princess Tuvstarr. She has 
fled into the great forest that lies beyond the castle. 
 ◆ Before departure, the old butler will join the Hunters 
to talk out of the King and Queen’s earshot. He will say 
that there is a curse upon the land. The Hunters must 
prepare themselves for the necessity of an extreme act 
to save the kingdom, perhaps even to kill the Princess 
herself.
 ◆ As the Hunters set out for the wild forest, people in 
the villages will report that many have seen Princess 
Tuvstarr ride a moose and disappear into the woods. 
And from that moment, the world has changed.
End of Scene:  The Hunters head into the great wild 
forest in search of Princess Tuvstarr’s trail. Go to SECOND 
SCENE - The Wild Forest.

185
The moose and the moths
“He was once a majestic being, the King of the Woods and 
Wildlands. The name Skutt was given to him by the rocks, 
torrents, and small streams, as he had the ability to make 
great leaps through any obstacle. One day, when the sun was 
bright, he ventured into the fields and flowers near the castle 
that humans built. There, he met Tuvstarr and was fascinated 
by the little creature with her white dress, long golden hair 
and sparkling crown. When Tuvstarr asked him to see the 
rest of the world, he couldn’t refuse and put her on his back to 
ride into the wilderness. 
He told her about the pond in the middle of the woods where 
all wishes can be fulfilled, but only if the wish is made by 
an innocent child. And so she asked to go there. During the 
journey the Princess traded both the crown and the dress for 
safe passage, and at the pond she disappeared into the black 
water, leaving only her reflection. Then there was a crack in 
the sky, which froze the sun in place forever.
Not knowing what to do, Skutt wandered the lands in shame. 
He came across one of the knights sent by the King and Queen. 
The noble warrior charged the moose with his spear couched 
under his arm. The moose faced him head-on and killed the 
armored foe and his steed. But the spear had hit its mark and 
Skutt fell, dying. However, the power of Tuvstarr’s affection 
would not allow him to die. The Princess was so anxious that 
she wished that Skutt could exist forever, even in death. 
The moose is now rotting. Large worms crawl into his flesh, 
and in the moonlight, they hatch into ghostly moths that 
flutter around his horns. His stench is carried by the wind 
and even carrion-eating birds avoid him. At the time of his 
death, he forgot his name. But he can’t forget Tuvstarr and 
the guilt he feels… ”
Skutt the Moose
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a majestic and magnificent moose, 
ravaged by the decay of death and Tuvstarr’s magic which 
keeps me alive.
Agenda:  I have to find someone to save Tuvstarr from the 
black pond and the illusion she fell into, because I can’t get in 
/ Protect Tuvstarr above all else, even if it means having the 
world rot away.
Gift - Spectral Presence:  Skutt cannot truly be 
eliminated as long as Tuvstarr’s wish keeps him alive. 
The moose can move through fog, disappearing and 
reappearing even many feet away. Within the fog, no one 
can stop or impede Skutt’s movement. When he suffers 
his third Wound, Skutt falls apart, only to revive in the 
following Scene.
Information: Skutt is a Main NPC that Hunters can 
encounter in any Scene. He will always appear accompanied 
by a thick fog, stinking of rot, and surrounded by pale 
moths. From his horns, like a ghostly veil, hangs the torn 
fabric of a knightly cloak, and a spear is half driven into 
his body.
If the Hunters fight:  Skutt is not interested in the fight 
but is still a proud animal, so he will lash out at the enemy, 
charging with his antlers. 
If the Hunters talk to Skutt:  He can tell how he took 
Tuvstarr to the sacred pond, deep in the woods. Whatever 
Tuvstarr did by diving into the lake, it wreaked havoc on the

2
186
world. Skutt wants to save Tuvstarr, but without endangering 
her. He cannot enter the pond himself, nor can any other 
inhabitant of these woods.
The magic of the pond binds anyone seeking it to make the 
same journey as the Princess, crossing the places where she 
exchanged her treasures to continue forward. If the Hunters 
bring those treasures to him, Skutt will lead them to the 
pond where Tuvstarr has disappeared.
SECOND SCENE – The Wild 
Forest
Read aloud to the Hunters: Sherazade remains silent for 
a moment, the audience motionless in ecstatic admiration. 
Then, the Weaver brings a glass of wine to her mouth, though 
she barely wets her lips. “You travel past the meadow covered 
with withered flowers, small villages, hills, and streams.  
The closer you get to the wilderness and the great forest, the 
more the atmosphere changes. The woods before you are 
shrouded in perpetual darkness. Only the faintest light of the 
sun hanging just beyond the horizon reaches this place. But the 
sun never really rises, leaving this land enclosed in a twilight 
and ghostly hour. The creatures of the woods have gone mad. 
Corrupted by the whispers in the grass, mesmerized by the 
moon’s reflections, they drink poisonous sap and foam that 
smells of sulphur. ”
Place: The wood that separates the kingdom from the 
deepest forest is basked in the faintest twilight glow. 
Time: Time has stopped and no longer has any meaning.
Information: When crossing the wood, the Hunters may 
have some encounters. Roll a die or choose. 
Note: During this Scene, the Hunters may encounter Skutt.
1: A fox is killing and eating its own pups while giving birth 
to them. 
2: Two ravens, Hugin and Munin, messengers of Father 
Odin, approach the Hunters. Even the ancient gods want 
to stop this disease. If a Hunter approaches the ravens, they 
will realize they can mentally communicate with them. The 
ravens want to follow the Hunters. Anyone who makes a 
pact with them receives the Hugin and Munin Scenario Gift, 
without the need to have an Interlude.
3: Black fumes fill the air, foamy and dense bubbles emerging 
from a dark pond. A knight in rusty armor lies dead near one 
of the springs. His eyes are white and puffy, like a sack of eggs 
ready to burst. The corpse holds an ashwood spear with an 
iron point. If a Hunter chooses to take the spear, he gains the 
Lance of the Fallen Knight’ Scenario Gift without the need to 
have an Interlude.

3a
187
4: The Troll Stallo sleeps sitting under a tree, and a bird has 
built a nest in his hair. If he is awakened by too much noise 
or for any other reason, he will try to eat the Hunters.
Stallo
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an old troll and my appetite is 
matched only by my strength. A bird has made its nest on 
my head and when it gets angry it starts to peck me in the 
eye, which is incredibly annoying!
Gift – Troll:  Stallo possesses enormous strength and 
each Position Check for a physical action attempted 
against him receives 1 Penalty. At the end of each of his 
actions, Stallo recovers 1 Wound he suffered. When he 
reaches 0 Wounds, the troll will collapse to the ground, 
only to regenerate within minutes. 
5: A saddled warhorse, magnificent and white as fresh 
snow, is grazing in the tall grass. This mount belonged to 
one of the knights who met a dire fate in the search for 
Tuvstarr. The steed will be reluctant to be approached, but 
if the Hunters succeed (Threat OL Medium 5, Descriptor: 
I am a fast, mighty, and frightened horse), it will take them 
to the body of its old master, where the Hunters can retrieve 
the knightly crest showing a fox with two tails. This crest 
will be useful when meeting the Gallant Knight (page 
191).
6: A ruined castle on a hill. The castle is the scene of a 
massacre. The men and women who once lived within its 
walls have apparently killed each other. Only a ten-year-old 
girl remains alive, but she is angry and will try to attack the 
Hunters with a hidden knife.
Angry Little Girl
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I survived the adults’ madness and now I 
struggle like a ferocious animal not to succumb to this 
cruel world.
Agenda: Find a safe place to stay or a group of people 
ready to accept and protect me.
End of Scene:  after one or more encounters (at the 
discretion of the Storyteller) the Hunters come across 
the crown of the Fairies (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - 
The Crown of the Fairies) or the Hulder (THIRD SCENE 
OPTION B - The Hulder and the white dress).
THIRD SCENE OPTION A – 
The Crown of the Fairies
Read aloud to the Hunters: Sherazade smiles at a young 
nobleman among her audience who has risen from his chair 
to get a better look at her. She winks at him and says: “Sit 
down my little guest, now the best is coming. ” 
“The swamps are shrouded in thin veils of a ghostly mist. All 
is silent. The moon appears to crack and bleed into the sky, 
and the faint stars die out one by one as you travel deeper 
into this nocturnal realm. There is a light in the distance, a 
glow that is reflected in the swamps and still water. This light 
comes from a small golden crown, and all around, many 
silhouettes dance as if enthralled by its light. Fairies look like 
pale women with long hair. They glide, dance, and writhe in 
the air as if adrift in an invisible current. They are dreamlike, 
ethereal, but their bodies meet, touch and brush against each 
other or, sometimes, through each other. Their eyes are shut

188
and they seem to moan with pleasure as they bask in the light 
that shines from the golden crown. Not far away, sitting in 
the damp mud, are the bodies of all those who have drowned 
here over the centuries. They have emerged from the earth, 
awakened from the dead, to watch the Fairies dance. They 
sit, motionless, observing the show with dull eyes and empty 
eye sockets. ”
Place: The swamp where the fairies have 
carried the crown of Tuvstarr.
Time: An eternal night, shrouded in fog and 
under the light of a faded moon.
Information: The crown is sitting on a small 
islet in an open swamp. It is possible to reach 
it by jumping between the various stones that 
emerge from the water.
Threat OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A marshy swamp with a stone 
track as its only path.
The water is black, cold, and deep enough to slow a 
Hunter who falls into it.
Note: During this Scene, the Hunters may encounter 
Skutt.
 ◆ The Fairies are corrupted by the splendor of the crown 
and will not allow anyone to touch it. It is possible to 
communicate with them by interrupting their dance. 
The Fairies will tell the story of a human princess who 
gifted them with the crown, as bright as a small sun, 
in order to cross the swamp. It is difficult to maintain 
a conversation with dreamlike entities, and in a short 
time they will slip back into their dreams and start 
dancing again.
 ◆ If the Hunters attempt to take the crown, the Fairies 
will awaken from their trance. Screaming in pain, they 
will attack the culprits without hesitation. Around the 
crown there are always twelve fairies.
 ◆ Somehow blocking the light emanating from the 
crown will awaken the Fairies from the spell. They will 
look at the cracked moon, screaming in agony as they 
realize how much the lands around 
them have been corrupted. Some will 
suffer so much that they will become 
dew in the grass or a pale reflection in 
the pond. Others will ask the Hunters 
to do something to save their lands.
Fairies
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are Fairies, spirits of nature 
who move gracefully in a world of clumsy humans.
Agenda: Defend the crown that Tuvstarr has given us and 
dance forever wrapped in its sweet light. 
Gift – Madness: Made mad by the light of the crown, the 
Fairies rage at anyone who tries to touch it. Two or more 
Fairies can attack together to make a single action with a 
bonus to the OL equal to the number of fairies involved. If 
the action succeeds, in addition to inflicting 1 Wound, the 
target will be thrown into the swamp to be drowned. Once 
in the water, the target can attempt to break out of the flurry 
of drowned, dead hands pulling from the bottom with a 
Defense Check against OL Medium 5.
End of Scene: After recovering the crown of the Fairies, 
the Hunters must also recover the dress from the Hulder 
(THIRD SCENE OPTION B - The Hulder and the White

3b
189
Dress) or, if they already have it, go through the forest to the 
black pond (FOURTH SCENE - The Black pond).
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – 
The Hulder and the White Dress
Read aloud to the Hunters: Sherazade takes out two paper 
shapes from a bag, the first is the silhouette of a graceful Fairy, 
the second that of a deformed creature. She places them in front 
of the flame of a candle and on the wall, as if by magic, the 
projected shadows swap places. “This area of the forest is even 
darker than any you have been through so far. The woods are 
deep, dark, and full of evil shadows. You come across giant 
boulders entirely covered in dense moss and bearded lichens 
hanging from tree branches, with the cold wind whipping 
through the foliage. On the ground you see a streak of blood and 
slime, as if forming a gloomy path that guides your steps. ”
Place: Among the trees of a dark forest, where not even the 
light of the moon dares to show itself.
Time: It is night. Darkness envelops the forest, giving the 
trees ghostly shapes.
Information: This is the Hulder’s realm. A spiritual forest 
entity with feminine traits that once lived in symbiosis with 
trees but is now corrupt and repulsive. Tuvstarr traded her 
dress for passage through the woods. The Hulder took the thin 
fabric in her hands, and it immediately made her feel unlike 
anything she had felt before. With the dress, she felt strong 
and beautiful, but also prey to a voracious hunger. Now the 
dress is nothing more than a dirty piece of cloth that the spirit 
has tied over her eyes like a blindfold. The Hulder obsessively 
repeats how beautiful, graceful and lovely she is. This can be a 
hint to help the Hunters realize that with the blindfold on the 
monster is unaware of her true appearance.
Note: During this Scene, the Hunters may encounter Skutt.
 ◆ It is easy to spot the tracks of the Hulder. A trail of blood 
and slime is left on the ground where the creature crawls. 
Her sense of smell is almost non-existent, but if the 
Hunters make a noise entering her part of the forest, she 
will charge furiously forward to devour them. 
 ◆ The Hulder is powerful and killing her in a fight will be 
difficult. The best way to defeat her is to lure her into a trap 
so as to tear the dirty piece of cloth – the dress Tuvstarr 
gave her – from her eyes. If this happens near a reflective 
surface (such as one of the many ponds that fill this area), 
she will see her reflection and what she has become. She 
will scream in agony and crawl away into the ashes of the 
night. There, she will throw up everything she ate before 
returning to her original form, filled with shame and guilt.
The Hulder
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an ancient forest spirit. I have eaten so many 
living things that I have become fat and swollen, and the pieces 
of the animals and people I have devoured are dripping from my 
slobbering lips.
Agenda: Devour everything I meet.
Gift – Corrupted Spirit:  The Hulder has a feminine form, 
but her skin is spongy and slimy. Her breath smells of rot. She 
doesn’t realize what a grotesque monster she has become, as in 
her mind she is still beautiful and seductive. When she suffers 
1 Wound, the Hulder vomits a cloud of foul vapor that reeks 
of rot. All the subjects hit by the cloud suffer 1 Wound in turn. 
The effect of this Gift can be avoided with a Defense Check. 
Gift – Swollen with Flesh and Blood: If killed, the Hulder 
will emit a long, heart-breaking cry that will echo in the 
woods, and all the dead she has eaten will scream in agony in a

4
190
cacophonous chorus. Anyone 
who hears the cry loses 2 Soma 
and receives 1 Penalty on any 
action for the rest of the Scene.
End of Scene: After recovering 
the dress, the Hunters must 
also recover the crown from 
the Fairies (THIRD SCENE 
OPTION A - The Crown of the 
Fairies) or, if they already have it, 
cross the forest to the black pond 
(FOURTH SCENE - The Black 
Pond).
FOURTH SCENE 
– The Black Pond
Read aloud to the Hunters:  
“My dear guests, after a long journey 
our heroes have finally reached 
their destination... or have they?” 
Sherazade winks at you and, for a 
moment, you have the impression that 
she has moved just a few steps away.  
“You arrive at the center of the forest. The 
trees are tall and menacing, and they seem 
clustered around a pool of freezing, black 
water. Any light that penetrates here looks 
thin and weak, as if it were an intruder in 
this land consecrated to darkness. Everything 
is still and silent. ”
Place: The center of the great forest, bathed 
in an unnatural darkness.
Time: An indefinite hour, in a place out of the normal flow 
of time.
Information: As Hunters approach the water, they can 
discover two things.
 ◆ While the princess is gone, Tuvstarr’s reflection can 
still be seen in the black waters, showing an image of 
a naked girl sitting on the shore and staring into the 
abyss. Next to her is a knight’s plate armor. The shield 
has a fox with two tails as a heraldic symbol. There are 
footsteps on the muddy shore leading into the black 
water.
 ◆ To enter the mirror world, the Hunters will have to dive 
and swim to the bottom of the Pond, through the cold 
and fatal darkness. When they are at the bottom, in 
the mud and slime, suddenly a light will appear from 
above. Sunlight in a clear sky. By swimming to the 
surface, the Hunters will enter the Dream World that 
Tuvstarr has built for herself.
The Dream World
Read aloud to the Hunters:  “What stretches out before 
your eyes is the Dream World that Tuvstarr created with 
her wish. It is a land of joy, happiness, and goodness. Here 
the days are eternal, bees never sting, and nature provides 
everything those who live there need. It is built on all that is 
good, siphoned out of the real world and remodelled according 
to the will of the Child Princess. The grass is emerald green, 
the sky is blue with perfect white clouds. A thousand and 
a thousand more flowers. Butterflies and pollen in the air. 
Everything is lush and new. What was once a quagmire is 
a field with rolling hills, what used to be a forbidding forest 
is now a lush wood with flourishing trees and meadows 
carpeted with flowers. In the distance, the high towers of the 
castle reflect the sunlight. ”

5
191
Place: The perfect dream world created by Tuvstarr.
Time: Early morning, on a beautiful summer day.
Information: When they arrive, the Hunters will feel like 
they have been reborn. Their wounds will stop hurting (it is 
considered a special rest that allows all Hunters to recover 
1 Wound). Their garments and clothes will be mended, in 
fact looking better than ever before. The water they drink 
and the fruit they eat are the best meal they have ever eaten. 
It is truly a paradise.
 ◆ Encounter with the Gallant Knight: The Gallant Knight 
sits under a tree, eating an apple. His sword is beside 
him. He is an old man, but there is still strength in his 
steps and voice. In fact, his hair isn’t as grey as it used 
to be. His pains have lessened. An old wound that once 
grieved him is now merely a white scar. The Knight has 
abandoned his quest. He found his way to the black 
pond, entered the deep waters, and arrived in the 
Dream World. He met Tuvstarr, tried to bring her back, 
but she offered to let him stay here. He agreed, vowing 
to continue to protect her from any threat. However, he 
can be convinced to pick up his quest once more if he is 
told that his brothers all died in the search and if he is 
shown the two-tailed fox crest (page 187). 
 ◆ If the Hunters possess the Lance of the Fallen Knight, 
the Gallant Knight will ask them to return it to him 
so that it can be brought back to the castle in the real 
world. Whether defeated or convinced, the Knight will 
reveal that Tuvstarr is in the Dream World.
The Gallant Knight
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a respected and experienced knight of the 
kingdom. My skill is only matched by my honor.
Agenda: Respect my knightly duties and serve the young 
Princess Tuvstarr / Save the kingdom from what’s happening.
Gift - Gallant Knight:  The Knight is incredibly skilled, 
but he would never use his full abilities against anyone 
he doesn’t consider truly evil. When defending against 
an enemy attack, his mastery increases his OL by 1. The 
Knight will never inflict the final Wound on a target if he 
doesn’t feel it truly deserves death.
End of Scene:  After finding out where Tuvstarr is, the 
Hunters can proceed to the FIFTH SCENE - The Castle of 
Dreams.
FIFTH SCENE – The Castle of 
Dreams
Read aloud to the Hunters:  “ Ahead, you see a castle 
bigger than any other building in the country. The roof shines 
with gold while the tallest tower is like another sun, shining 
ever so bright. Looking around you, however, you notice that 
everything seems to be nothing more than a false reflection 
of reality. Farmers at work, animals in the fields, the King 
and Queen themselves you see at the gates of the castle, all do 
nothing but smile and nod. They speak in a low voice and say 
comforting things like: “Stay here, everything is fine, the lands 
are fresh and full of life. ” You realize that they are nothing 
more than simple, idealized creations of Tuvstarr’s memories, 
but full of joy and warmth. ”

192
Place: The perfect dream world created by Tuvstarr.
Time: Early morning, on a beautiful summer day.
Information: The Hunters may encounter Tuvstarr in the 
village, the forest, or the castle. She is simply a child who 
wanders, naked and full of life. Tuvstarr goes around creating 
things in her dream world.
 ◆ Tuvstarr will ask the Hunters to remain in her kingdom, 
just like the Gallant Knight. If they agree, the Hunters will 
spend their lives in a dream and will soon forget about the 
world on the other side of the pond.
 ◆ Fighting Tuvstarr is a very difficult challenge. But if the 
Hunters manage to imprison her or push her near the 
lake, they may throw her in to make her 
emerge on the other side.
 ◆ Getting her to leave 
her dream is difficult as 
she only has vague 
memories of the real 
world, as if that 
were the dream 
to her. She 
does not 
like the pond in the forest. There is something about it 
that makes her uncomfortable or even scares her, but she 
does not have the ability to “remove” it with her power, 
as it is the gateway between worlds. Talking about Skutt’s 
painful existence will confuse her and could be a lever to 
convince her to follow the Hunters’ advice. The Gallant 
Knight, if persuaded back to his mission, could also be of 
help in convincing Tuvstarr to trust the Hunters.
 ◆ The Crown and the Dress: The Hunters will be able to 
show Tuvstarr the crown that enchanted the fairies and 
the dress that she gave to Hulder in order to make her 
recall memories of the real world. The Princess will have a 
vague memory of owning and trading them. Taking them 
in her hand, she will recognize them. She will remember 
the weight of the crown on her head and the warmth of 
the dress’s fabric against her fingers. Wearing her items, 
she will realize the difference between what is dream 
and what is reality, and will begin to cry. At this point, 
however, she will be even more strongly opposed to the 
idea of returning to the real world.
Princess 
Tuvstarr
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a pure and innocent soul, who just 
wants a world without suffering.
Agenda: I want to stay forever in my perfect world. I 
have been here for so long that I have forgotten what my 
life was like before.
Gift – Creative Will:  Tuvstarr has the power to create 
anything she wants in the Dream World. She can make a

193
bird appear out of nowhere or make an apple tree grow 
from nothing to bearing fruit in a couple of seconds. She 
can deflect a river, make sunlight warmer, shape clouds, 
and make small fish glow around her when swimming in 
a river. Each new creation draws life from the real world, 
making it more corrupt and darker. Tuvstarr can use her 
power to defend herself by literally manipulating the 
whole world. If Tuvstarr is killed in this world, she will 
transform into colorful butterflies to regenerate herself at 
her castle. Although Tuvstarr has virtually infinite power, 
her innocence means that even if threatened, she will never 
try to kill a living being. Rather, the Princess will try to hurt 
and scare any threats and, in the worst case, drive them 
away.
Gift – Pure Innocence: Tuvstarr radiates innocence and 
beauty. Whoever meets her will fall under her spell. She is 
always friendly, and her voice is clear and beautiful, with 
the sharpness of ice breaking on a winter pond. Those who 
interact with Tuvstarr must make a Defense Check or be 
fascinated by her. For the rest of the Scene, the bewitched 
subject will not take any action that could harm Tuvstarr 
and, furthermore, will defend her at the cost of their life 
from any threats. Seeing how the world outside the pond 
is rotting will make a bewitched subject instantly come to 
their senses.
The other Skutt
Skutt is always near Tuvstarr and will appear as soon as 
the Hunters encounter the Princess. For the Hunters it will 
be clear that this is not the real Skutt, but another dream 
created by Tuvstarr. Skutt follows the Princess and she rides 
him through the kingdom. Since he is a dream, this Skutt 
will have no recollection of the Hunters. In reality, a spark 
of the original Skutt resides in his dream projection and 
can be exploited by the Hunters to push him to remember 
the real world.
Skutt the Moose
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a majestic and magnificent moose who 
protects the sweet Princess Tuvstarr with all his might.
Agenda: Protect Tuvstarr from all threats and make her 
happy / Listen to a small part of my soul that cries out that 
this perfect world is just a wishful illusion.

194
A sad ending
Read aloud to the Hunters: “You take Tuvstarr back across 
the pond as her Dream World dissolves. The sun returns to the 
lands. The madness that has infested the animals is forgotten as 
a passing nightmare. Skutt can finally let go, sighing in relief as 
it is time to lay down his big head and forgive Tuvstarr for her 
transgressions. Tuvstarr, after mourning the death of her dear 
friend, returns to her parents’ arms filled with sadness. 
An uncertain future awaits her. Perhaps she will become a wise 
queen or maybe they will find her little body floating in a river, 
drowned as she tries to return to a Dream World that no longer 
exists. ”
“We have come to the end of this story. My thanks to all those 
who have had the patience to listen to the words of this humble 
storyteller. But even the most enchanting places cannot delight 
when you are there against your will, and at this point I think 
it is time to say goodbye to our protagonists, knowing that the 
tragic story of Princess Tuvstarr has also touched their hearts. 
But let the sadness at our parting be tempered by anticipation, 
for our heroes will be caught in another story before too long.
With gratitude, Sherazade the Weaver of Tales. ”

195
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Tuvstarr’s Reflection Scenario and can be acquired once 
the requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one 
Scenario Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have 
to forgo the previous one. Unlike with the normal rules, in 
this case an Interlude it is not necessary to acquire the Gifts.
Hugin and Munin
Requirement: To meet Hugin and Munin and make a 
pact with them.
Once per Scene, you can ask for advice from Hugin and 
Munin. Ask the Storyteller a question, choosing from 
among: “Where should I go?” “How can I defeat that 
enemy?” “What is missing in my search?” The Storyteller 
will answer with a cryptic sentence, which always includes 
a useful hint. Get 1 Additional Success on all Defense 
Checks against an attack from a non-human creature, 
thanks to the blessing of Odin himself. At the beginning 
of each Scene, you must spend 1 Soma to feed the spirits of 
Hugin and Munin, or lose the use of this Gift for the rest of 
the Scenario.
Lance of the Fallen Knight
Requirement: Collect the lance of the knight who died in 
the search for Tuvstarr.
When you wield this spear and obtain an Outcome with 
Increment on an attack, you can inflict 1 Additional 
Wound. The tip of the lance can ignite at will, casting a light 
and allowing you to have a burning fire always near at hand.

196
One Thousand and 
One Nightmares
A special Scenario inspired by One Thousand and One Nights
Safety Keywords: Mental manipulation / Malignant psychological manipulation

197
Once upon a 
time…
A beautiful storyteller from the mysterious 
East seduces all the courts in which she 
performs, telling dark stories about a group 
of Hunters of the Order. But where the story 
ends, a real nightmare begins, as some of the 
characters from the stories are brought to 
life as monstrous shadows ready to steal the 
souls of simple mortals...
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
One Thousand and One Nightmares is a special 
Scenario that can be used during the course of any 
Campaign or for a short game. In fact, once Sherazade is 
introduced as an opponent, this Dark Presence will have 
several ways of putting a spoke in the wheel of the Hunters. 
After each interference, the Hunters will be able to collect 
a clue as to the whereabouts of Sherazade and set out in 
search of her to finally put an end to her work.
 
How to use Sherazade
Sherazade can interfere in many ways during the adventures 
of the Hunters.
Summoning the Shadow of an 
enemy from the past
The Shadow is a unique opponent, a Dark Presence that 
reminds the Hunters of an opponent they’ve already faced. 
The Shadow sent by Sherazade will act in all respects as an 
Opponent Dark Presence (in addition to all the normal 
content of the chosen Scenario).
Shadow of Sherazade 
(Opponent Dark Presence)
OL Medium 5 – (1 Wound)
Descriptor: That of the original NPC.
Agenda: Interfering with and impeding the path of the 
Hunters of the Order, preventing them from achieving their 
mission.
Gift - Variable:  The Shadow has all the Gifts of the 
original NPC. 
Gift - Shadow Body:  A Shadow is a darker, sadder 
version of the original. They can speak and behave like 
the character they are modelled after, but on a careful 
inspection they will appear cold and mechanical. Once per 
Scene, a Shadow can appear or disappear from the darkness 
by moving through walls and other solid obstacles. When 
it spawns, it increases its OL by 1 because of the element

198
of surprise. When it suffers the Wound that would kill it, 
the Shadow dissipates until its turn in the Exchange. At 
that point, it will emerge from the darkness as if nothing 
had happened. The Shadow will not reappear if the area 
the Hunters are in is fully illuminated with light sources 
or magic. The only way to truly destroy the Shadow is to 
expose it to sunlight.
Telling a story starring the Hunters
Another way to involve the Hunters in the plots of 
Sherazade is to have them awaken within a story she’s 
telling. Tuvstarr’s Reflection on page 182 is a great example 
of a Scenario involving Hunters in this way.
The Scenarios imposed by the stories of Sherazade must be 
created in the classic way presented on page 91, but the 
introduction must always be the following.
Once upon a 
time…
Good evening my dear hosts, my name is Sherazade and I am 
a weaver of tales. My words will take you to incredible worlds 
and follow the feats of brave Hunters… however this story 
is not like the others and these feats are not just imaginary. 
Will our heroes succeed in their quest? Or will they perish, 
disappearing forever from the memory of time?
The Scenarios brought into the game by Sherazade allow 
you to create stories with more fantasy elements, and if you 
wish, even stories disconnected from the context of Broken 
Europe in which the Hunters normally move. In this way 
it is possible to explore mythology or stories that are not 
fairytale-related, focusing on the group of Hunters who, 
experiencing Sherazade’s story as both audience-member 
and Hunter, must solve the mystery to be able to awaken. 
Through a Scenario told by Sherazade, it is also possible 
to replay a Scenario already faced, adding a completely 
different element and creating a “what-if ” version of what 
the Hunters have already experienced. 
Just think of a version of Red-Hood Iskra where Elizaveta 
comes to Durfort to save her daughter Iskra, kidnapped by 
the dangerous werewolf Gerard Dubois. Or an alternate 
telling of The City of Pigs, where the three Browne brothers 
have turned the whole city into a sect of The God Under the 
Mine and are preparing the rite for his awakening, which 
obviously consists of sacrificing old Bardolf Greed.
Defeating Sherazade
From the moment she comes into play, Sherazade should 
become a constant presence in the adventures of the 
Hunters, sending Shadows to get in their way or throwing 
them in the middle of some absurd story. During the 
Scenario, however, the Hunters must be able to deduce the 
influence of Sherazade and find a clue to her location.
How Shadows release clues:
 ◆ When it has an obvious advantage, the Shadow says 
something like: “My Lady Sherazade will be proud of 
m e .”
 ◆ When defeated, the Shadow can exit the scene shouting, 
“My Lady Sherazade will avenge me. ”
 ◆ When defeated, alternatively, the Shadow can dissipate, 
leaving behind a small object typical of the area where 
Sherazade currently resides. This could be a coin, a 
garment, or a poem. Collecting these items, it will be

199
clear to the Hunters that they serve as focus to animate 
the Shadows and that, in fact, they all come from the 
same area.
How the stories told by Sherazade 
reveal clues:
At the end of each story, when the Scenario is solved, 
the Hunters will see a short scene of Sherazade with her 
audience entranced by what has just been told. The first 
time, the Hunters will be able to understand the region of 
Europe in which the Weaver of Tales is located; the second 
time, Sherazade herself will turn towards them smiling, as 
if to underline the mutual awareness of the bond that her 
story has created.
How to deal with Sherazade
At some point, it will become clear to the Hunters that 
Sherazade is a problem that needs to be 
addressed. The journey to find her can develop 
in several ways:
 ◆ The Storyteller creates a Scenario to face 
Sherazade. In the Scenario, the Weaver of 
Tales will be protected by at least one of the 
Main NPCs, fascinated by her Gift Words like 
Honey.
 ◆ The Storyteller adds Sherazade to one of 
the unplayed Scenarios. In this case, she 
will act as an Opponent Dark Presence 
that will arrive at the site of the 
investigation at the same time as 
the Hunters. Sherazade will work 
to ensure the Hunters’ failure, 
thus proving their powerlessness.
 ◆ The Hunters may find a way (using magic, with a 
Treasure of the Order, etc.) to drag Sherazade into one 
of the stories in which she has imprisoned them. In this 
case, the Weaver of Tales will be forced to collaborate 
with them so as not to remain a prisoner of what she 
has created. At the end of the story, the Hunters will 
meet once more with Sherazade, no longer allies, for 
the final confrontation.
Sherazade in a Broken Tales 
Campaign
Due to the recursive nature of her stories, Sherazade is a 
great addition to any Broken Tales Campaign. In the list 
of clues on page 246 there are also prompts to link her 
to the story of the Child Savior and The One who Plots in 
the Shadows. As written in her Agenda, Sherazade is not 
a purely evil character, but more of a wounded soul who 
has lost everything she cared about and who could even 
become an ally of the Hunters.
Note: Sherazade is featured as a Hunter of the 
Order in The Broken Ones expansion.

200
Sherazade  
the Weaver of Tales  
(Opponent Dark Presence)
Sherazade is a young storyteller who, together with her sister 
Dinarzad, was captured and taken to the court of a wealthy Sultan. 
The Sultan was a man with a dark soul who enjoyed inflicting 
fear and pain on other people, and who invented trivial excuses 
to torment poor innocents. The debauched, rich man decided to 
play a game with Sherazade. Every night she had to tell him a 
story. If he liked it, Dinarzad’s execution would be postponed for 
a day. After thirty days of tales, the two sisters would earn their 
freedom. Sherazade created a new story every night, always with 
the threat of displeasing the Sultan and thus condemning her 
beloved sister. But every evening, the Sultan was amazed by the 
beauty of Sherazade’s tales and granted her another day. Thus 
the fateful thirty days passed and on the morning of the thirty-
first, Sherazade was summoned by the Sultan. The man threw 
himself at her feet, weeping, having had Dinarzad executed 
the first night, certain of Sherazade’s failure and eager to 
see her despair even should she succeed. But now, after 
over thirty nights of beautiful stories, the Sultan’s heart was 
filled with love and admiration for Sherazade. He wanted 
her by his side as his bride. The Sultan offered Sherazade all 
his wealth, power, and everything she might desire to forget 
Dinarzad and her death. But Sherazade had her sister’s 
smile etched in her mind as the last happy moment she could 
recall, something not even the Sultan’s great wealth could erase. 
And so all the stories told took shape, black shadows emerging 
from the walls to seize the Sultan and hold him in terrible darkness

201
for eternity. Empty of any emotion, Sherazade wandered all 
over the known world and finally met a tired and stooped
