man, a writer who, like her, had seen every happy memory 
destroyed by the selfishness of others. The two forged a bitter 
alliance to bring new life into the stories now broken by the 
wish of the Child Savior.
OL Medium 5 – (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a storyteller who can bring the characters 
I invent to life. My charm is so beguiling that it cannot be 
ignored.
Agenda: Punish all those who believe they can change the 
world for the better / Find peace and smile once again.
Gift - Words like Honey:  Sherazade can tell one of 
her stories to bring out a memory, an emotion, or a fear. 
Highlight 1 Keyword from this brief story. If a Hunter or 
NPC hears the story and is linked to the Keyword, the 
related emotion will awaken, leading them to open up to 
Sherazade and share something about themselves. 
Gift - A Thousand and One Nights:  One of 
the characters from a Scenario already faced 
by the Hunters comes to life as a Shadow. 
Create a Descriptor to represent it. The 
Shadow thus created will be at the service 
of Sherazade and will do whatever they 
are ordered. Shadows cannot speak 
nor act in places where there is more 
light than shadow. During a Scene, 
Sherazade can create a number of 
Shadows equal to the Hunters she 
is facing plus one. For each Hunter 
who has been affected by Words like 
Honey, Sherazade can summon an 
additional Shadow.
The Shadows 
The Shadows of Sherazade are but the dark reflection of 
what they were in the story they come from.
See page 197.

202
The City of Pigs
Once upon a 
time…
The busy town of Whitehaven is experiencing 
a rebirth under the leadership of Bardolf 
Greed. The rich industrialist has invested his 
considerable fortune in the extraction of coal, 
employing a large part of the population at the 
large mine near the town. Soon, there came 
inevitable accusations of exploitation and 
starvation wages, giving rise to an anarchist 
group called the Pig Heads, named for the 
macabre masks with which they cover their faces. 
With his success darkened by disappearances, 
bloody sacrifices, and mysterious figures moving 
in the night, Bardolf turned to the Order to shine 
the light of hope to the streets of Whitehaven…
A Scenario inspired by The Three Little Pigs (Joseph Jacobs)
Safety Keywords: Body horror / Worker exploitation / Prostitution / Blasphemy

203
The story that led us here…
Bardolf Greed is a wealthy industrialist who made 
his fortune thanks to shrewd deals and trade. Reaching the 
ripe old age of seventy without an heir, Bardolf has decided 
to dedicate his life to the only true, great love of his life: 
money. Too old to travel, Bardolf has moved to Whitehaven, 
an unremarkable town whose only resource was the 
rich mines beneath the city. Within a few years, Bardolf 
transformed Whitehaven into an industrial city by trapping 
all its inhabitants into employment with starvation wages. 
But not all of Whitehaven’s citizens were willing to give 
in to the wealthy magnate. The brothers Clarence, Frank 
and Arthur Browne have always cared for the community. 
Arthur, in particular, worked for years to make Whitehaven 
a quiet town where every resident could have a home and 
a job to get by. Bardolf, however, has bought the town’s 
favor, seizing property and leveling houses to build both 
factories and dormitories for the now homeless workers. 
By falsifying some official papers, Bardolf managed to 
throw the three brothers out on the street, forcing them 
to accept a job as miners or starve. Clarence soon quit, 
choosing the life of a slacker over labor in the mines. Frank 
and Arthur’s time in the mines was ended by a major mine 
collapse, during which Frank lost an arm and a leg, and 
Arthur was trapped by falling rubble and left for dead. The 
collapse was caused by the awakening of The God Under 
the Mine, a huge pig-like creature embedded in the rock 
below the city. Alive and trapped underground, Arthur 
found the God’s head and after days of agony he fed on 
divine flesh, discovering that it confers incredible powers. 
Crazed with grief and a wild hope that Arthur wasn’t dead, 
Frank dug a long vertical tunnel, eventually managing to 
create a communication channel with Arthur. United in 
vengeance and endowed with the power of divine flesh, 
the three brothers then began their personal war against 
Bardolf Greed.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Whitehaven is devastated by acts of violence and sabotage, 
which have interfered with its industrial productivity 
considerably. People are afraid to walk the streets because 
a criminal gang, called the Pig Heads, goes around 
kidnapping the most skilled and capable workers. From 
under the Mine, Arthur leads the rebellion by passing 
pieces of the quickly regenerating God’s flesh to Frank. 
Frank has managed to get hired as a butcher at the big meat 
factory in the city and secretly mixes the flesh of the God 
with common meat, infecting the unsuspecting miners who 
eat it. When the mutation begins to take effect, Clarence 
approaches the targets, offering them the opportunity to 
join the gang. Bardolf senses that the city is under threat 
but does not imagine that his enemy is someone he already 
hates: Arthur, left for dead after the accident. With over fifty 
Pig Heads now working in the mines, Arthur counts the 
days before he can launch his personal revolution.

204
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Our story starts with you 
in conversation with the wealthy industrialist Bardolf Greed. 
Mr. Greed is a man in his seventies, his face plump and 
surrounded by thick, lupine sideburns, while a pair of shrewd 
eyes peek out over small round glasses. Bardolf ’s mansion is a 
perfect example of the changes that big industries are bringing 
to Europe and the rich magnate has spared no expense, nor 
has he spared the wretched workers whose sweat built it. 
With a bottle of fine wine near at hand, Bardolf fumbles with 
his box of tobacco. Behind him stands a dozen men from his 
personal guard, making it clear that the wealthy industrialist 
does not feel safe at all...
Place: The dining room of Greed’s palace. Paintings and 
cutlery with gold lining highlight Bardolf ’s great wealth. 
Greed’s personal guards stand with their backs to the wall, 
staring at the guests like silent statues.
Time: It is early afternoon. Visible through the window is a 
sky gray with rain and fumes from the factories, which are 
constantly active.
Initial Situation: Bardolf calmly explains the situation to 
the Hunters, while the trusty William records everything in 
a large black leather book.
Information: The following information / clues can be 
obtained from the interview with Bardolf.
 ◆ A gang of criminals wanders through the backways of 
the city. They kidnap or injure only the factory workers 
to make them unable to work. Nobody recognizes 
them, because they hide their faces under grotesque 
pig masks.
 ◆ The city had been given new life thanks to Bardolf ’s 
investments. Only one family (the Brownes) strongly 
opposed the birth of the Factories, but in the end, they 
too became Greed’s employees.
 ◆ The mine works at full capacity under the watchful 
control of Edward Right, an honest and dutiful man. 
The textile factories also operate constantly, as does the 
large slaughterhouse run by Frank Browne.
 ◆ For Greed, productivity is everything. If forced to 
choose, Hunters should put the preservation of property 
and machinery before the lives of the townspeople.
1

205
Greed’s Mansion 
Descriptor: A small castle in the center of Whitehaven. The 
pomp of each room underlines Greed’s incredible wealth.
Bardolf Greed 
the Wealthy Industrialist
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a wealthy and skilled businessman who 
always knows how to find a good deal, the one that turns a 
profit for me. My body is old, but my mind remains infallible.
Agenda: Own every business and aspect of trade in 
Whitehaven, so that the whole city is under my total control.
Gift - Smell the deal:  Bardolf is a master of negotiation 
and reading others. After talking to a subject, Bardolf 
can obtain one of the following pieces of information:  
1. Discover a secret regarding the person he is speaking 
with; 2. Obtain an object or piece of information valuable 
to the other party in the negotiation, and which he can use 
as leverage. A Hunter can make a Defense Check to prevent 
Bardolf from using this Gift on them.
William the Man of Law
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a prominent lawyer and I know all of 
Bardolf ’s secrets. I manage the accounts of both the Factories 
and mine with zealous attention.
Bardolf’s personal 
guard
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are long-time mercenaries, paid hand-
somely by Bardolf for our loyalty.

206
The Factories
Descriptor: A series of shoddy, stone buildings used as textile factories and 
foundries. Access is forbidden to outsiders.
If the Hunters talk to some workers, they can get the following information. 
Roll a die or pick from the list:
1-2: Everyone aspires to work at the slaughterhouse where the shifts are less 
pressing, but the unpleasant Frank Browne selects the staff. Frank ignores even 
the most piteous plea and no one understands how he chooses who can work 
alongside him.
3-4: Just outside the city there is an apple orchard that once employed many 
inhabitants of Whitehaven. Now it is just a place where slackers like Clarence 
Browne, as well as those who are unable to work in the factories or in the mine, 
take refuge.
5-6: Not long ago, there was a collapse in the mines that injured or killed many 
miners. Arthur Browne, a prominent citizen who did much for the city before 
Greed’s arrival, was one of the victims.
Whitehaven
Descriptor: An industrial city growing around a large coal mine. The noise and 
fumes from the factories fill the air at every hour of the day, while groups of workers 
go back and forth waiting for their shift.
When the Hunters roam the city, roll 1 die.
1-2: They meet Clarence, who will try to pump the Hunters for information by 
pretending to be well connected.
3-4: They meet Emma, who offers her services and is curious to talk to foreigners.
5-6: A group of Pig Heads equal to double the number of Hunters set up an 
ambush.

207
The Pig Heads
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are a band of revolutionaries in the service 
of the three brothers. The God’s flesh gives us power and fills 
us with the anger we need to wage our war.
Gift – Pigs’ Gang:  When they act, each member of the 
gang changes to become stronger. Their head becomes 
that of a pig and only another Pig Head will be able to 
communicate with them. Their mutation grants a random 
power, roll 1 die. 1. Strength, able to inflict 1 additional 
Wound once per Scene; 2. Speed , once per Scene acts 
2 times consecutively; 3. Resistance , +1 Wound;  
4. Abomination , +1 to Opposition Level; 5. Grunt , 
once per Scene can emit a loud squeal, enemies who hear it 
receive 1 Penalty on their next action; 6. Out of control , 
the OL rises to Hard 7 but, at the end of their action, the 
NPC explodes into a cloud of flesh and blood.
Martha Browne the Mother
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the mother of three brothers, and I am 
so old that I have almost completely lost my sight. My son 
Frank takes care of me, but he doesn’t tell me anything. I 
am convinced that Arthur is not dead, for my instinct as a 
mother tells me so.
Emma the Childhood Friend
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a childhood friend of Clarence and have 
a special relationship with him. He has always promised that 
he will take me away from this place, but I know that he will 
never abandon his brothers. I understand that he has a big 
secret, but tries to protect me by keeping me in the dark.
The Orchard
Descriptor: A large apple orchard which, before the arrival 
of the factories, was one of the main sources of work in the 
city. Now, however, it is abandoned and represents a home for 
all those who cannot get a job.
When the Hunters roam the orchard, roll 1 die, or present 
the encounters in sequence.
1-2 The orchard: The trees have been neglected and on 
the ground there are still abandoned tools and baskets for 
harvesting apples. A sign next to a tool shed reads “Gift  
courtesy of the Browne family” .
3-4 Clarence’s shack: A rickety rundown house with a 
thatched roof. Inside, it is possible to find a rusty key that 
says “slaughterhouse” and a sketched map of the mines that 
says “well” . Around the hut there are always 1d6 + 2 Pig 
Heads.
5-6 Clarence:  The youngest of the Browne brothers is 
lying on the ground, visibly drunk, under an apple tree. 
Clarence always pretends to be drunk with strangers to take 
them off guard.

208
Clarence Browne the 
Younger Brother
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the youngest of the Pig Heads and, despite 
believing in our fight, I am not going to waste much time on 
it. From hanging around, I know Whitehaven like the back 
of my hand.
Agenda: Having fun and lazing around all day, carrying on 
the Pig Head’s cause in my spare time.
Gift - Slacker:  Clarence is a master of the art of 
disappearing when things get tough. In the presence of 
even a small crowd, Clarence can leave a Scene when it 
is his turn to act, without anything or anyone being able 
to stop him. When using this Gift, Clarence hides in the 
orchard, passing the entire following Scene there. 
Gift - Gang Leader:  Eating the flesh of The God Under 
the Mine gives the Browne brothers the ability to transform 
into monstrous beings. Once per Scene, the NPC can 
mutate, gaining the following benefits: 1. Recover a Wound 
once they reach 0. Though apparently dead, the NPC will 
revive within minutes of falling; 2. Once per Scene, act a 
second time during the Exchange, even consecutively;  
3. Once per Scene, increase the Opposition Level by 2 for 
a single action where they can harness their superhuman 
strength.
The 
Slaughterhouse
Descriptor: A huge gray building inside which cattle from 
coastal pastures are slaughtered. Entry is forbidden to non-
workers.
When the Hunters wander around the slaughterhouse, roll 
1 die to see how far they go without attracting attention, or 
present the environments in sequence.
1-2 The outdoor garden:  It is more an open-air barn 
than a garden patch. Bloodstains and excrement suggest 
that the slaughter starts here. Several signs reading “No 
Trespassing” suggest that the area is very controlled.
3-4 The slaughterhouse: Composed of a series of large 
spaces, several stairs lead to underground chambers where 
the meat is preserved. Around ten workers are present day 
and night, all members of the Pig Heads.
5-6 The salting room: A small room at the back of the 
building, where Frank prepares the meat that will be served 
in the cafeterias throughout the city. During the day Frank 
is always present, intent on hiding the flesh of the God 
within freshly butchered beef.

209
Frank Browne the Middle 
Brother
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the middleman of the Pig Heads and act 
as the strongarm of the gang. I have control over all cultists, 
and the power of The God Under the Mine has given me 
strength and endurance equal to that of five men.
Agenda: Take revenge on Bardolf for what he did to me and 
find a remedy for the wounds that have marred me since the 
day of the accident / Mix the flesh of The God Under the Mine 
with beef from the slaughterhouse and feed it to the miners to 
add to the ranks of the gang.
Gift - The Pincer:  Frank replaced his left arm with a 
home-made mechanical arm with many functions. During 
a Scene, Frank can use each of the following one time:  
1. Crush, a hit with the pincer inflicts 1 additional Wound; 
2. Pulley, the pincer can launch from the arm and raise 
or lower Frank about thirty feet. Frank can carry 2 other 
subjects with him. 
Gift - Gang Leader:  Eating the flesh of The God Under 
the Mine gives the Browne brothers the ability to transform 
into monstrous beings. Once per Scene, the NPC can 
mutate, gaining the following benefits: 1. Recover a Wound 
once they reach 0. Though apparently dead, the NPC will 
revive within minutes of falling; 2. Once per Scene, act a 
second time during the Exchange, even consecutively; 3. 
Once per Scene, increase the Opposition Level by 2 for a 
single action where they can harness their superhuman 
strength.
The Mine
Descriptor: A gigantic coal mine where miners work day 
and night. Many areas are abandoned in favor of more 
profitable veins. 
Gift - Maze of Galleries: Whitehaven’s Mine is gigantic 
and without a guide it’s not easy to get to a specific location. 
With each exploration roll, you can get 1 or more Map 
Tokens which represent progress in learning the layout of 
the mine. Upon reaching 3 Map Tokens, you can go directly 
to the Special result.
When the Hunters explore the mine, roll 1 die or choose 
directly based on the narrative.
1-2 Work zone A:  Threading through the tunnels, 
deafened by the sound of steam engines and digging, the 
Hunters encounter a group of miners (2d6 + 6 Minor 
NPCs). If the Hunters do something weird, one of the 
workers will alert Edward. Exploration will yield 1 Map 
Token.
3-4 Work zone B:  Threading through the tunnels, 
deafened by the sound of steam engines and digging, the 
Hunters encounter a group of miners (2d6 + 6 Minor 
NPCs). If the Hunters do something weird, 1d6 + 6 miners 
will reveal themselves as Pig Heads and attack. Exploration 
will yield 2 Map Tokens.
5-6 Abandoned lodging: In an area close to the collapse, 
recent traces of passage can be found. The Hunters meet 
Frank and 5 Pig Heads who are going to the well to meet up 
with Arthur. Exploration will yield 2 Map Tokens.
Special (3 Map Tokens):  By moving a heavy stone, 
the Hunters will find the mouth of the well that leads to 
Arthur’s chamber. The shaft is narrow and thirty feet deep,

210
but with a little attention a person can descend it. Climbing 
down the pit can alert Arthur (see the Cave of The God 
Under the Mine location on page 210).
Edward the Miners’ Chief
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a long-time friend of Arthur and mourn 
his death. Everyone in the mine does what I say, and I hate 
foreigners who want to poke their nose into my work. I allow 
Frank into the mine often to visit the place where Arthur died.
The Cave of the 
God Under the 
Mine
Descriptor: A large natural cave, which Arthur has made 
into a home. You can enter only by descending from a narrow 
well. In the north wall, the enormous pig head of the God 
gasps and groans as it waits to wake up.
Gift - Arthur’s House: The mastermind behind the Pig 
Heads worked hard to make the Cave an intruder-proof 
haven. If someone drops from the well, Arthur will be 
alerted (a Position Check can avoid this). At the bottom of 
the well is a large tub that Arthur can fill with boiling water. 
Whoever arrives through the well suffers 1 Wound if they 
have no way of avoiding the hot water. Inside the room, 
Arthur can turn the lights on and off thanks to a system 
he has invented, throwing the chamber into either light or 
darkness. While intruders wander blindly, Arthur can use 
his knowledge of the place to move freely.

211
Arthur Browne the Big 
Brother
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the eldest of the Pig Heads and act as 
the mastermind of the gang. Living in my natural prison has 
taught me how to see even in total darkness.
Agenda: Take revenge on Bardolf for corrupting everything 
I built in Whitehaven and regain control of the city / 
Transforming all the inhabitants of Whitehaven into Pig 
Heads.
Gift - The Flesh of The God Under the Mine:  Arthur 
can tear pieces of flesh from the God’s head to harness its 
powers. When he spends an action, the piece becomes 
a Spawn of the God and begins to grow disgustingly, 
engaging all nearby enemies. Arthur can tear one piece of 
meat off per action, up to a maximum of 3.
Gift - Gang Leader:  Eating the flesh of The God Under 
the Mine gives the Browne brothers the ability to transform 
into monstrous beings. Once per Scene, the NPC can 
mutate, gaining the following benefits: 1. Recover a Wound 
once they reach 0. Though apparently dead, the NPC will 
revive within minutes of falling; 2. Once per Scene, act 
a second time during the Exchange, even consecutively;  
3. Once per Scene, increase the Opposition Level by 2 for 
a single action where they can harness their superhuman 
strength.
The God Under 
the Mine
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a creature as old as the world, trapped 
beneath the bedrock. Millennia of inactivity have made me 
catatonic and there is nothing that can really wake me up. 
Only pain can stir me even slightly.
Gift - The Head of the God:  Only the enormous head of 
the God emerges from the rock, and pieces of meat can be 
obtained from it. When he suffers 1 Wound, the God emits 
a loud grunt that sends everyone flying (a Defense Check 
can avoid this). As an additional effect of the grunt, the 
God’s fetid breath taints the air, granting 1 Penalty to the 
next action of all affected subjects, excluding Arthur. When 
reduced to 0 Wounds, the God falls into a deep sleep to 
fully regenerate in the following Scene.
Spawn of The God Under the 
Mine
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: An abomination of flesh and tentacles, obeying 
Arthur’s will.
Gift – Flesh Abomination:  A Spawn can perform its 
action with a flesh tentacle up to a range of 6 feet, and at the 
end of each Exchange it will grow as indicated in the table. 
If not engaged in a conflict, the Spawn will grow 1 step per

212
Scene. When the Spawn acts, roll 1 die: from 1 to 5 it will 
rage against one of Arthur’s enemies chosen at random. On 
a 6, it will act against an ally or Arthur himself. If the Spawn 
has more actions, roll the die for each single action.
Step Wounds Actions OL Special
1 +1 / +1 Range of 12 feet.
2 / +1 +1 If a target fails a Defense Check, in addition to suffering 1 Wound it will 
be dragged towards the Spawn or thrown away.
3 +1 / +1 Mass growth to a size of 18 cubic feet.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the supply of infected meat is not stopped:  after 
two days from the arrival of the Hunters the Pig Heads will 
number over one hundred and Arthur will give the order 
to attack the city.
If the Hunters discover that Arthur is still alive:  
Bardolf will give full access to the mine and its maps. With 
them, it will be possible to calculate the exact location of 
The God Under the Mine.
If the Hunters make their way to the mine and 
Clarence or Frank know about it:   Frank will set up 
a defense with a dozen Pig Heads, and Arthur will help his 
brother, passing him pieces of meat to create several Spawn 
of the God.
After Arthur creates the third Spawn in the same 
Scene: The God Under the Mine goes mad with pain 
and tries to move, causing a new earthquake. The mine 
collapses, overwhelming everyone inside, though the 
Hunters may find a quick way to escape.
If the Hunters talk to Bardolf about the three 
brothers and express any doubts about them:  the 
shrewd moneymaker will ask for time, and after 1 Scene he 
will bring to the Hunters either Emma, Edward, or Martha.
If the Hunters commit to more than one Scenes 
outside the city:  Clarence will attempt to assassinate 
Bardolf in his palace, but will fail and end up on the run, 
chased by the guards after being injured. Bardolf will

1
213
call the Hunters in to report, describing a Pig Head who 
was acting alone and who is now wounded and therefore 
recognizable.
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the The City 
of Pigs Scenario and can be acquired once the requirements 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if 
they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous 
one.
The Meat of The God Under the Mine
Requirement: Obtain a piece of the flesh of the God at the 
slaughterhouse or defeat a Spawn.
Y ou can consume the flesh of the God to temporarily gain 
power. Once per Scene, you can heal 1 Wound and gain 
1 Additional Success for all physical actions you take. Y ou 
have to wait an entire Scene for the remaining flesh to 
regenerate. If you use this Gift in 2 consecutive Scenes, the 
flesh is consumed and you won’t be able to use it anymore.
The Brand of Greed
Requirement: To gain the trust and respect of Bardolf 
Greed.
Bardolf trusts only those who demonstrate cunning and 
ability, granting such people a special pass called The Brand 
of Greed. With the Brand you get free access to any place in 
the city, without the person in charge being able to object to 
your entrance. Get 1 Additional Success when you exploit 
the Mark’s importance against a city dweller.
There Is No House That Can Stop 
Me (Special Gift for Garou the Old 
Wolf)
Requirement: Clash with one of the brothers who has 
made use of the Leader of the Gang of Pigs Gift.
Those damned pigs get on your nerves and you can’t stand 
them running away from your wrath. Y ou get 2 Additional 
Successes when you take action to destroy or overcome an 
environmental obstacle that stands between you and one 
of the three Pigs at the head of the gang. Y ou hate to lose 
to them. If you have to leave or flee from a place without 
eliminating them, you lose 1 Soma.
The Scenario The 
City of Pigs in 
Scenes
FIRST SCENE –  The interview 
with Bardolf Greed
It’s the same one you find on page 204.
End of Scene:  Bardolf asks the Hunters to gather 
information in the city and its factories, go to the SECOND 
SCENE - Visit to the city.

2
3a
3b
214
SECOND SCENE – Visit to the city
Read aloud to the Hunters: Whitehaven is the ultimate 
representation of what intellectuals in drawing rooms call 
the “industrial revolution. ” The small brick houses fade 
in the presence of the large factories which, in a few years, 
have completely changed the appearance of what was once 
little more than a peasant village. Busy workers run like 
industrious ants,    among them old people and children. 
Whitehaven no longer has anything to offer to those unable 
to work.
Place: The alleys of the city of Whitehaven, amongst 
workers and people who live for the day.
Time: It is late afternoon, and a faint rain begins to fall. In 
just over an hour, night will fall.
Information: Between the back alleys of the city and 
the factory workers, the Hunters receive all 3 pieces of 
information found at the factories location (page 206). 
After gathering these, it will be dark, and a group of Pig 
Heads equal to twice the number of Hunters will ambush 
them. Once the fight is resolved, the Hunters will see that 
one of the attackers is wearing a butcher’s coat and another 
is dressed as a beggar.
End of Scene:  The Hunters can decide to investigate 
the slaughterhouse (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - The 
Slaughterhouse) or the orchard (THIRD SCENE OPTION 
B - The Orchard).
THIRD SCENE OPTION A – 
The Slaughterhouse
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The city’s slaughterhouse is 
perhaps one of the largest buildings the Hunters have ever 
seen, and from the smell of blood that saturates the air it 
seems that it works at full capacity day and night. A rusty, old 
gate is all that separates you from the main door, but the bars 
on the windows and the many “No Trespassing” signs suggest 
that it is not a place where outsiders are welcome.
Place: The great slaughterhouse of Whitehaven.
Time: It is now night even if, from the lights in the 
windows, the slaughterhouse does not seem to have ceased 
its activities.
Information: The Hunters explore the place following 
the list in ascending order (page 208). Once they arrive at 
the salting room, they will find Frank intent on mixing the 
flesh of the God with the meat for the miners’ meals. Frank 
will fight to the death. Having knocked out the middle 
brother, and upon searching the room, the Hunters will 
find the flesh of the God and a map representing a specific 
area of the mine. 
End of Scene: Hunters can also go to the orchard (THIRD 
SCENE OPTION B - The Orchard ) or go to the mine, 
where it is clear that something is happening to the miners 
(FOURTH SCENE - The Mine).
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – 
The Orchard
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You walk for about ten 
minutes before you reach a large apple orchard, clearly 
abandoned. Old wooden sheds and rusty tools left out in the 
rain confirm your suspicions. Among the shadows you can 
see the silhouettes of the beggars and other poor people who 
rest in the only place in the city where money is not required 
to live.

4
5
215
Place: The orchard, a few minutes’ walk from Whitehaven.
Time: It is night and has stopped raining. A timid full 
moon appears among the open clouds.
Information: Clarence will approach the Hunters 
trying to figure out who they are and, once he realizes 
that they pose a danger to the gang, will seek to mislead 
them. He will attempt to highlight Greed’s shady business 
and that his loyal lawyer William has all the evidence of 
the industrialist’s illicit trafficking. If cornered, Clarence 
will fight with the support of the Pig Heads, but not to the 
death. Clarence will reveal everything that happens at the 
mine if that can save his life. In his shack, it is possible to 
find a map of the mine with a mark on a specific point.
End of Scene: Hunters can also go to the slaughterhouse 
(THIRD SCENE OPTION A - The Slaughterhouse) or go to 
the mine, where it is clear that something is happening to 
the miners (FOURTH SCENE - The Mine).
FOURTH SCENE – The Mine
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you arrive at the 
mine, you avoid the main entrance and, following the map, 
you come to a played-out vein of coal. Following the railroad 
tracks, now in disuse, you enter the darkness.
Place: The abandoned area of the Whitehaven Mine.
Time: It is night and the mine is completely dark.
Information: The Hunters will find Frank or Clarence 
(determined by which location they haven’t visited yet) 
heading for a meeting with Arthur. If both brothers have 
been eliminated, old Martha will be the one to visit her son. 
If not discovered, the Hunters will see the family member 
receive a piece of meat by pulling it up from the well. If the 
Hunters somehow reveal themselves, the 
threatened family member will activate 
the power of the God’s flesh to create a 
Spawn.
End of Scene:  The Hunters have 
discovered Arthur’s hideout, go to the 
FIFTH SCENE - The God Under the 
Mine.
FIFTH SCENE – The 
God Under the Mine
Read aloud to the Hunters:  From the 
narrow well you see a light and hear a man 
talking to himself. A long, low, prolonged moan 
seems to come from who-knows-what strange 
creature.
Place: The cave where Arthur is trapped.
Time: The time of reckoning.
End of Scene:  At this point, it will be up to the 
Hunters to decide which path to take, but it will soon 
become clear to them that Arthur is now insane and 
cannot control his obsession with Bardolf Greed’s ruin. 
If the Hunters waste too much time Frank, Clarence, or 
other Pig Heads could arrive, depending on how much of 
a challenge you want.

216
Saint George 
the Dragon Slayer
A special Scenario inspired by St. George 
and the Dragon
Safety Keywords: Religious zeal / Persecution

217
Once upon a 
time…
There was a brave and devoted fighter who 
made the fight against monsters his reason 
for living. Saint George is perhaps the most 
important and powerful member of the 
Order, but his zeal in punishing the Broken 
Tales can be a threat to the Hunters too... 
Perhaps the real enemy does not hide in the 
shadows, but walks beside them...
The story that led us here…
Saint George the Dragon Slayer is a special Scenario 
that can be used during any Campaign or for a short game. 
In fact, once Saint George has been included as a support to 
the Hunters, he will have several methods of foiling plans, 
whether laid by friends or foes. Saint George will fight 
alongside the Hunters, but his stern gaze and inquisitorial 
spirit must be kept at bay so that the Saint does not unleash 
the fury of the Church against its own agents.
How to use Saint George
Saint George will join the group of Hunters as soon as they 
have solved a few Scenarios and earned a bit of fame. As an 
Ally Dark Presence, Saint George will support the Hunters 
on their travels. However, it will soon become clear that 
his zeal for everything Broken Tales-related will make him 
difficult to manage. 
Saint George possesses a Gift called The Judgment of 
the Saint. When enough Suspicion Tokens have been 
accumulated, the Saint will become an Opponent Dark 
Presence and will try to arrest the Hunters as he sees them 
as representing the darkness that the Church fights.
Saint George  
(Ally Dark Presence)
Once upon a time there was a cheerful child named George 
who often wandered away from home to run and play in the 
woods. One day, George fell into the river and in the blink 
of an eye, he was drowning. On the verge of death, the child 
felt an incredible force pushing him to the surface and soon 
found himself on the bank of the river, soaked to the bones 
but still alive. His savior was a strange, lizard-like creature 
with incredibly human eyes. A Dragon! thought the child, 
throwing himself on the creature’s neck, and a deep friendship 
was born between the two. In the following years, George 
took every opportunity to meet his secret friend and learn 
from him the wonders of magic, and what is hidden from 
the eyes of ordinary people. Then, a plague struck the village 
where George lived, killing his parents. The lad’s appeals for 
the Dragon’s help fell on deaf ears, despite the creature being

218
capable of healing any disease. The Dragon, out of fear of being discovered 
and killed, refused to heal the people of the village. Twenty years passed 
and George took his vows, becoming a Holy fighter in the service of the 
Papacy. He led more than one battle against the forces of evil. When the 
Saint returned to his old village, he walked into the forest in search of 
his old friend. The Dragon was happy to see George again after such 
a long time and came out... only to realize that he had come before his 
executioner.
OL Medium 5 – (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a Holy knight in the service of the Church. I am skilled 
at hunting and defeating any monster. Nothing escapes my inquisitive gaze.
Agenda: Find every creature linked to the Broken Tales and exterminate 
them so that they can do no harm / Truly understand the nature of the Broken 
Tales.
Gift - The Holy Dragon Slayer:  Saint George starts the Scenario with 1 Fervor 
Token. When facing an enemy that is visibly monstrous, he can spend 1 Fervor Token 
to allow an allied Hunter to inflict 1 additional Wound. When such an enemy unveils one 
of their Gifts, Saint George can spend 1 Fervor Token to grant 2 Additional Successes on 
a Defense Check to any allied Hunter. Whenever he suffers 1 Wound, Saint George 
receives 1 Fervor Token.
Gift - The Judgment of the Saint:  Saint George keeps everyone under 
observation, even the Hunters he accompanies. During a Scene, if a Hunter 
activates their Dark Ego or uses any Gift that allows them to employ 
magic or terrorize ordinary people in the presence of Saint George, add 
1 Suspicion Token on this Gift. During a Scene, Saint George can keep a 
Hunter under observation, making them uncomfortable. When 
he does, the Storyteller may spend 1 Suspicion Token to 
impose 1 Penalty to a Hunter Check. Suspicion Tokens 
carry over from one Scenario to another. When there 
are Suspicion Tokens equal to twice the number of 
Hunters, Saint George engages in a Witch Hunt 
and becomes an Opponent Dark Presence.

219
How to manage Saint George
Once introduced in the Campaign or in the single Scenario, 
Saint George will be a recurring presence to whom the 
Hunters must be accountable. As the highest representative 
of the Papacy, the Saint has full authority over all members 
of the Order.
For the Hunters, it will be clear that Saint George could be 
a good ally, but that under his stern gaze not everything is 
permitted.
 ◆ The Storyteller can add Saint George to the party 
throughout the Scenario. Even if he has the authority 
to make all the decisions, it wouldn’t be fun to play the 
Session with an NPC leading the Hunters by a leash. The 
Saint will therefore remain in the background, curious 
to observe the Hunters and their work as members of 
the Order. The Storyteller must immediately make it 
clear that the Saint is at their disposal with his Gifts, 
but that The Judgment of the Saint can quickly turn 
him into an enemy.
 ◆ The Storyteller can add Saint George to the party, 
but the Saint will act independently and appear 
only in some Scenes. In this way, the Saint will apply 
more pressure on the Hunters, judging behaviors that 
he considers unbecoming for a member of the Order. 
He will always give support with his Gifts, but the 
Storyteller will be able to use him more as a nuisance 
than anything else.
 ◆ The Storyteller will only let Saint George step in 
when it is clear that a monstrous creature is featured 
in the Scenario. For example, in Red-Hood Iskra he 
could come after the appearance of Greskar (page 
125) or due to the presence of The Ancient Enemy 
as a Dark Presence (page 49). Saint George will 
arrive driven by a premonition that there is a monster 
to hunt down and smite. The Saint will be open and 
supportive towards the Hunters, at least until he has 
accumulated the first Suspicion Token. This is a good 
way to introduce Saint George to a Campaign for the 
first time.
 ◆ Once the Suspicion Tokens accrued are twice the 
number of Hunters, Saint George will launch a 
Witch Hunt, and will become an Opponent Dark 
Presence with the corresponding profile. From that 
moment on, like any Opponent, the Storyteller will be 
able to use him to impede the Hunters and to create a 
special Scenario leading to the final confrontation with 
Saint George.
Saint George 
(Opponent Dark Presence)
The Holy Fighter who eradicates darkness in all its forms. 
There are skilled Paladins in his service, ready to carry out 
his every command.
OL Medium 5 – (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a devout knight in the service of 
the Church. I am adept at hunting and capturing any 
manifestation of darkness. I know every Hunter in the Order 
and I know how to defeat them.
Agenda: Find the Hunters who have shown to be linked to 
the Broken Tales and lock them up in the Church’s prisons. 
Whoever opposes my sentence will perish, suffering the 
judgment of God himself.

220
Gift - Witch Hunt:  When he becomes an Opponent 
Dark Presence, Saint George acquires a number of Fervor 
Tokens equal to the number of Hunters. When facing a 
Hunter he’s been tracking down, he can spend 1 Fervor 
Token to inflict 1 additional Wound on top of the normal 
one, or just 1 Wound if the Hunter succeeds on their 
Defense Check. The only way to avoid this Wound is to 
have an Outcome with Increment and spend it to nullify 
the Gift’s effect. Saint George can spend 1 Fervor Token to 
transform any Position Check’s result for an action against 
him into a Failure so long as the Hunter performing it has 
at least one Descriptor related to magic or a monstrous 
characteristic.    Saint George regenerates Fervor Tokens 
equal to the number of Hunters at the beginning of each 
Scenario.
Gift - The Judgment of the Saint: Saint George keeps 
everyone under observation, even when hunting down 
Hunters. During a Scene, if a Hunter activates their Dark 
Ego to perform a good deed or uses any Gift that allows 
them to use magic or manipulate ordinary people to do 
a good deed in the presence of Saint George, he adds 1 
Judgment Token on this Gift. Judgment Tokens carry over 
from one Scenario to the next. When a number of Judgment 
Tokens equal to the number of Hunters are staked on this 
Gift, Saint George will make amends, declaring that he was 
wrong in his judgment, putting an end to the Witch Hunt.
Paladins of the Church
A group of faithful Paladins in the service of Saint George.
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a Paladin in the service of the great Saint 
George himself and his word, for me, is law. Years of training 
have made me adept at fighting the Broken Tales.
Note: Each Paladin has a different Downside, indicated in 
the Paladin Gift.
Gift - Paladins: Each Paladin has a name and a dedicated 
Gift, and a Downside to add to the standard Descriptor. 
The Storyteller rolls 1 die to decide which Paladin follows 
Saint George in the Witch Hunt. Saint Geoge has a number 
of Paladins equal to that of the Hunters he hunts.

221
Roll First Name Downside Paladin Gift
1
Trebizond, the 
princess of a 
Distant Country.
Sometimes, I am seized 
by an unshakeable 
curiosity about new 
things.
From a Far Country: Trebizond can make use of weapons and exotic 
objects with which to surprise opponents. Once per Scene, she can create a 
Descriptor that represents a special belonging, such as Magic-Finding Dust or 
A Whip as Sharp as a Blade. When using her special equipment, Trebizond 
increases her Opposition Level by 1.
2 Saint Demetrius
I put the defense of my 
companions before my 
own life.
Guardian: Once per Exchange, Demetrius can take 1 Wound in place of one 
of his allies. When he does, he increases his Opposition Level by 1 for the rest 
of the Scene.
3 Saint Theodore
The ecstasy of battle is 
for me the sweetest of 
pleasures.
Furious: Theodore always inflicts 1 Additional Wound when raging against 
an enemy. He still inflicts 1 Wound even if the Defense Check is a successful 
Outcome. The only way to avoid the effect of this Gift is to spend an 
Increment to cancel the Wound.
4 Saint Martha
I do not think that the 
Broken Tales should 
be eradicated, but only 
understood.
Friendly: Any Hunter who acts against Martha when there are other Paladins 
as potential targets will receive 2 Penalties on their action.
5 Saint Syrus
I am the oldest of the 
Paladins and I only trust 
my own judgment.
Veteran: Once per Scene, Syrus can negate the effect of a Gift activated by a 
Hunter. The Saint will find a method to counter Gifts, drawn from his great 
experience.
6 Saint Margaret
I want to affirm my 
role in the Church and 
overthrow that arrogant 
Saint George.
Ambition: Margaret increases her Opposition Level by 1 for each Wound 
that Saint George has suffered. Once per Scene, when Saint George suffers 1 
Wound, Margaret can immediately perform 1 additional action.
Saint George in a Broken 
Tales Campaign
By his very recursive nature, Saint George is a great addition 
to a Broken Tales Campaign. In the list of clues on page 246 
there are also indications to link him to the story of the Child 
Savior and of the One Who Plots in the Shadows. As written 
in his Agenda, Saint George is torn between his duty to the 
Church and the memory of when, as a child, he formed a 
sincere bond with The Ancient Enemy.

222
OZena  
the Suffering and Desperate
A Scenario inspired by 
the Wonderful Wizard of OZ (Lyman Frank Baum)
Part One – The Yellow Village
Safety Keywords: Disfiguring Illness / Mental Illness / Deformity / Plagiarism

223
OZena the Suffering and Desperate is a special Scenario 
divided into three parts, which when played consecutively 
completes the story of the unfortunate Dorothea. All 
Scenarios are built as a Scene Tree (see page 89) and 
are great for introductory Sessions or a mini-Campaign. 
A Storyteller who prefers an emerging storytelling style 
will still find Events and information useful for managing 
a freer playstyle for the Hunters.
Once upon a 
time…
Freiburg, West of the great Black Forest, 
Germany. The regent of the city, Nils Schulz, 
has invited the Order to investigate the 
activities of Margareta Westen, a renowned 
“witch” who is said to carry out strange 
experiments in a small village nearby. 
Although there is no apparent evidence of 
any heresy, the village of Gelb is shunned 
by all. Its inhabitants are deemed “strange” 
and it has, over the years, welcomed all 
manner of freaks and sick people looking 
for a safe place to live. Thus, the Hunters 
must go to Gelb and find out if Margareta 
is really the terrible witch that Nils fears.
The story that led us here…
Dorothea is a young American girl from the 
1900s who suffers from a very acute form of ozena. Under 
the care of the charlatan “Dr. ” Norman Henkle, she took 
part in a strange magical rite, and both were catapulted 
into the Black Forest of 1780. Here, under the guidance 
of Henkle, Dorothea took the name of OZena (a name 
that has not yet been made official for her disease). She’s 
hunting witches to procure ingredients and potions for 
her master, with the doctor promising to find a cure for 
the disease. Dorothea brought the Doctor other lost, 
ill people found along the way: Lowe, a scoundrel with 
Leontiasis; Dose, a thug suffering from leprosy who 
covers his skin with metal; and Hirnloser, a mental 
patient who believes he has no brain. 
After eliminating the witch Johanne in Schiltach (east of 
the Black Forest) from which she got her magic shoes, 
Dorothea arrived in Gelb with her companions. Here, 
pursuing the promise of her cure, she sent Lowe and 
Hirnloser to the village as spies to observe Margareta’s 
activities. The old witch is actually both learned and 
benevolent. She uses her magical knowledge to provide a 
home and support to many sick or deformed people who 
would otherwise have no place to live. 
Note: Nasal ozena (also known as chronic atrophic 
rhinitis) is a particular form of chronic rhinitis, 
characterized specifically by a marked and widespread 
atrophy of the nasal mucosa, so that the structure of 
the nose wastes away. The disease disfigures the face 
in a permanent way, affecting the appearance of those 
affected with devastating effects.

1
224
The story at the arrival of the Hunters
Margareta Westen is a capable and experienced witch, 
who served at many noble courts as a fortune teller and 
counselor from a young age. Affected by a very serious form 
of deforming osteitis, which has ruined her legs and arms, 
Margareta has experienced the unreasoning prejudice of 
society herself. She was marginalized and abandoned by 
everyone. The witch has thus decided to found a small 
community, where all those like her would be welcome. In 
Gelb, Margareta cares for those unfortunate enough not to 
have a place in society, and most of the villagers would give 
their lives for her. 
Margareta is part of a coven of witches called the Compass 
Rose. Johanne, the witch of Schiltach (east of the Black 
Forest), was also part of the same coven. The death of her 
companion has put Margareta on alert, aware that it must 
have been someone who knew the secrets of the coven 
who killed Johanne. Dorothea has long since crept into 
Gelb, hiding at the edge of the small village. Hirnloser has 
instead openly joined the community, though everyone is 
wary of his mental illness. Lowe serves as eyes and arms for 
Dorothea around town, while Dose, hidden in the swamp 
to the east, awaits a signal from Dorothea to spring into 
action.
FIRST SCENE – Approaching 
the village of Gelb
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Before your eyes you see 
the great Black Forest in all its splendor, a refuge for animals 
and supernatural beings from the slow expansion of civilized 
life. A wide beaten path leads through the trees. Near a sign 
with directions to the village of Gelb, you see two motionless 
figures, as if they’re awaiting your arrival. One is a man, old 
and hunched over by who-knows-what disease, while the 
other is a child with a disfigured face. The two hold hands 
like father and son. The man nods to you and you understand 
that he is a villager sent to guide you.
Place: The road that winds its way from the west into the 
great Black Forest.
Time: It is early in the morning. A cart has brought the 
Hunters from Freiburg to the edge of the forest and the 
driver is now anxious to leave.
Initial Situation:  Henrich and Klaus approach the 
Hunters and introduce themselves as emissaries on 
Margareta’s behalf. The witch was formally notified by 
Nils that he was sending some of his agents to check on 
Gelb’s activities, but the regent took great care not to reveal 
the true nature of the Hunters. Henrich offers to lead the 
Hunters along the road to the village.
Information: Walking towards Gelb with Henrich and 
Klaus you can get the following clues and information.
 ◆ The village of Gelb is a safe place for anyone with a 
disease or a deformity that makes them an outcast in 
usual society. Margareta welcomes anyone without 
asking questions about their past and with the aid of her 
potions, she soothes the suffering of all the inhabitants. 
Although Henrich is a poorly educated farmer, he will

225
do nothing but praise Margareta’s goodness throughout 
the journey with sincere conviction.
 ◆ Little Klaus is affected by Ozena and his nose is nothing 
more than a clump of rotten meat. Despite the disease, 
he has the lively and curious eyes of a child, and he will 
ask the Hunters a thousand questions.
 ◆ The road the group is traveling on was created to 
facilitate commercial trips to Gelb. However, Henrich 
will regretfully inform the Hunters that once merchants 
arrive in the village, they are so frightened by the 
sight of the inhabitants, they hurriedly leave without 
conducting any business. 
 ◆ Halfway to the village, the Hunters will realize that 
someone is following them. It’s Lowe, who hides among 
the foliage and tries to figure out who the newcomers 
are.
End of Scene:  The Hunters arrive at the village of Gelb. 
Go to SECOND SCENE - The Village of Gelb.
Henrich the Father
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am one of the many people Margareta 
saved from the miserable life of an outcast. My son Klaus is 
everything to me, which is why I don’t want the village of 
Gelb to be suspected of witchcraft.
Klaus, the Ozena-
Afflicted Son
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a curious child. I 
love foreigners because they always have 
interesting stories to tell. I know I have a 
dreadful disease, but I have learned to live 
with it.
Lowe the Coward 
Henchman
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC  
(2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I’m an eternal coward 
who has been given a monstrous aspect 
by fate. I can adapt to any situation 
and am quick to jump to the winning 
side.
Agenda: To help Dorothea in her 
mission, with the hope of recovering 
from my condition / Save my life, no 
matter who I have to betray.

2
226
SECOND SCENE – The Village 
of Gelb
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you arrive at the 
entrance to the village, the spectacle that appears before you 
is incredible. People with all kinds of diseases and deformities 
walk in the streets, occupied performing a variety of tasks. In 
reality, what you see is nothing more than an ordinary village 
in Bavaria, inhabited solely by people who, in any other place, 
would be considered monsters.
Place: The small village of Gelb.
Time: It is morning, and all the inhabitants are busy with 
their work activities.
Information: Henrich will tell the Hunters that Margareta 
will soon arrive in the town square to dispense her medicines. 
While waiting for the witch, several things will happen.
 ◆ Hirnloser will appear, dancing and chanting of distant 
worlds that overlap each other. Adults look at him with 
pity, children walk around him amused. If the Hunters 
get close, he will try to interact with them. If the Hunters 
ignore him, he will be the one to approach. Whatever 
happens, when Margareta arrives in the square, 
Hirnloser will leave.
 ◆ When Margareta arrives all the inhabitants will stop 
working to run to her. The witch dispenses strange vials 
among happy and grateful faces. After the distribution 
of the medicines, Henrich will lead the Hunters to 
Margareta.
 ◆ In the crowd you can spot Lowe, who gazes at the potions 
with dreamy eyes.
 ◆ Dorothea is in the crowd too, her face covered with 
a heavy green hood, the smell of her illness clearly 
distinguishable. The girl will disappear using her shoes 
at the slightest hint of a problem.
 ◆ Margareta will speak to the Hunters, saying she fears for 
her life. A week ago, she received news of the murder 
of the witch Johanne who lived in Schiltach, in the 
eastern part of the Black Forest. Margareta is convinced 
that someone is trying to eliminate all 4 witches of the 
Black Forest, each of which holds part of a powerful 
artifact. She believes someone is gathering the pieces to 
reassemble the artifact, no matter the cost.
 ◆ The conversation will be interrupted by Lowe who 
screams desperately: Hirnloser has kidnapped Klaus 
and is taking him to the great swamp near the village! 
Margareta will ask the Hunters to save the child. In 
exchange, she will reveal to the Hunters what she knows 
of the artifact. In the woman’s eyes, the Hunters will see 
only a sincere concern for the fate of little Klaus.
Margareta the Good Witch
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a capable witch who has devoted her life to 
caring for the unfortunate. My body is wracked with disease, 
but that doesn’t make me any less fearsome.
Agenda: Keep Gelb and everyone who lives there safe.
Gift - Witch:  Margareta can draw on her witch power 
to create a Descriptor to represent the effect of her magic. 
Her abilities are related to manipulating emotions, such as 
instilling fear or bestowing calm. The Descriptor thus created 
remains for the entire duration of a Scene. Alternatively, 
Margareta can move objects with the power of thought. These 
abilities can be used to hit someone from a distance, knock 
objects out of someone’s hand, and generally manipulate the 
environment.

3
227
Hirnloser the Scarecrow
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: Everyone thinks I’m crazy and maybe they’re 
right. I don’t feel any fear and I can see beneath the surface of 
those who pretend they are normal.
Agenda: I follow OZena because I can see past her pain. 
There is another world, the one where she comes from and 
where I want to go too.
End of Scene:  Margareta asks the Hunters to save little 
Klaus, offering to reveal all her secrets in exchange for the 
little one’s safety. If the Hunters set out to save Klaus go 
to SCENE THREE - In the Swamp . If, on the other hand, 
they decide to stay in the village or split up, for those who 
remain, go to the FOURTH SCENE - OZena’s Attack.
THIRD SCENE – In the Swamp
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You don’t have to go too 
far into the swamp before you hear little Klaus scream. You 
run to a small clearing where Hirnloser puts a sickle to the 
child’s throat. He is near an old, abandoned shed from which 
suddenly emerges a huge figure covered in grotesque armor 
made of debris. Hirnloser stares at you, his eyes pools of pure 
madness. “ And while we dance here in our little puppet show, 
the real performance unfolds in the village, ” the madman 
cries and smiles maliciously, as you realize that you have 
been lured into a trap.
Place: A clearing in the swamp twenty minutes’ walk from 
Gelb.
Time: It is late morning and the sun filters through the 
trees.
Information: Hirnloser and Dose want to engage the 
Hunters to give Dorothea time to kill Margareta. 
 ◆ Hirnloser is insane but he doesn’t really want to kill 
Klaus. It will soon become clear that he uses the child 
as a shield and lever to keep the Hunters busy.
 ◆ If Lowe is present, he will try to surprise the Hunters 
from behind but will flee at the slightest sign of danger.
Dose The Tin Man
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: Leprosy has destroyed my appearance, but 
my body is still that of the Mountain, the name by which 
I performed as a street artist in shows of strength and 
endurance. Under my heavy armor I fear nothing.
Agenda: I am in love with OZena, disfigured by the disease 
just like me. I am aware that she will never return my feelings 
and that is why the only thing I can do is serve her, even to 
my death.
Gift - The Tin Man: Dose is completely covered in thick 
armor made of metal and other salvaged materials, such as 
a large stove and the hull of a boat. The only unarmored spot 
is a small door in the stove that was once used to remove 
embers, which is located above his heart. It is not possible 
to hurt Dose unless one first finds a way to penetrate his 
armor or uses an attack that can clearly overcome his 
defenses.

4
228
Gift - Giant:  Dose stands nearly seven feet tall and 
possesses extraordinary strength. The disease consumed 
his nerves making him immune to pain. Once per Scene he 
can inflict 1 additional Wound with one hit of his mighty 
ax.
End of Scene: After the fight, Klaus will tell the Hunters 
that the real leader of the scoundrels is a girl with a green 
cape they called OZena. She told her accomplices to buy 
her time, while she would take care of the witch. Klaus did 
not see the girl’s face, but by the smell he recognized his 
own illness. Go to FOURTH SCENE - OZena’s Attack.
FOURTH SCENE – OZena’s 
Attack
This Scene concludes the first part of the Scenario 
and will change based on whether the Hunters 
choose to leave someone in the village while they 
go to rescue little Klaus. Dorothea will attempt 
to kill Margareta in her house. If no Hunter 
is present, the witch will succumb to 
the blows of the young assassin. If 
any or all the Hunters are present, 
Dorothea will flee as soon as it 
becomes clear that she is at a 
distinct disadvantage.
Place: Margareta’s house. 
A large circular house built 
around a centuries-old oak.
Time: It is late morning and the village of Gelb has resumed 
its activity. Many inhabitants remain waiting tensely in the 
Town Square to discover the fate of little Klaus.
Information:
 ◆ During the fight, Dorothea’s hood will slide back and 
reveal her face. She is a young blonde girl, eyes of an 
intense blue and a suppurating chasm in place of her 
nose. 
 ◆ When Dorothea escapes and disappears, it will be clear 
to the Hunters that her slippers are magical.
 ◆ After the battle, if still alive, Margareta will reveal 
details on the Compass Rose. If she was killed by 
Dorothea, the Hunters will be able to obtain some of 
the information by examining the witch’s house.
OZena (Dorothea)
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young girl who wants to return to 
her world. The disease that afflicts me has ruined my face 
and with it all the hopes of a normal life. I am willing to do 
anything to heal, which is why I became a witch killer.
Agenda: Kill every witch Henkle points out to me and 
retrieve all four pieces of the Compass Rose.
Gift - The Silver Slippers:  The wearer of this magical 
artifact can move through folds in space, disappearing and 
reappearing in a flash. Dorothea can move freely up to a 
maximum distance of thirty feet without anyone preventing 
her, but she must be able to see the point of arrival. Once 
per Scene, Dorothea can take a longer journey and reappear 
in any place she has seen at least once.

229
Epilogue
The Hunters will discover that OZena’s goal is the Compass 
Rose, a powerful artifact capable of summoning storms that 
are said to be able to throw people and objects into other 
worlds. The four Black Forest witches - Johanne (East), 
Margareta (West), Helga (South) and Karina (North) - split 
the artifact into four parts so that no one could abuse its 
power. Karina possessed the first piece of the Rose. Johanne 
had the second, as well as being famous for her magic 
slippers. For this reason, it is quite clear that OZena has 
already attacked and killed the first two witches. The next 
target will then be Helga, who lives in the village of Rot 
south of the Forest.
If Margareta is still alive, she will reveal some additional 
information. Helga is the cruelest and most powerful of the 
coven. Her magic is based on blood and, for this reason, 
the Hunters will have to be careful not to be injured in her 
presence. There is a potion that can break Helga’s blood 
curse, but Margareta doesn’t know its ingredients, apart 
from knowing it contains Burdock.
Note to the Storyteller:  OZena’s story continues with 
the Second Part, The Red Village. Keep track of who among 
Hirnloser, Dose and Lowe survived the Scenario. They will 
return in the third part, The Emerald Village.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
When the Hunters are in conversation with 
Margareta: Hirnloser will take the opportunity to 
kidnap Klaus and take him to the nearby swamp, creating a 
diversion to get the Hunters away from the village.
If the Hunters head to the swamps to save Klaus:  
Lowe will offer to “guide them” to try to waste more time 
and give Dorothea more leeway to kill Margareta.
If someone has wasted time interacting and 
getting to know Hirnloser in the village:  when the 
madman has Klaus with him, he will try to use the child 
as a lever to talk to his new friend again, forgetting about 
the plan.
If Dorothea is cornered:  she will flee thanks to her 
silver slippers. Dorothea cannot afford to risk being 
captured. This event occurs as soon as the situation turns 
in favor of the Hunters.
If Dorothea manages to kill Margareta:  she 
will recover the piece of the Compass Rose and then 
immediately escape thanks to her magical shoes.

230
Part Two – The 
Red Village
Safety Keywords: Rituals / Nudity / Blood / Body 
horror
Once upon a 
time…
Rot, South of the great Black Forest, Germany. 
After investigating Margareta Westen’s activities 
and discovering the secret of Gelb’s village, the 
Hunters are now aware of the threat from OZena, 
a young witch killer who is trying to rebuild a 
powerful magical artifact called the Compass Rose. 
As described by Margareta herself, whoever owns 
the Rose can invoke a storm so powerful, it can 
throw those who are swept up into other worlds and 
dimensions. OZena’s next target is Helga, a powerful 
witch who lives south of the great forest. Helga 
founded the cult of the Crimson Kiss, surrounded 
by faithful and devoted servants. The village of Rot, 
where Helga has made her home, is shunned even 
by state officials, who fear they won’t be able to 
leave that cursed place. But before going to Rot, the 
Hunters received a special invitation...
Principality of Transylvania, fortified church of 
Valea Viilor. Since the young OZena can move 
in space and time simply by clicking her magic 
shoes, the Hunters need an asset that allows them 
to anticipate the movements of the enemy. And 
the Order’s Treasury will surely have something 
suitable...
For this Scenario the Hunters will receive the support of 
the Order in the form of one of the many treasures kept in 
Valea Viilor.
The Mirror of a Thousand Wishes 
(Inspired by Snow White)
Gift - Mirror Mirror on the Wall: Those who use the 
Mirror of a Thousand Wishes can ask to see the near past 
or the imminent future of a subject they have met at least 
once. After spending 1 Soma, the Mirror will show a few 
moments of the target by displaying them on its surface. 
The observer will not know if what they see is the past or the 
future. The Storyteller secretly rolls a die: with 1-3 they will 
see the past, with 4-6 the future. The same subject cannot 
be observed more than once during a Scene. Alternatively, 
before attempting a specific task, the owner of the mirror 
can look into it and spend 1 Soma. Roll a die. If it’s a 4+ they 
will see a few moments of their imminent future. Thanks to 
this premonition they will receive 3 Additional Successes 
for the first action attempting to perform the task. 
Note to the Storyteller: The Order’s Treasure is an extra 
Gift that can be taken by one of the Hunters. The Broken 
Ones expansion book features complete rules for managing 
the Order’s Treasures and a long list of Treasures to choose 
from.

1
231
The story that led us here…
After the events in Gelb, Dorothea moved to Rot as the 
first step in eliminating Helga. The Red Witch, however, 
took the young woman by surprise. Thanks to the witches’ 
faithful servants, Helga captured the girl, holding her in 
her fortified villa. On the first night of the full moon, Helga 
intends to sacrifice Dorothea by performing a blasphemous 
rite which consists of drinking the victim’s blood to 
assimilate her memories. By doing so, Helga would learn 
of Henkle’s hiding place, where the other pieces of the 
Compass Rose are already located. 
Of course, the Hunters arrive only hours before the sun sets 
and a full moon rises, setting the stage for the ritual.
The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Helga is a powerful witch who has never had any qualms 
about using dark magic. Through blood magic, she kept her 
appearance young (despite her eighty-eight years) and over 
time she built a true cult, whose foolish devotees would 
do anything for her. Through her blood Helga can control 
plants and people, moving them like puppets. Although the 
village of Rot has acquired a grim reputation, Helga knows 
it is only a matter of time before some obsessed clergyman 
decides to attack her to eradicate the evil she represents. 
The witch aims to obtain the Compass Rose to escape to a 
safe place, where she can rebuild her kingdom of fanatical 
servants.
FIRST SCENE – Towards the 
Village of Rot
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You have finally arrived in 
the deep south of the Black Forest. Along the way, there are 
numerous signs warning that the village of Rot is a cursed 
place. Dolls of wood and rag, crosses and mounds of salt, all 
bear witness to the pointless, superstitious spells of protection 
attempted by the inhabitants of the area. As you follow the 
small path to the village, the forest thickens more and more, 
while the branches of the trees lower as if to grab you.
Place: The small path that leads to Rot.
Time: It is late afternoon and the last rays of sunlight are 
struggling to pass between the branches of the trees.
Initial Situation: The branches seem to stretch lower and 
lower, until it becomes clear to the Hunters that something 
is wrong. Shortly after, the animated trees will try to catch 
the Hunters and imprison them.
Information: If a Hunter has some understanding of 
magic, they will quickly understand that what animates the 
trees is an arcane power. Observing the branches’ motions 
for a while, it will be possible to see a figure not far away 
bathing the trees in a liquid which apparently animates 
them.

2
232
If the mirror is used to understand where 
OZena is:
 ◆ Past - Dorothea sneaks up to a large greenhouse, 
where she sees men dressed in white tending to 
plants and herbs. The mirror fills with the form of a 
nightmare creature that resembles a spider made up 
of people’s bodies, which scares Dorothea away from 
the greenhouse. In the ensuing chaos Dorothea grabs a 
sprig from a plant (Elderberry, which a Hunter with a 
relevant Descriptor will recognize).
 ◆ Future - Dorothea introduces herself to a beautiful 
young woman, dressed in an elegant red dress. The 
young assassin agrees to take a sip of the wine that is 
offered to her. Then, a trickle of blood runs down her 
lips and she falls to her knees. The woman in red smiles 
mockingly as a long knife falls from Dorothea’s hand.
Threat - The Animated Forest
OL Medium 5 
Descriptor: We grab and capture all intruders through the 
magic that animates our branches and roots.
Wolfgang the “druid”
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a skilled and renowned pharmacist, and 
there is no herb or remedy that I am not familiar with.
Agenda: I am enslaved by Helga’s power. My body moves at 
her will and therefore I sprinkle drops of her blood to animate 
the forest to capture potential intruders.
End of Scene:  If captured, Wolfgang will reveal what 
he knows to the Hunters. In the greenhouse, other poor 
people like him work incessantly under Helga’s magical 
control. In the village, a strange figure called the Porcelain 
Princess ensures that no one can harm the witch. During 
the interrogation, Wolfgang will be seized with sudden 
spasms and drown in his own blood. If Wolfgang has been 
eliminated, the Hunters will simply have to choose which 
way to go.
If they go to the greenhouse, go to the SECOND SCENE - 
The Greenhouse.
If they go to the Village go to the THIRD SCENE - The 
Doll’s Court.
SECOND SCENE – The 
Greenhouse
Read aloud to the Hunters: Following the path you reach 
an old manor house which has been remade as a greenhouse. 
Many men and women dressed in white, with tired and long-
suffering expressions, work on small gardens. Strong smells of 
herbs and medicinal infusions fill the air.
Place: The greenhouse where the base for the Infusion of 
Purity is produced.
Time: The sun is setting. 
Information:
 ◆ Pharmacists are working on a strange potion. Those 
who are experts in herbs will recognize the plants as 
Burdock, Nettle and Hyssop. 
 ◆ If the Hunters approach the greenhouse, Manycorpses 
will drop from the ceiling to stop the intruders.
 ◆ The Infusion of Purity cannot be completed, as the 
Elderberry, which Dorothea took in her fight with 
Manycorpses, is missing.

3
233
If the mirror is used:
 ◆ Past - Dorothea sneaks up to a large greenhouse, 
where she sees men dressed in white tending to 
plants and herbs. The mirror fills with the form of a 
nightmare creature that resembles a spider made up 
of people’s bodies, which scares Dorothea away from 
the greenhouse. In the ensuing chaos, Dorothea grabs 
a sprig from a plant (Elderberry, which a Hunter with a 
relevant Descriptor will recognize).
 ◆ Future - Dorothea is kneeling with her arms behind 
her back. Her body is stiff, as if paralyzed, as she is 
forced to look at herself in a mirror. She weeps for what 
she sees reflected: her face disfigured by a disease that

Manycorpses
OL Hard 7 – Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an abomination created by Helga’s 
experiments, in which she has united all her lovers into one. 
I hate anyone who still has a normal body and I use my 
incredible power to destroy them.
Agenda: Kill and dismember any human that comes within 
range.
Gift - Aberration: Manycorpses can act twice during an 
Exchange. Thanks to its multiple arms it can move on any 
surface.
End of Scene: After being freed, the greenhouse workers 
reveal that Helga is able to control people’s bodies with her 
blood magic. Something called the Infusion of Purity, a 
potion, can lift the curse. Helga is producing the Infusion 
because she plans to sell the antidote to rich and powerful 
nobles after poisoning them with her magical blood. To 
complete the Infusion, however, Elderberry is required, 
though the ingredient does not grow in this area. A strange 
girl in a green cape has stolen the small stash Helga had 
brought back from her last trip.
Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Dolls’ Court.
THIRD SCENE – The Dolls’ 
Court
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You come to a bleak, 
dilapidated village made up of small, ruined houses and 
brightly colored tents. Men and women in an evident state of 
distress lie sprawled along the streets. From their tired eyes, 
you understand that they are exhausted by the incessant work 
Helga magically forces them to do. Suddenly, figures wearing 
grotesque doll masks appear and head in your direction, 
drawing their weapons.
Place: The dilapidated village of Rot.
Time: The sun is setting, but time seems to have stopped 
in the small village.
Information:
 ◆ The Porcelain Dolls are the only individuals worth 
talking to. They will initially have their guard up, but if 
the Hunters are convincing, they will be brought to the 
Porcelain Princess.
 ◆ The village is just an old, dilapidated logging settlement. 
Not far away, it is possible to see a noble’s mansion 
where Helga now resides.
 ◆ No one can come before Helga without the approval of 
the Porcelain Princess, her favorite.

234
If the mirror is used:
 ◆ Past - Dorothea introduces herself to a beautiful 
young woman, dressed in an elegant red dress. The 
young assassin agrees to take a sip of the wine that is 
offered to her. Then, a trickle of blood runs down her 
lips and she falls to her knees. The woman in red smiles 
mockingly as a long knife falls from Dorothea’s hand..
 ◆ Future - Dorothea is kneeling with her arms behind

she sees reflected: her face disfigured by a disease that
