The Porcelain Princess
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young noble who has fallen from grace. 
My disfigured face is covered in a beautiful doll mask, and I 
sadistically manage the cult of the Crimson Kiss on behalf of 
my lady Helga.
Agenda: Carry out every order of Helga in the hope that she 
will heal my face / I would do anything to get my face back.
Gift - Vials:  The Princess always has five or six vials of 
Helga’s blood with her for newcomers to drink. She also has 
1 vial of the Infusion of Purity.
Porcelain Dolls
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the devoted guards of the Porcelain 
Princess. Everyone fears us for our martial ability. 
Agenda: Fulfill every wish of the Porcelain Princess.
Gift - Armed Guard:  Each Doll is armed differently. 
Roll 1 die: 1. Spear, the Doll can make exceptional jumps 
and can always keep an enemy at a distance; 2. Double 
knife, 2 attacks per Exchange; 3. Great Blade, once per 
Scene inflicts 1 additional Wound; 4. Whip, 9 feet range, 
whoever fails the Defense Check becomes entangled; 5. 
Blood, the blade of the weapon is soaked in Helga’s blood; 
6. Shield, +1 OL to defend themselves or an ally.
End of Scene:  By defeating the Porcelain Princess or 
persuading her away from Helga’s service, the Hunters will 
obtain the key to enter Helga’s palace. When questioned, 
the Princess will reveal that her lady intends to perform 
an arcane rite during the night that involves sacrificing 
Dorothea. Go to SCENE FOUR - The Red Witch of the 
South.

4
235
FOURTH SCENE – The Red 
Witch of the South
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Helga’s palace stands out 
from the whole village because it is the only well-maintained 
building. A heavy reinforced door and bars on the windows 
make you immediately understand that it will not be easy 
to enter. The soft sobbing of a girl catches your attention. It 
comes from the second floor, where there is a large balcony.
Place: The old mansion where Helga lives.
Time: It is now night and only the light of lanterns borne 
by the Hammerheads illuminate the mansion.
Information:
 ◆ Entering the main door means encountering 
Hammerheads equal to the number of Hunters +2.
 ◆ Entering through the window will take the Hunters 
to the room where OZena is held. If the Hunters have 
an Infusion of Purity, they can free her from Helga’s 
control. From Dorothea’s green cape, thrown carelessly 
in a corner of the room, it is possible to retrieve the 
Elderberry to complete a possible Infusion. Spending 
time finishing the Infusion will cause a number of 
Hammerheads equal to the number of Hunters +2 to 
arrive in the room.
 ◆ While the Hunters are busy in the mansion, Helga is 
taking a blood bath in a tub to prepare for the ritual. As 
soon as Dorothea is freed, Helga will understand that 
there are intruders and will get ready to welcome them 
with an escort of 8 Hammerheads.
If the mirror is used
 ◆ Past - Dorothea is kneeling with her arms behind her 
back. Her body is stiff, as if paralyzed, as she is forced 
to look at herself in a mirror. She weeps for what she 
sees reflected: her face disfigured by a disease that has 
completely devoured her nose.
 ◆ Future - Dorothea is naked and tied up on a large 
wooden table. A woman with beautiful red hair, also 
naked, utters strange arcane formulas, while she 
prepares to gut the young assassin with a long knife.
Helga the Red Witch
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful witch and I use blood to 
perform my spells. Drinking one drop of my blood is enough 
to become my slave.
Agenda: Accumulating power and collecting the four pieces 
of the Compass Rose thanks to the information obtained 
from OZena’s sacrifice / I am always willing to make an 
advantageous deal, even if this leads me to revise my plans. I 
would do anything to live forever.
Gift - Blood Magic:  Helga can unleash her powers to 
affect the blood of one or more subjects, inflicting 1 Wound 
or commanding their movements. Whoever has swallowed 
Helga’s blood is totally subject to her will. It is possible to 
resist the effects of Blood Magic with a Defense Check, 
but this does not eliminate Helga’s magic. Drinking the 
Infusion of Purity renders anyone immune to Blood Magic 
for the course of a Scene and cancels the crowd control. 
Those under the influence of the Blood Magic have a slight 
crimson tinge to their eyes.
Gift - Blood Witch:  Helga is made young and immortal 
by blood magic. At the end of each of her actions and if hurt,

236
Helga recovers 1 Wound. The flesh regenerates as the blood 
returns to her body. When she is eliminated by suffering 
her last Wound, the magic dissipates and the witch’s body 
returns to being a shriveled old woman.
The Hammerheads
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We were once a ferocious team of skilled 
mercenaries, but now we are in Helga’s service. We were 
tamed by her magic and therefore we do anything and 
everything for her.
Gift - Hammerhead Helmet: The heavy and distinctive 
Hammerhead helmet opens like a flower on the shoulders 
and grants total head and chest protection, making the 
NPC immune to any Wound that can’t get past a thick layer 
of metal. An enemy can aim at the lower parts of the NPC 
to ignore the helmet. However, they receive 1 Penalty for 
their Check.
OZena (Dorothea)
Dorothea’s profile is presented on page 
228, only her Agenda changes.
Agenda: Free myself from the prison 
where Helga locked me up.
End of Scene:  Helga’s threat is 
ended, either through combat or by 
convincing her to let Dorothea go free. 
The girl surrenders to the Hunters, 
revealing that everything she did was 
ordered by Norman Henkle, a doctor 
who, like her, comes from another 
world. Henkle settled in the center of the Black Forest, 
in a village called Smaragd. Henkle’s goal is to gather all 
the pieces of the Compass Rose to summon the magical 
tornado. Dorothea realizes that Henkle is insane and reveals 
to the Hunters that even though she has been stopped, the 
man will still try to activate the half-reassembled artifact, 
certainly with devastating results. Only Dorothea knows 
the location of the village and she is willing to accompany 
the Hunters, expressing sincere repentance.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are three Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters get the better of Wolfgang: Helga will 
let him speak for a while so she can observe the Hunters 
closely and then kill the man by drowning him in his own 
blood.
When the Hunters start to get the better of a conflict 
with Helga’s minions: the witch will detonate one of her 
minions in each Exchange to cover 
the Hunters in her blood. Y ou can 
try to control them later.
If Dorothea is saved by the 
Hunters: she will stay by their 
side as an ally to take revenge on 
Helga. She will try to kill her even 
if the Hunters intend to negotiate.

237
Part Three – The 
Emerald Village
Safety Keywords: Body horror
Once upon a 
time…
Smaragd, in the center of the great Black Forest. 
The Hunters are on the trail of Norman Henkle, 
a doctor and charlatan who apparently is the 
mastermind behind the young witch killer who 
goes by the name of OZena. The girl, whose real 
name is Dorothea, claims Henkle is actually 
a powerful wizard who fulfills wishes. His 
ultimate goal is to activate the Compass Rose 
to summon a storm and return to his own time. 
Given the young woman’s failure to retrieve the 
last two pieces, Dorothea fears that Henkle will 
activate the artifact anyway. Dorothea, however, 
remembers the first witch she killed saying that 
using the Compass Rose without all the pieces 
would be disastrous, opening a passage to 
another world, but at a very high price...
The story that led us here…
Norman Henkle has been settled in a small and remote 
village for a month, where he halted an epidemic thanks 
to the Penicillin in his doctor’s bag, brought back from the 
year 1900. 
Raised to the status of holy man, Norman began to believe 
himself a magician, giving free rein to his madness. In the 
center of Smaragd there is a small well whose true nature the 
inhabitants have always ignored. It is in fact a wishing well, 
which after centuries of inactivity, has awakened thanks to 
the desire of Henkle himself to become a magician. 
Now possessing new powers, Henkle is truly capable of 
making people’s wishes come true. However, his madness 
twists every wish and the results are always tainted. 
Determined to return to his time and become famous, 
Henkle lured young Dorothea into his service, promising 
to fulfill her one desire, to heal the disease that disfigures 
her face. 
Aware that he can’t really do it without risking some worse 
effect, Henkle is waiting for OZena’s return to retrieve the 
last two pieces of the Compass Rose so he can leave Smaragd, 
escaping in his hot-air balloon. In the meantime, Henkle 
has “experimented” with his power on the inhabitants of 
Smaragd, creating monsters and aberrations that now hope 
to earn a new wish to return to normal, unaware that they 
are locked in an endless cycle of warped wishes.

1
238
The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Henkle fortified Smaragd against any possible intruder, 
garrisoned by the villagers now corrupted by their wishes, 
as well as the former companions of Dorothea who 
survived the first encounter with the Hunters. While the 
hot-air balloon is almost done, Henkle awaits Dorothea’s 
arrival to complete the Compass Rose and escape.
Note to the Storyteller: This part of the Scenario differs 
from the first two parts, as many enemies and encounters 
will depend on what the Hunters have done previously. For 
this reason, you’ll find only three Scenes leading up to the 
Hunters meeting Henkle. It is possible to drop one or more 
of the NPCs presented in each Scene to provide a challenge 
for the Hunters. At the end of each Scene, or during a Scene 
itself, if the Hunters face NPCs in a conflict, place 1 Time 
Token on Henkle’s Hot-Air Balloon Gift. Until the hot-air 
balloon is revealed, the Hunters will only know the name 
of the Tokens that are accumulated by the Storyteller, but 
not their purpose.
FIRST SCENE – Approaching 
the Village of Smaragd
Read aloud to the Hunters: Finally, you reach the center 
of the great Black Forest. A small path, now 
almost completely lost among    weeds, is the 
only point of reference to find your way. 
Dorothea precedes you by a few steps, 
visibly nervous. Suddenly you see the 
girl stop and she wavers for a moment, 
suddenly finding herself on the edge 
of a huge ravine. “This wasn’t here 
the last time I was here, ” exclaims 
the amazed girl. A moat over 
sixty feet deep and thirty wide surrounds a small village that 
you can glimpse through the foliage. The fact that there is 
no bridge suggests that it was created for the sole purpose of 
keeping intruders away.
Place: A huge circular pit that Henkle created in order to 
secure Smaragd
Time: It is early morning. The sun is high in the cloudless 
sky.
Initial Situation:  It will be clear to the Hunters that 
the moat must be crossed somehow. Dorothea can use 
her silver slippers if she still has them, but she won’t do so 
without the consent of the Hunters. The girl is convinced 
that the Order can help her and will not betray the trust of 
the Hunters, unless she sees a possibility of returning to the 
future (see Events).
Information:  Henkle’s plan is to hit the Hunters as 
they cross the pit, so he has deployed some underlings to 
ambush them. Choose one of the following:
 ◆ Hirnloser will have discovered a good position from 
which to target the Hunters at a distance with the help 
of 6 Villagers. 
 ◆ Lowe will go to meet the Hunters to challenge them 
in the open field, bringing with him 3 Villagers and 3

239
Canine Tigers. 
 ◆ Dose will very simply attack the Hunters as soon as 
they cross the Pit.
 ◆ 4 Villagers with 4 Canine Tigers

OL Medium 5

End of Scene:  The Hunters cross the pit to enter the

SECOND SCENE – The 
Emerald Village
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Once you cross the Pit you

trees. It only takes a few steps to understand that something

track of which direction you are moving. The small village

Place: The small village of Smaragd which, by a wish

Time: It is impossible to mark the passage of time

Information:  Henkle’s plan is 
to waste the Hunters’ time and 
prevent them from getting to 
the center of the village. For 
this he has deployed some 
subordinates to set up an 
ambush. Choose one of the following:
 ◆ Hirnloser will plan an ambush with the help of 4

 ◆ Lowe will go to meet the Hunters to challenge them

Canine Tigers. 
 ◆ Dose will very simply attack the Hunters as soon as 
they enter the village.
 ◆ 4 Villagers with 4 Canine Tigers

OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A maze of trees and houses that change every

End of Scene:  The Hunters arrive at the center of the

THIRD SCENE – The 
Magician’s Fake Castle
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Finally in the center of the 
village, you find yourself in front of a huge oak that has been 
molded into the shape of a grotesque castle. Inside the tree, 
you can see a hot-air balloon made of patched fabric that is 
slowly inflating. You have never seen anything like it, but you 
understand it will not be good.
Place: Henkle’s workshop house in the center of the 
village, where the hot-air balloon is set up.
Time: The morning sun is high in a cloudless sky.

240

FOURTH SCENE - The End of OZena’s Sad Story.
FOURTH SCENE – The End of 
OZena’s Sad Story
The final battle with Henkle can go in many different ways. 
If he manages to activate the incomplete Compass Rose, 
a huge tornado will hit the whole Black Forest, affecting 
everyone and taking Henkle away to a place unknown to 
the Hunters.
Whatever happens, the fate of young Dorothea will be in 
their hands.
Lowe the Brave Fighter
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I became a proud warrior and realized my 
heroic potential. I never back down and set an example for 
my teammates.
Agenda: Stop the invaders and protect the village of 
Smaragd, which has elected me as its hero.
Gift - Hero: Lowe fears nothing and is immune to all forms 
of fear and magical control. After he acts, his allies receive 
+1 to their OL, inspired by their hero’s courage and words.
Hirnloser the Genius
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: My mind has become infallible, and I can 
calculate and check any variable in seconds. I have a strategy 
for every situation.
Agenda: Solve any potential problems that plague my 
benefactor Norman Henkle.

241
Gift - Genius: When someone uses a Gift, Hirnloser can 
prevent it from activating by deploying a countering system 
or strategy. This Gift can be used up to 3 times during a 
Scene, but only 1 time per Exchange. 
Dose the Giant in Love
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: My body has become pure metal and only the 
power of love can bring me back to flesh and blood. I fight 
and destroy anything that can threaten my sweet OZena.
Agenda: I am in love with OZena and I want her to loyally 
serve Henkle once more. I know that imprisonment in the 
hands of the enemy may have made her reluctant to trust us. 
I have to save her, whether she wants it or not.
Gift - Of Steel and Love: Dose is made of pure metal and 
cannot suffer any kind of physical Wound. The only way to 
get him to return to flesh and blood for a few moments is 
to make his heart beat with love. Seeing OZena in danger 
or well disposed towards him will change him back for 
one Exchange. Seeing OZena, Dose can’t help but declare 
his love as he fights, making his infatuation for the young 
assassin clear.
The Canine Tigers
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are the village dogs, turned into monsters 
by the desire of one of the children. Once the tiger’s ferocity 
takes over, we can no longer be controlled.
Gift - Fierce as a Tiger:  When a Canine Tiger suffers 
a Wound, it goes mad with rage and its Opposition Level 
rises to Hard 7. At the mercy of anger, the Tiger will attack 
the closest target without distinguishing between allies and 
enemies.
Norman  Henkle 
The Powerful Wizard Who 
Makes Wishes Come True
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful wizard and what I desire 
becomes reality. My skill as a barker always charms everyone 
to my side.
Agenda: I want to go back to my time alone and become a 
celebrity thanks to the power of magic.
Gift - Hot-Air Balloon: As soon as it is ready, Henkle will 
board and release his hot-air balloon into the air to activate 
the Compass Rose. As indicated by the Scenario, based on 
the actions of the Hunters this Gift will accumulate Time 
Tokens. When the third Time Token is placed, the hot-air 
balloon is ready to go.

242
The Villagers of Emerald
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are poor villagers changed by a wish we 
made. 
Agenda: We hate Henkle for what he did to us, but we serve 
him in the hope that we can make another wish.
Gift - Careful What you Wish For:  Each Villager has 
made a wish, but it has not had the desired effects. Roll 1 die 
to see what mutation has developed: 1. Wish for Wealth: 
The NPC spits out gold coins mixed with bile. With a single 
action, they can hit up to 3 targets at a distance of 15 feet; 
2. Wish for Beauty:  The NPC warps their body so as 
always to look beautiful in the eyes of the beholder. When 
there is more than one subject in view, their body changes 
constantly. Whoever witnesses the show receives 1 Penalty 
on their Checks out of disgust; 3. Wish for Health:  The 
NPC’s body regenerates as their hair and body hair grow 
out of proportion. The NPC recovers 1 Wound as soon as it 
loses 1, making them effectively immortal. The effect of the 
Wound suffered is applied, so the NPC can be defeated, but 
will regenerate on its turn to act; 4. Wish for Skill: The 
NPC raises their OL to Medium; 5. The NPC can no longer 
sleep because they are obsessed with the idea of working, 
and will constantly be chattering nonsense; 5. Wish to 
Grow Up: The NPC is a child with a body of a giant while 
their head has remained the same as before. They can take 
2 extra Wounds and inflict 1 additional Wound when they 
unleash their strength; 6. Wish for Love:  The NPC has 
merged with the one they love, literally obtaining the desire 
to “become one. ” As a double body the NPC can act twice.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are two Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
When Henkle realizes that Dorothea does not have 
the missing pieces of the Compass Rose with her:  
he will take off on the balloon as soon as it is ready, and 
then activate the power of the incomplete Rose.
If Dorothea realizes that Henkle is willing to 
abandon her to return to the future:  she will do 
anything to reach the man, begging him not to abandon 
her.

243
Playing a Broken Tales 
Campaign
Broken Tales is particularly suitable for episodic 
games, where the group of Hunters can change its 
composition from Scenario to Scenario to always 
experience different stories. That said, it is entirely possible 
to play a Broken Tales Campaign. In fact, a campaign is the 
perfect opportunity to explore the story of the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows and uncover the secret behind the 
Broken Tales.
To organize a Campaign, it is necessary to 
follow the following steps:
 ◆ Create a group of Hunters. Y ou can choose them from 
pre-generated options or create them from scratch 
with the rules on page 69. Even though a Campaign 
allows a different and more detailed development of a 
Hunter through experience, between one Scenario and 
the next in a campaign a Player can decide to change 
their Hunter. The newcomer will begin with an amount 
of XP to spend equivalent to that accumulated (even 
those already spent) by the Hunter who leaves the story.
 ◆ The Storyteller places the Map of Broken Europe at 
the center of the table, then asks the Hunters where 
they want to start their story from. The Storyteller can 
choose to offer only a few possibilities, based on the 
Scenarios they prefer to initially bring to the table.
 ◆ The first Session opens with a Scenario and with the 
Hunters intent on solving the investigation. During 
each Scenario, the Storyteller can decide to introduce 
one of the Clues  that will lead to the One Who Plots 
in the Shadow (page 246). Once the Scenario is over, 
the Hunters are free to move to any destination on the 
Map.
 ◆ Moving from one place to another takes a few days 
of travel. The journeys (page 111) are managed by a 
series of Encounters in which the Storyteller can detail 
the world of Broken Europe and introduce the Dark 
Presences.
 ◆ Once at the new location, the Hunters will encounter 
the corresponding Scenario and, by facing it, they will 
be able to obtain a potential new Clue about the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows.
 ◆ Once they discover his true intentions and where the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows is hiding, the Hunters 
will be able to meet him for the final confrontation.

244
The whole (true) story of 
the Broken Tales
The One Who Plots in the Shadows and the Child Savior 
are one and the same, two sides of the same coin who will 
never meet. The Child Savior, pure and innocent, who saved 
the world of fairytales with his love for stories, as an adult, 
became The Writer to Whom No One Gave Credit, driven 
desperately insane by all the rejection he had experienced. 
When the Child Savior made the wish, an echo of his future 
self had already burrowed into his heart. Without knowing 
it, the child had made a wish that destroyed everything 
he loved. As the years went by, the Child Savior grew up, 
and tried to save the fairytales from what he had done. 
But the more he wrote, the darker and more repulsive the 
stories became. Every refusal, every reader who mocked his 
creativity pushed him further towards his future self: The 
One Who Plots in the Shadows.
Broken Europe, the Hunters, and the whole world of the 
Broken Tales are nothing more than a giant shattered 
fairytale, constantly written by the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows, who breaks and reshapes every story that has 
ever been told. Safe in his hiding place, he has started with 
his own favorite fairytales. The Child Savior is now but a 
ghost, a faded memory of a lost innocence that wanders 
through Broken Europe in search of someone who can save 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows from the darkness of 
his own heart.
In all this, the Hunters are just puppets, distorted heroes 
created to bring justice to a world that shouldn’t exist, 
an immense machine that works despite the lack of a 
fundamental gear: the difference between good and evil, so 
dear to the fairytales of yore.
Finding the One Who Plots in the Shadows and confronting 
him with his errant ways is the only way to put an end to 
an infinite cycle that will inevitably bring every story to its 
breaking point. But once the truth is revealed, what will the 
Hunters choose? 
Returning to the status quo will mean returning to their 
lives as villains, shunned and destined to lose, spinning 
evil forever. In preserving all that has been created by all 
the adventures of Broken Europe, they condemn all other 
fairytales to be forever broken.
Fragments of a shattered mirror, still capable of reflecting 
an image, but distorted and incomplete.
TRUE

245
The Clues that lead to 
the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows
The One Who Plots in the Shadows lives in Borgo 
Maleanime, a remote mountain village located in Northern 
Italy. Safe inside a large mansion, the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows continues to break new fairytales day after day, 
feeding the dark world that he himself has created. Nobody 
knows who he really is and his face doesn’t linger in any 
of the villagers’ memories. Not even the oldest among 
the villagers can say when he appeared exactly, and the 
youngest assure each other he has been there at least since 
forever.
Only those who know the truth will be able to see him for 
what he truly is: a middle-aged gentleman with sad eyes, 
surrounded by deeply dark circles.
By gathering information along their travels, the Hunters 
will become more and more aware of the presence of the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows, until they can clearly 
distinguish him from among the inhabitants of Maleanime.
During or at the end of a Scenario, the Storyteller may 
introduce one or more Clues related to the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows so that over time it becomes clear to 
the Hunters that a strange, hidden opponent is scheming 
against the Order from the shadows. By studying the Map, 
the Hunters will be able to see Borgo Maleanime, but until 
they find the Clue of the Road of Memories, they will not 
be able to reach the mansion where the One Who Plots in 
the Shadows lives. 
Borgo Maleanime itself cannot be reached unless you are 
led there by someone who has already visited it at least 
once.
The order and the way in which the Clues are found are 
deliberately left open, to allow the Storyteller to develop 
stories and events without restraint. However, there are a 
few things to remember to keep the Campaign on track:
 ◆ The Clue the Road of Memories represents the way to 
find the mansion of the One Who Plots in the Shadows 
in Borgo Maleanime. It should only emerge when the 
Hunters have an idea of who is hiding in the small 
village.
 ◆ Borgo Maleanime’s name should only appear when the 
Hunters have realized that a dark and dangerous force 
is acting against the Order. But to get there they will 
need the guidance of someone who has already been 
there, such as the Hunter Niklaus von Krampus.
 ◆ If the Hunters decide to visit Borgo Maleanime before 
knowing what they are looking for, they will realize 
that it is impossible to reach the town and that the 
small road that leads to the mountain where the village 
is located will magically take them beyond it. Speaking 
with a wandering merchant along the road, they will 
discover that he himself goes to the village every now 
and then, but only if someone leads him, because Borgo 
Maleanime is a strange place, always lost in the mists. 
Unfortunately, for over a year now, no one has come 
or gone from the village. The Storyteller should make 
it clear to the Hunters that it is impossible to reach the 
town without a guide and that such a person will not 
be easy to find. Too much procrastination around the 
town will only be a waste of time.

246
The Clues
The following is a series of Clues that the Storyteller must 
drop during the course of the Campaign.
 The Road of Memories
The Hunters find a diary written in a child’s handwriting. 
The little book has a powerful magical aura, from which 
emanates sadness and echoes of the past. The written pages 
clearly illustrate an incredible passion for fairytales and for 
all the characters who are part of them. The diary is full 
of drawings sketched with a child’s hand. They represent 
imaginary adventures starring the Hunters themselves, 
painted as heroes who save the world of fairytales. The last 
page of the diary is a drawing representing a large mansion 
at the end of a tree-lined avenue.
If the Hunters have already been to Borgo Maleanime they 
will recognize the avenue, but they will not remember 
having seen the mansion. If they have never been there, they 
will have the clear perception that the diary is a magical key 
to get to a hidden place. 
If they open the diary to the drawing of the mansion facing 
the tree-lined avenue in Borgo Maleanime, they will see the 
house.
 The Child Savior
A strange child wanders at night, crossing the path of the 
Hunters. His expression is sad and he never speaks. He can 
come and go anywhere he wants, appearing and disappearing 
in the blink of an eye. Sometimes he watches the Hunters, 
sometimes he helps them for no obvious reason. It will soon 
be clear to the Hunters that this figure is endowed with great 
power, but that he acts towards inscrutable ends.
The Child Savior is but a ghost, a reminder of who the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows once was. He does, however, 
still share some of the power that led to the creation of the 
world of Broken Tales. The Child Savior will approach the 
Hunters to study them, trying to understand if they can 
be the “heroes” who could bring peace to the heart of the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows. After their first meeting, 
the Child Savior could come to their aid using his ability 
to come and go from stories as he pleases. It is not possible 
to communicate with him in a normal way, but only by 
trying to interpret his actions. The use of certain Gifts, at 
the Storyteller’s discretion, may allow a Hunter to meet or 
speak with the Child Savior for a short time. 
Through his messages, the Child Savior can impart the 
following information:
 ◆ The Broken Tales are not really evil, but someone is 
warping their destiny.
 ◆ Someone pulls the strings of the Hunters’ fate.
 ◆ The Hunters find an original fairytale that tells the true 
story behind one of the Scenarios they have already 
faced.
 ◆ The location of the Diary (see The Road of Memories).
 Sherazade the Weaver of Tales
Sherazade threatens the Hunters thanks to her power to 
make nightmares real. As the agent of the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows, the Weaver of Tales wants to prevent the 
Hunters from uncovering the truth behind the Broken Tales.
Sherazade will act as described in the relevant Scenario on 
page 196. In the Campaign, the Weaver of Tales resides 
in Italy, in the Kingdom of Naples, as a guest of the noble

247
Bourbon family. The clues that her Shadows will leave must, 
therefore, lead the Hunters to the city of Naples. Some 
examples may be: the Bourbon coat of arms on a precious 
ring, a small lava stone from Vesuvius, or a key with a finely 
worked head depicting the royal palace of Caserta.
Once defeated, Sherazade will tell her story and how a 
strange man whose face she can’t remember gave her the 
power to take revenge for her sister’s death. Sherazade has 
tried several times to tell a story with this strange figure 
as the protagonist, but each time her power fizzled when 
doing so.
 ◆ All she has seen is an old mansion at the end of a tree-
lined avenue. 
 ◆ In another story, the mysterious figure did nothing 
but write, as if obsessed with the idea of modifying his 
stories. 
 ◆ In the past, Sherazade has seen a strange child appear 
in some stories, able to put an end to the magic of her 
nightmares.
 ◆ If the Storyteller thinks the time is right, Sherazade 
will describe having visited Borgo Maleanime the first 
time she received her powers and can lead the Hunters 
there.
 Saint George the Dragon Slayer
Saint George puts pressure on the Hunters by judging their 
integrity. As the unwitting pawn of the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows, the Dragon Slayer becomes a threat to Hunters who 
have gone too far, considering them villains.
Saint George will act as described in the relevant Scenario 
on page 216. In the Campaign, the Saint will have received 
his assignment from the Pope himself, but will clearly 
remember that at the side of the pontiff there was a strange 
adviser that no one had ever seen before. Investigating in 
Rome, it will be obvious that no one has any memory of 
this person except for his frequent speeches on the danger 
of the Hunters, a weapon in the hands of the Order that 
has always risked turning into a greater threat than what 
it fights.
After making amends, Saint George will vow to help the 
Hunters by setting out in search of this strange figure. Later, 
the Saint will be able to bring various information to the 
attention of the Hunters by acting as an intermediary for 
other Clues. For example, he could suggest that Niklaus 
von Krampus has already been to Borgo Maleanime.
 Memories of the Old Exorcist
Niklaus von Krampus has already been to Borgo Maleanime, 
where an encounter with a mysterious demon convinced him 
to become a Hunter. What Niklaus does not know is that his 
whole story is but the umpteenth creation of the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows, to remove Niklaus from the game before 
he can reveal too much. The old priest has always had the 
strong feeling of having left an unfinished exorcism behind in 
Borgo Maleanime.
Niklaus is known in Borgo Maleanime and the villagers will 
be well disposed towards him. They will show him kindness 
that they would not to any other foreigner. If Niklaus is 
not among the Hunters played, the Hunters will be able to 
learn what he did in Borgo Maleanime when reporting to 
the Pope in Rome, or from Saint George once he is on their 
side. The Hunters will discover that the old exorcist is on 
a mission for the Order and the Storyteller will be able to 
introduce a new Scenario with von Krampus as an NPC.

248
 The Confession
At the end of an investigation, one of the Hunters’ opponents 
confesses that they were led to do evil or that they received 
their powers from a strange figure whose face they do not 
remember.
One of the Scenario opponents will reveal to the Hunters 
that they received some kind of help that led them to 
perform the actions at the core of the Scenario.
An example: Iskra will describe fleeing, cold and without 
strength, through the great Białowieża forest, with wolves on 
her trail ready to devour her. The little girl remembers a caress 
on the head and a male voice behind her that whispered, “I 
will give you the power you need to take revenge on your 
mother. ” After that, the little girl will say that she woke up 
stained with blood and found many dead wolves at her feet.
Over the course of various Scenarios, it will become clear 
to the Hunters that this mysterious figure is the one who 
acts as a catalyst for the events that require the Order’s 
intervention. However, the Storyteller will have to give 
this information at the end of the Scenario, so that the 
presence of the One Who Plots in the Shadows does not 
divert the Hunters from the events of the Scenario itself. 
From interrogations it will be possible to obtain, from time 
to time, some additional information on the strange figure:
 ◆ He has a strong Italian accent. 
 ◆ His hands are stained with ink and he claims to be a 
humble writer.
 ◆ He carries a book in which he takes notes and writes 
stories.
 Close Enough to Feel His Breath
The One Who Plots in the Shadows begins to perceive the 
Hunters as a potential threat and therefore observes them 
closely. In the most frantic scenes, a Hunter will always notice 
the silhouette of a man, safely hidden behind some shadows.
The One Who Plots in the Shadows appears when the 
Hunters are focused on something else, trying to better 
understand what they have discovered about him. If 
approached, he will disappear into thin air and it will be 
clear to the Hunters that no matter how hard they try, 
the face of the man is wiped from their memories. If the 
“Confession” Clue has already been found, the connection 
can be made. 
While it will not be possible for the Hunters to catch the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows, a good plan or a good 
Position Check in the attempt could knock some important 
object out of the opponent’s hands before he disappears:
 ◆ A notebook in which characters and facts of a Scenario 
still to be played are written. The Storyteller will prepare 
some Clues such as the Agenda of an NPC, the power 
of some Gift or some information that the Hunters 
will read from the notebook, and that therefore they 
will already know if they go to the indicated place. 
The Storyteller must be careful that the information is 
useful but does not reveal too much of the plot of the 
Scenario. That way, the Hunters will understand that 
the figure who lost the notebook plays an active role in 
pulling the strings of what is happening.
 ◆ An old and worn diary (see “The Road of Memories”).
After revealing two Clues in this way, the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows will no longer dare to follow the Hunters.

249
 The Assassin
The One Who Plots in the Shadows sends a Dark Presence to 
thwart the Hunters.
The Dark Presence will aim to impede the Hunters and, if 
defeated, will lead to a Clue taken from “The Confession. ”
 The Prophecy
A powerful group (such as the Zugarramurdi witches) or a 
Dark Presence (such as Ambrose, for example) will reveal to 
the Hunters a prophecy about a dark creator who gives birth 
to all the evil in Broken Europe.
The prophecy speaks of a human who became God and 
believes he holds the whole world in his hands. The prophecy 
will also reveal that the Child Savior, the protagonist of an 
ancient fairytale, has the power to stop the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows. This prophecy will reveal to the Hunters 
the name of their enemy and a place where the Child 
Savior was last seen. By choosing a location on the Map, 
the Storyteller will be able to drop the “Child Savior Clue” 
into the relevant Scenario.
 The Epilogue
Once the necessary Clues have been obtained, the Hunters 
will be able to see the path that leads to the mansion where 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows lives. The villain will 
unleash a group of Dark Presences against the Hunters, 
created ad hoc for the final confrontation. The Dark 
Presences must be equal to the number of Hunters and 
each must be linked to a Hunter’s personal story. These 
Dark Presences must have a background and an Agenda 
that leads them to hate the Hunters. 
Once the Dark Presences are defeated, the Hunters will face 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows. He is an anonymous 
man hunched over a large desk, scribbling incessantly on 
the pages of a book before tearing them out in anger. The 
One Who Plots in the Shadows will explain the whole true 
story of the Child Savior and how the world as the Hunters 
know it is nothing more than his creation.
The poor writer is now only a madman, prey to his own 
delirium of omnipotence. However, what will be clear to 
the Hunters is that his death will erase the history of Broken 
Europe. A story of which they are, in spite of themselves, 
the protagonists.
Killing the One Who Plots in the Shadows and bringing all 
the fairytales back to what they were will turn the Hunters 
back into being the villains, the losers, the ones everyone 
hates.
Leaving the One Who Plots in the Shadows alive will mean 
accepting a new world ruled by a mad God. But, it will be 
a world where Hunters have the free will to be whatever 
they want.
What will the Players choose?

250
The Map of Broken 
Europe
During the Campaign, the Hunters will move across 
the Map of Broken Europe. Some places will have a 
corresponding Scenario, while in others it will be possible 
to drop Dark Presences or encounters of various kinds. The 
Storyteller can modify at will the Scenarios and situations 
of each place according to the progress of the Campaign, 
but the following information is to be considered canon for 
how the general story of the Broken Tales was conceived.
 Black Forest
Here the Hunters can face a Scenario in three acts called 
OZena the Suffering and Desperate. Once the final scenario 
is completed, OZena could become a Hunter of the Order. 
OZena the Afflicted’s sheet is featured in the expansion 
book The Broken Ones.
 Borgholm
In this location, the Hunters will face the Scenario A 
Soldier’s Duty.
 Borgo Maleanime
Here, the Hunters can track the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows if they have collected enough Clues. It will also be 
the place of the final confrontation. Borgo Maleanime can 
only be accessed if led by someone who has already visited 
the town in the past.
 Devil’s Bridge
Europe is littered with bridges that manifest strange powers 
and that the local population always associate with the 
Devil. One Devil’s Bridge can lead Hunters to another such 
Bridge. Devil’s Bridges are spread across Europe, allowing 
the Hunters to cover great distances in no time. There is 
also a Devil’s Bridge in Borgo Maleanime, which could be 
an alternative way for the Hunters to get to the hideout of 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
 Durfort
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario Red-Hood 
Iskra. Once the Scenario is resolved, Iskra could become 
a Hunter of the Order. Iskra the Y oung Wolf ’s sheet is 
featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Martana Island
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario The House 
of Roses, featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones. 
Once this Scenario is completed, Beauty and her Beast 
could become Hunters of the Order. The sheet for Beauty 
and her Beast is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 Lohr
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario 
Bloodwhite’s Heart, featured in the expansion book 
The Broken Ones. Once this Scenario is completed, 
Bloodwhite could become a Hunter of the Order. 
Bloodwhite’s sheet is featured in the expansion book 
The Broken Ones.

251
 London 
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario I Don’t 
Believe, featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Malbork
This city is home to the Order of the Teutonic Knights, who 
have been in open conflict with the Church for years. The 
Teutonic Knights believe the Hunters have strayed from the 
straight path that should define a servant of God. 
Some possible situations related to the Knights:
 ◆ A Hunter of the Order has been taken prisoner (it 
could be Krampus, whom the Hunters may be hoping 
can lead them to Borgo Maleanime). 
 ◆ A Treasure linked to the Broken Tales has been found 
by the Knights and is jealously guarded in a stronghold. 
 ◆ The Knights are preparing an attack on Valea Viilor, 
with the intent of penetrating the fortified church and 
discovering its secrets.
 Naples 
In this city, the Hunters will be able to track down Sherazade 
and put an end to the threat she poses. Once the Scenario 
is resolved, Sherazade the Weaver of Tales could become 
a Hunter of the Order. Sherazade’s sheet is featured in the 
expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Nijmegen 
Here the Hunters can meet the last druids in all of Broken 
Europe. The druids are secretly in the service of the Dark 
Presence Ambrose the Sorcerer, known as Merlin. The 
druids are always ready to help the Hunters, but only 
because they secretly try to gather information about the 
Broken Tales to share with Merlin. 
Some possible situations related to Nijmegen:
 ◆ The druids ask for the Hunters’ help in driving the 
Dark Presence Oakheart from the forest. The spirit 
is in Nijmegen because she has fallen in love with a 
young druid.
 ◆ The druids ask the Hunters to capture a young novice 
who has betrayed their order and is on his way to 
Malbork to denounce the coven to the Teutonic 
Knights. If the Hunters help them, the druids will reveal 
the location of a Devil’s Bridge around Nijmegen.
 Paris 
In this location, the Hunters will face the Scenario The 
Smile in the Darkness. Once the Scenario is completed, 
Yvonne could become a Hunter of the Order. Yvonne of 
the Dark’s sheet is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.

252
 Prague
In this city the Hunters will face the Scenario I Will Stay 
with You Always, available in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 Rome
In the Church capital, Hunters can gather information 
from other Hunters, receive assignments, or hire new party 
members. Rome can also be a connection point for the 
Clues of the Campaign and a place to return to regularly 
to receive useful information. The Storyteller can use Rome 
as a connecting point during the Campaign. When talking 
with the Pope, the Hunters can learn useful information 
and receive suggestions on what to do next.
 Sinalunga
In this place, the Hunters will face the Scenario Of Flesh 
and Wood. Once the Scenario completed, Carlo the Puppet 
could become a Hunter of the Order. Carlo the Living 
Puppet’s sheet is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 Stegeborg 
In this place, the Hunters will face the Scenario Stegeborg’s 
Quiet, available in the expansion book The Broken Ones. 
Once the Scenario is completed, Talia the Sleeper could 
become a Hunter of the Order. Talia the Sleeper’s sheet is 
featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Valea Viilor
In this fortified church lies the Treasury of the Order. This 
is where the Hunters will have to return each time they 
want to pick up a Treasure to bring on a mission. Too much 
use of the Order’s Treasures may anger Karen. The Treasury 
of the Order and Karen the Mannequin are featured in the 
expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Vigo
In this place, the Hunters will face the Scenario In your 
Eyes, with your Feet, available in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 Vík í Mýrdal
In this place, the Hunters will face the Scenario Hunger 
Island, featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Whitehaven
In this town, the Hunters will face the Scenario The City of 
Pigs. Once the Scenario is completed, the Gang of Three 
Pigs could become a Hunter of the Order. The Gang of 
Three Pigs’ sheet is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 York 
In this place, the Hunters will face the Scenario 
Wonderbedlam. Once the Scenario is completed, Alice of 
Other Worlds and Hatta Carter could become Hunters of 
the Order. Alice of Other Worlds and Hatta Carter’s sheets 
are featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.

253
 Zugarramurdi
In this small town, a powerful coven of witches has always 
been hiding. Led by the sorceress Gabrina Furetta, who 
fled from Italy after attracting too much attention from 
the Papacy, the witches are available to provide power and 
information. However, they always charge a price for their 
help.
 
Some possible situations related to Zugarramurdi:
 ◆ Saint George arrives in the small town with his Paladins 
to capture Gabrina.
 ◆ The Hunters arrive during a magical challenge between 
Baba Y aga and Gabrina. The two witches have a strong 
mutual dislike (also because Baba Y aga never misses an 
opportunity to mock her rival) and the consequences 
of the many spells cast threaten to shed a bad light on 
the Order.
Encounters
Encounters are Game Scenes that are outside a Scenario 
and come into play when the Hunters move from one place 
to another along the Map. The purpose of the Encounters 
is to give variety to the Campaign game, highlighting the 
stories and details of Broken Europe. 
When the Hunters travel, based on the distance between 
the place of departure and the place of arrival, the 
Storyteller prepares 1 to 3 Encounters. This proportion is 
purely subjective and only serves to give a perception of 
distance, but it isn’t intended to be realistic. Simply put, it 
will be clear to the Hunters that the longer a journey, the 
more situations will arise along the way.
An Encounter follows the normal rules for Scenes but 
ends when the Storyteller believes it has run its course. 
Hunters are entitled to a Scenario’s end Interlude after each 
Encounter. This is a good incentive for them, as Encounters 
are a good way to earn XP without having to face the risks 
of a full Scenario. 
When planning the Encounters, the Storyteller can be 
inspired by one of these 3 options:
Encounter with a Dark Presence
The Hunters stumble upon one of the Dark Presences 
featured on page 41. Roll a die or pick from the list:
Ambrose the Sorcerer, known as the Old Merlin 
1-2 The Hunters see an old, ruined tower in the mists. It is 
the current home of Ambrose. The old wizard will welcome 
them if they knock at his door. 3-4 A monstrous beast sows 
panic in the square of a small village. In reality, the beast 
was unleashed by Ambrose, curious to see the Hunters at 
work. 5-6 Ambrose approaches the Hunters in the guise of 
an old farmer named Kurt. The man is desperate because a 
fairy creature destroys his crops every night. Ambrose hopes 
the Hunters will eliminate the creature (which is actually 
innocent and just upset by the wizard’s presence) because its 
skin is useful for some arcane preparations.
Frog Face
1-2 Frog Face falls in love with one of the Hunters. 3-4 The 
Hunters run into a young girl on the run. Frog Face and his 
trusty Heinrich watch the encounter from a distance, waiting 
to be able to retrieve their prey. 5-6 Heinrich approaches 
the Hunters to request their services. His lord offers immense 
riches if they manage to break a curse that hangs on him...
1

254
Hua Mulan the Sword Demon  
1-2 The sound of a fight draws the attention of the Hunters. 
A very skilled warrior faces more than ten brigands alone. 
Will she need help? 3-4 Hua Mulan is on the trail of one of 
the Hunters to avenge an injustice they inflicted to a former 
Scenario NPC. 5-6 The Hunters find a half-dead deserter. 
After recovering, the young man will say his name is Patrick 
and will tell of having escaped the fury of an invincible warrior 
who is hunting him down to punish him for his desertion. 
Patrick will weep, saying that he was forced to enlist and that 
he is just trying to reunite with his wife and daughter.
The Periwinkle Peer
1-2 The Peer tracks down the Hunters to challenge the most 
skilled among them to a duel. 3-4 The Hunters meet the Peer 
at a village celebration, and amidst music and libations, the 
NPC will use his Gift of Divine Charm on one of the Hunters. 
5-6 The Hunters meet Isabella, a young woman with a sad 
look. She is a noble who has forsaken her family to follow the 
Periwinkle Peer, who has now abandoned her for another 
woman. If only someone would recover the necklace that 
Isabella gave him as a promise of marriage, the young woman 
would be free from all bonds and could return to her family.
The Ancient Enemy
1-2 A group of desperate farmers approaches the Hunters. A 
large beast comes out every night and eats the cattle, and no 
one is able to stop it. 3-4 The Ancient Enemy approaches the 
Hunters in the guise of a small squirrel. He wants to observe 
them in the next Scenario they will face, to understand if they 
can be true allies who could save him from Saint George and 
his Paladins. 5-6 A little girl named Sara asks the Hunters 
to deliver a flower necklace to the “big lizard” who saved 
her from drowning in the lake near the village. None of the 
adults believe her story, but she would love to repay her savior. 
Details in Sara’s description will give the Hunters the distinct 
impression that this encounter actually happened.
The Wild Swans Gang
1-2 The Hunters arrive in a village where the church has 
been set on fire by a group of fierce young people. Anton, the 
parish priest, will say that there were eleven criminals and 
that he heard them boast of their deep hatred for the papacy. 
Now Anton fears that the gang is headed for the church in a 
nearby village. 3-4 The Hunters stumble upon a dilapidated 
house that everyone says is the home of a witch. Traces of 
blood which lead into the nearby forest and disappear do not 
bode well. 5-6 Arriving in the city, the Hunters discover that 
another Hunter of the Order has arrived before them but has 
mysteriously disappeared without a trace.
The Silent
1-2 The Hunters come across a traveling circus. Gustav, the 
director, is desperate because the previous night someone 
kidnapped Lucina, an unusual young woman with the power 
to control insects, and someone he thinks of as a daughter. 
Lucina has no control over her ability and now Gustav fears it 
will cause genuine harm. 3-4 In a small village, a rumor has 
spread that a beautiful young girl often baths in the nearby 
river. Many bold young people went to see, but none came 
back. 5-6 Marina the Sea Explorer contacts the Hunters via 
a magical seagull. The bird reveals to the Hunters that, in the 
village they are heading to, a dangerous killer with whom she 
has unfinished business is hiding. As a reward for eliminating 
this threat, Marina will be willing to donate one of her prized 
treasures.

255
The Wise Fox
1-2 In a smallpox-stricken village, the Hunters receive a call 
for help from the parents of a dying child. A fox spirit who has 
often helped the village can be found in a nearby mountain 
spring, but no one has the strength for such a difficult journey. 
3-4 The Hunters find along their way a bag full of coins and, 
shortly after, a poor blind man who claims to have lost them. 
Doing the right or wrong thing will inform the Fox’s judgment 
of the Hunters. 5-6 The Hunters find a wounded, old fox 
being chased by a hunter named Galvan. The man will say 
that the creature is an evil spirit and that it must die. The Fox 
will take cover behind the Hunters.
Fire Eater the Puppeteer
1-2 Marilena, a desperate mother, runs through the town 
streets. Her two beloved twin children, Rosa and Rosario, 
disappeared from the bed they slept in without leaving a 
trace. And to think that, just the night before, the family had 
enjoyed the show of an itinerant puppeteer... 3-4 Wandering 
around a large market, the Hunters notice a disturbing 
puppet with a sad look that seems to be watching them. 
5-6 The Hunters stumble upon Fire Eater’s street show and 
realize that his amazing wireless puppets are animated by 
dark magic.
The Masked Swashbuckler
1-2 The Hunters witness the duel between the Masked 
Swashbuckler and a local noble, who is guilty of killing the 
goat of a poor peasant family for fun. 3-4 The Masked 
Swashbuckler, hired by an NPC from a past Scenario, 
tries to assassinate one of the Hunters. 5-6 The Masked 
Swashbuckler, drunk in the tavern, remembers the past and 
the betrayals he suffered. If the Hunters keep him company, 
between one slurred word and the next, the swordsman will 
let slip a Clue about the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
Orcus the Guardian
1-2 The Hunters learn that a fearsome monster is garrisoning 
a bridge across the river and killing anyone who doesn’t pay 
him a toll. In reality, Orcus has settled under the bridge to live 
far from the village and avoid doing harm. But when he went 
out to ask for alms from a passing merchant, the situation as 
always ended badly... and the rumor of a ferocious creature 
guarding the bridge spread. 3-4 The Hunters arrive in 
a square where an execution is about to take place. Orcus 
unintentionally killed the arrogant son of the local noble 
and was arrested. But the villagers plead for the good giant’s 
fate, who over the years has always done his utmost to help 
everyone. 5-6 The Hunters come across a criminal called 
Vizzini, who keeps the Masked Swashbuckler and the gigantic 
Orcus the Guardian in his service.
Goldilocks and her War Bears
1-2 The Hunters encounter a band of savagely slaughtered 
mercenaries. Jacques, their leader, will tell them of a young 
girl with three Bears who were able to hold their ground 
alone against thirty of his men. 3-4 The Hunters encounter 
Goldilocks while she is desperately seeking a cure for one of 
her Bears, who has fallen ill. 5-6 The Hunters are in a tavern 
when their waitress accidentally pours soup on a girl’s hair. 
But the waitress’ apologies are far from enough to appease 
Goldilocks’ ire.
Sinbad the Traveler of a Thousand Seas
1-2 In an old village tavern, Sinbad delights patrons with 
tales of his adventures in the Far East. All his specific and 
detailed references to magic and fantastic creatures lead 
the Hunters to understand that Sinbad isn’t merely telling 
tales. 3-4 The Hunters will encounter Sinbad in the palace 
of a local noble. The Traveler boasts of having brought the 
talented Chronicler Sherazade to Europe, and of knowing

256
which court she is gracing with her services. Sinbad is willing 
to reveal this information, but only if someone can relieve 
him of his boredom. 5-6 Sinbad contacts the Hunters for 
help. He released a fire spirit out of curiosity, but now the 
being is putting the whole region to fire and the sword. Sinbad 
has the jar to seal the spirit away, but obviously he will want 
to go with the Hunters himself to “lend a hand” and relieve 
boredom.
Event
An Event is a series of Scenes that the Storyteller can 
introduce to act as a “link” to the general plot of the 
Campaign. These Scenes are great for:
 ◆ Introducing opponents such as Sherazade and Saint 
George.
 ◆ Introducing the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
 ◆ Letting the Hunters meet the ghost of the Child Savior.
 ◆ Having the Hunters meet another Hunter or a 
Dark Presence with useful information for their 
investigation.
Some Events will be simple Scenes, such as meeting in an 
inn with a Hunter to exchange information. In this case, the 
Storyteller can take advantage of the setting to give color 
and depth to the world of Broken Europe, and to the stories 
of other Hunters or to Dark Presences.
Events with Sherazade or Saint George as protagonists, 
on the other hand, could generate meaningful Scenarios 
that must be resolved before the Hunters can resume their 
journey.
Tackling a Hunter-specific issue 
The Hunters encounter a situation related to one of their 
own. Roll a die or pick from the list:
Baba Yaga the Child Witch
1-2 A witch who claims to have killed the famous Baba 
Yaga. 3-4 A family that mistakes little Yaga for their dead 
daughter. 5-6 A lady who knows of Morgana’s secret and of 
Yaga and Merlin’s sins.
Babai the Judge
1-2 A corrupt judge who uses the law for personal gain. 
3-4 A feud between two villages that has not been settled 
for over a hundred years because no one is willing to act as a 
judge between the parties. 5-6 An elusive murderer named 
Mormo, who is said to walk in the shadows to escape the law.
Garou the Old Wolf
1-2 A young wolf from the North named Fenrir who wants to 
beat the famous Garou. 3-4 An old hunter who is after the 
trophy he most desires, the skin of the wolf that has terrified 
humans for decades. 5-6 A ruthless brigand who uses the big 
bad wolf as a cautionary tale to prey on the weak.
James the Swordsman
1-2 A crew of smugglers who want to avenge their captain, 
killed in a duel. 3-4 A monstrous beast that persecutes an 
innocent, just as it happened to James. 5-6 A famous duelist 
who, however, wins his encounters through deceit.
The Astonishing Pied Piper Without a Name
1-2 A small village where all the animals have rebelled 
against man. 3-4 A young apprentice who has heard the 
2
3

257
Piper play and now wants him to become his teacher. 5-6 
The famous composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart has heard 
the Pied Piper play a melody to cheer some street children 
and now he cannot get such perfection out of his mind. The 
musician has hired a group of bounty hunters to find the 
Pied Piper and bring him to Salzburg so they can compose a 
symphony together.
The Immortal 
1-2 The grave of the only person the Immortal ever loved.  
3-4 The consequences of the Immortal’s past raids are the 
source of daily suffering for an entire village. Is it time to 
right the old wrongs? 5-6 A young boy who is identical to the 
Immortal in his prime. He is in fact a homunculus created by 
the Immortal himself and since forgotten. 
The Woman from the Wood 
1-2 A gang of street orphans being hunted by an avid meat 
trader, famous for the “special” taste of some of his delicacies. 
3-4 An old house in the woods, which is said to literally 
devour those who enter it. 5-6 A young woman, very strong 
and without memory, with the same physical features as the 
Woman from the Wood.
Marina the Sea Explorer
1-2 A magical item that she absolutely needs to add to her 
collection, but which an old nobleman does not want to let 
go of, as it is the sole memento of his deceased wife.  3-4 A 
child who has lost his sight and cannot be fooled by Marina’s 
disguises. 5-6 Katrina, an old customer who wants to take 
revenge on Marina for the gift she received, as it did not have 
the desired effects.
Niklaus von Krampus 
1-2 A young peasant woman seems to be possessed by a 
dangerous demon, who turns out to be the brother of the one 
trapped within Niklaus. 3-4 A devil wreaks havoc at night in 
a terrified village, kidnapping children. 5-6 An old traveler 
from Borgo Maleanime unlocks Krampus’ mind, letting his 
memories pour out.
Regina, the Thief of Hearts
1-2 A prominent general whose heart Regina broke. 3-4 
A judge who wants Regina to stand trial for her past sins.  
5-6 Ace, Jack, and Spades, a group of former servants of 
Regina who are demanding revenge for having been left for 
the gallows. 
Saint George the Dragon Slayer
1-2 Many villages live in fear of a “Dragon” who is taking 
cattle and careless peasants. 3-4 An injustice perpetrated 
by the Church against the peasants: a greedy priest who uses 
his fake promises of Holy redemption to fill his belly. 5-6 A 
Paladin in the service of George who betrayed the Church for 
a good cause. 
Sun Wukong the Amazing Monkey
1-2 The animals of a traveling circus are forced to perform 
often deadly routines and weep at their wretched life. 3-4 A 
raging spirit out of control awakened from a Chinese stone. 
5-6 Angus, a survivor of Wukong’s anger when he escaped 
from his first circus, who now wants the head of the monkey 
warrior.

258
Verdoux Bluebeard 
1-2 Beatrix, Verdoux’s second wife, is not really dead, but 
has rebuilt a life with the spy Anton, one of Bluebeard’s 
historical enemies. The man faked Beatrix’s death to hurt his 
rival, hiding her in an abandoned tower. Over time, however, 
Anton fell in love with the sweetness of Beatrix, whose 
memory was shattered by the pain of being away from her 
beloved. 3-4 An old door in an abandoned building which, 
apparently, no key can open. 5-6 A secret that, if revealed, 
triggers a powerful curse, which kills whoever revealed the 
secret within a week.
Yukie Onn the Traveling Artist
1-2 One of the brothers of Akihiro, her lost love, has come 
to the West as part of a diplomatic mission. He was the 
only one who never opposed the love between Yukie Onn 
and Akihiro. Will he now hate Yukie for what she did to his 
family? 3-4 The young lover Romeo mourns his impossible 
love with Juliet, opposed by her parents, who do not consider 
him worthy due to an old family feud. 5-6 A famous dancer, 
envious of the attention that Yukie attracts, denounces her as 
a foreign spy to the local government.

259
Experience in a 
Campaign game
During a Campaign, there are two types of Interludes that 
significantly alter the general rules.
Scenario Interludes
They are exactly like those presented on page 11, with the 
only exception that it is not possible to evolve a Descriptor 
into a Specialization.
Scenario’s end Interlude
These occur automatically at the end of each Scenario 
or after an Encounter if the Storyteller believes it was 
important to the Campaign. Each Hunter receives 3 XP 
for the end of the Scenario and 1 XP for an Encounter. 
They also recover all their Soma points and remove all the 
Wounds they suffered.
Y ou can spend 5 XP to get one of the following Advance-
ments.
 Permanently acquire a Scenario Gift
The Hunter gains the Gift earned during the Scenario or 
can purchase it now if the requirements have been met 
during the story. Using the Gift as a starting point, they will 
have to create a relevant Descriptor.
 Permanently acquire an Order’s 
Treasure
The Hunter gains an Order’s Treasure as a special 
equipment. They will have to create a relevant Descriptor 
for the Treasure.
 Acquire a new Gift
The Hunter gets a new Gift related to their story, which will 
be created with the rules on page 73. Together with the 
Gift, they will have to create a relevant Descriptor.
 Evolve a Descriptor into a Specialization
The Hunter can rewrite a Descriptor to make it more 
effective and powerful. Once per Scene, when they bring 
up the Descriptor in an action, they can decide to get 4 Base 
Successes instead of 3.
 Obtain 1 additional Wound
The Hunter can take 4 Wounds before being knocked out.
 Increase the Soma value by 1
The Hunter receives 1 permanent Soma point. This 
Progression can be acquired up to 3 times.
 Have an apprentice Hunter in tow
After resolving an investigation, one who was an enemy 
could become a future member of the Order. To take this 
Advancement, an NPC encountered during a Scenario 
must have been defeated or convinced to join the Order, 
and there must be a corresponding Hunter Sheet. Having 
an apprentice Hunter grants the following benefits:
 ◆ The Player gets a second Hunter to use during Sessions. 
At each Scene they can decide which of the two Hunters 
will be leading, and will therefore act directly in the 
Scene, and which will be backstage, and will therefore

260
work behind the scenes to grant bonuses.
 ◆ The Storyteller can manage the Hunter backstage by 
creating ad hoc Scenes for them, or by making them 
return to “report” to the lead Hunter at the beginning 
of the following Scene. This way, the Hunter may 
have achieved all or part of the assigned task and the 
Storyteller, in keeping with the narrative, will be able to 
inflict Wounds, retrieve Soma or activate Gifts.
 Forge a Bond with another Hunter
Going through the investigation together created a 
special relationship between two Hunters. To take this 
Advancement, the Player of the Hunter affected by the 
Bond must agree.
Having a Bond with another Hunter grants the following 
benefits:
 ◆ Once per Scenario, a Hunter can come to the rescue of 
their Bond wherever they are, appearing in the Scene 
when needed most.
 ◆ Once per Scene, a Hunter can give 1 or more Soma to 
their Bond to help them out in a situation.
 ◆ Once per Scene, a Hunter can take 1 Wound in place 
of their Bond, but only if it makes sense in the story for 
them to be able to stand between them and the threat.
For a two-way Bond, each affected Hunter must purchase 
the Advancement.
 Forge a Bond with a Dark Presence
A Hunter can create a special relationship with a Dark 
Presence they have encountered on their travels. The 
conditions for creating the Bond must be established by the 
Player in agreement with the Storyteller. Once the Bond 
is created, the Dark Presence will become an ally of the 
Hunter, following them on their adventures.
 ◆ If the Dark Presence was an Opponent or Neutral, the 
Storyteller will have to consider whether to modify 
some of their Gifts to make them more suitable for 
their new role.
 ◆ The Hunter must create a Descriptor to represent their 
Bond with the Dark Presence. This Descriptor can be 
brought into play during actions and marked to obtain 
XP like all the others.
 ◆ The Dark Presence will do their best to support the 
Hunter, within the limits of what the Bond Descriptor 
states. However, the NPC remains under the 
Storyteller’s control.
Limitations when choosing Advancements
With the exception of Increase the Soma value by 1, each 
Advancement can only be chosen once. A Hunter can never 
have more than 4 Gifts (with the exception of those obtained 
temporarily during a Scenario), so upon obtaining the fifth 
Gift the Hunter must choose which one is being replaced 
from among those they already have. The Dark Ego Gift  
can never be replaced in any way, but its Descriptor can be 
evolved through a Specialization.

Informations on the Sheet.
 ◆ A brief background is introduced under the Hunter’s 
name, explaining who the Hunter is and why they joined 
the Order.
 ◆ Descriptors indicate what the Hunter does best and 
what their flaws are. Incorporating a Descriptor into 
the narration of a Hunter’s actions allows a Position or 
Defense Check to start from 3 Base Successes. Getting in 
trouble during the narrative, thanks to the Descriptor’s 
Downside, allows you to check them off and gain XP .
 ◆ Gifts are powerful special abilities that only Hunters of the 
Order possess. They can be used whenever the opportunity 
arises, following the rules and limitations of the Gift itself.
 ◆ The Dark Ego  represents their essence and bond to the 
fairytale world, what the Hunter was before their Breaking 
Point. Y ou can activate the Dark Ego whenever the 
Activator’s condition is triggered, or when the Hunter 
suffers or decides to inflict 1 Wound on themselves. The 
effect of the Dark Ego persists throughout the course of 
the Scene.
 ◆ Equipment indicates what gear the Hunter possesses and 
can use in order to perform their mission on behalf of the 
Order.
 ◆ The Soma value represents the Hunter’s inner strength and 
ability to tap into the true essence of a Broken Fairytale. 
Spending Soma increases the chances of a positive 
Outcome in any Position and Defense Check, or allows 
the activation of certain Gifts.
Player’s Sheet
When the Storyteller asks for a 
Position or Defense Check…
Check if one of the Hunter’s Descriptors can help them in the action being 
performed. If so, the Hunter starts with 3 Base Successes. If no Descriptor 
is relevant, only 1 Base Success  is obtained. It’s possible to obtain 
Additional Successes (for example, as a result of a Gift) or Penalties (to 
be subtracted from the total), to modify the number of Base Successes. Once 
this is done, the number of  Successes  is compared with the NPC’s or 
Threat’s Opposition Level declared by the Storyteller. 
There are 3 Levels of Opposition:
Easy 3, Medium 5, Hard 7
For an action to have a positive Outcome, you must obtain a total of 
Successes (given by the sum of Base Successes and Additional Successes) 
that is at least equal to the Opposition Level you are facing.
To get ADDITIONAL 
SUCCESSES you can...
 ◆ Spend Soma from the Hunter’s reserve to obtain 1 Additional 
Success for each point.
 ◆ Decide to roll one or more 6-sided dice. Y ou gain an Additional 
Success for each die rolled. However, if even a single die rolls 
a 1, the entire Check fails. All other results (2-6) count as 
Additional Successes.
How many dice can you roll?
Rolling dice is a gamble. Y ou can roll as many dice as you wish to 
reach the Outcome you desire. However, each additional die rolled 
increases the chances of rolling a 1, even one of which results in a 
Failure. Even when aiming for the best Outcome (Outcome with 
Increment) the total number of Successes needed will never exceed 
the Opposition Level + 2.

Find out how it all turns out...
If the result is equal to the Opposition Level:  you’ll get 
an Outcome with a Cost. The Hunter gets what they want but 
at a Cost chosen by the Storyteller. If it makes sense, the Hunter 
can also inflict 1 Wound. In a Defense Check the incoming 
problem is avoided, but with a complication decided by the 
Storyteller.
By scoring 1 point above the Opposition Level:  you’ll 
get a Standard Outcome. The Hunter gets what they want. 
In a Defense Check, the problem is handled without any 
complications. If it makes sense, the Hunter can also inflict 1 
Wound.
By scoring 2 points above the Opposition Level:  you’ll 
get an Outcome with an Increment. The Hunter succeeds better 
than expected and gains an Increment in addition to what they 
wanted. The Storyteller decides on the additional effect or, 
alternatively, the special Increment of one of the Hunter’s Gifts 
can be activated. In the case of a Defense Check, the problem is 
avoided perfectly and an Increment is also obtained. If it makes 
sense, the Hunter can also inflict 1 Wound.
Once upon a time...
If during the Scenario the Hunter gets into trouble and highlights 
the Downside of one of their Descriptors, the corresponding 
box must be marked. During an Interlude, each box marked is 
converted into 1 XP.
Declaring an Interlude
If Hunters need to catch their breath, a Player can ask 
the Storyteller for an Interlude between Scene changes. 
During the Interlude, it is possible to recover 1 Wound 
or, alternatively, recover 2 Soma. Each Hunter can ask a 
question of another Hunter to learn more about their past.
During an Interlude XP may be spent to obtain 
additional effects:
 ◆ Recover 2 Soma for each XP spent.
 ◆ Recover 1 Wound for each XP spent.
 ◆ Evolve a Descriptor into a Specialization. For the 
rest of the Scenario, this will grant 1 Additional 
Success in each Scene in which it is used for the first 
time (1 XP).
 ◆ Obtain a Scenario Gift for the duration of the 
Scenario (1 XP).
Each Interlude, however, represents a use of time. 
Remember that the Scenario, and the NPCs, may continue 
to act even when the Hunters aren’t paying attention.A Wound is inflicted...
As an effect of a failed Defense Check or as a Cost for an 
action. Each Wound must be described and annotated in 
the Bookmark. The Wound narratively affects what the 
Hunter can do and the Opposition Level of what they 
face. Whenever you suffer 1 Wound, you can activate your 
Dark Ego power for the duration of the Scene. A Player 
may decide to voluntarily inflict a Wound on the Hunter to 
instantly activate their Dark Ego.
When they suffer a third Wound, the Hunter is out of 
the game or dead. The Storyteller will reveal their fate 
according to the situation.

James  
the Swordsman

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a skilled swordsman and possess fine manners, which is why 
the Order considers me an excellent candidate to move among 
High Society. I love indulging in good food and dissolute company.
• With a Sword or a Smile:  Once per Scene you may 
perform a gesture of extreme precision with your weapon, such as 
slipping an object off someone or writing something on a surface. The 
person observing your skill will either be impressed or intimidated, 
depending on the Storyteller’s decision. Y ou receive 1 Additional 
Success when you use the emotion you have aroused to your advantage.
I have learned to face monsters and aberrations without being 
intimidated. The Beast is on my trail because, having tasted my 
flesh, it can no longer do without it… 
• Tick-Tock, Here Comes The Beast: While outdoors, you 
may ask the Storyteller for the Beast to come in search of your flesh.
The Beast is a monstrous NPC with the following characteristics: 
OL Hard 7 – (4 Wounds) 
Descriptor: I am a great and mighty beast. I overwhelm everything I 
come across on my way to snatch my prey. 
Gift – Ravenous Madness: The Beast has an insane craving for your 
flesh, and will unleash its rage on anyone standing in its way. With its 
titanic strength, it can tear down trees and walls, and hurl a man away 
with a single swipe of its huge paws. The Beast is also quite stupid 
and will leave the Scene as soon as you are out of sight. Y ou receive 
2 Additional Successes to any Defense Check against it. If the Beast 
suffers the fourth Wound, it will dissolve. However, it will regenerate 
and can be used in the next Scene.
I have been a lot of things in my life: ship captain, duellist of great 
renown, and lover of the most beautiful noblewomen. After years of 
successes and victories, a mysterious child defeated me with deception. 
This despicable boy led me into the lair of a dangerous beast and, after 
imprisoning me, left me to be eaten by the monster. I survived, though 
I lost a hand... I know for certain he who I seek is no human, and I 
joined the Order to hunt down the child I hate above all else, and thus 
have my revenge.
James
the Swordsman
I am a man of the world and nothing can really surprise me 
anymore. I never question myself. After all, I’m the best at 
what I do.
• Dark Ego – I Will have my Satisfaction: When you 
choose an opponent, there is no escape for them. Once per Scene, you

you, you savor the taste of the challenge and receive 1 Additional 
Success at each Check against them for the rest of the Scenario. 
Activator: You challenge an enemy or otherwise make clear who and 
how skilled you are.

Garou  
the Old Wolf
Descriptors and Gifts
My senses are unfailing. Nothing can escape my sense of 
smell and that is why the Order uses me as a guardian. I 
hate strong smells and loud noises because they feel like 
burning irons to acute senses.
• Find Them Forever After: Once you catch someone’s 
scent, you never forget it. During a Scene, you can spend 1 Soma to 
memorize the scent of a person you are interacting with. Y ou will 
always recognize it and it will be easy for you to follow its traces 
even after some time. If you concentrate, you can hear a noise even 
at great distances. When someone approaches you, the Storyteller 
will warn you long before anyone else in the Scene would notice.
A true predator knows how to appear as the most helpless 
of lambs. I know how to disguise myself. My confidence is 
boundless and I often tend to underestimate danger, ready 
to risk anything for a tasty bite.
• Wolf Among the Lambs: Y ou may spend 1 Soma to 
assume the shape of a common human. Create a Descriptor that 
represents you and includes at least one of these words: Old, 
Helpless, Weak. In your human form you can speak and act 
without problems, but, as soon as you use your Dark Ego, you will 
immediately lose the effect of Wolf Among the Lambs. Y ou receive 
1 Additional Success when you use the Descriptor you created to 
deceive someone and catch them off guard.
I am a mountain wolf and far more cunning than the stupid villagers who 
tremble at the mere sight of me. I have lived proudly at the head of my pack, 
leading a thousand raids and eating succulent, tender morsels of human flesh. 
But I am not so old I cannot face those who hate me the most, though I may be 
hunted and reviled. Now that I’m part of the Order, I use my strength to defend 
the weak hunters who are my companions, knowing that with a single bite I 
could end their insignificant lives.
I am fiercer and more powerful than any human and my fangs 
can pierce even metal. My proud wolf spirit is everything to me.
• Dark Ego - The Big Bad Wolf: Y ou are hardened by a lifetime 
in the great mountains and your skill is matched only by your courage. 
When you unleash your rage, create a Descriptor for it that includes 
at least one of these words: Strong, Fierce, Unstoppable. Y ou can 
employ the strength of three men, gnaw through stone as easily as flesh, 
or break down any barrier. When you act using this special Descriptor 
for Position Checks, you get 1 Additional Success. Once per Scene, you 
can unleash your rage and receive 3 Additional Successes instead of 1.
Activator: You assume the form of a Wolf or reveal yourself for what you 
really are, scaring off weak humans.

Descriptors and Gifts
With my pipe I can play any melody and touch the hearts of 
living beings. Art needs time to express itself.
• Soul Music: When you play your pipes you can spend 1 
Soma to sing a special melody. Choose from:
1. Imparting an emotion:  Create a Descriptor that represents 
the emotional state you wish to convey. Everybody present will 
receive that Descriptor for the current Scene. Y ou may have to

2. Bewitching animals: Y ou may give simple commands to 
animals that hear your melody. If they show an aggressive attitude, 
you have to make a successful Position Check for your melody to 
take effect. As long as you are playing, you may ask the animals to 
do simple actions such as moving or following a simple command.
I am a skilled acrobat and musician. My talent is unrivaled 
and the Order has chosen me for my ability to capture people’s 
attention and stimulate their emotions. When I am in the grip 
of artistic inspiration, I am totally divorced from reality.
