grace, you always put on a great show. As an additional action, you 
can spend 1 Soma and decide on one of the following effects:
1. Y ou can make an incredible leap, many feet high, without 
anyone being able to stop you.
2. Y ou may take an object, even if it is worn by an enemy.
3. Y ou can perform a minor action with a stunt, such as opening 
a door or flipping a switch, and then return exactly to where you 
started.
As an artist I have traveled to many places and I know many 
myths and legends. I’m hungry for knowledge and I’m fascinated 
by everything I don’t know.

The Astonishing
Pied Piper       Without a Name
I have always made my art my reason for living. I can play so skillfully that it might as well be magic. I 
have learned to use the power of music to reach into the souls of both humans and animals. Unfortunately, 
I have lost count of the number of times I have performed dishonorable acts while pursuing the perfect 
melody. Having been guilty of various crimes and having taken revenge on those who mocked my sincere 
love for music, I serenely waited for my execution. The Order freed me in exchange for a promise to 
serve them and put my skills to work in the fight against Darkness. It was in the darkest moments that I 
glimpsed the perfect melody, so perhaps as a Hunter, I can find it and make it my own.

Babayaga
The Child Witch

I  am a child witch, and my power is equal only to my ambition. I did not have a childhood, for I was born 
already aware of my abilities. To most, I seem cynical and cruel. That is because they do not understand 
that they are pawns, insignificant pieces in the bloody games that witches and warlocks play. The Order 
does not look down on my age, which is why it has given me the leadership I deserve.
Like any respectable sorceress, I carry my trusty bag full of herbs, 
poisons, and all kinds of magic tools. My childlike appearance is often 
useful, but sometimes it leads others to underestimate me. I hate that.

1. Animals and ordinary people are tormented by your presence and will 
flee in terror. 
2. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success if you use your magic to punish someone 
for breaking a promise or agreement you made earlier.
3. In the area around you, you can summon daylight or darkness.
Activator: Scare ordinary people or dispense punishment through your 
magic.

I am a sorceress and my magical powers allow me to do

Regina
The Thief Of Hearts

I was born to be a leader and I have always surrounded myself with faithful followers ready 
to satisfy my every whim. Lacking the benefit of noble birth, the power I held was pried from the 
hands of those less deserving to wield it, no matter the cost. I ruled armies of thugs, and broke 
the hearts of both men and women. After each conquest, I set out in search of a bigger challenge. 
Because there is still so much out there that should be rightfully mine, I always want more. The 
Order has given me a new purpose, a new perspective, and new creatures to enslave.
I have an enviable charm and love to dress up to fit the role I have 
chosen. Even when I try to restrain myself, I never go unnoticed.

right way to do things. During the Scene, as an additional action, you can 
choose another character who will be issued a 
command. In the case of 
an ally, if they do exactly what you tell them, they will receive 1 Additional

from using one of their Gifts. Once per Scene, you can shout, drawing 
everyone’s attention to you. For a few moments, they will pause to listen 
to what you have to say.
Activator: Take control of a situation and impose your decisions by 
dramatically drawing attention to yourself.

my way. I will kill, humiliate, or conquer anything that stands

Thanks to my contacts and charisma, I always have an ace 
up my sleeve: followers ready to help me and allies who owe

After years of honorable service as State Inquisitor of the Republic of Venice, my body and 
spirit have undergone a rare mutation and fear is now unknown to me. People have always 
called me Bogeyman, fearing the justice that I represented. Perhaps my own absence of 
fear made me even more terrifying in the eyes of mortals. The Order has given me a new 
assignment because even in the fight against darkness, someone needs to be ready to judge 
what is right and what is wrong.
My presence intimidates people, touching the fear that dwells deep in 
their hearts. The presence of children and their innocent way of looking 
at the world makes me uneasy.
• Dark Ego - If you don’t Stop, I’ll Call the Bogeyman!: 
Y ou are like a sinuous shadow that can reach anywhere. During a Scene, 
you can arrive at a location by covering any distance and overcoming 
any obstacle if one of your allies or an NPC calls your name. When you 
manifest your true nature, everyone who witnesses it will be frightened 
and will not act against you unless you threaten them first. The Storyteller 
may ask you for a Position Check to frighten particularly powerful 
characters. Receive 1 Additional Success on all Defense Checks you make 
against the actions of someone who is now fearful.
Activator: Enter or leave a shadow by manifesting your true nature and 
scaring someone.

I am good at understanding, evaluating, and grasping every 
hidden detail, which is why the Order wanted me as a judge. 
I am cold, methodical, and cannot stand when plans change

My body is made of pure shadow and I can become intangible

Descriptors and Gifts
I am incredibly strong. Despite my petite looks, I am tougher than 
the sturdiest of men. I struggle to control my strength and often 
end up damaging things unintentionally.
• The Woman from the Wood:  Y ou receive 1 Additional 
Success to any Position Check that requires you to resort to your 
strength. If you eliminate a living being by causing a Wound, you can 
do it in an extremely disturbing way. Anyone who witnesses it, at the 
discretion of the Storyteller, will lower their Opposition Level by 1 for

Sometimes I manifest strange abilities, the legacy of a past that I 
no longer remember. But, when I need it, the world around me 
shapes itself to protect and shelter me. I hate being out in the open 
and being the center of attention.
• Home Sweet Home: Y ou can spend 1 Soma to draw on the 
strange powers of the witch you once were. When you do so, choose

1. Create a shelter for one person, perfectly camouflaged within the 
environment. It is very difficult to discover your shelter, though some 
NPCs can come close. At the Storyteller’s discretion, a Defense Check 
may be made to see if you’re discovered.
2. Reshape a plant. Y ou can, for instance, create a ladder with the 
branches of a tree or weave branches to act as a shield.
3. Make something disgusting/rotten edible, giving it a sweet and 
enticing appearance. Anyone other than you who consumes your 
sweet recovers 1 Wound.
My memory is gone. The first thing I remember is the Order finding me, wandering in 
the woods naked and covered in blood. I possess extraordinary strength and a feral instinct, 
such that any civilized society ought to chain me like a monster. I know that in my past is a sin 
so great, my memory could not bear the weight of it. But, I want to remember and perhaps 
by serving the Order I will be able to do so. I have a natural soft spot for children, and I can’t 
stand to see them get hurt. The Order uses me as a weapon in the most serious matters when 
it is necessary to act decisively and demonstrate that a Hunter is a force to be reckoned with.

Gifts.
I m a sorcerer who has found the key to immortality. Long life has only whetted my appetite for power 
and in its pursuit, I have been stained by every sin.  The secret behind my unending existence is that I 
have hidden my soul, removing it from my body. Unfortunately, the Order found its hiding place and now 
it is held beyond my reach in a fortified church, leaving me no choice but to submit to their commands. 
Guilty of one thousand and one crimes, the Order demands I balance each with one thousand and one acts 
of virtue. According to those foolish preachers, they are leading me down the path of redemption. In truth, 
the Order treats me as a servant, giving to me only the most thankless tasks, knowing that I can not be 
subdued by death. I remain humble and silent, waiting for the day when I will discover where they have 
hidden my soul. Then I will unleash my revenge and annihilate the entire Papacy...
My body is but a tool which I can manipulate at will, far beyond common 
human limits. In spite of my old and emaciated appearance, I feel neither fatigue 
nor pain. With matters of the flesh no longer of concern, I value beautiful things 
and works of art above anything else.
• Dark Ego - The Immortal:  When you suffer 1 Wound, you can 
immediately spend 1 Soma to remain conscious, allowing you to suffer many 
Wounds without dying. Y ou are in full control of your body, even parts that have 
been cut off. Y ou can see through a torn eye or continue to use your severed hand, 
so don’t skimp on describing a Wound when you suffer it. At the end of the Scene 
in which you activated your Dark Ego, you recover all the Wounds suffered except 
the first one. Y our body regenerates and the severed parts are reattached in a horrid 
spectacle that few have the stomach to watch.
Activator: Use your indifference to pain or death to your advantage, revealing your 
true nature for anyone to see.
Equipment 
Poor clothes / A worn out sickle, a reminder of times past / A 
small bag with some precious items that you have collected 
in your adventures / A key that opens the door of one of your 
many secret refuges / A papal ring, symbol of your indenture 
to the Church / A little toy, given to you by whoever you 
helped with your first good deed.
Wounds 
1.
2.
3.
Extra 
Wound.
XP
Soma
 7

Marina 
the Sea 
Explorer

I am a traveler, and, over the years, I have visited every location known to man. Being a 
traveling merchant has allowed me to easily hide my talents as a witch from prying eyes. Year 
after year, I have accumulated all kinds of magical and mundane wealth. But the Order found 
me and forced me to sign the most one-sided contract of my entire career as a trader. Now 
I must put my skills and my charisma at the service of the Order, trying to earn as much as 
possible to redeem the enormous debt that they have made me incur through deceit.
I have a massive collection of magical relics and treasures. I am very 
stingy with my collection and constantly seeking to add to my wealth 
so I can redeem my freedom.
• Dark Ego - Treasures of the Sea:  At the beginning of 
the Scenario, you receive 2 Treasure Tokens. When you show off your 
Treasures, spend 1 Treasure Token and 1 Soma, and create a Descriptor 
that represents it such as Wolf-Slaying Dagger or Lens of Truth. Receive 1 
Additional Success for any Check in which your Treasure may be helpful. 
Alternatively, spend 1 Token to draw a Potion from your pouch to give 
to someone. Create a Descriptor to represent its magical effect such as 
Seeing in the dark or Extraordinary strength. Whoever drinks it will be 
granted the Descriptor you created for 1 Scene, plus 1 Additional Success 
on the first action in which they use the advantage given by the potion. 
When you obtain a new treasure or close a favorable deal, you receive 1 
Treasure Token.
Activator: Turn a request for help or a conversation into a negotiation, 
making it clear how amazing your treasures are.
XP
Soma
 6
Equipment 
Exotic and colorful dresses / A large bag full of treasures / A 
travel bag / Rings and fine jewelry made out of seashells / 
A vial of sea water / Cosmetics and perfumes.
Wounds 
1.
2.
3.
Extra 
Wound.
Marina 
the Sea 
Explorer
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a sea witch, and as such, I have the power to shape water 
and move it at will. I hate enclosed places, cut off from the 
presence of my beloved sea.
• Sea Magic:  Y ou can unleash your witch powers and 
control seawater, manipulating it as if it were solid. Y ou can use 
it as a weapon or as an object to support your actions, giving it the 
consistency of a whip that can reach up to thirty feet. When you 
act, you can take a second supporting action, such as picking up 
something, moving someone you want to defend, or hindering an 
enemy’s movement thanks to the water you manipulate. Y ou can 
also use this Gift with fresh water, but you will need to spend 1 
Soma to do so.
Nobody knows my true form, which I hide under an appearance 
of eternal youth. I can change shape to some extent, while still 
remaining in human form. I am hopelessly attracted to all 
forms of beauty and I often get lost in contemplating it.
• Mutable Appearance: Once per Scene you can change 
your appearance. Create a Descriptor that represents your new 
appearance. Nobody can pierce your deception, except through 
magical means or Gifts that allow one to discover the truth. In 
addition, you may spend 1 Soma to take the specific appearance of 
a person you have already met, mimicking their voice and manners 
as well. Y ou can keep your new appearance until the Scene is over.

Niklaus
   von Krampus

Niklaus
von Krampus
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a skilled exorcist. I know how to identify and fight 
evil through prayer and the sacred arts. Despite what I 
am, I hate holy places and manifestations of faith and 
religion, as they cause the demon inside me to stir.
• Exorcist: When you face a clearly supernatural being, 
you gain 1 Additional Success on all Checks. Y ou can also 
invoke sacred rites and prayers to disturb the evil you face. If a 
subject, artifact, or supernatural manifestation uses a Gift that 
you know of or have already seen in action, you can spend 1 
Soma to prevent its use. Each time you activate this Gift, roll 1 
die. If you roll a 5 or a 6, you gain 1 Devil Token.
I am good at understanding people and helping them. 
Now, thanks to the demon that dwells in me, I can even 
perceive the dark side of people. Every so often, however, 
the demon takes over, making me behave strangely in 
front of ordinary people.
• The Inner Demon: Once per Scene, you can release 
the inner demon, whether that’ d be yours or someone else’s. In 
your case, create a Descriptor that represents an emotion that 
includes the word Anger, Envy, or Wickedness. Y ou receive 2 
Additional Successes the first time you use this Descriptor for 
a Position Check. If you unleash someone else’s inner demon, 
the Storyteller will create a Descriptor for them that showcases 
their hidden, dark side. When using this Gift on particularly 
powerful individuals, the Storyteller may request a Position 
Check from you to succeed. Each time you use this Gift, you 
receive 1 Devil Token.
I am a devoted clergyman who has always dedicated his life to helping others. Over the years I have 
become a talented exorcist and this vocation of mine led me to the remote village of Borgo Maleanime. 
There, I fought a particularly insidious demon, who concealed itself among the ranks of a gang of young 
thugs who merely pretended to be demons to scare people. It was a tough fight, but I finally got the better 
of the fiend. Or so I thought. Something in the exorcism did not go as expected and now the demon dwells 
inside me, sowing fear and pain whenever it re-emerges. The Order welcomed me into its ranks as an 
exorcist and I gladly accepted this role, with the hope that the missions entrusted to me would allow me to 
find a way to rid myself, once and for all, of that which hides within me.
The demon in me grants me its power, allowing me to act with savage strength 
and speed. It is not easy to contain this ferocity and, often, I end up hurting or 
destroying things I did not wish to.
• Dark Ego - The Demon Revealed: While generally retaining control, 
every now and then you have to allow the demon to manifest and unleash its 
anger. Create a Descriptor that represents your demonic form and that includes 
at least one of these words: Monstrous , Fierce, Beastly. Y our physical abilities go 
far beyond those of a mere mortal, but you will behave like a devil, enjoying the 
evil and fear you inspire. When you act using this special Descriptor, you gain 1 
Additional Success. Y ou can spend 1 Devil Token to inflict 1 additional Wound or 
to spend as Soma for a Check. Once per Scenario, the Storyteller can spend 1 Devil 
Token to activate this Gift against your will.
Activator: Give free rein to your demonic side or listen to the sound of bells or cowbells 
for at least one minute.
Equipment 
An old cassock, worn out by many travels / Exorcism vestments 
and tools / An old pouch where you find things that the demon 
takes when it is in control (often, things with unclear origins 
you don’t wish to know about) / A shepherd’s curved staff of 
wood and iron to beat the devil with / Precious and small relics 
received as offerings / An old and time-worn bible.

Soma
 6

The Holy
Dragon

Descriptors and Gifts
I have dedicated my life to slaying monsters and 
aberrations, honing my body and my skill for that 
purpose. My strict discipline makes me rarely inclined to 
forgive the mistakes of others.
• The Dragon Slayer:  Y ou’re skilled at slaying 
monsters and demonic creatures in the name of faith. Y ou 
start each Scenario with 1 Fervor Token. When facing an 
enemy that is obviously monstrous, if you get a successful 
Outcome on a Position Check, you can spend 1 Fervor Token 
to inflict 1 additional Wound. When such an enemy attempts 
to overwhelm you using one of their Gifts, you can spend 
1 Fervor Token to receive 2 Additional Successes to your 
Defense Check. When you suffer 1 Wound, you receive 1 
Fervor Token.
I have vowed to eradicate all evil from this world and 
nothing can escape my inquisitive gaze. I hate all secrets, 
for I know that the seed of evil may grow within anyone.
• Agent of the Papacy: When you speak to a crowd  
you can infuse their hearts with the light of faith. Spend 1 
Fervor Token and you can create a Descriptor that represents 
a feeling of faith, anger, and a desire for justice against evil. 
Alternatively, create a Descriptor that represents trust and 
submission in such faith. The crowd will act accordingly for 
the rest of the Scene, but particularly powerful NPCs may be 
immune to your words. Receive 1 Additional Success on any 
Check you make using the sentiment you inspired. When you 
publicly expose evil, you receive 1 Fervor Token.
I am the most skilled and trusted agent of the Papacy and as such, it is my responsibility 
to make sure that the fight against darkness never falters. As a child, I fell into a river and 
almost drowned, but was saved by a creature that I later discovered was a dragon. When I 
grew up, I took holy vows and joined the Church. In doing so, I discovered that what I believed 
to be my savior was actually a creature of darkness, ready to steal my soul. I vowed to fight 
the darkness, suppressing the feeling of gratitude toward the dragon that saved me, which has 
always plagued me. Such weakness represents a crack through which evil could slither in. No 
mercy for monsters, no forgiveness for those who would join and serve the forces of evil.
George The Holy
Dragon Slayer
I am a saint in the eyes of ordinary people and no door remains closed 
for me. My word is that of the Church itself. What I say is not to be 
questioned.
• Dark Ego - The Saint:  When you channel sacred divine power, 
everyone bows their heads. Ordinary people will show you reverence, while 
weaker enemy NPCs (at the Storyteller’s discretion) won’t target you unless 
you’re the only available option, nor will they try to get in your way. In 
your presence, anyone possessing powers linked to the supernatural lowers 
their Opposition Level by 1. Y our holy aura emits an intense light that can 
illuminate the whole area around you at your will.  
Activator: Invoke holy divine power by reproaching all sinners and providing 
an example of purity and righteousness.

 6

himself / My proud war stallion / A cross with the Keys of Saint 
Peter engraved, as a universal symbol of power / 
Aldebrand, 
my trusty squire and servant / Papal papers and seals / A cloak 
of fine workmanship.

Sun Wukong
the Amazing Monkey
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a skilled guardian. My body is strong and hardened, 
my senses sharp and alert. Years of captivity have made me 
submissive, and I struggle not to follow every order I am 
given.
• Guardian Disciple: Y ou can intercept a danger before it 
overwhelms your ally. Y ou may spend 1 Soma to get between an 
ally and a danger by standing in their place. Receive 1 Additional 
Success to any Defense Check you have to perform doing so. Once 
per Scenario, you can ignore 1 Wound you suffer.
I am stronger and faster than any human and I am a highly 
skilled fighter. I don’t shy away from a challenge because I 
always believe I can win.
• Monkey King: Y ou are able to perform athletic feats 
unthinkable to a mere mortal. Y ou may spend 1 Soma and choose 
an effect:
1. Y ou can jump incredibly far, up to thirty feet. Y ou can bring 
another person with you by carrying them on your shoulders. Y ou 
also ignore any damage related to a fall. 
2. Use your tail to manipulate an object and perform an additional 
action.
3. Ignore any physical impediments to movement by using your 
impressive skills.
I have no memory of my life before my captivity, although I often dream of the forests 
and snow-capped peaks of my beloved China. I grew up as a circus animal at London’s 
Royal Circus but, deep within I carry the conviction that I am destined for something else. I 
rebelled by killing all my captors, fleeing across a vast and labyrinthine city. When the Order 
reached me, I thought it was the end, but the Hunters took me with them as their guardian and 
defender. The more I live as a Hunter, the closer I draw to transcendence. Now I am aware 
that, someday, I will discover who I really am.
My essence is magical, and this makes me resistant to enchantments that 
might snare stupid humans. My spiritual presence is imposing, and I struggle 
to hide what I am, especially in the eyes of those accustomed to magic.
• Dark Ego - Descendant of the Awakening of the Void and 
the Emptiness: When you release your true power, your body emits 
a divine light and no one can stop you. Take 1 Wound to get 2 Additional 
Successes to any physical Checks you have to perform. For the rest of the 
Scene spells have little effect on you and you get 2 Additional Successes to any 
Defense Check you make to protect yourself from arcane effects. When you 
deal a hit to someone, you can spend 1 Soma to inflict 1 additional Wound. 
The effects of this Gift cease if you behave in a composed and obedient 
manner, as your role as guardian dictates.
Activator: Act with passion and strength, showing everyone the incredible 
power that lies within your body.
Equipment 
A circus stick with three segments that I can extend / Old 
and worn circus clothes / An old golden diadem so tight 
on my forehead that it cannot be removed / A sheet with 
the drawing of a Chinese landscape, gift from a child who 
watched one of my shows.

Verdoux Bluebeard

I am a French aristocrat, called upon by my sovereign to manage the most devious and disreputable 
matters. I have lived an unconventional life, between accomplishing impossible tasks and keeping 
unspeakable secrets. It has opened my eyes to the world beyond the prosaic limits to which most are 
accustomed. In an effort to escape my adventurous life, I have more than once tried to start a family. 
However, my efforts have always ended in ruin, a price I must pay for the secrets I keep. I have lost six 
lovers, each killed by enemies and dangers that I naively hoped I could exclude from my life. That is why I 
volunteered for the Order, took an oath to weed out the darkness in this world, and sought revenge for the 
lives that were stolen from me.
Under the facade of a respectable man hides a skilled, unscrupulous 
problem-solver. I know how to kill and how to act with decisiveness. 
Everyone has done something they are not proud of and I can’t stand 
seeing the hypocrites who pretend as if they are morally superior to 
others.
• Dark Ego - The Unscrupulous Assassin:  Y ou may appear as a civilized gentleman, but appearances can be deceiving. When 
necessary, you will not hesitate to kill. If you eliminate an NPC by inflicting 
a final Wound, you can also immediately eliminate a second Minor NPC. 
When you successfully defend yourself from a violent action, you can 
immediately eliminate a Minor NPC in response. Either way, the target 
must be within range of your wrath. After inflicting 1 Wound to someone, 
you can move freely as an additional action, allowing you to reach your 
true goal without anyone preventing you from doing so.
Activator: Reveal your dark side by injuring someone or acting aggressively 
despite your outwardly calm and staid appearance.
XP
Soma
 6

Nobleman’s clothes / A long black cape / A large 
bunch of keys / A fine dueling blade / Snuff of 
excellent quality / A few doses of deadly Aqua 
Tofana / A large amount of coins and precious 
trinkets / A heart-shaped locket hanging from a gold 
chain, within which are engraved the names of my 
lost loves.

Verdoux

Descriptors and Gifts
My specialty is problem solving and I have a talent for analyzing 
every situation, allowing me to find resources and discover 
clues. Managing danger requires calm and concentration, 
and so I hate acting precipitously.
• Problem Solver: If you’ve seen it once, you can do it 
yourself even better. Y ou may spend 1 Soma to replicate the effect 
of a Gift from a Hunter or NPC you witnessed during the Scenario. 
Particular Gifts may still be off limits or only yield a part of their 
effect at the Storyteller’s discretion. Y ou cannot replicate more than 
one Gift at a time. Once per Scene, you may ask the Storyteller to 
reveal to you a Gift of an NPC that you had time to study.
I am an educated man who has studied the arts and sciences. 
I use this knowledge to uncover truths and reveal mysteries. I 
hate secrets, but only when I don’t know them.
• The Key to Each Door: Once per Scene, you can use one 
of the magic keys you own. Choose an effect. 
1. Open a closed door, no matter how many locks or defenses it 
has.
2. If a subject touches your key, discover the strongest feeling they 
are experiencing. 
3. Close and lock a door so that no conventional methods will be 
able to open it. 
4. If you put your key in the mouth of a dead person you will see 
some of their memories. The Storyteller will describe them to you, 
focusing on your areas of interest.

Yukie Onn
the Traveling
Artist

I am a traveling artist who has known fame and honor in my homeland, Japan. That prestige meant nothing, 
however, when I found true love with Akihiro, son of a powerful noble family. When they forbade him from seeing 
me, we fled together to the remote region of Ezo. Even there we were not beyond the reach of Akihiro’s family and 
they pursued us into the high, snow-capped mountains. We had escaped every assassin, but could not outwit the 
snowstorm that trapped us. I survived, but only because Akihiro sacrificed the warmth of his life to save mine.  
But the cold has left me changed. It has become part of me and now the only thing that can make me feel warm 
is the hot blood of others. After taking revenge on all those who took Akihiro from me, I fled far away, using my 
talent for art to gain prestige and the protection of wealthy western nobles. Now, in the service of the Order, I 
save others from suffering the injustice that I have experienced, by punishing selfishness and wickedness.
My body is pure ice and, because of this, I can manipulate ice as I please. 
However, I hate to manifest my power because it is dangerous to those 
around me and, as an artist, my purpose is to make people smile.
• Dark Ego - Ice-Cold: Y ou have control over your body temperature 
and, consequently, you can freeze the environment or objects in your 
immediate vicinity. When you manipulate ice, choose an effect by creating 
a Descriptor that represents it such as: Frozen Door or Aura of Frost and 
spend 1 Soma to manifest it for the whole Scene. As an additional effect, 
your frost can inflict 1 Wound on those who interact with it or make what 
you freeze incredibly fragile or incredibly hard. Whoever exploits the effects 
of frost in their favor gains 1 Additional Success for their action. Y our frost 
has no direct effect on living things.
Activator: Shed your disguise as a simple artist and reveal your true, bitterly 
cold form or react passionately when fear and intimidation are used for selfish 
reasons.
XP
Soma
 6
Equipment 
A travel kimono / Stage kimono, make-up, and stage effects / A 
small ice sphere that never melts / A butterfly-shaped hair clip, 
a reminder of my lost love Akihiro / A finely crafted Shamisen, 
a traditional stringed instrument / Several daggers hidden in the 
Kimono / A pair of fine fans.

the Traveling

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a highly skilled artist. Music, dance, and painting hold no 
mysteries for me. My otherworldly charm captivates ordinary 
people, always drawing attention to me.
• Otherworldly Charm:  When you perform or flaunt 
your talents, all eyes will be on you. Receive 1 Additional Success 
for each Position Check you make to attract attention or charm 
someone. If you get an Outcome with an Increment, you may 
declare that a subject who has seen you has fallen in love with you. 
The Storyteller will create a specific Agenda for them that leads 
them to seek your attention.
I cannot feel pain and my frail appearance hides surprising 
physical abilities. Blood is the only thing that can warm me 
and its smell alone makes me shiver.
• Blood-Hot: Once per Scene, you can consume a person’s 
blood when inflicting 1 non-fatal Wound. Y ou recover 1 Wound, 
1 Soma and receive some information about your victim such as a 
memory, an emotion or their heart’s desire. When you drink blood 
roll a die: if you roll a 1, you lose control and you will kill your 
victim by draining their blood, unless someone stops you.

Cunning Machiavelli the Fox 
and Sly Bischeri
the Cat

We are two boys who survived and thrived on the street through trickery and deceit. 
Unfortunately, we made a terrible mistake by choosing a weird old lady as a mark. She 
turned out to be a goddamn witch. The witch cursed us… And from that day on, every twelve 
hours, one becomes a Fox and the other a Cat. Bound by this spell, we found a way to make 
a lot of money, deceiving stupid nobles and rich merchants. The Order has noticed us and 
taken us under its wing, with the promise of breaking the curse that afflicts us.
Cunning Machiavelli 
the Fox and Sly 
Bischeri the Cat
Cunning’s exclusive Descriptor and Gift
I have a natural talent for telling lies, which the Order often uses to its 
advantage. In spite of my origins, I know a great deal about many things 
because lying requires planning and preparation. I’m so used to spinning 
nonsense that I often lie even when I shouldn’t.
• Believe in Miracles:  Statements are true until proven otherwise. 
Once per Scene, you can tell everyone something that will be accepted 
without question, until the lie is uncovered. The deception will be obvious to 
your victims at the end of the Scene or once exposed. Receive 1 Additional 
Success on any Check in which you exploit the lie you have created.
Sly’s exclusive Descriptor and Gift
A life on the road has taught me to be a skilled thief, which is why 
the Order uses me to do its dirty work. If I can make a profit out of a 
situation, I’ll try to. “Everyone for themselves” is my motto.
• Rogue: Y ou always make sure you go unnoticed. By pretending to 
be weak during a Scene, NPCs will overlook you as a target unless you have 
previously acted against them. By spending 1 Soma, you can flee a Scene or a 
situation wherever there is a crowd. No one will be able to stop you.
Soma
 6
Note: During the night, use Cunning’s Descriptor, Gift, and equipment, while Sly will be a cat. During the day, use 
Sly’s Descriptor, Gift, and equipment, while Cunning will be a fox.
Common Descriptors and Gifts
I will stop at nothing to get what I want, and it only takes me a few seconds 
to evaluate what is happening and find the right way to take advantage of it. I 
hate acting alone because there’s no one to take the blame.
• Partner in Crime: Y ou are always ready to seize the right opportunity. 
Once per Scene, if one of your allies acts with your support, they receive 1 
Additional Success to their Check. Additionally, once per Scene, if one of your 
allies achieves an Outcome with an Increment after they have completed their 
action, you may immediately take an additional action.
Like it or not, we are bound by magic. We can share thoughts, each see 
what the other sees, and thanks to this we can work together to cheat others. 
Whoever is in animal form hates not being able to communicate and often 
annoys the other out of spite.
• Dark Ego - The Fox and the Cat:  The curse that binds you makes 
you a perfect team. At night as Cunning or during the day as Sly, you are a human 
and make use of the full support of your partner in his animal form. Y our 
partner can act individually to accomplish the task you give him. Y ou share the 
same Wounds and the same Soma. Whoever is in human form can spend 1 Soma 
to use the Gift of his accomplice. Once per Scene, if your partner is there to help 
you, you receive 2 Additional Successes on a Position Check.
Activator: Trick or deceive someone, taking advantage of the support of your 
trusted partner in crime.
XP
Cunning’s Equipment
Comfortable and light clothes / A set of small knives that I hide everywhere 
/ Cards, stamps, and other counterfeit objects / Some completely fake coins and jewelry / A volume of the famous Biblioteca Universale Sacro-
Profana Encyclopedia, which always reveals what I need to know.
Sly’s Equipment
Worn-out and lived-in clothes / A long, sharp knife / An old stick to 
pretend to be blind / An old purse with a hole in it / A sack full of junk 
that I collect while thieving.

3
Introduction
This book introduces the Broken Ones, the “heroes” from the original tales 
who were turned into the main antagonists in the world of Broken Tales, and later 
subdued by the Order into new Hunters following the events of the Core Book. 
Y ou won’t find any difference in the mechanics compared to regular Hunters, 
but it is important to take a few things into consideration when adding 
Broken Ones to the game.
Creating a Broken One Hunter
The creation of a Hunter from a Broken One follows the rules 
presented on page 69 of Broken Tales’ Core Book. The difference 
is that the story used to bring them to their breaking point 
won’t be the original tale, but rather the one in the Scenario 
they come from. In fact, creating a Broken One means 
“breaking a story that has already been broken. ” In some 
ways, this is easier and faster since one doesn’t have to 
reimagine the whole background story of a classic 
Hunter. In short: to create a Broken One, there 
needs to be a Scenario or a Broken story involving 
them.

4
Broken Ones during a Broken 
Tales’ Campaign
A Broken One is a natural fit for a long-term Campaign 
because Players need to have solved a given Scenario to 
unlock the new Hunter tied to it. Keeping in mind that 
the Players may not want to leave their original Hunters 
behind, there are a few ways to introduce the Broken Ones 
in the game.
An Alternative Group of Hunters
During the Campaign, an interesting clue may emerge 
from one of the Broken Ones captured by the Hunters. The 
new lead may be located far away from the group’s position, 
and the Order could instruct a second group of Hunters 
that includes a few Broken Ones. The Storyteller can then 
offer to let the Players play one or more Sessions with this 
different group of Hunters. At the end of those Scenarios, 
all the earned XP can be transferred to the Players’ main 
Hunters. 
Apprentice Hunters
Once they have been defeated in their Scenario and 
integrated into the Order, the Advancement option Have 
an apprentice Hunter in tow (page 259 of the Broken Tales 
Core Book) is perfect to introduce a Broken One in the 
game as a Hunter. 
Broken Ones as NPCs
It should be easy for the Storyteller to convert a Broken One 
Hunter into an Ally Dark Presence with a little adaptation 
so they can join the Hunters during their journey.

5
The Order’s 
Treasury
In its frantic quest for control over the Broken Tales, the 
Order established the Treasury shortly after its foundation. In the 
Principality of Transylvania, the fortified church of Valea Viilor was 
chosen for its series of secret rooms, perfect for storing objects and 
magic that would be incomprehensible to the common man. The 
management of the Treasury is entrusted to Hansen, known as the 
Swineherd. He is a nobleman fallen into disgrace, but very skilled 
at making items with incredible properties. An order of knights 
faithful to the Papacy watches over Valea Viilor so that only the 
Hunters and the most trusted agents of the Church can enter the 
secret halls, where all kinds of artifacts are accumulated and kept 
hidden from the world. The most daring Hunters often take one of 
the Treasures, one that may help them in their mission based on its 
properties. Possession of an Order’s Treasure is only ever temporary, 
except in rare cases when a particularly deserving Hunter will be 
able to keep the Treasure for themselves permanently.

6
Hansen known as the Swineherd
Inspired by The Swineherd (Neutral)
Once upon a time, there was a young nobleman with a great passion for craftsmanship and the 
creation of miraculous objects. Unfortunately, no matter how hard he worked, his creations were 
never as successful as he had hoped, or perhaps the problem was that the acclaim he received 
always fell short of what he thought he deserved. Over the years, Hansen squandered all his 
wealth, refusing offers of marriage and requests for his work, driven mad by his dream of the 
“perfect creation. ” With a new-found resolve, Hansen disguised himself as a humble swineherd, 
traveling from city to city to experiment with his creations, often with deadly results for the 
poor souls who happened to be within range. Captured by the Order, the Papacy gave 
Hansen a new purpose as a Hunter, deciding to put his talent at the service of the 
fight against darkness. Since that day, obsessed with all the wonders of the Treasury, 
Hansen studies every object that is delivered by the Hunters, frantically searching 
for a perfection that can never be achieved.
Descriptor: I am a skilled craftsman and I know every Order’s Treasure down 
to the smallest detail. I can dismantle any object with a few precise gestures of my 
hand.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: To thoroughly study every object the Treasury holds, as well as any Hunter 
who comes through.
Gift - Master Craftsman: Given a few minutes of study, Hansen can understand 
the functionality of any object related to the Broken Tales. His deep knowledge of 
magic allows him to understand the abilities of those in front of him, enabling him 
to figure out their Descriptors and Gifts after a short time. A Hunter can make a 
Defense Check to prevent Hansen from deducing certain facts about them, 
but it will be impossible to hide everything from the watchful eye of the 
Swineherd.

7
Karen the Mannequin 
Guardian of the Treasury
Inspired by Red Shoes (Neutral / Opponent)
Once upon a time, there was a beautiful girl 
named Karen. Despite being born a 
peasant, the girl won the affection 
of the young heir to a wealthy 
family. Before too much time had 
passed, she was a rich and happy 
bride. Freed from any responsibilities, 
Karen fell into a dissolute life devoted to 
pleasure, wasting her husband’s fortune. 
He soon regretted having taken her as his 
wife. The rich scion sought out a witch, who 
offered him an easy way to get rid of her. 
Returning from a distant journey, the 
man handed Karen a pair of beautiful 
red slippers, bright enough to dazzle 
the eyes. But those cursed shoes hid 
a fearsome secret. The shoes woke 
envy in the hearts of those that saw 
them, but it was only a fraction of 
the envy felt by the one wearing the 
shoes. Karen began to desire every 
beautiful thing she saw. She set out 
on a journey, leaving her husband 
behind in her relentless search for 
the beautiful things she craved. 
Over the years, Karen has stained 
herself with many crimes to 
obtain what she desired. Standing at the gallows, a messenger 
of the Order made her a proposal that she could not refuse. She 
would forever keep an eye on the most beautiful objects in the 
world.
Descriptor: I am obsessed with magical objects and anything 
that meets my standards of beauty. My body bears the wounds 
of all the punishments I have suffered for my greed, but the only 
pain I truly feel is that of waiting for a new treasure.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: To acquire any object that I consider special and 
wonderful, to keep it safe in the Treasury.
Special Agenda (Object Obsession): I can’t stand the idea 
of Hunters borrowing my precious Treasury items. I must watch 
over them and the sooner the Hunter dies, the sooner I will have 
the opportunity to bring my precious treasure back.
Note: This Agenda transforms Karen the Mannequin from 
Neutral to Opponent.
Gift - The Human Mannequin: Karen has suffered through 
torture and abuse. The girl has had her feet cut off to remove 
the damned red shoes and has lost her hands as a punishment 
for everything she has stolen over the years. Karen’s legs and 
arms are now metal and wood prostheses that allow her to 
move freely. During a Scene, thanks to her legs, Karen can 
move on vertical surfaces and anchor herself, ignoring any 
penalty to movement. Karen’s arms can also stretch to reach a 
range of nine feet.

8
Using the Order’s Treasures
To the Hunters who cross the sacred threshold 
of Valea Viilor, remember this warning: In this 
place, priceless treasures are kept that must not 
be released upon the common world. God and 
his emissaries watch over the Treasury so that 
the power enclosed within it is used to fight the 
darkness.
Evil against Evil, the strength of the shadow 
turned against itself.
If you receive the honor of wielding one of the 
Treasures of the Order, remember the three 
rules by which you must abide:
1. Do not reveal the power of your treasure 
within view of the commons.
2. Do not use your treasure to harm a 
representative of the Church or the Order.
3. Do not abuse the power of the treasure 
and return it at the end of your task.
As a Hunter you are aware that one of your 
duties is to deliver any potential Treasure to the 
Order, so that it can be studied, cataloged, and 
made available to other brave Hunters in the 
fight against evil.
Obtaining and using an Order’s Treasure
The Order’s Treasury contains a series of iconic items that, 
like the Hunters and the Broken Tales, come from a wide 
variety of stories. Each Treasure has a story and grants 
those who wield it a Gift linked to its powers. There are 
several ways for a Hunter to use an Order’s Treasure:
Replace a Gift:  A Hunter chooses a Treasure that will 
replace one of their Gifts (they cannot replace the Dark 
Ego). They will then have the possibility to use the Treasure 
for the duration of the Scenario and will have to rewrite one 
of their Descriptors so it relates to the Treasure. This option 
is particularly useful to spice up the Hunters, especially the 
pre-generated ones.
Special Experience Reward:  During a Campaign, 
through the Advancement Permanently acquire an Order’s 
Treasure. At this point, the Treasure becomes a permanent 
Descriptor and a permanent Gift. The Gift “set aside” for 
the Scenario is recovered, granting the Hunter a fourth 
Descriptor and a fourth Gift. The Hunter can still take 
other Treasures temporarily with the Replace a Gift option.
Mission Requirement from the Order:  A Scenario 
may require the use of an Order’s Treasure as part of it, 
or the Storyteller may grant a Treasure as support to a 
small group of Hunters, in a similar fashion to receiving 
the support of an Allied Dark Presence. In this case, the 
use of the Treasure is to be considered as a Scenario Gift, 
and Hunters can freely pass on the Treasure between them 
without adding a connected Descriptor.
TREASURES

9
Campaign Event’s Discovery: By playing a Campaign, 
Order Treasures can be found as part of an Event. Each 
Treasure comes with a Broken story of how it came into 
existence, which can inspire the Storyteller to create an ad 
hoc Event.
Treasure’s Descriptor
Whoever wields a Treasure must create a related Descriptor, 
like for any other Gift. Each Treasure comes with a series 
of Keywords that can help the Player create the Descriptor. 
Even better, the Treasure’s Descriptor can be mixed with 
some of the Hunter’s defining aspects. When creating a 
Drawback for the Treasure refer to at least one of these 
Order rules:
 ◆ Do not reveal the power of your Treasure in the eyes of 
the commons.
 ◆ Do not use your Treasure to harm a representative of 
the Church or the Order.
 ◆ Do not abuse the power of the Treasure.
Example: James the Swordsman is getting ready for a 
mission in the city of Paris. James’ Player believes that his Gift 
“Tick-Tock, Here Comes the Beast” may not be very useful, 
since it specifies “while outdoors”, which is not always easy 
in a big city. James’ Player decides, therefore, to replace this 
Gift with an Order’s Treasure and chooses The Old Sword 
(inspired by the Arthurian Myth).
Looking at the Keywords of the Old Sword we have: Weapon 
/ Hilt / Rock / Impact / Vibration / Power / Might. James’s 
Player then revises his Descriptor with:
I have learned to face monsters and aberrations of all 
kinds, using every weapon and power possible. The Old 
Sword is what I need, even if the abuse of its power has 
turned it against me more than once.
Treasure’s Gift
The Gift granted by an Order’s Treasure is considered a 
normal Gift of the Hunter wielding it: all activation costs 
and benefits refer to them. If the Treasure is instead the 
requirement of a Scenario, the Gift will be considered usable 
by whoever is wielding the Treasure during the Scene.
TREASURES

Durend
10
The Order’s Treasures
Durend the Broken Blade
Inspired by Orlando Furioso
Once upon a time, there was a justice-obsessed Paladin who asked God for a sword that 
could not be destroyed. But through some strange mockery of fate, he received Durend, a 
fragile sword that broke with every hit landed. Every night, Durend’s blade reassembled 
itself, because to be indestructible doesn’t mean to never fall, but to always stand back 
up afterward. Unable to understand the true essence of that boon, the Paladin threw 
Durend at the wall of the cathedral of Rocamadour in a fit of rage, where it remained 
embedded until the Order came along. Only those who are pure enough to understand 
its true value can wield the Broken Blade’s full power.
Keywords: Sword / Shards / Fury / Blessing / Faith / Metal
Gift - The Broken Blade: Durend will always have a Descriptor that determines 
its status, either Whole or Shattered. Whoever wields the Whole Durend can 
decide at any time to have it become Shattered. Later on, when acting with 
Shattered Durend, an attack can be made using either what remains of the 
sword or by wielding one of the blade shards. If this attack catches the opponent 
by surprise, gain 1 Additional Success for the Position Check. Durend’s owner can 
spend 1 Soma to make it Whole again at any time. When gaining an Outcome with 
an Increment by defending themselves with Whole Durend, its wielder can decide 
to have it become Shattered and inflict 1 Wound to the enemy with the metal 
shards generated by the impact. At the end of each Scene, Durend is made Whole 
again.

The Prodigious Beanstalks
Durend
11
The Prodigious 
Beanstalks
Inspired by Jack and the Beanstalk
As all Hunters know, there are many magical edibles with 
prodigious effects. These strange beanstalks can grow on 
command, allowing whoever uses them to manipulate the 
vegetation. It is said that the Prodigious Beanstalks were 
delivered to the Order by a young boy whose eyes were 
filled with madness. Among tales of man-eating giants and 
treasures in the clouds, the boy said that the magic stalks 
had been given to him by an old man, but that their use 
had proven to be a true curse. The Order took the crazed 
youngster to its healers and safely stored the magic stalks in 
the Treasury.
Keywords: Seeds / Plants / Growth / Vegetation / Flowers 
/ Tree.
Gift - Plant Growth:  This Gift grants the Player 5 Bean 
Tokens per Scenario. Spending 1 Token, the Hunter can 
throw 1 Bean into the bare earth, activating its power. 
This allows the Hunter to manipulate the vegetation of 
the immediate vicinity, making it grow. This Gift can be 
activated in a Controlled way by spending 1 Soma. This 
way, the Hunter has full control of plant growth and can 
create a Descriptor that makes it useful, such as a Climbing 
Ladder or a Bark Wall. The first time the Hunter acts by 
exploiting the created Descriptor, they gain 1 Additional 
Success to their action. The second method of activation 
is called Wild and differs from the first on two levels: 
there isn’t a Soma cost, but the growth is not fully under 
the control of the Hunter. After the Descriptor is created, 
the Storyteller will create a second one that represents the 
problem generated by the uncontrolled plant growth after 
using the Beanstalks.
Special Effect (Ambrose the Sorcerer known as 
Old Merlin): No one is aware of the fact that the old man 
who traded the Beanstalks with the poor crazy boy was, in 
fact, Merlin. The Prodigious Beanstalks are just yet another 
magical means for the sorcerer to manipulate the destiny of 
others. If Merlin is present in the Scenario, during a Wild 
use of the Gift, the Descriptor created by the Storyteller will 
not be a disadvantage to the Hunter. It will instead open the 
way to a special Scene that, if completed, will be beneficial 
to Merlin himself. The effect should lead to a creature’s lair 
or a potential trap, but it will also free or allow the Hunter 
to find someone or something useful for the sorcerer. 
When this effect is activated, the owner of this Gift and 
their companions will receive 2 XP if they choose to follow 
the new path suggested by Merlin.

The Clothes of the Late Emperor
12
The Clothes of the Late 
Emperor
Inspired by The Emperor’s New Clothes
Once upon a time, a vain nobleman was looking for new clothes to show off. Confident 
he would look magnificent in any dress, the nobleman accepted the challenge of a 
strange tailor who offered to let him wear a particular piece of clothing, apparently 
invisible to the human eye. It was, unfortunately, a scam. However, a very disturbing 
piece of clothing is featured in the Order’s Treasury, said to be made of human skin. 
The garment has always been hungry for flesh and blood, and whoever wears it 
becomes invisible to those who do not know how to look carefully. Legend has it 
that it is made with the skin of the tailor, skinned alive by the vain nobleman as 
punishment for his lies.
Keywords: Dress / Leather / Blood / Invisible / Cloak / Color
Gift - Invisible Cloak: Whoever wears the Clothes of the Late Emperor becomes 
invisible throughout the course of the Scene, obtaining the additional Descriptor: 
I am invisible to the human eye and only children see through the deception of 
my clothes. Whoever is made invisible because of this Gift receives all the narrative 
advantages of being unseen and 2 Additional Successes to each Defense Check in 
which being invisible represents a tangible advantage. Activating the power of the 
Invisible Cloak inflicts 1 Wound to the one wearing it, as the Clothes of the Late 
Emperor try to feast on their flesh and blood.

Eden’s Fruit
The Clothes of the Late Emperor
13
Eden’s Fruit
Inspired by Snow White
The magic power of the apple reaches back to the myth of 
Eden and to a power that God himself denied to man. For 
this reason, apples are the fruits most easily enchanted by 
wizards and witches. The most famous enchanted fruit is 
preserved in the Order’s Treasury, where scholars try to 
understand its apparent imperishability. The juicy red apple 
called Eden’s Fruit, in fact, does not decay with the passage of 
time and regenerates in a few hours after being bitten or even 
completely eaten. Those who feed on Eden’s Fruit see their 
body mutate or, in some cases, fall into a deep sleep because 
of the malignant effect of the mutagenic power of the fruit on 
their metabolism.
Keywords: Apple / Red / Good smell / Flavor / Mutation 
/ Sleep
Gift - Mutagenic Fruit:  Whoever eats part of Eden’s 
Fruit can choose one of the two effects. 1. Mutation: Yo u  
may change a part of your body throughout the Scene and 
create a specific Descriptor such as Membranous wings or 
Gills. The mutation will always be difficult to conceal and 
grants 1 Additional Success in all the actions in which it can 
be of help; 2. Torpor: This puts the body of those who ate 
the Fruit into a state of apparent death, in which, however, 
their cognitive ability is not lost. Whoever has eaten the 
apple will seem dead for all intents and purposes, even to 
the most in-depth medical investigations, while keeping all 
five senses active. One may only wake up from the Torpor 
at the end of the Scene. If Eden’s Fruit is given to a subject 
unaware of its power, whoever gave it to them will choose 
the effect to be activated. It is possible to attempt a Defense 
Check to resist the power of either of Eden’s Fruit effects. 
Once bitten, the apple will take the rest of the Scene to 
regenerate. A second use in the same Scene will make it 
useless for the rest of the current Scenario. 
Special Effect (Bloodwhite): Y our special metabolism 
rejects the power of Eden’s Fruit and you can only choose 
the Torpor effect. Y ou can recover from it whenever you 
want, without having to wait for the end of the Scene.

The 
Golem
14
There was a time when alchemists and sorcerers used magic and dark arts to gain power 
with impunity. In those days, the Church was not yet aware of the true power of darkness, 
which thrived on the ambition of those who were foolish enough to rely on strange magical 
practices. Among the many blasphemies produced by alchemy, Golems are one of the most 
incredible, considering they are animated giants composed of whatever materials came to 
hand. The Order also uses Golems as tireless minions, bestowing them with the dignity that 
suits every servant of God.
Keywords: Giant / Force / Mindless / Resistant / Clay / Bond
Gift - The Immortal Guardian: A Golem is at the service of the Hunter, executing all 
their orders at a slow, methodical pace. The Golem has the following profile.
Descriptor: I am a mighty and resilient Golem. I act only when prompted to carry out an 
order by my master.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5 
Gift - Immortal Body:  When suffering its third Wound, the Golem crumbles to a 
thousand pieces to regenerate in the next Scene. The only way to eliminate it permanently 
is to kill its master. Being animated by magic, an opponent versed in such arts can use 
their powers to try to temporarily take control of the Golem or prevent it from acting. The 
Hunter controlling the Golem may attempt a Defense Check to prevent the interference 
through the strength of their bond with the Golem. The Golem acts only at the command 
of its creator and giving it an order during an active Scene takes time. For every action, 
the Golem performs, the Hunter receives 1 Penalty in the next action. The bond, however, 
provides several advantages. During a Scene, the Hunter can use each of these effects.  
1. Protect me!:  The Golem takes 1 Wound in place of its master; 2. Deep Bond: The 
Hunter can consider the Golem as a “relay” of their presence and activate the effects of their 
Gifts, using them as if they occupied the Golem’s space. Not all Gifts fit this effect and the 
Hunter, in agreement with the Storyteller, must create an effect consistent with the narrative;  
3. Come to me!: If not present in the Scene, the Golem will arrive as soon as possible and 
use the fastest shortcut (literally breaking through walls and obstacles!) to reach its master’s 
side.
The Golem Inspired by Jewish Mythology

The Wind Carpet
15
The Wind Carpet
Inspired by the One Thousand and One Nights
Many special objects were created during antiquity and often 
preserved in the personal collections of wealthy merchants 
and spoiled nobles. The Order is ruthlessly efficient at 
feigning interest in purchasing items and then seizing them. 
The history of the Wind Carpet has its roots in the eastern 
lands where, apparently, it passed through many hands, 
sometimes stained with the blood of its previous owner. For 
years, the Order’s scholars believed that the Carpet had no 
special quality, only to discover that it could actually fly, but 
only if not under the gaze of anyone skeptical of it. After that 
revelation, it was easy to understand why the artifact was so 
dangerous.
Keywords: Wind / Carpet / Large / Oriental / Strange 
scriptures / Dust
Gift - The Flying Carpet:  The Wind Carpet can fly, 
carrying up to six people or very heavy loads. This power 
can be used at will by the Hunter but will dissipate as 
soon as someone who does not believe in magic looks at 
the Carpet for a few moments. If this happens, the Carpet 
immediately loses its power, causing its passengers to fall to 
the ground. The Wind Carpet is magically indestructible, 
allowing the Hunter to use it as a wall or shield against a 
potential hazard, such as fire, arrows, or anything else that 
could be blocked by it. Once per Scene, the Hunter can 
take advantage of this protection to receive 2 Additional 
Successes on their Defense Check. This bonus applies to 
anyone under the Carpet’s protection, but, as with flight, 
it cannot be used if a nonbeliever observes the Carpet in 
action.
NB: Magical creatures, witches, the Church, and the Broken 
Tales, in general, believe in magic and are not considered 
skeptical of the power of the Wind Carpet. What the item 
really reacts to are the doubting gazes of ordinary people.
Special Effect (Baba Yaga the Child Witch): Even if 
no one believes your story, the Wind Carpet is actually your 
creation, and the Order has put its dirty hands on it. As 
proof, once per Scene, when the stupid humans lay their 
eyes on it, you can spend 1 Soma to ensure that the power 
of the Carpet does not fizzle, leaving those poor fools 
speechless.

The Wind Fan
16
The Wind Fan
Inspired by Journey to the West
The East seems to hide incredible treasures, all forged in 
metal, which represents firmness, discipline, strength, and 
determination throughout Chinese mythology. In the cycle of 
elements, however, metal also represents decline and decay, 
concepts that are well suited to the dark parts of the world. 
This small metal fan is an ancient heirloom, said to have been 
forged at the dawn of time. Wave the fan and the wind will 
respond to the call by obeying the will of its new master.
Keywords: Metal / Antique / Wind / Air / Fan / Balance
Gift - Dominating the Wind:  Whoever owns the Fan 
can wave it to invoke the power of the wind and create 
a Descriptor that deals with the wind, such as A strong 
breeze or A powerful ascending air current. Using this Gift 
requires an expense of Soma from 0 to 2 points (decided by 
the Storyteller), based on the power of the invoked effect. 
Whoever owns the Fan can wave it to move the wind in 
their defense, gaining 1 Additional Success in each Defense 
Check in which the wind could help. 
Special Effect (Sun WuKong): When the power of the 
fan is released for the first time in the Scenario, you feel your 
blood boil as the ancestral spirit inside of you awakens. Y ou 
can activate your Dark Ego - Descendant of the Awakening 
of the Void and the Emptiness without paying the required 
Wound.

The Needle of Living Shadows17
The Needle of Living 
Shadows
Inspired by Peter and Wendy
Since ancient times, there has been a belief that a person’s shadow is nothing more than the 
extension of their soul and that it remains attached to the body only because it is forced 
to do so. However, there is an artifact that can separate the shadow from its owner, thus 
granting it freedom. The Needle of Living Shadows, as it is called, allows one to “unweave” 
the shadow from a body so that it can act independently. In the same way, a shadow can 
only be sewn back to its original body using the Needle, which is important because, after 
twenty-four hours of separation, neither one nor the other would survive...
Keywords: Shadow / Darkness / Light / Spite / Mimicking / Joking
Gift - Mischievous Shadow: Once per Scene, the Hunter who uses the Needle of 
the Living Shadows can mime a gesture as if undoing their own shadow. The shadow 
will then become alive, taking with it 1 of the owner’s Wounds. From that moment, 
the Hunter will be able to act independently through either their own body or their 
shadow. The shadow becomes solid and can be hit with weapons or other physical 
means, possesses all the Gifts, Descriptors, and equipment of the original body, but 
cannot speak. As soon as it suffers its only Wound, the shadow reattaches to its owner. 
The only other way to restore one’s shadow is to mime the gesture of sewing it back on 
with the Needle. However, the shadow, enjoying its freedom, will usually oppose this, 
unaware that exceeding 24 hours of separation will lead to both its and its owner’s end. 
This mischievous attitude may require the Hunter to do one or more Position Checks 
to restore things to their proper order.

The Magic Wand18
The Magic Wand
Inspired by Cinderella
Wizards and witches possess many objects of power. Most common among those are wands, 
genuine catalysts of energy. A wand allows one to express their latent mystical force at its highest 
levels, permitting whoever wields it to unleash the power of magic. But as the most cautious 
know, magic is pure chaos and often escapes the control of those who are foolish enough to evoke 
its power. Over the years, many witches have been captured by the Order, but despite torture 
and interrogations, none have ever revealed the inner works of their wands.
Keywords: Magic / Wood / Wish / Wand / Formula / Light
Gift - Magic Transmutation:  Whoever wields the Magic Wand can invoke its power 
to change the shape of one object into another. Just point the Wand and say the formula 
“[Target object Name] changes to [New object name]” and spend 0 to 2 Soma points for the 
spell. The exact value of the cost is decided by the Storyteller, in proportion to the difference 
between the starting object and what you want to obtain. The mutation lasts until the end of 
a Scene and can be maintained during the following Scenes at the cost of 1 Soma per Scene. 
Using the Wand on a living being does not generate mutations, but the magical force destroys 
the living matter in a vain attempt to manipulate it. For this reason, the Magic Wand can be 
used to attack an enemy even at great distances, ignoring obstacles and normal defenses. 
Using the Wand in this way costs 1 Soma point for each attack, but gives 1 Additional Success 
in the action and, in the event of an Outcome with an Increment, the Hunter can spend the 
Increment to inflict 1 Additional Wound.
Special Effect (Baba Yaga the Child Witch, Marina the Sea Explorer, and 
Morgana of the Lake):  The Magic Wand perceives your witch aura and shuns it, since 
it is not in your nature to do good deeds for others. Once per Scenario, when using the 
transmutation power of the Magic Wand, the Storyteller can decide on a retaliatory effect 
between the following. 1. Mutating Backfire: Something in your body changes for the 
rest of the Scene, the Storyteller creates a relevant Descriptor; 2. Explosion: The Magic 
Wand spits fire and sparks, inflicting 1 Wound to you; 3. Reject: Y ou will no longer be able 
to use the Magic Wand to transform something until the next Interlude. Y ou can still use it 
as a weapon.

The Newcomer’s Crown
19
Once upon a time, an old and dissipated king was served by a 
company of knights, also old and tired. Although in the past the 
kingdom had been an example of courage and righteousness, 
now it was nothing more than a land lost to pleasure and 
laziness. To fight his ennui, the king called a tournament in 
which anyone could participate. The dissolute ruler’s intent was 
to cruelly laugh while his knights were beating on some poor 
peasant attracted by the idea of becoming a noble. On the day 
of the tournament, however, only the knights had entered. The 
people were discouraged by fear, despite the fact that the grand 
prize, nobility, could change their lives forever. However, shortly 
before the start of the tournament, a mysterious challenger 
appeared, a small boy with a wooden sword. The king and 
his knights laughed heartily, warning the fool not to enter his 
name. In response, the young man said he wanted to become a 
knight, and what better way to achieve his dream than winning 
a tournament?
Taking pity, the king sought an excuse to prevent the young 
man’s death at the edge of some random blade. “If you don’t 
have a helmet to protect your head, I’m afraid you can’t 
participate. ” The young man did not lose heart. Instead, he took 
a small wicker basket, like those used as cradles, and put it on 
his head. “I am ready, Your Majesty, ” replied the young man.
The king began the tournament… and the knights fell one after 
the other. With absolute courage, against all expectations, the 
young man defeated all the challengers before the stunned gaze 
of the king. “ And now that I have demonstrated my bravery, can 
you make me a knight?”
The king, visibly amazed, smiled. He thought about it for a 
moment and then shouted, “ Arrest this young man! He’s the 
Devil himself!” The guards grabbed him. Realizing the danger, 
the boy begged the king for mercy. But the king certainly could 
not allow true courage and purity of heart to sprout in his 
kingdom, proving how unworthy he was to sit at its head. 
And so it was that the young man’s head was lopped off by the 
headsman’s ax, leaving the small basket as the only memory of 
his courage and his purity, soiled with blood.
Keywords: Purity / Attempt / Challenge / Tournament / 
Courage / Unexpected
Gift - The Newcomer’s Crown:  The Hunter who wears 
the small basket as a helmet is infused with purity and 
courage. When facing a threat for the first time, the Hunter 
gets 1 Additional Success at both the first Position Check and 
the first Defense Check they must perform. Once per Scene, 
when they take an action where they do not have a relevant 
Descriptor, they still get 3 Base Successes. Once per Scene, 
when the Hunter fails a Position Check, they gain back 1 
Soma.
The Newcomer’s Crown
Inspired by the Arthurian Myth

The Hair Rope20
The Hair Rope
Inspired by Rapunzel
Once upon a time, there was a young woman who, due to an unfortunate event, had ended 
up in the clutches of a powerful witch. The old hag locked the girl at the top of a tower with 
no stairs or door and cast a spell on her hair. Thanks to the spell, the girl’s long hair could 
move and grow impossibly long, becoming a rope that could be climbed. Every day, the witch 
went to visit the young woman in the tower, bringing her food and gifts. Over the years, 
she began to regard her as a daughter and not a prisoner. The young woman, however, 
had met an attractive young man from the nearby village. At night, he went up along the 
braid of hair to meet her and consummate with her a forbidden love. “The next time the 
witch asks you to come up, ” said the boy, “coil your hair around her neck and kill her, so we 
will be free. ” The girl agreed, eager to regain her freedom. But a guardian owl warned the 
witch of the deception and she, heartbroken, prepared her revenge. She bewitched the boy 
and took him by day to the foot of the tower, then she called the girl. The young woman did 
not think twice, animating her hair and, unknowingly, strangled her beloved. Realizing her 
terrible mistake, she jumped from the tower. Fate had her fall right on the old witch, killing 
them both instantly. Only the hair, still animated by magic, remained to witness this absurd 
tragedy.
Keywords: Hair / Rope / Crawl / Whip / Strangle / Animate
Gift - The Hair Rope: The Hunter who owns the Hair Rope can spend 1 Soma to animate 
it and move it for all its 30 feet of length. The rope can grab and drag objects, tie itself to 
a ledge, or execute any other order that comes to its master’s mind. Once animated, the 
Hair Rope will act for the entire duration of the Scene, being able to perform its action at 
any time at the order of the Hunter. Once per Scene, the Rope can hinder a danger that 
threatens its master, granting 2 Additional Successes on a Defense Check.

The Old Sword
The Hair Rope 21
The Old Sword
Inspired by the Arthurian Myth
Once upon a time, there was a powerful magic sword stuck in 
a rock that no one could pull out. According to the prophecy 
shared by the old sage Merlin, only someone destined to reign 
over all England would be worthy to wield the sword in the stone. 
In fact, Merlin himself had enchanted the weapon, merging it 
with the rock in which it was set. Following the dark plans of 
the magician, the prophecy attracted nobles, knights, and even 
ordinary people, all eager for the power and status derived from 
being the chosen one. But as Merlin had plotted, no one could 
really draw the sword, which should have remained forever 
in the rock, distracting the mortal fools from the true aims of 
the wizard. What Merlin had not considered, however, was 
the power of people’s desire. At every attempt, at every failure 
to draw the weapon, the sword was charged with a spark of 
magical power, growing more powerful by the day. Finally, 
after a hundred long years, the sword had reached immense 
power but remained inextricable. Merlin stopped basking in his 
deception when the Order came. The Hunters did not hesitate 
to break the stone around the sword, taking it away, under the 
astonished gaze of its creator.
Keywords: Weapon / Hilt / Rock / Impact / Vibration / Power 
/ Might

22
Gift - The Sword of the Myth: The Old Sword does not 
have a blade, being nothing more than a block of rock with 
a hilt to hold it. The weapon’s power is strong and tangible, 
but also incredibly unstable. Whoever holds it must roll a 
die every time they use it to see how the latent energy in the 
Old Sword manifests. 
1. Heavy as a rock: The power does nothing but increase 
the weight of the weapon, so actions receive 1 Penalty 
because of the cumbersome block; 
2. The Nothingness:  The Old Sword loses all power, 
resulting in a common piece of rock; 
3. Spark of Power: A flash of power gives 1 Additional 
Success to the action; 
4. Impact: The Old Sword releases a shock wave that hits 
the enemy upon impact. If the action inflicts 1 Wound, and 
the target is thrown several feet away as an additional effect; 
5. Devastation: The Old Sword falls upon the target to 
pulverize it into a thousand pieces. If the action causes 1 
Wound, it will also cause 1 additional Wound; 
6. The True Excalibur:  For a moment, the Old Sword 
manifests all its true power. Whoever wields it inflicts 
1 additional Wound and can cut through anything, 
even rock or metal. The enemies are dazzled by the 
power of the Old Sword and whoever wields it receives 
1 Additional Success on the next Defense Check they 
make to defend themselves from an enemy attack.  
Once per Scene, whoever wields the Old Sword can spend 
1 Soma before rolling the dice to choose the desired effect. 
Special Effect (Ambrose the Sorcerer known as 
Old Merlin): Merlin hates the Old Sword, as it represents 
his greatest failed deception. Whoever wields the Old 
Sword will never benefit from Merlin’s help but will also 
receive 2 Additional Successes to defend against any effect 
generated directly or indirectly by the sorcerer’s magic.

The Razor-sharp Glass Slippers25
The Razor-sharp Glass Slippers
Inspired by Cinderella
Once upon a time, there was a young girl, the youngest of three sisters. When the mother 
of the three decided to offer them in marriage to a rich noble, the youngest girl was 
immediately discarded because of the color of her hair, which did not please the noble. 
Wounded in her pride, the young woman turned to a powerful witch who gave her a 
pair of magic shoes. While wearing the glass shoes, each click of the heels clouded the 
minds of those who were close to her, forgetting the most insignificant details. “Thanks 
to these shoes, ” said the witch, “you can approach your beloved and make yourself 
known for your inner beauty, while he will not remember the hair that he does not 
appreciate. ” But the young woman’s heart was not as pure as the old witch believed. 
Drunk with the power of the slippers, she set out to trick men and women, stealing 
gold and precious items. When the old witch returned to the city, she wept with sadness 
seeing what the young woman had become. Now a rich and greedy noblewoman, she 
refused to return the shoes and accused the witch of blasphemy, condemning her to 
burn at the stake. With her last breath, the witch invoked one last spell, and the shoes 
animated, leading the young woman towards the flames. Everyone watched, horrified 
but unable to intervene since at every click of the heel the power of the shoes clouded 
their mind. The glass slippers, shiny and unmarked, were all that remained once the fire 
was extinguished.
Keywords: Bright / Precise / Clicking / Reflective / Dazed / Attention
Gift - Glass Heels:  Whoever wears the shoes can click the glass heels and create

The Mirror of a Thousand Wishes26
The Mirror of a Thousand Wishes
Inspired by Snow White
Once upon a time, there was a queen so obsessed with her appearance that she never stopped 
admiring her reflection. She was a beautiful woman, the most beautiful of her time, but 
despite this, she could not stop scrutinizing herself in search of the slightest defect. Her worried 
spouse turned for help to a powerful wizard, asking for a mirror that could show what was 
most desired. The wizard took advantage of that gesture of love to dispose of a cursed mirror 
of which he himself was a victim. “The Mirror of a Thousand Wishes can show anything, ” said 
the wizard to the poor man, “but only if it’s in the past or the future. ” Even better, thought the 
man, the one I love will see her perfection before and after, enjoying her present with me. The 
queen was happy with the gift, but she began to spend even more time in front of her new 
mirror. She saw her future and past selves, two perfect women, identical and beautiful. But 
why couldn’t she see herself at this very instant? Maybe the mirror was hiding something from 
her? The queen stopped doing anything else, asking the mirror to show her what she was like at 
that very moment. Insistence became obsession; obsession became desperation. Then one day, 
for a second, the mirror showed her the present. The last thing the queen saw was her beloved 
with a knife in his hand and, in his eyes, the pain of those who have forever lost their love.
Keywords: Reflection / Blur / Mirror / Vanity / Glass / Gold
Gift - Mirror Mirror on the Wall:  Those who use the Mirror of a Thousand Wishes

29
This chapter expands Broken Tales with a solo play 
mode, without the need for a Storyteller or other Players. 
This type of experience will look much more like a creative 
writing exercise than a usual role-playing Session. Due 
to its more introspective nature, Untold Stories  will allow 
you to glimpse into the Hunters’ past, living intense and 
engaging adventures and bringing out different facets of 
each Hunter and their relationship to the Order.
That way, you can explore novel elements of each Hunter’s 
past, based on their background and their Descriptors. 
Who really is the mysterious little boy who deceived James 
the Swordsman? Is Baba Y aga really the most powerful 
witch of them all? These and other adventures await you in 
the Untold Stories.
Initial Setup
The first step is to choose the Hunter protagonist of your 
story. Hunters have a personal list of 3 possible narrative 
hooks linked to their past, each giving some hints useful to 
determine the ultimate goal of the Hunter. 
Keywords are thematic elements linked to the possible 
storylines and clues available to the Hunter. Their use 
is relevant for the Key Scenes, as explained in the Initial 
Situation Table on page 44.  
Alice of Other Worlds
1-2 Beyond the mirror:  Alice wakes up in front of 
the great mirror of Wonder. Facing her reflection, she 
unexpectedly activated her mental control ability. However, 
the body she took over isn’t hers, but that of a young girl 
named Alix. The young woman is locked in a house full of 
mirrors and is tortured by a jailer whose face is shrouded. 
Only Alice can save her.
3-4 The trial: Alice has been summoned to London’s court 
to be part of the jury in an important trial. Along with her, 
on the jury, there is a strange inventor named Hatta Carter 
and an arrogant noblewoman named Regina the Thief of 
Hearts. Alice quickly understands that the old soldier called 
Jack Hearts, accused of murder, is a scapegoat while the true 
culprit is one of those judging him...
5-6 The diary: Alice recovers a diary from when she was 
little more than a teenager. While reading it, she realizes that 
there are notes about travels she does not remember. To cure 
such severe amnesia, she will have to visit those places again.
Keywords: Truth merges with madness / Journey into 
memories / Injustice / Reason turned upside down / 
Accusation / Underground / Strange food / The den / The 
playground / The house that no one sees.
