Once upon a time…
London’s outskirts. Among the fumes and vapors 
of newly-built factories, the disappearance 
of several children has led to widespread 
discontent among the workers. However, it 
is only after the disappearance of Gwendolyn 
Carnarvon, daughter of a titled noble, that the 
royal family itself decided to turn to the Order. 
Strange rumors are circulating in the suburbs 
of London, where it seems that a young gang of 
hooligans is committing violent acts to slow the 
creeping spread of modernity, particularly in 
the form of heartless factories. The Crown has 
been deeply terrified by the macabre discovery 
made by police looking into reports of violence 
in the streets. According to rumors, factory 
guards had engaged in a clash with the young 
terrorists. The Crown investigators who tracked 
down the battlefield were confronted with a 
scene of unbelievable violence featuring broken, 
bloody limbs and burst hearts inside shredded 
rib cages. In great secrecy, the investigation 
was shortly thereafter entrusted to the Hunters 
of the Order with the aim of shedding light on 
the danger that threatens the city and bringing 
young Carnarvon to safety.
A Scenario Inspired by Peter 
and Wendy (J. M. Barrie)
I Don’t Believe
Safety Keywords:  
Kidnapping / Terrorism

81
The story that led us here…
A powerful fairy, known as the Cauldron Fae, has 
been residing in London since time immemorial, long 
before the city became what it is now. Under the false 
identity of a personable purveyor of pots, she has lived in 
secret on grounds she considers sacred: a series of caves and 
galleries under the gardens of the Kensington Palace. The 
Cauldron Fae is bothered by the modernization overtaking 
London, slowly poisoning the clear air of the city with 
smoke and stink. The fairy has begun to surround herself 
with young orphans and misfits, creating a gang of ruthless 
killers ready to do anything to please her. At the head of the 
gang is Pietrik, a young Eastern immigrant who watched his 
little sister die of fatigue, crushed by inhumane shifts at the 
factory. Pietrik’s sole purpose in life is now to undermine 
industrialization. The Cauldron Fae gave Pietrik, as her 
most trusted servant, a magical object able to animate 
his shadow so that he can more ably be her servant and 
guardian. When a group of Crown investigators found the 
secret passage from the slums to the Kensington Gardens, 
the Cauldron Fae joined Pietrik on the field, unleashing her 
great powers. The Cauldron Fae can control the Magic of 
Disbelief, which allows her to imbue words with truth, but 
only when expressing concepts of denial. By pronouncing 
“I don’t believe in guards, ” the bodies of her enemies 
twisted and then exploded, destroyed by devastating magic. 
The only way to kill the Cauldron Fae is to use her own 
magic against her, pronouncing the formula “I don’t believe 
Mtyrya exists. ” Only Pietrik, however, knows her true name. 
The young man fell in love with Gwendolyn and, after an 
initial friendship, decided to kidnap her because she was 
about to leave for Wales. As a prisoner in the Kensington 
Gardens, the girl fears for her life, but she is perhaps the 
only one who can lead Pietrik to reason.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Pietrik is the leader of his gang, the Lost Boys. They are out 
of control, striking at a different business in London every 
night. Only Gwendolyn’s entreaties can dampen the young 
rebel’s bloodlust. The Cauldron Fae is harnessing power 
for her greatest spell yet, which will be: “I do not believe 
that there are people in London. ” The fairy’s goal is to get 
rid of all the unwelcome humans in her beloved home 
(including the Lost Boys), saving only Pietrik, to whom she 
has become attached. To succeed, however, the fairy needs 
power, which can only be generated by the passionate spirit 
of young people rebelling against oppressive modernity. 
For this reason, the Cauldron Fae encourages Pietrik and 
his followers to strike London’s businesses more and more 
often. Around the city have been engraved the Seals of the 
fairy, and only those who already possess one can see the 
others. The Lost Boys use theirs to activate the other Seals, 
tap into the Cauldron Fae’s power, and perform small feats 
of magic.
Distraught by the disappearance of his daughter, George 
Carnarvon sent his best agents into the city. Most 
disappeared and the remains of the few who were found 
had been torn by savage wounds. Only at that point was 
Carnavon willing to admit that the Hunters had skills his 
men lacked and that made them uniquely suited to confront 
occult powers. George is concerned for his daughter’s life, 
and the fact that no one asked for a ransom makes him fear 
the worst for Gwendolyn.

1
82
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You have just arrived in 
London but, despite the exhaustion of your recent journey, 
Lord Carnarvon has peremptorily summoned you. Tucked 
away in one of the great homes in the city center, you find 
yourself at the table with a fifty-year-old nobleman. The 
puffy, dark circles around his eyes and his pale face leave you 
with the impression that your host is very concerned about 
what is happening in London.
Place: The London villa of the Carnarvon family.
Time: It is the late afternoon of a gloomy autumn day. 
Initial Situation: George will update the Hunters with 
all that happened so far. The nobleman will not hide his 
deep concern for the fate of his daughter Gwendolyn.
Information: From the interview with George, the 
Hunters will obtain the following information.
 ◆ London is under attack by a large gang of young rebels. 
The boys, all between the ages of ten and sixteen, hit 
the most prosperous activities of the city, causing 
serious accidents, damaging the tools and machinery 
and, often, even the workers. The gang exhibits a 
clear penchant for rebellion, focused on the nascent 
industrial hub of London.
 ◆ George’s sixteen-year-old daughter, Gwendolyn, was 
abducted two days ago. The young woman has always 
been a little rebellious but had never openly revolted 
against her family. George fears that she has approached 
the gang of youngsters driven by curiosity and that she 
has been taken by them against her will. No ransom 
demand has been made for the young woman, and that 
makes George fear the worst. A desperate father before 
anything else, Lord Carnavon will make it clear that 
finding his daughter is more important to him than the 
fate of the entire city.
 ◆ After the clash that ended in a bloodbath the night 
before, it is clear that the young rioters have something 
occult on their side. Basil, the only survivor of the 
attack, said that afterward the young attackers literally 
disappeared through one of the walls. 
At the end of the interview, George will be available to lead 
the Hunters to several places:
 ◆ To Gwendolyn’s rooms inside the palace, where the 
Hunters can search for clues to the disappearance of 
the young woman and interrogate her personal maid: 
Joanna.
 ◆ At the Sirens’ Inn, in the lower neighborhoods of 
London, where Basil rests to recover from his injuries.
 ◆ At the Skullem Factory, a shipyard that George fears 
will be the next target of the young rioters.
Note: George will be as helpful as possible to the Hunters, 
but it will be clear that the man is psychologically and 
physically worn out by the events of the last days. The 
Hunters can decide whether to keep him by their side or 
not.
Note: In this Scenario, Pietrik and the Lost Boys Gang 
can use the power of the Seals that the Cauldron Fae has 
engraved all over London. At least one Seal, to be activated 
at will, is always present in all the Places of the Scenario.

83
Threat – Seal of the Cauldron Fae
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A magic seal capable of manifesting the magic 
of the Cauldron Fae.
Gift – Seals Everywhere:  At least one Seal of the 
Cauldron Fae is present in all the Places of the Scenario. 
Whoever possesses the Gang Seal or Personal Seal Gift  
can activate the effect of the Seal of the Cauldron Fae as an 
additional action; this allows them to teleport to another 
place featuring a Seal. The Seals are invisible to the human 
eye and can be used only by those who have a tattooed eye 
on their hand, placing said tattoo in front of the right eye. 
Those who use magic can detect the presence of a Seal, but 
the Seal of Disbelief Scenario Gift is necessary to activate it. 
Note: Some Places feature more than one Seal to grant 
the Lost Boys more narrative options in case the Hunters 
manage to impede access to a Seal or negate the Gift. 
George Carnarvon
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the head of the Carnarvon family and my 
service to the Crown has brought me a great deal of influence. 
I fear the occult and magic. I can’t sleep due to worry over my 
daughter’s abduction.
Agenda: Stop the gang of rebels that plagues London, but 
more than anything else find my beloved daughter Gwendolyn.
Carnarvon House
Descriptor: A rich and large mansion in the heart of 
London.
Joanna called Nana the Servant
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I have been Lady Gwendolyn’s trusted personal 
servant for over ten years. I saw the young heir grow up and I 
am as fond of her as if she were my own daughter.
Information: Joanna will tell the Hunters that she often 
heard Gwendolyn chatting with a boy in her room, but 
when she came in to figure out who it was, she never found 
anyone. Gwendolyn has always avoided revealing the boy’s 
identity, convincing Nana that he was some noble son of 
a good family in the city. Searching Gwendolyn’s room, 
the Hunters will find some poppy petals under the bed. 
Nana will say that those flowers can only be found in the 
Kensington Gardens in London.
Seals: There is only one Seal in the Carnarvon villa, 
inscribed on the east wall of Gwendolyn’s room.

84
The Cauldron Fae’s Lair
Descriptor: An extensive maze of caves and galleries stretching beneath all of 
Kensington’s Gardens. Within it, all kinds of plants and flowers grow luxuriantly 
thanks to the Cauldron Fae’s power.
Threat – Nature Rises Up
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Navigating inside the lair without the consent of the Cauldron Fae 
exposes the intruders to the ire of the many plants. Ivy tentacles and pollen mists rise to 
block those who walk without their lady’s approval.
Mtyrya the Fairy also called the 
Cauldron Fae
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an ancient fairy bound to nature. I hate humans and modernity. Thanks 
to my power, anything others believe to be impossible, I can make possible.
Agenda: Prevent humans from covering my beloved land with dirty, stinking factories / Save 
Pietrik from the punishment I plan to inflict upon London.
Gift – The Magic of Disbelief:  As an action, the Cauldron Fae can formulate a magic 
sentence including the fragment I don’t believe and make its effect concrete. For example, 
“I don’t believe in gravity” would allow her to fly, while “I don’t believe the Hunters of the 
Order exist” would allow her to hurt the Hunters in an attempt to make them disappear. 
If the statement does not have offensive effects, the duration of the power is generally for 
the entire Scene. If, on the other hand, the effect is likely to harm a target, it will inflict 
1 Wound without the possibility of attempting a Defense Check. Those who use magic can 
try to counteract the Magic of Disbelief, and preventing Mtyrya from speaking words would 
automatically cancel the Gift’s effect.

85
Gift – Ancient Fairy:  The Cauldron Fae is immortal as 
long as her beloved plants thrive under the Kensington 
Gardens. The true focus of her power is the rebellious spirit 
of her young servants. The fairy starts the Scenario with 
3 Power Tokens and can spend 1 to return from death, 
mending all the Wounds she has suffered. Each time a 
factory or equivalent is destroyed in retaliation against 
modernity, she receives 1 Power Token. The Cauldron 
Fae can spend 4 Power Tokens to unleash all her power 
and apply the effect of the Magic of Disbelief Gift to all of 
London.
London’s Slums
Descriptor: Wretched alleys and houses where all the poor 
of London live.
Information: In the slums, you can always find 1d6+6 
members of the Lost Boys Gang. When he’s not on a 
mission or at Gwendolyn’s, Pietrik is here too.
Seals: Almost every main street in the slums has a Seal 
somewhere.
The Sirens’ Inn
Descriptor: A dirty inn in the lower neighborhoods, hiding 
a clandestine stall. Poor people and prostitutes flock inside to 
find a roof to rest under.
Information: Basil resides at the inn because it is the 
only one he can afford with the low salary provided by the 
Carnarvon family. The man will badmouth his employer (if 
not present) and will tell the Hunters that his companions 
were literally ripped apart before exploding horribly. The 
youngsters’ leader (a boy with typical Eastern European 
features) pronounced the words “I don’t believe in guards” 
right before the bloodbath. Basil is the only one who 
wasn’t affected, but he hurt his leg falling down while 
fleeing (a detail he omitted during George’s account) and is 
determined to leave London as soon as he can walk again.
Pietrik has been watching Basil since he came to the inn, and 
the Hunters will notice the young man (which corresponds 
to Basil’s description) after some time there. If cornered, 
Pietrik will escape thanks to the power of the Seal.
Seals: There are two Seals in the Sirens’ Inn, one in the 
hallway in front of Basil’s room and the other in the main 
hall near the front door.
Basil the Survivor
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a coward only concerned with making 
ends meet. 
Agenda: To get back on my feet and escape from London, 
away from rebels and magic.

86
Pietrik
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young rebel and I fight the creeping industrialization of London with 
all my strength.
Agenda: Destroy all the factories to avenge my sister’s death / Convince Gwendolyn to 
join my gang because I have fallen in love with her. 
Gift – The Needle of Living Shadows: Thanks to the power of the magic needle 
the Cauldron Fae gave him, once per Scene Pietrik can separate the shadow from 
his body and make it act like an NPC with its own profile. The shadow has 1 
Wound and can slip through the smallest crack. 
Gift – Personal Seal: Pietrik has the Seal of Disbelief tattooed on the palm 
of his hand, so once per Scene, he can activate the Gift The Magic of Disbelief 
(page 84). As with the Gang Seal Gift (page 87), Pietrik can activate any 
Seal present in London. 
The Skullem Factory
Descriptor: A large shipyard in which metal components for ships are 
being assembled.
Information: Inside the factory, there are two large industrial 
ovens for metal welding. The goal of the Lost Boys is to blow 
them up by throwing the magic berries that Pietrik has given 
out into them. The raid will take place at night and involves 10 
Lost Boys, in addition to Pietrik. 
Seals: The only Seal in the area of the Skullem Factory is 
located in the alley at the back of the building.

87
The Gang of the Lost Boys
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are young rebels who have lost everything 
and are ready to die for our leader Pietrik.
Agenda: To help Pietrik in his plan for the destruction of 
London. 
Gift – Gang Seal:  Each gang member has a tattooed eye 
on their right hand that allows them to see and use the Seals 
of the Cauldron Fae scattered throughout London.
Gift – Rioter:  Each gang member increases his OL by 
1 when acting with the support of at least one teammate. 
Each member has a magic berry that they can throw which 
will explode on contact, inflicting 1 Wound on all targets 
within several feet. A Defense Check allows a target to leap 
away from the blast zone.    
Kensington Gardens
Descriptor: A large green area in the heart of London. 
Access to the gardens is prohibited due to ongoing 
maintenance work. 
Information: The Kensington Gardens have been long 
abandoned because the Cauldron Fae uses her magic to 
create unending problems for the gardeners. No one has 
entered the gardens for over a year and Pietrik has used an 
old gardeners’ house as a prison to keep Gwendolyn locked 
up. The girl hasn’t tried to escape and hopes, so far in vain, 
to convince Pietrik to give up on his destructive plans. 
It is possible to descend through a well in 
the eastern part of the park to access the 
Cauldron Fae’s Lair. 
Seals: The Gardens are literally full of 
Seals.
Gwendolyn
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young heir of 
the Carnarvon family, but I dream 
of a life of travel and freedom, 
exploring the world. I am good at 
telling stories, and that is how I 
got closer to Pietrik.
Agenda: To convince Pietrik 
to desist from his revenge. 
I understand how much 
pain his heart holds and 
I would like to welcome 
him into my family to 
give him a different 
future.

88
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Cauldron Fae reaches 4 Power Tokens:  she 
will be ready to kill all humans in London but will not do so 
until she has secured Pietrik safely in her lair.
If Pietrik understands the true objective of the 
Cauldron Fae: he will betray his mistress to try to save 
Gwendolyn and the Lost Boys.
If the Hunters free Gwendolyn: the young woman will 
tell the sad tale of Pietrik’s past, trying to make the Hunters 
understand that the boy acts out of resentment and not true 
evil.
If the Hunters capture one of the Lost Boys:  if put 
under pressure, he will reveal that the whole gang follows 
Pietrik’s lead and he is the only one who knows the identity 
of the sorceress who has bestowed powers on everyone. 
If the Hunters use Gwendolyn as bait for Pietrik:  
the young man will give up any involvement with the gang 
to be with his beloved. 
If the Lost Boys succeed in destroying the Skullem 
Factory: one of them will be injured in the explosion and 
can be captured by the Hunters. 
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the I Don’t 
Believe Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if 
they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous 
one.
Seal of Disbelief
Requirement: Capture or eliminate a member of the Lost 
Boys.
Y ou studied the strange Seal that unites the entire gang 
of young rebels and created one just like it on your hand. 
When you bring your hand over your right eye you can see 
the Seals of the Cauldron Fae engraved throughout London. 
By spending 1 Soma you can cross a Seal to get to another 
Seal whose location you know. Thanks to the Seal, you can 
perform a Defense Check to prevent the Magic of Disbelief 
from having an effect on you. 
Support of a Lord 
Requirement: Save Gwendolyn from Pietrik’s clutches.
Y ou saved the young heir to the Carnarvon family and for 
this, you were given a medallion bearing the family coat 
of arms. Receive 1 Additional Success when you use the 
Carnarvon coat of arms in a context when having a powerful 
noble’s favor might be useful. Add the Medallion bearing the 
Carnarvon Coat of Arms and a large sum of money to your 
equipment.
The Challenger (Special Gift for James the 
Swordsman)
Requirement: Face Pietrik or the Lost Boys at least once.
The young rioters and their boss remind you of the band

1
2a
2b
89
of cowards who trapped you and are responsible for you 
losing your hand. Receive 1 Additional Success for every 
action you take against them if it means you defeat or 
humiliate them. If you abandon a fight or are forced to 
retreat, you lose 1 Soma out of shame.
The Scenario I Don’t 
Believe in Scenes 
FIRST SCENE – The summoning
It’s the same one you find on page 82. 
End of Scene:  After the interview with George, the 
Hunters can decide whether to collect clues at the 
Carnarvon villa (SECOND SCENE OPTION A – On 
Gwendolyn’s trail) or go to the Sirens’ Inn to talk to Basil 
(SECOND SCENE OPTION B – Basil’s tale).
SECOND SCENE OPTION A 
– On Gwendolyn’s trail 
Read aloud to the Hunters: George takes you through the 
private rooms of his villa, to Gwendolyn’s room. Waiting for 
you is the girl’s personal maid, a thirty-year-old woman with 
typical Eastern European features. The servant introduces 
herself as Nana, a nickname Gwendolyn had given her. The 
room of the young Carnarvon girl is the classic room of a 
young lady, full of books and maps of the world.
Place: Gwendolyn Carnarvon’s bedroom.
Time: A bit later in the afternoon of a gloomy autumn day.
Information: Speaking with Nana, it will be clear to 
the Hunters that the servant is sincerely concerned about 
Gwendolyn’s fate; this should dispel any doubts about the 
servant’s involvement in the disappearance. Nana will tell 
the Hunters about the frequent night visits that Gwendolyn 
received, but without being able to put a face or name to 
her nightly visitor. At the end of the interview, Nana will 
give the Hunters the poppy petals she found under the bed. 
From a quick investigation, the Hunters will discover that 
those flowers are typical of the Kensington Gardens.
End of Scene: The Hunters will move towards the gardens 
to follow the petals’ trail. Go to the THIRD SCENE – The 
Gardens.
SECOND SCENE OPTION B – 
Basil’s Tale
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You easily find the Sirens’ 
Inn; it is a dive bar in the slums where the poor and drunks 
belly up at the bar for a cheap beer. You recognize Basil from 
the leg bandage and the old wooden crutch resting next to 
his chair. The young man looks drunk, sipping a beer while 
complaining about the misfortune he had in ending up in the 
service of Lord Carnarvon.
Place: The run-down Sirens’ Inn, full of patrons and poor 
people.
Time: The sun has just set and the large fireplace of the inn 
casts the patrons’ long shadows on the dirty walls. 
Information: Talking to Basil, you will get all the 
information on page 85. During the interview, the 
Hunters will notice a boy watching them sitting in front of 
one of the large windows. Once Pietrik understands that

3
4
90
the Hunters are investigating the Lost Boys, he will launch an 
assault supported by 8 of his crew. If the fight turns against 
them, Pietrik and his comrades will escape thanks to the 
Seals. However, at least one of the Lost Boys will stay behind 
and can be captured by the Hunters. After the interrogation, 
the Hunters will ascertain that the gang’s next target is the 
Skullem Factory. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters must run to prevent the 
impending attack on the factory. Go to FOURTH SCENE – 
The assault on Skullem.
THIRD SCENE –  The Gardens
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The Kensington Gardens are 
an architectural and botanical wonder, and even months of 
abandonment by the gardeners can’t entirely spoil their beauty. 
Lord Carnarvon got you a key with great ease and you can 
venture into the park using the main gate. As you step forward, 
your attention is drawn to a small house in the middle of the 
park where you clearly see a human figure holding a lantern. 
It is more than a little suspicious, as no one is supposed to be 
in the Gardens.
Place: The beautiful Kensington Gardens. 
Time: Night has just fallen.
Information: Once at the house, the 
Hunters will surprise Pietrik as he brings 
food to Gwendolyn. The young man will 
try to drive the Hunters out. If it fails, he 
will flee, flying away (thanks to the power 
of the Personal Seal Gift). By interrogating Gwendolyn, the 
Hunters will learn of the attack on the Skullem Factory. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters must run to prevent the 
impending attack on the factory. Go to FOURTH SCENE – 
The assault on Skullem.  
FOURTH SCENE –  The assault 
on Skullem
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you reach the factory, 
the first flames are already devouring the building. Among 
workers and guards on the run, you clearly see a gang of boys 
throwing strange berries that explode like bombs when they 
hit the ground. The youngsters’ targets are the two large ovens, 
which, if blown up, would intensify the flames, engulfing the 
entire district.
Place: The Skullem Factory, besieged by the Lost Boys.
Time: It is late at night, but the flames that lick the building 
illuminate everything as if it were day.
Information: Pietrik, with the support of 
10 Lost Boys, will fight the Hunters. At the 
end of the clash, several situations may occur.
 ◆ If Pietrik is captured by the Hunters, he 
can be persuaded to speak (using Gwendolyn 
as a lever will be a great strategy); the young 
man will reveal the location of the Cauldron 
Fae’s Lair.
 ◆ If Pietrik escapes, one of the captured 
Lost Boys will reveal what Pietrik said: the

5
91
power of the Seals comes from a sorcerer hiding under 
Kensington Gardens.
 ◆ In both cases, the captured gang member will reveal that 
the goal of the Lost Boys is to destroy what is modern in 
order to bring peace to London. 
End of Scene: It will be clear for the Hunters that the one 
who pulls the strings of the whole story hides under the 
Kensington Gardens. Go to FIFTH SCENE – The Cauldron 
Fae.
FIFTH SCENE –  The Cauldron 
Fae
This Scene has no precise breakdown because it can be 
approached in different ways by the Hunters. The Cauldron 
Fae will be willing to compromise to save Pietrik, or fight to 
the death if the Hunters prove hostile. Whatever happens, 
it will be clear that the old fairy cannot accept the change 
that is slowly transforming her beloved city into a large 
industrial metropolis.

92
Once upon a time…
Strange news comes from far away Iceland: the 
great volcano Laki has unleashed its full might 
and has stricken down the poor inhabitants of 
the small villages on the island, like a divine 
punishment. Few have managed to escape the fury 
of fire and ash, but one in particular, the humble 
logger Heino, tells of strange events in his village 
and of an ancient power that seems to have taken 
possession of many young inhabitants. The first 
rescue expedition was sent to the small village of 
Hosn, but no news has reached Denmark for over 
a month. Considering Heino’s delirious tales, the 
crown has decided to turn directly to the Papacy 
for help, hoping the Church can determine if the 
Devil himself could be behind the terrible famine. 
A small ship has set sail for Iceland, though the 
island is still shrouded in the fumes of Laki’s fury. 
Among the crew is a group of Hunters, handpicked 
to unravel this mystery.
A Scenario Inspired by Hansel 
and Gretel (Grimm Brothers)
Hunger Island
Safety Keywords:  
Cannibalism / Catastrophe / Deprivation

93
The story that led us here…
A few months have passed since the eruption of 
the volcano Laki, which has wiped out most of the livestock 
and inhabitants of the island, throwing Iceland into the 
greatest famine of its history. In the village of Hosn, the 
residents have found a macabre way to stave off starvation. 
They sacrifice and feed on the weakest among them. Heino, 
a poor woodcutter, sensed that his beloved children Hanse 
and Gréte would be the next victims. Rather than see them 
served up as meat, Heino led them to the base of Laki, 
where he hoped they would find a quicker and less painful 
end. In the mists and fumes of the volcano, however, 
the two young people did not find death, but an ancient 
stone, fallen from the sky at the dawn of the world itself. 
Corrupted by the power enclosed in it, Hanse and Gréte 
have changed, becoming superhuman beings who need 
fresh meat to constantly regenerate their bodies. Deciding 
to spread their new ‘gifts’ , the two children returned to 
Hosn. Brother and sister infected the other village children 
with the stone’s power, putting power in new hands. Now 
adults have become livestock for the children exposed to 
the power of the stone. The new mutants call themselves 
Maws, the name by which Hanse and Gréte baptized this 
new breed of Icelandic people. In one case, however, Hanse 
granted his sister’s Gréte request for mercy. The two Maws 
escorted their father to the port of Vík í Mýrdal, putting 
him on one of the last ships to leave for the continent.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Most of the island is under the control of the Maws, who 
lack fresh food and now guard Vík í Mýrdal in the hope of 
ambushing incoming ships. Berend, captain of the ship of 
the previous expedition, and Althea, a traveling nun in the 
service of the Order, are also hiding in Vík í Mýrdal, now 
a ghost town. The Maws control the only two inhabited 
places that escaped the fury of the volcano Laki: the port 
of Vík í Mýrdal and the village of Hosn. They also guard 
the volcano itself, where the stone is venerated as a deity. 
The lack of food has made the Maws increasingly aggressive 
and violent, and even Hanse and Gréte are beginning to 
struggle to keep them in control. In Hosn, where the few 
remaining humans are kept in a large cage waiting to be 
slaughtered, Hanse must increasingly maintain order by 
force, to prevent the most hungry Maws from massacring 
the few survivors and consuming all the food stocks in 
one night. In Vík í Mýrdal, Gréte leads a group of the most 
faithful Maws, looking for a way to attract new boats to the 
island.

1
94
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  After more than a week of 
sailing, you finally catch a glimpse of the Icelandic coast and the 
outlines of the port of Vík í Mýrdal. The smoking silhouette of the 
great volcano stands out on the horizon like a hulking monster 
looming over the entire island. With a few skillful maneuvers, 
Captain Arne guides the ship into port, easily navigating the 
thick blanket of fog and soot that seems to envelop everything. 
The air is heavy and, around you, the world is a clouded, smoky 
gray. You can feel a thrill of fear going up your back as you 
contemplate the unnatural silence surrounding the port. No one 
is present to welcome you and, at a closer look, the city seems 
deserted.
Place: The port of Vík í Mýrdal, lost in fog.
Time: It is early afternoon, even though the fog that covers 
the whole island means the light is dulled to the point of soft  
twilight.
Initial Situation: The ship is docked in the port and it is 
immediately clear to everyone that the city is uninhabited. 
The ship that brought the previous expedition is docked not 
far away, looking abandoned as well. 
Information: When interacting with Captain Arne, the 
Hunters will learn that the entire crew is quite tense. The sailors 
are skilled sea wolves, and for this reason deeply superstitious. 
Only Arne, who comes from a military background, remains 
cold and calculating at the spectacle of the ghost port. Arne 
will guarantee the full support of his men to the Hunters: it 
will be up to them to decide whether or not to make use of 
the support. 
Note: In this Scenario the Hunters will travel between Vík 
í Mýrdal, the village of Hosn, and the volcano Laki. Each 
journey takes 2 days on foot and must pass through The Mist 
Roads (page 98).
Arne the Captain
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I once was a military man and I’ve earned the 
crown’s respect. Now, as a ship’s captain, I serve my country and 
boast about my crew. 
Agenda: Do my best to carry out the mission I was entrusted 
with. However, I won’t put the people under my command at 
risk.
Sailors
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are sailors under Captain Arne’s orders. We 
are skilled sea wolves, more comfortable at sea than on land.
Agenda: Execute Arne’s orders and avoid in any way having to 
face the supernatural.

95
The Port of Vík í 
Mýrdal
Descriptor: The large port of a city 
that was built to be a point of arrival 
from the mainland. Ships and houses are 
in a state of complete disuse.
Information: Walking around the 
port and on the ship of the previous 
expedition, the Hunters can obtain in 
order the following information:
 ◆ The city is indeed deserted. Crates 
still hanging on hoists and torches 
left untended, so that parts of the 
docks are charred and burned, 
suggest that the inhabitants have 
abandoned what they were doing 
when disaster struck.
 ◆ Traces of blood and the remains of 
devoured humans can be found in 
the dark recesses of the harbor. It is 
as if a pack of wild beasts assaulted the inhabitants to 
drag them into darkness and devour them.
 ◆ In the small harbor master’s office, the Hunters will 
find on the front door a message written in blood: the 
lighthouse is safe. The message is written in poor Latin, 
with the clear intention of making it unreadable for the 
natives of the island. It indicates the place where Althea 
and Berend took refuge, and was written by the nun for 
a possible group of Hunters. 
For any information the Hunters receive, 
you can decide to roll a die or choose 
what happens:
1-2: A group of 8 Maws ambushes the 
Hunters.
3-4: A group of 8 Maws led by Gréte 
ambushes the Hunters. Gréte will 
remain on the sidelines to lead the 
attack and gather information about the 
newcomers.
5-6: Berend watches the Hunters from 
afar to discern who they are and why 
they’ve come. He will eventually try to 
approach them for help, but only when 
it is safe from the Maws.
Gréte
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC  
(4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the incarnation of 
the power of the great Maw. My body 
regenerates thanks to the flesh I eat, my strength and my 
speed are unbelievable. 
Agenda: Keep capturing new humans to ensure a future 
for the Maw’s offspring. Unlike my brother Hanse, I still have 
a shred of humanity and I wouldn’t want to devour anyone 
indiscriminately / I want to convince my friend Ina to become 
a Maw.
Gift – Mutant Maw:  As long as they maintain their 
original appearance, a mutant Maw appears in every way as 
a common child. When they unleash their power, the body 
shifts and the mouth widens disproportionately, revealing a

96
long line of sharp teeth. A mutant is considered to be of a 
higher OL category for every physical action they perform 
involving speed, strength, or ferocity. A mutant perceives 
the smell of human flesh, which they find intoxicating, and 
for this reason every attempt to hide from their sense of 
smell receives 1 Penalty. When a mutant begins to devour 
flesh (human or animal), they cannot stop until they have 
finished it. Once per Scene, when a mutant Maw inflicts 
1 Wound by devouring fresh meat, they recover 1 Wound 
they suffered.
Gift – First Maw:  As the firstborn among the Maws, 
Gréte has learned to manipulate the power that she 
harbors in her body. During a Scene, Gréte may self-inflict 
1 Wound to benefit from one of the following effects:  
1. Superhuman Strength, if she inflicts 1 Wound with 
an action and does not devour the target, Gréte can throw 
it 15 feet away. The target will fall to the ground and suffer 1 
additional Wound if they impact an obstacle. Gréte can use 
her strength to throw one enemy into another, in which case 
the target can try to dodge the attack with a Defense Check. 
2. Superhuman Speed , Gréte can make an incredible 
leap of over 30 feet. She does not need any preparation, 
and she can take another subject with her while moving. 
3. Rebirth, Gréte falls into a state of apparent death to 
regenerate in the next Scene. She wakes up completely 
healed. Only a highly skilled doctor or someone using 
magical means is able to discern Gréte is not truly dead.  
The Maws
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are a new people, children of the great 
Maw. Our bodies regenerate thanks to the flesh we eat. Our 
strength and speed are incredible. 
Agenda: We obey Gréte and Hanse, they are the firstborns, 
they know the right path / Satiate our infinite hunger, even at 
the cost of disobeying the firstborns.
Gift – Mutant Maw:  As long as they maintain their 
original appearance, a mutant Maw appears in every way as 
a common child. When they unleash their power, the body 
shifts and the mouth widens disproportionately, revealing 
a long line of sharp teeth. A mutant is considered to be of a 
higher OL category for every physical action they perform 
involving speed, strength, or ferocity. A mutant perceives 
the smell of human flesh, which they find intoxicating, and 
for this reason every attempt to hide from their sense of 
smell receives 1 Penalty. When a mutant begins to devour 
flesh (human or animal), they cannot stop until they have 
finished it. Once per Scene, when a mutant Maw inflicts 
1 Wound by devouring fresh meat, they recover 1 Wound 
they suffered.

97
Berend the Crazed Captain 
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the captain of the previous expedition 
and the only one left of my crew. I learned to survive by 
masking my smell with the stench of rotten fish.
Agenda: Wait for someone to come from the mainland to 
save me.
Gift – Stinking Survivor:  Berend managed to avoid 
being devoured by the Maws, but what he saw happen 
to his companions scarred his mind forever. The man 
constantly covers himself with rotten fish because he has 
discovered the stench conceals him from the Maws. 
Berend will never be targeted by a Maw unless he 
takes direct action against them. As long as he 
is at the port of Vík í Mýrdal, Berend can move 
with great skill through the alleys and take 
any companions anywhere in the city without 
attracting the attention of the Maws. Each 
time he uses this ability, he rolls 1 die: on 5 
or 6, Berend convinces himself that he has 
seen other survivors and makes noise in an 
attempt to get their attention. It will be 
clear to those that accompany him that 
the whole situation is nothing 
more than a figment 
of the poor man’s 
distressed mind.
Vík í Mýrdal’s Lighthouse
Descriptor: An old lighthouse in the oldest part of the port 
of Vík í Mýrdal. Crates of fish have been broken open and 
spilled around the lighthouse, their contents left to rot in the 
sun. Now, the air reeks in a nauseating way.
Gift – Safe Haven from the Maws: The stacks of rotten 
fish that Berend and Althea constantly throw at the foot of 
the lighthouse keep the Maws away. The Maws lose all trace 
of someone who takes refuge in the lighthouse. While near 
the lighthouse, Maws reduce their Opposition Level by 1.
Information: Althea will have a great deal of information 
for the Hunters. She has been trapped for a month now, and 
has taken the opportunity to study the Maws in depth.
 ◆ The Maws look like children mutated by something. 
They are strong, fast, and seem to follow the smell of 
human flesh.
 ◆ Some desperate people fleeing from the village of Hosn 
have told them of two “leaders” of the Maws, brother 
and sister, who apparently are smarter and more 
powerful than the others.
 ◆ After overhearing Maws talking to each other, Althea 
realized that the volcano is a sacred place for those 
strange beings that humans must not enter.
 ◆ Both the village and the volcano are two days’ walk 
from Vík í Mýrdal, but in different directions.

98
Althea the Agent of the Order
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a nun in the service of the Order and, 
despite my ecclesiastical background, I have skills as a spy and 
a fighter. The Order entrusts me with high-risk tasks because 
it has confidence in my unwavering faith and ruthlessness.
Agenda: Solve the mystery of the Maws and provide support 
to the Hunters of the Order.
Gift – Agent of the Order: Althea is talented and trained to support the Hunters. Althea can spend her action to 
support a Hunter’s action and give them 1 Additional 
Success in any Check.
The Mist Roads
Descriptor: All the communication routes between Vík í 
Mýrdal and the nearby villages are surrounded by a dense, 
dark fog that does not allow you to see more than a few feet 
ahead.
For each trip on the Mist Roads, you can choose or roll for 
a Threat/encounter with an NPC.
1-2: A crevasse created by the eruption of Laki has 
opened up, barring the way.
Threat – Crevasse in the Mist 
Descriptor: A crevasse eighteen feet deep, hidden by the 
dense mist.
OL Medium 5
Falling into the crevasse will cause 1 Wound. At the bottom, 
you can find the body of a desperate runaway. As a cost for 
a Defense Check Outcome, a Hunter may lose equipment 
that will fall down into the mist.
3-4: The mist becomes even thicker, making it 
impossible to even distinguish the road.
Threat – Mist Wall
Descriptor: A fog so dense that it is impossible to see 
anything.
OL Hard 7
If they fail a Check to stay on the road, the Hunters will get 
lost and end up in the wrong place.
5-6: A group of Maws. A group of 4 Maws who travel in 
search of fresh meat.

99
The Village of Hosn
Descriptor: A small village lost in the hills. Many houses 
have been burned down, and the stench of corpses hovers in 
the air.
Information: The village is under Hanse’s control. In the 
small central square, a rudimentary wooden cage was built 
where the last six inhabitants of the village are trapped. 
Every day, one inhabitant is taken out to be devoured, but 
now there are more than twenty Maws and so little flesh is 
not enough to satisfy their hunger. 
By saving the inhabitants, the Hunters will be able to obtain 
some information:
 ◆ Ina is a little girl who refused to become a Maw. She is a 
friend of Gréte, who explicitly asked her brother Hanse 
to keep Ina last, in the hope that she will change her 
mind and convert. Ina is aware of the friendship that 
Gréte feels towards her and how this is a weak point 
within the Maw.
 ◆ Askell is a young hunter who killed a Maw when Hanse 
and Gréte took control of the village. As punishment his 
hands were devoured and now Askell is kept alive only 
to see all his loved ones die one after the other. He hates 
the Maws and killing them has become an obsession 
for him. Askell knows a secret road that allows you to 
reach the volcano, avoiding the complications of the 
Mist Roads. 
 ◆ Alba is Askell’s mother. She is now utterly devastated, 
after seeing her husband Helg eaten alive by the Maws 
and her son reduced to impotence. Alba has a key to 
the cellars of the town hall, a shelter from volcanic 
eruptions where many supplies are stashed. 
 ◆ Enoch is one of the village elders. He was the first to 
acknowledge the idea of eating the helpless to survive. 
All the Maws hate him and would attack him without 
a second thought, if only they had the chance. Hanse 
keeps him alive, pondering the most convoluted 
possible way to eliminate him when the time comes.
 ◆ Erna is a young woman without children. She has 
always hated living on the island and was only here to 
follow her husband who has already been devoured 
by the Maws. Erna has heard Hanse talking to other 
Maws about a “stone that came from the sky” kept in 
the volcano, which must be defended at all costs. 
 ◆ Hafnar is a plump businessman who is being fed with 
the little food left in the village. Hanse’s intention is to 
fatten up Hafnar to make him more delicious for his 
Maws. Hafnar possesses a dose of a potent rat poison 
that he has already used to kill two Maws in the first 
few days. His intention is to sprinkle his body with 
the poison on the day he is devoured, to take as many 
Maws with him as possible.

100
Hanse
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor:  I am the incarnation of the power of the great Maw. My body 
regenerates thanks to the flesh I eat. My strength and my speed are unbelievable. 
Agenda: Keep capturing new humans to ensure a future for the Maw’s offspring. 
Unlike my sister Gréte, I have no regard for humans and, now that I spared my 
father to make my sister happy, I just want to protect the stone that came from 
the sky.
Gift – Mutant Maw:  As long as they maintain their original appearance, a 
mutant Maw appears in every way as a common child. When they unleash their 
power, the body shifts and the mouth widens disproportionately, revealing a 
long line of sharp teeth. A mutant is considered to be of a higher OL category 
for every physical action they perform involving speed, strength, or ferocity. 
A mutant perceives the smell of human flesh, which they find intoxicating, 
and for this reason, every attempt to hide from their sense of smell receives 
1 Penalty. When a mutant begins to devour flesh (human or animal), they 
cannot stop until they have finished it. Once per Scene, when a mutant Maw 
inflicts 1 Wound by devouring fresh meat, they recover 1 Wound they suffered.
Gift – First Maw:  As the firstborn among the Maws, Hanse has learned 
to manipulate the power that he harbors in his body. During a Scene, 
Hanse may self-inflict 1 Wound to benefit from one of the following effects:  
1. Superhuman Strength , if he inflicts 1 Wound with an action and 
does not devour the target, Hanse can throw it 15 feet away. The target 
will fall to the ground and suffer 1 additional Wound if they impact an 
obstacle. Hanse can use his strength to throw one enemy into another, in 
which case the target can try to dodge the attack with a Defense Check.  
2. Superhuman Speed , Hanse can make an incredible leap of over 30 
feet. He does not need any preparation, and he can take another subject with 
him while moving. 3. Power of the Stone that Came from the Sky , 
Hanse can tap into the latent power in his body. Once per Scene, all human 
subjects in his presence lose 2 Soma or alternatively can resist by taking 1 
Wound. Suffering the last Wound for this effect does not cause death, but a 
loss of consciousness.

101
Laki the Volcano
Descriptor: A great volcano that dominates all of Iceland. 
The rock walls have cracked due to the powerful eruptions, 
creating a series of natural tunnels and galleries. 
Information: The volcano hides the stone that came 
from the sky among a maze of tunnels. After the first 
eruptions, the stone has re-emerged, manifesting its power. 
To protect the stone, Hanse has left all the Maws that have 
had issues managing the mutations at the volcano. Those 
abandoned wretches have grown into Crazed Maws, 
brainless monsters who wander in search of food. It’s 
possible for the Hunters to track the Crazed Maws to the 
stone’s hiding place. Otherwise, the Hunters will face the 
Threat Daedalus of Tunnels.
Threat – Daedalus of Tunnels
Descriptor: A series of natural tunnels that change shape 
according to the tectonic shocks that hit the volcano.
OL Hard 7
Failing the Check means getting lost, meeting one or more 
Crazed Maws, or ending up caught in a sudden lava flow, 
which inflicts 1 Wound and the destruction of one or more 
pieces of equipment.
Crazed Maws
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: A mad abomination whose form constantly 
shifts, as they wander in search of living beings to devour. 
Agenda: Eat every living thing, except other Maws.
Gift – Mutant Abomination:  A Crazed Maw does not 
feel any pain. For this reason the Wounds it suffers have 
no narrative effect but are only a counter to understand 
when the abomination will crumble into a thousand pieces. 
Tentacles and arms are constantly born from the body of 
a Crazed Maw, and as a result it can act 2 times during 
an Exchange. With one of its actions, a Crazed Maw can 
slam its huge mass to the ground to create a strong tremor. 
All targets in the immediate vicinity must pass a Defense 
Check to avoid falling to the ground.
Threat – The Stone that Came from 
the Sky
Descriptor: An alien stone, the size of a fist, that thrums 
with energy. 
Gift – Alien Stone:  All the powers of the Maws derive 
from the stone. With its destruction, the mutations will 
deteriorate, causing the death of all mutants except Hanse 
and Gréte. The stone has 6 Wounds and has no OL, because 
it cannot interact directly or defend itself. For each Wound 
suffered, a spark of the power comes out, affecting the one 
who inflicted the blow. Whoever suffers this effect can 
decide between losing 2 Soma or suffering 1 Wound. If the 
stone is destroyed or stolen, Hanse will be able to unleash 
all the latent power in him and, by joining with Gréte, give 
life to the Great Maw.

102
Threat – The Great Maw
Descriptor: A colossal titan of flesh and tentacles born from 
the union of the two first Maws. The creature acts to recover 
the stone or avenge its destruction, at the cost of destroying the 
entire island in the process.
OL Hard 7
Note: The Storyteller must immediately clarify that the 
Great Maw is a monster so powerful that it cannot be faced 
in combat. Its ever-growing mass instantly regenerates every 
wound, and its colossal size is beyond the reach of any magic. 
Rather than confronting the Great Maw, the Hunters must 
curb its action as they rescue the survivors.
Here is a list of potential situations:
 ◆ The stone can be used as bait to force the Great Maw to 
follow it. Throwing it in the middle of the volcano will 
cause the titan to follow it, burning to death in a lava pit.
 ◆ One can still leverage Gréte’s consciousness and her 
sympathy for Ina, making the Great Maw hesitate for a 
few moments before Hanse regains control.
 ◆ When a Defense Check against the Great Maw is failed, 
in addition to suffering 1 Wound, the target will be 
pushed away by over thirty feet, suffering 1 additional 
Wound if it impacts something.
 ◆ With the force of its roar alone, the Great Maw can hurl 
a human to the ground.
 ◆ With a tentacle, the Great Maw can “pick up” a house and 
hurl it at someone.
 ◆ Escaping the Great Maw is not easy and the survivors of 
the village will be a burden for the Hunters.
 ◆ The Great Maw cannot swim and will stop its chase once 
the Hunters successfully take to the sea.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel free 
to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.
If the Hunters dismantle the Maw packs in Vík í 
Mýrdal: Gréte will approach the Hunters pretending to 
be a survivor. The intention of the Maw is to study these 
new enemies and provide useful information to her brother 
Hanse.
If the Hunters send sailors alone to explore the port 
of Vík í Mýrdal: in a short time, the poor unfortunate will 
be attacked and overwhelmed by the Maws.
If Gréte understands that the Hunters are here to 
save the inhabitants:  the Maw could reveal herself and 
negotiate to save her friend Ina.
If the Hunters arrive in Hosn without worrying 
about being seen: all the Maws will present themselves as 
common children and ambush the Hunters when their guard 
is down. 
If the Hunters steal or destroy the stone that came 
from the sky:  mad with rage, Hanse will join his sister 
Gréte to fuse into the Great Maw.
If Gréte is cornered by the Hunters:  she will use her 
gift to appear dead and, as soon as she wakes up, she will run 
to join her brother.

103
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Hunger 
Island Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions are 
met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.
Stinky
Requirement: Meet Berend and learn from him how to 
mask your scent.
Y ou know how to make your scent unrecognizable and 
confuse enemies and predators. Y ou can spend 1 Soma and 
cover yourself with a rotting substance that will make you 
smelly. An enemy who bases their actions on smell (like a 
Maw) will never target you unless you act directly against 
them. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success to all Defense Checks 
you make to defend from an attack carried out by an enemy 
sensitive to your aroma. Y ou receive 1 Penalty at all Checks 
with ordinary people when your nauseating smell is a clear 
obstacle. 
The Stone that Came from the Sky
Requirement: Recover the stone that came from the sky 
from the slopes of the volcano.
Y ou hold an alien stone with great powers, which you can 
activate by suffering 1 Wound. When you do, choose from 
one of the following effects. 1. Might: Y our strength triples 
and you get 1 Additional Success to any Position Check 
based on sheer strength for the rest of the Scene. 2. Speed: 
Y our speed triples and you get 1 Additional Success at any 
Position Check based on speed for the rest of the Scene. 
3. Energy: Y our body is infused with pure energy and you 
receive 3 Energy Tokens for the rest of the Scene. Y ou or 
another nearby magic practitioner can use 1 Energy Token 
to have 1 Additional Success when using any kind of magic. 
Y ou can activate more than one effect of this Gift in the same 
Scene by suffering additional Wounds. Wounds you suffer in 
this way cannot be recovered in the current Scene.
This is My House (Special Gift for the Woman 
from the Wood)
Requirement: Reach the village of Hosn.
Y ou remember this place, this is where you lived, and this 
is where you worked as a baker. With your delicious sweets, 
you fed the poor children of the village, but then someone 
whose face you don’t remember poisoned what you created, 
killing those you cared about. Y ou forgot what you did to 
keep from succumbing to pain. Now you can decide:
 
 Forget what happened again.
In this case, deep anger will accompany you throughout your 
stay on the island. Y ou always get 1 Additional Success when 
you act to eradicate a threat that puts the life of a helpless 
human at risk.
 Remember who you were. 
Y ou will return to be the witch of your past, but this will 
mean profoundly changing who you are now. Permanently 
replace your sheet with that of Katharina the Sweet Witch 
found at the end of this Scenario.

2
Special
104
The Scenario 
Hunger Island in 
Scenes 
FIRST SCENE –  Arrival at the 
port of Vík í Mýrdal
It’s the same one you find on page 94. While wandering 
around the port, the Hunters will be attacked by the 
Maws and then will meet Berend, who will invite them to 
the lighthouse. Gréte, left in the shadows to observe the 
situation, will flee to Hosn to warn her brother of the arrival 
of the Hunters.
End of Scene: Berend takes the Hunters to the lighthouse 
to meet Althea. Go to the SECOND SCENE – The lighthouse.
SECOND SCENE –  
The lighthouse
Read aloud to the Hunters: An excited Berend takes you 
to the oldest part of the port of Vík í Mýrdal. Crates of fish 
have been spilled in the area around the lighthouse and now 
rot in the sun, causing a reek that makes sore vomit rise at 
the back of your throat. Berend explains, with witty eyes, that 
the monsters he calls Maws are drawn by the scent of humans 
and are confused by the smell of rot. With pride, Berend 
raises his dirty shirt and shows you a hairy torso encrusted 
with dirt and fish guts. As he starts to pull his pants down, 
a female voice tells him to stop. Althea, the emissary of the 
Order who came here before you, comes out of the door of 
the old lighthouse and yells at the poor madman who led you 
here.
Place: The old lighthouse of the port that Althea and 
Berend have fortified to keep the Maws away.
Time: It is late afternoon as the sun begins its descent, 
hidden by the dense clouds fed by the fumes of the volcano.
Information: Althea will be grateful to the Hunters 
for their arrival. Although the woman is extremely worn 
by exhaustion and hunger, she will give the Hunters the 
information on page 97. Althea will ask the Hunters to 
accompany her to Hosn, to strike at the “leaders” of the 
Maws and put an end to the threat they represent. It is clear 
to the Order’s agent that the island’s stocks of both animals 
and humans are rapidly depleting, and she fears it’s only 
a matter of time before the Maws organize themselves to 
set sail for the mainland. Alternatively, Althea will follow 
the Hunters to the volcano if that’s where they intend to 
investigate.
End of Scene: The Hunters leave with Althea for Hosn or 
the Volcano. Go to SPECIAL SCENE – Along the way.
SPECIAL SCENE –  Along the 
way
This Scene must be played every time the Hunters set out 
on a journey. Select one of the effects on page 98.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at the village of Hosn. 
Go to THIRD SCENE OPTION A – The village of Hosn or 
to THIRD SCENE OPTION B – Laki, the Volcano.

3b3a
105
THIRD SCENE OPTION A –  
The village of Hosn
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You arrive at Hosn following 
a little used farm track, convinced that your enemies are posted 
everywhere. A series of cries and moans draws your attention. 
In the center of the small town square, a rudimentary wooden 
cage holds six people prisoner. They seem to be ordinary 
villagers, emaciated and shaken by the hardships they have 
endured. Then, the sound of footfalls resonating among the 
semi-destroyed houses make it clear to you that your enemies 
are practically everywhere.
Place: The small village of Hosn, shrouded in fog.
Time: It is a cold day’s dawn and the rays of the sun reflect in 
the fog, making it look almost like a white veil.
Information: Gréte stayed at the village to organize the 
defenses, while Hanse moved to the volcano. The Maws will 
first attempt to overcome the Hunters, taking advantage of the 
numerical superiority. 
 ◆ The weak point of the Maws, however, is to attack without 
any strategy, running at the Hunters like rabid dogs. 
 ◆ If cornered, Gréte will be willing to compromise by 
freeing the villagers, with the exception of Enoch.
 ◆ If released, Ina will try to reason with Gréte, proving to be 
an asset to the Hunters.
 ◆ In the village, there are a total of 18 Maws, moving in 
groups of 6.
End of Scene: After freeing the village, talking to prisoners, 
or interrogating Gréte, the Hunters will clearly understand 
that the origin of all this is in Laki, where Hanse has retreated. 
Go to THIRD SCENE OPTION B –  Laki, the Volcano. If, on 
the other hand, the Hunters come from the volcano, go to the 
FOURTH SCENE – The Great Maw.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B –  
 Laki, the Volcano
Read aloud to the Hunters: Finally, the volcano is no longer 
just a looming shadow that hangs over all of you since you 
landed. Now, it stands out in all its grandeur, its top lost among    
dense clouds. The earth thrums as if following the beating of a 
huge heart, which is nothing more than the core of the volcano 
itself. Among the split rocks, you can see several tunnels that 
seem to unravel inside the volcano, and grotesque and guttural 
sounds make you realize that the frequent eruptions are not the 
worst danger you could encounter.
Place: Laki, the Volcano.
Time: It is a cold day’s dawn and the rays of the sun reflect in 
the fog, making it look almost like a white veil.
Information: The Hunters will almost immediately come 
across several Crazed Maws. To get to the center of the 
volcano, they will have to deal with at least 2, or find a way to 
follow them to the tunnel where the stone is located.
 ◆ Hanse is protecting the stone with 2 Crazed Maws to back 
him up. The boy is willing to bargain with the Hunters for 
the promise that they will leave the island and leave him 
and his new people alone.
 ◆ If cornered, Hanse will throw himself into the volcano 
carrying the stone with him, and the Hunters will see him 
burn in the lava.
 ◆ Hanse will not die in the lava but, consuming all the power 
of the stone, he will emerge a few hours later filled with 
anger for the possibilities lost because of the Hunters.
End of Scene:  Hunters can move to the village of Hosn 
to save the remaining inhabitants. Go to THIRD SCENE 
OPTION A – The village of Hosn. If, on the other hand, the 
Hunters come from the village, go to FOURTH SCENE – The 
Great Maw.

4
106
FOURTH SCENE –  The Great Maw
This Scene begins after the defeat of both siblings. Hanse, driven mad by the 
loss of the stone, returns to Hosn as the Hunters prepare to leave. Gréte, who 
pretended to be dead thanks to her powers, will be reunited with her brother 
and merge with him into the Great Maw. Play the Great Maw Threat as described 
on page 102.
End of Scene: Hunters manage to escape from the island or end the life of the 
Great Maw by making it fall into the volcano. Whatever choice they make, only 
the Hunters can end the danger the Maws represent.

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a capable cook and my body has a sweet smell that relaxes whoever 
is near me. I can tell a lot about people from their food tastes. I deeply 
hate those who hurt or bully children and the helpless.
•  The Sweet Witch: Y ou can spend 1 Soma to offer one of your sweets 
to someone to make them well disposed towards you. Y ou gain 1 Additional 
Success to convince and manipulate those who have eaten one of your sweets 
until the end of the Scene. Y ou can decide that your dessert has an additional 
effect based on its flavor: 
1. Marzipan: Whoever eats it will fall into a deep sleep for several hours. 
2. Candied fruit: The taste of the treat pushes those who are intoxicated to 
reveal what they cherish the most. 
3. Cinnamon: Those who eat it will feel the urgent desire to do more than 
what they are doing, even at the expense of their duty. 
4. Raisins: The taste of the treat pushes those who are intoxicated to reveal 
what they hate the most.
Other Hunters or particularly capable individuals may require a Position 
Check to make use of the power of your sweets.
I am a capable witch and when I need it, the world around me shapes 
itself to protect and shelter me. I hate being out in the open and avoid 
being the center of attention.
•  Home Sweet Home: you can spend 1 Soma to draw on your strange 
witch powers. When you do, choose one of the following effects. 
1. Create a shelter, for one person, perfectly camouflaged within the 
environment. It is very difficult to discover your shelter, though some NPCs 
can come close. At the Storyteller’s discretion, a Defense Check may be made 
to see if you are discovered. 
2. Change the texture of an inanimate object into a soft sweet. Y ou can turn a 
door into marzipan or a sword into colored jelly. 
3. Make an edible sweet and more nutritious. Whoever consumes your sweet 
recovers 2 Soma, except yourself.
Katharina 
the Sweet Witch
I am a witch specializing in creating magic sweets. I have always used my skills to help all the poor children 
who cannot afford something good to eat. Giving a little joy to the poor was what warmed my heart the most. But 
one day, a strange figure called the One Who Plots in the Shadow deceived me by poisoning what I baked with 
so much love. That day, an entire village lost its future forever with the passing of all the children. I am aware 
that I am not really guilty of the deaths of so many innocents, but this doesn’t lift the weight from my heart. All 
I have left is revenge, which I will take as a Hunter of the Order against the one who, on a whim, destroyed 
everything I cared about.

 6
