108
Once upon a time…
Germanic Bavaria. Prince Charles 
Theodore has summoned the Order after 
the strange death of his nephew Ottone. 
He died traveling to the small village of 
Lohr where he had gone to meet his future 
wife, Bianca Karenmann. Although official 
reports speak of a fatal fall from the horse, 
in truth, the young man’s corpse was found 
torn apart, his lungs and other organs 
crushed as if a bomb had exploded within 
his body. Despite the condolences of Karl 
and Elsa Karenmann, Prince Charles wants 
to shed light on his nephew’s death, with the 
hope that doing so would dispel any shadow 
of witchcraft and dark rituals. After all, 
such suspicions had seen Lohr play host to a 
relentless witch-hunt once before.
A Scenario Inspired by Snow 
White (Grimm Brothers)
Bloodwhite’s Heart
Safety Keywords:  
Gore / Body Horror / False Accusations

109
The story that led us here…
During the witch hunt that shook the quiet village 
of Lohr in 1603, a capable and powerful witch named Ursa 
Radh managed to escape the clutches of the inquisition 
for fifteen years, until 1618, when the hunt, unfortunately, 
ended with her capture. While burning at the stake in 
the village’s public square, Ursa cursed the lineage of the 
Karenmanns, invoking the curse of the Restless Blood. 
Karl’s then great-grandfather decided to walk away from 
Lohr, hoping he would escape the curse, and he did. 
Later on, his grandson Karl returned to Lohr, tempted by 
promises of a good political career in the service of Prince 
Charles Theodore. But his return to Lohr awakened Ursa’s 
resentful spirit and the curse struck the Karenmann family. 
Its victim was Bianca, Karl and Elsa’s firstborn daughter. 
From an early age, she could only be satisfied with raw and 
bloody meat. The Karenmanns kept their daughter safe 
from prying eyes, but when Bianca grew old enough, Karl 
couldn’t help but offer her hand in marriage to Ottone, the 
Prince’s nephew. Arriving in Lohr, the greedy and spoiled 
Ottone tried to consummate a relationship with naive 
Bianca, who unintentionally made him explode to defend 
herself, thus discovering another of the powers derived 
from the curse. Panicked, the Karenmann spouses asked 
Svenn, a skilled huntsman and longtime friend, for help, 
asking him to secure the young Bianca. The man took 
Bianca to an old mine where a group of seven Hessian 
deserters had settled, each of whom had been on the run 
for years because of war crimes. The seven of them, all 
suffering from dwarfism, have accepted Bianca into their 
ranks, swearing to keep her safe, provided the Karenmann 
family does the same with them.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Karl and Elsa live in fear that the Prince may send others 
to investigate and, in particular, the Karenmann couple 
know about the Order and its agents who once took part 
in the witch hunt that once befell Lohr. That is why they 
have commissioned Svenn to prepare a trap to mislead any 
potential investigation. Svenn targeted Bruna, an old widow 
who lives apart from the village because many rumors point 
to her being a witch. Svenn hid a book of witchcraft and a 
lock of hair recovered from Ottone’s body in Bruna’s house 
so that any investigation would lead to accusing the woman 
of witchcraft, diverting attention from Bianca. Regarding 
the Karenmanns’ offspring, it was announced that she left  
for Dresden, to enter a prestigious university. In fact, Bianca 
lives hidden in the old mine half a day from the village, kept 
safe by Hessian mercenaries. Bianca and the mercenaries 
have formed a friendly relationship and the young woman 
spends her time caring for the soldiers and preparing their 
meals. Abel, a young man from the village who has always 
been in love with Bianca, did not believe that the young 
Karenmann moved to Dresden to study. Her parents’ love 
and attachment to their daughter are well known and 
they would never have sent her away so easily. Abel has 
repeatedly tried to understand the mystery behind Bianca’s 
disappearance, clashing with the indifference of the village 
and Svenn’s veiled threats.

1
110
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at the small village 
of Lohr early in the morning, while most of the inhabitants are 
busy with the many daily chores. Were it not for the macabre 
discovery of the body of the young Ottone, Lohr would be the 
portrait of a happy and carefree village. In front of you, right in 
the center of the village, the Karenmann family palace stands 
out in all its splendor.
Place: The village of Lohr.
Time: The early morning of a beautiful, sunny day.
Initial Situation: The Hunters are led to a meeting with 
Karl and Elsa Karenmann.
Information: The Karenmann spouses will welcome the 
Hunters with open arms, trying to appear as cooperative 
as possible. From the interview, you can have the following 
information, much of which is tainted by Karl and Elsa to 
mislead any investigation.
 ◆ The body of poor Ottone has already been returned to the 
Prince’s messengers so that he could be taken to his family 
to receive the funeral he deserves.
 ◆ Ottone was found in his room, and it appears that he died 
during the night. The body was in a gruesome condition.
 ◆ The Karenmanns have hired Svenn, a skilled huntsman 
and trusted person who has always provided for the 
village. Svenn’s house is located a few minutes from the 
Karenmann palace and the man is waiting for the Hunters 
to share with them what he has discovered during the last 
week of investigation.
 ◆ The Karenmanns will carefully avoid mentioning Bianca. 
If asked about her, they will say that their daughter has 
moved to Dresden, eager to forget what happened and to 
devote herself to her studies.
Karl and Elsa Karenmann 
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are two loving parents who only want what’s 
best for Bianca. We have great wealth and allies throughout 
Lohr.
Agenda: To have Ottone’s death case closed as soon as possible, 
placing the blame on Bruna / Protect Bianca from a world that 
could not accept her for what she is.
Karenmann Palace
Descriptor: A large and magnificent palace that contrasts 
with the humble appearance of the rest of Lohr’s dwellings.
During the interview, the Hunters will realize that the palace 
is full of paintings depicting the Karenmann family. In many 
paintings, a beautiful girl with long black hair, snow-white 
skin, and a pure and innocent look can be seen. From the 
paintings the great love that unites Elsa and Karl to their 
daughter Bianca is clear.
The Village of Lohr
Descriptor: A small village that is experiencing new prosperity 
thanks to the Karenmann family.
The Hunters may hear some rumors while exploring the 
village. Choose or roll 1 die:

111
1. Ottone quickly showed himself to be a stuck-up, 
arrogant nobleman. Many are happy that sweet 
Bianca Karenmann no longer has to marry him.
2. In the past, Lohr has been at the center of the largest 
witch hunt in all of Germany. Several stakes were left 
in the town square as a warning to all inhabitants.
3. The Karenmann family administers the village with 
care and justice. Father, mother, and daughter have 
always been inseparable.
4. Old Bruna is not well-liked, ever since her husband 
got sick and then died within a week of a strange 
illness. Many people think that it 
was Bruna herself who performed 
witchcraft on her husband, to 
punish him for being a drunk.
5. In years past, the large mine, 
located just over half a day’s 
walk away, provided jobs to 
the villagers. Since it played 
out, the mine has become 
a dangerous place where 
fugitives and deserters often 
find shelter for the night.
6. Bianca is a girl of rare 
beauty, sweet and kind to 
everyone. She has become 
famous in the village for her 
commitment to helping the 
weak and needy. Despite her 
noble background, she never 
hesitated to get her hands 
dirty to help her neighbor.
Information: During the visit to the city, Abel will 
approach the Hunters trying to warn them of the 
disappearance of Bianca Karenmann, which is suspicious 
to him.
Abel the Young Lover
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a young baker who has always been in 
love with Bianca.
Agenda: To find Bianca, as I cannot believe that she has 
abandoned Lohr, nor that her parents would let her go so 
easily.
Svenn’s House
Descriptor: A beautiful manor house, now converted 
into a museum of hunting trophies.
Information: Svenn will greet the Hunters 
with kindness, boasting that he is the man the 
Karenmann family trusts the most. Svenn will tell 
them of the doubts he has about Old Bruna and 
of the fact that some secrets related to the death 
of Ottone could be hidden in the woman’s house. 
Svenn will advise the Hunters to act with caution, 
so as not to alarm the inhabitants of Lohr, but he 
will make himself available to distract the woman, 
driving her away from home with an excuse.

112
Svenn the Skilled Hunter
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled huntsman and I boast of all the 
prey I have bagged in my long career. I’m a man of the world 
and I’m used to handling every situation without too many 
scruples.
Agenda: To make Bruna take the blame to divert attention 
from Bianca / Pretend to help the Hunters to make sure they 
close the case as quickly as possible.
Gift – The Solver:  Svenn takes great care of solving and 
fixing all the problems of those who offer him a job. Against 
any attempt at mind control, gathering information, or 
trying to intimidate him, Svenn’s OL is Hard 7. 
Bruna’s House
Descriptor: A small old house with a 
garden full of medicinal plants.
Information: Bruna lives alone since 
her husband’s death. Anyone who 
knows herbs will understand that 
the woman has good botanical 
knowledge. Under the bed, 
Svenn hid a bag with a book of 
blasphemous rituals and a lock 
of blond hair that belonged to 
Ottone.
Bruna the Poor Witch
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a poor old woman to whom life has 
offered only suffering. Sometimes I manifest strange powers, 
but I have always concealed this ability out of fear of ending 
up at the stake.
Agenda: To live in peace, seeking acceptance from the 
inhabitants of Lohr.
Gift – Latent Witch:  Bruna has magic abilities, but 
she never studied and uses her powers unconsciously. If 
threatened, Bruna will activate her power and the objects 
in the immediate vicinity will begin to move and then fall 
upon everyone. Acting in the area affected by this power 
confers 2 Penalties on each Check due to the uncontrolled 
magic manifestations. If Bruna is killed, she will curse her 
killer who, for the rest of the Scenario, will receive 1 Penalty 
at each Defense Check. A practitioner of magic or someone 
who can communicate with spirits can soothe Bruna’s soul 
to undo the curse, but a Position Check will be required 
against an OL Hard 7. 
The Surroundings of Lohr
Descriptor: The village of Lohr stands on the edge of the 
great Black Forest and is surrounded by many particular and 
mysterious places.
While wandering around Lohr, the Hunters can reach one 
of the following places. Roll a die (considering the results 
from 1-3 as 1) or choose based on the information you want 
to bring into play. If the Hunters move in free exploration, 
each place is related to a cardinal direction.

113
1. To the North of the village flows a river that the 
inhabitants use to collect water and fish. Far from 
prying eyes, every morning, Bianca goes to wash 
the clothes of the Hessian mercenaries. During the 
afternoon, you may run into them (see “The escort” 
on the next column). At night, this is the place 
where Elsa and Bianca meet for a hug.
2. Continuing North (past the river) extends a large 
area that Svenn uses as a hunting ground. The man 
has scattered traps in the area and it is often possible 
to find a dying animal, Svenn not even bothering to 
put an end to its suffering.
Threat – Svenn’s Traps
Descriptor: A series of dangerous traps cleverly positioned.
OL Medium 5
Gift – Alarms:  Svenn’s traps are all designed to cause 
deep wounds, and failing a Defense Check means suffering 
1 Wound. In addition, they generate a loud noise that can 
easily alert the Hessian mercenaries in the mine and warn 
them against potential intruders. 
3. Further North, after Svenn’s traps, the Hunters will 
reach the old mine (see the next column).
4. To the West of the village, the Hunters will come 
across a soldier on horseback traveling to Dresden. 
The soldier, if questioned, will say that he is bringing 
a dispatch to the city because seven dangerous war 
criminals have been seen in the area, fleeing after 
having killed an officer.
5.  To the South, the Hunters will come across a 
small, abandoned house. Inside, there is a thug called 
Berno. The man is terrified and, if questioned, he will 
reveal that, two days earlier, he and his companion 
Folke tried to take advantage of a beautiful young girl 
with black hair who was washing military uniforms 
at the river. When Folke touched the young woman, 
the man’s body first swelled and then exploded into 
a pool of blood and guts. Berno then ran for his life, 
without looking back.
6. Traveling East, the Hunters will come to the great 
road that leads out of the forest. The remains of a 
camp made by the Hessian mercenaries indicate that 
someone went to the mine after recovering their 
strength.
The Mine
Descriptor: A large, abandoned mine riddled with 
hundreds of tunnels. Hidden by trees and plants, there is a 
small building that used to house the head of the excavations.
Information: Bianca lives in the small building on the 
top of the mine, but a tunnel dug by the Hessians allows 
her to descend into the mine at any time. After Folke and 
Berno’s attempted aggression, the band of Hessians will 
divide into two groups:
 ◆ The escort: Includes Wrath, Corpse, and Grinner. 
They always follow Bianca when she leaves the mine 
and will act as a guard in frequent late-night meetings 
with her mother Elsa.
 ◆ The guardians: Includes Crucible, Stitch, Shadow, 
and Rookie. This group is working to turn the mine 
into a new home, as the mercenaries have decided to 
settle permanently in Lohr.

7
114
Moving around the mine without knowing it is difficult. A 
map of the area that the mercenaries are renovating can be 
found in the house where Bianca lives. The map is inside a 
small chest, with a trap built by Crucible.
Threat – Safety Deposit Box
Descriptor: A small reinforced wooden box engraved with 
strange runes.
OL Medium 5
Gift – Get Your Filthy Hands Away!:  Opening the 
box by failing the Position Check or without using the 
password, i.e. beer and prostitutes, causes the activation of a 
strong electric shock that inflicts 1 Wound and alerts Stitch 
of the attempt to break in. In this case, increase the Our 
New Home Gift Trench Tokens by 1.
Threat – Mine Tunnels
Descriptor: A series of intricate passages that all look alike.
OL Medium 5
Gift – Our New Home:  Start the Scenario with 2 Trench 
Tokens on this Gift to represent the defenses put in place 
by the Hessian mercenaries. For each section of mine 
traversed, the Storyteller must set up a Scene and the 
mercenaries can spend 1 Trench Token to activate each of 
these effects once per Scenario. 
Note: To reach Bianca in the mines, it is necessary to travel 
at least 2 sections (3 if the Hessian mercenaries use Escape!).
1. Escape!: The screams of the Hessians rumble between 
the tunnels and Bianca moves away from the Hunters by 1 
section. 
2. Boom!: The mercenaries cause a collapse in the passage, 
overwhelming the Hunters, who will have to pass a Defense 
Check against OL Hard 7 not to suffer 1 Wound. 
3. Screw you!:  One or more mercenaries ambush the 
Hunters from behind, starting a conflict in which at least 
two of them act first in the Exchange.
4. Lights Out!:  Every source of light is blocked and the 
Hunters find themselves in total darkness. If Shadow is 
present, he will take advantage of it to attack the Hunters, 
possibly taking Didi the Rookie with him. 
The Seven Hessian Mercenaries
Seven skilled mercenaries, united by dwarfism syndrome, who 
have worked with various military forces. Their condition 
has always put them on the margins of the army, despite the 
impressive results they’ve obtained on the battlefield. When 
Siegbold fell ill, his execution was ordered, but the team 
rebelled by killing all the officers present. Now the seven 
mercenaries are wanted for war crimes and desertion.
Note: To facilitate the management of a group of 7 NPCs, 
all mercenaries have the same basic profile. However, 
everyone has an exclusive Gift that characterizes the 
way they act. In addition, each mercenary has a different 
ending for his Descriptor. All mercenaries are ex-soldiers 
accustomed to war and are armed to face hand-to-hand 
and ranged opponents. 
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (1 Wound)

7
7
7
115
Descriptor: I am a capable mercenary who has experienced 
a thousand battles and…[to be completed with the specific 
Descriptor of each mercenary]
Agenda: Protect Bianca to maintain the support of the 
Karenmann family / Transform the mine into our new home.
Dolf called “Crucible”
Descriptor: I know alchemy and magic, which I have 
studied with interest over the years. My glasses are 
constantly crooked.
Gift – Antimagus:  Crucible knows how 
to create magical interference through 
the use of vibrations generated by 
slamming an iron rod on the barrel 
of his gun. All actions involving 
the use of spells and magic in his 
presence receive 1 Penalty.
Grimbert called 
“Wrath”
Descriptor: When I 
get angry, my strength 
doubles. I chew tobacco all 
the time.
Gift – Warrior’s Fury:  
Wrath ignores the first 
Wound he suffers in each 
Scene. When he makes 
an attack, regardless of 
the outcome, the enemy is 
overwhelmed by Grimbert’s fury and receives 2 Penalties to 
their next action.
Siegbold called “Corpse”
Descriptor: My skin is hard and I have survived many 
wounds. My body is covered in old, dirty bandages.
Gift – Leather Skin:  Corpse can suffer 2 additional 
Wounds.
Bertold called “Stitch”
Descriptor: I am a good field doctor. I smell 
of the alcohol I use to heal wounds and lighten 
the soul.
Gift – It is not the Time to Die, Friend:  
Once per Scene, Bertold can heal 1 Wound 
from a patient which receives care from 
him. This can also bring someone who has 
lost their last Wound back into the action. 
Stani called “Shadow”
Descriptor: I am a perfect spy, easily 
moving through the shadows. I hate 
sunlight because I suffer from albinism.
Gift – Nocturnal:  Stani ignores any 
penalty derived from effects that reduce 
the ability to see and perceive an enemy. 
This also applies to magical shrouds.

116
 Didi called “Rookie”
Descriptor: I am the youngest in the group and I am good 
at following orders. I can never stand still, and I speak quickly, 
words tumbling over each other.
Gift – Yes, Sir!: Didi is always where others need him to be. 
Once per Scene, another mercenary can call Didi to attention, 
making him appear in the Scene ready to execute any order. 
Didi can follow a partner in action and imitate his deeds, and 
thanks to this he can make use of the exclusive Gift of those 
he is supporting. 
Hagen called “Grinner”
Descriptor: I am good at torture and I know how to make 
a prisoner talk. A conspicuous scar gives my face a perpetual 
sneer.
Gift – Grim:  Hagen knows how to exploit fear and mind 
control, so he is completely immune to it. In his presence, his 
teammates increase their Opposition Level by 1 to resist the 
effects of fear and mind control.
Bianca
 Karenmann
OL Easy 3 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I’m a sweet young girl and I just want to live in 
peace. The power of the curse makes me strong and resilient. 
It also gives me the power to make living beings explode from 
within.
Agenda: I want what’s best for the whole village of Lohr, even 
if it means sacrificing myself.
Gift – The Curse of Bloodwhite:  Thanks to the power of 
the curse, Bianca is stronger and more resilient than normal. 
For this reason, at the end of each Scene she recovers 1 
Wound. This effect even allows Bianca to return from 
death. Bianca’s power can detonate the body of living 
beings and, once per Exchange, her action can inflict 
1 automatic Wound to a target. No Defense Check is 
allowed.

117
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters pay too much attention to Abel:  
Svenn will intervene as soon as possible by making the 
young man disappear. Abel will be imprisoned in a pit in 
the hunting ground.
If the Hunters capture Bianca: Elsa and Karl will call 
a public assembly, trying to demonstrate to the Hunters 
how beloved Bianca is and that she does not deserve to be 
locked up by the Order.
If Bianca sees the people she cares about 
endangered by the Hunters: she will surrender of her 
own free will, trying to negotiate for the salvation of those 
she loves.
If the Hunters openly declare that they do not 
believe Bruna is guilty:  Karl and Elsa will make the 
Hunters understand that they are no longer welcome in 
Lohr and they will threaten to write a letter to the Prince 
to complain.
If the Hunters defeat or capture at least three 
Hessian mercenaries: the other mercenaries will try 
to start a negotiation with them. It will be clear that, for 
mercenaries, their comrades come first. 
The first night after the arrival of the Hunters: Elsa 
will go to the river with Svenn as her escort to meet Bianca 
and warn her of the presence of the Hunters. 
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Bloodwhite’s Heart Scenario and can be acquired once the 
conditions are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario 
Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Gift – Stake’s Spirit
Requirement: Examine the old stakes in the square of 
Lohr or show interest in the past history of witch-hunting.
Y ou are aware of the magical power that resides in the 
village of Lohr and of the extent of the tragic witch trials 
it once hosted. Once per Scene, you can open your mind 
to the spirits of the dead witches. Spend 1 Soma and ask 
a question about the village, one of the inhabitants, or the 
surrounding areas. The Storyteller will answer with useful 
information, but keep in mind that it will be conditioned by 
the witch’s hatred of this place.
Gift – One of Us
Requirement: Defeat and spare one or more of the Hessian 
mercenaries.
Y ou defeated the fearsome Hessian mercenaries and 
earned their respect. Once per Scenario, you can call the 
team to your rescue, and they will arrive in no time. The 
Hessian mercenaries will act to help you within their NPC 
capabilities for the current Scene, and then retreat to start 
brewing some trouble in your location. This Gift can be 
maintained in all future Scenarios and does not count 
towards the maximum of the Gifts you have available.

118
The Scenario 
Bloodwhite’s Heart 
in Scenes 
FIRST SCENE –  Arrival in Lohr
It’s the same one you find on page 110. After the interview 
with the Karenmanns, the Hunters go to Svenn to talk about 
his investigation. Before arriving, Abel will intercept the 
Hunters, revealing his doubts about Bianca’s departure.
End of Scene:  The Hunters meet Svenn. Go to THE 
SECOND SCENE – The huntsman.
SECOND SCENE – The huntsman
Read aloud to the Hunters: Svenn’s house is a few minutes 
from the Karenmann palace. With the words of the young Abel 
still in your head, you find yourself in front of a once lovely 
mansion. Unfortunately, the trophies of Svenn’s hunting skills 
spoil its beauty from the entrance: a huge, tacky stuffed bear is 
placed next to the door. 
Place: Svenn the huntsman’s house.
Time: Mid-morning.
Information: Svenn will welcome the Hunters inside 
the house and will describe everything he has “discovered. ” 
He’ll also eagerly volunteer to distract Bruna with an excuse, 
luring her away from her home. When asked about Bianca 
and Abel, he will say that the young man is the classic loser 
who fell in love with a girl to whom he should not even speak.
End of Scene:  If the Hunters trust Svenn, go to THIRD 
SCENE OPTION A – Witch hunt , if instead, they decide to 
search the surroundings of Lohr as requested by Abel, go to 
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – The river. 
THIRD SCENE OPTION A –  
Witch hunt
Read aloud to the Hunters: Entering Bruna’s small and old 
house isn’t difficult for you. Apart from an abundant garden 
full of medicinal herbs, little of what you see qualifies as proof 
that this is the home of a witch. But, maybe you just have to 
look more closely...
Place: Bruna’s crumbling house.
Time: It is late afternoon when Svenn sends for Bruna to ask 
her for advice on some herbs he found in the woods.
Information: Searching the house, it will be easy to discover 
a bag under the bed, inside of which is a book about magic 
and a lock of blonde hair. Taking the evidence to Svenn, he 
will confirm that the hair color is the same as that of Ottone’s 
and that the young scion kept his hair tied in a tail (which, 
coincidentally, was cut from the corpse).
 ◆ If the Hunters have no doubts, Svenn will have Bruna 
removed from the house, and accuse her of witchcraft. 
Elsa and Karl will congratulate them on the speed with 
which they have solved the case. 
 ◆ Convinced or not, Abel will approach the Hunters to 
tell them he made a macabre discovery as he searched 
the outskirts of the village for tracks. He’ll make an 
appointment with them after sundown to avoid Svenn’s 
attention.

3b
119
 ◆ If they go along, it will be clear to the Hunters that 
they are no longer welcome in Lohr, and that from this 
moment on, their stay is no longer viewed favorably by 
the Karenmanns.
End of Scene: Shortly before sunset, Abel will contact the 
Hunters saying that some very suspicious deserters have 
settled at the old mine and that the mine would be a perfect 
place to keep someone in captivity. Go to THE FOURTH 
SCENE – The mine.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B –  
The river
Read aloud to the Hunters:  To the north flows a large 
river, which separates the inhabited part of the village from 
the old mine. Abel told you that the mine is now abandoned 
and that it is often used as a refuge for fleeing deserters and 
thugs. Your attention is drawn by a melodious voice, a young 
girl with long black hair is washing military uniforms at the 
river. Even if you cannot see her well, the resemblance to the 
daughter of the Karenmanns that you saw in the paintings of 
the palace is quite striking. Noticing your presence, the young 
woman flees to the mine while three short, squat men come out 
of the bush to block your way.
Place: The river that flows between the village of Lohr and 
the abandoned mine.
Time: It is early afternoon. 
Information: The Hessian mercenary escort group (page 
113) will instruct the Hunters to go somewhere else, making 
it clear that the mine is now to be considered their property. 
If they are overwhelmed or outnumbered, the mercenaries 
will call Rookie for support. However, it is not the intention

120
of the Hessians to enter into conflict with the Hunters and 
if asked about who the girl is, they will say that it is a young 
woman from the village whom they pay to do some chores. 
 ◆ If the Hunters get into a fight, Svenn will arrive after 
one Exchange, trying to put an end to the hostilities. 
The huntsman will instruct the Hunters not to seek 
trouble with the group of mercenaries who are “passing 
through, ” and will tell the mercenaries that they have 
a day to “get out of the way. ” The Hunters will catch 
a clear look of understanding between Svenn and the 
mercenaries.
 ◆ If the Hunters return to report, both Svenn and the 
Karenmanns will attempt to divert attention from the 
mine, prompting the Hunters to investigate Bruna. 
End of Scene:  If the Hunters trust Svenn, go to THIRD 
SCENE OPTION A – Witch hunt , if they decide to 
investigate the girl at the mine, go to FOURTH SCENE – 
The mine.

54
121
FOURTH SCENE –  The mine
This Scene can be reached in different ways, but once 
they get to the mine it will be clear to the Hunters that the 
Hessians keep Bianca Karenmann with them. The mine 
must be managed as indicated on page 113, taking into 
account the intentions and strategies of the Hunters.
Place: The old abandoned mine near Lohr.
Time: The sun is setting.
Information:
 ◆ The Hessians will fight to the death if necessary, but 
Bianca herself will surrender if the Hunters seem to be 
getting the better of the situation.
 ◆ Bianca will confess to everything if faced with the 
staging that her parents created to save her, especially 
if this can prevent an innocent woman like Bruna from 
an unjust accusation.
End of Scene:  The Hunters retrieve Bianca Karenmann 
and return to Lohr, go to the FIFTH SCENE – The trial.
FIFTH SCENE –  The trial
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you return to Lohr 
with Bianca, all eyes of the village are on you. The smiles and 
greetings with which the inhabitants welcome Bianca are a 
clear sign of how much the daughter of the Karenmanns is 
loved and esteemed. Arriving at the central square, Elsa and 
Karl await you with a group of armed guards. There is no 
hatred in their eyes, but only the concern given by a parent’s 
love for their daughter.
Place: The central square of Lohr, near the old stakes 
where witches were bound and burned still stands as a 
warning to all the inhabitants.
Time: The sun has just risen, after a night of clashes at the 
mine.
Information: Elsa and Karl will go all-in by asking the 
Hunters not to imprison their beloved daughter. The two 
nobles will be supported by the testimonies of the villagers. 
It will quickly become clear that everyone considers Bianca 
a pure and kind soul.
End of Scene: It will be up to the Hunters to decide the 
fate of Bianca Karenmann.

122
Once upon a time…
The Papal State. Father Cristoforo, ministering 
to the city of Capodimonte, wrote to the Papacy 
to request the intervention of a group of Hunters. 
Several years ago, the formerly abandoned 
convent on Martana Island became the home of 
a mysterious cult called the House of Roses. It is 
sponsored by the rich Dionysus Gonsalva, heir to 
the family that has always lived in Capodimonte, 
a town on the shores of Lake Bolsena. Many 
young girls in the area have left their families to 
go to Martana Island, severing all ties with their 
past. It seems that this blasphemous cult is led by 
a mysterious priestess, but access to the island is 
prohibited by order of the Gonsalva family. Given 
the excellent relations between the Gonsalva 
family and the Papacy, the Hunters must go to 
Capodimonte and seek permission to access the 
island.
A Scenario Inspired by 
Beauty and the Beast 
(Jeanne-Marie Leprince de 
Beaumont)
The House of Roses
Safety Keywords:  
Animalistic Savagery / Cult / 
Brainwashing / Drugs / Reduced 
Inhibitions

123
The story that led us here…
Dionysus Gonsalva is a young noble, victim of a 
curse that has deformed his body and made him monstrous. 
He is always in search of a bride, as he has been through 
several over the years. The marriage has always ended in 
blood when the bride first sees her prospective husband and 
rejects Dionysus in horror. Bellina Cantalupo (known as 
Bella) is a humble but ambitious servant who arrived at the 
Gonsalva court in service to Marianna De Rossi, Dionysus’ 
most recently deceased wife. Bella soon sensed the distress 
of her lord and, without a second thought, worked her way 
into his good graces to become his secret lover. Despite the 
disgust she feels for Dionysus, Bella beguiled her lord with 
the promise of true love. She also convinced him to move 
to the old, abandoned monastery of Martana Island. The 
two then founded the House of Roses, a place to welcome 
young girls in search of a life away from the poverty that 
plagues the current era. In the House of Roses, the young 
novices are cared for and pampered, unaware that once 
a month, one of them will be sacrificed to Dionysius to 
appease his distorted need for love. Bella even arranged for 
her two sisters, Francesca and Letizia, to join her in the cult. 
She had always envied their beauty and planned for both of 
them to be torn apart by her captive lover. Francesca, the 
older sister, saw the true nature of Dionysus when he was 
unleashed against Letizia. She escaped and now roams the 
island, hunted by the servants of Dionysus, waiting for the 
chance to escape.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Capodimonte. Pietro Gonsalva, head of the noble family, 
receives more and more complaints from the population 
due to the continuous disappearance of young girls, who 
flee to the House of Roses in search of a comfortable life. 
Mere months after its foundation, the House of Roses 
has become so famous as to attract young people from all 
over Italy. Although Pietro does not know what is really 
happening on Martana Island, the delusional letters of 
his son Dionysus have made him worried. Now the old 
nobleman hopes that an outside authority (such as the 
Hunters) will take care of the situation.
Francesca hides in the dense vegetation of the island, 
unable to leave it by swimming because the waters of the 
lake are too cold in the middle of winter. The poor fugitive 
keeps an eye on the pier in the hope of being able to secretly 
embark on one of the ships that, after unloading the new 
arrivals, returns to Capodimonte. In the House of Roses, 
Bellina is consumed with anger at her sister’s escape and 
now has total control over Dionysus’ psyche, treating him 
like an animal. Bellina is surrounded by faithful servants, 
all brainwashed to serve her as a goddess, and maintains 
control over the island thanks to a pack of trained mastiffs 
that obey Dionysus due to his bestial curse.

1
124
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Capodimonte is a rich city 
near Lake Bolsena that, thanks to the huge investments of the 
noble Gonsalva family, has grown quite prosperous. After a 
few minutes’ walk, you come across the church of San Carlo, 
the smallest in the city, amidst small shops packed with busy 
apprentices. Now you just have to decide whether to enter to 
meet Father Cristoforo or first introduce yourself to the noble 
Pietro Gonsalva, the regent of the city.
Place: The rich and prosperous city of Capodimonte.
Time: It is a cold morning.
Initial Situation: Hunters can decide who to meet and in 
what order between the noble Pietro Gonsalva (at the city’s 
fortress) and Father Cristoforo (in the small church of San 
Carlo). Both men will have information about the House of 
Roses, bringing a different point of view to the attention of 
the Hunters.
Capodimonte
Descriptor: A rich and prosperous city protected by the Papal 
State.
Pietro Gonsalva Regent of 
Capodimonte
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a rich and powerful nobleman, and I have 
managed to ingratiate myself with the Papal State. I love being 
in charge, but over time I’ve learned to trust those who are more 
experienced than I am.
Agenda: Preserve the House of Roses because it is the first 
place where Dionysus appears to be restrained / Not to lose the 
advantageous agreements made with the Church.
Information from the conversation with Pietro:  
The Regent of Capodimonte will welcome the Hunters with 
all honors, praising their importance to the Church and for 
humanity’ salvation. Pietro is sincerely respectful of the work 
of the Order. 
 ◆ Pietro will say that the island was once a monastery and 
that, thanks to the efforts of his son Dionysus, it has now 
returned to a renewed splendor.
 ◆ Martana Island has become a place of worship, where 
people can take refuge and contemplate God.
 ◆ Pietro cares a lot about the House of Roses because 
his son Dionysus is suffering from a rare disease that 
covers his whole body with hair and, unfortunately, his 
monstrous aspect is not accepted by society. To Pietro, it

125
seems the island is the only place Dionysus has ever 
been judged for the good he can do and not for his 
fearsome appearance.
 ◆ At the end of the interview, it will be clear to the Hunters 
that Pietro has no idea of what life is like on the island, 
he has never been there and, because of the many 
diplomatic involvements around it, he has no interest 
in discovering more. For the reasons mentioned above, 
Pietro will make sure the Hunters understand that the 
House of Roses must remain active.
 ◆ With great generosity, Pietro will offer the Hunters a 
boat that will take them to the island in the afternoon.
Father Cristoforo
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a humble provincial priest and I have 
been responsible for the religious education of the Gonsalvas 
for years. 
Agenda: I have reason to believe that the House of Roses is 
a place of blasphemy and damnation, so I want the Church 
to investigate.
Information from the conversation with 
Cristoforo: The humble priest will welcome the Hunters 
in the small church of San Carlo. The man will show 
reverence to the work of the Order and will seem aware of 
who the Hunters really are.
 ◆ Cristoforo will say that the island once was a 
monastery where the residents dedicated themselves to 
blasphemous rites. The Church intervened by putting 
them all at the stake.
 ◆ Martana Island is a name on the lips of many young 
people, who tell of a place of feasts and pleasures far 
from the severe but holy eye of God. Many young women 
go out of their way to get to sail to the island. 
 ◆ Cristoforo taught the ways of God to the unfortunate 
Dionysus, being troubled by the savagery that the young 
man struggled to control. 
 ◆ At the end of the interview, it will be clear to the Hunters 
that Cristoforo does not really know what is happening on 
the island, but he is firmly convinced that it is nothing good.
 ◆ Trying not to be overheard by prying ears, Cristoforo will 
tell the Hunters that a couple of faithful friends have a small 
boat and can take the Hunters to the island at night, landing 
on a little-used beach. 
Martana Island
Descriptor: A small island in the center of Lake Bolsena. Covered 
mostly by dense vegetation, the island includes a sanctuary, a 
small pier, and a mansion recently built by the Gonsalvas.
There are five locations available on the island:
 ◆ The Pier: Where all ships arriving from Capodimonte dock. 
The pier is under the control of Bella and her worshippers. 
If the Hunters accept Pietro Gonsalva’s transportation offer, 
they will arrive there in the late afternoon.
 ◆ The White Beach: A small beach of rocks and earth to the 
east of the island. It is located on the opposite side of the 
island from the few inhabited places. If the Hunters accept 
Father Cristoforo’s offer of transportation, they will reach 
the beach at night.
 ◆ The Sanctuary: All the young people who have become 
prisoners of the House of Roses live here.
 ◆ The Mansion: The House of Roses is a sumptuous villa that 
Dionysus had built for Bella.

126
 ◆ The Forest: A large wooded area where Dionysus’ 
Mastiffs roam. In the thick of the forest, there is a 
mass grave where Bella dumps the bodies of the 
Beast’s unfortunate victims.
The Pier
Descriptor: A small pier with a watchtower.
Information: At the pier there are always 8 Servants, 
armed and ready to welcome every arrival. In the guard 
tower there are 6 other Servants equipped with firearms.
Bella’s Servants 
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are young people in the service of Bella 
and we are trained to defend the House 
of Roses. 
Agenda: Follow all of Bella’s 
orders to the letter. 
Gift – For Our Lady:  The Servants are brainwashed 
and totally dependent on Bella. Every attempt to scare or 
mentally manipulate them receives 2 Penalties. If Bella 
is targeted by a threat, once per Exchange, a Servant can 
sacrifice themselves to take the hit in her place. 
The White Beach
Descriptor: A small beach of pebbles and earth hidden 
among the dense vegetation.
Information: Docking at the beach without the 
necessary precautions will attract 1d6+2 Mastiffs.
The Forest
Descriptor: A large wooded area that almost entirely 
covers Martana Island.
Information: The forest is overrun by mastiffs loyal to 
Dionysus. Avoiding them requires overcoming the Pack of 
Mastiffs Threat. In case of a Failure, 8 Mastiffs will appear 
and surround the intruders. At the center of the forest is 
a mass grave where the bodies of Dionysus’ victims are 
dumped. Francesca hides in the pit to escape the hungry 
dogs.
Threat – Pack of Mastiffs
OL Medium 5 
Descriptor: We patrol the forest, looking for intruders. No 
one can escape our sense of smell.

127
Dionysus’ Mastiffs
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are big and fierce mastiffs loyal to our 
leader, Dionysus.
Agenda: Hunt down any intruders arriving on the island.
Gift – Pack:  Any action taken while surrounded by 2 
or more Mastiffs receives 1 Penalty. A Mastiff can use its 
action to howl and alert Dionysus, who will arrive within 
2 Exchanges.
Francesca Cantalupo
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am Bellina’s sister, the eldest of three.
Agenda: Flee the island to return to my father / Avenge 
Letizia’s death and punish Bellina for always being a selfish, 
jealous child.
The Sanctuary
Descriptor: An ancient monastery converted into a small 
pleasure town. 
Information: 20 Handmaids and 10 Servants are 
always present in the Sanctuary. All the inhabitants of the 
Sanctuary live surrounded by food and pleasures of the 
flesh, in a state of constant and perverse ecstasy. Bella adds 
drugs to both food and drinks so that the inhabitants of the 
Sanctuary lose their inhibitions.
Threat – The Food of the Gods
OL Medium 5 
Descriptor: All the food in the Sanctuary is tainted with a 
powerful drug that loosens one’s inhibitions.
Gift – The Drug of the True Self:  Whoever eats food 
treated with this powerful drug loses all inhibition. Anyone 
who fails the Defense Check to resist the effect receives 2 
Drug Tokens. During a Scene, the Storyteller can spend 1 
Drug Token to activate one of the following effects. 1. The 
True Self: Immediately activates the Hunter’s Dark Ego 
without them suffering the Wound generated by not using 
the Activator. 2. Dulled Senses: The Storyteller decides 
what type of Outcome the Hunter is aiming for after they 
have declared an action. 
Bella’s Handmaids 
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are young girls loyal to our lady. We are 
ready to have a good time – anytime.
Agenda: We have tasted happiness in the House of Roses 
and we will not allow anyone to take us back to our former, 
miserable lives.

128
The Mansion also known as the 
House of Roses
Descriptor: A large and rich mansion, with a wide garden, 
a hedge maze, and beautiful red roses.
Information: Bella lives in the Mansion where 8 Servants 
are executing any of their lady’s wishes. Dionysus lives in 
the cellars of the estate and only goes out at night or when 
his Mastiffs get his attention.    
Threat – Hedge Labyrinth
OL Medium 5 
Descriptor: A large labyrinth of hedges and roses that 
extends throughout the estate garden. 
Gift – Secret Passage:  Bellina and Dionysus know a 
series of secret passages to avoid wasting time in the maze. 
Anyone else might need several hours to negotiate it.   
Bellina Cantalupo known as Bella
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a cunning manipulator and the only queen of the House of Roses. I 
take advantage of the power I wield whenever I have the chance.
Agenda: Increase the influence of the House of Roses at any cost / Getting rid of 
Dionysus, but without destroying the House of Roses.
Gift – Manipulative:  During a Scene, if Bella has enough time to talk to a 
subject, she will learn a secret about them, such as a Descriptor or 
their Gift. Bella is immune to any kind of mental manipulation 
or fear. 
Gift – My Beast:  If Bella is in danger, Dionysus will come 
to her rescue within 1 Exchange. To help Bella, Dionysus 
can free himself from any kind of impediment, whether 
physical or mental. Nothing can stop Dionysus, who 
will break through doors and walls if necessary.

129
Dioniso Gonsalva called the 
Beast
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young man afflicted by a curse 
that turned me into a fierce beast. Only Bella’s scent 
keeps my incredible ferocity in check.
Agenda: I would like to live alone with Bella 
in this paradise, and I only tolerate the House 
of Roses for the sake of my love for her / I 
would like to show the full extent of my love 
to Bella, but until she gives herself to me, I 
will have to take my frustration out on 
her handmaids.
Gift – The Beast:  Dionysus can 
act twice during an Exchange. When 
moving, Dionysus can jump up to thirty 
feet and easily destroy any obstacle. 
When acting to save Bella, Dionysus 
increases his Opposition Level by 1.

130
Scenario Events
In this Scenario there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel free 
to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.
If the Hunters capture Bella:  the Lady of the House of 
Roses will threaten to unleash Dionysus’ wrath on all the 
innocents on the island. Bella will demand that the Hunters 
release her, while trying to negotiate a deal that works for 
everyone.
If the Hunters kill Dionysus:  Bella will thank them by 
telling them that it was all the fault of the mad nobleman 
who forced her to do everything. Her aim is to escape the 
judgment of the Church by passing herself off as a victim.
If the Hunters find Francesca:  the young woman will 
describe how all the Handmaids ignore what truly happens 
to Bella’s chosen ones. Francesca will tell the Hunters who 
Bellina really is, and how she brainwashed everyone on the 
island thanks to her position of power and the drugs in the 
food.
If the Hunters officially show up at the pier:  Bella 
will welcome them with all honors by offering them food 
and drinks. Bella’s purpose is to gather information about 
the newcomers and then feed them to Dionysus one by one.
If the Hunters accuse Bella or Dionysus:  Bella will 
seek an advantageous agreement by mentioning that the 
Gonsalva family is one of the greatest supporters of the 
Papacy, and that a scandal could end the huge subsidies that 
Pietro regularly pays to Rome. 
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the The 
House of Roses Scenario and can be acquired once the 
requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario 
Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the 
previous one.
Gift – Faithful Mastiffs
Requirement: Have defeated one or more of Dionysus’ 
Mastiffs.
Y ou defeated the Mastiffs, proving you are a worthy pack 
leader. Y ou gain 1 Mastiff who follows you as a faithful 
companion. Y ou can spend 1 Soma to assign a task to your 
Mastiff for the rest of the Scene. 1. Find: The Mastiff can 
track an object or a person if you are able to provide it with 
a sample of its smell; 2. Defend: The Mastiff stands in your 
defense, granting you 1 Additional Success in all Defense 
Checks. In case of an Outcome with a Cost, the Mastiff  
will die taking the blow in your place as a Cost; 3. Attack: 
The Mastiff attacks the enemy you point to, reducing their 
Opposition Level by 1 for the course of the Exchange. Each 
time you use this effect, roll a die. With 4+ the Mastiff is 
killed in the action. If your Mastiff is killed, you can get 
another one at the beginning of the next Scene.
Gift - Uncovering Deceptions
Requirement: You know how to expose a charlatan and lay 
bare lies.
All NPCs who possess a Descriptor, Gift, or Agenda that has 
to do with lying or deceiving others reduce their Opposition 
Level by 1 in your presence. When you’re around, you also 
weaken their followers: once per Scene you can prevent a 
subject from using a Gift that depends on using or sacrificing 
a follower.

The Scenario The 
House of Roses in 
Scenes 
FIRST SCENE –  Arrival at 
Capodimonte
It’s the same one you find on page 124. The Hunters will 
go first to interview Pietro Gonsalva and then to Father 
Cristoforo.
End of Scene:  Hunters can decide whether to land on 
the island through the passage offered by Pietro, go to 
SECOND SCENE OPTION A – The pier. Or land secretly 
thanks to the help of Cristoforo, go to SECOND SCENE 
OPTION B – The White Beach.
SECOND SCENE OPTION A 
– The pier
Read aloud to the Hunters: In less than an hour’s sailing, 
you can already see the shape of Martana Island looming in 
front of you. The boat arrives in a small covered port, where 
you find a large group of men with smiling faces waiting for 
you, though it’s difficult to ignore the weapons they carry. 
A young girl with wavy brown hair seems to be the person 
in charge, and when you get off the boat, she greets you, 
smiling. “I am Bella Cantalupo, and I serve my lord Dionysus 
Gonsalva so that the House of Roses may be a place of peace 
and prayer. Please be our guests for at least one night. ” Bella 
beckons you to follow her to what appears to be a completely 
renovated monastery.
Place: Martana Island’s pier.
Time: It is almost time for dinner.
Information: Bella will be extremely helpful with the 
Hunters, trying to answer all their questions. The woman’s 
goal is to impede the investigation and pass the House of 
Roses off as a place of worship and peace.
 ◆ If the Hunters ask her about Dionysus, Bella will say 
that her master shuns situations in which there are 
many people because of his appearance. Bella takes 
care of everything, but if the Hunters want to, she will 
set up an interview with Dionysus.
 ◆ Bella will highlight the peace and prosperity in which 
servants and handmaids live, and all the young people 
will be helpful and happy.
 ◆ Bella will invite the Hunters to stay for the night and 
organize a banquet in their honor. The Hunters will 
be asked to stay within Sanctuary so as not to risk 
provoking the many mastiffs that Dionysus breeds in 
the forest.
 ◆ The Mansion is reserved for Dionysus, and for this 
reason it is inaccessible to the Hunters. Sneaking into 
it, you will find ample supplies of Mandrake and other 
herbs useful to produce drugs. 
 ◆ Bella will watch the Hunters closely, always maintaining 
her fake, friendly smile. 
End of Scene:  After talking to Bella and seeing the 
Sanctuary, the Hunters will be invited to a banquet set up 
just for them. Go to THIRD SCENE OPTION A – Welcome 
guests.

3a 3b
2b
132
SECOND SCENE OPTION B  – 
The White Beach
Read aloud to the Hunters: You land on a small beach of 
pebbles and mud. As soon as you step off the boat, the sailors 
hastily set sail once more, making the sign of the cross. Only a 
few seconds pass before you hear growls and howls. Apparently, 
a wolf or dog pack is moving towards you, attracted by your 
smell.
Place: A small beach on Martana Island.
Time: It is almost time for dinner.
Information: A group of 8 Mastiffs will try to intercept 
the Hunters and, if the clash begins, they will immediately 
howl to summon Dionysus. The beastly lord of the island 
will attack without hesitation but will flee after suffering 2 
Wounds. If, on the other hand, the Hunters manage to hide 
from the pack of Mastiffs, they will see Dionysus coming and 
will be able to observe him in all his savagery. Dionysus will 
remain for a few minutes before leaving and returning to the 
Mansion.
End of Scene:  Having faced or avoided the Mastiffs, the 
Hunters will come across the mass grave, go to THIRD 
SCENE OPTION B – Unwanted guests.
THIRD SCENE OPTION A –  
Welcome guests
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The banquet Bella has set up 
for you is sumptuous to say the least. Wine and delicacies of 
all kinds cover the great table of the Sanctuary, while around 
you, servants and handmaids dance and sip wine, indulging 
in the pleasures of the evening. Even Bella seems particularly 
euphoric, inviting you to enjoy the fine things her generous lord 
Dionysus has to offer. 
Place: The Sanctuary of the island.
Time: The sun has just gone down.
Information: Bella will try to drug the Hunters to lower 
their defenses and discover how much of a threat they are. If 
the Hunters insist on meeting Dionysus, Bella will ask that 
only one of them go, since her lord is not accustomed to being 
seen by many people at once. If the Hunters consent, the 
chosen one will be taken to the Mansion’s rose garden, where 
Dionysus will attack, trying to kill them. If the Hunters keep 
a low-profile during dinner, at some point Bella will be called 
away by a servant to deal with an emergency. The Hunters 
will be able to see concern in the eyes of the young woman, 
since Dionysus is having one of his fits and needs to consume 
one of the handmaids to appease himself. Bella will take a 
girl with her, saying goodbye to the Hunters. Following Bella 
will require overcoming two groups of 8 Servants, which the 
woman will put on the road between the Sanctuary and the 
Mansion.
End of Scene:  The Hunters now clearly understand that 
something dark animates Bella’s actions, and they arrive at 
the Mansion to witness the sacrifice, go to the FOURTH 
SCENE – The sacrifice.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B – 
Unwanted Guests
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You have overcome the 
terrible Mastiffs patrolling the forest and, as you cross the 
rugged woodland, a nauseating smell draws your attention. A 
little more than ten meters away, a large grave contains the 
bodies of a multitude of young girls, all horribly mutilated, as 
if a ferocious creature had devoured them. Suddenly, one of the 
girls seems to be moving, making a soft cry.

4
5
133
Place: The mass grave in the middle of the island’s Forest.
Time: It is night, and a soft moonlight filters through the 
branches of the trees.
Information: It will be possible to find Francesca within 
the pit, looking worn and on the edge of madness. The young 
woman will describe all the crimes Bellina has committed and 
how Letizia was sacrificed to the fury of Dionysus. The young 
woman’s only wish is to run away but, if the Hunters play on 
her anger, she will ask to follow them to avenge her dead sister. 
Francesca’s voice is known to the Mastiffs, and within a short 
time, a pack of 8 will reach the pit, led by Dionysus. In the 
event of a confrontation, the beastly noble will do everything 
he can to kill Francesca. As in the previous clash, Dionysus 
will flee as soon as he receives the second Wound.
End of Scene: Once Bellina’s faults have been discovered, the 
Hunters can move to the Mansion to capture her. Go to THE 
FOURTH SCENE – The sacrifice.
FOURTH SCENE – The sacrifice
There are two ways to get to this Scene.
From the banquet: The Hunters follow Bella and catch her 
red-handed as she prepares to feed Dionysus an unsuspecting 
handmaid.
From the grave:  The Hunters arrive at the garden of 
the Mansion led by Francesca just as Bella prepares to feed 
Dionysus an unsuspecting handmaid.
Place: The rose garden of the Mansion.
Time: It is night, and only the full moon illuminates the small 
labyrinth of hedges and roses.
Information: The ritual consists of bringing the handmaid 
to the center of the maze of hedges, and then letting Dionysus 
unleash his desires. Only Bella and Dionysus are present at the 
rite, since none of the servants or handmaids must discover 
the truth. If the Hunters intervene, Bella will not hesitate to 
unleash Dionysus against them. While they battle, she will 
flee to the Sanctuary to stir up all the servants and handmaids 
against the Hunters, accusing them of having been sent by the 
Papacy to end the House of Roses.
End of Scene:  After facing Dionysus, the Hunters must 
handle Bella who sends all the inhabitants of the island against 
them. Go to SCENE FIVE – Face to face.
FIFTH SCENE – Face to face
This Scene can have many variables, based on what the 
Hunters have discovered so far. There will be 20 Servants and 
20 Handmaids in the Scene.
Place: The Sanctuary.
Time: It is late at night and the only light is cast by the banquet 
bonfires.
Information: Bella will use all her influence to cast the 
Hunters in a bad light, also accusing them of the attempted 
(or successful) murder of Dionysus, the one who has been 
supporting everyone at the House of Roses. Bella will make 
the Hunters understand that she is willing to come to terms 
with them, but her power of manipulation is very strong and 
she can do with the innocents who live on the island as she 
likes.
End of Scene: Bella will be determined not to lose the power 
she has gained and has an entire island at her command. The 
Hunters will have to play their cards well to put an end to the 
House of Roses once and for all.

134
Once upon a time…
The Hunters are sent to Sweden to 
investigate the mysterious Stegeborg 
Castle. The property, once run by the 
Schwernis family, has been abandoned 
for decades. The surrounding villages 
had survived the departure of the noble 
family, remaining populated and lively. 
However, in the last few weeks, they 
have gone ominously silent. Strange 
lights have been seen coming from 
the Stegeborg Castle, and seers in the 
service of the Order have received a dark 
omen. Something evil, hidden within 
the meandering corridors of Stegeborg, 
has been awakened. Now, everyone 
will pay for the sins of one man. The 
Hunters must go to Stegeborg and face 
the danger the seers have foretold.
A Scenario Inspired by Sleeping 
Beauty (Charles Perrault)
Stegeborg’s Quiet
Safety Keywords:  
Incest / Physical and Psychological 
Abuse / Rape / Body Horror

135
The story that led us here…
The last member of the Schwernis family, former 
lords of Stegeborg, remains in the castle. Talia never left  
when the castle was abandoned fifty years ago, and instead 
was bound in eternal slumber. Talia had always submitted 
to her father Alexander and mother Alexandra, twins 
united in a perverse relationship. The couple was obsessed 
with their daughter, who was the last of their children. 
Each of the previous pregnancies had ended with the birth 
of deformed children, all of whom had been smothered 
by their disappointed parents. Talia represented the pure 
beauty Alexander and Alexandra had been longing for. 
Their dreams realized, they withdrew within the walls of 
the castle and hid themselves from the rest of the world. 
In her stone prison, Talia suffered all kinds of abuse at her 
parents’ hands, finding support only in Carabosse, the old 
cook of the castle. Unbeknownst to Talia, Carabosse was 
once a powerful witch who escaped the great inquisition of 
1645 in France. When Talia bled for the first time, her father 
tried to force himself on her. The young woman fought 
back and, unintentionally, pushed Alexander out of one 
of the castle towers, causing his death. Crazed with pain, 
Alexandra tried to kill her daughter, but was stopped by 
Carabosse. The witch was moved with compassion for all of 
Talia’s pain, and cast her last and greatest spell: she froze the 
Stegeborg Castle in time. Talia, asleep in the underground 
crypt, was imbued with the witch’s power so that she could 
now move objects or grow plants merely through force of 
will. Alexandra was transformed into a monster and forced 
to be her daughter’s guardian for eternity. Talia’s wish 
covered Stegeborg with ivy and brambles, a symbol of its 
separation from the rest of the world. 
The young woman, forever asleep, waits for someone pure 
of heart and capable of breaking the magic that once saved 
her, only to deliver her to a life of fading dreams. 
Fifty years later, Accursio De Celeste, a renowned mercenary 
and researcher, went to Stegeborg, attracted by rumors of a 
rich treasure hidden in the castle’s dungeons. Overcoming 
uncounted dangers, the man reached the dungeons to 
discover the sleeping body of Talia, who was so impressed by 
so much courage and skill that she decided to let him come 
even closer. But Talia’s dreams were once more shattered 
by the darkness of the human soul. Like her father fifty 
years earlier, Accursio tried to abuse the beautiful Sleeper. 
Anger and resentment once again overcame Talia’s mind, 
animating the castle and cursing the entire village next to it. 
In one night, all the inhabitants were killed or assimilated 
by the castle, becoming monsters and prisoners. Accursio 
took refuge in the Master Tower, waiting for his chance to 
escape. Her soul forever scarred, Talia’s will is now out of 
control, expanding beyond the walls of Stegeborg, in a slow 
but inexorable attempt to assimilate the whole world.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The village near the castle is nothing more than a ghost town 
where Georg, the only survivor of Talia’s fury, takes refuge 
from the Quiet Guards: men and women merged with the 
furnishings of the castle and transfigured into monsters, 
now serving the Sleeper. Accursio lives in the Master Tower 
of the castle where Talia’s power struggles to reach since 
it is there that Alexander tried to rape his daughter. The 
memory of so much pain weakens her abilities. Accursio 
is aware that he cannot escape on his own, since the tower 
has a single exit to the inner courtyard of the castle, and 
Talia’s power can reach him there. Alexandra, now reduced

1
STEGEBORG
136
to a deformed monster, wanders through the castle and waits 
for someone to end her life. She had trusted Accursio, and 
now that the man has proven as despicable as Alexander, 
Alexandra has resigned herself to the idea that no one can 
ever give Talia peace and thus put an end to the curse. A few 
villagers led by a woman named Lisbet are kept alive inside 
the castle, in the rooms that once belonged to the Schwernis 
family. Talia holds them hostage, forcing them every night to 
keep her company by reading stories and singing songs.
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  It is almost sunset when you 
glimpse the first houses of the village of Stegeborg. The imposing 
shape of the castle looms over the small village, casting its great 
shadow as if to swallow it. While an old stray dog raises its ears 
in your direction, the unnatural silence that accompanies your 
footsteps does not bode well.
Place: The small village of Stegeborg, devastated by the fury 
of Talia.
Time: It is almost sunset, but the sky is clear and there is still 
plenty of light.
Initial Situation: It’s apparent the village has suffered some 
sort of disaster as soon as the Hunters arrive there. Doors 
and windows are literally crumbling, while dozens of corpses 
lie in the streets. On the wrists and neck of the bodies, one 
can see the clear marks of something that seized them tightly 
and dragged them with incredible force. Moving around the 
village will immediately attract the attention of the Quiet 
Guards, who will converge on the Hunters. There are a total of 
10 Guards in town. Georg took refuge in the church, but the 
fear of being captured led him to stay there for days and he 
almost died of hunger. 
Information: Saving Georg will yield some information. 
The man arrived in Stegeborg a few years ago and does not 
know the history of the Schwernis well. He does know that 
fifty years earlier something so upset the noble family they 
packed up and left in a single night, disappearing. Georg will 
also reveal that, a week ago, an Italian treasure hunter named 
Accursio De Celeste passed through the village, asking for 
information about the castle. That same evening, he went 
there, never to return. Soon after, all hell broke loose, and the 
plants in the village came to life, dragging and strangling its 
inhabitants. Georg survived by fleeing to the church. He hid 
there, afraid to move after witnessing several villagers being 
transformed into Quiet Guards.
Important note: Thanks to her Sleeping Spirit Gift, Talia 
can interact with all the Places in the Scenario. Depending 
on the actions of the Hunters, the Sleeper can be an ally or an 
enemy. On page 142, you can find instructions for managing 
the Sleeping Spirit Gift.

STEGEBORG
137
The Village of 
Stegeborg
Descriptor: A small village at the foot of Castle Stegeborg, 
devastated by the fury of Talia.
Georg the Survivor 
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I’m a poor laborer without a family. I once was 
living in the streets.
Agenda: Escape from Stegeborg as soon as possible, so I can 
leave what I saw behind me and start a new life.
The Quiet Guards 
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the poor inhabitants of the village, fused 
with some of the castle’s furnishings. Our transformations 
have driven us to madness and we blindly obey the will of 
Talia.
Gift – Quiet Guards:  A Quiet Guard no longer possesses 
a mind and acts as an object under the control of Talia. That 
is why they cannot be mentally manipulated or frightened 
in any way. Each Quiet Guard is a human body fused with a 
part of the castle. Roll a die or choose a mutation.
1. Tools: The Guard’s arms are an agglomeration of tools, 
such as shears and hammers. When attacking, the Guard is 
considered to have an OL Medium 5.
2. Grating: The Guard’s body is covered with metal grates 
and frames. It can suffer 1 additional Wound.
3. Chains: Ropes and chains hang from the Guard’s body. 
With a single action, the Guard can interact with 2 targets.
4. Door: The Guard’s body is a huge pile of metal doors 
and hinges. When defending itself, the Guard is considered 
to have an OL Medium.
5. Fireplace: Burning embers are kept ablaze within the 
Guard’s stomach. Once per Scene, the Guard’s attack inflicts 
1 additional Wound.
6. Plates:  The Guard’s body is a cluster of plates and 
glasses embedded in the living skin. When the Guard is 
eliminated, it explodes into a thousand shards, inflicting 
1 Wound to the one who dealt the last blow unless they 
succeed a Defense Check.

138
The Castle’s Courtyard
Descriptor: A large inner courtyard, which was once a 
lavish garden. Now, ivy and brambles infest every crevice, 
forming a real forest.
Threat – Forest of Animated 
Brambles
Descriptor: Brambles and ivy animated by Talia’s ability. 
We act to capture and strangle any intruders. We are guarding 
the courtyard, waiting to end Accursio’s life.
OL Hard 7
Gift – Forest of Brambles:  The mass of brambles acts 
under the magical power of Talia and can perform 1d6 
actions per Exchange. Each individual action can be used 
in a different way. 1. Attacking:  Whip at or strangle a 
target and inflict 1 Wound; 2. Ensnaring:  Immobilize 
a target, preventing it from acting; 3. Manipulating: 
Manipulate objects or the setting to open or close passages;  
4. Communicating:  Take the shape of rudimentary 
letters. The Forest cannot be destroyed unless it is 
completely set on fire and, even then, it will regenerate 
within a few minutes. By inflicting 1 Wound, it’s possible 
to hack away some branches and slow down the plants’ 
growth, for example, to free an ensnared target. If Accursio 
is present the Forest of Animated Brambles will focus on 
capturing him, devoting at least half its actions to that 
purpose.
Information: Access to the courtyard is easy, as the large 
drawbridge of the castle is lowered. From the Courtyard, 
you can access the Master Tower and the Main Court.
The Master Tower
Descriptor: A large six-story fortified tower, with barred 
windows. The large main door is walled by brambles and 
plants.
Information: Talia has blocked the only exit with her 
Forest of Animated Brambles and attempting to force it will 
activate its Threat (see previous column). By inflicting a 
total of 3 Wounds to the brambles, you can open the door. 
Accursio has taken refuge on the third floor where, slowly, 
he is sawing the bars out of a window with the intention 
of stretching a rope to the walls and escaping. Accursio 
is the only one, besides Alexandra, who has a key to the 
Dungeons.
Accursio De Celeste

/ Take advantage of the arrival of the Hunters in Stegeborg to

140
Formal Dining Room
A large and lavish ballroom where there are 6 Quiet Guards.
Kitchens
The great kitchens of the castle. By crawling up inside the fireplace’s 
chimney, you can reach the Noble Rooms on the second floor. By 
inspecting the Kitchens, you can see that meals are still being prepared. 
From the Kitchens, one can access the Cellars (page 141).
Servants’ Chambers
Upon entering the Servant’s Quarters, the Hunters will find the bodies 
of servants half melded with walls and sinking into the floor, frozen in 
expressions of terror. Getting too close to the walls will rouse the servants, 
animating them.
Connection Corridor
A large corridor in the east wing of the castle. From the large, barred 
windows, the Hunters will notice the figure of a man moving in the 
Master Tower. It is Accursio, working hard on his escape.
Threat – Walls of Memory
Descriptor: Bodies and stone come alive to hold intruders in 
a crazy embrace.
OL Easy 3
Gift – The Horror Sculpted in the Stone:  When the 
Walls of Memory come alive, surprised targets receive 1 
Penalty to any potential Defense Check. The walls grab a 
target and whisper words of despair and suffering in their 
ears. Those who hear them must decide whether to lose 1 
Soma or suffer 1 Wound before freeing themselves.
Second 
floor

141
The Guard Room
A narrow, small chamber tucked between the two floors 
of the castle, divided into two by a wooden weapon rack. 
A large, barred door leads to the Noble Rooms. Trying 
to open the door without Talia’s permission activates the 
Phantom Weapons Threat, which will attack the intruders 
as long as they remain in the room.
Threat – Phantom Weapons
Descriptor: A series of weapons that float in the air, ready 
to strike at unwelcome visitors.
OL Medium 5
Gift – Blade Storm:  When this Threat is activated, 1d6 
different targets will be attacked by a blade during each 
Exchange. If there are more attacking blades than targets, 
each target receives 1 Penalty to Defense Checks to avoid 
being hit. Two additional attacking blades impose 2 
Penalties to Defense Checks to all targets, and so forth.
The Tapestry Room
A large and sumptuous room covered with tapestries and 
paintings depicting the Schwernis family. Almost all the 
paintings are untouched, but the largest on the north wall 
is ruined. It’s possible to make out that the painting depicts 
a family: father, mother, and daughter. The parents are 
so similar that they look like twins. The husband’s face is 
obscured by old blood, while the mother’s body has been 
warped by cuts and stains making it look like a monster. 
The daughter, a cold and sad figure in the pose, has two 
scratches on her eyes, making them appear closed.
Cellars
In the large Cellars of the castle, the old barrels and piles of 
dirt are home to Alexandra. The monster will not hesitate 
to attack any intruder. Here a large metal hatch allows 
access to the Dungeons of the castle. The hatch is locked 
with Talia’s magic, but it can be opened with her key. There 
are two copies. One is in the possession of Alexandra and 
the other is held by Accursio.
Alexandra the Mother
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (5 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a monster, deformed in body and mind. 
The curse that keeps me alive means that I cannot forget 
what I have done. Now, with my misshapen mouth, I can 
only grunt and whine, preventing me from imploring the 
forgiveness of my daughter.
Agenda: After more than fifty years of a cursed existence, 
I have come to understand the damage my sins have done. 
Unable to beg for her forgiveness, I show my daughter how 
much I regret the past by doing anything she asks of me.
Gift – Deformed Orc:  Alexandra is a huge monster with 
herculean strength. She can act twice during an Exchange, 
but not consecutively. The curse makes Alexandra resistant 
to magic. Any action using magic that involves her receives 
1 Penalty.
Information: Upon her death, Alexandra’s body 
will reverse to that of a gorgeous middle-aged woman. 
Alexandra is the only one, besides Accursio, who has a key 
to the Dungeons.

Talia

girl by slowly gaining her trust. Lisbet’s hope is that sooner or

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: A series of tunnels and galleries full of barred

Talia the Sleeper 
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young girl put to sleep for eternity by the

Agenda: Revenge myself on Accursio, who betrayed my trust

assimilating everything I can with my power.
Gift – Sleeping Spirit:  Talia can literally manipulate the 
entire castle as she pleases. During each Exchange, she can 
perform up to 3 of the following actions. 1. Manipulate: 
Move or reshape an object of the castle into something else; 
2. Attack:  Throw and move objects to inflict 1 Wound;  
3. Reject:  Prevent people from moving, entering, or 
leaving by manipulating objects, or even the castle itself. 
Talia cannot speak, but she can use Manipulate to write or 
compose rudimentary messages through objects. Talia can 
concentrate all 3 actions into one with devastating effects. It 
can collapse an entire wing of the castle or open a deep well 
in the ground. The effects of this action are wide-ranging and, 
at the discretion of the Storyteller, may remove one or more 
Hunters who fail a Defense Check from the Scene.
Information: Next to Talia’s sleeping body, one can find her 
diary, written with a pen manipulated by her hair. It tells the 
Sleeper’s whole sad story.

143
Note: Talia’s Gift, Sleeping Spirit, can be used in 
countless ways throughout the Scenario. Below is a 
set of suggestions for the Storyteller. Extracts of Talia’s 
diary are on page 148.
Talia can:
 ◆ Leave messages on pictures and furniture or send 
the Hunters a page from her diary.
 ◆ Lock and open doors to provide guidance.
 ◆ Move the curtains on the windows to illuminate a 
dark room.
 ◆ Distort the paintings in the Tapestry Room so 
her tragedy is better understood.
 ◆ Lock the drawbridge to prevent someone from 
escaping.
 ◆ Withdraw the brambles from outside the castle, 
pulling them in through the windows.
 ◆ Use one of the prisoners as a warning.
 ◆ Draw the Hunters’ attention to one of Accurio’s 
belongings that has been left in the castle, and 
which can hint at his presence.
 ◆ Open a well in the treasure room as a way of warning 
the Hunters away.

144
Scenario Events
In this Scenario there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel free 
to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.
At dawn on the day following the arrival of the 
Hunters: Accursio will finish sawing the bars and will try to 
escape by descending from the walls. Talia will unleash her 
abilities and will capture him, taking him to the dungeons to 
punish him.
If the Hunters manage to rescue Accursio from 
Stegeborg: Talia will give into her fury, killing all the 
prisoners except Lisbet. Now, the new target of the Sleeper’s 
hatred will be the Hunters.
If the Hunters show interest in the history of the 
Schwernis family:  Talia will ease the pressure on the 
intruders, trying to guide them to the Tapestry Room.
If the Hunters make it clear to Lisbet that they are 
there to save her:  the woman will say that she has read 
some pages of Talia’s diary and that the spirit acts only out of 
pain and trauma.
If the Hunters find a way to communicate with 
Alexandra: she will ask them to save Talia from herself by 
helping her to feel hope once more. Then she will drive the 
Hunters to kill her by attacking them, so she can finally be 
free from the curse.
If the Hunters reveal themselves to Accursio: he will 
tell them that a spirit roams in the castle and that perhaps 
something the adventurer stole has enraged it. The scoundrel 
will seem sincerely repentant and will deliver what he has 
stolen to the Hunters so that they can bring it back to the 
basement and “appease” the spirit.
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Stegeborg’s 
Quiet Scenario and can be acquired once the requirements 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.
Adept of the Quiet
Requirement: To have witnessed a manifestation of Talia’s 
abilities within the castle.
Y ou realize that having a calm spirit prevents the castle from 
perceiving you as a threat. Receive 1 Additional Success in 
all Defense Checks you make against a manifestation of the 
power of Stegeborg or a creature associated with the powers 
of spirits.
The Castle’s Secrets
Requirement: Learn the secret passages of the castle from 
Accursio or Lisbet.
Y ou know some of the many secret passages that connect the 
whole castle. During a Scene, you can spend 1 Soma to find 
a passage from one place to another in the castle, as long as 
you have been there before. Y ou can’t leave the castle walls 
with this Gift.
