2
145
FIRST SCENE –  Arrival at the 
Village
It’s the same one you find on page 136.
End of Scene: After learning from Georg what happened 
to the village and Accursio’s disappearance, the Hunters 
continue their investigation at the castle. Go to the SECOND 
SCENE – The Castle’s Courtyard.
SECOND SCENE – The Castle’s 
Courtyard
Read aloud to the Hunters:  After crossing the large 
drawbridge, you finally arrive at the castle’s immense garden. 
Brambles and ivy cover everything, and Georg’s description of 
the havoc the plants caused in the village sounds more like 
a story made up to frighten children. Two lights attract your 
attention. One comes from the third floor of the large Master 
Tower to your left. The second light shines from the second 
floor of the castle. All the windows have thick bars and you 
soon understand that the entrance doors of the two structures 
are the only way to access the buildings.
Location: The large inner garden of the castle.
Time: Night has now fallen, a light coming from the tower 
and one from the second floor of the castle shine in the dark.
Information: Talia will follow the actions of the Hunters 
to understand their intentions and then animate the castle to 
close them in by raising 
the drawbridge. See 
the Animated Bramble 
Forest Threat on page 
138.
End of Scene:  To 
escape from the animated 
brambles, the Hunters can 
decide to enter the tower, 
go to THIRD SCENE 
OPTION A – Master Tower, 
or the castle, go to THIRD SCENE OPTION B – Main Court.
THIRD SCENE OPTION A –  
Master Tower
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you eventually enter 
the tower, the brambles seem to recoil as if frightened of the 
place. A call for help comes from the stairs. It seems whoever 
inhabits the third floor has witnessed your escape and is 
descending at a rapid pace towards you.
Place: The old Master Tower, where Talia killed her father 
to defend herself fifty years earlier.
Time: It is night and only Accursio’s lantern’s light 
illuminates the bare, gray rooms of the tower.
Information: Accursio will ask the Hunters for help, but 
he will soon understand that they too can do little against 
the power of Talia. The cunning adventurer will tell the 
Hunters that an evil spirit resides in the castle and that, 
The Scenario Stegeborg’s 
Quiet in Scenes

3b
4
146
in his search of treasures, he must have inadvertently 
awakened it. Appearing repentant, Accursio will ask the 
Hunters to do something to save him since they are here 
to solve the mystery. However, he will refuse to leave the 
Tower, pointing out that the thorns almost killed him 
when he tried to escape. To help the Hunters, Accursio will 
show them one of the secret passages leading directly to 
the Noble Rooms on the second floor. He will explain to 
the Hunters that from there you can descend into a large 
fireplace, reach the Kitchens, and then descend into the 
Cellars where the evil spirit is located, avoiding most of the 
dangers of the castle. 
End of Scene:  Following Accursio’s directions, the 
Hunters enter the castle through a secret passage, go to the 
FOURTH SCENE – Noble Rooms.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B –  
Main Court
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you finally enter the 
castle, the brambles seem to settle and still once more as if 
nothing had happened. In front of you is the huge castle of 
Stegeborg, a maze with over one hundred rooms.
Place: The gloomy and abandoned castle of Stegeborg.
Time: It is night and only the light brought by the Hunters 
illuminates the large dust-covered rooms.
Information: When exploring the castle, the Hunters 
will pass through various Places (presented on page 139) 
in the following order:
Servants’ Chambers (page 140)
Tapestry Room (page 141)
Guard Room (page 141)
Talia will observe the Hunters and can intervene thanks to 
her Sleeping Spirit Gift (page 142).
End of Scene:  Past the Guard Room, the Hunters will 
have access to the second floor, go to the FOURTH SCENE 
– Noble Rooms.
FOURTH SCENE – Noble Rooms
Note: The Hunters can reach this Scene after exploring the 
castle or thanks to the secret passage indicated by Accursio. 
In the second case, before setting up this Scene, they will 
go through the Tapestry Room (page 141) and then the 
Guard Room (page 141).
Read aloud to the Hunters: You close the big door behind 
you and you can hear the clang of the ghostly weapons that 
still slam on the heavy wrought iron reinforcements. This 
area of the castle seems illuminated and in fact, within a few 
moments, some human figures stare at you from the bottom 
of a large dining room. A woman with a thin and tired face 
raises a hand in greeting.
Place: The castle’s main room, where the Schwernis family 
lived.
Time: It is past midnight.
Information: Lisbet will welcome the Hunters, telling 
them that all the prisoners are kept alive by a monster that 
lives in the cellars, which regularly brings food and water. 
Lisbet is often taken to the castle’s basement by the creature 
to read or sing old stories to a girl who seems frozen in 
perpetual sleep. Talking to Lisbet, the Hunters will also 
discover:
 ◆ The monstrous creature that feeds the prisoners moves 
freely around the castle and possesses incredible 
strength.

5
6
147
 ◆ From the large fireplace in the dining room, you can 
go downstairs to the Kitchens. From there you can 
reach the Cellars where you can find the hatch for the 
Dungeons. If the Hunters have spoken to Accursio, 
they will then have confirmation that the man was not 
lying.
 ◆ Lisbet will say that the sleeping girl seems unutterably 
sad and, despite what she has done in the village, Lisbet 
cannot help but feel compassion for her rather than 
hatred.
End of Scene:  Hunters can wait until Alexandra arrives 
to ambush her or climb down the chimney and face her 
directly in the Cellars. Go to THE FIFTH SCENE – The 
Castle’s Orc.
FIFTH SCENE – The Castle’s Orc
Note: This Scene kicks off when the Hunters face 
Alexandra.
Read aloud to the Hunters:  What stands before your 
eyes is a grotesque creature with vaguely human features. 
An old noble woman’s dress dangles from its shape like a rag, 
soiled with blood and ichor. The being is short of breath, but 
its muscles are tense and powerful. Only the eyes glow with 
a deep light of suffering and madness, protruding from the 
deformed face, which is dotted with pustules and bubbles. 
The being roars with all its anger before hurling itself against 
you.
Place: The castle cellars or the dining room where all the 
prisoners are kept.
Time: A few hours left until dawn.
Information: Alexandra will fight to the death, in the 
hope that the Hunters will end her life, freeing her from the 
curse. Once she’s killed, she’ll turn back into the beautiful 
woman she once was. Around her neck, she wears a chain 
with the key to the Dungeons’ trapdoor.
End of Scene:  Hunters can descend into the Dungeons 
and finally meet Talia. Go to SCENE SIX – The Sad Sleeper.
SIXTH SCENE –  The Sad 
Sleeper
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You pass down a few 
long corridors before arriving at the castle’s large treasure 
room. The door is wide open, and an unnatural green light 
illuminates the entire room. The strange glow radiates from 
a young girl floating in the air, asleep in an unnatural torpor. 
Her skin is as cold and pale as that of a corpse. Only the very 
long blond hair that lashes and moves unsettlingly allows you 
to understand that this being is alive.
Place: The treasure room of the castle.
Time: The sun begins to rise as Accursio attempts his great 
escape.
Information: Talia’s reaction will vary depending on 
what the Hunters have done so far. Shortly after the Hunters 
arrive, Accursio will be carried into the room by a group of 
Quiet Guards and dragged in front of Talia to be executed.
End of Scene: The Hunters will be key to tipping the scales 
in the epilogue of the sad story of the Sleeper of Stegeborg.

148
Talia’s 
diary
Page 1 
How long will this captivity last? My parents say it is 
for my own good, but in the meantime, sixteen years of 
my life have passed without me ever setting foot outside 
these walls. Father finally quieted my complaints by 
giving me the keys to the Master Tower. I love spending 
hours and hours in the tower, studying the villagers. 
They go about their daily tasks, never knowing that I 
sit here in a gilded cage and watch, longing to join in 
their normal lives.
Page 2
My mother got very angry with the cook. Apparently, 
she’s not as good a cook as Mother thought. The 
problem is, Mother can be quite unreasonable. More 
than once, I have seen her take out a fit of bad temper 
on the servants. My father does nothing to stop her. On 
the contrary, he seems to enjoy watching my mother 
mercilessly wielding her authority to misuse the 
servants. In the end, the cook ran away, leaving the 
castle. My mother started calling her terrible names, 
saying that such terrible food would have shamed 
the whole Schwernis family. At dinner, my father 
whispered something in my mother’s ear, running his 
finger down her throat, and then together they laughed 
rudely. I felt a shiver down my spine.
If I were a magician I would turn my mother into a 
monster, because that’s what she is. Even if she looks 
like a noblewoman, her soul is black and overflowing 
with evil.
Page 3
The new cook is called Carabosse. She is a foreigner. 
With the rumors about Mother’s cruelty, I don’t 
wonder why no one in the village wants to work at the 
castle anymore. Carabosse has a sweet face like that of 
a loving grandmother, but also deep green eyes, which 
seem to shine. She says she hails from the east and, if 
I remember correctly, the prelate of the village on his 
last visit spoke to my father of many witches fleeing 
from the numerous stakes that are in use in Eastern 
Europe. If Carabosse really is a witch, I hope she uses 
magic and turns me into a bird. It would be nice to be 
able to fly beyond these walls, above the clouds.

149
Page 4
Mother caught me. Some servant must have tattled on 
my little visits to the village at night. Or maybe it was 
the villagers who betrayed me, despite all the coins I 
gave them... They too are just bloody selfish. Now, every 
single window in the castle has a thick metal grate, and 
the transformation from a palace to prison is complete. 
My parents say this is for my own good, that the world 
outside the castle is dangerous for a pure soul like mine. 
That I am the daughter they have always desired, their 
most precious treasure. But their intentions stifle my 
soul, drowning it in utter darkness. Only Carabosse 
understands me, but she seems to grow more and 
more tired and sick. My father often stares at me with 
frightening eyes, whispering the word “desired. ” 
Page 5
I’ve discovered the truth and it ruined me. My father 
and mother are twin siblings. Their love has never been 
blessed by God and their obsession with a daughter has 
led them to so many miscarriages. What remains of my 
brothers and sisters is in the basement, too deformed 
to survive. When my mother learned what I had 
discovered, I was sure she would kill me. Instead, she 
smiled, a sweet smile like I have never seen on her face 
before. She said I am ready, that I am old enough to run 
the family. I have been living in this room for a week 
now, but tomorrow my father will return and a strange 
feeling grips my heart. I am afraid because no one is 
coming to save me.
Page 6
It happened. Mom is devastated by grief, but I don’t 
regret what I did. When my father tried to… I can’t even 
write it down. I fought back with all my might. I pushed 
him away from me, but I didn’t mean for him to fall 
down the tower. But even though I didn’t intend it, I felt 
relieved. And at that very moment, I realized that my 
soul had been forfeited. Now I’m waiting for my mother 
to decide my fate. She howled in sorrow for the three 
nights after Father’s fall, cursing God and promising me 
infinite suffering as punishment for taking away the love 
of her life.
Today, Carabosse came to bring me dinner, gave me a 
caress on the head, and told me that all this will end. 
“Sleep, my little one”... were her last words. And with 
that one kind gesture, I let myself go to sleep. The only 
real means at my disposal was to escape the walls of 
Stegeborg.

150
Once upon a time…
Vigo, a Galician port city. The Hunters were 
summoned by the Countess of Vigo herself to 
investigate two dangerous assassins with very 
particular methods. Several mutilated bodies 
have been found in recent weeks: three handsome 
young girls, whose faces were removed by the two 
murderers. An old spinner from the port saw two 
cloaked figures wandering through the alleys of 
the lower city, and that is why the Countess firmly 
believes that this is the work of a pair of murderers 
and not a single individual. It is not the first time 
that something like this has happened. Ten years 
ago, similar murders had disturbed the city, never 
to be solved. The murders included ritualistic 
elements using blood and flesh that brought to 
mind the blackest of magic. The Countess fears 
that, whatever hand held the knife, something dark 
has come to hunt the people of Vigo. A ceremony 
commemorating the ten-year anniversary of the 
Count and the Countess is to be held soon, and the 
shadow of the murders darkens what should be a 
day of celebration for the whole city.
A Scenario Inspired by Cinderella (Charles Perrault)
In your Eyes, with 
your Feet
Safety Keywords:  
Murder / Torture / Mutilation

151
The story that led us here…
Ten years ago, Maria Di Bello, a wealthy widow who 
had always been faithful to the King of Spain, lived in Vigo 
with her three daughters. The elder two were the fruit of her 
relationship with her beloved husband, while the youngest 
was the unfortunate result of a one-night stand with a ship 
captain. Maria had always loved everyone the same way: 
Francisca the elder, Marta the middle daughter, and Cinder 
the youngest. Cinder had been named for the strange gray 
hair she had inherited from her father, strikingly contrasting 
the intense blond hair of her mother and sisters. When the 
current Count Azul of Vigo reached marrying age, Maria 
offered all her daughters. Cinder was immediately rejected 
due to her hair, described as “horrible” by the noble Azul. 
Resentful and envious, Cinder turned to the Kind Mother, a 
powerful witch residing in the port of Vigo who had hidden 
her identity behind the curious name. With the Mother’s 
help, Cinder committed several murders, removing skin 
and hair from the victims and reshaping her body to look 
exactly as Count Azul desired. Her mother and sisters 
noticed the change, and Cinder was forced to silence them. 
With her death near at hand, Maria appealed to what little 
humanity she knew her daughter still had, asking her to at 
least spare her sisters. Cinder agreed, but to ensure their 
silence she maimed them both, digging out an eye and 
cutting off one of their feet. One-eyed and lame, the two 
sisters fled the city in hopes of a new life. 
But fate was not kind to Francisca and Marta and, after 
ten years as beggars and outcasts, they returned to Vigo. 
Fueled by resentment and transformed by hate for their 
cruel sister, the two have sworn revenge. In this, they are 
supported by the now elderly Kind Mother, whose tongue 
Cinder cut out after she asked the new Countess for one too 
many favors. The ambition and ruthless cruelty of Cinder 
has therefore forged a strange alliance, formed by all the 
women she has wounded along the bloody road to a life of 
luxury at the court of Vigo.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Cinder presents herself to the Hunters under the false 
name of Princesca, the identity she used to seduce Azul. 
The woman has many powers, such as enslaving animals 
or disguising her appearance, as she has learned the art of 
magic from the Kind Mother. The last remaining piece of 
evidence that could link Cinder to her crimes is a pair of 
glass slippers that the Kind Mother had created for her ten 
years earlier. Clicking the heels of the magical slippers can 
confuse or bewitch people. They were used to commit the 
murders ten years ago, as Cinder bewitched her victims 
so she could dissect them alive without interference. The 
shoes fit Cinder’s feet perfectly, proving her involvement. In 
addition, every night at midnight, Princesca returns to her 
original appearance for a few minutes, revealing her true 
identity before the magic recovers.
Francisca and Marta each wear a glass slipper and have 
staged a return of the killer for two reasons: to put Cinder 
under pressure, and to attract the attention of the Hunters 
of the Order so that they shed light on the wicked actions 
of their sister. The objective of the two is to be caught by the 
Order and then try to bring the Hunters to their side.
Meanwhile, the Kind Mother plots her revenge. The old

1
152
witch is helping Francisca and Marta only to destabilize 
Cinder. Once the Hunters begin closing in on Cinder, the 
Kind Mother will ask the Countess to use magic to return 
her tongue, promising to deal with the Hunters in return. 
However, having regained speech, and consequently the 
ability to perform magic, the Kind Mother will instead be 
able to take revenge on that wretched girl who, ten years 
earlier, trampled on everyone for her own gain.
Nota: In this Scenario, two NPCs have a double identity.
Cinder poses as Princesca, the Countess of Vigo. The 
Kind Mother poses as the Old Port spinner. 
Keep in mind that either name may be used without 
explanation when discussing these characters.
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You are led by a servant to 
the great reception hall of the noble Vigo palace. The rich decor 
and the large number of servants at work suggest that Count 
Azul of Vigo is among the richest nobles in all of Galicia. 
You are greeted by the Count himself, a handsome man in 
his forties, and his wife Princesca. According to rumors still 
circulating, she is a girl of humble origins picked from among 
many candidates for her incredible beauty.
Place: The magnificent palace of Count Azul of Vigo.
Time: It is a hot summer morning.
Initial Situation: After a quick presentation by Count 
Azul, Princesca will take the floor. The Countess will 
explain what happened (the discovery of three corpses) and 
how they were killed, which sadly recalls the events that 
transpired ten years ago. From the interview, Hunters can 
obtain the following information:
 ◆ Ten years earlier, a murderer terrorized Vigo by killing 
six young girls and removing parts of their skin and 
hair. All the victims had in common were their blond 
hair and their beauty.
 ◆ The killer’s actions culminated in the massacre of an 
entire family. Maria, Francisca, Marta, and Cinder Di 
Bello were all killed the same night. This was the last 
act of the killer before he disappeared forever. Note 
that the other two victims were commoners killed 
by Cinder so she could steal their beauty. They are 
unrelated to the Di Bello family, and not important to 
the current investigation. 
 ◆ In the last week, the bodies of three prostitutes who 
frequented the port were found. All were known for 
their beauty and a healthy head of blonde hair. Each of 
them had the skin from their faces removed.
 ◆ An old spinner who lives near the harbor is the only 
witness. She told the guards of two black-clothed 
figures who lured one of the victims into the shadows. 
The girl followed the figures as if hypnotized, as if 
sorceries and dark arts were involved.
 ◆ As lord and lady of Vigo, Princesca and Azul instruct 
the Hunters to unravel the mystery and thus put an end 
to the murderer’s ten-year slaughter.
 ◆ In a day, it will be the wedding anniversary of Azul and 
Princesca and to celebrate the occasion there will be 
a party in the summer palace located just outside the

153
city. The only obvious concern of the Count is 
not to appear weak in front of his noble guests. 
During the interview, it will be clear to the Hunters 
that it is Princesca who holds the reins of the city, 
and that her husband Azul is only a rich brat with 
little concern for the sufferings of his subjects. It 
will also be apparent that Princesca is nervous when 
speaking to the Hunters. If asked about it, she will 
deflect by reminding them of her (overly vague) 
humble beginnings and highlighting how lucky she 
was to marry the Count.
After the interview, Princesca will reveal to the 
Hunters where to find the old spinner and the last 
corpse. They’re both in the port area. 
The Palace of Count Azul 
of Vigo
Descriptor: A sumptuous noble palace in the city 
center.
The Count Azul of Vigo
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a rich and bored nobleman who 
cares only about social position. 
Agenda: To waste time between parties and 
pleasures, leaving the burden of leadership to my wife 
Princesca.
Cinder Known to all as 
Princesca of Vigo
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC  
(3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am Cinder and, thanks to 
magic, I pretend to be the attractive and kind 
Princesca. Thanks to the power of the Kind 
Mother, I control the entire city. I am cold and 
determined and I won’t let anyone stand in 
my way. 
Agenda: Gain wealth and power by 
manipulating my stupid husband / Solving 
the mystery of the new killer who behaves 
exactly as I did ten years ago. 
Gift – Kind Magic:  Cinder is able 
to use the magic that the Kind Moth-
er taught her. As an action, Cinder 
can cast one of the following spells.  
1. Animal Servants:  A group of 
animals (up to 6 subjects) blindly 
obeys the will of Cinder, even per -
forming suicidal actions. Cinder 
has used this power many times 
on a pair of doves until they 
have been completely enslaved 
(see The Doves on page 154);  
2. Transmutation:  An ob -
ject or a portion of the envi-
ronment mutates to be trans-
formed into something else. 
The mutation lasts through-

154
out the Scene and cannot affect living beings; 3. Wish: A 
subject (other than the one casting the spell) can express a 
wish and it will come true. The Storyteller will decide the 
nearest mechanical effect to accomplish the wish, whether 
it is a new Descriptor, to heal one or more Wounds, or to 
obtain a Gift. The effect of Wish cannot bring the dead back 
to life, it only affects the subject who expresses it and lasts 
until midnight.
Gift – Beauty Wish:  Thanks to the power of magic, 
Cinder can assume any appearance she wants. The effect 
lasts until midnight and usually Cinder uses it to maintain 
the appearance of Princesca.  
The Doves
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC  
(1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are two doves enslaved by the power of 
Cinder. Magic has permanently broken our will and we 
defend our mistress from all danger. 
Gift – Go for the Eyes:  The Doves are skilled at hitting 
the enemies’ weak spots. When a Dove manages to inflict a 
Wound, it inflicts 1 additional Wound. In case of a positive 
Outcome in a Defense Check, once per Scene, the Dove’s 
attack will still inflict 1 Wound. This Gift cannot be used 
more than once against the same target 
during the Scenario.
The Port of Vigo
Descriptor: The great city’s port where ships from all over 
the world are docked. The alleys in front of the port are a 
crossroads of taverns, shops, and leisure houses. Even in the 
middle of the night, there’s always life in the harbor. 
Information: The Old Spinner resides in a small house 
in the eastern part of the pier, while the last corpse was 
found in the western area.
East of the pier:  The Hunters will be able to meet the 
Kind Mother, who will present herself as an old silent 
seamstress. Cinder knows that the spinner is the Kind 
Mother, but gives no sign that she knows. The old witch 
will communicate with the Hunters with some difficulty 
and give them a brooch she found near the alley where 
she saw the two cloaked silhouettes pass. The brooch bears 
the coat of arms of the Di Bello family: the Kind Mother 
herself will give directions to the Di Bello house, located in 
the upper quarters of the city. The old witch will not follow 
the Hunters, as she does not want to be seen by Francisca 
and Marta.
West of the pier:  The Hunters will find Eduardo and 
Felipe, two guards at the service of the Count of Vigo, 
guarding the crime scene. The body of the young prostitute 
(known as Estella) was moved to the icebox of an old fish 
warehouse nearby.
 ◆ The two guards will tell the Hunters that no one has 
claimed her body. She is most likely one of the many 
poor people just trying to get by. 
 ◆ Examining Estella’s body will confirm the removal 
of all facial skin, performed with insane care. A very

155
precious ring was inserted in the young woman’s right 
eye orbit. The ring has the coat of arms of the Countess 
of Vigo engraved on it. The object was placed there by 
Francisca and Marta to draw attention to Cinder. 
The Kind Mother Known to all 
as the Old Spinner
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am an old and powerful witch whose tongue 
Cinder cut out. I can no longer use my magic, but I still have 
potions and magic items to use for my purposes.
Agenda: Put Cinder under pressure so she asks for my help, 
make her use Wish to give me back my tongue, and then use 
my magic to take revenge on her. 
Gift – Kind Magic:  As an action, the Kind Mother can 
cast one of the following spells. 1. Animal Servants: A 
group of animals (up to 6 subjects) blindly obeys the will 
of the Kind Mother and even performs suicidal actions; 
2. Transmutation:  An object or a portion of the 
environment mutates to be transformed into something 
else. The mutation lasts throughout the Scene and cannot 
affect living beings; 3. Wish: A subject (other than the 
one casting the spell) can and will express a wish. The 
Storyteller will decide the nearest mechanical effect to 
accomplish the wish, whether it is a new Descriptor, to heal 
one or more Wounds, or to obtain a Gift. The effect of Wish 
cannot bring the dead back to life, it only affects the subject 
who expresses it and lasts until midnight. Note: The Kind 
Mother cannot use this Gift unless she first finds a way to 
heal her severed tongue.
Gift – Witch’s Treasures:  The Kind Mother possesses 
a large number of magical objects and potions, which she 
can use or give to her allies. Once per Scenario, she can 
make use of each of these effects. 1. Potion: If ingested, 
a potion bestows a Descriptor that provides an incredible 
ability, such as flying or being invisible for the duration 
of the Scene. No potion can grow her tongue back. 2. 
Heirloom: The Kind Mother can get a hold of a Treasure 
of the Order (page 5) that has not already been found 
and is not in the possession of the Hunters.
Eduardo and Felipe
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are two guards who are content to leave 
thinking to other people. We do as we’re told while doing as 
little as possible.    
The Di Bello Family 
House
Descriptor: A large, abandoned villa in the bourgeois 
districts of Vigo.
Information: Francisca and Marta live in the house 
their mother abandoned. The two sisters traveled around 
the world for a long time, but it was in mystical Prague 
they underwent the strange rite that sewed their bodies 
together. Now, the two women act as one creature, each 
wearing on her foot one of the glass slippers that the Kind 
Mother gave them. Francisca and Marta will be hostile to 
the Hunters until they discover their identity. At that point,

156
the sisters will fall at their feet, begging them to expose 
Cinder, who has escaped the consequences of her crimes 
for ten years, hiding under the false identity of Princesca.
 ◆ The two sisters are willing to do anything to get revenge 
on Cinder, including going to prison or even giving up 
their lives.
 ◆ They will reveal to the Hunters that Cinder uses magic 
to alter her appearance. Ten years earlier their mother 
Maria had discovered the deception when she surprised 
Cinder at the stroke of midnight, the moment each day 
when she must recast her transfiguration spell.
Francisca and Marta the Vengeful 
Sisters
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are two poor sisters sewn together in one 
body. We move with perfect coordination and cooperate 
effortlessly, each aiding the other against any danger. 
Agenda: Discredit Princesca and reveal to everyone that 
she is Cinder, our despised sister.
Gift – The Razor-sharp Glass Slippers:  Thanks to 
the magical slippers the Kind Mother gave them, at every 
step Francisca and Marta can click the glass heels and create 
a resonance that generates a state of confusion in subjects 
within forty feet. When a Hunter acts under the influence 
of the Slippers, they must perform a Defense Check against 
OL Medium 5 or lose their action for the Exchange. This 
effect is considered mental manipulation. Once per Scene, 
the two sisters can click their heels and cause a vibration 
that damages the minds of those within reach. All affected 
subjects must pass a Defense Check against OL Hard 7 or 
suffer 1 Wound. Activating the Slippers is considered a free 
additional action for the sisters. 
Gift – As One Body:  Francisca and Marta can act twice 
during an Exchange, once for each sister.
Count Azul of Vigo’s 
Summer Residence
Descriptor: A beautiful villa in the countryside of Galicia.
Information: The feast for the tenth wedding anniversary 
of Azul and Princesca is being held in the villa. Noblemen 
and wealthy bourgeois are among the guests at the reception, 
while a large troupe of acrobats has the task of brightening 
up the evening with songs, dances, and juggling. A skilled 
animal tamer has been arranged at the explicit request of 
Princesca, who will therefore always have animals (a tiger 
and a bear) to control with magic if need be.
 ◆ Talking to the guests during the reception, the Hunters 
will discover that after ten years, many are still 
wondering how a girl of humble origins could steal the 
heart of Count Azul.
 ◆ At the stroke of midnight, before the great final toast, 
Princesca will fake being dizzy to get away for those 
few minutes she needs to cast her transfiguration spell.

157
Akim the Tamer
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC 
(2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a brave and skilled 
tamer. The tiger Tasha and the bear 
Berto are both dear to me, as they obey 
my every command.
Agenda: Perform and amaze the 
audience with my animals / Prevent 
anyone from harming Tasha and 
Berto, whom I consider as my children.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters keep Princesca informed of the 
investigation: the clever woman will quickly understand 
that someone is framing her and will send Eduardo and 
Felipe to deal with the Kind Mother. If the Hunters don’t do 
something, the old witch won’t escape her fate. 
If the Hunters defeat Francisca and Marta: the two 
sisters will tell them everything they know, imploring the 
Hunters to help expose Cinder.
If the Hunters unmask Cinder:  The Countess will 
use the power of her magic to escape and run to the Kind 
Mother for her help. Once she regains her voice thanks to 
Cinder’s use of Wish, the Kind Mother will assist Cinder 
to eliminate the Hunters and then take her time making 
Cinder pay. 
If the Hunters understand who the Kind Mother 
really is: The witch will offer her aid in exchange for her 
life and will seek their help in regaining her voice. 
If the Hunters seek the support of Azul:  The Count 
will pretend to be a fool and will even avoid taking measures 
against Cinder once she has been unmasked. The man is 
so willfully ignorant that he will ban the Hunters from the 
city in order to keep his wife where she is, managing all the 
responsibilities so he can lead a life of laziness. 
If the Hunters trap Cinder during the anniversary 
party: The woman will use magic to take control of Tasha 
and Berto, sending them against the Hunters. Akim will 
hinder the Hunters if they try to harm his beloved animals. 
Cinder will try to escape to the Kind Mother by taking 
advantage of the chaos. 
Tasha the Tiger
OL Medium 5 – Minor 
NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fast and 
aggressive, though trained, tiger.
Berto the Bear
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC  
(3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a strong and massive trained 
bear.

158
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the In your Eyes, 
with your Feet Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.
Gift – Kind Blessing
Requirement: Show a sincere interest in solving the mysterious 
murders in Vigo to the Kind Mother.
A powerful witch has blessed you with protection against magic 
and sorcery. Receive 2 Additional Successes when you make a 
Defense Check against a magic effect. The first time you inflict 1 
Wound on a target using magic, the blessing dissipates until the 
next Interlude.
Note: The Storyteller can offer this Gift to the Hunter who has met 
the requirements without revealing who the witch who casts the 
blessing is. Simply, the Hunter will become aware that someone is 
trying to help them.
Gift – The Razor-sharp Glass Slippers (Treasure of 
the Order)
Requirement: Defeat Francisca and Marta.
Obtain the Treasure of the Order The Razor-sharp Glass Slippers, 
see page 25 of the Treasury of the Order.

1
2a 2b
159
The Scenario Through your 
Eyes, with your Feet in 
Scenes
FIRST SCENE –  The Regents of 
Vigo
It’s the same one you find on page 152. The Hunters will 
talk to Azul and Princesca.
End of Scene:  The Hunters can decide whether to 
interrogate the Old Spinner, go to SECOND SCENE 
OPTION A – The Old Spinner. Or examine the body of the 
last victim, go to SECOND SCENE OPTION B – The crime 
scene.
SECOND SCENE OPTION A 
– The Old Spinner
Read aloud to the Hunters: The port of Vigo is very busy 
and represents the heart of the city. Its importance can be seen 
clearly in the constant flow of goods and the ships crowded at 
the docks. Following the directions given by Princesca, you 
come to a small house in one of the alleys right next to a large 
fish market, each fisherman setting up his stall directly on his 
docked boat. You find the door to the small open dwelling, 
and when you enter you see the figure of an old lady sewing. 
The woman opens her mouth as if to greet you, but no 
sound emerges. It quickly becomes clear her tongue has been 
removed, leaving only a stump behind in her mouth. 
Place: A small house that the spinner also uses as a 
tailoring workshop. 
Time: It is late morning and the smell of fresh fish coming 
from the fishermen’s ships tickles the nostrils. 
Information: The Kind Mother will welcome the 
Hunters, trying to make herself understood to the best of 
her ability, and will give the same information presented 
on page 155. 
End of Scene: Having obtained the brooch and directions 
to the house of the Di Bello family, the Hunters can go there 
to investigate. Go to THIRD SCENE – The old house of the 
Di Bello family. Or examine the body of the last victim, go 
to SECOND SCENE OPTION B – The crime scene.
Note: For correct management of the clues, the Hunters 
must go to an interview with the Old Spinner before the 
THIRD SCENE – The old house of the Di Bello family.
SECOND SCENE OPTION B – 
The crime scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The port of Vigo is very 
busy and represents the heart of the city. Its importance can 
be seen clearly in the constant flow of goods and the ships 
crowded at the docks. Following the instructions given by 
Princesca, you arrive at an old warehouse that is now being 
used as a morgue. Two men who look quite drowsy guard the 
front door.
Place: An old warehouse in the western part of the port. 
Time: It is late morning. A large ship from the Americas is 
docking at the port.

3
160
Information: Eduardo and Felipe will be well disposed 
towards the Hunters, letting them in to examine the body 
of the victim. They can discover the information on page 
155. 
End of Scene: With all the doubts that the ring can put in 
the minds of the Hunters, there are still some fundamental 
clues missing. They can go and talk to the Old Spinner. Go 
to SECOND SCENE OPTION A – The Old Spinner. Or, if 
they’ve been there already, they can head to the THIRD 
SCENE – The old house of the Di Bello family.  
THIRD SCENE –  The old house 
of the Di Bello family
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The house of the Di 
Bello family is an elegantly imposing villa that, despite its 
abandonment, stands out among the houses of the upper 
districts of Vigo. Sun-bleached curtains in the second-floor 
windows have been disturbed, perhaps by new occupants of 
the long-abandoned home.
Place: The grand, abandoned house of the Di Bello family.
Time: It is just past noon.
Information: Francisca and Marta are in the house. As 
soon as the Hunters enter, they will be attacked by the sisters. 
Once defeated, the two women will reveal their identities 
and the whole story of Cinder, asking for the Hunters’ help 
in unmasking their sister. The two will suggest that the 
Hunters confront Cinder during the wedding anniversary 
party that will be held the following evening.
 ◆ The two sisters will confess that they are using the 
same methods and plan that Cinder used ten years 
earlier. They will also say that they put the ring in the 
victim’s eye (if the Hunters have found it) to guide the 
investigation towards the Countess.
 ◆ The two sisters will say they were helped by an old 
witch who gave them the slippers. The woman left  
them the shoes without saying a word, so the sisters 
could not see her face.
End of Scene:  It will now be clear to the Hunters that 
the Countess hides a secret, go to the FOURTH SCENE – 
Unmasking the Countess.

4
161
FOURTH SCENE –  Unmasking the 
Countess
This scene can develop in many ways and in many possible places, so 
it’s presented below with different tips for managing your Scenario.
If the Hunters want to unmask Cinder: The best way will be to 
catch her red-handed while casting the spell to alter her appearance 
at midnight. If they wish to, the Hunters will be welcome to attend 
the reception. Once unmasked, Cinder will stir up the beasts of 
Akim against the Hunters, then try to flee to the Kind Mother 
for help.
If the Hunters tell everything to Princesca: The Countess 
will thank them, trying to dismiss them as soon as possible, and 
then move her guards to arrest her sisters and the Kind Mother. 
If the Hunters do nothing, the three women will disappear into 
the dungeons of the castle never to be seen again.
If the Hunters attempt to expose the Kind Mother:  
The old witch will try to play on their pity to avoid being 
handed over to the Order. The old woman’s primary goal  is 
to regain her tongue thanks to Cinder’s magic. Once she has 
done that, she will try to eliminate every potential witness, 
Hunters included.
End of Scene:  However the Scene goes, once again the 
fate of the unfortunate protagonists of this story will be in 
the hands of the Hunters.

162
Once upon a time…
Prague is a great city recovering from war, after 
Fredrick the Great’s recently failed siege. Ostromir 
Malý, the Bohemian regent of Prague, has sent 
an urgent request for help to the Order. The 
nobles charged with rebuilding Prague are being 
murdered in a way that has shocked the city, one 
each night and all by the same hand. Each corpse 
has been found with a body part removed and a 
message scrawled on the walls reading, “I will stay 
with you always. ” Ostromir has commissioned 
the well-known alchemist Konstantin Svoboda to 
construct a new guardian for the city – a Golem 
– but the construction has been delayed. Until 
its completion, the Hunters have been called on 
to track down the noble-stalking killer, while the 
commoners of the city, enthralled, watch like the 
audience at a play.
A Scenario Inspired by The 
Happy Prince (Oscar Wilde) by 
Tommaso De Benetti
I Will Stay with You Always
Safety Keywords:
  
Body Horror / Murder / Torture

163
The story that led us here…
In the city of Prague stood a statue called the Happy 
Prince, so-called for its extraordinary beauty: the body of 
the statue was covered with a thin layer of gold and the eyes 
were sapphires. On the hilt of the sword are set beautiful 
rubies. One quiet evening, a young swallow on his yearly 
migration to Egypt decided to rest under the sculpture. The 
Prince saw the way the ordinary people of Prague suffered 
from hunger and fear in the half-ruined city, and the statue 
wept with pity. The Prince’s tears moved the bird to feel 
the same compassion for the commoners of the city. The 
Prince convinced the swallow to take the precious things 
from his body and bring them to those in need. “The living 
think that gold can make them happy, ” said the Prince, and 
on hearing that, the swallow consented. Day after day the 
swallow stripped the statue of everything that was valuable 
to give that wealth to the people of Prague, who were 
ground endlessly beneath the bootheels of the powerful. 
Doing so, however, took so long that winter arrived. The 
swallow could no longer reach Egypt to be reunited with 
his flock. He decided to stay near to the Prince, who the 
bird now loved, until one sad day the cold froze the swallow 
into a grim little statue. Being powerless to save his friend 
finally broke the Prince’s heart. Now worthless, the statue 
was removed and handed over to Konstantin Svoboda, the 
alchemist, for his experiments. For some reason, the lump 
of lead at the statue’s heart refused to melt.
At the request of God Himself, an angel descended from 
Paradise to retrieve the two most precious things Prague 
had to offer: the Prince’s heart and the body of the 
swallow. But the angel returned to Heaven empty-
handed, for in both the heart of the Prince and the spirit 
of the swallow pity had been replaced by hatred for the 
way Prague had rewarded their generosity.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
Deemed unworthy of Paradise, the spirit of the swallow 
instead possessed the body of Alexandr Pták, known 
professionally as Alexandr the Bird. He was a circus 
acrobat whose trademark was long flights through the 
air during the dangerous stunts that made up his act. 
Alexandr, who officially disappeared a few weeks ago, is 
now almost completely under the control of the spirit. 
With the little lucidity he had left, the acrobat returned 
to Černobog, the young son of a seamstress to whom 
the swallow had donated two rubies. Černobog’s mother 
died from hardship and deprivation she suffered in 
making a noble’s party dress. The boy had long been 
considering retaliation against the client who had made 
such unreasonable demands. Černobog and Alexandr 
overcame their communication barriers to reach an 
agreement. First they would recover the Prince’s body 
and then they would dedicate themselves to avenging his 
memory. They arrived at the laboratory of Konstantin, 
the alchemist in charge of building the Golem. 
Konstantin was desperate. Despite all the resources that 
had been provided to him, the work couldn’t proceed 
without the essential missing ingredient, which is, 
unfortunately for him, human flesh. The regent had 
threatened to expel him from Prague in the absence of 
developments, but the arrival of Černobog and Alexandr 
seemed providential.

1
164
Although Černobog had been ready to pay for it with a 
ruby, the Prince’s body had already been melted down. 
His lead heart, however, is still relatively intact. Konstantin 
thus proposes a pact: Černobog will invest the ruby to buy 
the old circus from which Alexandr comes, which will 
become the new secret laboratory of the alchemist. Then 
the boy and Alexandr will be able to devote themselves 
to their revenge, each time taking a piece of the body of 
the victims as part of their bargain with the alchemist. In 
return, Konstantin will rebuild the Prince, unleashing him 
during the upcoming dance at Prague Castle...
Note: This Scenario presents an investigation in which 
Hunters have a wide range of options on how to proceed. 
The initial presentation of the places corresponds to the 
Read aloud to the Hunters of the Scene Tree mode. This 
method makes it easier for Hunters to review the clues and 
overall situation at the beginning of each Scene.
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The regent of Prague, 
Ostromir Malý, welcomes you in his brightly lit study, located 
inside the castle of the city, a fortified complex recently 
renovated by Empress Maria Theresa of Habsburg. Behind his 
slick desk of fine wood, the short, ragged little Ostromir almost 
disappears. He welcomes you with a certain enthusiasm and 
you immediately notice how nervous he is: after introducing 
himself he begins to compulsively clean his glasses, while 
trying in vain to attract the attention of two servants in order 
to offer you refreshment. Without even letting you speak, 
he tries to fill the silences with bizarre questions about the 
nature of the Order, but he doesn’t seem really interested in 
your answers. Around a marble table, seated on chairs lined 
with red satin, there are three other people: an old man with 
a long beard and gray mustache, a gentleman in his forties 
with thick brown hair, and a gorgeous blonde girl in elegant 
clothes. Ostromir introduces them respectively as Konstantin 
Svoboda, a well-known alchemist, Bartoloměj Novotný, 
assistant to the director of the National Theater, and Beáta 
Czernin, a member of one of the noble families of the city.  
He then invites you to sit on empty chairs and, sinking into 
his, begins to explain the situation.         
Place: A beautiful room in Prague Castle, too big for a 
modest presence like that of Ostromir. In addition to the 
table where much of the conversation will take place after 
the arrival of the Hunters, there’s a beautiful display of 
golden chandeliers hanging from the ceiling, large mirrors 
with precious frames, fine carpets on polished floors, and, 
on the walls, huge tapestries darkened by time. 
Time: Late afternoon, an ideal time for tea.

165
Initial Situation: Ostromir appears obviously nervous as 
if he knows how much his success depends on the Hunters’ 
help. 
Information: From the interview with Ostromir, 
Konstantin, Bartoloměj, and Beáta, the following 
information can be obtained:
 ◆ Someone is killing (or trying to kill) prominent 
people in Prague. The connection between the cases is 
obvious, as the words “I will stay with you always” have 
been found written on the wall with various substances 
at the crime scenes. 
 ◆ Beáta found her murdered lover in her rooms, wearing 
a dress he’ d bought for her. He was found missing an 
arm.
 ◆ Bartoloměj found the theater director, Gabriel Černý, 
lying with one leg severed on the stage where a new 
show was to be performed just a few hours later. 
 ◆ Konstantin seems to have escaped the killer through 
a coincidental routine change (he had been delayed 
longer than usual in a nearby tavern), but his alchemical 
lab was devastated.
Hunters can now choose where to start their investigations.
Ostromir Malý
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I rule Prague on behalf of Empress Maria 
Theresa and, after the failed siege, I hold the city’s fate in the 
palm of my hand. The recent murders are complicating my 
life, though so far the noble families support my authority.
Agenda: Capture the assassin who is causing panic among 
the nobles / Avoid a popular uprising.
Beáta Czernin the Noblewoman
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am part of the city’s elite and I take advantage 
of it. Sometimes, my vanity and arrogance unexpectedly 
cause annoyances. 
Agenda: Distancing myself from my late lover Danek in the 
eyes of my husband and family / Finding out which monster 
may have murdered him in this way.
The Alchemical 
Laboratory
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You reach the workshop 
from a small staircase on one side of Haštalská, the street 
in Prague that runs alongside the Church of St. Castulus. A 
stone corridor leads you to what looks like the entrance to 
a cell, protected by a wooden door reinforced by large iron 
bars. Konstantin pulls out a large key that he hangs on his 
neck and makes his way inside the laboratory, where the 
soft light of lanterns hanging on the wall illuminates a cold 
space similar to a tomb. Built of red brick and furnished with 
wooden tables and benches, the workshop houses hundreds 
of stills, bottles, and parchments abandoned on dusty shelves. 
Dried plants and other esoteric trinkets hang from the ceiling. 
In the center of the room is a forge, which Konstantin uses 
to process minerals of various kinds, while in the corner an 
elegant wooden desk is covered with paperwork, inks, and 
pens for writing. What strikes you most is the almost total 
devastation of a wing of the laboratory, now a pile of rubble, 
glass, and split wooden boards. On the ground, a dry puddle 
of god-knows-what emits a nauseating stench. Konstantin

166
apologizes for the mess, saying he didn’t have the strength to 
fix it, “now that he’s alone. ” On the wall, written in smears 
with dark mineral powders, you see: “I will stay with you 
always. ” 
Descriptor: An underground laboratory with a large forge 
and esoteric trinkets. The east wing is a pile of rubble and on 
the wall in front of you can clearly be read the phrase: “I will 
stay with you always.”
Information: Depending on their ability to ask questions 
and examine clues, Hunters can access some or all of the 
following information:
 ◆ Anything that was broken or stolen had little value, 
with the exception of a lead heart that belonged to an 
old statue that was recently removed from the city. 
Konstantin says that the heart was the subject of his 
studies, as he had not been able to melt it. 
 ◆ Konstantin is working on a secret project on behalf 
of the city of Prague (the Golem), which he is very 
reluctant to talk about. Apparently, it’s not proceeding 
as planned. 
 ◆ A few days ago, a man who was in charge of cleaning 
was fired: Georg Slanina, who lives in the nearby 
rookery. Konstantin claims he was often intoxicated, 
which is true. The real reason for the dismissal, not to be 
revealed to the Hunters directly, was to allow Alexandr 
to recover the Prince’s heart and stage the devastation 
of the laboratory without inconvenient witnesses.
Konstantin Svoboda the Alchemist
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am one of the most prominent alchemists in 
Prague. My brilliant experiments could easily backfire on me. 
Agenda: Create a Golem thanks to my alchemical studies 
and prove that no noble title or institutional position can 
possibly compare to my alchemical knowledge. 
Gift – The Secrets of Metal:  Konstantin knows arcane 
words that allow him to control metal. Any action against 
him involving a metal weapon or bludgeoning tool receives 
1 Penalty. Starting from moderate amounts of raw materials, 
Konstantin can also forge various metal objects with 
unusual properties. The Storyteller must create a Descriptor 
that outlines this property (for example: The alloy of this 
dagger violently repels other metals). 
Gift – The Secrets of Flesh:  Once per Scene, when a 
metallic body is in the vicinity of warm flesh, whether it’s still 
attached to the body or only recently removed, Konstantin 
can utter some arcane words, forcing the two elements to 
merge. The procedure causes 1 immediate Wound, and 1 
additional Wound during the painful medical procedure 
necessary to untangle flesh and metal.

167
The Rookery
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As you enter the rookery, 
you realize that you are being spied on: tired and hostile faces 
observe you from half-open balconies; mothers hurriedly pick 
up the children playing in the street and the men spit on the 
ground when they pass you by. On several street corners, you 
notice men and women in brightly colored but worn clothes, 
looking hungry and cold, asking for alms while standing on 
their heads, juggling, or performing other little tricks.
Descriptor: A maze of old houses with faded walls. Weary 
and hostile faces watch those who pass, while the poor and 
hungry ask for alms along the streets.
Information: Depending on their ability to ask questions 
and examine clues, Hunters may discover some or all of the 
following information: 
 ◆ The general sentiment of the population with regard 
to the recent murders, rumor of which has spread, is of 
ill-concealed approval. The blank, unconcerned gazes 
of any commoners interviewed makes it clear they 
think the nobles deserved it. 
 ◆ This is a good point of contact between the Hunters 
and some artists of the Circus of the Azure Sun. See 
the section Cibulka Tavern for available information. 
 ◆ If they visit Georg, the characters find him drunk in his 
house, and very aggressive. If subdued or persuaded to 
speak, Georg will say that Konstantin is at his wits’ end 
as the project he is leading on behalf of the city is not 
going well. 
 ◆ The attic where Josef Slezák lived (see page 169) 
burned down two days earlier. By convincing a couple 
of soldiers to grant them access, the Hunters will 
discover that the writer seems to have bought a huge 
amount of wood, with which he feverishly filled the 
stove until it exploded. The embers set fire to the rest 
of the attic, devouring the roof of the house and Josef 
himself. 
 ◆ If the Hunters are looking for him, Černobog (see 
page 172) is not at home, as he is now permanently 
stationed at the Circus of the Azure Sun. By breaking 
in, however, they will be able to access his rooms. 
There, the boy still keeps his mother’s sewing tools 
and the notebook where she noted expenses and 
orders, including that for Danek Brzobohatý (one of 
the victims, details on page 168). On the front of the 
notebook, there is the name of the woman: Hedvika 
Potměšil.
Georg Slanina the Helper 
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I work very hard. I like to drink, and when I 
do, I become a beast. 
Agenda: Supporting my daughter, who now has to sell 
matches on the street / Understanding why Konstantin fired 
me.

168
Cibulka Tavern
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Cibulka’s entrance is 
blackened by dirt, soot, and the effects of bad weather. You 
cross a stone arch and, pushing a heavy wooden door, you find 
yourself in a tavern built under sturdy red brick arches, with 
solid wood tables and stools. Despite the slightly suffocating 
atmosphere caused by the smoke from the kitchens and the 
faint light from the torches, the climate seems jovial. The 
smell of meat and beer mixes with the sweat of the patrons 
and the smell of urine in the corners. Cibulka is a place 
where workers and merchants mix, without paying too much 
attention to the social class. You make your way through the 
noise, looking for valuable information.      
Descriptor: A dark tavern, built under massive, red brick 
arches. The smell of meat and beer mixes with the smell of 
sweat and urine.
Information: Depending on their ability to ask questions 
and examine clues, Hunters may discover some or all of the 
following information:
 ◆ Several patrons can confirm that on the evening of the 
intrusion Konstantin was in Cibulka until late. This is 
rare, as he usually leaves the place in the late afternoon 
to devote himself to his lab work during the night. 
 ◆ At a table, you can find several members of the Circus 
of the Azure Sun, which had been put up in a field 
near the walls of Prague. They seem disconsolate and 
complain about the lack of work and prospects, even if 
they continue to order food and drinks. If questioned, 
they will reveal that the circus was purchased roughly a 
week earlier with a single ruby. The owner of the Circus 
of the Azure Sun sold everything, tents, and animals, 
without thinking twice. Most of the performers were 
fired shortly after by the new owner, a strange boy 
named Černobog Potměšil. 
 ◆ Some claim to have seen Konstantin and Bartoloměj 
engaged in a heated discussion at one of the tables a 
few nights ago. 
The Czernin Family Palace
Read aloud to the Hunters: As you climb the stairs that 
line the large fountain in front of the baroque palace of the 
Czernin, you can only wonder how many rooms it contains 
beneath its massive roof. “Here you can see the damage from 
cannonfire during the siege, ” says a laconic servant as he 
guides you through the majestic halls that lead to the room 
where Danek’s body was found. When you arrive, Beáta 
welcomes you, and apologizes for the absence of her husband, 
who is apparently “indisposed. ” The room has not been 
touched: on the canopy bed lies the rotting corpse of Danek 
Brzobohatý, conspicuously lacking an arm. The man’s body 
has cuts and abrasions all over his torso. He is dressed in an 
elegant woman’s dress, now completely ruined.    
Descriptor: A large castle in the Baroque style, which still 
bears the marks of the siege of Prague.
Information: Depending on their ability to ask questions 
and examine clues, Hunters may discover some or all of the 
following information:
 ◆ Beáta admits that the dress was a gift for her which, 
apparently, Danek had ordered a few weeks earlier. She 
would have worn it to the upcoming ball organized by 
the regent at Prague Castle. 
 ◆ On the dress, if examined carefully, the initials H.P . 
(Hedvika Potměšil, mother of Černobog) can be found.

169
Beáta doesn’t know exactly how Danek got his dress, but 
she suggests he probably used a common seamstress, 
since in her opinion the dress is not even that beautiful. 
 ◆ If the body is examined carefully, it will be clear that the 
cuts and abrasions were caused by a whip, while the arm 
was removed with a hacksaw. With the right knowledge, 
it will be possible to discover that the whip is similar to 
those used to tame animals, while the hacksaw is of high 
quality, as might be in the possession of certain men of 
science. 
The Theater
Read aloud to the Hunters: You’re led inside the impressive 
theater that stands out in front of the waters of the Vltava 
river. A warm light is reflected on the golden mezzanines, and 
beautiful red drapes lend the auditorium an extraordinary 
elegance. This place is only for those who can afford it. Although 
the murder happened several hours ago, nothing has been 
touched while awaiting the Order’s investigation. The body of 
the theater director Gabriel Černý is on Cleopatra’s throne, 
missing a leg. The puddle of now-dried blood has stained the 
stage. Bartoloměj looks at it with concern and declares: “We will 
get back on our feet, and we will bring the audience back to the 
theater even after this horrible tragedy. ”   
Descriptor: A large theater richly furnished, reserved only for 
the wealthiest in Prague.
Information: Depending on their ability 
to ask questions and examine clues, 
Hunters may discover some or all of the 
following information: 
 ◆ The corpse is missing a leg, which 
has been severed cleanly, but 
the man appears to have died of 
strangulation. An analysis might 
suggest that a large reptile, such as 
a Boa constrictor, was used. Such 
an animal is incredibly exotic in 
Prague (it comes, in fact, from the 
Circus of the Azure Sun). 
 ◆ At the time of the murder, all 
the staff, including the actors, had 
returned home. Since the script had 
arrived late, Gabriel was finalizing the 
last details, something he often did.
 ◆ Bartoloměj had a deep disagreement with 
the director, especially about the opening show: 
a reinterpretation of Anthony and Cleopatra by 
Shakespeare, which Gabriel Černý had commissioned 
from “a hack of a writer” named Josef Slezák. Bartoloměj 
had repeatedly advocated instead for a focus “on the 
masterpieces of various Bohemian artists. ”
 ◆ Some janitors say that Konstantin and Bartoloměj have 
met a couple of times lately to go drinking together, in a 
place called Cibulka. 
 ◆ To be revealed only after the visit to the alchemical 
laboratory and after some pressuring: on the night of the 
murder, Bartoloměj agreed to leave a side entrance to 
the theater open, at Konstantin’s request. The alchemist 
argued that he could “resolve the problem. ” Unless the 
Hunters have Konstantin with them, the alchemist is 
already hiding at the Circus of the Azure Sun.

170
Bartolomej Novotný the Assistant 
Director of the Theater
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young and ambitious assistant of 
Gabriel Černý. Our differences were known. 
Agenda: Make sure I am not blamed for the murder / 
Make sure it doesn’t turn out that I facilitated the murder. 
The Circus of the Azure Sun
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Just outside the walls of 
Prague, a battered blue and white tent stands in a muddy 
field. Some smaller tents are surrounded by wooden wagons, 
while a secondary tent connected to the larger one acts as 
a shelter from the weather for animals. There is a strange 
silence, as if the field had been abandoned, except for a 
subdued metallic sound coming from the animal tent.
Descriptor: A dilapidated tent in the middle of a muddy 
field. Wooden carriages and abandoned animal cages.
The camp is not actually abandoned, and at several hours 
of the day it’s possible to see a young boy hanging around 
the tents (Černobog). Konstantin spends every free 
moment in the animal tent, where he is assembling the 
Prince, while the main tent is mostly empty, except for the 
presence of a huge nest made up of brushes and mud built 
a few meters from the ground, and accessible only through 
the stairs or the ropes of the acrobats. 
Information: Entering the Circus of the Azure Sun 
without being seen by Černobog is difficult but, once 
inside, Hunters can access some or all of the following 
information:
 ◆ There is a large basin containing human remains, 
still wearing some uniforms of the Circus of the 
Azure Sun. These are the artists who did not leave the 
circus after the acquisition of Černobog, now simple 
spare parts for the Prince. It will be possible to find a 
hacksaw nearby. 
 ◆ On a desk you can find an invitation to the dance at 
Prague Castle, under the name Konstantin Svoboda. 
On another sheet there seems to be a rudimentary 
map of the city, with a path traced (the one Konstantin 
plans to use to get the Prince to the Castle). 
 ◆ The nest, suspended in the center of the tent, is made 
up of mud and branches and is extremely similar to 
that of a swallow. It’s big enough to accommodate a 
human being. This is where Alexandr rests when he’s 
not out murdering someone. 
 ◆ Approaching the circus at night, the Hunters will be 
able to see strange lights coming from the animal tent: 
Konstantin is in fact finalizing the preparations before 
activating the Prince.
Refer to the Events section of the Scenario to better manage 
the situation. 
^

171
The Prince
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC 
(6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a Golem of blood 
and flesh. There is only hatred in my 
broken heart. 
Agenda: Killing and tearing apart the ungrateful 
people of Prague / I prefer to take my revenge on the 
rich and powerful above all others. 
Gift – Broken Heart:  Once per Scene, the Prince can 
emit a freezing scream of pain that affects all living beings 
in the area, causing 1 Penalty to any action against him. 
Gift – Tornado of Blades:  The Prince’s body is 
composed of arms, legs, and heads held together by a 
metal structure. Any limb that can hold a blade 
does. The Prince can start spinning, hitting 
each opponent on his way in a single 
attack, inflicting 1 Wound on anyone 
who fails a Defense Check.

172
Aleksandr Pták
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am divided between who I was before and 
the spirit who took possession of my body. I have the urge to 
kill, but since my mind is clouded, I don’t always know why.
Swallow’s Agenda: Finding an ally in the people I helped 
in my previous life / Restoring the Prince, my only true love / 
Avenging wrongs suffered. 
Alexandr’s Agenda: Finding peace and freeing myself 
from the spirit that possesses me. 
Gift – Swallow’s Flight:  Aleksandr can easily climb 
anywhere and perform inhuman leaps. As he does, his OL 
increases by 1. If he hits someone plunging down from 
above, he can automatically escape the enemy’s threat range 
with a long jump. 
Gift – What is Yours is Mine:  As an additional action, 
Aleksandr can steal any object in the hands of another 
character with extreme ease. A Hunter can avoid the theft  
by succeeding in a Defense Check.          
 
Cernobog Potmešil The Child
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: The rubies I received allowed me to recover 
from the fever that had plagued me and, thanks to them, I 
am now strong and healthy, but at what price? I feel anger 
and resentment towards those who live on the shoulders of 
the poor people of Prague. 
Agenda: Avenge my struggling mother / Make sure the rich 
pay for their crimes.
Gift - The Ruby of Hate:  Černobog uses one of the two 
rubies he obtained from the Swallow as bait to achieve 
his goals. When he distracts someone with the ruby, he 
prevents the use of a Gift for the duration of the Scene. If 
the ruby is crushed, Černobog suffers 1 Wound.
Prague Castle
Read aloud to the Hunters: On the evening of the party, 
you are greeted in the majestic ballroom of Prague Castle. 
A cascade of crystal chandeliers illuminates white arches 
finished with gold, while large mirrors magnify the already 
remarkable size of the room, full of people dressed elegantly. 
You hear your name announced aloud, and everyone seems 
intrigued by your presence. Ostromir introduces you to the 
various families of the city, but despite the superficially happy 
atmosphere, the tension is palpable. 
Descriptor: The great and magnificent Prague Castle.
When the ball is at its busiest, a wall will be violently 
destroyed by a large creature of flesh and metal crashing 
^^

173
in: the Prince. Behind him will follow Konstantin, drunk 
with power, cursing Ostromir and those who lacked faith in 
him. Konstantin will try to control the Prince, as much as 
possible, directing the creature first towards Ostromir and 
then against the others. 
Refer to the Events of the Scenario for other potential 
situations at Prague Castle. 
In the event that all threats have already been eradicated, 
you can use the dance as an epilogue, changing the vibe: 
the aristocracy can go back to sleeping peacefully, even if 
the ordinary people of Prague see this return to normality 
as an additional sign of contempt that sooner or later will 
result in open rebellion.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
The second night since the beginning of the 
investigation in Prague:  there is rumor of another 
murder of a prominent citizen (the Captain of the soldiers, 
known for harassing the population). The details are left to 
the Storyteller, but this is a good time to have the Hunters 
near the crime scene and stage a chase with Alexandr on 
the rooftops of the city. 
The dance in Prague Castle will be held the third 
night after the arrival of the Hunters:  unless 
Konstantin’s plan is stopped. The Hunters will be invited as 
guests of honor by Ostromir himself.  
If Konstantin is discovered or killed:  Konstantin 
will try to turn the Prince against the Hunters. Lower the 
Prince’s OL by 1 if Alexandr’s only murders are those of 
Danek and Gabriel. If Konstantin is killed before activating 
the Prince, the Hunters will only have to worry about 
Alexandr and Černobog. The ball at Prague Castle can also 
be used as a trap to attract remaining threats. 
In case the Prince is eradicated, but not the others: 
Alexandr and Černobog will attempt a desperate move to 
infiltrate Prague Castle during the ball to kill Ostromir and 
every other possible aristocrat. Konstantin, if alive, will flee 
the city knowing he has lost everything. 
If the Hunters prowl around Prague without 
concealing their identity:  the less affluent classes 
will be generally hostile towards the Hunters, especially 
since word has spread that they are working for the hated 
Ostromir Malý. More than a few thugs will be happy to 
block their way, accusing them of protecting the powerful. 
Even some soldiers, knowing their Captain will protect 
them from repercussions, could hinder the Hunters or turn 
violent against the population in the area of the rookery.

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a skilled physician and psychiatrist, and 
diagnosing illnesses is simple. My work has made me 
particularly cold and detached.
• Physician: Once per Scenario, when you take the 
time to treat an injury, you may heal 1 Wound suffered 
by one of your patients, also bringing them back from 
apparent death or unconsciousness. Y ou may spend 1 
Soma to reassure and calm someone who is frightened or 
experiencing any negative feeling. If you do, you receive 
1 Additional Success to any Check involving interactions 
with that patient for the remaining of the Scenario. 
Cheshire, my faithful trained lynx, is the most 
trustworthy of companions – a shadow keeping 
me safe from threats. Cheshire’s attachment is an 
obsession and she may even threaten those who don’t 
want to hurt me.
• Cheshire the Lynx: Cheshire is an NPC fully 
under your control, with the following profile:
OL Medium 5 – (2 Wounds) 
Descriptor: I am a big, short-tempered wild lynx and I only 
live to serve Alice.
Gift - Two Souls: Y ou share your soul with Cheshire, and 
you can see and feel through her senses. Y ou can spend 
1 Soma to make Cheshire immaterial for an action and 
allow her to cross any obstacle.
I am a young doctor who has dedicated her whole life to the treatment of mental illnesses. I understand 
them, for I am suffering from one as well, and only those who burned themselves while playing with 
fire can understand its dangers. After a dark period during which I managed a sanatorium, the Order 
stripped me of all academic honors and degrees, reducing me to the status of a criminal. But it didn’t 
take long for them to understand the extent of my studies and the magnitude of my abilities. After 
possessing the Pope himself, I struck a deal with the Order. They will allow me to continue my studies, 
but I may only study the creatures they fight. And perhaps, through the distorted minds of the Broken 
Tales, I can prove that the worlds I visited as a child are real and not the ramblings of a mad mind.
Alice of
Other Worlds
As a child I traveled to other worlds and my mind can perceive and see 
things beyond common perception. Memories of my childhood haunt me, 
and I often get lost in visions of the past.
• Dark Ego - Alice of Other Worlds: As an action, you may spend 
1 Soma to try to possess the body of a person or animal that is looking into 
your eyes. Particularly powerful individuals may require a Position Check for 
you to succeed. Y ou can act through the body you possess and continue to use 
your Gifts and Descriptors, but you receive an additional Descriptor created 
by the Storyteller and representing the abilities of the body you are possessing. 
The possession ends at the end of the Scene, when the possessed body suffers 1 
Wound, or you decide to return to your body of your own volition. While you 
control someone else’s body, your original body is inert and at the mercy of the 
world. Y ou cannot possess Broken Tales or supernatural creatures. Y our mind is 
not easy to control, so you gain 1 Additional Success for Defense Checks against 
any kind of mind control and magic. 
Activator: You notice a detail that does not match what you consider normal.

Descriptors and Gifts
Dionysus follows me and protects me like a faithful beast, 
and responds to all my commands. As useful as this is, I feel 
disgusted every time he comes near me.
• The Beast: Dionysus is an NPC fully under your control, 
with the following profile:
OL Medium 5 – (Wounds: as Bella’s current value) 
Descriptor: I am a powerful and feral beastman. Only Bella’s scent 
keeps me under control.
Gift - Monstrous:  If he intends to kill someone, Dionysus may 
act twice in each Exchange. Once per Scene, Dionysus can break 
or lift even a large object with extreme ease. 
During Scenes, you decide how Dionysus acts. Any Wound one 
of you suffers also applies to the other. When Dionysus is within 
a few steps, you receive 1 Penalty to each Check due to the strong 
disgust you feel for him.
I am charismatic and affable. My innate air of superiority 
fascinates simple people. I love having all my eyes on me.
• The Beauty: Once per Scene, when you meet a new subject, 
you may spend 1 Soma and declare that you have met them in the 
past and that you made some kind of deal with them. Create, in 
agreement with the Storyteller, an Agenda for them representing 
the deal. If you take the time to talk to someone, the Storyteller 
must reveal a clue about their current Agenda or a drawback of 
one of their Descriptors. When someone shows a strong interest 
in you, Dionysus, if present, loses the use of the Monstrous Gift  
because of the strong jealousy and shame he feels for himself.
I am Bellina Cantalupo, but everyone calls me Bella. I was once a servant to the bride of the monstrous Dionysus 
Gonsalva. I played my cards well, charmed the nobleman, and rose to power. Unfortunately, the Order got in the 
way and sabotaged everything I had built. As a punishment, I was magically bound to Dionysus and now our 
souls are intertwined. I feel his pain and his beastly instincts. Dionysus perceives, in turn, my disgust for him, 
but it doesn’t affect the mad love he feels for me. I serve the Order in the hope of gaining its grace and being 
released from this punishment. But, deep down in my heart, I still long for the power that I was denied being born 
a peasant, but that I know I deserve.
I am linked to Dionysus and we share pain and perceptions. He will 
always know where I am and vice versa. For me, that’s the most 
humiliating part of the curse.
• Dark Ego - Beasts in Body and Soul: When you reveal your 
true nature everyone is horrified. One is a beast in body, while the other 
is a beast in her soul. Y ou receive 3 Savagery Tokens that you can spend 
during the Scene to: 
1. Inflict 1 additional Wound, if Dionysus spends an action to help you in 
the attack. The Wound will be a strikingly bloody gash.
2. Have a minor NPC lose their action as they are intimidated, having 
witnessed the fury of Dionysus.
3. If Dionysus is not present in the Scene, he can appear immediately 
(possibly tearing a new opening in the environment, demolishing any 
possible obstacle) to support you. 
4. Perform a second action in the Exchange, if Dionysus spends one of his 
to open the way for you.
Activator: Unleash the feral power of Dionysus, or humiliate or betray 
someone, revealing your true nature.

dresses and jewelry / A rose that never fades / A bag 
with dried meat with which to keep Dionysus’ hunger at 
bay / Soaps and feminine perfumes / A poisoned stiletto 
that I keep hidden for any eventuality.

Starting at my birth, I have been called strange, an abomination or, at best, a mutant. And indeed, 
my body is unlike any other and I possess abilities that my beloved parents have always called 
“special”. All the inhabitants of Lohr, the city where I lived, have always shown me kindness,  so 
I grew up driven by a great desire to help others. My family has gone to great lengths to protect 
me, even resorting to crime to keep me safe. The arrival of the Order upset everything and, after 
being captured, I was offered a deal which I couldn’t refuse. By entering the service of the Order, 
I spared my family any trial and all their crimes are forgiven. After a lifetime of being protected, 
it is my turn to protect them.

Carlo
the Living
Puppet

I am a puppet who has been animated by a magic ritual and though I remember a life, it is a lie. The spell gave me 
memories of a childhood and feelings for the one I once called “father,” but none of it was true. Now, my only wish is 
to become a real child. That desire has led me to trust dishonest people and commit crimes of which I was not even 
aware. Everyone has always deceived me and promised things they couldn’t give me, starting with my father. But 
the Order has forgiven me for my misdeeds, standing in the role of a new father figure: honest, straightforward, and 
understanding. If I do everything I am told, I will be rewarded. I will finally become a real child… or maybe I lie to 
myself as easily as everyone else?
I am an animated puppet and my body is made of wood. I don’t feel 
pain, nor do I fear most things that would kill a real person. Only fire 
scares me because it’s the only thing that could really end my life.
• Dark Ego - Soulless Puppet: Y our body is made of wood and 
so you ignore any penalty derived from dangers that would affect a living 
being. Y ou receive 1 Additional Success to all your Defense Checks. Y ou

at the Storyteller’s discretion. If you stay still, you will pass as a common 
puppet in the eyes of those who see you and you cannot be discovered 
except with special skills or magic.

an action, or have your nose grow dramatically (i.e. when you accumulate 
the third Lie Token), proving to everyone that you are not human.

Descriptors and Gifts
My life is animated by the magic of lies, and for this reason 
I am skilled at recognizing and using both falsehoods and 
magic. Even if admitting it is hurtful, my own life is a lie and 
I move only because I pretend to be a real child.
• Lies: When you tell a lie, you can temporarily make it a 
reality. Create a Descriptor that represents your lie and concerns 
something about you that you pride yourself on, such as: I have the 
strength of five men, or I can fly through the air. The Descriptor 
remains throughout the Scene, and you gain 1 Additional Success 
the first time you use it for an action. Each time you make use of this 
Gift you receive 1 Lie Token. Y our nose will grow in proportion 
to the amount of Lie Tokens you have. During the Scenario the 
Storyteller can spend 1 Lie Token to give you 1 Penalty on a Check, 
describing how your clumsy puppet body prevents you from 
performing at your best. With every Lie Token spent, your nose 
shrinks closer back to normal.
I want to prove that I am a good boy, and that is why I strive 
to perform every task that is assigned to me. I naively tend to 
trust adults blindly, even if I just met them.
• Always in the Middle of Trouble: Once per Scene, 
when you face a danger or problem arising from a previous choice
