supervision. Blanca walks through the crowd, approaches the 
children, and asks them if they want a mother. To the first one 
who answers yes, Blanca gives a kiss on the forehead, marking 
them as the intended victim for the evening. 
Afternoon: Square activity is at its peak. By spending 
time searching for information, Hunters can learn a rumor 
(page 98).
Evening: Blanca returns to the square to reunite with the 
child she branded, taking advantage of the quiet of the eve-
ning to take them to the Segura Riverbed.
Madre Blanca de las 
Lágrimas 
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a spirit without peace and I wander the city of Mur-
cia in search of my beloved children. My skin is cold and I feel no pain. 
The lullaby I used to sing to my children is the only thing that gives me 
a moment of peace. 
Agenda: I will bring seven children into the water. They will become 
my children.
Gift - Spirit of Tears: Blanca’s body does not follow the laws of life 
and death, and until her heart is at peace, she cannot be permanent-
ly eliminated. When Blanca’s Wounds are all marked, she crumbles 
to dust and her spirit returns to Blanca’s House, summoned by the 
Mother’s Heart (page 107). At the beginning of the next Scene, Blan-
ca heals all her Wounds. She starts each Scene as Intangible . If she 
acts to harm a living subject (inflicting 1 Wound, grabbing them, and 
similar actions), she becomes Tangible for the rest of the Scene. Once 
per Scene, Blanca can choose to become Intangible again.

103
Intangible: Blanca can move through solid objects and 
living creatures. When she passes through a living being, 
she forces the target to make a Defense Check. If the target 
fails, they will burst into tears for 1 minute and be unable to 
act. While crying, the target will see one of Blanca’s memo-
ries (see Gift - A Mother’s Love).
Tangible: Blanca can interact with the physical world like 
a normal creature. Once per Scene, Blanca can cry and all 
who hear her cry will suffer the following effect:
 ◆ Minor NPC: Must run from Blanca as fast as possible 
until they can no longer hear her cries. If unable to do 
so, they will faint.
 ◆ Main NPC/Dark Presence: Reduces their OL by one 
while in Blanca’s presence. If they already have OL Easy 
3, they suffer the effect of Minor NPCs.
 ◆ Hunter: Loses 2 Soma. If they cannot pay this cost, 
they suffer 1 Wound. 
Gift - A Mother’s Love: Blanca can only be permanent-
ly eliminated by confronting her with her terrible deeds, 
by awakening the love she feels for her son Pepe, and by 
making her realize that the young man has created a new 
family for himself thanks to the Bats. Alternatively, it is 
necessary to destroy the Mother’s Heart in Blanca’s House.  
If Blanca is Intangible and passes through a living being, 
roll a die or choose which memory is shared:
1: Blanca embraces two newborns. They are twins and she 
looks at them with love. She calls them by their names: Abel 
and Annita.
2: Blanca prepares hot soup and gives a bowl to a child 
huddled in the cold under a large blanket. She approached 
him and said softly, “Cristóbal, the soup is ready. ”
3: Blanca returns home with a book in her arms. As soon as 
she opens the door, a little girl hangs around her neck. “Did 
you get it, Mommy?” she asks impatiently. “Of course, my 
little Lola. Here is your new book. ” The little girl’s eyes light 
up as she takes the book. 
4: Blanca finishes sewing an old shoe while a barefoot child 
stares at her. “I’m sorry, mama, if I broke the shoe, ” the child 
says in a soft voice. Blanca smiles and strokes her son’s head, 
“Don’t worry, Ricardo, everything will be fine. ” 
5: Blanca teaches a young girl the art of sewing. The young 
girl tries hard but ends up pricking her finger. She bursts 
into tears, but Blanca takes her injured finger and kisses it. 
“Don’t cry, Julia, ” she tells her lovingly. “The pain will pass 
before you know it, but the knowledge that you felt it will 
make you stronger. ”
6: Blanca is running down the street, holding the hand of 
a little boy who is jumping up and down, when suddenly 
the little boy stumbles and falls, injuring his knee. The child 
bursts into tears, so Blanca kisses him on the forehead and 
embraces him. “It’s nothing, little Pepe, ” she reassures him.
Gift - My Beloved Children:  Once per Scene, Blanca 
can summon 6 Lost Children (page 105) to appear at her 
side.

104
The Clock Tower
Descriptor: A crumbling tower with a large clock that no 
longer works. The building is part of the Church of San Felipe, 
now abandoned and devastated by the Segura flood.
Morning: The Gang of Bats is out to steal, leaving only 
Benedicto and Inez to guard the cave.    
Afternoon: The Gang is in the Clock Tower except for Ru-
fio and Soledad who are at the Old Mill Tavern.
Evening: The entire Gang is present in the den. 
Pepe
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the leader of the Bats and responsible for the sur -
vival of my companions. I am brave and skilled with knives, and I like 
to whistle my mother’s lullaby, a happy memory from my childhood.
Agenda: Robbing the people of Murcia as revenge for their indiffer -
ence to those who have nothing / Taking care of the Bats, whom I con-
sider my new family.    
Gift - Big Brother:  Pepe will not allow anyone to harm his com-
rades. Against a target that threatens a Bat, Pepe inflicts 1 Additional 
Wound. All Bats increase their OL by 1 when Pepe is present. 
The Gang of Bats
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are street urchins, used to stealing to survive. 
With Pepe at our head, we are not afraid of anything.      
Agenda: Help Pepe make the Gang prosper / Help Pepe overcome 
the trauma of his family’s death. 
Gift - United Gang: Each member of the Gang possesses a trait 
that is identified by an addition to their Descriptor. 
Benedicto: I am the oldest in the Gang, and my strength is feared 
even by adults.
Carmina: I am agile, fast, and a good climber. 
Felix: I am the smallest of the Gang and can squeeze into any hole. 
Inez: I am the smartest of the Gang and have read many books.

105
Rufio: I am a big talker and know everyone in town. 
Soledad: I am a good dancer and know how to draw atten-
tion to myself. 
The Segura Riverbed
Descriptor: The river that runs through all of Murcia 
brings many job opportunities to the poor, but also the threat 
of its murky, deep waters.    
Morning: The River is crossed by many boats, and plenty 
of people work along its riverbed. By spending time talking 
to the townspeople, Hunters can learn a rumor (page 98). 
Afternoon: Blanca wanders the Segura River, waiting for 
nightfall to return to the child she branded at the Martyr’s 
Plaza. When the spirit is cornered, it throws itself into the 
waters of the River to disappear. 
Evening: Staying too long near the water of the River will 
attract 6 Lost Children. 
Lost Children
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are restless spirits spawned by Blanca’s 
madness. We hate living things and try to take them with us 
to the waters of the Segura River to drown them, just as we 
were. 
Agenda: Choose a target and drag it to the Segura River to 
drown it.    
Gift - Suffering Spirit: As an action, a Lost Child will 
always attempt to snatch a target. In the event of a Defense 
Check Failure, apply the result based on where the action 
takes place.
Within 100 feet of the Segura River:  The Lost 
Child jumps into the river, taking the target with it. 
The Lost Child suffers 1 Wound while the target is in 
the depths of the River, see Threat - The Segura River 
on page 106.
Anywhere else: The Lost Child envelops the target

106
and turns into river water, suffering 1 Wound. The tar
-get gains 3 Drowning Tokens. The Storyteller gains 3 
Drowning Tokens, which can be spent each time the 
Storyteller wishes to apply one of the following effects 
to the target. The same Lost Child cannot generate 
more than 3 Drowning Tokens to be spent on a single 
target.
1. Confer 1 Penalty on a Check.
2. Automatically drop the target into the Segura 
River if the action takes place near its banks.
3. Force the target to pay 1 Soma to activate one of 
their Gifts, with the exception of the Dark Ego. 
Threat - The Segura River
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Strong currents and icy waters make the Segu-
ra River dangerous even for experienced swimmers. 
The Monastery
Descriptor: An old monastery that once housed a group 
of monks. Now only a few rooms and the large courtyard are 
usable.
Morning: From the Monastery garden comes the cackle 
of children playing. With them is Sister Esperanza, a nun 
who helps Father Neron keep the little marked ones safe 
from Blanca’s sight. There are a total of six children in the 
Monastery (all reported missing from the River Market). 
When questioned, Sister Esperanza will not hesitate to 
stand up for Father Neron. The nun will say that the priest 
wanders around the Market in the morning, while in the af-
ternoon he worries about finding the children’s families to 
tell them that their little ones are safe under the protection 
of the Church. Father Neron makes donations to the chil-
dren’s families to ensure their cooperation, with the prom-
ise of a reunion on the first day of December. 
Afternoon: Father Neron is present as he prepares dona-
tions for the families of the children who live in the Monas-
tery. Sister Esperanza takes care of the children.
Evening: Father Neron is the small library of the Monas-
tery, studying books on exorcism. Sister Esperanza sleeps 
in the underground rooms with the children.
Sister Esperanza
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a young nun who has dedicated her life 
to helping others. I follow Father Neron in his crusade against 
evil and am willing to give my life to save the innocent.    
Agenda: Helping Father Neron and taking care of the chil-
dren in the Monastery.

107
Blanca’s House
Descriptor: A destroyed house that still bears the marks of 
the Segura River’s fury. In the wood of the door and its frame 
are religious symbols, roughly carved by the inhabitants in a 
superstitious attempt to keep evil away.    
Morning, Afternoon, and Evening: The House is un-
occupied, but a search of the interior will reveal the bare 
footprints of a woman who appears to be descending into 
the basement, which is impractical as it appears to be com-
pletely underwater. Diving in is extremely risky and acti-
vates the Dark Water Threat. The layer of water actually 
hides the Dungeon from prying eyes: once the Threat is 
overcome, the Dungeon containing the Mother’s Heart can 
be accessed. 
Threat - Dark Waters
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Frigid waters, animated by Blanca’s resent-
ment of all living things, form a barrier to keep the Mother’s 
Heart safe.
Gift - The Frigid Embrace of Death:  When a subject 
plunges into the Dark Waters, they are attacked by the 
water, which will try to enter their nose and mouth. If the 
subject fails the Defense Check, they suffer 1 Wound from 
the cold and lack of oxygen and become stuck in the water, 
unable to move. A successful Defense Check is required to 
get free and return to the surface or to descend into the 
Dungeon. Singing the lullaby that Pepe knows causes the 
waters to recede for one hour, allowing safe access. 
The Dungeon
Descriptor: A bare dungeon where the remains of six small 
bodies are huddled together. In the center of the room, a mass 
of plants, seabed debris, and animal bodies are formed into a 
heart that pulsates with malevolent energy.
Morning, Afternoon, and Evening:  The Mother’s 
Heart is the fulcrum that gives life to Blanca’s spirit and to 
destroy it is to put an end to Mother Blanca de las Lágrimas.
The Mother’s Heart
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: An agglomeration of floating garbage, pulsat-
ing like a rotting heart. 
Gift - A Mother’s Fury:  As soon as the Heart suffers 
1 Wound, Blanca will appear to defend it. The call of the 
Heart cannot be prevented in any way.

108
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Mother 
Heart Scenario and can be acquired once conditions are 
met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario Gift. If they 
buy a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Exorcism
Requirement: To be taught the rites of exorcism 
by Father Neron Lobo. 
Y ou are able to sense damned souls, and this 
allows you to see and interact with all spiritu-
al beings. The Storyteller will alert you when 
a spirit is present, a feeling that manifests as a 
chill down your spine. Y ou can spend 1 Soma 
to open your mind to the spirits. If you do, you 
can communicate with them and perform ritu-
als and prayers to harm them for the rest of the 
Scene.
City Guide
Requirement: Succeed at least twice against 
the Threat - City Chaos.   
Y ou have figured out how to navigate the alleys 
of the chaotic city of Murcia. When you move 
from one Place to another, you automatically 
get a Standard Outcome on Threat Checks for 
City Chaos.

109
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As Storyteller, feel free 
to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunt-
ers.
If Blanca sees Pepe or someone describes the 
boy to her:  she will recognize her lost son and become 
obsessed with him, and the spirit’s only goal will be to take 
Pepe to the basement to kill him.
If the Hunters arrive at the Clock Tower on the 
Morning of the second day:  they will find the gen-
darmes capturing Inez and Benedicto. 
If the Hunters arrive at the Clock Tower after the 
Afternoon on the second day: the gendarmes have cap-
tured Benedicto and Inez. Pepe’s only goal will be to free his 
comrades, but it will be obvious to the Hunters that the Bats 
are no match for the city guards. 
If the Hunters attempt to capture Father Neron:  
the priest will try to escape and seek refuge in the Monas-
tery. If captured, he will try to use his position to obtain his 
release. Only when he has no other option will he speak of 
his doubts about the Madre Blanca de las Lágrimas and the 
threat she poses to Murcia. 
If the Hunters confront Blanca in public:  panic will 
break out in the city and everyone will start talking about 
the existence of the Madre Blanca de las Lágrimas. From 
that moment on, the chaos in the city will increase and the 
Hunters will be slowed by citizens asking for their help. In-
crease the OL of the Threat - City Chaos by 1. 
Whether the Hunters describe Blanca to Pepe or 
the boy sees the spirit directly:  the young man will 
fall to his knees in tears, overwhelmed by memories. When 
he calms down, he will tell of the end of his family and how 
he was saved. Pepe will teach the Hunters the lullaby he 
always whistles, telling them that the tune is of great value 
to Blanca.

1
2a
110
A Mother’s Heart in Scenes 
FIRST SCENE – The Old Mill 
Tavern
It is the same one you find on page 99. Talking to Ma-
ria Rosa will reveal the information provided in that Scene 
to the Hunters, except for the information about the Bat 
Gang, the Clock Tower, and the location of Blanca’s House.  
Note: Since the Scene Tree does not provide the freedom 
of action of the normal Scenario, Hunters do not have to 
face the Threat - City Chaos every time they move from one 
area to another.  
End of Scene: The next morning the Hunters can investi-
gate the River Market, go to SECOND SCENE OPTION A 
- The River Market, or the Martyr’s Square, go to SECOND 
SCENE OPTION B - Martyr’s Square.
SECOND SCENE OPTION A  
– The River Market
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The market in the slums 
of Murcia is a crossroads of makeshift vendors and hawk-
ers who crowd the roadsides trying to attract the attention 
of passersby. Lost in the crowd is a group of children running 
barefoot behind the horse of a wealthy nobleman, haughtily 
passing between the stalls. Chaos reigns, and no one seems 
to be paying the slightest attention to anything but their own 
interests. 
Place: The chaotic River Market that stretches for more 
than a mile. 
Time: Early morning, while the people of Murcia are al-
ready in full swing. 
Information: Father Neron can easily be seen talking to 
some children, asking them if they have seen a pale, ghostly 
woman. After talking to the children, he will leave, followed 
by a child. If the Hunters approach, the priest will try to run 
away and, once caught, will try to use his position as a cleric 
as a shield. After several hesitations (and at least one suc-
cessful Position Check to convince Father Neron of their 
good intentions), the priest will tell the Hunters what he 
is doing to save the children of Murcia. Neron will say that 
one of the little ones at the market told him a strange story. 
It seems that the leader of the Bats often brags about sur -
viving the flood that hit Murcia seven years ago. This date 
corresponds to the first appearances of the Madre Blanca de 
las Lágrimas, and Neron wants to question the Bats to see if 
there is any connection. 
End of Scene: The Hunters accompany Father Neron to 
meet the Bats, go to SCENE THREE - The Clock Tower.

2b
111
SECOND SCENE OPTION B 
– Martyr’s Square
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You arrive at the larg-
est slum square in Murcia. Ragamuffins and the unemployed 
huddle together, waiting for the city’s gendarmes to bring 
some leftover food from the wealthy uptowners. Through the 
confusion and shouting, you catch a glimpse of a pale woman 
shuffling slowly through the crowd. Her eyes are swollen with 
tears, and she has the expression of someone who has seen 
death up close. A few children pass by, and the somber figure 
beckons them to her for a moment, a sad smile on her face.
Place: The large Martyr’s Square where all the city’s poor 
flock. 
Time: Early morning, the sun shines high in the sky but 
fails to dispel the November chill.
Information: If the Hunters simply observe Blanca, she 
will kiss a child on the forehead and then walk off into the 
crowd, after which the Hunters will see her vanish as if she 
were a ghost. On the other hand, if the Hunters approach 
Blanca, the ghost will attempt to escape, and once cornered, 
she will use her power to walk through the walls of houses 
and shake them off her trail. If the Hunters trap her, Blan-
ca will summon the Lost Children before disappearing. 
However the encounter with Blanca is resolved, the Hunt-
ers will be approached by the boy Blanca kissed: he is Felix, 
the youngest of the Gang of    Bats. The little boy will say 
that Pepe told him to stay away from a strange lady who 
looks like a ghost, but without realizing it, he ended up 
right in her presence. Now Felix is very afraid because the 
lips of the woman who kissed him were as cold as those of 
a dead person. Felix will gladly accept an offer of help from 
the Hunters. 
End of Scene: The Hunters follow Felix to meet Pepe, go 
to SCENE THREE - The Clock Tower.

3
4
112
THIRD SCENE – The Clock 
Tower
This Scene varies depending on who accompanies the 
Hunters to the Clock Tower.
If the Hunters arrive with Father Neron, read 
aloud to the Hunters: Father Neron leads you to a large, 
abandoned church and tells you that when the city was 
founded, this was the main place of worship. Murcia’s steady 
growth, however, soon relegated the church of San Felipe to a 
mere Clock Tower. When that stopped working, the mainte-
nance expense led the Curia to close the entire building. 
If the Hunters arrive with Felix, read aloud to the 
Hunters: Felix leads you to a large abandoned church and 
tells you that this is the headquarters of the Gang of Bats. 
Pepe is the undisputed leader of the gang, and it is because 
of him that everyone can live free from the strict rules of the 
town’s adults. 
Place: The Clock Tower of San Felipe’s Church. 
Time: In the afternoon, clouds have covered the sunshine 
and there is a whiff of rain. 
Information: Pepe will treat all visitors badly and scold 
Felix when he is with them. The young man seems very 
anxious and is unwilling to trust adults. If the Hunters are 
aggressive, the Gang of Bats will not hesitate to get physical, 
while a more diplomatic approach may lead to a brief 
discussion. At Blanca’s description, Pepe will fall into a crying 
fit and tell his sad story. The boy will then tell the Hunters that 
the first year after his family’s death, he returned to his old 
home, where Blanca’s ghost tried to pull him into the river. 
Pepe will then reveal the location of Blanca’s house.  
If the Hunters can handle the situation without getting 
physical, Pepe will teach them his mother’s lullaby, 
saying that when he encountered the ghost of his 
mother, the song saved him from being captured.  
At the end of the Scene, Father Neron (if not present) 
or Sister Esperanza will arrive to seek help. Blanca has 
appeared at the Monastery to kidnap the children that 
Father Neron was trying to protect. Pepe will be terrified 
to face his mother’s ghost and will ask the Hunters to do it 
for him. 
End of Scene: The Hunters rush to the Monastery to res-
cue the children from Blanca’s clutches, unaware that it is a 
trap, go to SCENE FOUR - The Monastery.
FOURTH SCENE – The Monastery
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As you rush to the gate of 
the Monastery, you can clearly hear the cries of the children 
inside. Y ou then rush into the courtyard, and before you are 
ghostly-faced children trying to drag others away. 
Place: The abandoned Monastery that houses the children 
protected by Father Neron. 
Time: The sun is setting. 
Information: There are 6 Lost Children trying to take the 
children from the Monastery. In reality, the ghosts are stall-
ing to distract Father Neron and the Hunters while Blanca 
goes to the Clock Tower to kidnap Pepe. The Lost Children 
fight to the last. At the end of the battle, Felix arrives, dazed, 
and tells them that Madre Blanca de las Lágrimas has ap-
peared and kidnapped Pepe.

5
113
End of Scene: The Hunters must run to Blanca’s House to 
stop the spirit before it kills Pepe, go to FIFTH SCENE - A 
Mother’s Heart.
FIFTH SCENE – A Mother’s 
Heart
Read aloud to the Hunters: Y ou find the broken door in 
the small house that was once the safe haven of Pepe’s fam-
ily. A faint cry comes from the basement, but it is not easy 
to get to, as the river water is quickly flowing into the house.
Place: Blanca’s House, which floods very quickly.
Time: The sun has now set, while a heavy rain has begun 
to fall on Murcia. 
Information: To access the Dungeon, it is necessary to 
go through the basement and face the Dark Water Threat 
(page 107), unless the Hunters have learned the lullaby 
from Pepe. In the Dungeon, the Hunters find Pepe kneeling 
in shock, while Blanca promises that he will soon be reunit-
ed with his beloved brothers. The Mother’s Heart throbs 
rottenly, punctuating the passage of time. 
End of Scene:  If the Hunters succeed in rescuing Pepe 
and destroying the Mother’s Heart, they will be able to give 
peace to Blanca’s damned soul.

114
Nikola’s Pledge
 
A Scenario inspired by the story of Santa Claus
 
Safety Keywords: Kidnapping / Gore / Manipulation
 
Once upon a time...
The Hunters arrive in the far north, in the village of Rovaniemi, summoned by a letter from a young 
couple, Tommi and Jutta, who have recently arrived there. As described in the letter, the community of 
prospectors who founded the village has always been supported by a wealthy craftsman named Nikola 
Lupei. After years of apparent tranquility, Nikola has begun asking for a pledge and donation in return 
for his patronage. Gold and valuables can be given as gifts, which each family must leave at their door on 
the night of December 24 as a token of gratitude. At the first request, however, some ignored the demand. 
The next morning, they each discovered that one of their loved ones was missing. 
No one believed it was a coincidence. The furious village elders went to Nikola to make him answer for 
his actions, only to return and declare that the craftsman was in no way connected to the disappearances. 
From that moment on, however, the villagers accepted the tribute had to be paid, and no one disappeared 
anymore. Tommi and Jutta ask the Order for help, because they fear for the life of little Lari, their eldest 
son, since December 24th is approaching and they have nothing to pay for their own tribute. The Hunters 
then arrive in the remote village of gold prospectors, on the edge of civilization, knowing that they are the 
only link between the law of the civilized world and the harsh rules of the cold north...

115
The story that led us here...
Nikola Lupei was born and raised in the comfort 
of a noble family in Walachia. Always in poor health, but 
extremely intelligent, Nikola devoted himself to the study 
of esoteric arts from an early age. After years of research, 
Nikola found a mysterious patron willing to support him 
and moved into the old man’s mansion to study his im-
mense collection of books. The old man later revealed 
himself to be an ancient vampire and passed his curse 
on to Nikola one bloody night. Rejuvenated in body but 
tired in mind, Nikola fled Wallachia in search of a safe 
place to live in peace and quiet. The Village of Rovaniemi 
turned out to be just right, far from civilization and with 
needy families looking for help to build their prospecting 
community. Using his powers, Nikola allowed the village 
to flourish. In his mansion, he built a workshop where 
he could devote himself to making toys, a great passion 
of his since childhood. Safe from prying eyes, Nikola has 
always kept his vampirism hidden, feeding on the many 
animals in the forest around Rovaniemi. Three years ago, 
however, things changed drastically: while on a quest 
for magical artifacts, Nikola came into possession of the 
Badalisk, a powerful bag-shaped creature. The Badalisk, 
cornering Nikola, forced the vampire to feed him pieces 
of his body. But animal blood is less powerful than hu-
man blood, so Nikola’s body could not regenerate quick-
ly enough to feed the Badalisk. Desperate, Nikola began 
to demand a pledge of valuables from the people of Ro-
vaniemi, knowing that many would find it difficult to pay. 
With that justification, Nikola used his powers to infil-
trate the homes of those unable to pay tribute, kidnapping 
a family member and making them his servants through 
the Rite of Servitude. His new servants helped Nikola kid-
nap poor travelers passing through. Thanks to the riches he 
accumulated from the tribute, he lured wealthy merchants 
seeking good deals to the village. All the innocent souls 
went to feed to the insatiable Badalisk...
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters...
As it feeds, the Badalisk becomes more powerful and its 
hunger grows month by month. Desperate, Nikola tries 
every possible way to satiate the demon and preserve the 
lives of the villagers of Rovaniemi. Every night, the vampire 
meets with Asmo, one of the Village Elders, who keeps him 
informed of events. Thanks to Asmo, Nikola learned of the 
arrival of the Order’s first Hunter and sent his reindeer to 
hunt them down. After a long battle, the Hunter has taken 
refuge in the old hunting lodge east of the Village and is 
waiting for reinforcements. Nikola plans to eliminate the 
Hunter himself, convinced that a subject of such power 
would sate the Badalisk for a long time.
Note: In this Scenario, a Hunter from the Order has al-
ready been sent out before the Players’ group arrives. The 
Storyteller is free to choose which Hunter this is, to best fit 
with their Campaign. The Hunter is effectively an NPC un-
der the Storyteller’s control. The choice of Hunter can affect 
the events of the Scenario and create interesting situations. 
For example, if it is the Immortal, the Hunters may find 
only his head in the hut, while the body is scattered in the 
forest, torn apart by the Reindeer. Retrieving the body parts 
would then become an interesting situation to play out.

116
Note: In this Scenario, the Badalisk has eaten a Treasure of 
the Order, providing it a source of great power. The Story-
teller must decide which Treasure it is, fitting it into their 
Campaign. If the Scenario is solved successfully, the Hunt-
ers will be able to retrieve it.  
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  The sun is setting as you 
finally see smoke billowing from the chimneys of the small 
town of Rovaniemi. After more than a week of traveling 
through impassable forests and snow-covered plains, you 
can’t wait to shed your heavy coats and warm yourself by a 
fire. Instead, you are greeted by locked doors and suspicious 
looks from behind windows. It seems that you are not wel-
come in the village. A voice attracts your attention, a young 
man beckons you to his house and when you approach he 
introduces himself as Tommi, the one who sent the letter to 
the Order with his wife Jutta. 
Place: Tommi and Jutta’s little house. 
Time: The sun is setting as the icy wind bangs on closed 
windows, announcing the arrival of another snowstorm.
Initial Situation: The hosts thank the Hunters for com-
ing to their rescue, while Tommi rekindles the fireplace, 
where a pot of tempting stew is simmering. A loving Jutta 
cradles a baby sleeping peacefully in her arms.
Information: From the interview with Tommi and Jutta, 
the Hunters can get a lot of information.
 ◆ Nikola Lupei is considered the most important man in 
the area. He lives in a large mansion a day’s walk from 
Rovaniemi and is known as a benefactor who has con-
tributed to the growth of the Village. Lupei is a very ec-
centric individual who always wears an eye-catching red 
robe with large slippers to match. He has an emaciated, 
pale face with the large dark circles of someone who 
never seems to sleep well. Despite his ghostly appear -
ance, he is a gentle man who is always kind to everyone.
 ◆ The Village is run by a group of six elders who meet in 
the small town hall during the day. The elders talked to 
Nikola after the first night of the pledge, but after the 
meeting they kept a mysterious silence, urging everyone 
to pay the pledge without fuss. In their opinion, Nikola 
had nothing to do with the disappearances.    
 ◆ After the first pledge, the Village always paid, even at 
great sacrifice. Coincidentally, no one else has disap-
peared since then.    
 ◆ A week ago, a Hunter from the Order arrived in the Vil-
lage. Tommi and Jutta met them briefly, but the next day 
the Hunter left for Nikola’s estate, never to return. Jutta 
overheard a discussion among the elders and is sure that 
the Hunter received information from one of them, but 
she could not say who. Tommi and Jutta can describe 
the Hunter in broad strokes, given their short meeting.
 ◆ Tommi and Jutta can describe all six elders, giving their 
names and the most common information from their 
Descriptors.
 ◆ Today is December 15th, so there are only nine days left 
until the next pledge payment.

117
Tommi and Jutta
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPCs (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are two young prospectors, made tough by 
a life of hardship and strong by the love that unites us.
Agenda: To save our son Lari from that absurd pledge.
The Village of Rovaniemi
Descriptor: A small town of prospectors where everyone 
knows everyone else.
Information: The streets and the small square are littered 
with gold panning equipment, carts, and baskets for sort-
ing. The numerous wrought-iron plaques remind everyone 
that these are all donations from Nikola Lupei. The villagers 
are shy towards the Hunters, avoiding any contact. 
Rovaniemi Town Hall
Descriptor: One of the few brick houses in the village. The 
imposing wrought iron door has a large plaque engraved with 
words of gratitude for the town’s benefactor, Nikola Lupei.
Whenever the Hunters visit the Town Hall, they will find 2 
Village Elders. The Storyteller can decide which ones they 
are, or roll a die to determine. The following information 
can be obtained from all elders:
 ◆ A week ago, a Hunter from the Order arrived, asked 
a few questions, then left for Nikola’s estate and was 
never seen again. 
 ◆ Nikola rarely comes to the Village now and seems to be 
very busy. The villagers never go to him spontaneously, 
but wait for him to show up.
It is possible to meet all the elders, but it takes time: for 
every hour the Hunters spend in the Village, they can meet 
one elder.  
The Village Elders  
1: Asmo
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the leader of the elders, and my word is 
final when there is disagreement.
Agenda: To serve Nikola by reporting everything that hap-
pens in the village / To keep outside attention away from Ro-
vaniemi and prevent Nikola from being forced to harm the 
villagers.

118
Information: Asmo sings Nikola’s praises, calling him 
the benefactor of Rovaniemi. Rumors that he is a kidnapper 
are false, and the people who are no longer in the Village 
are fine and have voluntarily moved to work in Nikola’s toy 
factory.
2: Elna
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a good weaver and know many legends 
of the area.
Agenda: I want my daughter Jaana to come home, but 
without openly siding against Nikola Lupei.
Information: Elna’s family is among those who did not 
initially pay the pledge, and her daughter Jaana has disap-
peared. The old woman will tell you that her daughter is 
well and has settled in Nikola’s mansion, where she works 
as a toy factory worker. When questioned about the night 
of the disappearance, Elna will say that no one noticed any-
thing and that there were no signs of forced entry. Talking 
to Elna, it will become clear that she misses her daughter 
very much. 
3: Mikko
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 
Wound)
Descriptor: I am an accountant who is in 
charge of the gold trade between the village 
and the merchants. 
Agenda: Make the village prosperous and 
earn good money. 
Information: Mikko is the newest addition 
to the group of elders and was not present during the first 
night of the pledge. He does not believe in rumors and sees 
the “kidnapped” people simply as workers who choose to 
stay in the comfort of Nikola’s warm and cozy mansion. 
However, Mikko noticed that some of the traders missed 
the appointment to load the gold and he has not heard 
from them. The old man has noted everything in his re-
cords, where it can be seen that the missed appointments 
and disappearances of the merchants occur on a regular ba-
sis. However, the old accountant is extremely jealous of his 
work and will not share the records with anyone. 
4: Katri
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a good hunter, but now my venerable age 
keeps me from hunting. I know the area around Rovaniemi 
very well.
Agenda: Defend the village of Rovaniemi from any possible 
threat from the wilderness.    
Information: Katri leads the hunters in the Village, and 
lately she has been told that the presence of animals has 
decreased dramatically. There are several hunting lodges 
in the forest around the Village, where one 
can find shelter. The area where Nikola Lu-
pei’s mansion was built is full of ravines and 
natural caves.    
5: Usko
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 
Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a prospector and direct the 
work of recovering metals from the big river 
east of Rovaniemi.

119
Agenda: Promote the community’s gold trade.
Information: Usko will tell the Hunters that most of the 
equipment the searchers use was donated by Nikola. Usko 
will speak of Nikola as a benefactor who has always worked 
for the good of the Village. As for the disappearances, Usko 
simply thinks that working in Nikola’s toy factory is much 
easier than panning for gold in the cold and frost of the big 
river. That is why several villagers have decided to move to 
the factory, which is next to Nikola Lupei’s mansion. Usko 
considers the missing inhabitants to be savvy layabouts 
who have found a way to live comfortably while everyone 
else labors. The toys Nikola produces are of the highest 
quality, and Nikola has built his fortune by selling them to 
wealthy families all over Europe. 
6: Soila
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a teacher and I am responsible for the 
education of the children in the village.        
Agenda: Keeping Rovaniemi families together. 
Information: Soila will say that Nikola’s behavior was al-
ways strange. For example, he always came to the village 
after sunset and never entered anyone’s house without a 
proper formal invitation. More than once, Soila has seen 
him lose his temper over the issue. But the Patron’s eccen-
tric behavior is nothing compared to all the good he has 
done for the Village. Soila knows all the families who live 
in the Village and is responsible for welcoming newcomers. 
She will tell you that Tommi and Jutta are two good peo-
ple, but too reserved to adapt to the rules of the communi-
ty. Soila has even offered to help them pay the pledge, but 
Tommi is too proud to go into debt.
The Forest around 
Rovaniemi
Descriptor: A dense forest, always covered by a thick blan-
ket of snow. It is easy to find tracks in the snow.
Information: It is possible to reach Nikola’s estate from 
the forest in a six-hour walk. Every three hours, the fol-
lowing Encounters are possible. The Storyteller can choose 
which or roll a die. 
1-2: The Tracks
3-4: Nikola’s Reindeer
5-6: Remains of the Merchant Wagon
The Tracks
The Hunters see the tracks of a single person who veered 
off the path and got lost in the forest. They were left by the 
first Hunter to arrive in Rovaniemi and lead to the Hunting 
Lodge. See page 120.

120
Nikola’s Reindeer
Reindeer enslaved to Nikola occupy the entire forest around 
the Village and the mansion, with the task of killing and 
dragging to the Badalisk’s lair anyone who is not a resident 
of Rovaniemi. A Reindeer herd consists of four members.
Remains of the Merchant Wagon
Not far from the main road, you can see the remains of a 
wagon, now completely covered in snow. If you examine 
it, you will find the remains of several mangled bodies and 
goods that no one has taken. This is a merchant shipment 
that never made it to Nikola’s estate. It will be easy to see that 
many of the bodies were dragged into the forest thicket to 
be taken somewhere. If the Hunters follow the tracks, they 
will come to the Badalisk’s Lair. See Threat - Lost Trails on 
page 121.  
The Hunting Lodge
Descriptor: A small hut, fortified as best it can be. A faint 
trail of smoke from the chimney indicates that someone has 
taken refuge inside.
Information: The Hunter who arrived a week ago was 
badly injured in a confrontation with Nikola’s Reindeer and 
is unable to escape. The Reindeer are patrolling the area, 
ready to strike. Indeed, shortly after the Hunters arrive, a 
herd of Reindeer attacks the cabin. The Hunter has suffered 
their last Wound and is unable to act, but at the Storytell-
er’s discretion, they may use one of their Gifts to help. The 
Hunter can report information about 2 of the Village El-
ders, chosen from those the Hunters have not already in-
terviewed. They will also say that Nikola himself showed up 
at the door the night before and asked to be invited in. The 
Hunter refused, fearing a trap. Nikola asked for their help in 
“defeating a terrible monster, ” but refused to give any more 
information. After another refusal to enter, he left mutter -
ing.  
Nikola’s Reindeer  
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are reindeer mutated by Nikola’s magic, 
and we crave to sink our teeth into the flesh of living creatures.    
Gift - Carnivore: Nikola’s Reindeer have large, clumsy jaws 
that they use to eat meat. Each Reindeer in the herd increases 
its OL by 1 when it attacks a marked target. A target is marked 
when it suffers 1 Wound from a Reindeer bite. Alternatively, 
a Reindeer can use its action to inflict a Wound on itself and 
cover up to 3 targets within 20 feet with blood to mark them. A 
target can dodge the attack with an OL Hard 7 Defense Check.

121
Threat – Lost Trails
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: It is not easy to follow the drag tracks in the 
forest and keep alert for predators. 
In case of failure, the Hunters will be caught off guard by a 
herd of Nikola’s Reindeer. 
The Badalisk’s Lair
Descriptor: A large natural cave that emits a strong stench 
of death and decay.      
Information: The tracks end at the mouth of the gorge, 
where there is no snow on the rock: this indicates that a 
large creature often moves to the mouth. Many human re-
mains and various objects can be found inside the cave. The 
deeper you go, the darker it gets.
Threat – The Sleeping Badalisk
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: The Badalisk sleeps most of the time, but if you 
disturb it, it will wake up ready to devour anything.
Note: This Threat is activated when Hunters make a loud 
noise, turn on a light, or come close enough for the Badal-
isk to smell them. If the Badalisk does not awaken, it can be 
examined closely while it is dormant. If the Badalisk suffers 
1 Wound, it will wake immediately.
The Badalisk
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a huge dirty red sack encrusted with the 
bloody remains of past meals. I have a large mouth with 
which I devour my prey in one gulp.    
Agenda: Eating whatever comes my way to satiate my end-
less hunger.    
Gift - Huge Mouth:  When the Badalisk attacks a target 
and the target fails their Defense Check, the Badalisk can 
choose between two effects:
1. Inflicts 1 additional Wound with its bite. The target can 
also be thrown a distance of thirty feet or have one of its 
pieces of equipment swallowed by the Badalisk.
2. Swallow the target so that they end up in its giant belly. 
This effect can also be activated when the target gains an 
Outcome with a Cost on a Defense Check, but in this 
case, they will only lose one piece of equipment as it is 
swallowed by the giant maw.
A subject trapped inside the Badalisk can use their action to 
actively attempt to escape. Depending on their ability, this 
will be resolved as follows:
NPC with OL Easy 3: They cannot free themselves.
NPC with OL Medium 5:  They are released during the 
Scene following the one in which they were swallowed.
NPCs with OL Hard 7, Dark Presences, and Bro-
ken Tales: Frees itself during the Exchange, but cannot 
do anything else.
Hunter: Gets free with a Defense Check against OL Me-
dium 5.

122
A subject trapped inside the Badalisk follows the rules of the 
Great Stomach Sack Gift.
Gift - Great Stomach Sack:  The Badalisk has swal-
lowed a Treasure of the Order, and it is making the creature 
incredibly powerful. It can act 2 times during an Exchange 
and regenerates all Wounds suffered between Scenes. To 
retrieve the Treasure, a Hunter must be swallowed by the 
Badalisk and make a Position Check against the creature. 
If the Check fails, the Hunter suffers 1 Wound caused by 
the objects inside the sack hitting him. On the other hand, 
if the creature is asleep, the subjects inside it must make a 
Position Check against the Badalisk’s OL for each action to 
avoid waking it. Removing the swallowed Treasure sends 
the Badalisk into hibernation: the creature shrinks to its 
original size and becomes the Treasure of the Order known 
as The Badalisk.
Nikola’s Estate
Descriptor: An estate consisting of a large garden and two 
main buildings.    
Information: A herd of Nikola’s Reindeer roam the es-
tate’s garden. The larger building is the toy factory, while 
the smaller mansion is Nikola’s home.  
The Toy Factory
Descriptor: An imposing building crammed with toys and 
other wooden creations. Tools and workbenches are buried 
in a huge mess.
Information: The situation in the factory differs depend-
ing on whether the Hunters arrive by day or by night.
Night: Everything is off, and the Servants rest by sleeping 
on the floor or on worktables. They are awakened by loud 
noises. 
Day: All the villagers enslaved by Nikola’s power, 12 in all, 
work in the factory. The Servants all wear a green uniform 
and a funny pointed hat.

123
Threat – Littered with Junk
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Extreme clutter and piles of toys stacked every-
where make it difficult to move around.    
Gift - Damn Junk!:  Moving around while trying not to 
make noise or leave a trail activates this Threat. If the De-
fense Check fails, roll a die:
1-2: The Hunter suffers 1 Wound.
3-4: The Hunter will make a loud noise.    
5-6: Junk sticks to the Hunters, causing a penalty of 1 on 
all Checks that require movement. It is possible to get rid of 
it by spending a few minutes.
Nikola’ Servants
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We live and work only to serve our master Ni-
kola. We are skilled in handicrafts and in using the toy facto-
ry to eliminate any intruders. 
Agenda: Produce high-quality toys and then ship them to 
poor children / Eliminate any intruder who is not a resident 
of Rovaniemi.
Gift - Enslaved to Nikola: While Nikola’s charm is active, 
a Servant is immune to all mind control effects and can-
not be manipulated by social interaction. Nikola’s charm is 
considered dark magic performed by a supernatural being.    
Gift - Toymakers: As long as they are inside the factory, 
a Servant receives the following effects.
1. They increase their OL by 1 when acting to eliminate or 
capture an intruder.
2. They can move around the factory using hooks, toys 
on wheels, and more without anyone being able to stop 
them.
Nikola’s Mansion
Descriptor: A small mansion, richly furnished and filled 
with all sorts of occult heirlooms.
Information: The situation at the Mansion is different if 
the Hunters arrive by day or by night.
Night: Nikola is present and can fully use his powers. With 
him are the 3 Helpers. 
Day: Nikola is resting in the crypt and will only wake up 
if he hears a racket at its entrance. The Helpers are present 
and active.
A large painting in the main hall shows Nikola when he was 
still a Wallachian nobleman. The painting is dated 1602. 
Below the painting is a lever that, when pulled, opens the 
trapdoor to Nikola’s crypt.

124
Helpers 
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We live and work only to serve our master Ni-
kola. Our bodies are made stronger by the power that has 
been infused into them.    
Agenda: Defend Nikola from all threats.
Gift - Servants of the Mansion: Each Helper can avail themselves of a different effect based on their role.
Jori the Cook:  Can inflict 1 additional Wound with his 
cleaver. Injuries inflicted by Jori count as a mark for Niko-
la’s Reindeer.
Pete the Butler: When moving inside the mansion, Pete 
ignores any movement penalty and no one can block him.
Verna the Accountant:  Increases her OL to Hard 7 
when defending against an attack inside the mansion.
Nikola Lupei
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful vampire with a 
good heart, and I just want to live in peace 
and quiet. The power I draw from blood 
makes my body strong and fast. 
Agenda: Find a way to get rid of the 
Badalisk without harming the people 
of Rovaniemi / Keep my secret and my 
life in Rovaniemi. 
Gift - Power of the Vampire:  
The curse that plagues Nikola’s 
blood gives him great powers, but 
also some weaknesses.
Powers: Each can be used by Ni-
kola once per Scene.
1: Recover 1 Wound.
2: Turn into mist.
3: Enslave a subject with his gaze. Perform a Defense 
Check against Nikola’s OL to resist. An enslaved subject 
shares what they see and hear with Nikola and will do 
whatever the vampire commands. Nikola does not have to 
be present to command the slave, but the subject must be 
awake and alert. Enslavement ends when the subject suf-
fers 1 Wound.
Weaknesses: Nikola cannot enter a place he has nev-
er been unless first invited by the person who lives there.    
When exposed to sunlight, Nikola loses 1 Wound per 
minute. Nikola can only be permanently eliminated if the 
last of his Wounds is inflicted by a magical or consecrated 
weapon.
Gift - Occult Scholar:  Nikola knows many rituals and 
possesses arcane objects to cast spells. He used these pow-

125
ers to create his Reindeer and make his Helpers stronger. Up to 3 times per Scene, Nikola can block the use 
of a Gift he has seen in action. Once per Scene, Nikola can imbue an NPC with power by increasing 
their OL by one category. The Storyteller must rewrite the NPC’s Descriptor to reflect the mutation 
caused by Nikola’s spell. 
Nikola’s Crypt
Descriptor: A gloomy, dark, unadorned crypt in the center of which rests a gold and 
diamond-encrusted coffin on a small pedestal.
Information: By day, Nikola rests in his coffin, sleeping in a deep slumber. A swarm 
of Bats, mutated by his power, protects him.  
Nikola’s Bats  
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (5 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are a swarm of bats ready to devour any intruder.    
Agenda: Protect Nikola while he sleeps.
Gift - Swarm: Bats are considered a single unit with 5 Wounds. 
Any damage dealt reduces the total by 1, and attacks that hit an 
area inflict 1 additional Wound. As a swarm, Nikola’s Bats can 
bite up to 3 different targets in a single action. The flapping 
of the Bats’ wings causes discomfort to living creatures: 
as the swarm rises, each Hunter present loses 2 Soma, 
while NPCs suffer 1 Wound from fatigue.

126
Scenario Events
There are six Events in this Scenario. As the Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the Hunters’ actions.
If the Hunters have an NPC in their retinue: Nikola 
will arrive in Rovaniemi on the first evening to enslave the 
NPC. When threatened by the Hunter’s presence, he will 
flee by turning into mist. 
If the Hunters do not rescue the captive Hunter in 
the Hunting Lodge within one day:  Nikola will attack 
with his servants and take the Hunters to the toy factory to 
prepare them as a meal for the Badalisk.
If the Hunters capture Nikola:  The vampire will ask 
for their help in getting rid of the Badalisk, warning them 
that if the creature does not find a meal when it awakens, it 
will rampage through the village. 
If the Hunters eliminate the Badalisk before meet-
ing Nikola: The vampire will thank them and invite them 
to his mansion to understand the Order’s intentions toward 
him and to mediate a peaceful resolution. 
If the Hunters enter the mansion during the day:  Jori and Pete will attack the Hunters while Verna runs to 
wake her master. 
If the Hunters bring a Rovaniemi resident with 
them: Nikola will try to capture and enslave the resident, 
but under no circumstances will he risk their life. 
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Nikola’s 
Pledge Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift, and if 
they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous 
one.
Aberration Killer
Requirement: Eliminate a herd of Reindeer or a swarm 
of Nikola’s Bats. 
Y ou have learned how to deal with creatures empowered by 
dark magic. Once per Scene, if you vanquish an enchanted 
creature (at the discretion of the Storyteller), you will re-
cover 1 Soma and gain 1 XP .
A Special Gift
Requirement: Free one or more of the enslaved inhabi-
tants of the toy factory or negotiate a deal with Nikola.
Y ou have received a special toy, a carving of a bearded man 
on a sled. Y ou can spend 1 Soma to animate the toy, which 
will be able to move around and perform small tasks such 
as carrying an object or delivering a message to someone. 
The toy remains active for the duration of the Scene. Y ou 
can spend 1 Soma to return the toy to you. It will disappear 
from its location and reappear in your hand.

127
The Scenario Nikola’s Pledge in Scenes 
FIRST SCENE –  Arrival in 
Rovaniemi
It’s the same one you find on page 116.
End of Scene: Tommi and Jutta will tell the Hunters that 
they can meet the Village Elders the next morning, go to 
SECOND SCENE – The Town Hall.
SECOND SCENE –  The Town 
Hall
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As you walk through Ro-
vaniemi, you may notice that many of the equipment and 
houses have engraved plaques identifying them as donations 
from Nikola Lupei. It is very clear that this Nikola is a true 
patron and that with his support the small village of miners 
was able to prosper. The town hall is one of the few mason-
ry buildings in the village, rising above the roofs of the sur -
rounding wooden houses.
Place: Rovaniemi’s Town Hall. 
Time: Early in the morning, the villagers are already work-
ing feverishly to take advantage of every hour of sunshine.
Information: Asmo and Mikko are present at the Town 
Hall and are busy examining some documents. Asmo will 
be very helpful and try to clear up any doubts about Nikola 
Lupei. The Elder will prepare a letter of introduction for the 
Hunters to take to Lupei’s estate. The Elder will recommend 
that they arrive at night, as Nikola is a real night owl, while 
during the day he is too busy working at the toy factory. 
Mikko will complain that a wealthy merchant who was sup-
posed to be in the village missed his appointment and did 
not send a messenger. The two Elders will also report gen-
eral information (see page 116), urging the Hunters to go 
to Nikola to clear up any doubts.
End of Scene: Ask the Hunters if they want to go to Niko-
la’s by night (as Asmo recommended) or by day. To get to 
the estate, they can choose to take the main road (THIRD 
SCENE OPTION A - The High Road) or venture into the 
woods (THIRD SCENE OPTION B - The Lodge). 
THIRD SCENE OPTION A –  
The High Road
Read aloud to the Hunters: The road to the Lupei estate is 
wide and well-trodden. Once again, it is easy to see how much 
effort and money Nikola has put into creating a good connec-
tion between his home and the village of Rovaniemi. Several 
tracks in the fresh snow indicate that herds of animals are con-
stantly moving through the area, and just as you are about to 
look around, a group of reindeer emerges from the trees and 
gallops toward you. By the time they come into focus, the mon-
strous muzzles of the creatures are only a few feet away...
Place: The main road leading to the Lupei estate. 
Time: It’s either night or afternoon, depending on the Hunt-
ers’ choice. Snow is starting to fall, promising a blizzard soon. 
2 3a
1

128
Information: The Hunters are attacked by a herd of 4 
Reindeer. When they examine the bodies of the creatures, 
using Descriptors related to hunting or magic, it is easy to 
see that the creatures have been altered by dark magic. 
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at the Lupei Estate, go 
to the FOURTH SCENE - The Estate.
THIRD SCENE OPTION B –  
The Lodge
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As you make your way 
through the bush, your attention is drawn to a cabin where 
you see signs of life. Someone has barred the doors and win-
dows as if to defend against an attack, but you can tell from 
the light you can see between the boards that they are proba-
bly still barricaded inside. 
Place: An old hunting lodge often used by hunters in Ro-
vaniemi. 
Time: It’s either night or afternoon, depending on the 
Hunters’ choice. Snow is starting to fall, promising a bliz-
zard soon.
Information: The wounded Hunter has taken refuge in 
the hut, the Scene takes place with the information associ-
ated with a result of 1-2 The Tracks on page 119.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at the Lupei Estate, go 
to the FOURTH SCENE - The Estate.
FOURTH SCENE –  The Estate
If the Hunters arrive during the day, read aloud:  
The Lupei estate consists of two large buildings. The sound 
of work comes from what looks like a large factory. The other 
building is a large baroque-style villa, its doors and windows 
barred as if it were unoccupied.
If the Hunters arrive at night, read aloud:  The Lu-
pei estate consists of two large buildings. From the first one, 
which looks like a big factory, everything seems to be quiet, 
as you would expect from a workplace after work. From the 
other, a large baroque villa, you can see the lights on and a 
few silhouettes passing by the windows.
Place: Nikola Lupei’s estate. 
Time: Day or night. 
Information: The snow is falling heavily and the icy 
wind is blowing, making it easy to move around the es-
tate inconspicuously. At this point, the Hunters can decide 
which building to go to; the Factory is on page 122 and 
the Mansion is on page 123. 
The Factory: During the day, the Servants will be at work 
and will not hesitate to attack any intruders. Once defeated, 
they will be free from Nikola’s subjugation and can give the 
Hunters useful information about the vampire. At night, 
they will sleep. But when awakened, they will attack.
The Mansion: During the day, the Helpers will guard Ni-
kola’s coffin. Once defeated, it will be easy to imprison Ni-
kola. At night Nikola will be awake and will try to confront 
the Hunters with his Helpers. 
Either way, Nikola will surrender if the Hunters prove too 
strong or hurt the Helpers. The vampire will then ask for 
their help in getting rid of the Badalisk and explain the 
reasons behind all his dark business and why the pledge is 
needed. 
3b
4

129
End of Scene: The Hunters have discovered Nikola’s se-
cret and can now try to defeat the Badalisk, go to FIFTH 
SCENE - In the Badalisk’s Lair.
FIFTH SCENE –  In the 
Badalisk’s Lair
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at the mouth 
of a large cave, from which emanates a terrible stench of 
death. As you prepare to descend, you hear a deep snor-
ing in the distance.
Place: The Badalisk’s Lair. 
Time: The moment of reckoning.
End of Scene:  See page 121 for instructions on how to 
deal with the Badalisk. If present, Nikola will actively as-
sist the Hunters in two ways: he can either be devoured to 
give a Hunter time to enter the Badalisk unharmed, or he 
can distract the creature for an Exchange, allowing every-
one else to act without fear of the monster’s powerful bite. 
After defeating the Badalisk and recovering the Treasure, 
Nikola will ask the Hunters for permission to return to his 
reclusive life. 
5

130
The Last Wish
 
A Scenario inspired by The Tinderbox (Hans Christian Andersen)
Once upon a time...
Bastian Soldat is a deserter guilty of a serious crime: assigned to guard the garrison 
general, Bastian fled during battle, abandoning his charge in the midst of the enemy. It 
took three years to track down the fugitive, who has since turned to a life of debauchery 
and lawlessness. But a week ago, Bastian Soldat was sentenced to the gallows and 
executed in the public square of Heidelberg, the city where he had been hiding. Up 
until this point, it may have seemed like just another story, but according to the Order’s 
research, Bastian Soldat had already been executed twice. So a group of Hunters was 
sent to the German city to retrace Bastian’s life and understand why this deserter seems 
to keep coming back from the dead to continue his misdeeds.
Safety Keywords: Scam / Demonic Presence

131
The story that led us here...
After his desertion, Bastian fled to the small town 
of Heidelberg to start a new life. Here, the soldier met 
Britta, an old witch who lived near the town cemetery. 
The woman needed an intrepid young man to retrieve a 
Magic Tinderbox, a memento of her late grandmother. 
In return, Britta offered Bastian a bag of coins that would 
allow the soldier to live off his earnings for many years. 
After retrieving the object, the two parted on friendly 
terms, but Bastian gambled away all the money he had 
received within a few days and reappeared at the witch’s 
house to ask for a new job. When Britta refused, Bastian 
lost his temper and, in a fit of rage, decapitated the wi-
tch with one blow of his saber. Determined not to go to 
prison, Bastian threw the body into the hollow tree in 
Britta’s garden and the head into the river near the town. 
He also took possession of the Magic Tinderbox, all the 
money he could find, and anything else that caught his 
eye. A few days later, Bastian found himself with nothing 
in his pockets and several debts. Determined to fix the si-
tuation, he attempted to rob a wealthy merchant but was 
caught by the city’s gendarmes. When his identity as a 
deserter was discovered, the head of the gendarmes did 
not hesitate for a moment to condemn him to the gal-
lows, but... the soldier found himself back in front of the 
merchant before committing the robbery.    In his jacket 
pocket, Bastian felt the witch’s Magic Tinderbox throb-
bing with power and realized that time had rewound to 
allow him to escape. Exalted by this new power, Bastian 
began to commit all sorts of mischief, counting on the fact 
that he could always turn back time if things took a turn for 
the worse. Meanwhile, Britta’s spirit could not rest, and the 
witch took possession of a black she-cat that often roamed 
near the hollow tree. Britta then set out to find some of the 
items Bastian had stolen from her: the Witch’s Shawl, the 
Black Coral Pendant, and the Devil’s Ladle. What appears 
to be ordinary objects are actually three powerful magical 
focuses in which Britta has stored some of her power. The 
witch’s big problem is that without outside help, she cannot 
get her hands on the three objects and bring them to where 
her corpse is...
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters...
Bastian has “died and risen” several times, including twice 
on the gallows. Despite the great opportunity that the Ma-
gic Tinderbox offers him, the soldier is so stupid that he 
never manages to do anything that would allow him to turn 
the tide forever. Meanwhile, he has fallen in love with the 
daughter of the city regent, the young Alena Wagner, who 
does not seem to disdain his attentions. With huge debts 
at the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House, the city’s worst 
gambling den, Bastian plans to clean out the Wagner fa-
mily’s coffers and then flee to Copenhagen.
 
Note: Unlike a normal Scenario where the Hunters move 
between different locations to investigate, in this adventure, 
every time Bastian is captured or eliminated by the Hun-
ters, there is a Reset that brings the events back to the first 
Scene. The cycle will continue until the Hunters manage 
to revive Britta to reveal the word, chosen by the Storytel-
ler, that can deactivate the Magic Tinderbox. Because of its 
structure, the Scenario handles the evolution of the story 
with a series of Resets that replace the normal Events.

132
Bastian Soldat, the Black Cat, and each Place associated with 
Bastian have a progressive Reset list showing the differences 
the Hunters will find the first, second, third, and fourth time 
Bastian uses the power of the Tinderbox.
In this Scenario, the Hunters track Bastian by visiting Pla-
ces that he is known to frequent. If the Hunters manage to 
find him by other means, a Reset will still take place that 
allows Bastian to escape and 1 Reset Token will be added to 
the soldier’s Magic Tinderbox Gift. However, in those circu-
mstances, the details of the setting aren’t altered and events 
don’t progress to the next change on the Reset list. For the 
sake of clarity, the chronology of pre-determined Resets in 
the Scenario is as follows:
First Reset
The first change occurs in the first Scene, when the Hunters 
accidentally run into Bastian at the entrance to the city. 
Second Reset
The second change happens when the Hunters find Bastian 
in one of the three Places he usually hangs out: the Drun-
ken Hussar’s Gambling House, Villa Wagner, or the Golden 
Plate Tavern. 
Third Reset
The third change occurs when the Hunters meet Bastian at 
one of the two Places left after the Second Reset.
Fourth Reset
The final change happens when the Hunters hit Bastian at 
the spot left after the Second and Third Reset.
After the Fourth Reset
Bastian will take refuge in the Old Barracks to begin his 
plan to rob the Wagners. 
All Resets not generated as indicated by the previous list 
should not be counted for the progression of Scenario Re-
sets, but generate 1 Reset Token for the Magic Tinderbox 
Gift.
Whenever the Hunters visit the Drunken Hussar’s Gam-
bling House, Villa Wagner, or the Golden Plate Tavern, 
roll a die to see where Bastian and the Cat are at that mo-
ment:
1-2: Bastian is in the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House, 
trying to play dice with a group of soldiers. The Cat is at 
Villa Wagner, trying to get the Black Coral Pendant from 
Alena’s room.    
3-4: Bastian is at Villa Wagner, chatting amiably with 
Alena. The young woman is overlooking the terrace of her 
room while Bastian recites a poem to her from the garden 
below. The Cat is in the Golden Plate Tavern, trying to re-
trieve the Devil’s Ladle from the kitchens. 
5-6: Bastian is at the Golden Plate Tavern, bargaining to 
eat some delicacies without paying. The Cat is at the Drun-
ken Hussar’s Gambling House, trying to retrieve the Witch’s 
Shawl from an old drunk who is using it to protect himself 
from the cold of the night. 
 
If the result has the Hunters either meeting Bastian at a 
location where they have already met him or meeting the 
Cat at a location where they have already recovered Britta’s 
item, go to the next result.

133
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  It is late in the morning 
when you finally arrive at the gates of Heidelberg: the city 
is buzzing with activity, with merchant carts and groups of 
soldiers going back and forth. This city is a crossroads, and 
as you might expect, the traffic is heavy. As you contempla-
te the difficulty of finding Bastian Soldat in the midst of the 
city’s hustle and bustle, a voice catches your attention: “Ba-
stian! Bastian Soldat, you scoundrel! Come out and give me 
the money you owe me!” The shouter is a bearded man in a 
tattered potter’s apron, while the recipient of his insults is a 
small man with an anonymous face, shaggy blond hair, and 
a drunkard’s belly. Obviously, you have already found your 
target.      
Place: The Entrance Plaza of Heidelberg. 
Time: It is midday and the day is slowly warming up as 
the cackle of taverns and the smell of good food tickle the 
senses. 
Initial Situation:  Bastian Soldat rushes through the 
crowd to get away from the man shouting at him, almost 
tripping over a black cat that seems to have deliberately got-
ten in his way.    
Information: The potter complaining about Bastian is 
called Hugo, and he is angry because the soldier bought 
some pots in Britta’s name a week ago and never paid for 
them. Talking to Hugo will give you the following infor -
mation:
 ◆ Bastian is no good and a loafer: he boasts of being an 
honorably discharged soldier, but when asked to show 
the medal he received upon his discharge, he always 
shies away. No one knows where he sleeps, but he is 
always seen hanging around three Places.
 ◆ The Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House: A cheap 
dive bar known for its bad wine and gambling tables.
 ◆ Villa Wagner: The home of a noble family. Bastian 
has told everyone he knows that he has fallen in love 
with the young and beautiful Alena Wagner, dau-
ghter of Anselm Wagner, the wealthiest aristocrat in 
town. 
 ◆ The Golden Plate Tavern: The most expensive and 
valuable restaurant in the city, where only the weal-
thiest can afford a meal.
 ◆ Britta is an old woman who lives near the town ceme-
tery. Many believe her to be a witch, but no one has 
ever seen her do anything strange enough to support 
these accusations. 
Bastian Soldat
OL Easy 3 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a deserter with no qualities other than a 
great desire to live a good life with minimal effort. My only 
virtue is that I have incredible nerve.
Agenda: Emptying the coffers of the Wagner family in order 
to pretend to be rich in Alena’s eyes and convince her to run 
away with me to Copenhagen. 
Gift - Magic Tinderbox: Thanks to this powerful magi-
cal object, Bastian can go back in time whenever the situa-
tion would prevent him from completing his Agenda. This 
magical effect cannot be stopped except by the magic word 
that only Britta knows. Each time Bastian rewinds time, the 
Storyteller secretly accumulates 1 Reset Token. Each token

134
accumulated makes the three Demonic Dogs more power-
ful (see page 142). 
First Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to Heidelberg’s 
Entrance Plaza and will stay in one of the other Places he is 
accustomed to wandering.
Second Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to the Place 
where the Hunters found him and will wander to one of the 
other two remaining locations. 
Third Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to the Places 
where the Hunters found him, and will wander to the last 
available location.    
Fourth Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to all the Pla-
ces where the Hunters found him. From that moment on, 
he will take refuge in the Old Barracks, plan the robbery of 
Villa Wagner, and then try to escape with Alena. 
Hugo the Potter
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am an honest craftsman and have been a 
potter all my life. I have a friendly nature and tend to trust 
people.         
Heidelberg’s Entrance Plaza
Descriptor: A large square always filled with citizens and 
travelers. 
Information: As the first Place that Bastian will use the 
Magic Tinderbox to escape the Hunters, this is where the 
Hunters will start again after each Reset. Unlike normal hu-
mans, the Hunters will retain a memory of what happened 
at each Reset.
First Reset: The Hunters will notice that some powerful 
magic has taken effect, and time seems to have rewound 
to a few minutes before they meet Bastian. The deserter is 
absent, but Hugo the Potter is behind his stall, smiling at 
passersby. The Black Cat walks between the legs of one of 
the Hunters, stops to watch them with intelligent eyes, and 
then walks away.    
Second Reset: Same as the First Reset, except the Cat 
will stop with the Hunters and try to get them to follow her, 
leading them to Britta’s house.
Third Reset: Like the First Reset, but this time the Hun-
ters will find Anselm Wagner waiting for them with an 
escort of ten gendarmes. The nobleman will tell the Hun-
ters that he has received a report from a trusted man about 
their impending arrival in the city. The nobleman has made 
no request of the Order and wants no trouble, so he will 
ask the Hunters to leave. It will be clear that Anselm has

135
nothing to hide but is simply afraid that the Order’s presen-
ce will scare away visitors and ruin the merchants’ business. 
Fourth Reset: Like the First Reset, except that Bastian 
has hired a gang of Cutthroats (2 per Character) to elimi-
nate the Hunters. 
After the Fourth Reset: Same as the First Reset.
•
Anselm Wagner
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the most important person in the city, 
involved in politics and with many investments in various 
activities. Everyone admires my efforts to make Heidelberg 
prosperous. 
Agenda: Keep an eye out for potential problems for the city 
and maintain a clean appearance. 
•
Armsmen of the City
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are swordsmen and have gained experien-
ce during the war. We carry out the orders of those who pay 
us without making too much trouble.  
Gift - Companions in Arms:  During an action, they 
increase their OL by 1 if at least one other Armsman is pre-
sent.  
Cutthroat 
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are ruthless killers who will do anything to 
make a profit. We eliminate the target with chilling efficiency. 
Gift - Assassin: If they act to kill, they are considered to 
have an OL Medium 5.
Information: If questioned, they will not hesitate to reve-
al that they were hired by a certain Bastian Soldat. 
•
The Black Cat
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a snappy kitten bound to the spirit of old 
Britta. Thanks to the witch’s power I can understand what 
people are saying. 
Agenda: Have the humans help me retrieve Britta’s three 
items. 
First Reset:  The Cat will observe the Hunters in the 
Heidelberg Entrance Plaza to understand why they are not 
affected by the Magic Tinderbox. It will then go 
to one of the Places to retrieve Britta’s items. 
Second Reset:  If the Hunters are intere-
sted, the Cat will lead them to Britta’s Hou-
se to find her diary. If the diary has already 
been found, the Cat will take the Hunters to 
one of the Places to retrieve the items. The 
Cat will also have retrieved one of the items if 
the Hunters have not already done so.

136
Third Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the Cat will 
lead them to Britta’s House to find her diary. If the diary has 
already been found, the Cat will take the Hunters to one of 
the Places to retrieve the items. The Cat will also have retrie-
ved one of the items if the Hunters have not already done so.
Fourth Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the Cat will 
lead them to Britta’s House to find her diary. If the diary has 
already been found, the Cat will take the Hunters to one of 
the Places to retrieve the items. The Cat will also have retrie-
ved one of the items if the Hunters have not already done so.
After the Fourth Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the 
Cat will lead them to Britta’s House to find her diary. If the 
diary has already been found, the Cat will take the Hunters 
to one of the Places to retrieve the items. The Cat will also 
have retrieved one of the items if the Hunters have not alre-
ady done so.
The Drunken Hussar’s 
Gambling House
Descriptor: A dive bar at the end of a slum alley. Amidst 
the smoke and dim lights, the shadows of the city’s underworld 
move. 
Information: The Hussar is run by Ulrich, a former sol-
dier who lost his right hand in the war. Ulrich knows Ba-
stian well and will not hesitate to tell you that the soldier has 
gambled away a fortune at the tables of his dive bar. Everyo-
ne jokingly refers to Bastian as “the Golden Goose, ” and 
when he is present, everyone competes to play with him. 
Ulrich knows that Bastian has fallen head over heels for the 
Wagner’ young scion and is often at their Villa trying to win 
Alena’s heart. 
If Bastian is present: He gambles the last of his coin on 
dice, and as soon as he sees the Hunters, he tries to escape 
through the window behind him.
If the Black Cat is present:  The Hunters will see her at 
the entrance to the Gambling House, harassing an old beg-
gar. The pet pulls a fine shawl toward her, which the beggar 
holds tightly. It will be obvious that such a poor man cannot 
afford such an expensive garment.
First Reset: If this is the first time he has encountered 
them at this Place, Bastian will try to escape. However, if the 
Hunters have already met him here, Bastian will not show 
up again. 
Second Reset: Like the First Reset, except that Ulrich will 
be offering the Hunters a round of welcome drinks. Bastian 
has paid him to lace the drinks with poison. If discovered, 
Ulrich will not hesitate to confess.

137
Third Reset: Same as the First and Second Reset, but if 
the Hunters have not read Britta’s diary and the Black Cat is 
present, the animal will try to make the Hunters realize that 
the Witch’s Shawl is an item to be retrieved. If the Hunters 
help her, she will lead them to Britta’s House to find the 
diary.
Fourth Reset: Same as previous Resets. 
After the Fourth Reset: The Gambling House is closed 
because Ulrich is joining Bastian to rob Villa Wagner. 
Ulrich Rotten Eye
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a former criminal with experience in the 
use of poisons. I opened my gambling den looking for a less 
dangerous life, but I don’t back down when there’s money to 
be made.
Gift - Poisoned Dagger: Ulrich’s weapon is imbued with 
a powerful poison. The poison first enters play when the 
weapon inflicts 1 Wound, causing 1 Penalty to the target’s 
actions. The effect lasts until the victim takes an antidote or 
has an Interlude. 
Threat - Poisoned Drink
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A fine, tasteless, odorless poison that can be 
added to any beverage. 
Gift - Poison: Those who ingest the Poisoned Drink suf-
fer 1 Wound and receive 1 Penalty until they take an anti-
dote or rest in an Interlude. 
Villa Wagner
Descriptor: An imposing mansion surrounded by gardens, 
ponds, and mazes of hedges. 
Information: It’s impossible to enter the Villa without an 
invitation from the Wagner family, but there are no fences 
and, except for a few gardeners, it is easy to wander incon-
spicuously around the large gardens. Alena is often found 
on the large balcony of her room on the second floor, rea-
ding a good book and sipping tea. Alena will tell you that 
Bastian often brings her very good and expensive cakes that 
he buys at the famous Golden Plate Tavern. The young wo-
man is unclear about who Bastian really is, but it will be ob-
vious that she is attracted to the funny little man and finds 
his awkward attempts to court her tender.
If Bastian is present: He will be under Alena’s balcony 
reciting some love poetry. As soon as he sees the Hunters, 
he will try to escape by slipping into one of the hedge ma-
zes.
If the Black Cat is present:  It can be seen on the bal-
cony being petted by Alena. The Cat pretends to purr in 
affection as she clearly tries to snatch a pendant from the 
young woman’s neck.
First Reset:  If this is the first time he encounters the 
Hunters in this Place, Bastian will try to escape. However, 
if the Hunters have already met him here, Bastian will not 
appear again.
Second Reset:  Same as the First Reset, except that a 
group of guards (2 per Hunter) will enter the Scene when 
the Hunters approach Alena. The leader of the guards will 
say that by Lord Wagner’s order, no one can enter the gar-
den of the Villa. If the Hunters can get the captain to talk

138
more, he will say that an informant has alerted Lord Wa-
gner that a group of robbers are roaming the city and rai-
ding the villas of the nobles.
Third Reset: Same as the First and Second Reset, but if 
the Hunters have not read Britta’s diary and the Black Cat 
is present, the animal will try to make the Hunters under -
stand that the Black Coral Pendant is an item to be retrie-
ved. If the Hunters help the Cat, she will lead them to Brit-
ta’s House to find the diary.
Fourth Reset: Same as the previous reset. 
After the Fourth Reset:  Alena will not be on the bal-
cony as usual. Bastian has written her a letter asking her 
to meet him behind the Golden Plate Tavern at sunset. Ba-
stian wants to do the job at the Villa without Alena being 
there. Afterward, he’ll suggest that they run away together.  
Villa Wagner’s Guard
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are loyal and well-trained for our duties as 
guardians. 
Gift - Guard Corps: During an action, increase their OL 
by 1 if they are defending Villa Wagner from a threat or 
perceived threat.
Alena Wagner
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young scion of the Wagner family. I 
love adventure and romance novels. I’m cultured and dream 
of becoming a teacher one day.  
Agenda: To have a good adventure like the ones I read 
about in novels.
Wagner Treasury
Descriptor: A small fortified tower connected to 
the Villa by an underground tunnel. Aside from the 
tunnel, the only way to access the tower is to know 
the combination of the large metal door that 
blocks the main entrance.
Information: The Treasury is permanently 
manned by 4 Guards who change shifts every 
eight hours.
First, Second, and Third Reset:  Bastian

139
has obtained the information he needs for the robbery 
days before the Hunters arrive, so he will not be wandering 
around the Treasury beforehand. 
Fourth Reset: Bastian will attempt to rob the Treasury. 
The plan is simple: Bastian and four other soldiers he met
