N

188
Zone 1 – Landing 
Description: The disembarkation point for the Hunters 
expedition, where all the sailors are guarding the two ships 
that have arrived on the island.    
Information: The sailors are led by Captain Bennett, 
who is always attentive to the Hunter’s needs. The crew is 
unwilling to leave their ship for any reason other than to 
recover survivors from the First Expedition.
Note: In this Zone, you never roll on the Encounter Table.
Zone 2 – Beach 
Description: A large Beach filled with ruins of what were 
once fishermen’s homes. The wreck of the Wavecrest (the 
ship of the First Expedition) is surrounded by the mangled 
bodies of torn sailors.    
Information: Investigating near the wreck, it will be easy 
to notice the footprints of a huge creature, and the damage 
done to the ship also seems to indicate an unequal battle 
between a monster and a ship of unfortunate men. Going 
deeper into the wreck, it is possible to find Captain Boyce’s 
diary. However, there is a risk of falling overboard because 
of the rotten wooden planks.
Threat – Rotten Wreck
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Rotten wood can give way even under light 
weight. 
Gift - Splinters and Scrap: If a Defense Check fails, the 
target falls overboard and suffers 1 Wound, and the Sto-
ryteller gets 1 Wreck Token. When the number of Wreck 
Tokens reaches 3, the ship collapses in on itself, burying 
everything inside.
Hidden among diary pages filled with uninteresting travel 
notes are entries about the island.
Note 1: Boyce followed the creature that destroyed his ship 
and discovered that its lair was east of the beach. 
Note 2: With a group of trusted sailors, Boyce headed to the 
castle that towers in the center of the island in search of help. 
Note 3: The only survivor of the journey to the castle, Boyce 
writes in shaky handwriting, asking God’s forgiveness for his 
sins and for the companions he had to leave behind to escape.
Note 4: A knight in strange red armor roams the beach. Now 
lonely and desperate, Boyce decides to try his best to get his 
attention.

189
Zone 3 – Glatisant’s 
Lair
Description: A wide expanse of rocks and bushes stretch-
es as far as the eye can see. The terrain drops sharply, lead-
ing to what appears to be a chasm. An old and worn sign 
warns, “Steer clear, lair of Glatisant the Beast. ” 
Information: At the bottom of the chasm lies Glatisant, a 
giant flesh-eating monster. When potential prey approach-
es the chasm, the Storyteller rolls a die:
1-3: Glatisant is dormant. If someone descends into the 
lair, a Defense Check is required to prevent the beast from 
waking.    
4-6: Glatisant awakens in hunger. Pretending to be asleep, 
it waits for prey to approach the edge of the chasm, then 
leaps out and devours it. 
Glatisant 
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a giant snake-headed monster with a de-
formed body, the result of the union of more than ten different 
animals. I have an insatiable appetite for flesh.
Agenda: I devour every delicious human I meet, though my 
favorite prey is the tasty Twrch Trwyth.
Gift - Monstrous Abomination:  Glatisant can act 2 
times during an Exchange, but not back to back. Its long 
neck allows it to snatch prey up to thirty feet away, and 
when it moves, it can make great leaps, ignoring enemies 
and terrain in its path. Glatisant is obsessed with the flesh of 
the Twrch Trwyth and, if given the opportunity, will spend 
the entire Exchange devouring it, ignoring even potential 
enemies.
Gift - Virtue of Courage: Glatisant unknowingly carries 
the Virtue of Courage, encased in a sword embedded in his 
right side. Defeating the beast will allow the Hunters to re-
cover the power of Courage. 
Zone 4 – Village of 
Corrinton 
Description: A fishing village buzzing with activity. The 
people are friendly and hospitable, though it is clear from 
their faces that something is troubling the community. 
Information: Chatting with the locals is another way to 
get the information revealed in the Encounter with the Vil-
lagers (page 181). At the explicit request of the Hunters, 
some fishermen will take them by boat along the coast but 
will ask for payment. This arrangement allows the Hunters 
to choose any coastal Zone as their destination (i.e., all ex-
cept 7, 8, and 9). Unless special arrangements are made, the 
fishermen are in a hurry to return to their activities, and 
once they have unloaded the Hunters, they will not wait for 
them to return.

190
Zone 5 – Merlin’s 
Tower 
Description: An old, seemingly abandoned tower stands 
on a headland overlooking the sea. The building appears to 
have no windows, and the only entrance is an old wooden 
door engraved with strange runes. 
Information: If the Hunters have not removed Merlin’s 
Shadow, the Storyteller rolls a die.
1-3: Merlin’s Shadow is not in the Tower, but if someone 
opens the door, it will be there within half an hour to drive 
out any intruders. 
4-6: Merlin’s Shadow is in the Tower, and if anyone forces 
the door open, it will attack without hesitation. 
If the Hunters have not retrieved the Tower Key from Mer-
lin’s Shadow, any attempt to open the door will unleash the 
power of the Haunted Door. 
Threat – Haunted Door
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A creaking old door imbued with all of Merlin’s 
magical power.
Gift - Away with the Harassers:  Attempting to open 
the door without the Tower Key triggers the magical effects 
below. If the Defense Check fails, the Storyteller rolls a die 
to determine the consequences. 
1-2: Loses all remaining Soma.
3-4: Suffers 2 Wounds. 
5-6: Ends with the whole group in another Zone. 
Inside the Tower, the Hunters will find all manner of mag-
ical devilry, as well as clear evidence in books and scrolls 
that the place belongs to a Sorcerer named Merlin. With 
time, the Hunters can uncover Merlin’s diary and, at the 
Storyteller’s discretion, a Treasure of the Order.
The diary is mostly written in an incomprehensible lan-
guage, but there are notes written in red ink that are in 
common English.
Note 1: An overly righteous and beloved king is more danger-
ous than the worst of tyrants, for when raising a hand against 
him an entire people will rise to shield him.
Note 2: Virtue is like a weed, it grows despite all efforts to 
pull it up.
Note 3: It may not be handy, but the bottom of a lake is al-
ways a good place to hide. 
Note 4: White beach, safe harbor.

191
Zone 6 – Forest
Description: An intricate Forest stretches in all directions 
and a light layer of fog that seems to have no regard for the 
weather or the time of day envelops everything. An unnat-
ural silence hangs in the air, broken only by the cries of a 
few animals.
Information: Traversing the Forest without getting lost 
takes time and skill. While exploring the Forest, Hunters 
can find two locations: the Magic Glade and the Green Gar-
den.
Threat – Intricate Maze
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: The great forest of Camelot is a tangle of vege-
tation perpetually shrouded in mist.
In case of Failure, the Storyteller decides which Zone the 
Hunters will land in.
The Magic Glade
Descriptor: A small clearing within a circle formed by an-
cient oaks. Inside, one can clearly feel the magic hovering in 
the air, tickling the skin.
Gift - Place of Peace and Magic:  The Magic Glade is 
a place of peace and serenity. Those who rest in it for a few 
minutes recover 1 Soma. The same subject cannot bene-
fit from this effect more than once per Scene. Invoking an 
Interlude within the Magic Glade does not generate an ad-
vancement in Merlin’s Agenda. 
The Green Garden
Descriptor: A lush field of flowers, in the center of which 
stands a small stone altar engraved with pagan symbols. 
Information: As soon as a subject enters the flowery 
field, a figure covered in ancient armor and foliage stands 
threateningly. The Green Knight will never leave the flower 
field, remaining to defend the altar. The only way to avoid 
the wrath of the Green Knight is to show respect to the an-
cient gods. By eliminating the Green Knight, the Hunters 
can get their hands on the Treasure of the Order, the Ax of 
Retribution (page 192). 
The Green Knight
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the spirit of a knight sworn to protect the 
ancient cults. My armor is old now, but the plants and flow-
ers that have grown on it protect me. With my big ax, I can 
chop off a head with ease. 
Agenda: Protect the altar and allow those who worship the 
old gods to have a place to worship. 
Gift - The Just Punishment:  The Green Knight starts 
each Scene with 3 Retaliation Tokens, each time he suffers 
1 Wound he can spend 1 Token to inflict 1 Wound on who-
ever hit him.

192
The Ax of Retribution
Inspired by the Green Knight of the Arthurian 
Myth
Once upon a time, there was a knight who had made justice 
his calling. He was respected by all the commoners and had 
married a beautiful princess with whom he shared a rich and 
prosperous life. The knight had not always been upright in 
spirit. In the past, he had often fallen into excesses of anger 
and jealousy, even to the point of staining his hands with 
blood. These lower instincts were appeased by a magical ax 
the knight had taken from a fallen enemy. The ax accumulat-
ed all the negative thoughts of the wielder, transforming him 
into a person filled with righteous thoughts. But as is often 
the case, man is not really capable of changing his nature. 
Despite the magical power of the ax, the knight’s thoughts be-
came dark. Convinced that his wife was cheating on him, the 
knight invited a companion to his court and then pretended 
to go on a quest, while actually hiding within the castle walls 
to spy on them. A week passed in which absolutely nothing 
happened, while the princess and her guest chatted amiably 
about the knight’s qualities. Blinded by guilt, the knight came 
to court and told his wife and trusted companion everything. 
He cried tears of remorse, but only the ax, at the height of the 
magical power that gave it life, sensed the lie in the Green 
Knight’s words. To the amazement of all present, the weapon 
came to life and decapitated him with a single blow, unleash-
ing all the anger and envy it had accumulated over the years. 
Ironically, the widow, saddened by her beloved’s untimely de-
parture, found solace in his trusted comrade-in-arms. When 
the mourning was over, they married and began a long and 
successful dynasty. 
Keywords: Trust / Punishment / Retaliation / Sense of 
honor / Beheading / Masterstroke.
Gift - Scales of Justice: The Ax of Retribution protects 
those who wield it from negative thoughts and punishes 
those who behave spitefully. At the beginning of each Sce-
nario, the Ax contains 3 Retaliation Tokens. The wielder 
can spend 1 Token to activate one of the following effects:
1: Get 2 Additional Successes on a Defense Check against 
mind manipulation effects.
2: Once per Scenario, decapitate a target that suffers 1 
Wound from the Ax. This effect immediately reduces any 
remaining Wounds to zero, but only applies to those who 
are truly evil, with the Storyteller having the final say. If the 
target of this effect is not evil, it is the Hunter wielding the 
Ax who takes the Wound in retaliation.
3: Give 1 Additional Success to an ally’s action, but only if 
the goal is to reveal a truth or punish a villain. 
Once per Scene, if the wielder succeeds in changing an 
NPC’s attitude from aggressive to cooperative without re-
sorting to violence, the Ax produces 1 Retaliation Token.

193
Zone 7 – Battlefield 
Description: A vast wasteland littered with corpses. War-
riors in armor bearing the crest of Camelot mingle with the 
bodies of skin-painted barbarians. The stench of death and 
destruction is everywhere, while distinctly Germanic cries 
come from an encampment in the center of the Battlefield.
Information: The remnants of the Saxon army that in-
vaded Camelot are in the camp, ready to celebrate their 
victory over the Camelot army. There are a total of 30 Sax-
on Marauders (page 180), but for every Battle Token the 
Hunters have accumulated, the number drops by 5. Un-
aware that they have landed in a future era not their own, 
the Marauders plan to march on Camelot Castle. Tied to a 
post in the middle of the camp is an old, dying knight. He 
is Uther Pendragon, Arthur’s father, who was at the head of 
the defending army. The Saxons are torturing him, trying to 
extract information. 
Uther Pendragon
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (0 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an experienced knight and a great strate-
gist. I am too old for war now, but that will not stop me from 
saving my kingdom.
Agenda: Protect Camelot and bring Arthur to his senses so 
that he can once again be a worthy ruler of these lands. 
Information: Uther is dying, and even if he is saved, it 
will be clear that his injuries are fatal. Before he breathes 
his last, however, the old man will reveal important details 
to the Hunters.
 ◆ Arthur was always a good ruler, as long as Merlin re-
mained at his side as an advisor. Uther, however, could 
always see envy in the Sorcerer’s eyes, envy for the 
young king that was his protégé. When Merlin sudden-
ly disappeared, Arthur went mad and lost all the traits 
that made him a righteous and benevolent man.
 ◆ In the southeast, past the great Lake, lies an old Aban-
doned House. Arthur spent his childhood there, and 
Uther is convinced that the spirit of all that is good in 
his son’s heart dwells there.
 ◆ There is a secret passage to enter the Camelot Castle. It 
is located inside a large oak tree that has been struck by 
lightning. From there, an underground tunnel leads to 
the hall of the Round Table. 
Zone 8 – Camelot Castle
Description: Camelot Castle appears to be an ancient 
abandoned ruin, with only the cackling of soldiers prepar-
ing for war revealing that it is still inhabited. Knights and 
villagers alike are busy crafting weapons and war machines. 
Information: Arthur is in the hall of the Round Table, 
where he sits with his most trusted Knights. There are thir-
teen seats in total, one is occupied by Arthur, and one is 
empty as it belongs to Merlin. There are then eleven more 
Knights (page 181), but for every Empty Seat Token that 
the Hunters have collected, the number is reduced by one. 
Without his Virtues, Arthur is an invading madman whose 
goal is the conquest of Europe, second only to finding his 
beloved Guinevere. Arthur’s behavior is greatly influenced 
by finding the Virtue of Wisdom (page 194).

194
Arthur
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the king of Camelot, and my word is law. 
Thanks to the power of the Grail, my body is immortal and I 
possess abilities far beyond those of ordinary men.
Agenda: Bring back my bride, Guinevere, to rule all of Eu-
rope by my side / Prepare the army for battle and make all my 
subjects immortal warriors. 
Gift - The True Power of the Grail:  Arthur is immor-
tal, which means that any Wounds inflicted on him will 
heal immediately. Arthur can act up to 3 times in an Ex-
change, but not back to back.
Gift - Merlin’s Curse: Deprived of the Virtues by Mer-
lin’s magic, Arthur has become a mad and powerful being 
who has abandoned his humanity. Activating the power of 
the Virtues awakens the king’s true soul, making him more 
human. Each Virtue consumed in front of Arthur will have 
the following effect:
Love: Lower his OL by 2.
Innocence: Lower the number of Wounds by 3. 
Faith: He loses his immortality and, if brought to 0 
Wounds, is permanently killed. 
Courage: Acts only 1 time per Exchange. 
Wisdom: He regains his wits and realizes that what he is 
doing is wrong. Arthur’s Agenda changes to: Lead my king-
dom to peace by finding a place in this new world.

195
Zone 9 – Fairy Falls
Description: A small wooded area hides a series of natu-
ral waterfalls. A keen eye might catch a glimpse of female 
figures dancing gracefully on the water.
Information: There are 6 Fairies who will welcome any-
one who arrives with smiles and dancing. The Fairies do 
not hesitate to reveal what they know, engaging guests in 
a long talk.
Fairies
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPCs (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are fairies and we love company and good 
stories. We like humans, even though they tend to age too fast 
by our standards.    
Agenda: Entertain nice people for as long as possible.    
Gift - A Midsummer Night’s Dream: Talking to Fair-
ies for a long time can be fatal for a human, as time passes 
faster in their presence. After a minute of conversation, the 
subject can make a Defense Check to see if they are getting 
old. If successful, the subject can end the conversation. If 
unsuccessful, the subject loses 2 Soma, and if the subject is 
already at 0 Soma, the subject loses 1 Wound. Succeeding 
with a Cost is possible, but the easiest way to interrupt the 
effect of the Gift is to feel pain, so the Cost will always be 
1 Wound. A new Check can be made after each minute of 
conversation. An outside party may attempt to break the ef-
fect of this Gift with words (Position Check) or by inflicting 
1 Wound (Automatic).
Fairy information:
 ◆ They come from the great lake where the house once 
stood, the home of the Lady who cared for all the mag-
ical creatures on the island. The Lady then decided 
to leave, fed up with the many brave young men who 
came to the lake, insistent on gaining her blessing.
 ◆ The Fairies have always stayed to guard the Lake, but 
on the day the Hunters arrived, a grumpy old man who 
clearly looked like a Sorcerer appeared at the lake ac-
companied by a huge knight in armor as black as night. 
The old man frightened off the Fairies, who, peering 
from a distance, clearly saw the two of them walking 
along the shores of the Lake, searching for something.
 ◆ The island contains many illusions that prevent ordi-
nary mortals from discovering certain truths. The Fair-
ies possess a Fairy Crystal that allows those who look 
through it to see the illusions. They are willing to give 
up the Crystal, but only in exchange for something of 
equal value. If the Hunters do not know what to pro-
pose, the Fairies will say that a beautiful treasure is 
guarded by a Knight in the Forest to the east.

196
Scenario Gift - Fairy Crystal
Requirement: Receive the Crystal as a gift from the Fair-
ies. (No need to spend XP)
Thanks to the power of the Crystal, you can see beyond il-
lusions and receive 3 Sight Tokens. Y ou can spend 1 Token 
and look through the Crystal to reveal an illusion, a dis-
guise, or the effect of magic that makes something appear 
different than it really is. When you spend the last Token, 
the Crystal shatters and you lose the effect of this Gift. 
 
Zone 10 – Circle of 
Stones
Description: Huge megalithic stones are arranged in a 
circle at the top of a low hill. The aura of ancient power is 
easily felt even by those unfamiliar with magic. 
Information: Inside the large stones are a series of small-
er stones that replicate the shape of the island. A person 
with a Descriptor related to magic can decipher the code 
of the stones and understand that the Circle is a system for 
magically moving from one place to another. Naming a 
Zone with the correct code will immediately transport ev-
erybody in the Circle there, but travel is only one-way and 
can’t be used to return to the Circle of Stones. Using the 
Fairy Crystal allows one to understand the workings of the 
Circle, even if the person looking at it does not understand 
magic.
Threat – Magic Menhirs
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A circle of ancient stones laid at the dawn of 
time. 
Gift - Magical Journey:  Activating the power of the 
stones is easy, but getting exactly where you want to go is 
much less so. The journey requires a Position Check: with a 
single roll, the person activating the power can take all ob-
jects within the Circle with them. Depending on the result 
of the Check, different effects are achieved.
Failure: Successful journey but the wrong destination. 
The Storyteller chooses the arrival Zone from among those 
adjacent to the original destination. 
Outcome with a Cost: Successful journey but with some 
interference. Each subject loses 1 Equipment decided by 
the Storyteller. The missing Equipment has been transport-
ed in a Zone adjacent to the one of arrival.
Standard Outcome: Successful trip, no problems. 
Outcome with an Increment:  Complimentary pan-
oramic view. Before arriving, travelers can observe the 
destination while lingering safely in the astral dimension 
they have traveled through. The Storyteller will reveal some 
information about the Zone.

197
Zone 11 – Town of Stow  
Description: A small farming town. The people are 
friendly and hospitable, though you can see in their faces 
that the shadow of war hangs over their lives. The streets 
are also filled with outsiders looking for work and a place 
to sleep. 
Information: By chatting with villagers, it is possible to 
obtain the information provided in the Villagers Encounter 
(page 181). 
If the Hunters have not already met Guinevere (page 185), 
the queen will be in Stow, where she has been hired as a 
handmaiden to hide her true identity. When Guinevere re-
alizes that the Hunters are on the island to stop Arthur, she 
will present herself to them and ask for help. 
If the Hunters have not already eliminated the Scarred 
Knight (page 184), he will come to Stow in search of his 
beloved. 
Zone 12 – Cliff 
Description: An impassable overhanging Cliff with the 
sea crashing against it. The Cliff rises on a small promonto-
ry, on the top of which a small house can be seen. 
Information: If the Hunters have not already encoun-
tered Percival the Seeker (page 186), they will find him in 
the house, cooking fish and recuperating.
If the Hunters have not already eliminated the Red Knight 
(page 183), he will appear to confront Percival.
Interacting with the confused Seeker can provide you 
with relevant information and the story of how he threw 
the Holy Grail into the sea to awaken Arthur. Percival will 
lead the Hunters to the Cliff where the current carried the 
cup after the island emerged. The Grail now lies embedded 
ninety feet below. 
Threat – The Sacred Cup Among the 
Rocks
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A steep cliff overlooking the sea that hides the 
cup of the Holy Grail in its rocks. 
Note: Getting to the cup requires moving along the slip-
pery, pointy rocks as the sea crashes relentlessly.    Retriev-
ing the embedded cup requires breaking the rock or find-
ing a way to slide it out.

198
Zone 13 – Lake
Description: A large Lake surrounded by dense vegeta-
tion. There seems to be no human settlement. A few boats 
lie abandoned on the shore, victims of time and weather. 
Information: In this Zone, the initial situation changes 
according to the number of Interludes the Hunters have 
called before their arrival.
Up to Two Interludes: Walking along the shore, Merlin 
can be seen coordinating Mordred’s search for the Virtue. 
The Knight walks until he disappears into the water, only 
to reappear a short time later with some muddy objects. 
Merlin sorts out what his servant has recovered and, with a 
displeased expression, orders him to continue. The Virtue 
of Wisdom is in the form of a Crown. Once retrieved, Mer-
lin instructs Mordred to wait for him at the bottom of the 
lake and then he leaves, abandoning his servant.
Three or more Interludes: Merlin has already rescued 
the Virtue and made his way to the Zone 15 - Secret Land-
ing (page 199). Hovering on the shore and looking into 
the Lake, it’s possible to see a motionless suit of armor un-
derwater. If the Hunters attempt to interact with Mordred, 
he will awaken to eliminate any newcomers.
Mordred the Peaceless
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an undead knight in the service of the evil 
Merlin. I do whatever my master tells me to do, though I only 
wish to return to the grave from which I was torn.    
Agenda (if Merlin is present):  Obey my master’s or-
ders.
Agenda (if Merlin is not present):  Eliminate anyone 
who approaches the waters of the lake, as ordered by my mas-
ter / Be destroyed and return to death, receiving a farewell 
prayer. 
Information: Mordred can be persuaded to surrender in 
exchange for the promise of a burial that will bring peace 
to his soul. If peace is granted, Mordred will reveal Merlin’s 
plans for why he wants the Virtue of Wisdom and the Se-
cret Landing.

199
Zone 14 – Abandoned 
House 
Description: A dilapidated cottage sits in the middle of a 
flat area. Cultivated fields once stretched in every direction, 
but now only ruins and sun-browned earth remain. 
Information: The House appears to be in ruins and long 
abandoned, but with careful investigation, you will notice 
a trapdoor in the floor. Opening the trapdoor will lead you 
to the basement, where old clothes and farming tools are 
piled high. Searching through the junk, the Hunters will 
find a small wooden crucifix that seems untouched by time. 
Once picked up, it will reveal its power as the Virtue of In-
nocence.
Zone 15 – Secret Landing 
Description: On the surface, this place looks like a quiet 
beach. The pale coloring of the sand makes it look almost 
like an expanse of white snow. Merlin’s personal ship waits 
about 30 yards from the shore, but it cannot be seen be-
cause it is hidden by an illusion cast by the Sorcerer.
Information: In this Zone, the initial situation changes 
according to the number of Interludes the Hunters have 
called before their arrival.
Up to Two Interludes:  On the ship, Captain Edgers 
awaits Merlin’s arrival. 
Three Interludes: The situation is the same as in the 
first option, but Merlin has regained the Virtue of Wisdom 
and will arrive at the beach shortly. If the Hunters wait, they 
can catch him off guard.    
Four Interludes: Merlin has returned to the ship and 
preparations for departure are in full swing. Unfortunately, 
part of the crew has contracted a strange disease that makes 
it difficult for Captain Edgers to control the ship. Merlin, 
amidst a thousand curses, does his best to create a cure.
Threat – Illusory Panorama
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A false image that adapts to weather condi-
tions. Small, repetitive details are the only sign that some-
thing is wrong, such as a flock of seagulls flying following a 
cyclic trajectory and clouds that seem stuck in the same po-
sition.

200
A Frozen Reflection 
 A Scenario Inspired by The Snow Queen
Part One – The Two Healers
Safety keywords: Illness / Poisoning / Identity theft
A Frozen Reflection is a special three-part Scenario that tells the story of 
the mysterious con artists Gerda and Kay. All Scenarios are structured as 
a Scene Tree (see page 100 of the Broken Tales Core Book) and are great for 
introductory Sessions or a mini-Campaign. A Storyteller who prefers an 
emergent style of storytelling will still find Events and information useful 
for handling more free-form Hunter action.

201
The Fragments of the 
Mirror of the Other Self
In this Scenario, several NPCs have been affected by the 
Shards of the Mirror of the Other Self, magical Shards 
that can reverse a person’s abilities and dispositions. All 
NPCs affected by the Shard receive the Gift - Power of the 
Shard, which provides a Shard Descriptor and a Shard 
Agenda to replace the original ones until the Mirror 
Shard is removed. To remove a Mirror Shard, you must 
deal with the Threat - Shard of the Mirror of the Other 
Self. 
Threat – Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A tiny shard of mirror capable of emitting 
icy cold.
Gift - The Shard:  Removing the Shard is a complex 
procedure that requires medical skills or the assistance 
of magic. Once removed, the Shard loses all of its power, 
disintegrating into thin air and inflicting 1 Wound on the 
person who removed it. This Wound can only be avoided 
by spending an Increment on a potential Check to re-
move the Shard. 
Miss Snow and Lady Storm in this 
Scenario
When a Hunter is connected to the story on which a Sce-
nario is based, an Exclusive Gift is usually presented that 
can be obtained under certain narrative conditions. This 
is a special case, however, because the Hunter Miss Snow 
and Lady Storm is directly involved in the plot of A Frozen 
Reflection. For this reason, the Storyteller must carefully 
consider whether to introduce this Hunter and how to deal 
with the resulting plot changes. Here are some tips:
 ◆ None of the NPCs who have met Lady Storm will rec-
ognize her while she is in the form of Miss Snow. In-
stead, the Hunter’s identity will be revealed when she 
activates her Dark Ego. All NPCs who contributed in 
any way to Lady Storm’s downfall will fear her and try 
to have as little to do with her as possible.
 ◆ No one knows that Miss Snow is Lady Storm, so there 
will be no qualms or special care in saying the word 
Eternity.
 ◆ During PART THREE - The Winter Castle, if the Ice 
Heart sees Lady Storm in action, it will be willing to 
sacrifice itself to get its mistress back; if Lady Storm 
manifests during the Scene, the Ice Heart will shatter, 
warmed by the love for its queen.

202
Once upon a time…
A terrible epidemic has struck the provinces of Norway, on 
the borders of the Kingdom of Denmark. Frozen Madness is a 
disease that only affects humans and literally drives them in-
sane. Not even the best doctors in the kingdom have been able 
to find a cure for the terrible disease, leaving the families of 
the sick in utter despair. The sudden appearance of the myste-
rious healers Kay and Gerda has brought a ray of hope to the 
fight against the Frozen Madness: after proving that they can 
cure the disease, the two have taken refuge in a remote castle 
in the far north, forcing the families of the sick to undertake 
a long and costly journey to obtain the cure. There are many 
rumors of expeditions that have ended in tragedy, as brigands 
and vicious animals make it seem impossible to reach Gerda 
and Kay without paying handsomely for a good guide. But a 
disease that has only one doctor is a strange thing, especially 
since the dreaded disease appeared shortly before the arrival 
of the two dubious healers. This fact did not go unnoticed 
by the Order, which decided to send a group of Hunters to 
investigate... 
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay are two young scoundrels who have 
always lived from hand to mouth. By a stroke of luck, the 
two have come into possession of many Shards of the Mir-
ror of the Other Self, a powerful magical object capable of 
reversing the character of those injured by one of its Shards. 
With the help of a witch named Flora, Gerda and Kay in-
troduced themselves to the powerful witch Lady Storm and 
managed to strike her with a magical Shard. Storm lost her 
abilities and became a naive and bungling sorceress. Us-
ing Flora’s power, Gerda and Kay forced poor Miss Snow 
(Lady Storm’s new identity) to create a great storm that 
would sweep across Northern Europe, releasing most of the 
Shards from the Mirror of the Other Self to affect as many 
innocent people as possible, spreading the Frozen Mad-
ness. Unable to handle such a powerful atmospheric event, 
Miss Snow was carried away, only to be found by the Order 
with no memory. After the Frozen Madness spread, Gerda 
and Kay used Lady Storm’s magic sleigh to travel to the ma-
jor cities of Norway, presenting themselves as healers of the 
mysterious disease that no one could eradicate. After be-
coming famous, the pair retreated to Lady Storm’s castle. As 
a consequence,    the families of the sick are cruelly forced 
to spend large sums of money on a journey of hope, only 
to find that hope dashed when they are led straight into the 
clutches of Gerda and Kay’s servants.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The Hunters arrive in Bergen, where nobleman Aleksander 
Okland has fallen victim to the dangerous Frozen Madness. 
Fredrika, the nobleman’s daughter, has been trying to cure 
her father, finally turning to the Order when it became clear 
that her beloved parent’s complete change of character was 
not the result of an ordinary illness. Meanwhile, Kay and 
Gerda struck Flora with a Shard, turning the cunning old 
charmer into a naive and clueless old woman. Flora had 
made too many demands in exchange for cooperating with 
the two imposters, so Gerda and Kay immediately took ac-
tion to get her out of the way. Since Flora is quite famous in 
Bergen, where she used to live, the two led the witch back 
to her old home, so that everyone would believe that the 
woman had simply succumbed to dementia.

203
FIRST SCENE – Bergen
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive in Bergen as the 
sun is setting, but what you see is hardly the tranquility of a 
city resting after a long day of activity. Men and women are 
wandering the streets shouting and gesturing outrageously, 
then wriggling away from desperate relatives trying to calm 
them down. As described in the letter from the young Okland 
scion, the Frozen Madness seems to have hit Bergen hard, 
driving many of its inhabitants mad. A young servant runs 
up to you and, after a brief bow, waves for you to follow him 
to the large mansion down the street. 
Place: The Okland family mansion in the city’s wealthiest 
neighborhood.
Time: The sun is setting as the cries of people 
suffering from Frozen Madness echo from the 
streets. 
Initial Situation: Fredrika Okland will intro-
duce herself to the Hunters by inviting them to 
dinner, accompanied by her father Aleksander, 
who suffers from the Frozen Madness. Fredrika 
will ask the Hunters to forgive her father’s excess-
es in light of his condition. 
Information: Fredrika will tell the Hunters 
that three months ago a very heavy snowstorm 
hit Northern Europe, especially the Norwegian 
coast. After the storm, many people began to 
show signs of madness, behaving in ways that 
were the opposite of their usual behavior. Her fa-
ther Aleksander, who had always been a calm and 
jovial man, became hot-tempered and arrogant, 
with outbursts of unnecessary violence. At first, 
no doctor could find a cure. Then two mysteri-
ous healers from the north named Gerda and Kay 
cured some nobles for a hefty fee. As soon as word 
spread of healers who could cure the Frozen Madness, the 
two disappeared, and now to meet them one must contact 
the Black Feathers: two local guides who charge exorbitant 
sums of money to bring desperate people to Gerda and Kay.
During the discussion, this information will also emerge:
 ◆ Near Bergen lives a famous witch named Flora. Many 
have turned to her for healing, but the old woman 
seems to have fallen victim to dementia, or perhaps she 
herself has been struck by the Frozen Madness. Flora is 
famous for her potions, which she distills in the large 
greenhouse where she grows all sorts of herbs. 
 ◆ The only contact with the Black Feathers in town seems 
to be an elderly woman named Lene. The woman was 
1

204
once the head of a criminal organization but 
has retired to private life to enjoy the fruits of 
her misdeeds. Few have the courage to both-
er her without offering something of value in 
return. 
During dinner, Aleksander behaves inappropri-
ately, trying in every way possible to humiliate 
his daughter and question the Hunters’ actions. If 
provoked, Aleksander will not hesitate to use vi-
olence.
Fredrika Okland
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young scion of the Okland family, I believe in jus-
tice and honesty as my father taught me. 
Agenda: To heal my father and make him the good man who raised me.
Aleksander Okland
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a rich and powerful nobleman, and I use my position to help 
all unfortunate people. I can’t stand the bullying of the weak by the powerful, 
and I strive to change society so that everything is more equal.    
Agenda: Helping others find their way and realize their plans.    
Shard Descriptor: I am a rich and powerful nobleman, and I use my 
position to bend everyone to my command. I can’t stand to be told no, and I 
abuse all the people I consider inferior.
Shard Agenda: Satisfying my thirst for power by crushing those who are 
weaker. 
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of the Other Self re-
verses the Descriptor and Agenda of the person it injured. The holder increas-
es their OL by 1 if they act in accordance with the altered part of their De-
scriptor or Agenda. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters must decide whether to contact Flora or the 
Black Feathers through Lene. Go to SECOND SCENE OPTION A - Flora’s 
Greenhouse or SECOND SCENE OPTION B - Lene’s House.

205
SECOND SCENE OPTION A –  
Flora’s Greenhouse
Read aloud to the Hunters: The home of the famous Flo-
ra is located just outside the city limits, along a large stream 
that runs along the eastern side of Bergen. The witch’s home 
stands out in the middle of the countryside as a colorful 
building flanked by a huge greenhouse. When you reach the 
entrance, there are no lights on in the house, while someone 
in the greenhouse seems to be happily humming a 
childish song.
Place: The House and Greenhouse of the witch 
Flora.
Time: The sun has set and the moonlight softly il-
luminates the Bergen countryside.
Information: Flora has taken up permanent residence 
in the Greenhouse and, in the grip of the Shard effect, be-
witches and imprisons those who visit her, then forgets her 
victims among the plants. Inside the House, the Hunters 
can get their hands on the witch’s diary, in which Flora has 
written some important information about Gerda and Kay. 
In the House:  The House is protected by Flora’s magic, 
and breaking in without her permission will activate her 
defenses. The House is well-maintained and filled with an-
cient magical relics. There are four rooms in total, two of 
which are of interest to Hunters: the Storeroom on the first 
floor and Flora’s Bedroom on the second floor. 
Threat - Haunted House
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A house that emits an aura of ignorant 
well-being, relaxing intruders into sleep. 
Gift - Induced Fatigue:  For each room visited in the 
House, roll one die for each intruder:
1-3: Nothing happens.
4-6: They lose 1 Soma. 
On the second point of Soma lost to this Gift, a Hunter 
must pass a Defense Check to avoid falling into an eight-
hour deep sleep from which they cannot be awakened. For 
an NPC, the effect is triggered when they lose Soma equal 
to the category of their OL: 1 for Easy, 2 for Medium, and 
3 for Hard.
Flora’s Bedroom
Descriptor: A richly decorated room with a large canopy 
bed. Flowers and plants of all kinds grow on the walls, giving 
the impression of being in a small greenhouse. 
Information: The Hunters will quickly notice a small box 
on the wardrobe next to the bed. Inside is Flora’s diary, but 
as you might guess, a witch’s box can’t be opened by just 
anyone.
2a

206
Warden Casket
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fierce Warden Casket, ready to bite any 
thief with my sharp teeth. I am very sensitive to bad smells, 
which makes me sneeze until I vomit my contents. 
Information: Flora’s diary describes Gerda and Kay, two 
criminals who proposed the Frozen Madness scam to Flo-
ra. Flora seems to have agreed mainly to take revenge on an 
old rival of hers, a witch named Lady Storm. The contact 
between Flora and the two criminals was facilitated by a 
woman named Lene, who is identified in the diary as Ger -
da’s mother. Flora reports that she is not too happy about 
involving so many innocent people in this plan, but the 
chance to teach the arrogant and haughty Lady Storm a 
lesson was too good to pass up. Flora does not trust Gerda 
and Kay, but she does not believe that the two criminals can 
pose any real danger to her. 
Storeroom
Descriptor: A small room filled with books, gardening 
tools, and strange vials.
Information: Hidden among the many stacked objects it 
is possible to find a Treasure of the Order. The Storyteller is 
free to decide which one.
The Greenhouse
Descriptor: A large greenhouse filled with vegetation. A 
thick mist of pollen chokes those who walk through the rows 
of plants and flowers with its smell. 
Information:  Flora has taken up residence in the Green-
house, where she repeats the same tasks over and over 
again. Without her careful oversight, many of the plants 
have grown beyond control, and the ecosystem of the place 
has become mixed with the witch’s out-of-control magic. 
Moving inside the Greenhouse activates the Threat - Magic 
Greenhouse. Flora is in the center of it and will not move 
under any circumstances.
Threat - Magic Greenhouse
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A greenhouse filled with strange plants im-
bued with Flora’s magical powers. 
Gift - Wild Plants:  The Greenhouse plants will identify 
anyone other than Flora as a threat. The plants rely on smell 
to detect intruders, so they can be fooled. If a person enters 
the Greenhouse, roll 1 die:
1-2: A carnivorous plant attempts to bite the target to in-
flict 1 Wound. 
3-4: A colored pollen covers the target, which takes 1 
Penalty on all actions while in the Greenhouse. A Defense 
Check is needed to avoid the pollen.
5-6: A large flower emits a sharp hissing sound that stuns 
the target and causes them to lose 1 Soma (1 Wound, in the 
case of an NPC).

207
Flora the Witch
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful witch who has dedicated her 
life to the study of nature-based magic. I wish to be admired 
and recognized for my abilities as a witch.    
Agenda: To gain the respect of others through my powers 
and live a comfortable life. 
Shard Descriptor: I am a clumsy witch who loves 
plants, but unfortunately I have forgotten how to take care 
of my beloved greenhouse. I am humble and don’t care what 
others think of me, because all I want is to speak to the hearts 
of plants. 
Shard Agenda: Take care of my greenhouse and keep 
away any pests that come after me. 
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the 
person it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they 
act in accordance with the altered part of their Descriptor 
or Agenda.    
Gift - Plant Witch:  Flora can manipulate nearby plants 
by animating them, and thanks to this power, she can per-
form up to 2 actions in the Exchange with a range equal to 
the ground covered by the plants themselves. Through the 
plants, Flora can attempt to injure, immobilize, or manip-
ulate a target. 
Information: If the Hunters succeed in removing the 
Shard from Flora’s eye, the old witch will come to her 
senses, but the effects of the Shard will have permanent 
consequences for her. Drained of all magical power, the 
old woman will try to bargain with the Hunters for the in-
formation she possesses in exchange for being exonerated 
of all charges. Flora will then talk about Gerda and Kay’s 
plan, as described in The story that led us here. Flora will 
not know the location of Lady Storm’s castle, as its entrance 
is protected by powerful magic. However, she will tell the 
Hunters that if they continue along the river bordering 
Bergen, they can reach the small White Hospital: a facility 
where Gerda and Kay reside when they leave the castle. To 
get there, Flora will tell the Hunters to look for the Black 
Feather boat in the small river port of Bergen.
End of Scene:  The Hunters decide to meet the Black 
Feathers through Lene. Go to SECOND SCENE OPTION 
B - Lene’s House. If they decide to leave directly instead, go 
to the THIRD SCENE - The Ambush of the Black Feathers.

208
SECOND SCENE OPTION B –  
Lene’s House
Read aloud to the Hunters:  In the slums of Bergen you 
will find a small, seemingly abandoned house. Only the lights 
inside indicate someone’s presence. You feel several eyes on 
you and realize that hooded figures are keeping watch on the 
street. There is no doubt that you have entered the territory 
of a gang...
Place: Lene’s little abandoned house in the slums of Ber-
gen. 
Time: The sun has now set and the only source of illumi-
nation is the many lights coming from the houses in town.
Information: Lene welcomes every visitor to the house 
and offers herself as a contact for the two famous healers. 
For this service, the old woman demands an outrageous 
amount of money that only the truly wealthy can afford. 
Lene’s arguments are especially persuasive because of the 
numerous cutthroats who lurk around her home, ready to 
strike at the slightest sign of trouble.  
Lene the Old Mobster
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the ruthless queen of Bergen’s under -
world, and I have no qualms about conducting my shady 
business. Everyone in the city fears me, and I use my repu-
tation to intimidate those foolish enough to turn against me. 
Agenda: Making money by taking advantage of people’s 
desperation, taking what they have to offer without really 
leading them to Gerda and Kay. 
Information: When the Hunters confront Lene, the old 
woman will reveal that Gerda and Kay are staying at the 
small White Hospital, a medical facility that can be reached 
by sailing north along the river. Lene will give the Hunters 
a pass that the Black Feathers will recognize as valid for 
boarding their boat to make the journey.
Lene will try hard to hide the fact that she is Gerda’s moth-
er, fearing that this information will get her into even more 
trouble than her daughter’s scheme. When this connection 
is made and Lene is cornered, she will reveal some infor -
mation about her daughter to the Hunters. At this point, 
the Storyteller can decide what to reveal about Gerda by 
consulting her profile on page 223.
Lene’s Cutthroat
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC 
(1 Wound - 12 Gang Members)
Descriptor: We are ruthless scoundrels and protect our ter-
ritory from any intruder.
Gift - Neighborhood Gang: A Cutthroat increases their 
OL by 1 when they act to wound or capture a target they 
consider an intruder on their territory. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters decide to meet the Black 
Feathers. Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Ambush of the 
Black Feathers. If they decide to meet Flora instead, go to 
SECOND SCENE OPTION A - Flora’s Greenhouse.
2b

209
THIRD SCENE –  The Ambush 
of the Black Feathers
Read aloud to the Hunters: After a night of prepara-
tions, you arrive at the small river port on the eastern side of 
the city, the sun is rising as the port activities have already 
begun. You recognize the small, black boat that has been 
pointed out to you, and as you approach, a young woman 
with clear skin and long raven hair looks at you with suspi-
cious eyes and then nods at you. 
Place: Bergen’s river port. 
Time: It dawns as the city awakens to a new day of work. 
Information: The woman will introduce herself as Kråke 
and ask the Hunters for a pass. If they took one from Lene, 
the boatwoman will wave them aboard. If the Hunters are 
coming on Flora’s instructions, Kråke will instead ask them 
to pay a fare in exchange for her services. 
On the ship, the Hunters will notice a second black-haired 
boatman, Ravn, busy making preparations for departure. 
Regardless of how they got on board, the Black Feath-
ers know the Hunters are unwelcome guests: Lene’s pass 
is marked, a sign the woman leaves specifically to let the 
crewmembers know who should meet a bad end along the 
way because they have not paid enough. If, on the other 
hand, they were sent by Flora, the Black Feathers know that 
the witch has been struck by a Shard and should no longer 
be able to provide this information. Shortly after leaving, 
the Hunters notice a flock of crows that seems to be follow-
ing the boat. Eventually, many birds will rest on the boat, 
and that is when Ravn and Kråke will start their attack. Af-
ter defeating the enemies on the boat, the Hunters will find 
a map to the White Hospital. 
Ravn
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled assassin, and my throwing 
knives can hit a target even in total darkness.    
Agenda: Escort those who have paid the correct amount to 
the White Hospital / Eliminate any potential threat to Gerda 
and Kay’s business.    
Gift - The Raven:  Once per Scene, Ravn can transform 
into a raven and escape by flying away. If he makes a sur -
prise attack, Ravn inflicts 1 Additional Wound.
Kråke
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled assassin, and I can corner any 
opponent with my saber. My swordsmanship is such that I 
can deflect any weapon. 
Agenda: Escort those who have paid the correct amount to 
the White Hospital / Eliminate any potential threat to Gerda 
and Kay’s business.    
Gift - The Rook:  Once per Scene, Kråke can transform 
into a rook and escape by flying away. If she makes a sur -
prise attack, Kråke inflicts 1 Additional Wound. 
3

210
Threat - Murder of Crows
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: We are a great murder of crows and watch over 
our beloved human brothers Ravn and Kråke.
Gift - The Three Feathers:  The Murder of Crows acts in 
the service of Kråke and Ravn to protect them and treat them 
as if they were a Crow. Once per Exchange, both Kråke and 
Ravn can enjoy one of the following effects:
1. Coat of Feathers:  The Murder shields a Black Feather, 
which increases their OL to Hard 7 against an attack.
2. Flight of the Crow:  The Murder lifts the Black Feather 
off the ground and allows it to run over their feathery bodies. 
The Black Feather can move anywhere within a thirty-foot 
radius, and no one can block them. If they make an attack 
after moving, the Black Feather increases their OL by 1.
3. Feather Storm: The Murder encircles a target while it 
performs an action that is not a physical attack. This effect 
can be activated when an opponent acts, but it still counts 
against the limit of one effect per Exchange. The target must 
pass a Defense Check or lose the action.
Epilogue
It takes two days of sailing to get to the White Hospital.
If a Black Feather is captured, they will not agree to talk, but 
the Hunters can use magic or other means to force them to 
reveal the following information:
 ◆ The mission of the Black Feathers is to take the wealthiest 
patients to Os, where the famous White Hospital is lo-
cated, but even that is a cover-up by Gerda and Kay. The 
real goal is to get the patients into the hands of a certain 
Reinsdyr, who will then lead them through the forest to 
be robbed by a group of bandits.
 ◆ The Black Feathers do not know how to get to the cas-
tle where Gerda and Kay live, but they have heard more 
than once from the two fraudsters about witches who 
help them.  
Storyteller’s Note: The Hunters’ journey continues in 
PART TWO - Towards Lady Storm’s Castle. Keep track of the 
fate of the Black Feathers, for they will be recurring enemies 
in the next parts. 
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunter’s 
actions.
If the Hunters use violence to stop Aleksander:  his 
daughter will stand up for him, going so far as to beg the 
Hunters to spare him. If the situation is not resolved, Fredri-
ka will send the Hunters away with less information to end 
the dinner quickly. 
If the Hunters try to destroy Flora’s Greenhouse to 
force her out:  the old witch will use her magic to call for 
help, causing many townspeople to rush to her defense. 
If  the  Hunters  inflict  at  least  one  Wound  on  both 
Black Feathers or corner them: the two will attempt to 
escape by transforming into a rook and a raven and mingling 
with the Murder.

211
Part Two – Towards Lady Storm’s 
Castle
Safety keywords: Illness / Identity theft / Medical fraud
Note: If the Hunters eliminated one of the Black Feath-
ers in the previous Scenario, the other will return to the 
attack in this Scenario to avenge their fallen comrade. A 
new Agenda will be applied to the NPC’s profile, replacing 
the previous one. The Black Feather will always use Murder 
of Crows as in PART ONE - The Two Healers.
Agenda: Eliminate the Hunter responsible for my compan-
ion’s death. 
At the beginning of each Scene, roll 1 die:
1-2: The Black Feather does not intervene in the current 
Scene.
3-4: The Black Feather tries to get in the Hunters’ way by 
acting from a distance, either through the use of the Mur -
der or by providing support to the present NPCs.
5-6: The Black Feather attacks the Hunters by ambushing 
them.  
Once upon a time…
Sailing down the river, the Hunters reach the town of Os, 
home to the famous White Hospital. The town seems to be 
a peaceful place to live, and there is no sign of the Frozen 
Madness.
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay never really stay in Os, but they 
did leave imposters in their place: Magnus and Iselin, two 
young upstarts who managed to get married thanks to Ger-
da and Kay’s intervention. Vegard, Iselin’s old father, did 
not approve of the two lovers’ marriage because of some 
debts that Magnus’ family never paid off. Thanks to a Shard 
that Kay blasted into the man’s eye, Vegard was transformed 
from a grumpy breadwinner into a gentle old man who 
willingly accepted his daughter’s marriage. In exchange for 
their help, Gerda and Kay have the two newlyweds pose 
as the famous healers, sending those in need of a cure to 
Reinsdyr. The thug poses as a guide, but instead, he takes 
the unfortunate people in a carriage into the forest to be 
attacked by Rikke’s gang. Magnus and Iselin are not evil, 
but they agree to deceive people to protect their marriage.

212
The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Magnus and Iselin are in charge of the Hospital’s activi-
ties, trying as much as possible to help those most in need. 
Both have studied to become doctors, but their efforts only 
contribute to Gerda and Kay’s reputation, giving the two 
scoundrels an aura of competence far removed from reality. 
Reinsdyr offers himself as a guide to the rich and needy that 
the two doctors pass on to him, only to lead them into the 
thick of the forest and have them attacked by a group of 
brigands in Gerda and Kay’s service.
FIRST SCENE –  The arrival at Os
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You leave the 
boat in the small port of Os: in front of you you 
see a quiet little town, very different from the cha-
os in which Bergen, hit hard by the Frozen Mad-
ness, finds itself. A large sign clearly points in the 
direction of the famous White Hospital: could it be that the 
strange disease has really been eradicated from this town by 
the two famous healers?
Place: The White Hospital, an austere and clean medical 
facility. 
Time: Early morning as the church bells ring. 
Initial Situation: The Hunters will have no trouble find-
ing the White Hospital and getting an interview with the 
famous healers. Magnus and Iselin are not expecting a visit 
from the Hunters of the Order and naively introduce them-
selves as Gerda and Kay. Under pressure, they do not hesi-
tate to expose the deception of the real Gerda and Kay. The 
following information can be gleaned from the interview 
with the two doctors:
 ◆ Gerda and Kay are two enterprising young people, 
and it is easy to guess from their behavior that they are 
accustomed to the shady dealings of the underworld. 
Kay seems to know about magic, while Gerda shows 
off magical and powerful objects. 
 ◆ Magnus and Iselin’s job is to refer only the wealthiest 
patients to Reinsdyr, whose carriage will take them to 
a mysterious woman a day’s journey from Os. Magnus 
and Iselin do not know what will happen to the pa-
tients. 
 ◆ The village was once protected by a band of nomads 
who took a liking to the locals and began a fruitful 
partnership. After Gerda and Kay passed through, 
young Rikke, who leads the nomads, changed dramati-
cally. Now, she and her companions have taken up ban-
1

213
ditry. Reinsdyr is the only one who knows the 
safe ways through the forest, which is why his 
customers pay him well.
 ◆ Magnus and Iselin are aware that they are doing some-
thing wrong by helping Gerda and Kay, and so they 
serve the poor and needy without asking for anything 
in return, trying to atone for their sins. If only Vegard 
would accept their union, there would be no problem 
and they would stop deceiving everyone.  
When the interview is over, if Iselin has not been coerced 
or threatened by the Hunters, she will say that she has spent 
the last few months studying the Frozen Madness and dis-
covering its magical origins. If the Hunters wish, she will 
teach them how to remove the Mirror Shards from those 
affected. Unfortunately, the process also requires the use of 
magic, which is why Iselin does not dare perform it on pa-
tients herself.
End of Scene: The Hunters decide whether to learn from 
Iselin how to remove the Shard (go to SECOND SCENE 
OPTION A - A Lesson in Medicine) or approach Reinsdyr 
instead (go to SECOND SCENE OPTION B - Reinsdyr the 
Guide.)
SECONDA SCENE OPTION 
A –  A Lesson in Medicine 
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Iselin leads you into her 
study and unrolls a scroll of complex magical formulas, 
saying she found the paper in her father’s great library. The 
inscriptions reveal that the Mirror of the Other Self is an an-
cient and powerful object capable of completely altering the 
souls of those reflected in it. Iselin noticed an ominous glint 
in the eyes of those suffering from Frozen Madness, and the 
symptoms of total behavioral change led her to believe that a 
Shard from the Mirror might be the cause of the illness.
Place: Iselin’s private study.
Time: Late morning.    
Initial Situation: Iselin will help the Hunters decipher 
the scrolls, a task that will take all day. At the end of their 
research, all Hunters who participated will receive the Sce-
nario’s Exclusive Gift - Knowledge of the Mirror of the Oth-
er Self - without spending any XP to acquire it. 
Gift - Knowledge of the Mirror of the Other Self:  
The Hunter receives 2 Additional Successes when facing 
the Threat - Shard of the Mirror of the Other Self. 
End of Scene: Since a whole day has passed and Reinsdyr 
has moved away from the town, the Hunters can only fol-
low his trail. Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Journey.
2a

214
SECOND SCENE OPTION B 
–  Reinsdyr the Guide
Read aloud to the Hunters: Reinsdyr can be found 
in the large central square of Os, his reindeer-drawn 
carriage standing out from the crowd. Reinsdyr is a 
tall man with a thick dark beard and a reindeer skin 
cloak that covers him from head to toe. The headdress 
he wears is also made from a reindeer head, and the 
large antlers above it make the man look almost monstrous.
Place: The central square of Os, filled with caravans and 
merchants. 
Time: In the early hours of the morning, baggers and mer-
chants are already hard at work in the marketplace.    
Initial Situation: Hunters will find Reinsdyr sitting in 
his carriage, waiting for potential customers: the man has 
a surly demeanor, but is willing to talk if money is shown. 
If cornered, he will fight the Hunters to the death, refusing 
to talk while free. If the Hunters manage to subdue him 
somehow, Reinsdyr will reveal the following information:
 ◆ He has an agreement with Gerda and Kay to lead 
wealthy clients to the home of Pernille, an ice witch. 
Pernille and her sister Sinikka were banished by Lady 
Storm for an old betrayal, but the two witches know 
the only way to the secret castle where Gerda and Kay 
have taken refuge.
 ◆ Along the road Reinsdyr travels, a gang of brigands 
has taken up residence. They are led by Rikke, a young 
woman afflicted with the Frozen Madness that has 
turned her into an unscrupulous thief. Rikke’s gang 
robs Reinsdyr’s passengers of all their possessions and 
then lets the coach continue on to Pernille’s house. 
Reinsdyr the Guide
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a very experienced guide and know all 
the secrets of survival in the north. I can take down any en-
emy with ax and pistol without making too much of a fuss.        
Agenda: Escort wealthy Frozen Madness victims to Pernille’s 
house and share the proceeds of the robberies with Rikke.
Gift – Die Hard:  Reinsdyr is resilient and a difficult op-
ponent even for Hunters. The guide can act twice during 
an Exchange, using his ax to hit an enemy in melee combat 
and his gun to hit a target at a distance. When he suffers 
1 Wound, Reinsdyr can immediately make an additional 
attack against the one who wounded him.
End of Scene:  With or without Reinsdyr, the Hunters 
must go to Pernille’s house, go to SCENE THREE - The 
Journey.
2b

215
THIRD SCENE –  The Journey

 ◆ The Hunters reach Reinsdyr.
 ◆ The Hunters choose an alternative route, avoiding both 
the brigands and Reinsdyr.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at Pernille’s house, go to
