FOURTH SCENE - The Lapland Woman.
Rikke the Nomad
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a northern nomad and have always tried 
to lead my companions on the right path. I am used to the 
hard life of the forest and know how to move and hunt well.
Agenda: To make my nomadic family prosperous.    
Shard Descriptor: I am a nomad from the north, 
trying to accumulate as much wealth as possible in order to 
move to a more hospitable climate. I hate the hard life in these 
latitudes and to survive I exploit my faithful companions.    
Shard Agenda: Gather as much wealth as I can 
through banditry and then run far away, leaving my entire 
clan to their fate. 
Gift - Power of the Shard: The Shard of the Mirror of the 
Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the person 
it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they act in ac-
cordance with the altered part of their Descriptor or Agenda. 
3

216
Nomads
Easy OL 3 - Minor NPC 
(1 Wound - 12 Nomads)
Descriptor: We are nomads from the north and have al-
ways lived by hunting and trading what we find.
Agenda: We want to live in peace, but our loyalty to Rikke 
drives us to follow her in her madness. We would like to find 
a way to cure her and return to our nomadic ways.    
Gift - Nomad Clan:  A Nomad increases their OL by 1 
when they act to defend Rikke from a threat. 
FOURTH SCENE –  The Lap -
land Woman
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at a small house 
in the woods, where the river widens into a small lake. Thick 
smoke pours out of the chimney on the roof into the icy eve-
ning air.    
Place: The house of Pernille, known as The Lapland Wom-
an.
Time: The sun is setting.
Initial Situation: The meeting with Pernille can vary de-
pending on what the Hunters have done so far. The witch is 
only loyal to Gerda and Kay because she is being rewarded 
for her services, and she will not hesitate to betray them if 
the Hunters corner her. Pernille’s approach to dealing with 
the Hunters will vary depending on the possible events pre-
sented at the end of this Scenario.
Pernille The Lapland Woman
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fearsome ice witch who learned the art 
of magic from the famous Lady Storm.
Agenda: Earn as much as possible without risking my life.
Gift - Ice Magic: Pernille can manipulate frost to create 
ice at a range of sixty feet. When she acts, she can choose 
one of the following effects:
1. Ice Rain: The sharp fragments can hit up to 3 targets, 
causing 1 Wound.
4

217
2. Ice Trap: Up to 3 targets are gripped in ice, preventing 
them from moving their legs. To break free, it is necessary 
to pass a Defense Check.
3. Ice Golem:  Summon an ice creature under Pernille’s 
control. 
Ice Golem
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a mighty creature of pure ice and live only 
to serve my creator. 
Information: Pernille is the only one who knows the 
magic formula that opens the portal located in the small 
lake, leading to the house of Sinikka, the Finland Wom-
an. A Hunter who understands magic can also activate the 
portal by studying the passage. If both of these options are 
impractical, Hunters can ask Iselin for help.
Threat –  The Magic Lake Portal
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A magical portal leading close to Lady Storm’s 
castle. Only those who know the magic formula can plunge 
into the lake’s waters and pass through.
Epilogue
Once they plunge into the waters of the lake, the Hunters 
are transported to the home of Sinikka the Finland Wom-
an, a few miles from Lady Storm’s castle. 
Storyteller’s Note: The Hunters’ journey continues with 
PART THREE - The Winter Castle. 
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunt-
er’s actions.
If one of the Black Feathers or Reinsdyr can still 
act after a fight: they will have anticipated the Hunters 
and alerted Pernille to the danger. 
If no one has warned Pernille of the arrival of the 
Hunters: the witch will act as usual and tell the Hunt-
ers that to get to Gerda and Kay’s castle, they must pass 
through a secret passage that only she knows. However, this 
passage is very expensive, and Pernille will ask the Hunters 
for a large sum of money.
If Miss Snow and Lady Storm are among the 
Hunters: Pernille will take refuge in the house and try to 
intimidate the Hunters into leaving. The old witch is afraid 
that Lady Storm will punish her for helping Gerda and Kay.

218
Part Three – The Winter Castle
Safety keywords: Illness / Identity theft  
Once upon a time…
The Hunters emerge from the lake and find themselves in 
front of the house of the Finland Woman. It is a small build-
ing, equal to Pernille’s in every way. Turning their gaze to the 
north, the imposing silhouette of a castle is a dark shadow in 
the middle of a snowstorm.
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay retired to Lady Storm’s castle to en-
joy the fruits of their misdeeds. In a fit of boredom, Gerda 
began to study in Lady Storm’s library. The young wom-
an had always had a flair for magic and found great sat-
isfaction in learning the arcane art. Meanwhile, Kay took 
to the good life, using Lady Storm’s magic sleigh to travel 
around Europe, wasting a lot of money on revelry. A week 
ago, however, Gerda attempted a very powerful spell and 
lost control at the climax of the ritual. The castle’s magic 
resonated with the power generated by the unwary Gerda, 
awakening a malevolent consciousness in the structure that 
took the name Ice Heart. The Ice Heart then unleashed a 
powerful blizzard, freezing Kay in an ice shrine, while Ger-
da was struck by a Shard from the Mirror of the Other Self, 
the thing that made her deception possible turning against 
her. The ice reached Sinikka’s house and mercilessly swept 
everything away.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The Hunters have arrived at Sinikka’s house, but it will not 
be easy to find out where they really are and how far they 
have traveled thanks to the magic of the lake. The Finland 
Woman’s house is completely covered in a blanket of ice as 
if a blizzard has swept over everything in its fury. In Lady 
Storm’s castle, Gerda desperately tries to save Kay: now that 
her character has been changed by the Shard, the contempt 
she felt for her partner in mischief has become a strong 
love. Unable to thwart the will of the castle, Gerda has taken 
refuge in Lady Storm’s great library in search of a solution.

219
FIRST SCENE –  The Finland 
Woman
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As Pernille indicated, the 
magic lake has led you to the home of Sinikka, her coven 
partner. What puzzles you, however, is the total desolation 
of the dwelling. Everything is covered in a thick layer of ice 
as if a very powerful blizzard has been punishing the place 
for years.    
Place: Sinikka’s completely frozen house.
Time: The sun rises as if the journey across the magic lake 
had taken a whole night.
Initial Situation: Everything is covered in ice. Upon en-
tering the house, the Hunters will see the figure of Sinikka, 
frozen as she was about to turn the ladle in a pot of soup 
above the hearth. It is clear that the witch has been taken 
by surprise. 
Information: Observing the house, it will be easy to see 
that the ice covering it is of magical origin. Sinikka may be 
alive, but melting this ice will require a great deal of effort. If 
the Hunters succeed in freeing Sinikka, the witch will thank 
them and explain that a week ago she sensed the activation 
of powerful magic, summoned by a strong and malevolent 
presence that seemed to emanate from the castle itself. The 
witch did not have time to understand what was happening 
before she was struck by a blizzard. Sinikka is extremely 
weak and will not follow the Hunters into the castle for any 
reason. She has no useful information, other than that since 
Gerda and Kay settled there, only a few people have come 
from the lake to be cured of the Frozen Madness, and none 
have ever returned. 
Threat – Ice Layer
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A thick layer of steel-hard ice. 
End of Scene: The Hunters can head to Lady Storm’s cas-
tle, go to SECOND SCENE - Lady Storm’s Castle.
SECOND SCENE –  Lady Storm’s 
Castle
Read aloud to the Hunters: In front of you is an ancient 
castle that appears to be made entirely of ice. The large en-
trance door is open, and there seems to be no guard at all. As 
you walk across the bridge, which is also frozen, a shiver runs 
down your spine, and you know that the cold is not to blame. 
It may sound crazy, but you feel a gaze directed at you, as if 
the whole structure has a malevolent will of its own.    
Place: Lady Storm’s Castle.
Time: Early morning of a clear-sky day. 
Initial Situation: The castle is open, so it will be easy for 
the Hunters to enter. Each room is completely covered in 
ice, and moving around the structure takes time, consider-
ing there are over two hundred rooms. 
Information: Exploration of the castle is handled by 
Encounter Rooms, each with a different challenge for the 
Hunters. These places represent the will of the Ice Heart 
to eliminate unwanted guests. After each Encounter Room, 
the Hunters will wander through other neutral rooms, 
where they will find information about Lady Storm, the 
true owner of the castle. These rooms are important be-
cause they provide vital clues as to what kind of heat can 
scratch the Ice Heart, as explained on page 224.
1
2

220
Roll or choose what kind of information to give for rooms 
without an encounter.
1: A huge painting depicts the figure of a beautiful woman 
with skin as icy as snow. Her eyes are ice blue and her hair is 
light and flowing. The plaque reads, “Lady Storm, Queen of 
Winter, everything in her is pure frost except her heart. ”
2: A completely frozen old book lies open on a lectern. From 
the few lines that can be made out, it appears to be a treatise 
on ice magic. Among the many pages, the word “friendship” 
is underlined. 
3: An array of gold jewelry and bracelets lies in a completely 
frozen display case. This is jewelry of the highest value, and 
each has a small plaque identifying it as a gift of love from a 
powerful European ruler. 
4: A large painting covers the entire wall, with a beautiful 
woman wrapped in ice in the center and a coven of witches at 
her feet, smiling and offering her gifts in gratitude. 
5: A huge globe is painted on the vaulted ceiling of the room. 
Under the ice covering it, routes can be seen that seem to con-
nect a castle to various parts of the world. A sleigh is depicted 
on the lines of travel, and one of the routes is clearly in the 
shape of a heart.    
6: An ice sculpture stands in the center of a large bedroom. 
The figures of two children are carved in ice, embracing each 
other in an affectionate gesture of friendship.
As soon as the Hunters enter the castle behind them, a wall 
of ice will fall and the windows will turn to frozen slabs. It 
will be clear that there is no way out. 
The following pages contain several Encounter Rooms, and 
you, as the Storyteller, are free to decide which ones you 
want to use. After at least two Encounter Rooms, the Hunt-
ers will find the Special Room - Library, where Gerda 
has locked herself up, and through which it will be possible 
to access the Special Room - Heart of the Castle  and 
end the spell. To get there, Hunters will have to go through 
at least one other Encounter Room.   
Exploration will follow this order:
 ◆ At least 2 Encounter Rooms 
 ◆ Special Room - Library
 ◆ At least 1 more Encounter Room
 ◆ Special Room - Heart of the Castle
A room with information should be inserted between each 
room.

221
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Guard Post 
Descriptor: A large armory with a series of ice statues de-
picting soldiers. 
Information: Walking through the hall will cause the ice 
statues to activate and move to eliminate intruders. There 
are 16 statues in total, activated in groups of 4. The activa-
tion of the statues is determined by the vibrations caused by 
the Hunters walking across the icy floor of the hall. 
Ice Soldier
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a soldier made entirely of ice and I defend 
Lady Storm’s castle. My priority is to always focus on the tar-
gets that make the most noise.
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Col-umns Walk
Descriptor: A very long corridor leads into the heart of 
Lady Storm’s castle. A series of ice pillars divides the corridor, 
and in them, you can see the silhouettes of trapped people.    
Information: The trapped people are mostly treasure 
seekers, but if you look closely, you can see some of the 
Bergen nobles. It will be clear to the Hunters that the sick 
people who made it to the castle are trapped inside the col-
umns. The columns are difficult to melt, and the people 
inside them are dead. Approaching a column without an 
occupant will activate Threat - Columns of Eternal Sleep.
 Threat – Columns  of Eternal Sleep
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A column of magical ice capable of absorbing 
living things and enveloping them in a deadly frost.
Gift - Glacial Embrace: A target that fails a Check when 
the column tries to absorb them is wrapped in ice and takes 
1 Wound. During each action, the encased subject can at-
tempt to free themselves by making a Position Check, suf-
fering 1 Wound if they fail. When they run out of Wounds, 
the target is completely absorbed by the column. An NPC 
automatically suffers 1 Wound with each of its actions un-
til it has consumed all of its Wounds. At the Storyteller’s 
discretion, a particularly skilled NPC may choose to free 
themselves with one of their actions. 
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Me-morial Church
Descriptor: A large Gothic church made entirely of ice. 
Small snowflakes fall from the large vault in the ceiling and 
float gently to the ground.    
Information: The snowflakes are imbued with Lady 
Storm’s power and are capable of erasing the memory of 
those they touch. If a subject does not find a way to protect 
themselves from contact, they will suffer the effect of the 
Threat - Snow of Memory. In the main altar is a large shrine 
with an object inside: this is a Treasure of the Order, which 
the Storyteller must choose at will from those presented 
from page 19. It takes two minutes to sprint through the 
Church.

222
Threat – Snow of Memory
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Tiny snowflakes that can freeze a person’s 
memories forever until they forget everything. 
Gift - Ice Oblivion:  For each minute a subject is under 
the snowfall, it is touched by a number of flakes equal to 
the result of a die roll. The result determines the effect to be 
applied. If the subject does nothing to protect themselves 
from the snow, they cannot make a Defense Check against 
the Threat. The Storyteller must evaluate, based on protec-
tive measures and the subject’s clothing, whether they are 
more exposed to snowflakes (roll 2 dice and choose the 
higher value) or are more sheltered (roll 2 dice and choose 
the lower value). 
1-2: A severe headache accompanies a cherished memory 
that slowly fades, leaving the target with the perception of 
an event lost forever. The target loses 2 Soma. An NPC re-
duces their OL by 1 for the remainder of the Scenario.
3-4: The face of a loved one appears fleetingly in the tar -
get’s mind before disappearing, leaving them with the feel-
ing of having lost the memory of a loved one. The target 
loses the use of one of their Descriptors (except the Dark 
Ego) until the end of the Scenario. An NPC loses their only 
Descriptor and replaces it with this one: I’ve lost my memo-
ry and have no idea where I am. 
5-6: The target hears a phrase whispered so quietly it is 
unintelligible, leaving them with the perception that a part 
of themselves is lost forever. The target loses the use of one 
Gift for the rest of the Scenario (except Dark Ego). An NPC 
who has no Gifts to lose reduces their OL by one category. If 
they already have OL Easy 3, they suffer 1 Wound instead.  
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Troll 
Workshop
Descriptor: A small room with the remains of a large bro-
ken mirror. Twelve more mirrors are placed in each corner, 
creating a striking play of reflections. 
Information: A Troll stands in the small hall. It is the cre-
ator of the Mirror of the Other Self. After the artifact broke, 
the craftsman went completely insane and now tries to re-
produce the original, failing miserably each time. The Troll 
hates to be interrupted, and any discussion topic other than 
the re-creation of his beloved mirror will incur his wrath. 
Over time, the Troll has blinded himself with splinters from 
the many mirrors he is not satisfied with and, unaware that 
Lady Storm is no longer in the castle, he continues his mad 
work.
Troll Craftsman
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled craftsman and have served Lady 
Storm for decades, creating stunning ice mirrors for her. I can 
manipulate the reflected images as if they were real.    
Agenda: Rebuilding the Mirror of the Other Self for my 
Lady Storm.    
Special Agenda (Prince Trollo the Innocent): I have 
always wanted an apprentice to pass my art on to, and he 
seems perfect. Whether he wants to or not.    
Gift - The Mirrored Reality:  Although blinded, the 
Troll can see the world around it through the mirrors scat-
tered throughout the room, thanks to the glare of the shards 
in his eyes. The Troll can act against a target by striking at 
a reflected image without the target being able to make a 
Defense Check to avoid it.

223
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Guest 
Room
Descriptor: A luxurious bedroom. The ice on the walls 
seems to have no effect on the furnishings, and even the tem-
perature seems decidedly milder than in the rest of the castle.    
Information: The room is filled with valuables and com-
forts. Time flows differently here, which is understandable 
since the Hunters will feel extremely rested after a few min-
utes. If they stay in the room for at least an hour, they will 
be able to call an Interlude without the Scenario Agendas 
continuing.
SPECIAL ROOM –  Library
Descriptor: A vast library carved from the ice, with walls 
of books stretching as far as the eye can see. A magical seal, 
pulsing with power, is clearly visible at the library’s entrance. 
Information: The seal is a protective spell that Gerda 
cast to prevent the Ice Heart from reaching her. As Hunters 
wander through the Library, they will hear Gerda mutter -
ing as she conducts her research. The young woman has 
retained the memory of her misdeeds, but now her priority 
is to save Kay, and she will not hesitate to ask the Hunters 
for help. Gerda will give them the following information:
 ◆ The Ice Heart is a core of magical energy that sits atop 
Lady Storm’s throne. Through her research, Gerda dis-
covered that the Heart is a simulacrum of Lady Storm’s 
consciousness that the castle has assimilated over the 
years. 
 ◆ In an old diary hidden in the Library, Gerda has read 
that the only thing Lady Storm fears is heat, which 
means that the Ice Heart can be defeated if it is exposed 
to heat. Gerda has already tried this, but even when she 
brought a flame close to the Heart, nothing happened, 
so Gerda is racking her brain to find out what kind of 
heat the diary is referring to.
 ◆ The Ice Heart has total control of the castle and will 
not allow anyone to leave. Now even the Hunters are 
prisoners like her.
The warmth referred to in the diary, as can be deduced from 
the many scenes depicted in the rooms through which the 
Hunters pass, is that of emotional warmth. However, Ger-
da, who is not a good person, is unable to intuit this truth. 
Gerda the Swindler
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled trickster, my sharp mind matched 
only by my nerve. While studying Lady Storm’s books, I dis-
covered that I can also dabble in magic, and I have no qualms 
about using it to my own advantage.    
Agenda: Living in luxury without working and increasing 
my power to get my hands on more wealth / Supporting Kay 
in his whims, because even though I think he is an idiot, I 
need a trustworthy sidekick who will always do what I say. 
Shard Descriptor: I am a naive girl who struggles 
to express her feelings. Thanks to the magic I have learned, I 
can help others. 
Shard Agenda: Save my beloved Kay from the sad 
fate that befell him / Defeat the Ice Heart.
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the 
person it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they 
act in accordance with the altered part of their Descriptor 
or Agenda.

224
Gift - Magician’s Apprentice: Gerda has learned the 
rudiments of magic and can choose one of the following 
effects as an action. 
1. Create 3 ice darts to hit up to 3 different targets. The same 
subject cannot be the target of more than one dart at a time.
2. Create a blizzard that will knock down all subjects with-
in a sixty-foot radius of Gerda. 
3. Manipulate memory by touching the subject’s forehead: 
Gerda can create a false memory or remove the memory of 
something that happened during the Scene.  
SPECIAL ROOM –  Heart of the 
Castle
Descriptor: Lady Storm’s throne room, where a sphere of 
pure ice floats. The magic in this place is so powerful that 
even ordinary people can feel it.    
Information: Above the great throne hovers the Ice 
Heart, which will greet the Hunters by speaking directly 
to their minds. The Ice Heart will ask the Hunters to turn 
Gerda over to it so it can punish her for what she did to 
Lady Storm. If they cooperate, the Ice Heart will be willing 
to allow the Hunters to leave the castle, delaying their end 
until it unleashes an eternal frost upon the world. 
In the center of the room is an ice pillar with Kay inside, 
trapped in a fleeing position with a terrified expression 
on his face. To free Gerda’s accomplice, you must face the 
Threat – Ice Layer (see page 219).
The Ice Heart
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fragment of pure power and live only 
to punish all humans guilty of hurting my Lady Storm. I am 
the castle and within it, all is in my power. I have no human 
malice and speak my mind without a filter. 
Agenda: Punish Gerda for what she did to Lady Storm / 
Punish humanity and freeze it in eternal ice.    
Gift - I Am Winter:  The Ice Heart is a manifestation of 
power and can manipulate anything within the castle. As 
an action, the Heart can inflict 1Wound from cold on any 
subject in the castle without the ability to make a Defense 
Check. The only safe place in the castle is the Library, where 
the Heart’s power cannot reach because of the seal Gerda 
created. A sincere expression of love or friendship in the 
presence of the Ice Heart can immediately shatter and de-
stroy it.

225
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunt-
ers’ actions.
If the Hunters succeed in calming the Troll:  the 
Troll will be able to teach them the Scenario’s Exclusive Gift 
- Knowledge of the Mirror of the Other Self. 
If the Hunters remove the Shard from Gerda: now 
a con artist once more, she will try to escape by using her 
magic. 
If the Hunters free Kay:  the scoundrel will try to help 
Gerda, but only if doing so can help him escape. Otherwise, 
he will just try to escape to save himself. 
Epilogue
Eliminating the Ice Heart will cause the castle to collapse, 
while leaving it active will create a threat that the Hunters 
know they will face in the future. If Miss Snow and Lady 
Storm were among the Hunters who participated in the 
playthrough, a choice must be made:
 ◆ Forgetting the Past: Miss Snow’s personality takes 
over, forever relegating Lady Storm to the role of sec-
ond personality. Her Hunter Sheet remains unchanged, 
as the memory of what happened is once again erased, 
and Miss Snow embarks on another search for her past.
 ◆ Remembering the Past: Lady Storm regains control of 
her body, but, feeling indebted to Miss Snow, infuses 
some of her magic to give her life. Her Hunter Sheet is 
replaced with the following one.

Miss Snow
OL Easy 3 - (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an apprentice ice mage, although I often cause disasters with 
my powers. I am naive and always kind to others. 
 
Gift – Snow Sorceress Apprentice: Miss Snow can use the Gifts of the 
Snow Sorceress and Winter’s Kiss, but when she does, she rolls a die, and 
if she rolls a 4 or higher, a magical disaster (determined by the Storyteller) 
occurs, complicating the situation.
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a powerful ice sorceress and can manipulate the weather. I want 
Snow to learn to be a Hunter, so I limit the use of my powers to solving 
problems for her. 
•  Snow Sorceress: Y ou can use magic to create and manipulate the cold. 
Describe the effects of a spell that has one of the following Keywords as a con-
dition: Ice, Snow, or Frost. Y ou could, for example, Create an Ice Bridge 
to cross a crevasse or Freeze an Enemy to immobilize it. Pay a cost of 1 to 2 
Soma, decided by the Storyteller, to activate the spell. The effects you describe 
last until the end of the Scene. The Soma cost is increased by 1 when environ-
mental conditions are not compatible with cold.
I am impassive and know how to assert my authority, nothing can 
shake my heart. I have a weakness for those who show true love or 
friendship, and it comes naturally to me to help these people, although 
I do not want anyone to notice this weakness of mine. 
•  Winter’s Kiss: In you lies the power of winter. When you give someone 
a chaste kiss, you can choose one of the following effects:
1. Erase one of their memories. It must be the memory of an event in the Scene 
in which you use this effect. Particularly capable Hunters and NPCs may requi-
re a Position Check for the effect to succeed.
2. Make the subject immune to cold for the rest of the Scene. This will prevent 
them from suffering Wounds from frost and make them immune to the effects 
of the Snow Sorceress Gift. 
3. Make an object extremely fragile. 
Y ou can spend 1 Soma when you use this Gift to blow a kiss to the target, thus 
avoiding physical contact.
I have a special insight into understanding people and their intentions, 
and with my magic, I can quickly create help for the weak. I dislike peo-
ple who talk too much and social events.  
•  Dark Ego - Deus Ex Machina:  When all seems lost, use your magic 
to make truth triumph. Reduce by 1 the Soma cost of spells you cast with 
the Gift of the Snow Sorceress when your goal is to help someone. Once per 
Exchange, you can spend 1 Soma to prevent one of your allies from taking 
1 Wound by protecting them with a wall of icy frost. Y ou can use the Gift of 
Winter’s Kiss as a free action once per Exchange.
Activator: Step in and show your power to save a good-hearted person from the 
danger that threatens them.
Equipment
A brooch in the shape of a sleigh / A small knife with an ice blade that never 
melts / A scarf handmade by Miss Snow / Books of magic / A mirror fragment 
with a strange magical aura.

3
Introduction
With Lost Stories, the Broken Tales product line 
grows with new, never-before-seen content that expands 
the storytelling and campaigning options for both Story-
tellers and Players. In addition to a plethora of new Hunt-
ers, Dark Presences, and Treasures of Order, Lost Stories 
features several completely original pieces of content:
Nemesis
Five incredible enemies inspired by the heroic archetypes 
of classic fairytales. Nemesis introduces a new type of NPC 
and allows for the expansion of Scenarios and extension 
of the game’s Campaign, providing a set of adversaries as 
powerful as The One Who Plots in the Shadows.
Rome, City of the Order
The seat of the Papacy comes to life, showcasing locations, 
Encounters, and NPCs associated with the Order’s head-
quarters. Rome is a good starting point for any game Cam-
paign and a place to return to periodically to reorganize 
ideas for long-term play.
Coteries
Thematic rules for groups of Hunters who share similar 
characteristics. A Coterie receives a special Gift for all of its 
members, as well as special rules to address the issues and 
peculiarities of the Coterie itself.
The Villain’s Journey
A set of optional tables and rules to expand existing Scenar-
ios by adding all kinds of elements such as NPCs, locations, 
and more. The Villain’s Journey is also a useful tool for the 
Solo Sessions of the Untold Stories mode introduced in The 
Broken Ones. 
New Scenarios
As with every Broken Tales book, Lost Stories offers a fan-
tastic selection of adventures based on classic fairytales. 
Many of the Scenarios you will find in this book were writ-
ten with the express purpose of adding variety to the al-
ready established structure of the first two books, and to 
provide each Storyteller with useful hints for creating in-
creasingly unexpected and original Scenarios.

5
Dark Presences
Harlequin the Servant
Inspired by Harlequin of the Italian tradition (Ally)
Many are under the Order’s “special” surveillance, men and women with gifts above 
the norm but not enough to make them candidates for full-fledged Hunters. For 
many of them, the role of aide is enough, and many are happy to join the ranks of 
the Order in this way. Many, but not Harlequin. This strange figure, who likes to 
wear garish robes, has long been a servant of the Order, a role he fills with ill-con-
cealed reluctance and great skill. No one knows his real name or origin, and some 
rumors within the Order say he is the Devil himself, who likes to disguise himself 
to mock the Church. All that is certain is that Harlequin is the most capable of 
servants, but only when he feels like doing his duty...
Descriptor: I am a skilled servant and can adapt to any task given to me. I 
always find ways to carry out orders with as little effort as possible.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Helping the Hunters as ordered by the Church, but never letting 
go of having fun. 
Gift - Skillful and Unwilling: Harlequin can do anything he is orde -
red to. During a Scene, a Hunter can make a request to Harlequin, 
such as “Find out this person’s secrets” or “Deliver this letter with-

6
out being seen. ” Harlequin will appear at the beginning of the 
next Scene, having completed the task. He will categorically 
refuse to hurt anyone or, more generally, to use violence.  
As skilled as he is, Harlequin is also incredibly unwilling 
to serve. At the beginning of the Scenario, the Storyteller 
secretly rolls a die to determine which vice he indulges in.
1-2 Slacker: Harlequin has very little desire to work, and 
when he does perform a task, he does so by following ex-
actly what he has been told. For example, if the request is 
“Deliver this letter without being seen, ” he will not even be 
seen by the recipient of the letter, without caring if they 
read it or not.
3-4 Servant of Two Masters:  Harlequin divides his 
services between the Hunters and one of the Scenario’s 
Main NPCs. When he completes a job for the Hunters, the 
Main NPC will be notified.
5-6 In Love: Harlequin is in love with a beautiful young 
woman and takes advantage of every moment to be with 
her. When he leaves the Scene to perform a task, the Sto-
ryteller rolls a second die: if the result is even, Harlequin 
returns as usual at the beginning of the next Scene. If the 
result is odd, Harlequin returns in the middle of a Scene 
that has already begun, making up an imaginative excuse 
to justify his delay.
.Polonia “the 
Fairy” and Perez 
“the Rat” 
Inspired by the Tooth Fairy and the Little Mouse 
(Neutral)
Selling components to witches and sorcerers for their ritu-
als is highly profitable, and many mercenaries throw them-
selves into this field to amass great wealth. One of the most 
famous couples in this field is that of Polonia, known as the 
Fairy, a doctor specializing in dentistry, and Perez, known 
as The Rat, a sinewy grave robber. The two apparently met 
while plundering the crypt of a wealthy nobleman on behalf 
of a mysterious sorcerer. Passion was ignited amidst family 
treasures and corpses, and from that day forward, Polonia 
and Perez became partners in both work and love. The two 
discovered that their mysterious patron wanted the teeth of 
the wealthy dead since teeth are perfect catalysts of power for 
certain magical practices. Aware that they had an untapped 
market on their hands, Polonia and Perez became tooth deal-
ers, amassing horrendous quantities of them and becoming 
the main point of contact for all European magicians. The 
constant demand for this type of merchandise soon exhaust-
ed the teeth of the dead, however, and Polonia and Perez will-
ingly switched to the living.

7
Polonia “the Fairy”
Descriptor: I am a skilled doctor who can operate with extreme precision. 
With my scalpels, I can extract a tooth with a single stroke.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Procuring teeth for my trade without caring who the victim is. I 
hate being conspicuous, so I prefer to operate on sleeping patients.
Gift - Surgical Precision:  Polonia’s scalpel skills are so precise that 
she can extract a tooth in a single stroke. Once per Scene, Polonia can use 
an action to inflict 1 Additional Wound by attacking with her scalpel. If 
her victim is asleep, Polonia can operate for as long as she wishes without 
waking them.
Perez “the Rat”
Descriptor: Where my partner works with precision, I am more 
direct. With one punch I can shatter any smile. My physique is 
powerful and I am used to bare-knuckle fighting.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: I procure teeth for my business without caring who 
the victim is. I always leave the valuable merchandise to Pola-
nia’s skilled hands, while I make up the numbers by knocking out 
low-value teeth with my fists.
Gift - Brawler:  Against Perez, Gifts that grant bonuses in 
combat have no effect.

8
The Regiment of 
Giants
Inspired by the Giants of many fairytales 
(Opponents)
Once upon a time, there was a King with very bizarre ideas, 
one of which was creating a regiment of real Giants. The King 
was a great lover of myths and stories, and he was convinced 
that nothing could frighten his enemies more than an impos-
ing Giant. So the first Regiment of Giants was formed in the 
city of Potsdam, including about 20 giants from all over Eu-
rope. The Giants inspired awe with their mere presence, but 
during a particularly peaceful period, they never once had to 
prove themselves on the battlefield. Worshiped and cared for 
without having to do more than march in public parades, the 
Giants soon became lazy and forgot all training. When the 
extravagant King was deposed by his son, the Regiment of 
Giants was immediately disbanded, and its members found 
themselves on their own for the first time. Devoid of skill, 
but still feared and famous, the Giants formed 
their own mercenary company in the hopes 
of scraping together some spare change 
by trading on their fame, while hoping 
they would never have to prove 
anything in the field.
Descriptor: We are cowards and poorly trained soldiers, 
but our reputation as fearsome Giants allows us to fool peo-
ple.
1 Wound (20 Company members)
Opposition Level: Easy 3
Agenda: Serving those who hired us and using the fear we 
instill as a weapon to solve any problem / Avoiding actual 
action as much as possible.  
Gift - Here Come the Giants:  All normal humans fear 
the Giants and have heard stories of their incredible feroci-
ty. No NPC who is not a Broken Tale will act directly against 
the Giants. Even the Hunters are afraid of the Giants, so 
any action against them will result in 1 Penalty. Once the 
Giants’ inability has been demonstrated, the effect of this 
Gift no longer applies to anyone who witnesses the scene.

9
Gemba Tanuki and Mayu Kitsune
Inspired by the Yokai of Japanese tradition (Neutral)
Once upon a time, there was a duo of skilled con artists who enjoyed robbing the wealthy families of Japan. 
Gemba Tanuki and Mayu Kitsune were two Yokai who could change their appearance to suit their need, 
allowing them to fool many unsuspecting people. The duo’s most well-rehearsed scam was to use Mayu’s 
power of possession to take control of a high-ranking young female scion and fake an exorcism to free 
her through the prayers of Gemba, who pretended to be an exorcist monk. The duo became so 
famous that they attracted the attention of the fearsome Yokai Hunters, the Japanese equiv-
alent of the Hunters of the Order. Backed into a corner, the two scoundrels were forced 
to take to the sea and flee to Europe in search of new places to carry out their scams.
Gemba Tanuki
Descriptor: I am a good-natured acrobat who enjoys music and poetry. 
Thanks to my friendly nature, I can make everyone like me. 
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Make money through the well-trodden Possession Scam. 
Gift - Yokai Spirit: Gemba Tanuki can change his appearance 
at will so that he always looks like a different person. There are 
some limitations to this power, however: he cannot take on the 
appearance of a specific subject, he cannot take on the form of 
a female subject, and each identity shares a characteristically 
prominent belly.
Gift - The Possession Scam:  Gemba truly possesses 
some exorcist powers and is therefore able to see Broken 
Tales and Hunters for what they really are. A Hunter can 
make a Defense Check to prevent Gemba from reading 
their true nature.

10
Mayu Kitsune
Descriptor: I am an attractive performer, skilled in danc-
ing and singing. Thanks to my charm, I make many suitors 
fall at my feet.   
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Make money through the well-trodden Possession 
Scam.
Gift - Yokai Spirit:  Mayu Kitsune can change her ap-
pearance at will so that she always looks like a different 
person. There are some limitations to this power, however: 
she cannot take on the appearance of a specific subject, she 
cannot take on the form of a male subject, and each identity 
possesses a fox tail that Mayu must conceal somehow. 
Gift - The Possession Scam: Mayu can take control of 
a Minor NPC by biting them and transferring herself into 
their body. As long as the possession is active, Mayu can 
act at will through the host’s body but does not gain their 
memories or knowledge. Suffering a Wound or being the 
target of a hex-breaking spell will force Mayu to leave the 
possessed body.
Special Agenda (Karasu Kuroi the Wandering 
Monk): That boring monk talks so much about justice and 
honesty, but he conceals his true nature as much as we do. 
For once, we will be the ones to expose a fraud.
Hans the Handsome
Inspired by the Ugly Duckling (Opponent)
Once upon a time, there was an ugly and clumsy little Duck-
ling who, rejected and ridiculed by his peers, decided to run 
away in search of a different life. Caught in a snowstorm, the 
Duckling was in danger of freezing to death, but was res-
cued by an old Huntsman who took him into the warmth of 
his shelter. After being warmed up, the Duckling asked the 
Huntsman how he could repay him, and the man asked him 
to help him on his next hunting trip. With the cold of winter, 
it was hard to find game, and the Duckling was good bait. 
“But I’m ugly, no one would come near me, ” said the Duckling. 
“ All they have to do is hear your call, ” replied the Huntsman, 
“and before they see you, I will have killed them. ” The next 
day they went hunting, and thanks to the little Duckling’s call, 
the old Huntsman killed nine ducks. Satisfied, the Huntsman 
asked the little Duckling to continue their partnership. The 
little Duckling thought about it and finally agreed. It was a 
good way to make those who had always rejected him pay 
dearly. Years passed, and the Huntsman, on his deathbed, 
summoned a powerful Witch and bought from her a wish for 
his hunting companion.
“What is your wish?” the Witch asked the Duckling, and he 
replied, “Turn me into a man. I want to be beautiful, so beau-
tiful that everyone will have to stop and admire me. ” The 
Witch nodded and cast her spell. Once human, Hans (that 
was the name the Duckling chose for himself) touched his 
face with his hands: his features were delicate, his hair light, 
long, and flowing. He ran to the mirror, but what he saw in 
the reflection was a frightening figure: eyes crooked and hol-

11
low, hair black and dirty, skin as scaly as a snake’s.
“You promised me a beautiful body!” cried Hans. “ And that is 
what I have given you, ” replied the Witch. “Everyone else will 
see you as dashing and handsome, but what only you will see 
in the mirror is your soul. ”
Descriptor: I am adept at flushing out and dealing with 
beasts of all kinds. I avoid any reflective surface, for I cannot 
bear the sight of my true self.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Stalking my prey / Being adored for my loveliness 
/ Breaking every mirror in my vicinity.
Gift - Handsome on the Outside:  Hans has a hand -
some appearance and charm that cannot be ignored. 
In all situations where his physical appearance can be 
used to his advantage, he is considered OL Hard 7.
Gift - Ugly on the Inside: Hans hates the beauty of 
others with all his heart. When he wounds someone 
he considers beautiful, the injuries are intended to 
disfigure the target, which must create an appropri-
ate Descriptor. Wounds inflicted in this way cannot 
be healed by the use of Gifts, but only during an 
Interlude.

12
Brunhilde the Fighter
Inspired by the Valkyrie of Norse Mythology 
(Opponent)
Once upon a time, there was a commander named Brun-
hilde who distinguished herself in the field for her tactical 
skills. Under her leadership, soldiers were safe and losses 
were kept to a minimum. Brunhilde had an illustrious career, 
racking up victories and quickly becoming the king’s favor -
ite. But all this made the other army 
commanders jealous, and they 
hatched a plan to end Brun-
hilde’s success. By falsifying 
information from the field 
and presenting it as a 
normal offensive action, 
a suicide mission into 
the heart of enemy 
territory was 
organized. Brunhilde confidently led a thousand soldiers 
into battle, only to find herself in the midst of a massacre. As 
the soldiers fell like flies, cursing their commander, Brunhilde 
went mad with guilt and began telling her subordinates that 
what was happening was written in fate. They were not going 
to die. Instead, they had been chosen to ascend into heaven, 
where an even greater and more glorious battle was in prog-
ress. Brunhilde was the only one to survive this conflict, and 
as punishment, she was banished from the army in disgrace. 
But the madness had now taken root in the poor woman’s 
mind, and she began to wander in search of brave soldiers to 
ascend to heaven and replenish the ranks of the divine army.
Descriptor: I am a seasoned commander, and my swords-
manship rivals even the most skilled warriors. I can tell the 
quality of a fighter at a glance.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Identify and kill all individuals who I believe can 
become soldiers for the armies of heaven.   
Gift - She Who Chooses the Fallen: If Brunhilde fights 
a subject she believes to be a brave warrior, she is consid-
ered to have OL Hard 7 and inflicts 1 Additional Wound for 
each strike with her sword. Brunhilde has a strong mil-
itary pride and hates to raise her weapons against the 
defenseless. Against a target she considers defenseless, 
Brunhilde is considered to have OL Easy 3.

13
The Roc
Inspired by the Travels of Sinbad the Sailor 
(Neutral)
Once upon a time, there was a greedy Merchant who traveled all 
the way to the Ottoman Empire in search of rare and valuable 
goods. In the distant lands of the south, the Merchant saw strange 
giant eggs and asked where they came from. The sellers replied 
that they were the very rare eggs of the Roc, a titanic bird that 
came from the farthest deserts. The Merchant immediately asked 
the price of this miracle but received a categorical refusal. It was 
too dangerous to give the eggs to a foreigner. But the Merchant 
would not listen to reason and offered so much gold that the pile 
covered the sellers up to their heads. Imagining the fortune he 
would make at the courts and aristocratic drawing rooms, the 
Merchant set sail for Europe again, but two misguided Servants 
had overheard the negotiations and plotted to get their hands 
on the treasure. On the last night of the voyage, the two Ser -
vants killed the Merchant and escaped in a lifeboat. Ironically, 
the two scoundrels were caught in a rough sea that capsized 
the boat, and as the Servants drowned, the five Roc eggs were 
lost to the current. Nearly a year later, a powerful earth-
quake shook the people of Cordova to the core. The earth 
split open, and from its bowels a giant creature took 
flight to return to its home, leaving only death 
and destruction in its wake.
Roc’s Egg
Descriptor: I am a huge egg, growing unnaturally large when 
safely underground. My shell is hard as metal.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Hard 7
Agenda: Grow by leaps and bounds to hatch.
Gift - Hatching:  The egg of the Roc grows when buried in 
soil and will hatch within a year. Each month, the egg gains 1 
Hatching Token. When it reaches 12 Tokens, it will hatch and 
give birth to the Roc. Magic nourishes the egg, so when a very 
powerful spell is cast near the nest, 1 Hatching Token is added 
as a side effect. Whenever 1 Hatching Token is added, the vi-
brations from the incubation will cause a natural disaster, such 
as an earthquake, which will cause extensive damage to the sur-
rounding area.
Roc
Descriptor: I am a huge bird with a wingspan of over sixty feet. 
With a flap of my wings, I can create a whirlwind. 
5 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: To return to the lands from which I came, regardless of 
the damage caused by my leaving. 
Gift - Wingflap: By flapping its giant wings, the Roc creates 
gusts of wind that can topple the houses of a city. Those hit by 
the wind take 2 Penalties to any action.

14
Poor Nello and The 
Faithful Patrasche
Inspired by A Dog of Flanders (Neutral)
Once upon a time, there was a poor boy named Nello, for 
whom fate had reserved only suffering. Orphaned at a young 
age, Nello lived with his elderly grandfather and earned 
a few coins by selling milk on the streets of Antwerp. One 
day, word spread that a terrible creature was stalking its 
prey near the city. Immediately, groups of soldiers set out to 
hunt down the monster. When they returned, the captain 
of the guard swore that the terrible beast had been mortally 
wounded. That same evening, on his way home, Nello found 
a huge wounded dog along the road. Without giving it much 
thought, Nello dragged the animal home and took care of it. 
Months passed and the dog made a full recovery, becoming 
very loyal to his new master. Nello named him Patrasche, 
and the two became inseparable. But that was the only joy 
in a life of hardship for the little boy. One evening, a group of 
cutthroats appeared at the door of Nello’s home and threat-
ened his little family. Nello’s grandfather had borrowed 
money to get by, and now his tormentors were demanding 
it back with stratospheric interest. The ruffians attacked the 
poor grandfather. Nello tried to defend him, but a blow to 
the head knocked him unconscious. When he regained con-
sciousness, he found himself surrounded by the remains of a 
bloodbath, the dismembered bodies of the cutthroats and his 
poor grandfather scattered everywhere. In the middle, the 
faithful Patrasche wagged his tail, happy to have saved his 
master.
Poor Nello
Descriptor: I am a poor boy who no longer has a home 
to live in. I dabble in painting and can turn my hand to all 
kinds of work. 
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Easy 3
Agenda: To fulfill my dream of becoming a painter. 
Gift - Poor and Unlucky:  Nello has bad luck and is 
always in the wrong place at the wrong time. He may ap-

15
pear during a Scene in which an illegal act is taking place 
or a secret is being revealed that no one should know. Nello 
will appear by chance, completely unaware of the danger he 
is in. When introduced into the game, Nello will always have 
a connection to an evil NPC who is using the young man for 
personal gain.
The Faithful Patrasche
Descriptor: I am a great monster, and my ferocity is matched 
only by my strength. I take the form of a simple dog to deceive 
humans and stay close to Nello, the only one I consider a friend.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Protect Nello by tearing apart any danger that 
threatens him / Feed on people to satisfy my hunger without 
Nello discovering my true nature.
Gift - Monstrous:  Although he appears as a simple dog, 
Patrasche can take his true form, becoming a huge and mon-
strously ravenous wolf. In this form, Patrasche increases his 
OL by 1 when he acts to defend Nello. When Patrasche is re-
vealed for what he really is, ordinary people flee in terror, Mi-
nor NPCs will never choose to attack him, and Main NPCs 
decrease their OL by 1. A Hunter must pass a Defense Check 
to avoid being shaken by the sight and receive 1 Penalty on 
any action while Patrasche is in the Scene.
Gift - Loyal Friend: Once per Scene, if Nello is in danger, 
Patrasche can appear out of nowhere in his monstrous wolf 
form to defend his little friend. 
The Little Oni
Inspired by the Oni Demons of Japanese 
Mythology (Ally)
Once upon a time, a child was born with strange powers. 
Frightened, his parents took him to the temple on the near -
by mountain to be cared for by the monks. But even they 
found the task of raising the strange child too difficult. Oni, 
as the baby was called because of the two strange horns on 
his forehead, possessed herculean strength, matched only by 
his resistance to every natural element. Even the older monks 
found it impossible to keep up with the child’s tantrums. One 
day, the famous monk Karasu Kuroi passed by the temple, 
and when he saw the little boy, he had no doubt. In his blood 
flowed the power of the Yokai. Karasu took Oni with him and 
assured the boy that he would take care of his education. But 
even for Karasu, it was impossible to handle this little force 
of nature. Oni had become attached to Karasu, and although 
the monk had tried several times to place the child in the lov-
ing care of a family, he found Oni had followed him to Europe, 
ready to wreak havoc.
Descriptor: I am a six-year-old child and I easily get angry. 
I possess the strength and endurance of ten men. I become 
attached to the Hunters because I feel they are similar to me. 
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Easy 3
Agenda: Follow the Hunters and help as I see fit / Throw 
tantrums until I get what I want.

16
Gift - Blood of Oni:  The Little Oni has unusual strength 
and endurance. If he acts to satisfy a whim or to get what 
he wants, the Little Oni is considered OL Hard 7. Natural 
elements such as fire and lightning pose no danger to the 
Little Oni, as he is completely immune to them. Each time 
he performs a task for a Hunter, he receives 1 Whim Token.
Gift - Tantrum: The Little Oni is a simple child, after all. 
Once per Scene, the Storyteller may spend 1 Whim Token 
to roll for or choose one of the following problems:
1. I want it!: The Little Oni decides that he wants an im-
portant object for himself during the Hunters’ investi-
gation. He will use it as a toy for the rest of the Scene 
and flatly refuses to give it to anyone else.
2. Will you take me there?:  The Little Oni wants to 
visit a place that has nothing to do with the Hunters’ 
investigation or a place that they are intentionally 
avoiding. The Little Oni will want to be accompanied 
by a Hunter, but will also go alone if no one chooses to 
go with him.
3. Play with me: The Little Oni chooses a Hunter as his 
playmate and wants their attention for the rest of the 
Scene. If the Hunter does anything else, the Little Oni 
will get in the Hunter’s way to get their attention.
4. Sing: The Little Oni sings at the top of his lungs at the 
worst possible time, creating a terrifying dirge.
5. Can I have a puppy?: The Little Oni wants to bring 
back an animal or monster he encountered during the 
Scenario. He has a preference for large and dangerous 
creatures.
6. Stomping my feet: The Little Oni has become angry 
at a Hunter for some pointless reason and will refuse to 
do anything for the rest of the Scene except annoy the 
target of his anger.

17
White Rose and Red Rose
Inspired by Snow-White and Rose-Red (Opponents)
Once upon a time, there was a poor widow who had two 
good and hardworking daughters. White Rose and Red Rose 
were the girls’ names. Both had a strict religious upbring-
ing, following their Mother’s unwavering faith. The two 
sisters often played in the woods near the house, but 
their Mother feared nothing, convinced that her prayers 
made the whole family immune to any misfortune. 
One day, a strange talking Bear appeared at their door 
and told them that he was a prince cursed by an evil 
witch. The woman and her daughters made the 
Bear comfortable by offering him shelter. They 
fed him and kept him warm, and when he 
fell asleep, they mercilessly slit his throat. A 
talking animal was surely the work of the 
devil and as such had to be eliminated. A 
short time later, a well-dressed Dwarf ap-
peared at the house. He was looking for the 
prince he served, who had been cursed 
by an evil witch who had turned him 
into a Bear. As a reward for finding 
his master, the Dwarf offered a 
bag full of magical treasures. The 
woman and her two daughters 
made the Dwarf comfortable 
in the house, and they set 
about sewing the cloak 
that had been torn

18
from him on his journey. While the Mother prepared nee-
dle and thread, White Rose held the Dwarf down and Red 
Rose thrust the scissors into his eyes. Blind and frightened, 
the Dwarf tried to flee, but White Rose finished him off with 
a deft blow. The offering of magical treasures was surely the 
work of the devil, and once again the two young girls had 
carried out God’s word as their Mother had intended. As the 
years passed, the two girls became women. When their Moth-
er ascended to heaven, she left her beloved daughters with 
one last task: to find the devil in the world and put an end to 
him, just as divine will dictates.
White Rose
Descriptor: I hunt monsters and I have learned every bit 
of knowledge relevant to the fight against darkness. I am the 
mind that uncovers evil and my sister is the arm that pun-
ishes it.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Seek out every supernatural creature and elimi-
nate them in the name of God.
Gift - The Mind:  White Rose is immune to any kind of 
mind control or fear effect, even if it is magical in origin. 
Through her words, White Rose can interrupt mind control 
and fear effects on other targets. These effects end automat-
ically on White Rose, while for other targets a Hunter is 
forced to make a Defense Check to keep their power active. 
Red Rose
Descriptor: I hunt monsters and have trained my body 
solely to fight the darkness. I am the arm that punishes evil 
and my sister is the mind that uncovers it.
3 Wounds 
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Seek out every supernatural creature and elimi-
nate them in the name of God.
Gift - The Arm: Red Rose increases her OL by 1 when she 
attacks a Broken Tale with the specific purpose of killing 
them.

19
Order’s  
Treasures
The following are new Treasures that Hunters 
can find during their adventures or that are kept in 
Valea Viilor. Ali the Treasure Seeker and Faithful Mor-
giana are two Dark Presences related to the Treasures 
of the Order and can be introduced in any Scenario or 
Game Campaign. Ali and Morgiana can help Hunters 
in their search for a Treasure, but they will also be will-
ing to do anything to add it to their collection.
Ali the Treasure 
Seeker and 
Faithful 
Morgiana
Inspired by Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves 
(Neutral)
Once upon a time, there was a poor woodcutter named 
Ali who, by sheer luck, found the secret hiding place of 
a band of thieves. With the help of his faithful servant 
Morgiana, Ali stole the thieves’ treasure, but the thieves 
noticed the theft and immediately sought revenge. Fortu-
nately, Ali had Morgiana, a clever spy who posed as a ser-
vant girl to evade the authorities of many lands who were 
hunting her. Ali had given Morgiana a home, unaware of her 
true identity. Morgiana easily overpowered the forty thieves, 
viciously killing them one by one. Hiding under the kitchen 
table, Ali emerged at the end of the confrontation to find him-
self in a sea of corpses and entrails. The two fled to avoid 
having to explain themselves to the authorities, and thanks 
to the thieves’ treasure, they made a new life for themselves 
in Europe. But all that wealth had transformed Ali from a 
simple woodcutter into a wealthy merchant. Enslaved by his 
greed, he began a frenzied search for the most valuable trea-
sures. Always at his side was the faithful Morgiana, who was 
only too happy to interrupt the monotony of her comfortable 
life with a chance to unleash her murderous fury.

20
Ali the Treasure Seeker
Descriptor: I am a skilled treasure seeker and nothing escapes 
my expert eye. I hate getting my hands dirty and don’t even know 
how to use a weapon. I rely on Morgiana to do all the dirty work.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: Recover as many magical treasures as possible to in-
crease my personal collection. 
Gift - Treasure Finder: With a single glance, Ali is able to 
locate a magical treasure, always being able to distinguish a 
Treasure of the Order or an object of equivalent power, even if 
the magical aura is somehow masked. Ali does not need to see 
the object but can sense its presence. Ali increases his OL by 1 
whenever he can act to obtain the treasure directly.  
Gift - Personal Treasure:  Ali possesses a Treasure of the 
Order and can use it at will. 
Faithful Morgiana
Descriptor: I am a notorious assassin who has worked in the 
service of sultans for years. I have a natural talent for death and 
have been trained since childhood to inflict pain on my enemies. 
I fear nothing and have complete control over my emotions.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Hard 7
Agenda: Helping Ali in his search for treasure. I do it more to 
kill boredom than out of any real interest / To savor a confron-
tation with a worthy opponent and enjoy the thrill of tempting 
death. 
Gift - Death Machine: Morgiana is trained to be an unpar-
alleled assassin and can take advantage of the following effects.
1. Once per Scene, even if a target succeeds in a Defense 
Check against Morgiana’s attack, they will still suffer 1 
Wound.
2. Morgiana can act twice in the course of the Exchange if her 
actions are likely to kill someone. 
3. Morgiana is totally immune to any effects of fear or mental 
manipulation, even those of a magical nature.

21
Tell’s Crossbow
Inspired by the legend of William Tell
Once upon a time, there was a skilled crossbowman named 
William who had the audacity to disrespect the Baronet who 
ruled over his home. The arrogant nobleman decided to punish 
William for his insult and forced him into a test of skill with 
his crossbow. Unbeknownst to the nobleman, William’s cross-
bow was magical, allowing the man to overcome any challenge. 
The arrows shot from the weapon seemed to move with a life 
of their own, dodging obstacles and always hitting their mark. 
Humiliated, the nobleman devised a cruel trick to punish the 
arrogant Tell. As a final test, he asked the crossbowman to hit 
an apple while blindfolded. William agreed, not knowing that 
once blindfolded, the apple would be replaced by his son’s head, 
appropriately gagged. The whole village was silent as the exe-
cution took place. William shot the arrow and, as always, it hit 
the mark. When the man removed his blindfold and saw his son 
shot to death, he went mad with rage, but he barely had time to 
reload his trusty crossbow before he fell, pierced by the guards’ 
blades. But the crossbow was enchanted, and as if in obedience 
to its master’s last command, it fired its arrow anyway. The no-
bleman fled to his palace, locking every door and barring every 
window, but when he heard a hissing sound from the 
chimney, it was too late. The deadly arrow flew 
inexorably, plunging straight into the fore-
head of the petty Baronet.
Keywords: Darts / Crossbow / Sniper / Deception / Aiming / 
Center the target
Gift - Infallible:  Whoever uses Tell’s Crossbow to shoot 
a dart gets 1 Additional Success to their Position Check. In 
addition, spending 1 Soma can activate one of the following 
effects:
1. Impossible Trajectory:  The arrow can dodge any ob-
stacle or follow complex paths such as flying down a chimney. 
As long as you know where the target is and that it is in range, 
there are no external conditions that can interfere with its tra-
jectory.
2. Precise Shot: The arrow can hit a specific spot without 
damaging it. This can be used to activate switches or to ex-
tinguish or light a candle. When shot at a target, the arrow 
can knock an object in its possession to the ground without 
damaging it.
3. Automatic Firing: The crossbow will fire the shot even if 
it is not held by its user. The target must still be visible to the 
Treasure user at that time.

The Seven-league Boots
22
The Seven-league Boots
Inspired by the Boots of the Seven Leagues from various Fairytales and Novels
Once upon a time, there was a powerful wizard who was ob-
sessed with not wasting time. For this reason, the old man 
created a pair of magic boots that could cover great distances 
with a few simple steps. Enthusiastic about his creation, the 
old wizard took a step and found himself in another land. 
Elated, he took another step and then another. What he had 
not considered was that even though the journeys were in-
stantaneous, the fatigue still took its toll on his body, and by 
the seventh step, the old wizard died of exhaustion. So the 
boots passed from master to master, first to an evil orc, then 
to a dwarf, then to an alchemist, and so on, killing off one fool 
after another, each incapable of handling such power.
Keywords: Travel / Run / Boots / Fatigue / Fast / Explore
Gift - Long Step:  The wearer of the Seven-league Boots 
can cover a distance of up to twelve miles in a single step. 
The wearer of the Boots must know their destination and 
can carry two other people by holding hands. Using the 
Boots is extremely tiring, and the wearer takes 1 Wound 
of exhaustion after moving. At the end of the Scene, a die 
is rolled, and if it is even, the Wound remains, while if it is 
odd, it can be healed immediately, since the journey was 
not so tiring. The Seven-league Boots cannot be used for 
short-distance travel, but only for journeys that take the 
wearer a significant distance from where they are: for ex-
ample, one step can take the wearer across town. Wearing 
the Seven-league Boots increases foot speed and allows the 
wearer to run at the pace of a horse, gaining 1 Additional 
Success on all Checks where speed of movement is import-
ant.

23
The Badalisk
Inspired by the Legend of Santa Claus
Once upon a time, there was a strange creature called the 
Badalisk that lived in the woods north of the Kingdom of Italy. 
Worshipped as a god by the local people, the Badalisk would 
often descend into the small villages to eat the offerings the 
commoners made to it. The Badalisk had a big mouth and an 
insatiable appetite, but it was content with the offerings and 
the attention it received. It soon became a constant and pop-
ular presence among the locals. One day, a curious child got 
too close to the Badalisk while it was eating its offerings, but 
the creature only had eyes for food. A naive greeting turned 
into a tragedy when the Badalisk accidentally devoured the 
child in one bite. The townspeople were horrified and grabbed 
their pitchforks, but by then the taste of fresh meat had in-
toxicated the Badalisk, and it began to eat everything and 
everyone. When the Order’s Hunters arrived, summoned by 
the few survivors of the massacre, they found nothing but a 
large sack of red cloth. Finally satiated, the Badalisk had gone 
into hibernation, taking the shape of a sack. It is said that the 
monster slept for a hundred years before waking from hunger.
Keywords: Gift / Devour / Hunger / Bite / Set aside / Sack
Gift - Devourer: The Badalisk can hold an infinite num-
ber of objects and creatures. A Hunter can use an action to 
place objects and creatures up to the size of a horse into the 
sack and then remove them later. While inside the Badalisk, 
whatever is placed inside has no weight or bulk. The Badal-
isk is often reluctant to spit out its contents, and to retrieve 
it a Hunter must pass a Position Check with OL Medium 
5. If they fail, they must wait until the next Scene to try 
removing an object again, and if they obtain an Outcome 
with a Cost, they will take 1 Wound from the Badalisk’s bite. 
The Badalisk can also be used as a weapon: by holding it 
bottom up, a Hunter can grab their enemies with its large, 
toothed mouth. When an attack action results in an Out-
come with an Increment, it can be spent on one of the fol-
lowing special effects:
1. Inflict 1 Additional Wound to the target.
2. Swallow the target, trapping it inside the Badalisk.
A subject imprisoned in the Badalisk can actively attempt 
to get out in any Scene, depending on their abilities.
NPCs with Easy OL 3:  They are unable to free them-
selves.
NPCs with OL Medium 5:  They will free themselves 
during the Scene following the one in which they were 
swallowed.
NPCs with OL Hard 7, Dark Presences, and Bro-
ken Fairy Tales in general:  They will free themselves 
on their turn to act in the Exchange, consuming their ac-
tion.
Hunters: They will need to make a Position Check at OL 
Medium 5. 
The Badalisk

24
The Two Watching RavensInspired by Huginn and Muninn of Norse mythology.
Once upon a time, the god Odin traveled the world to carry 
out his plans, always accompanied by two faithful Ravens. 
Huginn and Muninn were the eyes of the deity in the world, 
and every morning they took flight to observe creation, re-
turning at sunset to tell their master all they had seen. But 
the day came when the gods fell, killed by an unknown ene-
my, and the two poor animals were orphaned of their master 
and their purpose. The two Ravens became wooden statues, 
waiting for a new master eager to observe the world.
Keywords: Raven / Observe / Animal companion / Truth 
/ Bird / Wisdom
Gift - Eyes on the World: The two Ravens are made en-
tirely of wood, but are capable of flight. Careful observation 
will reveal their magical nature. The Ravens follow their 
master and can perform small tasks, such as delivering a 
message or standing guard and squawking 
in case of danger. There is no limit to the 
distance the Ravens can fly from their 
master. Spending 1 Soma can activate 
one of the following special effects:
1. See through the Ravens’ eyes for 
the rest of the Scene.
2. Use your own Gift through the 
Ravens, who become the source 
of the effect. The Storyteller 
will determine if a particular 
Gift can be used in this way. 
3. Call the Ravens back to you immediately, no matter 
how far away they are.
If a Raven suffers 1 Wound it will fall asleep, becoming ac-
tive again only after an Interlude. 
Special Effect (Loki the Deceiver): Infused with Odin’s 
own power, the two Ravens can easily see through Loki’s 
deceptions. His Gift - Thousand Faces has no effect on the 
Ravens, and in the sight of the one who commands them, 
the Trickster will appear as he truly is.

The Mirror of the Other Self
25
The Mirror of the Other Self
Inspired by the Snow Queen
Once upon a time, there was a Troll who was very skilled 
at forging wondrous magical objects. Obsessed with being 
the best of all, the Troll created a mirror capable of reversing 
the qualities of those who saw its reflection. In this way, he 
thought, he would be able to handicap his rivals and purge 
the market of craftsmen who could compete with him. But 
while basking in his dreams of glory, the foolish Troll mistak-
enly looked into the mirror, and when he saw his reflection, 
he became incapable of any art. No longer knowing any bet-
ter, he struck his creation with a hammer, shattering it and 
with it the chance to break the curse. Mad with rage, the Troll 
fled far away in search of a witch to help him. That night, the 
young apprentice who regularly cleaned the Troll’s workshop 
arrived. The young man knew about the mirror and was the 
first to find its shards. Unfortunately, the young man was ab-
solutely clueless in the manual arts, but he was very clever. 
When he saw himself reflected in a shard of the mirror, he 
was robbed of his cleverness. He collected all the broken pieces 
and threw them into the garbage instead of keeping them for 
himself and making a fortune from them. It is said that even 
today the mirror’s shards are jealously guarded only by those 
who understand its true power.
Keywords: Inversion / Flaw / Preciousness / Craftsman / 
Mirror / Curse
Gift - Fragments of the Other Self: Those who obtain 
this Gift receive 4 Shard Tokens at the beginning of the Sce-
nario. During a Scene, they can spend 1 Token to activate 
one of these effects:
1. Reverse the Downside of one of their Descriptors into 
its positive counterpart for the rest of the Scene. Using 
the Descriptor in this way confers 1 XP (maximum 1 
per Downside per Scene).
2. Reverse the Descriptor of a Minor NPC for the rest of 
the Scene.
3. Activate a Hunter’s Dark Ego without using the Acti-
vator. 
Special  Effect  (Miss Snow and Lady Storm) : The 
power of the Mirror is always active on you, pulsing in your 
eye until it hurts. When someone spends 1 Shard Token, 
you can activate your Dark Ego while ignoring its Activator.

Karakasa Umbrella
26
Karakasa 
Umbrella
Inspired by the Yokai of Japanese folklore
Once upon a time, there was a strange umbrella that no one 
wanted to use. It was not a particularly bad umbrella, nor 
was it defective, but for some inexplicable reason, those who 
held it felt seized by a strange uneasiness. Neglected by every-
one, the umbrella was eventually forgotten in a dusty cellar. 
After more than a hundred years of loneliness, the umbrella 
came to life because, as some legends of ancient Japan tell us, 
an object over a hundred years old deserves a life of its own. 
Free to roam the world, the Karakasa Umbrella sought an 
owner who would enjoy using it.
Keywords: Misfortune / Persecution / Umbrella / Animat-
ed object / Spirit / Restlessness
Gift - Cursed Umbrella:  The unfortunate owner of the 
Karakasa Umbrella is haunted by this strange object that 
can move by hopping on its “leg” handle. The Umbrella 
cannot be destroyed in any way and always finds its way 
back to its master within minutes. Whoever owns the Um-
brella will receive the Special Descriptor: The Karakasa 
Umbrella follows me like a faithful dog, frightening ordinary 
people who observe its eerie gait. 
This Descriptor can be used as a Downside during the Scene. 
During frantic actions, the Karakasa Umbrella jumps around its 
master, trying to be helpful. For each Check made by the Hunter, 
it is mandatory to roll 1 die:
1-2: The Umbrella is in the Hunter’s way, inflicting 1 Penalty.
3-6: The Umbrella is helpful to the Hunter, who receives 1 Ad-
ditional Success.
Once per Session, the Hunter can open the umbrella in an at-
tempt to stop a threat. Doing so allows you to ignore 1 Wound 
taken or give everyone behind the umbrella 2 Additional Suc-
cesses on any Defense Check. Once opened, the Umbrella can’t 
close itself easily, and its owner will be able to escape from it for 
an hour or two.

The 
M
agic 
B
room
27
The Magic Broom
Inspired by the legends about witches
There are objects that have nothing special 
about them, yet become magical in the eyes of 
ordinary mortals. One of these is the broom: an 
object found in every home, but which, through 
a series of incredible coincidences, became asso-
ciated with the myth of witches. Brooms became 
tools used in Sabbaths and in magic, and grad-
ually became imbued with power through pop-
ular belief. To this day, there are scholars who 
believe that there is a spark of magic in every 
broom, but only those in touch with the super -
natural can harness that power.
Keywords: Broom / Witch / Sabbath / Flying 
/ Witchcraft / Magical focus
Gift - Magic Tool: Those who wield a Mag-
ic Broom receive a significant boost to their 
special abilities. The Broom can be used in a 
variety of ways.
1. When using a Gift that requires a Soma 
cost, if the owner of the Broom uses it 
meaningfully in their action, they can re-
duce the cost by 1 to a minimum of 0, at 
the Storyteller’s discretion.
2. The Broom is able to accumulate magi-
cal power that manifests in its presence. 
Each time a spell is cast, the Broom gains 
1 Power Token, up to a maximum of 3. 
During the Scene, spending 1 Power To-
ken can replicate the effect of the magic 
that generated the Token. For this reason, 
it is a good idea to keep track of the spells 
cast in the Broom’s presence. However, 
using magic in this way is very dangerous. 
When 1 Power Token is spent, roll a die: 
on a 6, the Broom releases an overload 
energy discharge, causing 1 Wound to 
its wielder. Afterward, it cannot be used 
again until the next Interlude.

The Tinderbox of Second Chances28
The Tinderbox of Second Chances
Inspired by the Magic Tinderbox
Once upon a time, there was a Soldier who, returning from war, 
met a Witch standing in front of a large, hollow tree. The Witch 
asked him to help her retrieve an old Magical Tinderbox from 
inside the tree in exchange for a large reward. Intrigued by the 
old woman, the Soldier agreed and entered into the tree. It is 
not known what he saw, but when he emerged, the man’s hair 
and beard were completely white from fear. Despite the terror 
he apparently experienced, the Soldier had managed to retrieve 
the Magic Tinderbox. However, instead of a simple reward, he 
asked to keep the object for himself in exchange for what he had 
endured in the cursed tree. The woman refused and admonished 
the Soldier, “You are a fool. You would have been wiser to ask 
for gold. ” Outraged by her insults and made heartless after years 
at war, the Soldier stabbed her to death with his sword. After 
robbing the Witch’s corpse of her possessions, the man tried the 
Tinderbox and, as if by magic, found himself back in front of the 
tree with the revived old woman. Understanding that the power 
of the Tinderbox was to rewind time, the Soldier marveled at the 
thought of all the money he could make with such a trick. But 
when he saw the Witch’s cold expression, he realized that only 
those who understand magic and evil spells should deal with 
them. “You are a fool. You would have been wiser to ask for 
gold, ” were the Witch’s last words to him once more. Before he 
could cut her down again, a giant demon in the shape of a dog 
appeared and took off her head with a single bite before leaping 
at the Soldier’s throat.
Keywords: Time / Hound / Second chance / Tinder / Sum-
moning
Gift - Second Chance:  At the beginning of the Scenar -
io, there are 3 Second Chance Tokens in the Tinderbox. By 
spending 1 Token you can activate one of these effects.
1. Re-do a failed Check. Time rewinds a few moments, giving 
the opportunity to repeat the Check. Soma used in the 
previous Check is not regenerated. It is possible, howev-
er, to modify the action based on the information gained 
from the previous Check in order to lower the OL or take 
advantage of other benefits. After the second Check, the 
Storyteller rolls a die: on a 5-6, you get 1 Hound Token.
2. Spend 1 Second Chance Token to use 1 Hound Token and 
hurl the Demonic Hound at an enemy.
The Storyteller can spend 1 Hound Token during the Scenar-
io to make a Demonic Hound appear, which will attack the 
Hunter to punish them for playing with time. If the being is 
summoned through the second effect of this Gift, the Hunter 
will decide the target of the Hound.
Demonic Hound
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a huge, demonic dog hunting my prey until 
I devour them.

The Shield of Arimathea
29
There are many relics that the Church craves, not 
only because they are weapons to be used against 
the darkness, but also because some have significant 
religious value. Anything that belonged to a saint 
brings strength and pride to the Church. The Shield 
of Arimathea is said to have belonged to a brave pal-
adin guardian of the Holy Grail. Whether the legend 
is true or not, the Shield of Arimathea shines with 
its own light, and whoever wields it feels a noble 
knight’s irrepressible instinct to defend the weak, as 
befits a saint.
Keywords: Sacred / Knight / Relic / Defend the 
weakest / Faith / Crusade
Gift - Shield of the Weakest:  When wielded, 
the Shield of Arimathea emits a holy light that 
can illuminate a room as if it were daylight. The 
light is of divine origin and is a clear sign of the 
artifact’s holiness. The effect confers 1 Addition-
al Success in all interactions with believers and 
churchmen. Even for those who are hostile to the 
Church, the origin of the Shield will be very clear. 
By spending 1 Soma, the owner can take 
1 Wound in place of a defenseless NPC or 
Hunter with fewer Wounds left.    
This effect cancels the original target’s Wound in a flash of holy light. 
For each Wound received in this way, the wearer of the Shield gains 1 
Additional Success in any action taken to rescue helpless people until 
the end of the Scene.
The Shield of Arimathea
Inspired by the Arthurian Myth

The Threads of Destiny
30
The Threads of 
Destiny
Inspired by Chinese tradition
Once upon a time, there was a Young Man who was obsessed 
with finding a wife. During his search, he saw an unhappy Old 
Man separating red Threads at the foot of a large temple. The 
Young Man asked why he was so sad. With a sigh, the Old Man 
answered that a war had just ended in which many soldiers 
had died. The red Threads were the destinies that could no lon-
ger be fulfilled. Each of them led to someone who would never 
find their beloved again, stolen by the cruelty of war. The Young 
Man begged the Old Man to give him a Thread so that he could 
find the matching soul and marry her. But the elder flatly re-
fused. His work was in the name of God Himself, and the rules 
of destiny could not be broken. Fueled by great anger, the Young 
Man struck him with a club and fled with the Threads in his 
hand. Once safe, he chose ten Threads, put one on each finger, 
and sat down to wait for destiny to take its course. Ten young 
girls arrived at once, each obsessed with the idea of having 
the Young Man as her husband. He offered to choose the most 
beautiful, but the girls would not listen to reason, each wanting 
the man for themselves. They each grabbed hold of him and 
began to pull until each had her piece of the bridegroom.
Keywords: Bonds / Destiny / Help / Finding each other / 
Teamwork / Companions
Gift - Red Bond:  Whoever obtains this Gift receives 4 
Thread Tokens at the beginning of the Scenario. Consuming 
1 Thread Token will create a bond between two subjects. Ei-
ther can avail themselves of these effects:
1. Know precisely the location of the other.
