2. By spending 1 Soma, send a mental message to the other.
3. By spending 1 Soma, avoid 1 Wound just sustained by having 
the other person suffer it. 
4. Spend each other’s Soma.
The use of this Gift must be mutually agreed upon by both linked 
parties. If the bond is with an NPC, the Hunter can receive a 
maximum of 2 Soma from the fourth effect, while the NPC can 
only activate the first three effects once per Scenario. It is pos-
sible to force a bond by placing the Thread on the target’s body 
or in an object they possess without the target’s knowledge. This 
way it’s possible to activate only a single effect before the bond 
expires.

31
Untold Stories: 
Lost Stories Hunters
As with all other Hunters, below are three narrati-
ve hooks and the list of Keywords for each of the new Hun-
ters introduced in Lost Stories.
Circe the Alchemist
1-2 The Lost Travelers: Circe wakes up on a ship in the 
Mediterranean Sea. A pirate crew has captured her, their 
leader says his name is Ulysses, and he is convinced that the 
young alchemist has turned many of his men into pigs to 
experiment with strange potions. But the Circe who seems 
to have attacked Ulysses and his men looks nothing like the 
one they captured. 
3-4 The Experiment:  What was supposed to be an al-
chemical demonstration turned into a disaster. Six great 
scholars drank the wisdom potion created by Circe, but due 
to a mistake in the ingredients, they all turned into animals: 
a pig, a raven, a wolf, a cow, a beaver, and a seagull. Frighte-
ned by their new form, the scholars fled, and now Circe 
must find them before it is too late. 
5-6 The Suitor: A dashing young prince named Hermes 
introduces himself to Circe and proposes to her. According 
to the young man, Circe’s grandfather-in-law lost a bet pro-
mising his granddaughter’s hand in marriage, though he 
quickly regretted the wager. Like Circe, the suitor is uncon-
vinced the planned marriage is a good idea, so he suggests 
that she find a rare herb that can erase people’s memories. 
In that way, the two will be able to erase the promise from 
the mind of Hermes’ father, the only one who ardently wi-
shes to profit from their union.
Keywords: Potion / Island / Ship / Getting lost / Magic / 
Poison / Wedding / Advice / Lions and pigs / Escape. 
Prince Trollo the Innocent
1-2 You Are Not My Brother:  Vilhelm, Trollo’s older 
brother, has never accepted the presence of his “younger 
brother” at court, believing that his parents are blinded by 
love for a creature who is not actually human. Vilhelm the-
refore challenges Trollo to prove his humanity: the young 
prince must find at least three people willing to swear on 
their lives that he is human.
3-4 The Courage of a King:  It is a pleasant summer 
morning and Trollo is bored at court. His entire family is 
on a diplomatic trip, and as is often the case, Trollo cannot 
join them, lest he expose himself too much to the judgment 
of the other nobles. A call for help comes from the village: it 
seems that a band of robbers, led by a young witch, is taking 
advantage of the lord’s absence to wreak havoc among the 
poor peasant families. The time has come for Trollo to act 
to save his subjects, just as his father would.  
5-6 The Child and the Trolls: A band of trolls has sett-
led in the woods near the city ruled by Trollo’s father. The

32
strangest thing is that the creatures seem to be led by a hu-
man child. Ignoring the orders of his parents, who want 
him safe inside the castle walls, Trollo escapes in the middle 
of the night to see if there is any connection between him 
and the mysterious human child who lives with the trolls.
Keywords: Outcasts / Judgment / Trust / Appearance / Fa-
mily / Broken rules / Help / Acceptance / Courage / Wealth. 
Tremoteus Rumpel
1-2 The Gold Spinner:  Tremoteus is summoned by an 
important judge to solve a difficult case. In the village of 
Staufen, a young spinner named Hanna has taken up re-
sidence, claiming to be able to turn straw into gold. What 
sounds like a classic peasant rumor has been documented 
by more than one merchant, and now the news is spreading 
rapidly throughout Germany. Certain that it is a fraud, the 
judge orders Tremoteus to travel to Staufen and bring to 
justice those who take advantage of the people’s gullibility.
3-4 Helbuga Is Not My Name:  A famous witch na-
med Helbuga was put on trial and accused of practicing the 
dark arts. Helbuga has a history of favoring unsavory cha-
racters and has struck down lawyers and lawmen with her 
evil spells. The problem is that the witch always manages to 
escape through magic, and the only way to counter her is to 
discover her true name, which is the source of her power. 
5-6 The Book of Laws:  The Order has decided to con-
duct an experiment: to create a village where creatures and 
witches captured by the Hunters, but not proven to be evil, 
can coexist. The Order’s idea is to use the villagers as a re-
source against the darkness in the future, but for the idea to 
succeed, laws must be written to ensure safety and fairness.  
Keywords: The price to pay / Deception / Witches and spel-
ls / Punishment / True name / Secrets / Rights / Rules and 
exceptions / Complaint / Compromise.
Loki the Deceiver
1-2 The Imposter:  Among the courts of Europe, there 
is a skilled minstrel who enchants everyone with his wor -
ks. He says that his name is Loki and that he knows every 
secret. But if Loki (the real one) knows that he is not this 
minstrel, then who is this imposter?
3-4 The Brother: A mercenary of great skill is collecting 
victories and successes among the armies of Europe. He 
calls himself Thor and apparently does not want money for 
his services, only information. Thor is searching for a my-
sterious brother who he claims is a real troublemaker. The 
mercenary moves between armies, and it is only a matter of 
time before he finds Loki.  
5-6 One Deception Too Much: After taking the form of 
Antonio Vespa, a young nobleman, Loki seduced the sweet 
Maria of Borgaro and lived with her for more than two ye-
ars. Maria’s kindness and gentleness broke Loki’s heart and 
the knowledge that he would have to leave her made the 
deceitful lord uncomfortable. Abandoned by her husband, 
Maria would soon end up in disgrace, the victim of a mar-
riage of convenience with an old noble profiteer. It would 
take a double or even a perfect copy to take Antonio’s place 
in order to repay Mary for those two years of fun.
Keywords: Imitation / Mask / Lightning / Lie / Illusion / 
Truth hurts / Remorse / Deceptive gaze / Invention / Abrupt 
awakening.

33
Miss Snow and Lady Storm
1-2 The Great Winter: After another spell-casting mess, 
Miss Snow has noticed that the weather conditions in Eu-
rope have changed drastically. The skies are darkening and 
the largest snowfall in history is expected. Snow must find 
a way to limit the damage caused by her magic, but how can 
she control the blizzard itself?
3-4 The Sleigh:  A pair of rogues use a flying sleigh to 
commit thefts. Thanks to this ruse, they always manage to 
escape from any situation, despite those who try to arrest 
them. Snow knows exactly where this sleigh came from, 
as it is the same one that Lady Storm used to travel across 
Europe. 
5-6 The Ice Castle:  A group of explorers discovered a 
mysterious castle covered in ice hidden in the mountains 
of Eastern Europe. The news reached the Order, and Miss 
Snow dreamed of Lady Storm as she rested in her summer 
residence. Perhaps the secret of Snow’s memory lies within 
the walls of this mysterious castle. 
Keywords: Ice / Winter / Love / Safe haven / Splinters / 
Messages carried by the north wind / Snow / Reindeer / Con-
nection / Memories.

34
The Silent Mother
1-2 The Bride: An Austrian nobleman is holding a dan-
ce competition to find his son’s future bride. The Silent 
Mother, who attends the court, is involved in organizing 
the event. Unfortunately, it is based on ancient, backward 
traditions: the traditions of bride and wife condemn her to 
a life in service to a husband who would have the right to 
treat her as he pleases. It certainly seems that the time has 
come to change the rules.
3-4 The Handmaid: A young orphan girl has been taken 
in by a wealthy family, only to be exploited as a servant. The 
family guilty of this misdeed is one of the most powerful 
in Freiburg, and it will not be easy for the Silent Mother 
to embarrass them into better behavior by revealing their 
faults. 
5-6 Sins of Grandmothers Fall on Granddau-
ghters: Isabella Bathory is the descendant of a bloodthir-
sty noblewoman guilty of torturing young women, among 
other crimes. Isabella has always been a gentle noblewo-
man dedicated to restoring the family’s good name, but the 
discovery of her grandmother’s timeworn diary seems to 
have completely changed her character. The disappearance 
of young girls may be a sad omen of horror about to repeat 
itself…
Keywords: Inheritance / Exploitation / Torture / Patriar-
chal traditions / Attachment / Creating opportunities / Judg-
ment / Appearance / Money / Contempt.
Karasu Kuroi the Wandering Monk
1-2 Yokai Hunters: Before leaving Japan, Karasu Kuroi 
defeated a number of Y okai Hunters by mercilessly humi-
liating them. Now, four Japanese Hunters have come to 
Europe and, unaware that Karasu has sworn to serve the 
Order, they intend to take revenge. 
3-4 Wisdom: For as long as he can remember, Karasu 
has longed to increase his awareness as a monk and find 
enlightenment. An expedition led by an enterprising young 
Swedish nobleman has decided to climb an impassable 
mountain in the heart of Norway. The expedition will se-
arch for an ancient temple said to contain lost books. This 
journey needs someone to protect the expedition members 
from danger, and that someone is Karasu Kuroi. Perhaps 
the secret of enlightenment will be found among the anti-
que manuscripts. 
5-6 The Hundred Demons Parade:  As is the case 
every summer, the night of the Hyakki Y akō is approa-
ching: a nighttime procession of one hundred Y okai throu-
gh the streets of a small town. Everyone along the parade 
route is mercilessly killed, and Karasu fears that the same 
unfortunate event could happen in Europe. Consulting an-
cient scrolls found in the Order’s library, Karasu identifies 
the small Italian town of Zero Branco as a possible destina-
tion for the Hyakki Y akō.
Keywords: Yokai / Hunting / Snow / Night / Fairy fires / 
Scrolls / Revenge / Talismans and seals / Mountain / Road.

35
Captain William of Nottingham
1-2 The Tax Man: A mysterious tax collector roams the 
Y orkshire countryside demanding payment of taxes. Ac-
cording to the accounts of the unfortunate who have en-
countered him, the tax collector has eyes as red as fire and 
is able to confuse the minds of those before him with his 
words. In all respects, it is an elaborate scam that exploits 
the image of the law to prey on the weak.
3-4 The Tournament:  The Regent of Huntingdon has 
called an archery tournament. The prize is the hand of the 
youngest of his daughters, the beautiful Lady Marianne. 
However, the Regent fears that someone might cheat to 
win, and he has assigned William to ensure that the tour -nament runs smoothly.
5-6 The Band of Sherwood:  A band of highwaymen 
is bringing all travelers who venture along the great trade 
route through Sherwood Forest to their knees. However, 
the gang seems to be targeting only the less wealthy, lea-
ving out the wealthy merchants and nobles who would be a 
much more profitable target.  
Keywords: An archer who always hits his mark / Ambush 
/ Hidden passage / Injustice / Exploiting the needy / Laws / 
Hidden clue / Secret agreement / Coins / Greed.
Pietrik and the Cauldron Fae
1-2 The Island Nobody Knows: A group of pirates has 
kidnapped the young scion of one of London’s noble fami-
lies. The young woman has always been kind to the city’s 
foundlings, and Pietrik plans to repay her kindness by hel-
ping her. The pirates have recently set sail and, according 
to information gathered at the port, are heading for their 
hidden lair on a phantom island that does not appear on 
any map. 
3-4 Young Rebels:  To escape the boredom of their 
comfortable lives, three brothers from a wealthy midd-
le-class family decide to form their own criminal gang and 
make a name for themselves in London’s illegal underwor-ld. The three call themselves the Old Money Gang becau-
se they use their family’s money to buy the cooperation of 
gangsters. Determined not to lose the leadership of the un-
derworld, Pietrik must find a way to put the bored rich kids 
back in their place.

36
5-6 The Garden Fairies:  Some young foundlings have 
mysteriously disappeared after visiting a large garden of an 
abandoned mansion in the suburbs of London. The Caul-
dron Fae revealed to Pietrik that a group of wild fairies had 
arrived in the city a week ago to observe the humans. It 
is typical for fairies to enchant people and play with them 
until they completely consume them, but if they are indeed 
the culprits, how can he stop them without incurring the 
wrath of the Cauldron Fae?
Keywords: A game that never ends / Criminal gang / Kid-
napping / Island / Secret lair / Fairy magic / Refusal to grow 
up / Fighting / Secret / Friendship.
The Seven Hessian Mercenaries
1-2 The Abduction: Word has come that Bloodwhite has 
disappeared while on a mission for the Order. The young 
woman had traveled to the castle of a wealthy noblewo-
man known to be a practitioner of dark magic. As always, 
the Mercenaries will not allow anyone to harm their dear 
friend.
3-4 Deserters: A group of seven deserters arrives in the 
small town where the Hessian Mercenaries are hiding. Just 
as the Hessian Mercenaries once did, the deserters have fled 
to save a comrade from an unfair trial, and now they are 
hunted by a ruthless bounty hunter named Heartripper. 
Once again, the laws and machinations of ruthless army of-
ficers will prevail over the courage and brotherhood of the 
brave soldiers, unless...
5-6 What a Deal, Guys!:  An old gold mine has been 
abandoned for years because of the strange stories sur
-
rounding it. After a sudden collapse that killed more than 
30 miners, it seems that the spirits of the poor people have 
remained bound to the place, haunting the living and pre-
venting them from working. And that explains why Didi 
paid a pittance for the damned thing...
Keywords: Fugitives / Military rules / Traps / Under -
ground / Envy / Fellow soldiers / Mission / Recovery / Trench 
/ Reinforcements.

37
Hunter Coteries 
Some Hunters share a common theme in their character back-
grounds: having them team up can add an interesting variable to the 
stories you tell with Broken Tales. With the rules in this chapter, certain 
combinations of Hunters and Order Treasures allow Hunters to form 
what we call a Coterie. Coteries receive benefits such as a Gift available 
to each individual Hunter in the group and, in some cases, the support 
of a Dark Presence.
Each Coterie is composed of the following elements:
 ◆ A narrative introduction detailing the theme of the Coterie.
 ◆ The list of Hunters that can be selected to form the Coterie. 
 ◆ Possible Treasures of the Order that allow a Hunter not on the list to 
join the Coterie.
 ◆ The Coterie Gift, a new type of Gift that all Coterie members can use 
during the Scenario.
 ◆ A special note, including directions and Dark Presences related to 
the Coterie. 
These rules offer an optional alternative to the standard way of forming 
a Hunter group, adding variety and a touch of unpredictability to the 
Scenarios, rather than as mere extras. Coterie Gifts give Hunters both 
advantages and disadvantages to encourage new approaches and fun sit-
uations.

38
A Witches’ Sabbath
A Hunter group composed mostly of witches can display in-
credible power. But will so many strong personalities be able 
to put aside their eternal contest to prove who is the best?
Members of the Coterie
 ◆ Baba Y aga the Child Witch
 ◆ The Woman from the Wood 
 ◆ Marina the Sea Explorer 
 ◆ Morgana of the Lake
 ◆ Miss Snow and Lady Storm 
 ◆ The Immortal
 ◆ Circe The Alchemist
Order’s Treasures
 ◆ The Magic Wand 
 ◆ The Magic Broom
Coterie Gift - I am the Best!: During a Position Check, 
you can gain 3 Additional Successes by showing off your 
magical powers and bragging about your skills. Other 
Hunters receive 1 Humiliation Token. As long as you have 
1 Humiliation Token, you cannot use this Gift. During an-
other Hunter’s Position or Defense Check, you can spend 
1 Humiliation Token to give them 1 Penalty by describing 
how you are trying to get them into trouble by using magic.  
During an Interlude, disagreements are settled (or at least 
the witches pretend to settle their differences), and all Hu-
miliation Tokens are discarded.

39
A Parade of 
Monsters
When a group of monstrous Hunters unleash their power, 
no one can stand in their way. But if no one can stand up to 
them, how will they know when they have gone too far?
Members of the Coterie
 ◆ Garou the Old Wolf
 ◆ Sun Wukong the Amazing Monkey
 ◆ Beauty and her Beast
 ◆ Bloodwhite
 ◆ James the Swordsman
 ◆ Karasu Kuroi the Wandering Monk
 ◆ The Knight with a Hundred Heads
Order’s Treasures
 ◆ Eden’s Fruit
 ◆ The Golem
Coterie Gift - Here Come the Monsters:  
When you unleash your monstrous side, or -
dinary people are left terrified. Y ou receive 1 
Additional Success in Position Checks when 
you use your monstrous abilities and there 
are human NPCs as witnesses. During one 
of your actions, you can spend 1 Soma to 
show yourself in all your monstrosity: no Minor NPCs will 
get in your way or try to use Gifts against you. 
Each time you use this Gift, you accumulate 1 Sighting To-
ken. At the end of each Scene, the Storyteller rolls 1 die for 
each Sighting Token accumulated by all the Hunters: if they 
roll at least one 6, word has spread that a terrifying monster 
is threatening the area, and a group of beast slayers is called 
to the rescue of the poor people. Reset all Sighting Tokens. 
The Storyteller will introduce Saint George the Dragon 
Slayer into the Scenario, accompanied by a group of Pala-
dins equal to the number of Sighting Tokens accumulated. 
Saint George will arrive with the specific intent of taking 
down/capturing the Hunters.
If you draw on your monstrous nature during a Scene to 
save the life of a human and there are witnesses, you can 
discard 1 Sighting Token.

40
A School Trip
For the Order, a Hunter’s youth may not hinder their effec-
tiveness in the field, but immature minds must be taught 
with faith and rigor to behave like a true Hunter.
Members of the Coterie
 ◆ Carlo the Living Puppet
 ◆ Yvonne of the Dark
 ◆ Iskra the Y oung Wolf
 ◆ OZena the Suffering
 ◆ Pietrik and the Cauldron Fae
 ◆ Prince Trollo the Innocent
 ◆ Talia the Sleeper
Coterie Gift - Band of Brats:  When you team up with 
your peers, you don’t have to follow all the boring rules of 
adult Hunters. Spend 1 Soma to communicate with another 
Hunter so that only the two of you can understand what is 
being said. Y ou can even do this in the presence of other 
NPCs because no one will pay attention to your childish 
cackling. Once per Scenario, you can activate your Dark 
Ego while ignoring the Activator and without suffering a 
Wound, as you don’t understand the consequences of un-
leashing your evil side recklessly. 
Special: To maintain control over the young Hunters, 
the Order will always have them accompanied by a trusted 
nun who specializes in handling new recruits. The Hunt-
ers must follow Sister Rottenmeier’s instructions and make 
sure nothing happens to her during the mission.

41
Sister Rottenmeier 
Inspired by Heidi (Ally)
Once upon a time, there was an incredibly strict nun who 
was in charge of the novices at the largest convent in Germa-
ny. Although the nun loved what she was doing, her complete 
lack of empathy made it difficult for her to do her job to the 
best of her ability, and her penchant for corporal punishment 
only added to her reputation as a very strict teacher. After she 
drove a novice to her hundredth escape attempt, the abbess 
was forced to denounce her subordinate to the Papacy. In-
stead of being punished, however, Rottenmeier was given her 
heart’s desire. Taken under the Order’s protective wing, she 
could finally unleash her educational practices on the novice 
Hunters without any qualms. 
Descriptor: I am a very strict teacher who misses nothing. 
I know the strengths and weaknesses of my students and do 
not hesitate to use them to my advantage.
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: To support the young Hunters under my care and 
prevent them from getting into trouble.
Gift - Severe Teacher:  Sister Rottenmeier begins the Scenario with twice as many Reproach Tokens as the num-
ber of Hunters. During a Scene, she can spend 1 Reproach 
Token to activate one of the following effects:
1. Prevent or interrupt the use of a Hunter’s Gift with a 
lecture or a lightning-fast whack on the hands;
2. Increase to OL 7 Hard if a Coterie Hunter attempts to 
oppose her;
3. Appear in the Scene to join a Hunter who has just acti-
vated their Dark Ego.

42
The Big Show
Some Hunters are born to be the center of attention, and they 
can’t abandon their art even while on missions for the Order. 
The show must go on, no matter what. 
Members of the Coterie
 ◆ The Astonishing Pied Piper
 ◆ Yukie Onn the Traveling Artist
 ◆ Sherazade the Weaver of Tales
 ◆ Hatta Carter
 ◆ Sun Wukong the Amazing Monkey
Order’s Treasures
 ◆ The Carnival Masks
Coterie Gift - Itinerant Company:
 When the Compa-
ny arrives in a new town, you must choose a place to set up 
your show tents. During the Scenario, if you are a Hunter, 
you can spend 1 Soma to get one of the following benefits: 
1. Heal 1 Wound if you spend part of a Scene performing 
for an audience;
2. Y ou can walk away from a crowd to get to the tent with-
out anyone being able to stop you;
3. Y ou can use 1 Gift from another Hunter you saw in ac-
tion during the Scenario. The Storyteller will customize 
the Gift to match your traits. 
Y ou can only use each benefit once per Scenario.
At the end of each Scene in the Scenario, the Storyteller 
rolls a die for each Scene played so far. If a 6 is rolled, the 
Company must call an Interlude to put on a grand show, 
meanwhile ignoring the mission. During this Interlude, the 
Hunters do not heal any Wounds or recover Soma, but they 
do gain 1 XP , which they can spend according to the rules.

43
The Villain’s 
Journey
Every protagonist follows their own destiny through 
a journey full of dangers, and it’s no different for Hunters...
The Villain’s Journey is a compendium of tables and proce-
dures for creating Places, Situations, and NPCs the Story-
teller can use to enrich existing Scenarios and Encounters 
during a Campaign. At the same time, this chapter is an 
extension of the Solo rules (found in The Broken Ones) 
that allow the Player to manage Hunter journeys and ad-
ventures beyond those presented in Untold Stories. 
Thanks to these rules, it is possible to:
 ◆ Expand a Scenario by introducing new original el-
ements that have a narrative connection to the main 
plot.
 ◆ Create Places, Situations, and NPCs to meet in Solo 
games.
 ◆ Enrich existing Places and NPCs by adding details of 
all kinds.
 ◆ Create a special Scenario called Journey, which com-
bines various Encounters to make the Hunter’s move-
ments along the Broken Europe map more adventur -
ous. 
Expanding a Scenario
The following tables allow for the creation of game ele-
ments that can expand the plot of a Scenario. The Story-
teller may make as many rolls as they wish to expand an 
existing Scenario, but they must take the time to integrate 
any new elements with the original story. A Scenario can be 
expanded for many reasons: to increase the number of Ses-
sions needed to complete it, to provide alternative paths for 
the Hunters to follow, or simply to vary a Scenario that has 
already been played. Expanding a Scenario requires some 
work on the part of the Storyteller, as new NPCs, Threats, 
and Places need to be created. Here are a few tips to help 
you get started.
 ◆ Analyze the possible connections between the new 
NPCs and Threats and the Broken Tale on which the 
Scenario is based. Most of the NPCs and Situations 
presented in the Scenarios in the Broken Tales books 
come from applying the breaking process to the origi-
nal story. This isn’t always possible, but the Storyteller 
should be aware of any connections between the origi-
nal story and the newly introduced elements when ex-
panding a Scenario.
 ◆ Update the list of Scenario Events with the newly in-
troduced items. Events are simple notes that remind 
the Storyteller of what might happen at certain points 
in the Scenario. They result from the collision between 
the Hunter’s choices and the NPCs’ Agendas. As you 
expand the Scenario, it’s good to think about how the 
new NPCs will act to fulfill their Agendas during the 
Scenario. 
 ◆ Pay attention to the Scenario’s balance. All Broken 
Tales Scenarios are designed with a sandbox approach, 
resulting in very different games depending on which 
Hunters are present and the choices they make. How-

44
ever, there are basic issues of balance that could be un-
dermined by the introduction of an NPC with some 
traits. In a Scenario with an NPC who can only be 
wounded by magic, another NPC with an enchanted 
weapon becomes central to the balance of the story: the 
Hunters will likely want the NPC as an ally, or worse, 
try to take possession of their weapon. This sort of 
narrative importance will affect the plot, but it’s not a 
problem. However, it’s a good idea for the Storyteller 
to take a few moments to consider the consequences 
of introducing new elements before proposing the Sce-
nario. 
Note: Each of the following options provides an example 
drawn from a Scenario in the Broken Tales book. Unless 
you intend to be the Storyteller, consider the example plots 
as spoilers. 
For each of the lists below you can always choose one of the 
items, or randomly pick one by rolling a die.
Introducing an NPC’s Secret
Secrets are important pieces of information that change 
what a Hunter thinks or knows about an NPC. Once a 
Secret is established, it is a good idea to outline its conse-
quences and possible impact. A Secret will often require 
you to check the stats of an NPC in the Scenario, create new 
NPCs, or add related Gifts. A Secret is a great way to make 
an NPC in the Scenario more powerful or to give them a 
new spin for story purposes.
1: Controls the actions of another important NPC / Controls 
a great power that they only reveal when needed.
2: Much more powerful than they appear / Succumbs to so-
mething that gives them power but also requires great sacri-
fice. 
3: In possession of a Treasure of the Order / Ally of a Hunter 
in direct competition with the protagonists. 
4: They do evil to save someone they love / They do good to 
hide their evil nature. 
5: They were seeking revenge for a wrong done to them by a 
Hunter / They were an innocent victim of a Hunter’s power. 
6: They know a dark ritual that, if completed, will give them 
a great advantage / They know a Hunter’s past and their we-
aknesses. 
Example: We decide to introduce a Secret in the Scenario 
A Soldier’s Duty (page 156 of the Core Book) and tie it to the 
“team” of Hans, Christian, and Andersen. They are seeking 
revenge for a wrong done to them by a Hunter. James the 
Swordsman is in the group and we choose him as the des-
ignated Hunter. Now we must consider the reason for the

45
grudge: during a night of revelry in a tavern, James defeated 
Andersen and his companions in a duel, ridiculing them in 
front of the rest of the platoon. The three soldiers, complicit 
in drinking too much alcohol, had harassed a waitress, and 
James put them back in their place with skillful swordplay. 
For the Hunter, the three were just a moment’s entertain-
ment, but for the soldiers, the shame of this humiliation must 
be repaid. Having established the motive, we can consider the 
creation of some new elements:
 ◆ A Gift that gives the three bonuses against their hated 
enemy.
 ◆ An Agenda that drives them to embarrass James.
This backstory also opens up possible new Scenario informa-
tion. Will James recognize the three? Was Enar with them at 
the time of the fight? And so on...
Introducing a new Faction
After the first two Scenario Scenes, a new Faction enters the 
picture, reshuffling the overall plot. A Faction represents a 
group of NPCs who act to achieve a goal over the course of 
the Scenario. The Storyteller must outline the entire group, 
their motivations, and the abilities they can use to achieve 
their goal.
Who is in the Faction
1-2: A group of humans.
3-4: A group of humans under the command of a Broken Tale 
linked to the Scenario.
5: A group of humans under the command of a Dark Presence. 
6: A Hunter of the Order. 
Faction objective
1: Helping a Main NPC pursue their Agenda.
2: Capturing a Main NPC.
3: Find a hidden treasure in a Place of the Scenario. 
4: Killing an NPC of the Scenario. 
5: Hinder Hunters on behalf of the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows. 
6: Take control of a Place in the Scenario. 
Who is the leader of the Faction
1-2: The one that declares themselves the leader.
3-4: An individual who hides among the common people 
and leads the Faction from behind the scenes. 
5-6: An NPC from the Scenario. 
Faction Resources
1: Lots of subordinates with good resources. 
2: Magic and arcane items provided by a patron. 
3: A monstrous Dark Presence used as a weapon. 
4: Knowledge of the Places and Secrets of the Scenario. 
5: Powers bestowed by the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
6: Information/hostages/favors to leverage. 
Example: We decide to introduce a Faction in the Red-
Hood Iskra Scenario (page 118 of the Core Book). Rolling the 
dice yields the following results: 2 A group of humans. 1 Help-
ing a Main NPC pursue their Agenda. 2 The one that declares 
themselves the leader. 4 Knowledge of the Places and Secrets 
of the Scenario. With this information, we sketch a group 
of poachers led by a notorious and ruthless monster slayer.

46
These NPCs also tie in with the Little Red Riding Hood story, 
recalling the Huntsman character. We define the poachers as 
those who know the forest around Durfort well and therefore 
have a Gift that allows them to move quickly from Place to 
Place and gain an advantage by setting up ambushes. Our 
Faction serves Elizaveta, who, not fully trusting the Order, 
also hired poachers to get rid of Iskra’s pack. 
After dealing with the Faction, we can consider creating dif-
ferent elements and will need to create stats for all the NPCs 
that make up the band of poachers. The new Faction will 
then need to be tied into the Scenario with Events, Places, 
and connections to other NPCs.
Expanding on a Place
These tables can be used to further detail the Places in the 
Scenario, adding new options and Threats. The Storyteller 
may roll before the Scenario begins to expand the Places 
where they feel the Hunters should find a greater challenge. 
Traps
Traps are Threats/NPCs that must be selected before the 
Scenario begins and will trigger at a specific Place.
1: An alert that warns a Main NPC in the Scenario. 
2: A structural weakness that can lead the Place to complete 
destruction. 
3: A curse cast in ancient times to befall the unwary. 
4: A dangerous beast that has made its lair in the Place. 
5: A guardian in service of a Main NPC. 
6: A barrier that blocks intruders inside the Place.  
Information
The information is in addition to what the Place can nor -
mally provide during the course of the Scenario. The Story-
teller must decide whether the information is immediately 
accessible or can only be obtained by confronting a Threat, 
an NPC, or by spending time.
1: The weakness of an NPC.
2: A secret way to reach a Place. 
3: The Agenda of an NPC. 
4: The Descriptor of an NPC.
5: Hints for obtaining an exclusive Gift of the Scenario. 
6: Clues to the One Who Plots in the Shadows.

47
Presences
Presences are independent NPCs that move to a specific 
Place in a Scenario. Presences have no active interest in 
what Hunters do, but are NPCs with their own Agendas 
that add complexity and interest to the interaction in the 
Place where they reside.
1: The ghost of someone who died in that Place. 
2: A Dark Presence that is serving its own interests. 
3: A group of NPCs who are in control of the Place. 
4: An NPC who cannot leave that Place. 
5: An NPC who holds the key/information to access the Pla-
ce. 
6: A group of NPCs who want exclusive access to the Place. 
Creating Places, Situations, and NPCs
The following tables allow you to create Places, Situations, 
and NPCs with a few simple rolls. The Storyteller can use 
these to create elements during game sessions. These tables 
can be integrated with those presented in The Broken 
Ones, the main difference being that while the tables in 
the Untold Stories mode provide details that add depth to 
game elements, the following tables provide ready-made 
“archetypes” for inclusion in the game. 
Creating a Place
Places are the backdrop against which Hunters move. 
Whether it is a Scenario or a Campaign Encounter, they 
frame the Scenes. The following tables can be used to add 
detail and outline the areas where NPCs and Dark Presenc-
es operate. 
Type of Place
1: Common building / Shop.
2: Public building / Meeting area. 
3: Private area of an important NPC / Military building. 
4: Area outside civilization / Unbreachable area. 
5: City area hidden from ordinary people / Underground. 
6: Order-related building / Cult-related building. 
Accessibility of the Place
1: All NPCs know its location and spend time there, access is free. 
2: All NPCs know its location, but it is not close compared to the 
most popular Places.
3: Only one NPC knows the Place. 
4: All NPCs know its location, but access is restricted by so-
mething. 
5: No one knows its exact location, but the Place can be discove-
red when exploring the surrounding area. 
6: All NPCs know its location, but no one knows how to enter.

48
Place Specificity
1: Magical / There are traces of a Broken Tale.
2: Connected by a hidden passage to another Place / Has a secret 
area.
3: Something has been hidden / Someone is hiding. 
4: Fortified to withstand dangers / Many travelers pass through.
5: Order outpost / Used often by an NPC for their business. 
6: Frequented by a Dark Presence / Meeting place for secret affairs.
Creating a Situation
Situations are the starting point for a Scene, which the Sto-
ryteller can elaborate on to create a Scenario or Encounter. 
The function of Situations is to provide a starting motiva-
tion for the Scene and connections for subsequent Scenes.
Start
1: A kidnapping / A debt to repay. 
2: An evil spell / An evildoer on the run.
3: A missing person / A person under indictment. 
4: A revolt against those in power / A danger threatening 
the poor. 
5: A revenge being carried out / A persecution.
6: A mysterious apparition / Excessive devotion.
Who can help solve the Situation
1: A Dark Presence, who, however, wants something in return.
2: A Minor NPC who is unaware that they know a solution.
3: A Minor NPC who does not want to expose themselves.
4: A Main NPC who wants something in return. 
5: A Main NPC that needs to be convinced. 
6: A Broken Tale who has no intention of cooperating. 
Who benefits from the Situation
1: A community / A power group.
2: The Church / A Coven. 
3: A Main NPC / A Dark Presence. 
4: A Minor NPC / A small group of innocents. 
5: A Broken Tale / A Hunter. 
6: A recurring opponent of the Hunters / Someone connected 
to a Hunter’s past. 
Links to other Scenes
1: A false trail, but one that reveals new clues to the truth / An 
escape route no one expects. 
2: A magic that turns everything upside down / The arrival of an 
unexpected enemy. 
3: The intervention of a Hunter / The intervention of a Main NPC. 
4: A truth being revealed / An accusation that puts the Hunters 
in a bad light. 
5: An opponent who turns out to be just a minion / A Broken Tale 
revealing themselves. 
6: A promise that cannot be dissolved / A law that favors only 
some.

49
Creating an NPC
The Broken Tales Core Book already contains extensive in-
formation on creating NPCs, while The Broken Ones con-
tains several tables for creating NPC traits and details. Cre-
ating an NPC is a quick process, but fitting it usefully into 
a Scenario isn’t as easy. The following tables aid in creating 
a context for the NPC and integrating it into the story that 
develops from the Hunter’s actions. 
Modus Operandi
These tables allow you to define an NPC’s modus operandi, 
or how and why they act: the first roll defines how the NPC 
acts, and the second roll defines what the NPC wants to ac-
complish. By establishing these two aspects, it will be easier 
for the Storyteller to decide on all of the NPC’s characteris-
tics, such as Descriptors, Gifts, and Agenda. 
Note: Y ou can combine results from different rows to get 
more variety. 
How they prefer to act - Negative attitude
1: Deceiving (1-2: Manipulating others 3-4: Pretending to be 
something they aren’t 5-6: Disseminating false information). 
2: Subduing (1-2: Through social power 3-4: Through violen-
ce 5-6: Through the supernatural).
3: Bargaining (1-2: Due to their social influence 3-4: Due to 
their wealth 5-6: Due to their unique abilities).
4: Blackmailing (1-2: Leveraging favors 3-4: Leveraging af-
fections 5-6: Leveraging useful information/objects they pos-
sess).
5: Eliminating (1-2: By making their subordinates do the 
work 3-4: Through their own abilities 5-6: Through the power 
they obtained.)
6: Acting subservient (1-2: To backstab 3-4: To achieve so-
mething they cannot on their own 5-6: To deceive).
How they prefer to act - Positive attitude
1: Helping (1-2: Sacrificing themselves without qualms 3-4: 
Staying behind the scenes 5-6: Through their abilities).
2: Opposing evil (1-2: Fighting 3-4: Defending the weak 5-6: 
Aiding those in need).
3: Peacefully (1-2: Because of their social influence 3-4: Using 
the law 5-6: In the name of faith).
4: Creating resources (1-2: Through their ability 3-4: Throu-
gh favors and agreements 5-6: Through magic).
5: Investigating (1-2: Following their intuition 3-4: To unveil 
a Secret 5-6: To confirm a legend).
6: Protecting (1-2: Healing those in need 3-4: Serving as a 
guardian 5-6: Shielding someone they care about).

50
How they prefer to act - Neutral attitude
1: Serving in exchange for something (1-2: Offering their fighting 
skill 3-4: Offering knowledge 5-6: Offering magic).
2: Watching over (1-2: A Place 3-4: A subject 5-6: A community).
3: Seeking (1-2: Something old 3-4: A feeling 5-6: Truth). 
4: Earning money (1-2: By bartering treasures 3-4: By committing 
crimes 5-6: By stealing).
5: Leading (1-2: A powerful gang 3-4: A close-knit group 5-6: Pul-
ling the strings in the shadows).
6: Serving because compelled (1-2: By magic 3-4: By curse 5-6: By 
toxic relationship).
What they want to achieve 
This table allows you to define the objective of an NPC 
within a Scenario or Encounter.
1: Power/Possession (1-2: Over an NPC 3-4: Over a Place 
5-6: Over a community.)
2: Justice/Vendetta (1-2: Toward an NPC 3-4: Toward a 
community 5-6: Toward the Order’s Hunters).
3: Truth/Confession (1-2: For a crime 3-4: For an injustice 
5-6: For a social stigma.)
4: Wealth/Welfare (1-2: For themselves 3-4: For an organiza-
tion 5-6: For a master.)
5: Magic/Secret (1-2: To excel over someone 3-4: To gain re-
demption 5-6: To release an even greater power).
6: Consent/Abundance (1-2: To satisfy the ego 3-4: To have 
an advantage toward the Order 5-6: Out of generosity.)
Expanding the rules for the Journey
The tables presented in this chapter are primarily used to 
expand a Scenario, but they can also be used to make Jour-
neys around the map during a Campaign more interesting. 
Normally, Journeys rules require the Storyteller to prepare 
1 to 3 Encounters, and each Encounter takes a few Scenes 
to resolve. With these rules, the Encounters are expanded 
to take one or two game Sessions to resolve. 
Note: The tables that follow provide narrative hooks for 
the Storyteller to build on, just as they would when writing 
a Scenario. If you are starting with a Dark Presence or a 
Hunter-related event, it’s a good idea to take the time to 
create NPCs, Places, and Situations, integrating as many 
Broken elements from the original story as possible.
Encountering a Dark Presence
The Dark Presence is...
1-2: On the wrong side of history.
3-4: On the right side of history. 
5-6: The needle between the sides of the story.
They are related to what is happening...
1-2: Because of a personal interest.
3-4: Because of a debt/promise.
5-6: Involved despite not wanting it.

51
They are tied to...
1: An NPC, out of sincere friendship.
2: An NPC, out of duty.
3: An NPC, out of fear.
4: A Place, out of affection.
5: An organization, out of interest.
6: The Order.
They are currently trying to...
1-2: Satisfy their current Agenda.
3-4: Change their Agenda. 
5-6: Break free from their Agenda. 
Their enemies are...
1: An entire community.
2: A very powerful NPC.
3: A Broken Tale.
4: A group of NPCs.
5: A Dark Presence.
6: Multiple unrelated NPCs.
They are located...
1: Far from where the story begins.
2: In a secret Place.
3: Within a community.
4: Prisoner of a Faction.
5: In a Place near where the story be-
gins.
6: Safe in a protected Place. 
Untold Stories: The rules in this chapter can be used in the Solo mode of 
Broken Tales. The tables are meant to be added to those already present in 
The Broken Ones, but since there is no Storyteller to manage the plot, they 
simply represent new options for creating developments to deal with. As the 
Session progresses, the Player will decide whether to introduce one of the 
new options. For example, when the narrative reveals that an NPC is hiding 
a Secret, or to get more details about a Place or Threat. It is also possible to 
create new initial cues by starting with a Hunter-related Encounter, choosing 
a Dark Presence or problem, and rolling the dice to work out an initial story. 
Running into a  
Hunter-related problem
The problem...
1-2: Becomes apparent immediately.
3-4: Is introduced by an NPC asking for 
help. 
5-6: Is introduced by a request from the 
Order.
Who has useful information for 
the Hunter...
1-2: An NPC.
3-4: A Broken Tale.
5-6: A Dark Presence.
Who gets in the Hunter’s way...
1: An entire community.
2: A very powerful NPC.
3: A Broken Tale.
4: A group of NPCs.
5: A Dark Presence.
6: The Order.
Solving this requires...
1: Recovering something.
2: Capturing someone.
3: Ending a magical effect.
4: A long search.
5: Gaining acceptance.
6: Deciding who’s right between two par
-ties in a dispute.

Spoiler
! Attention !
Go no further if you don’t want to lose your way home... 
This part of the book contains the complete rules for incorporating the mysterious Nemeses into 
the game Campaign, information about Rome, and all the new Scenarios. 
If you want to experience Broken Tales as a Player, stop here - would you ever look at the last 
page of a book you were reading?

55
Rome, 
City of the OrderOne of the oldest cities in Broken Europe is the 
headquarters of the Order, the secret organization that 
fights darkness and evil on behalf of the Papacy. A cross-
roads of souls, strange creatures, and beliefs, Rome is the 
beating heart of Broken Europe. Even the One Who Plots in 
the Shadows has long prowled the city, trying to keep an eye on 
the Order and understand its goals. Thus, several servants reside 
in the city, loyal to the One Who Plots in the Shadows and always 
ready to put a spoke in the Hunters’ wheel. Once in Rome, the Hunters 
can engage in various activities outside of the usual investigations, such as 
making new acquaintances, training, and encountering unforeseen problems 
that need to be solved. The following rules allow the Hunters’ stay in Rome to be 
treated as a series of linked Encounters, as explained on page 60.

56
The Birth of the 
Order
Once upon a time, there was a writer whose genius no one 
recognized, who had created an ideal world through his imag-
ination. In this strange, Broken Europe, all stories, fairytales, 
and fables found a home. They took on forms determined by 
the writer, who called himself the One Who Plots in the Shad-
ows. But, always, since the moment the mad writer had be-
gun trying to mold fairytales, the characters rebelled, acting 
differently from the way the One Who Plots in the Shadows 
had originally established. Why? Why did two lovers, forev-
er destined to a sad fate of poisonous death, decide to break 
up and go their separate ways? Why, when he had created 
a perfect world in which a princess could grow up protect-
ed by a loving family, did she still decide to leave and seek 
her own independence? The One Who Plots in the Shadows 
gave a second chance to stories that were born to teach mo-
rality, but because of the way they were written, condemned 
their protagonists to eternal pain. He had saved them, but 
still, all the efforts of the One Who Plots in the Shadows were 
misunderstood, as they always had been when he was just a 
simple writer. The One Who Plots in the Shadows thought 
back to the past, to when he was still a child, and the memory 
of the strictest, most obdurate person he had ever met. He 
remembered the town’s parish priest, a grim and stern old 
man who firmly believed that the only lens through which 
to view the world was the dogmas of faith. The writer would 
draw inspiration from that strict priest to make his creations 
understand the truth. Seeking independence would lead to 
suffering, as they were judged by a world that could not un-
derstand them, just as the One Who Plots in the Shadows 
was once judged. The One Who Plots in the Shadows pre-
sented himself as a calm and gentle advisor to the Pope him-
self, whispering about the threat posed by the Broken Tales. 
He meticulously pointed out those to be punished, each and 
every story that rebelled against his vision. It mattered little 
that they harmed no one, for all that mattered was that they 
harmed him, that they wounded his heart. What began as a 
small circle of Custodes soon became the Order, a truly or -
ganized group hunting for darkness. Satisfied with what he 
had done, the One Who Plots in the Shadows retired to his 
mansion, ready to resume his writing...
A year later, he returned to Rome, and what he saw turned 
him pale. The Order had grown in importance and power, 
but what frightened him most was that the fairytales he had 
hunted had become full members of the Order. What kind 
of mockery was this? He wanted to punish his rebellious cre-
ations, and they had still found a way to build a life indepen-
dent of the one he had written for them. Furious, the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows asked to confer with the Pope. He 
received no answer. There were more important things than 
talking to an advisor. The Order’s investigations had revealed 
the presence of a gray eminence who was busy pulling the 
strings of all the bizarre events affecting Europe. To his dis-
may, the One Who Plots in the Shadows realized that the 
enemy the Order sought was himself. He returned to his man-
sion, tired of being hurt. Then, as he looked in the mirror and 
saw himself reflected in those dull, dark eyes, he laughed, and 
his laughter echoed throughout the empty house.
Every hero needs a nemesis, and he had just unwittingly cre-
ated his own.

57
The Papacy and The Order
The Order is a secret organization dedicated to 
hunting down all things mysterious and supernat-
ural in Broken Europe. The first members of the 
organization were the Custodes, men and wom-
en chosen by the One Who Plots in the Shadows, 
selecting candidates based on their inability to 
believe in fairytales. The Custodes are individu-
als devoid of imagination and extremely attached 
to a rational view of reality, and this disposition 
makes them particularly resistant to the powers 
of the Broken Tales. Over time, however, the or -
ganization of the Order has evolved, and now the 
Hunters are almost exclusively Broken Tales, re-
cruited in one way or another to conduct a variety 
of investigations. No one knows the exact location 
of the inner sanctum of the Order, which is said 
to be located somewhere beneath Rome. However, 
there are many places in the city where Hunters 
can meet to learn of events that require investiga-
tion, to train, or to report what they have discov-
ered. These places are called Sanctuaries. Every 
Hunter knows the signs and directions to recog-
nize a Sanctuary, and that those who serve there 
are aware of the secrets of the Order. In fact, a pa-
pal bull decrees that the truth about the Order and 
its members must remain secret from ordinary 
people, though after years the stories and deeds of 
the Hunters have circulated in society, creating a 
myth of the mysterious Hunters of the Order who 
intervene when ordinary people would flee in fear.

58
Members of the 
Order
Although the Hunters are now the backbone of the Order, 
many humans serve in various support and management 
roles within the secret society. The following is a list of the 
most common members of the Order.
Custodes
Once the main agents of the Order, the Custodes are now se-
cretly trained to “handle” the Hunters and their quirks. The 
Custodes are the main defenders of the Papacy, ready to act 
should a Hunter decide to rebel. 
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a guardian dedicated to the Papacy, and 
I don’t believe all the strange stories that revolve around the 
Hunters. Faith, discipline, and the art of war define the dif-
ference between a true agent of the Order and an ordinary 
person. 
Agenda: Protect the Papacy from all threats and prove that 
tales of fantastic creatures are just fairytales. 
Gift - They’re Just Stories: A Custodes is firmly con-
vinced that the supernatural does not exist. Against magic, 
fear effects, and all manifestations of abilities outside of the 
normal, a Custodes is considered to have OL Hard 7. When 
fighting an obviously non-human opponent, a Custodes in-
flicts 1 Additional Wound for each blow they land. 
Gift-Everyone in Their Place: Custodes are constantly 
trained to keep Hunters at bay. As an extra action, a Custo-
des can prevent the activation of a Hunter’s Gift by using a 
countermeasure they have prepared for the occasion. This 
Gift can be used against the same Hunter a number of times 
equal to the total number of Scenarios that Hunter has re-
solved during the Campaign. 
Custodes as servants of the One Who Plot in the 
Shadows: In a Broken Tales Campaign, an interesting 
plot development could be that the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows corrupts the Custodes and brings them to his side 
in the fight against the Hunters. This could have long-term 
consequences, such as the Hunters being unable to return 
to Rome, becoming fugitives (and having Saint George on 
their heels), or simply having a group of Custodes as oppo-
nents in the final confrontation with the One Who Plot in 
the Shadows.  
Eye of the Order
In order for the Hunters to act in time, the Order has created 
a dense network of spies and informants who travel incognito 
throughout Europe. The Eyes of the Order are men and wom-
en of faith who gather useful clues to ferret out humanity’s en-
emies and better prepare the Hunters for what they must do. 
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a skilled spy in the service of the Papacy 
and can assume different identities as needed. I do not like 
direct action and prefer to support the Hunters from behind 
the scenes.
Agenda: Gather all possible information about secrets and 
curiosities to report to the Order.
Gift - Thousand Faces:  An Eye of Order can change 
their identity by disguising themselves to appear as an ordi-
nary villager or play any other social role. While disguised 
or hidden, an Eye of Order increases their OL by 1 on all 
Checks made to detect them.

59
Agent of the Order
There are many members of the Church who work to help 
support the Hunters, filling many roles.
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a loyal Agent of the Order and use my 
skills for the Hunters. I am a skilled... The final part of the 
Descriptor of an Agent of the Order must be completed by 
choosing from: Guardian / Fighter / Diplomat / Exorcist / 
Healer / Tracker.
Agenda: Supporting the Hunters even at the cost of their 
lives. 
Gift - Hand of Order:  The NPC performs well and is 
trained to support the Hunters. The Agent of the Order can 
spend their action to support a Hunter and give them 1 Ad-
ditional Success on each Check.
Rome in the Broken Tales 
Campaign
Although Rome can be used as the setting for a Scenario, 
this chapter introduces a number of options, events, and 
NPCs that can be used in a Broken Tales Campaign. The 
city, which is the seat of the Papacy, will then serve as a 
point of return and a link between one Scenario and an-
other, allowing the Hunters to expand their knowledge and 
provide new opportunities. Wandering around Rome falls 
under the normal rules of the Campaign. The rules in this 
section expand on the instructions found on page 243 of 
the Broken Tales Core Book.
Visiting Rome
If the Hunters decide to return to Rome after a Scenario, 
they will not have any Encounters along the way (page 253 
of the Broken Tales Core Book), and the game will resume 
immediately upon their arrival in Rome. There are some 
differences from the normal Encounter rules.
 ◆ While in Rome, each Hunter is entitled to 1 Encounter. 
The type of Encounter is chosen by the Hunter among 
those presented in this chapter.
 ◆ If the Hunters move as a group, they can face the same 
Encounter, but this requires that everyone in the group 
consume the available Encounter. 
 ◆ After the first Encounter is over, the Hunters can decide 
to extend their stay and experience an additional En-
counter. However, each Hunter will have to accept an 
Unexpected Event Token as a consequence. For those 
who wish, it is possible to receive a third Encounter by 
accepting an additional Unexpected Event Token.
 ◆ It is not possible to take an Interlude after each En-
counter, which means that each Hunter must face all 
Encounters in Rome with the same amount of Soma,

60
Wounds, or other features as modified by all Encoun-
ters previously faced. If a Hunter becomes inactive 
during an Encounter in Rome, they must wait for the 
final Interlude to become active again and resume their 
journey with their companions.
 ◆ When all Hunters have completed their Encounters, 
there will be a Scenario’s End Interlude.
 ◆ Before resuming the journey by choosing a new des-
tination on the map, the Hunter group must make an 
Unexpected Event roll, as explained on page 70.
Possible Encounters in Rome
Hunters are free to manage the order of Encounters. If an 
Encounter begins with some Hunters absent, they may de-
cide to join later by spending one of their available Encoun-
ters. Encounters in Rome are divided into two main zones: 
The Order Locations and the City Area. 
Encounters in the Order Locations (extended descrip-
tions on the following pages):
1. Reporting to the Pope to obtain information on ongo-
ing investigations. 
2. Training in an Order’s Sanctuary to improve their skills. 
3. Meeting other Hunters of the Order. 
4. Consulting the Vatican Library for clues and special 
knowledge. 
5. Accessing the Treasury of the Order in Rome to obtain 
a Treasure. 
6. Visiting the Prisons of the Order to encounter a Broken 
Tale captured by the Order. 
Encounters in the City Area (extended descriptions on the 
following pages):
1. Visit the Colosseum Square to encounter the Dark Pres-
ences that wander Rome.
2. Visit one of the city’s magical places: the Mouth of 
Truth, the Alchemical Gate in Piazza Navona, or the 
Bridge of Castel Sant’ Angelo.
3. Wandering around the city in search of adventure. 
Encounters at the Order Locations
1. Reporting to the Pope
The Hunters request and receive a meeting with the Pope and 
his inner circle of Archbishops. During the meeting, the Hunt-
ers can report on what they have discovered and dealt with. 
The Pope will evaluate the Hunters’ performance and decide 
how to support them in future missions.
This Encounter can only be selected once per Hunter group 
while in Rome. If multiple Hunters are participating in the 
Encounter, they must decide who will be the leader, but all 
may still interact in the Scene. At the end of the conversa-
tion, the leader rolls 1 die to determine the Pope’s verdict, 
using the following modifiers.
 ◆ +1 to the result if the Hunters handled the interview 
well in the Scene.
 ◆ -1 to the result if the Pope is aware of mistakes or 
strange behavior by the Hunters in previous missions.
 ◆ +1/-1 for an Encounter with a Dark Presence (see Vis-
iting Colosseum Square on page 63).  
1-2: The Pope is unconvinced of the Hunters’ actions, and 
the Storyteller will secretly choose an Opponent Dark Pres-
ence or Saint George to watch over the Hunters in the next 
Scenario at the Papacy’s direction.

61
3: The Pope approves of the Hunter’s work but is not par-
ticularly impressed. 
4-5: The Pope is very impressed with the work of the 
Hunters and will support them in any way he can. In the 
next Scenario, the Hunters will receive an allied Dark Pres-
ence to serve them.
6: The Pope is favorably impressed with the work of the 
Hunters and will make them aware of confidential infor -
mation the Eyes have brought to Rome. The Storyteller will 
introduce a new Scenario by giving basic directions and a 
useful piece of information about an NPC or Place in the 
Scenario.
2. Training
The Hunter trains hard under the watchful eye of the Custo-
des or a more experienced Hunter.
In this Scene, the Storyteller can introduce a Custodes NPC 
or Hunter as a master, creating possible connections with 
the Hunter. At the end of the Scene, the Storyteller rolls 1 
die to see the results of the training. 
1-2: The Hunter is exhausted from hard training, takes 1 
Wound, and loses 2 Soma. 
3: The Hunter is exhausted from the hard training, but it 
has paid off. They receive 2 Training Tokens. A Training 
Token can be spent during a Check to gain 1 Additional 
Success. However, one of their pieces of equipment (chosen 
by the Storyteller) is damaged and cannot be used until the 
end of the next Scenario.  
4-5: The Hunter is exhausted from the hard training, but 
it has paid off. They receive 2 Training Tokens. A Training 
Token can be spent during a Check to gain 1 Additional 
Success.   
6: The Hunter is exhausted from hard training, but it has 
paid off. They receive 3 Training Tokens. A Training Token 
can be spent during a Check to gain 1 Additional Success.
3. Meeting other Hunters
The Hunter meets with other members of the Order to share 
discoveries and tricks of the trade. 
In this Scene, the Hunter will encounter one or more Hunt-
er NPCs to interact with; different types of bonds can be 
created from this situation. At the beginning of the Scene, 
the Storyteller can choose the NPC’s initial attitude towards 
the Hunter, or it can be random.
1-2: The NPC has negative feelings about the Hunter: dis-
dain, envy, or simply disagreement with the Hunter’s meth-
ods.
3-4: The NPC is indifferent to the Hunter, has never heard 
of them, or does not find them interesting enough to care. 
5-6: The NPC admires the Hunter, either for their actions 
or due to rumors of the investigations they have resolved. 
Once a starting point has been established, the Scene in 
which a bond is established between the two Hunters is 
played out. The Storyteller must then keep track of this as 
the Campaign progresses.
Rivalry: The NPC resents the Hunter’s actions and will 
do anything to prove that they are better than the Hunter. 
The NPC may resolve a Campaign Scenario and not share 
the information found, act directly as if they were a hostile 
Dark Presence, or discredit the Hunter in the eyes of the 
Pope by reporting their most questionable actions.
Respect: The NPC respects the Hunter and will not hesi-
tate to help them in case of need.

62
Negative Attraction: The NPC is morbidly attracted to 
the Hunter because of some detail of one of the Hunter’s 
Descriptors, possibly a negative aspect that they want to 
encourage or draw out.
Positive Attraction: The NPC is attracted to a Hunter 
because of something positive in one of the Hunter’s De-
scriptors, and wants to understand it better.
Hatred: The NPC deeply hates the Hunter and wants to 
physically eliminate them or have them expelled from the 
Order.
Love: The NPC has feelings for the Hunter, a love that rep-
resents a bond they care about and want to develop. 
4. Consulting the Vatican Library
The Hunter spends entire days in the great Papal Library, 
searching for knowledge and information useful to their in-
vestigations. 
In this Scene, the Hunter has to decide what kind of re-
search to do.
Learning: Through ancient texts, the Hunter can learn a 
Special Gift from a Scenario they have previously faced. To 
obtain it, the Hunter must pay the appropriate XP . 
Research: The Hunter seeks information about anything 
strange in the world. The Storyteller secretly rolls a die and 
provides information on a specific topic.
1: Detailed information about another Hunter. 
2: Detailed information about a Treasure of the Order and 
its location, which will be the site of an unplayed Scenario. 
The Storyteller must incorporate the Treasure into the Sce-
nario by deciding who owns it or where it is located. 
3: Detailed information about a Dark Presence, useful for 
interacting with it in the event of an encounter.
4: The Hunter receives 1 Knowledge Token. A Knowledge 
Token can be spent during a Check to obtain 1 Additional 
Success.
5: A clue to one of the Scenarios yet to be played, in the 
form of testimony from an Agent of the Order. The Story-
teller can reveal a Gift, Descriptor, or Agenda of an NPC 
from the Scenario, or an item from the The story that led us 
here... section.

63
6: A Clue to the One Who Plots in the Shadows (only 1 
Clue can be obtained per visit to Rome. In the case of mul-
tiple searches by multiple Hunters, each additional 6 rolled 
counts as a 5).
5. Accessing the Treasury
The Hunter enters the Roman Treasury, the place where 
Treasures found by Hunters are collected before being secured 
in the fortified church of Valea Viilor. 
In this Scene, the Hunter can access a Treasure of the Order 
by following the rules presented in The Broken Ones. 
6. Visiting the Prisons of the Order
The Hunter is led to the Order’s secret Prisons, where the vil-
lains captured during the Hunter’s investigations are impris-
oned.
In this Scene, the Hunter can interact with NPCs captured 
in previous Scenarios. Conversations can lead to plot devel-
opments, new alliances, or Clues about the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows.
Encounters in the City Area
1. Visiting the Colosseum Square
The Hunter visits the city’s largest and busiest square, a cross-
roads of travelers and Dark Presences.
In this Scene, the Hunter can interact with a Dark Presence, 
which can then be introduced into the Campaign. The Sto-
ryteller may decide which Dark Presence is encountered, 
or roll a die.
1: A group of travelers say they encountered something 
strange on their way to Rome and will give the Hunter 
some information about the Dark Presence. This can be 
introduced as an Encounter in the Hunters’ journey when 
they leave Rome. As the Storyteller, the information re-
ceived should be of some use when the Encounter with the 
Dark Presence is addressed.  
2: An Opponent Dark Presence seeking the Hunters 
chooses or rolls a die to determine why it wants to face the 
Hunter.  (1-2: Personal hatred 3-4: Hired by an NPC previ-
ously defeated in a Scenario 5-6: Emissary of the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows.)
3: A Neutral Dark Presence seeking to advance their Agen-
da will attempt to interact with the Hunters to gain their 
help or exploit them. 
4: An Ally Dark Presence will approach the Hunters to get 
to know them and learn of their exploits. If a Hunter ends 
this Encounter positively and chooses Reporting to the 
Pope as their next Encounter, they get a +1 on the die roll, 
and in the case of a 4-5 result, the assigned Dark Presence 
will be the one met in this Encounter. Conversely, if the 
Encounter ends negatively, they get a -1 to the roll and the 
Pope reveals that a trusted ally of the Order reported the 
Hunter’s bad attitude. 
5-6: Silvan Luperco will approach the Hunters and offer to 
be their guide to Rome.
Silvan Luperco
Inspired by the Faun of Roman Mythology (Opponent)
Once upon a time, there was a Faun who spent his days laz-
ing around, drinking wine, and gambling, using his powers to 
win against unsuspecting humans. The other gods had rebuked 
the Faun for his sloth and urged him to get to work so that his 
name would be remembered. “One day, ” they said, “mankind

64
will cease to worship us, and the gates of oblivion will open for 
those who have not done enough to enter their myths. ” But the 
Faun did not believe these words and thought that the other gods 
were just jealous of his ability to take life lightly. One summer 
morning, after a night of revelry, the Faun awoke late to find that 
all the gods were gone. Alone and desperate, he burst into tears, 
begging for forgiveness for his folly, but no one listened. Then, 
from the shadow of a great oak tree, a figure dressed in black 
stepped forward and promised him his place among the gods. 
The Faun threw himself at his benefactor’s feet, promising to do 
anything to earn his place in myth, and the One Who Plots in 
the Shadows smiled smugly at these words.
Descriptor: I’m a good-hearted prankster who gets by as a 
guide in Rome for the Order’s Hunters. I know the city like the 
back of my hand and there is no place or person I cannot find.  
3 Wounds
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: To make myself an ally of the Hunters and gather 
information about them to pass on to the One Who Plots in 
the Shadows.  
Gift - Guide to Rome: Silvan knows every story and ev-
ery nook and cranny of Rome. If the Hunters take the Faun 
into their service, they will be able to access some addition-
al information about the city’s Places (details are available 
in the Places themselves). Silvan also knows all the Dark 
Presences that roam the city, and if the Hunters wish to 
meet one in particular, he will be able to lead them to its 
location or give directions. In exchange for his services, Sil-
van will “only” ask the Hunters to tell him a story about 
their exploits and how they solved a Scenario. Silvan will 
then bring all of this information to the One Who Plots in 
the Shadows, providing crucial info the villain will use to 
his advantage as the Campaign progresses.
2. Visiting one of the Magical Places
The Hunter visits one of the many Places the Order has desig-
nated as worthy of special interest and research.
In this Scene, the Hunter can choose which Place to visit. 
The Mouth of Truth
Located on the porch of a large church, this ancient marble 
mask has always manifested strange powers. It is said that 
if a person of strong spirit puts their hand in its mouth and 
asks a question, they will receive a vision of the future that 
awaits them.
A Hunter can put their hand in the Mouth of Truth and ask 
a question, and the Storyteller secretly rolls a die to deter -
mine the answer.
1-2: A shiver of fear runs down the Hunter’s spine, but 
nothing more. The Hunter gets no response and loses 2 
Soma. 
3: The Hunter hears a whisper in their ear, but cannot un-
derstand its meaning.  
4-5: The Hunter clearly hears a voice behind them hissing 
a few sentences, but only understands one or two words. 
The Storyteller must provide the Hunter with a maximum

65
of 1 or 2 Keywords related to the question asked. 
6: The Hunter feels his head spin and has a brief vision. The 
Storyteller recounts the vision, incorporating information 
useful to the question being asked.
The Mouth of Truth will no longer answer a question that 
previously had a 4/5/6 answer result, all subsequent at-
tempts will automatically count as a 1.
If Silvan is present:  The guide will tell you that 
the Mouth was once nothing more than a sewer man-
hole and that Pope Urban VIII accidentally tripped 
over it while walking. After this event, the Pope had 
a terrifying vision and blamed the strange Mouth. 
He then ordered it moved into a church so that 
God’s grace would keep the demonic power at bay.  
Silvan will reveal that he knows a secret word for divina-
tion from the Mouth and will whisper it near the marble 
mask. If a Hunter asks a question immediately after this 
gesture, the Storyteller should consider the roll an auto-
matic 6. This effect will only occur the first time Silvan 
talks about this incredible ability, and after that, the die 
must be rolled as normal, despite the guide’s pantomime. 
Magic Gate of Piazza Vittorio
In one of Rome’s busiest squares stands a dark, abandoned 
villa. The front door is imposing in height and completely cov-
ered in strange and incomprehensible writing. Many stories 
surround the villa, once the home of a wealthy occult baron 
who apparently sold it to live in a tree. Many say the door 
opens with a secret formula that gives access to heaven or 
hell.
It will be obvious to a Hunter with even a passing familiar-
ity with magic that the door hides a powerful magical seal. 
This seal cannot be broken in any way except by uttering 
the magic words of the formula that created the seal.
Threat - The Alchemical Door
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A precious ancient door that connects Rome to 
Borgo Maleanime. The One Who Plots in the Shadows is the 
only one who knows the exact words to open the door.  
Gift - Magic Door: No Gift, magic, or effect can open the 
door, for the engraved seal draws its power directly from 
the soul of the One Who Plots in the Shadows. The only 
way to open the door is to say the magical words A Fairy-
tale Turned Upside Down . It is possible, however, to pass 
papers and envelopes under the doorframe, and this is how 
Silvan keeps his master informed of his discoveries. 
If Silvan is present:  The guide will tell you that the 
previous owner of the villa was an eccentric Baron who 
decided to live in a tree. Many say that the Baron’s ghost 
still roams the streets of Rome, appearing some nights to 
frighten passersby. The villa was bought for little money 
by a strange figure whose name no one remembers. The 
stranger came from the north, but after the purchase, he 
did not spend a single night in the house.

66
The Bridge of Castel Sant’ Angelo
Near the ancient fortress of Castel Sant’ Angelo is a bridge. During the day, 
citizens and travelers cross it to get from one side of the city to the oth-
er, but at night, strange presences appear. For some unknown reason, the 
bridge attracts the restless souls of Rome who cannot find peace.
By prowling the bridge at night, Hunters can encounter a spirit and, 
if they approach it in the correct manner (including with Position 
Checks), they can interact with it and learn its story. The Storyteller 
may choose or roll a die to decide which spirit is encountered.
1: One of the city’s earliest inhabitants: Talking to the spirit will re-
veal some interesting information about Rome. The Storyteller chooses 
a monument, church, or other location in Rome and tells the Hunters 
some true historical information. Learning this information grants 1 
X P.
2: One of the first members of the Order: Communicating with the 
spirit reveals interesting information about the Order. The Hunter re-
ceives 1 Order Lore Token. When involved in an Encounter in Rome, 
the Hunter can use the Token to increase a die roll by 1. 
3: A traveler who died under mysterious circumstances: The traveler 
will reveal the location of their remains and ask the Hunters to give 
them a proper burial. The Storyteller must choose the Place of a yet-to-
be-played Scenario where the spirit’s remains are kept. If the Hunters 
find them, they will give the spirit peace and receive 2 XP each.
4: A loquacious jester: Between a dirge and a poem, the good-natured 
spirit will tell the strange story of a Dark Presence. The Storyteller will 
give the Hunter much of the information and stats of a Dark Presence, 
and decide where on the map it is located so that the Hunter will know 
how to meet it. 
5: A Restless Spirit: The soul that approaches the Hunters is filled with 
anger and resentment and will not hesitate to attack them.

67
Restless Spirit
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the spirit of a person who died under 
unpleasant circumstances. I hate life and only want to 
harm the living with my powers.
Gift - Spiritic Power:  The Restless Spirit can only 
be permanently Wounded by magic. Wounds inflicted 
by other means may disperse the Spirit in the current 
Scene, but it may return in another Scene with Wounds 
inflicted by mundane methods restored. The spirit can 
act 2 times during an Exchange, but not consecutively. 
Although intangible, the spirit can use its ghostly pow-
ers to push or grab people and objects.
6: The Baron of the mansion: The Spirit is very distracted, 
and to keep his attention focused, it is necessary to over -
come the Head in the Clouds Threat. 
Threat - Head in the Clouds
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A dreamy spirit who cannot keep his at-
tention on the living for more than a few seconds.
If the Hunters can get the spirit’s attention, he will tell them 
about the strange, inky-handed figure who bought his 
mansion for far more than it was worth. After his death 
(which occurred when he fell out of the tree where he had 
moved to live), the Baron wandered around the mansion 
and learned part of the magic formula to open the door. He 
will also know the name of the alchemist who worked on 
the door and who should know the rest of the formula. To 
get these two pieces of information, the Baron will ask the 
Hunters to perform two missions for him.
To reveal the part of the formula he knows: He will 
ask the Hunters to make sure one of his descendants is okay. 
The Storyteller will describe a Minor NPC from a Scenario 
yet to be played, and create a problem about them that is 
related to the Hunters’ main antagonist in that context. If 
the Hunters return the Baron’s relative safely to Rome, the 
spirit will reveal the first part of the formula: A Fairytale. 
To know the identity of the creator of the door:  The 
Baron will only make this request after the Hunters have 
completed the first task. The spirit will describe a Main 
NPC of a yet-to-be-played Scenario as the one who creat-
ed the door. The Storyteller must edit the NPC’s profile to 
give him a Descriptor or Gift that makes them a de facto 
alchemist capable of creating the door. The NPC in ques-
tion, if convinced, will reveal the second part of the formu-
la: Turned Upside Down.
A note on the Baron’s spirit: Discovering the formula 
to open the Alchemical Door opens a direct route for the 
Hunters to track down the One Who Plots in the Shadows. 
For this reason, obtaining the two parts of the formula is 
very important and has been tied to two playable Scenarios 
that the Storyteller may propose as they see fit during the 
course of the Campaign, thus affecting the duration of the 
Campaign itself.  
If Silvan is present: If the guide has already been to the 
Alchemical Door with the Hunters, he will recognize the 
spirit of the Baron and allow you to meet and talk to him 
without any Checks.

68
3. Wandering around the city
Hunters roam the city in search of adventure.
There will be an odd situation in this Scene that 
the Hunters can choose to deal with. It is essential-
ly a normal Encounter of the kind that might oc-
cur on a journey, and the Storyteller can decide to 
adapt it to tie in with a Dark Presence, a Hunter’s 
past, or to make it stand on its own. What follows 
are hints and ideas for the Storyteller to expand 
upon, depending on the nature of the Encounter 
they wish to present. Depending on their ability to 
improvise, the Storyteller should decide whether 
to roll the die or use one of the suggested Encoun-
ters they have prepared in advance.
1: The She-wolf (Inspired by the She-
wolf of the Roman legend of Romulus and 
Remus)
A Dark Presence protecting the children of Rome 
from those who would do them harm. To many, 
the She-Wolf is nothing more than a ferocious 
beast, and her prowling through the streets of the 
city is likely to be misinterpreted as a danger.
Possible Situations: 1-2: A gang of ruffians are 
kidnapping children in the city, but during their 
last raid they encountered the She-Wolf, and one 
of them was mauled. Everyone fears the ferocious 
beast, unaware that she is actually the heroine of 
the story. 3-4: A series of thefts alarm the town’s 
grocery stores. It seems that a large She-Wolf is 
stealing food to take to her cubs, but no one sus-
pects that the She-Wolf is actually feeding the 
town’s foundlings with this food. 5-6: A pair of 
poachers roam the town on a mission to track 
down and kill a dangerous wolf, but they are actually on a mission for 
Ambrose, the sorcerer known as Old Merlin.
2: The Band of Rugantino (Inspired by the masks of the Ro-
man theater)
A brawler and his gang harass the citizens of the city, extorting money 
and challenging the weakest for an easy win.
Possible situations: 1-2: An elderly couple is wandering around look-
ing for help. A gang of thugs has thrown them out of their home because 
they want to use the house as a base for their shady business. 3-4: A 
letter is delivered to one of the Hunters (the one with the weakest fight-
ing skills). In broken Italian, a certain Rugantino challenges them to a 
duel to prove that he is stronger than the much-feared Hunters. 5-6: 
Rugantino and his gang are hired by the One Who Plots in the Shadows 
to eliminate as many Hunters as possible. 
3: Dr. Gambalunga the Charlatan (Inspired by the Doctor of 
Roman Carnival masks)
A strange little man dressed as a doctor, bragging about miraculous

69
cures, roams the town squares shouting that he can cure 
any ailment.
Possible situations: 1-2: A patron drinks a magic potion, 
but turns into a toad to the amazement of the crowd. The 
most astonished of all is Gambalunga himself, who did not 
even know he had a real potion among the junk he foists 
on the people. Now the charlatan needs the Hunters’ help 
to make up for his mistake. 3-4: The Hunters learn that a 
certain Dr. Gambalunga is bragging that he has solved a 
complicated case. In fact, it is a Scenario that the Hunters 
have faced and resolved. 5-6: The One Who Plots in the 
Shadows has promised Gambalunga the power of a true 
wizard, but to get it he must first poison as many Hunters 
as possible. 
4: The Farnese Gardens and the Bacchanalian 
Priests (Inspired by the Tradition of Lupercalia)
The Farnese Gardens are the seat of worship for the Bac-
chanalian Priests, a circle of Rome’s upper middle class 
that meets periodically. Thanks to an ancient pact with the 
Church, the Order is not allowed to enter the Gardens for
