4

129
End of Scene: The Hunters have discovered Nikola’s se-
cret and can now try to defeat the Badalisk, go to FIFTH 
SCENE - In the Badalisk’s Lair.
FIFTH SCENE –  In the 
Badalisk’s Lair
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at the mouth 
of a large cave, from which emanates a terrible stench of 
death. As you prepare to descend, you hear a deep snor-
ing in the distance.
Place: The Badalisk’s Lair. 
Time: The moment of reckoning.
End of Scene:  See page 121 for instructions on how to 
deal with the Badalisk. If present, Nikola will actively as-
sist the Hunters in two ways: he can either be devoured to 
give a Hunter time to enter the Badalisk unharmed, or he 
can distract the creature for an Exchange, allowing every-
one else to act without fear of the monster’s powerful bite. 
After defeating the Badalisk and recovering the Treasure, 
Nikola will ask the Hunters for permission to return to his 
reclusive life. 
5

130
The Last Wish
 
A Scenario inspired by The Tinderbox (Hans Christian Andersen)
Once upon a time...
Bastian Soldat is a deserter guilty of a serious crime: assigned to guard the garrison 
general, Bastian fled during battle, abandoning his charge in the midst of the enemy. It 
took three years to track down the fugitive, who has since turned to a life of debauchery 
and lawlessness. But a week ago, Bastian Soldat was sentenced to the gallows and 
executed in the public square of Heidelberg, the city where he had been hiding. Up 
until this point, it may have seemed like just another story, but according to the Order’s 
research, Bastian Soldat had already been executed twice. So a group of Hunters was 
sent to the German city to retrace Bastian’s life and understand why this deserter seems 
to keep coming back from the dead to continue his misdeeds.
Safety Keywords: Scam / Demonic Presence

131
The story that led us here...
After his desertion, Bastian fled to the small town 
of Heidelberg to start a new life. Here, the soldier met 
Britta, an old witch who lived near the town cemetery. 
The woman needed an intrepid young man to retrieve a 
Magic Tinderbox, a memento of her late grandmother. 
In return, Britta offered Bastian a bag of coins that would 
allow the soldier to live off his earnings for many years. 
After retrieving the object, the two parted on friendly 
terms, but Bastian gambled away all the money he had 
received within a few days and reappeared at the witch’s 
house to ask for a new job. When Britta refused, Bastian 
lost his temper and, in a fit of rage, decapitated the wi-
tch with one blow of his saber. Determined not to go to 
prison, Bastian threw the body into the hollow tree in 
Britta’s garden and the head into the river near the town. 
He also took possession of the Magic Tinderbox, all the 
money he could find, and anything else that caught his 
eye. A few days later, Bastian found himself with nothing 
in his pockets and several debts. Determined to fix the si-
tuation, he attempted to rob a wealthy merchant but was 
caught by the city’s gendarmes. When his identity as a 
deserter was discovered, the head of the gendarmes did 
not hesitate for a moment to condemn him to the gal-
lows, but... the soldier found himself back in front of the 
merchant before committing the robbery.    In his jacket 
pocket, Bastian felt the witch’s Magic Tinderbox throb-
bing with power and realized that time had rewound to 
allow him to escape. Exalted by this new power, Bastian 
began to commit all sorts of mischief, counting on the fact 
that he could always turn back time if things took a turn for 
the worse. Meanwhile, Britta’s spirit could not rest, and the 
witch took possession of a black she-cat that often roamed 
near the hollow tree. Britta then set out to find some of the 
items Bastian had stolen from her: the Witch’s Shawl, the 
Black Coral Pendant, and the Devil’s Ladle. What appears 
to be ordinary objects are actually three powerful magical 
focuses in which Britta has stored some of her power. The 
witch’s big problem is that without outside help, she cannot 
get her hands on the three objects and bring them to where 
her corpse is...
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters...
Bastian has “died and risen” several times, including twice 
on the gallows. Despite the great opportunity that the Ma-
gic Tinderbox offers him, the soldier is so stupid that he 
never manages to do anything that would allow him to turn 
the tide forever. Meanwhile, he has fallen in love with the 
daughter of the city regent, the young Alena Wagner, who 
does not seem to disdain his attentions. With huge debts 
at the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House, the city’s worst 
gambling den, Bastian plans to clean out the Wagner fa-
mily’s coffers and then flee to Copenhagen.
 
Note: Unlike a normal Scenario where the Hunters move 
between different locations to investigate, in this adventure, 
every time Bastian is captured or eliminated by the Hun-
ters, there is a Reset that brings the events back to the first 
Scene. The cycle will continue until the Hunters manage 
to revive Britta to reveal the word, chosen by the Storytel-
ler, that can deactivate the Magic Tinderbox. Because of its 
structure, the Scenario handles the evolution of the story 
with a series of Resets that replace the normal Events.

132
Bastian Soldat, the Black Cat, and each Place associated with 
Bastian have a progressive Reset list showing the differences 
the Hunters will find the first, second, third, and fourth time 
Bastian uses the power of the Tinderbox.
In this Scenario, the Hunters track Bastian by visiting Pla-
ces that he is known to frequent. If the Hunters manage to 
find him by other means, a Reset will still take place that 
allows Bastian to escape and 1 Reset Token will be added to 
the soldier’s Magic Tinderbox Gift. However, in those circu-
mstances, the details of the setting aren’t altered and events 
don’t progress to the next change on the Reset list. For the 
sake of clarity, the chronology of pre-determined Resets in 
the Scenario is as follows:
First Reset
The first change occurs in the first Scene, when the Hunters 
accidentally run into Bastian at the entrance to the city. 
Second Reset
The second change happens when the Hunters find Bastian 
in one of the three Places he usually hangs out: the Drun-
ken Hussar’s Gambling House, Villa Wagner, or the Golden 
Plate Tavern. 
Third Reset
The third change occurs when the Hunters meet Bastian at 
one of the two Places left after the Second Reset.
Fourth Reset
The final change happens when the Hunters hit Bastian at 
the spot left after the Second and Third Reset.
After the Fourth Reset
Bastian will take refuge in the Old Barracks to begin his 
plan to rob the Wagners. 
All Resets not generated as indicated by the previous list 
should not be counted for the progression of Scenario Re-
sets, but generate 1 Reset Token for the Magic Tinderbox 
Gift.
Whenever the Hunters visit the Drunken Hussar’s Gam-
bling House, Villa Wagner, or the Golden Plate Tavern, 
roll a die to see where Bastian and the Cat are at that mo-
ment:
1-2: Bastian is in the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House, 
trying to play dice with a group of soldiers. The Cat is at 
Villa Wagner, trying to get the Black Coral Pendant from 
Alena’s room.    
3-4: Bastian is at Villa Wagner, chatting amiably with 
Alena. The young woman is overlooking the terrace of her 
room while Bastian recites a poem to her from the garden 
below. The Cat is in the Golden Plate Tavern, trying to re-
trieve the Devil’s Ladle from the kitchens. 
5-6: Bastian is at the Golden Plate Tavern, bargaining to 
eat some delicacies without paying. The Cat is at the Drun-
ken Hussar’s Gambling House, trying to retrieve the Witch’s 
Shawl from an old drunk who is using it to protect himself 
from the cold of the night. 
 
If the result has the Hunters either meeting Bastian at a 
location where they have already met him or meeting the 
Cat at a location where they have already recovered Britta’s 
item, go to the next result.

133
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters:  It is late in the morning 
when you finally arrive at the gates of Heidelberg: the city 
is buzzing with activity, with merchant carts and groups of 
soldiers going back and forth. This city is a crossroads, and 
as you might expect, the traffic is heavy. As you contempla-
te the difficulty of finding Bastian Soldat in the midst of the 
city’s hustle and bustle, a voice catches your attention: “Ba-
stian! Bastian Soldat, you scoundrel! Come out and give me 
the money you owe me!” The shouter is a bearded man in a 
tattered potter’s apron, while the recipient of his insults is a 
small man with an anonymous face, shaggy blond hair, and 
a drunkard’s belly. Obviously, you have already found your 
target.      
Place: The Entrance Plaza of Heidelberg. 
Time: It is midday and the day is slowly warming up as 
the cackle of taverns and the smell of good food tickle the 
senses. 
Initial Situation:  Bastian Soldat rushes through the 
crowd to get away from the man shouting at him, almost 
tripping over a black cat that seems to have deliberately got-
ten in his way.    
Information: The potter complaining about Bastian is 
called Hugo, and he is angry because the soldier bought 
some pots in Britta’s name a week ago and never paid for 
them. Talking to Hugo will give you the following infor -
mation:
 ◆ Bastian is no good and a loafer: he boasts of being an 
honorably discharged soldier, but when asked to show 
the medal he received upon his discharge, he always 
shies away. No one knows where he sleeps, but he is 
always seen hanging around three Places.
 ◆ The Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House: A cheap 
dive bar known for its bad wine and gambling tables.
 ◆ Villa Wagner: The home of a noble family. Bastian 
has told everyone he knows that he has fallen in love 
with the young and beautiful Alena Wagner, dau-
ghter of Anselm Wagner, the wealthiest aristocrat in 
town. 
 ◆ The Golden Plate Tavern: The most expensive and 
valuable restaurant in the city, where only the weal-
thiest can afford a meal.
 ◆ Britta is an old woman who lives near the town ceme-
tery. Many believe her to be a witch, but no one has 
ever seen her do anything strange enough to support 
these accusations. 
Bastian Soldat
OL Easy 3 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a deserter with no qualities other than a 
great desire to live a good life with minimal effort. My only 
virtue is that I have incredible nerve.
Agenda: Emptying the coffers of the Wagner family in order 
to pretend to be rich in Alena’s eyes and convince her to run 
away with me to Copenhagen. 
Gift - Magic Tinderbox: Thanks to this powerful magi-
cal object, Bastian can go back in time whenever the situa-
tion would prevent him from completing his Agenda. This 
magical effect cannot be stopped except by the magic word 
that only Britta knows. Each time Bastian rewinds time, the 
Storyteller secretly accumulates 1 Reset Token. Each token

134
accumulated makes the three Demonic Dogs more power-
ful (see page 142). 
First Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to Heidelberg’s 
Entrance Plaza and will stay in one of the other Places he is 
accustomed to wandering.
Second Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to the Place 
where the Hunters found him and will wander to one of the 
other two remaining locations. 
Third Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to the Places 
where the Hunters found him, and will wander to the last 
available location.    
Fourth Reset: Bastian will avoid returning to all the Pla-
ces where the Hunters found him. From that moment on, 
he will take refuge in the Old Barracks, plan the robbery of 
Villa Wagner, and then try to escape with Alena. 
Hugo the Potter
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am an honest craftsman and have been a 
potter all my life. I have a friendly nature and tend to trust 
people.         
Heidelberg’s Entrance Plaza
Descriptor: A large square always filled with citizens and 
travelers. 
Information: As the first Place that Bastian will use the 
Magic Tinderbox to escape the Hunters, this is where the 
Hunters will start again after each Reset. Unlike normal hu-
mans, the Hunters will retain a memory of what happened 
at each Reset.
First Reset: The Hunters will notice that some powerful 
magic has taken effect, and time seems to have rewound 
to a few minutes before they meet Bastian. The deserter is 
absent, but Hugo the Potter is behind his stall, smiling at 
passersby. The Black Cat walks between the legs of one of 
the Hunters, stops to watch them with intelligent eyes, and 
then walks away.    
Second Reset: Same as the First Reset, except the Cat 
will stop with the Hunters and try to get them to follow her, 
leading them to Britta’s house.
Third Reset: Like the First Reset, but this time the Hun-
ters will find Anselm Wagner waiting for them with an 
escort of ten gendarmes. The nobleman will tell the Hun-
ters that he has received a report from a trusted man about 
their impending arrival in the city. The nobleman has made 
no request of the Order and wants no trouble, so he will 
ask the Hunters to leave. It will be clear that Anselm has

135
nothing to hide but is simply afraid that the Order’s presen-
ce will scare away visitors and ruin the merchants’ business. 
Fourth Reset: Like the First Reset, except that Bastian 
has hired a gang of Cutthroats (2 per Character) to elimi-
nate the Hunters. 
After the Fourth Reset: Same as the First Reset.
•
Anselm Wagner
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the most important person in the city, 
involved in politics and with many investments in various 
activities. Everyone admires my efforts to make Heidelberg 
prosperous. 
Agenda: Keep an eye out for potential problems for the city 
and maintain a clean appearance. 
•
Armsmen of the City
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are swordsmen and have gained experien-
ce during the war. We carry out the orders of those who pay 
us without making too much trouble.  
Gift - Companions in Arms:  During an action, they 
increase their OL by 1 if at least one other Armsman is pre-

Cutthroat 
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are ruthless killers who will do anything to

Gift - Assassin: If they act to kill, they are considered to 
have an OL Medium 5.
Information: If questioned, they will not hesitate to reve-
al that they were hired by a certain Bastian Soldat. 
•
The Black Cat
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a snappy kitten bound to the spirit of old 
Britta. Thanks to the witch’s power I can understand what 
people are saying. 
Agenda: Have the humans help me retrieve Britta’s three 
items. 
First Reset:  The Cat will observe the Hunters in the 
Heidelberg Entrance Plaza to understand why they are not 
affected by the Magic Tinderbox. It will then go 
to one of the Places to retrieve Britta’s items. 
Second Reset:  If the Hunters are intere-
sted, the Cat will lead them to Britta’s Hou-
se to find her diary. If the diary has already 
been found, the Cat will take the Hunters to 
one of the Places to retrieve the items. The 
Cat will also have retrieved one of the items if 
the Hunters have not already done so.

136
Third Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the Cat will 
lead them to Britta’s House to find her diary. If the diary has

ved one of the items if the Hunters have not already done so.
Fourth Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the Cat will

the Places to retrieve the items. The Cat will also have retrie-
ved one of the items if the Hunters have not already done so.
After the Fourth Reset: If the Hunters are interested, the 
Cat will lead them to Britta’s House to find her diary. If the

to one of the Places to retrieve the items. The Cat will also 
have retrieved one of the items if the Hunters have not alre-
ady done so.
The Drunken Hussar’s 
Gambling House
Descriptor: A dive bar at the end of a slum alley. Amidst 
the smoke and dim lights, the shadows of the city’s underworld 
move. 
Information: The Hussar is run by Ulrich, a former sol-
dier who lost his right hand in the war. Ulrich knows Ba-
stian well and will not hesitate to tell you that the soldier has 
gambled away a fortune at the tables of his dive bar. Everyo-
ne jokingly refers to Bastian as “the Golden Goose, ” and 
when he is present, everyone competes to play with him. 
Ulrich knows that Bastian has fallen head over heels for the 
Wagner’ young scion and is often at their Villa trying to win 
Alena’s heart. 
If Bastian is present: He gambles the last of his coin on 
dice, and as soon as he sees the Hunters, he tries to escape 
through the window behind him.
If the Black Cat is present:  The Hunters will see her at 
the entrance to the Gambling House, harassing an old beg-
gar. The pet pulls a fine shawl toward her, which the beggar 
holds tightly. It will be obvious that such a poor man cannot 
afford such an expensive garment.
First Reset: If this is the first time he has encountered 
them at this Place, Bastian will try to escape. However, if the 
Hunters have already met him here, Bastian will not show 
up again. 
Second Reset: Like the First Reset, except that Ulrich will 
be offering the Hunters a round of welcome drinks. Bastian 
has paid him to lace the drinks with poison. If discovered, 
Ulrich will not hesitate to confess.

137
Third Reset: Same as the First and Second Reset, but if 
the Hunters have not read Britta’s diary and the Black Cat is 
present, the animal will try to make the Hunters realize that 
the Witch’s Shawl is an item to be retrieved. If the Hunters 
help her, she will lead them to Britta’s House to find the 
diary.
Fourth Reset: Same as previous Resets. 
After the Fourth Reset: The Gambling House is closed 
because Ulrich is joining Bastian to rob Villa Wagner. 
Ulrich Rotten Eye
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a former criminal with experience in the 
use of poisons. I opened my gambling den looking for a less 
dangerous life, but I don’t back down when there’s money to 
be made.
Gift - Poisoned Dagger: Ulrich’s weapon is imbued with 
a powerful poison. The poison first enters play when the 
weapon inflicts 1 Wound, causing 1 Penalty to the target’s 
actions. The effect lasts until the victim takes an antidote or 
has an Interlude. 
Threat - Poisoned Drink
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A fine, tasteless, odorless poison that can be 
added to any beverage. 
Gift - Poison: Those who ingest the Poisoned Drink suf-
fer 1 Wound and receive 1 Penalty until they take an anti-
dote or rest in an Interlude. 
Villa Wagner
Descriptor: An imposing mansion surrounded by gardens, 
ponds, and mazes of hedges. 
Information: It’s impossible to enter the Villa without an 
invitation from the Wagner family, but there are no fences 
and, except for a few gardeners, it is easy to wander incon-
spicuously around the large gardens. Alena is often found 
on the large balcony of her room on the second floor, rea-
ding a good book and sipping tea. Alena will tell you that 
Bastian often brings her very good and expensive cakes that 
he buys at the famous Golden Plate Tavern. The young wo-
man is unclear about who Bastian really is, but it will be ob-
vious that she is attracted to the funny little man and finds 
his awkward attempts to court her tender.
If Bastian is present: He will be under Alena’s balcony 
reciting some love poetry. As soon as he sees the Hunters, 
he will try to escape by slipping into one of the hedge ma-
zes.
If the Black Cat is present:  It can be seen on the bal-
cony being petted by Alena. The Cat pretends to purr in 
affection as she clearly tries to snatch a pendant from the 
young woman’s neck.
First Reset:  If this is the first time he encounters the 
Hunters in this Place, Bastian will try to escape. However, 
if the Hunters have already met him here, Bastian will not 
appear again.
Second Reset:  Same as the First Reset, except that a 
group of guards (2 per Hunter) will enter the Scene when 
the Hunters approach Alena. The leader of the guards will 
say that by Lord Wagner’s order, no one can enter the gar-
den of the Villa. If the Hunters can get the captain to talk

138
more, he will say that an informant has alerted Lord Wa-
gner that a group of robbers are roaming the city and rai-
ding the villas of the nobles.
Third Reset: Same as the First and Second Reset, but if 
the Hunters have not read Britta’s diary and the Black Cat 
is present, the animal will try to make the Hunters under -
stand that the Black Coral Pendant is an item to be retrie-
ved. If the Hunters help the Cat, she will lead them to Brit-
ta’s House to find the diary.
Fourth Reset: Same as the previous reset. 
After the Fourth Reset:  Alena will not be on the bal-
cony as usual. Bastian has written her a letter asking her 
to meet him behind the Golden Plate Tavern at sunset. Ba-
stian wants to do the job at the Villa without Alena being 
there. Afterward, he’ll suggest that they run away together.  
Villa Wagner’s Guard
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are loyal and well-trained for our duties as 
guardians. 
Gift - Guard Corps: During an action, increase their OL 
by 1 if they are defending Villa Wagner from a threat or 
perceived threat.
Alena Wagner
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young scion of the Wagner family. I 
love adventure and romance novels. I’m cultured and dream 
of becoming a teacher one day.  
Agenda: To have a good adventure like the ones I read 
about in novels.
Wagner Treasury
Descriptor: A small fortified tower connected to 
the Villa by an underground tunnel. Aside from the 
tunnel, the only way to access the tower is to know 
the combination of the large metal door that 
blocks the main entrance.
Information: The Treasury is permanently 
manned by 4 Guards who change shifts every 
eight hours.
First, Second, and Third Reset:  Bastian

139
has obtained the information he needs for the robbery 
days before the Hunters arrive, so he will not be wandering 
around the Treasury beforehand. 
Fourth Reset: Bastian will attempt to rob the Treasury. 
The plan is simple: Bastian and four other soldiers he met 
at the barracks will enter through Villa Wagner and use the 
secret tunnel to get into the Treasury. Meanwhile, Ulrich 
will have slipped a powerful sleeping pill into the food in-
tended for the four Guards guarding the entrance. Once 
the Guards are asleep, Bastian will open the door and each 
gang member will fill two large sacks with gold and va-
luables. Unbeknownst to the other gang members, Bastian 
is planning to betray them. Once outside the Villa, he will 
separate from the group, warn the gendarmes of the theft, 
and then pick up Alena to escape with her. Bastian’s inten-
tion is to cover his escape by drawing attention to the other 
gang members.  
 
Threat - Treasury Door
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A huge metal door reinforced to resist any 
burglary attempt. The combination to open it is 05031762, 
Alena’s date of birth.
Robbers
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are veteran soldiers and have seen the hor-
rors of war. We do not shy away from danger and have no 
qualms about committing acts of cruelty.  
The Golden Plate Tavern
Descriptor: The finest and most famous tavern in Heidel-
berg: Brigit’s cuisine is of the highest standard, but only the 
very rich can afford it.
Information: There are no rooms to sleep in at the Ta-
vern, which is best known for the haute cuisine of Chef Bri-
git. Nobles and rich men come from all over Europe to taste 
the food of the Golden Plate. Brigit became accustomed to 
Bastian’s presence, always showering her with complimen-
ts on her cooking. At first, Bastian was a good customer, 
but soon he had no money to pay for the meals, and Brigit 
gave him credit until she realized that the soldier was a lost 
cause. If asked about Bastian, Brigit will say that he is a go-
od-tempered man who tries to live far beyond his means. 
When he is not eating at the Golden Plate, Bastian is often 
at the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House, where he gam-
bles and tries to accumulate some money.
If Bastian is present:  He will sit at the table and beg 
Brigit to serve him one of her fantastic dishes. As soon as 
he sees the Hunters, he will try to escape by sneaking into 
the kitchens.
If the Black Cat is present:  The Hunters will see her 
through the half-open door into the kitchen as she tries to 
knock down an old ladle hanging on the wall with her paw. 
The Cat is unsuccessful, as someone always intervenes to 
chase it away. Brigit will complain that the animal seems 
to be obsessed with the old ladle given to her by the elderly 
Britta. 
First Reset:  If this is the first time he encounters the 
Hunters in this Place, Bastian will try to escape. However, 
if the Hunters have already met him here, Bastian will not 
appear again.

Second Reset: Same as the First Reset, except that a 
group of Cutthroats (2 per Hunter, see page 135) are 
preparing to ambush the Hunters when they leave the Ta-
vern.    
Third Reset: Same as the First and Second Reset, but if 
the Hunters have not read Britta’s diary and the Black Cat 
is present, the animal will try to make the Hunters under-
stand that the Devil’s Ladle is an item to be retrieved. If 
the Hunters help her, she will lead them to Britta’s house 
to find the diary.
Fourth Reset: Same as the previous reset. 
After the Fourth Reset:  Brigit is cleaning a table. If 
asked she will say that Bastian came to dinner with four 
other shady characters. They spent the whole time bab-
bling about tunnels and guard shifts. He paid for everyone 
and then they left.   
Brigit the Cook
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a good cook, the best. I love my tavern 
and can’t stand any argument between my tables.     
The Old Barracks
Descriptor: A dirty old building where soldiers on leave 
can sleep for free.
Information: Bastian sleeps in the Old Barracks, but 
has never told anyone about it because it would damage 
the image of wealth and prosperity he is trying to build 
in Alena’s eyes. Bastian has joined forces with four other 
deserters who will help him in his plan to rob Villa Wa-
gner. In the Barracks, many people know Bastian and will 
describe him as a blowhard, but too stupid to actually 
amount to anything.
If Bastian is present: At night, Bastian sleeps in a se-
cret room on the second floor of the building. If disco-
vered, he will try to escape by throwing himself out the 
window to cause a Reset.
After any Reset caused in this Place:  Bastian will 
not go back to sleep but will use Ulrich to send messages 
to his co-conspirators.
Britta’s House
Descriptor: A small abandoned house near the gra -
veyard. In the garden, in the middle of a vegetable patch 
and near a well, there is an old hollow tree with no leaves.
Information: The Hollow Tree in the garden hides a 
burrow that leads to a series of Caves. Descending into 
the Caves, Hunters can find Britta’s decapitated body. The 
witch’s spirit is still bound to the body, but the only way to 
communicate with her is with a Gift that allows it. In the 
little house, Hunters can find Britta’s diary open on the 
kitchen table. In the diary, the witch describes in detail

141
her agreement with Bastian to retrieve the Tinderbox from 
the Caves, and the soldier’s insistence on having more mo-
ney after the reward. In the diary, Britta also regrets asking 
for Bastian’s help, as the soldier turned out to be a stupid 
opportunist.
Resets: Bastian will never be in Britta’s House, and the 
Cat is busy collecting magic items to revive the witch, so 
the situation in this Place is always the same, no matter the 
Reset. 
Caves under the 
Hollow Tree
Descriptor: A series of caves that were once the home of a 
witch, but are now uninhabited.    
Information: Traces of ancient magic and strange mar -
kings on the walls are the only evidence of what the Caves 
once were. Britta’s headless corpse stands in the center of 
the first room, her hands and feet making unconscious mo-
vements, making it clear that the body is not dead, but also 
incapable of action.
Britta the Beheaded Witch
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled witch who only desires a quiet 
life. Since I no longer have a head, I cannot speak or utter 
magic formulas. 
Agenda: To find my head that Bastian threw in the river. 
Information: Britta’s body will revive when two of her 
three magical items are placed on her. In this state, Britta 
will not be able to speak, but she will be able to write, in-
forming the Hunters that she knows where her head is and 
will go to retrieve it. In the meantime, Britta will ask the 
Hunters to retrieve the third object to complete her resur -
rection. 
Britta the Witch
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled witch who wishes only to live a 
quiet life, using my powers only to help others.
Agenda: Stop the power of the Magic Tinderbox before the 
Demonic Dogs inside it become too powerful.    
Gift - Britta’s Witchcraft:  Britta can perform various 
rituals to achieve different effects: she can find someone or 
something, she can change the weather in the area, or she 
can communicate with and take control of an animal. All of 
these rituals take about ten minutes to complete. Britta can 
also dispel magic, a practice in which she is a true master. 
In a single action, Britta can end a spell or block the power 
of a Treasure of the Order until the end of the Scene. 
Information: Britta will tell the Hunters the secret of the 
Magic Tinderbox and that she can use her magic to stop the 
disasters Bastian is causing, but there is a catch. Once the 
Magic Tinderbox’s power is blocked, the three Demonic 
Dogs inside will be released and must be defeated to send 
them back into the magical object.

142
Demonic Dogs
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC  
(2 Wounds - 3 Dogs)
Descriptor: We are ancient demons and take the form of 
dogs to mingle with humans. We can fly and with our fangs, 
we tear at the soft flesh of our prey. 
Agenda: To kill as many humans as possible and thereby 
increase our power.     
Gift - Demonic Powers:  These creatures can fly, with 
all the narrative benefits that brings. As supernatural crea-
tures, Demonic Dogs are immune to all effects of fear and 
mind manipulation. Those who use magic against them 
take 1 Penalty to all related Checks and must pay 1 additio-
nal Soma if it is required to activate magic. A Demonic Dog 
can spend 1 Reset Token to increase its OL to Hard 7 for a 
single action, but not to defend itself. 
Information: When the Demonic Dogs are defeated, 
Britta or a witch who has read her diary can place a ma-
gical seal on the Magic Tinderbox: 
the object will lose some of 
its power and become the 
Treasure of the Order, 
The Tinderbox of Se-
cond Chances (see page 
28).  
Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to The Last 
Wish Scenario and can be acquired once conditions are 
met. A Hunter can acquire one Scenario Gift. If they ac-
quire a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Déjà vu 
Requirement: Witness the effect of one or more Resets.
By experiencing temporal shifts, you are able to notice the 
small differences in the fabric of reality. Once per Scene, 
you can spend 1 Soma to activate each of the following ef-
fects:
1. I’ve Already Got It: Y ou already have an item that be-
longs to the Place you are in, and if it is a unique item, no 
one has noticed that it is missing.
2. I Was Already Here: Y ou can move to any Place you 
can see from where you are without anyone being able to 
stop you; to the eyes of anyone present, it will look as if you 
have always been where you are now.
3. I’ve Seen It Before: The Storyteller will reveal the 
effect of a particular Gift or equipment from an NPC in 
front of you that you must have known or met before.
Note: This Gift can only be used when the use of a 
spell that affects the normal flow of time is manife-
sted, like a Reset. Outside of this Scenario, it is up 
to the Storyteller to determine if the Hunter can use 
this Gift again.

143
Tremble, the Witches are Back
Requirement: Learn from Britta the magic of infusing an 
object with the essence of life.  
A portion of your vitality is infused into an item in your 
Equipment. As long as you have the item with you, once 
per Session you can restore 1 Wound and 1 Soma by cal-
ling upon the power of your own soul. Y ou can activate this 
effect even if you have already taken your last Wound and 
are unconscious.  
The Last Wish 
in Scenes 
FIRST SCENE –  Entering Hei-
delberg
It is the same as on page 134, except that after Bastian’s Re-
set, Hugo will give the following information to the Hun-
ters:
 ◆ Hugo is angry because Bastian bought some pots in 
Britta’s name a week ago and never paid for them.
 ◆ Bastian is no good and a layabout, bragging that he 
is an honorably discharged soldier, but when asked 
to show the medal he received for his discharge, he 
always prevaricates. No one knows where he sleeps, but 
he is always seen hanging out at the Drunken Hussar’s 
Gambling House, a cheap dive known for bad wine and 
gambling tables.
End of Scene: Hugo will then tell the Hunters that, if they 
want to find Bastian, they must go to the Drunken Hussar’s 
Gambling House; go to SECOND SCENE - The Drunken 
Hussar’s Gambling House.
SECOND SCENE –  The Drun-
ken Hussar’s Gambling House
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at what appears 
to be a small tavern, a few candles barely illuminating an 
old, rotting building that has not been cleaned in years. Grim 
faces stare at you, letting you know that you may not be wel-
come. Then the sound of a cup shattering makes you all turn 
around: Bastian is standing at the bar, watching you with 
wide eyes. He turns to the window behind him and makes a 
desperate escape. 
Place: The filthy Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House. 
Time: It is late in the morning, but there are already many 
customers at the tables trying their luck.
Information: Once Bastian is captured, the power of the 
Tinderbox will be activated and the Hunters will find them-
selves back in Heidelberg’s Entrance Plaza, where they will 
be approached by the Black Cat. It will be easy to tell by the 
light in her eyes that this is no ordinary animal. The Cat 
will lead the Hunters to Britta’s House, and here they can 
get their hands on the witch’s diary and understand what is 
happening. In the diary, Britta also notes the spell she used 
to imbue three objects with her life force, which, when re-
trieved, can bring her back to life. A Pendant, a Shawl, and 
a Ladle are drawn in the diary. The Cat will make it clear 
that she can lead the Hunters to the objects, pointing with 
her paw at the diary to figure out which one to start with. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters must decide in which order 
to retrieve the items, go to THIRD SCENE - The Retrieval.
2
1

144
THIRD SCENE –  The Retrieval
Note: This Scene will be played three times to allow the 
Hunters to retrieve all 3 of Britta’s items. The first two Places 
visited must be Villa Wagner and the Golden Plate Tavern, 
leaving the Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House for last. 
Places: Villa Wagner, the Golden Plate Tavern, and the 
Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House. 
Time: Early afternoon, late afternoon, and evening. 
Information: Depending on the Place, the following si-
tuations will occur.
 ◆ Villa Wagner: Alena will be kind and helpful to the 
Hunters and will gladly give them the Black Coral Pen-
dant if they ask for it. When the Hunters leave the Villa, 
they will be ambushed by the Cutthroats (page 135) 
hired by Bastian. 
 ◆ The Golden Plate Tavern:  Brigit will not want 
anyone to enter her beloved kitchen, and the Hunters 
must find a way to distract the cook and retrieve the 
Devil’s Ladle. 
 ◆ The Drunken Hussar’s Gambling House:  Ulri -
ch will attempt to poison the Hunters as described on 
page 136. Backed into a corner, Ulrich will say that 
Bastian is planning to rob Villa Wagner’s Treasury to 
get his hands on the family treasures. 
End of Scene:  If the Hunters have not recovered all the 
items, repeat this Scene at a new Place. If the Hunters also 
recovered the Shawl at the Hussar and questioned Ulrich, 
go to the FOURTH SCENE - Britta’s Return.
FOURTH SCENE –  Britta’s Re-
turn
Read aloud to the Hunters: The little cat leads you back 
to the house near the graveyard, and here, in the garden of 
the house, it starts scratching on the bark of an old hollow 
tree. At closer inspection, the tree appears to be the entrance 
to caves descending into the darkness.
Place: Britta’s House and the old Hollow Tree leading to 
her corpse. 
Time: Late night. 
Information: Upon recovering Britta’s body and appro-
aching her three objects, the witch will come back to life 
but will be headless. Britta’s animated body will then lead 
the Hunters to the nearby river to find her head. With this 
bizarre task completed, the witch will come to her senses 
and be able to explain to the Hunters the danger posed by 
the misuse of the Magic Tinderbox. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters have learned from Ulrich 
about the robbery Bastian is planning at Villa Wagner, go 
to the FIFTH SCENE - The Great Heist.
3 4

145
FIFTH SCENE –  The Great Heist 
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As you enter the large garden of Villa Wagner at dawn, a group 
of shadows draws your attention, and Bastian’s shout forces everyone to turn around. The soldier 
gestures nervously as he tells you with tears in his eyes, “Enough! Why are you always getting in my 
way? Leave me alone!” Then Bastian gestures to his comrades, and the small group approaches you 
menacingly. 
Place: The large garden of Villa Wagner. 
Time: The sun is rising, and soon the whole city will wake up. 
End of Scene: Unwilling to risk his life, Bastian will try to escape after giving the order to his 
people. If caught, he will activate the Magic Tinderbox for the umpteenth time and accidentally 
summon the three Demon Dogs. Having dealt with this threat, the Hunters must now decide 
what to do with Bastian. 
5

146
Journey of a Thousand 
and One Nights
 
A Scenario inspired by One Thousand and One NightsJourney of a Thousand and One Nights is a special Scenario constructed as a Scene Tree 
(see page 100 of the Broken Tales Core Book) and is well suited for staging the final 
confrontation with the Dark Presence, Sherazade the Weaver of Tales. This Scenario 
takes place within a story told by Sherazade, an emissary of the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows (page 196 of the Broken Tales Core Book). Awakening aboard a lost ship in 
a mysterious archipelago, the Hunters must retrieve enough Tarì (Arabic gold coins) 
to defeat powerful Djinn guarding the Dream Gate, the only way to return to the real 
world.
In this Scenario, Sherazade can be introduced in two ways.
As an Enemy. She follows the normal rules of a Dark Presence: Hunters can capture her 
once she is defeated in the Fourth Leg - The Execution of Sherazade. 
As a future ally. The One Who Plots in the Shadows urged Sherazade to abuse her power 
and lead the Weaver of Tales to self-destruction. In the Fourth Leg - The Execution of 
Sherazade, she will be a prisoner of the Sultan. 
Once upon a time...
Good evening my dear hosts, my name is Sherazade and I am a weaver 
of tales. My words will take you to incredible worlds and follow the feats 
of brave Hunters… however this story is not like the others and these 
feats are not just imaginary. Will our heroes succeed in their quest? Or 
will they perish, disappearing forever from the memory of time?
Safety Keywords: Blackmail / 
Imprisonment / Toxic relationships 
/ Animal violence / Slavery

147
The story that led us here...
Tired of the Hunters’ continued success, the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows commands Sherazade to cre-
ate the deadliest of tales to eliminate the Hunters once 
and for all. Since the power of Sherazade’s stories comes 
from the emotions the weaver evokes in the audience, 
Sherazade gives voice to the most powerful emotion she 
possesses: grief over the loss of her sister, Dinarzad. Thus 
a long tale is born, one that encompasses all the stories 
the weaver told in the vain hope of saving her sister when 
they were captives of the Sultan. Unable to cope with the 
pain of those memories, Sherazade fell prey to her own 
power and was eventually sucked into the story, as were 
the Hunters. Now back in the Sultan’s court, the weaver 
struggles to find a way back to the reality of Broken Eu-
rope. Absorbed by yet another of Sherazade’s tales, the 
Hunters instead wake up on a great ship, where they see 
an old acquaintance of theirs: Sinbad the Traveller of a 
Thousand Seas.
How to manage the Scenario
This Scenario unfolds as a ship voyage, where each Leg 
(randomly generated) will put the Hunters in front of a 
specific mission to face. If the problem is solved, the Hunt-
ers will receive a Tarì. Each accumulated Tarì will make the 
fight against the Djinn in the Dream Gate easier. 
The Scenario unfolds as follows:
1. First Leg - The group generates a Type A Leg.  
2. Second Leg - The group generates a Type B Leg.    
3. Third Leg - The group generates a Type A Leg.    
4. Fourth Leg - The Execution of Sherazade.
5. Fifth Leg -The group generates a Type B Leg. 
6. Sixth Leg - The group generates a Type A Leg.    
7. Seventh Leg - The group generates a Type B Leg. 
8. Eighth Leg - The Dream Gate.
Each Leg, with the exception of the Fourth and Eighth, 
presents a Place, Information, and a list of challenges to be 
solved in order to obtain the Tarì. The Storyteller is then 
free to develop the events according to their taste, and to 
decide when to end the event and begin the journey again. 
Each Leg should last a maximum of two or three Scenes, 
the equivalent of a Campaign Encounter. In this Scenario, 
calling for an Interlude will make the Fourth and Eighth 
Legs more complex, as explained in the relevant sections. 
To reduce the length of the Scenario, simply remove the 
Third and Fifth Legs, and remove the Seventh and Eighth 
Djinn in the Eighth Leg. 
The unique nature of this Scenario may require a great deal 
of improvisation on the part of the Storyteller, as each Leg 
provides clues and potential situations, but doesn’t go into

148
as much detail as typical Scenarios. There are two ways to 
manage this issue:
Preparation
The Storyteller creates all the Legs and sets up the Scenario 
in its entirety before proposing it for the Session. With this 
approach, the various Legs can be planned in detail, Places, 
and NPCs can be added, and the Villain’s Journey rules on 
page 43 can be used for additional ideas.
Improvisation
The Storyteller prepares nothing and details everything on 
the fly, generating the Leg when it is actually played. This 
system allows much more freedom for the table: for it to 
work best, it is recommended that the Storyteller give the 
Hunters all the Information they deem useful at the begin-
ning of the Leg, so the group can decide on their intentions 
right away. 
First Leg
Read aloud to the Hunters: The rocking of the waves ac-
companies your awakening. Startled, you jump out of your 
bunks. You fell asleep the night before in an inn on the road 
to Rome, and now, judging by what you can see through the 
porthole of a ship’s cabin, you are on the high seas. Once at 
deck, you can admire an entire crew of sailors, moving like 
an efficient swarm of ants, working diligently to maintain the 
course of a large sailing ship. “ Awake at last!” A rough, famil-
iar voice comes from behind you. It is that of Sinbad, an old 
sea dog who served the Order long before you.
Place: Sinbad’s sailing ship. 
Time: Early in the morning, the sun is high and bright. A 
soft, warm breeze blows, gently moving the sails. 
Initial Situation: Sinbad will greet the Hunters by trying 
to calm their confusion. This is not the first time the old 
sailor has ended up in a Sherazade story, and from what he 
heard in Rome, the Hunters know what he is talking about. 
Information: Sinbad will provide all of the following 
info. 
 ◆ The Hunters and Sinbad have found themselves in one 
of Sherazade’s stories. Sinbad was the first to wake up 
and by talking to a sea Djinn he was able to get some in-
formation. To return to the real world, it is necessary to 
pass through the Dream Gate, a magical portal located 
on the last island of the great archipelago that the sailing 
ship is passing through. The Dream Gate is guarded by a 
group of very powerful Djinn, and each of them requires 
a token to gain access to the door. What the Djinn crave 
is the magical Tarì.
 ◆ Several Legs will be required to reach the final island, 
each of which will give the Hunters one day to retrieve 
the Tarì. The coins are not simply hidden, but in the 
1

149
possession of a character from the story. Resolving the 
situations they encounter will be the only way for the 
Hunters to receive the Tarì as a reward.
 ◆ At the end of each day, the ship must leave port, because 
a giant wave in the shape of a turned page will sweep 
everything away, engulfing each island as if it had faded 
into history. The only way to escape the wave and save 
themselves from disappearing forever is to move on to 
the next destination.
Sinbad is very excited about this voyage, which will allow 
him to relive the glory of his youth 
as a sailor. The Hunters know 
that if they can keep the old sea 
dog from getting bored, he will 
do his best to make the Hunt-
ers’ journey easier. 
After providing all this infor -
mation, you can create a First 
Leg (A). 
Sinbad the Traveler 
of the Thousand Seas
Inspired by Sinbad of the Thousand and One 
Nights (Allied)
Descriptor: I am an experienced traveler and have seen 
every known land. I get bored easily if I’m not interested in 
what I’m doing.
1 Wound
Opposition Level: Medium 5
Agenda: I am willing to do anything to experience 
the thrill of discovery once again.
Special Agenda (Sherazade the Weav -
er of Tales):  I boast of knowing every story 
and legend, and I love to mock those who pride 
themselves on limited knowledge. I want to rid-
icule the stories Sherazade tells, highlighting the 
truths that only I know.
Gift - Traveler of a Thousand Places:  
Sinbad has seen the whole world and much 
of what the Broken Tales hide. In the 
course of a Scene, a Hunter can invoke 
his help to obtain information or 
create an additional Descriptor 
that represents a specific area 
of Sinbad’s knowledge. But 
the old sailor is an eccentric 
type, and to provide his help 
he first must be entertained. 
The Storyteller will evaluate 
the current situation and

150
Sinbad’s level of boredom. A Hunter can capture Sinbad’s 
interest with creative actions, as long as they’re appropriate 
to the current Scene.
Gift - I Know How to Do It:  Once per Scene, a Hunter 
can get 1 Additional Success in their Check, asking Sinbad 
for helpful advice. As with Traveler of a Thousand Places, 
Sinbad needs to be intrigued before helping. Sinbad be-
gins each Leg bored. If the Hunters take the old sailor with 
them, the excitement of discovery will immediately relieve 
Sinbad’s boredom. Alternatively, the Hunters can ask Sin-
bad to stay on the ship, and if they fulfill one of his requests, 
he will not be bored.
Sinbad Requests
Roll 1 die or choose.
1: Bring me a rare and precious item. 
2: Bring me someone who can tell me a good story.
3: Bring me rare and tasty food. 
4: Bring me a strange person. 
5: Bring me a sincere promise.
6: Bring me a picture of a beautiful place.
If Sinbad suffers all his Wounds, he will be out of action for 
the current and next Leg. In addition to his normal Gifts, 
Sinbad also has the Gift - The Sailing Ship.
Gift - The Sailing Ship:  Sinbad’s large sailing ship con-
tains all kinds of comforts and knowledge that the old sail-
or only makes available to his guests. At the end of a Leg, 
if Sinbad is not bored, he will allow each Hunter to choose 
one of the following effects:
1. Rest in the Sultan’s Rooms: To recover 1 Wound.
2. Meditate in the Lotus Hall: To recover 3 Soma.
3. Study in the Library of Alexandria:  To obtain the 
effect of a Descriptor Specialization in the following Leg.
4. Access Solomon’s Treasury: To obtain a Treasure 
of Order for the next Leg. 
5. Call of Duty (from the Fifth Leg onwards only):  
A new Hunter is summoned into the story by Sherazade, 
allowing the group to switch members or replace a Hunter 
who is out of action.

151
“A” Legs (First, Third, and Sixth)
Roll 1 die to determine the Leg. Increase the result by 1 
if you obtain a Leg you have already faced. Alternatively, 
choose directly which Leg you prefer to play.
1A The Merchant and the Djinn
The story that led us here...
The ship lands on a small island where there are only two vil-
lages of poor people. Some of the villagers throw themselves at 
the Hunters’ feet, begging for their help. A powerful Djinn is 
killing an innocent person every day, and the blame lies with 
a clever Merchant who escaped the spirit’s wrath by claiming 
to be honor-bound to settle a debt. Thirty days ago, the Mer-
chant accidentally killed the Djinn’s son in a heated argument 
over date seeds. Enraged, the Djinn showed up to behead the 
culprit, but the cunning man asked for a day to settle some 
unfinished business before he died. The Merchant then fled 
and has not been seen since. The Djinn demanded the entire 
population find him, or else he would cut off the head of an 
innocent person every day. 
Place: It is not possible to leave the island without a ship. 
Therefore, the Merchant must be hiding in one of the two 
villages. 
Information: The Djinn arrives in the village at dusk to 
see if the Merchant has been captured. The Merchant can-
not have fled the island, as the Hunter’s ship is the first to 
arrive in over a month.
Challenges: The Merchant has simply disguised himself 
as a local woman, and is impatiently awaiting the arrival 
of a ship like the Hunters’ . The Djinn is furious and resents 
being tricked. 
How to get the Tarì: If the Hunters succeed in appeasing 
the Djinn’s wrath without killing the Merchant, the Mer -
chant will give them the Tarì as a thank-you gift.
The Merchant
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a cunning old Merchant, I am a good 
mimic and I love to disguise myself. 
Agenda: Escape the island by secretly boarding a ship, or 
find a way to appease the Djinn’s wrath.

152
The Furious Djinn
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful Djinn, skilled with a scimitar. 
I hate lies and those who break agreements.
Agenda: To avenge my son’s death by punishing the Mer -
chant who caused it. 
Gift - Frenzied: When the Djinn suffers 1 Wound, it can 
immediately attack with its giant scimitar to strike the one 
who did the damage. 
2A The Island Fish
The story that led us here...
Sinbad’s ship has docked at a large island dense with plants 
that bear delicious fruits. The entire crew has gone ashore to 
resupply, but what no one can imagine is that this lush land is 
actually resting on the back of a giant, sleeping fish. While the 
sailors are busy harvesting fruit, the island begins to tremble 
and the land cracks. Sinbad begs the Hunters to save his men, 
or no one will be able to sail the ship to continue the journey.  
 
Place: A large natural island filled with plants and fruits. 
Information: Sailors are divided into six groups, each in a 
different part of the island. 
Challenges: Traveling by land is very dangerous due to the 
constant movements of the Island Fish. When a group of 
sailors is rescued, roll 1 die: if you roll a 6, the sailors are 
carrying a strange barrel found in the vegetation. If a 6 is 
never rolled, the barrel is automatically in the hands of the 
sixth party.
How to get the Tarì: Found inside the barrel.
Threat – The Island Fish
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: The earth cracks and trees fall on those unlucky 
enough to be nearby. Seawater slowly submerges everything. 
Gift - The Island Moves!:  Moving around the island is 
very dangerous, rescuing a group of sailors requires at least 
2 Checks against this Threat, one to get to the group and one 
to return to the ship. The Check can only be made by the 
Hunter leading the search party. For each failed Check, roll 
1 die and apply the result to one of the Hunters.
1: Suffers 1 Wound caused by falling trees and rocks.
2: Receives 1 Penalty at the next Check due to mud.    
3: Loses 1 Soma due to fatigue. 
4: Loses 1 Equipment in the waves of the sea.
5: Loses more time than expected. Increase the OL of this 
Threat by 1 for those performing the next Check.

153
6: The Hunter falls into a crevasse and remains separated 
from the others, they will have to do a Position Check to 
return to the ship or be lost until the end of this Leg. 
3A The Roc
The story that led us here...
The Hunters are jolted from their beds, and to their horror, 
they discover that the ship is tilting dangerously. Climbing 
on deck, they see a giant bird perched on the bow, apparent-
ly intending to build a nest. However, the weight of the bird 
threatens to sink the ship...
Place: Sinbad’s ship in the middle of the sea. 
Information: Sinbad warns the Hunters that they must 
move the Roc calmly, for if the creature takes off at full 
power, it could completely overturn the ship. In the dis-
tance, a huge spire of stone juts out of the sea. Perhaps that 
is where the Roc came from.
Challenges: Move or knock down the Roc without mak-
ing it shake the boat. The stone island in the middle of the 
sea is constantly battered by great waves, and among the 
surrounding rocks are the remains of many nests that the 
Roc attempted to build, but which were destroyed by the 
fury of the sea. 
How to obtain the Tarì:  When the Roc leaves the ship, 
the Tarì can be found in the remains of its nest.
Roc
See Dark Presence on page 13.
4A Monkey Island
The story that led us here...
It is early morning when the Hunters are summoned by Sin-
bad: the ship has landed on a large natural island full of cute 
little monkeys. The problem is that seven sailors disappeared 
during the night, and tracks found on the deck lead Sinbad 
to believe that the monkeys kidnapped them. Sinbad asks the 
Hunters to explore the island and rescue his men. 
Place: A large wild island with a small volcano in the mid-
dle. 
Information: The Monkeys’ tracks seem to lead straight 
to the volcano, but what the Hunters don’t know is that the 
poor animals bring the unfortunates they capture to the 
volcano to appease the hunger of a ferocious Giant who, in 
the absence of prey, pounces on primates.
Challenges: Monkeys (2 per Hunter) trying to scare off  
the Hunters by throwing rocks from trees, or laying traps 
and false trails; the Elephant-Eared Giant that lives in the 
volcano. 
How to get the Tarì:  It is guarded by the Monkeys and 
they will give it to the Hunters if they eliminate the Giant.

154
The Monkeys
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are agile and fast, and we move quickly 
through the trees to escape threats. 
Agenda: Satisfy the Giant’s hunger to prevent it from de-
vouring us. 
The Elephant-Eared Giant
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a huge Giant of herculean strength, I 
have no natural predators on the island. I consider all crea-
tures smaller than me to be weak, and I am not afraid that 
they will harm me.
Gift - Elephant Ears: As a free action, the Giant can use 
its massive ears to create a gust of wind capable of hurling 
a human thirty feet. The gust affects 2 targets and can be 
avoided with a Defense Check. If someone is pushed back, 
roll 1 die:  
1-2: Loses an Equipment.
3-4: Suffers 1 Wound.
5-6: Ends up in impassable terrain, requiring a Check to 
get out.  
5A An Old Bride
The story that led us here...
The ship lands on a large island that is familiar to Sinbad. 
The old sailor goes down with some of his crew to resupply, 
but only one of his men returns. The survivor tells the Hunt-
ers that Sinbad once married the daughter of the island’s king, 
but she died shortly after the wedding. According to local tra-
dition, Sinbad was buried alive with his bride to be united 
with her forever. The cunning traveler, however, managed to 
escape, and now that more than fifty years had passed, he be-
lieved that all had been forgotten by the new ruler. But to his 
astonishment, Sinbad and his people found themselves in the 
presence of a centenarian king who did not hesitate to return 
poor Sinbad to the tomb of his wife. 
Place: A large pyramid-shaped tomb not far from the 
town where the island’s ruler resides. 
Information: The tomb is protected by a notorious order 
of Guardians who are said to devour intruders alive. 
Challenges: The tomb is patrolled by over a hundred 
Guardians. Entering it requires some sort of distraction. 
On the east side of the tomb, there is a crack in the wall 
through which a person can safely pass. While waiting for 
it to be repaired, a team of six Guardians stands watch by 
the wall. The king is the only one who insists on enforcing

155
the old traditions, but no one dares to contradict him. The 
old monarch lives protected by hundreds of guards, but his 
death would end the absurd law. 
How to get the Tarì:  In the funeral ritual, the groom is 
given seven loaves of bread and some water. Back on the 
ship, Sinbad takes a bite of the last loaf, breaking a tooth on 
the Tarì hidden inside. 
Guardians of the Tomb 
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are an order of fighting Guardians, and 
our vow of loyalty comes before all else. Only the taste of hu-
man flesh can distract us from our duty.
Agenda: Defend the tomb from intruders. 
6A The Crystal Cliffs
The story that led us here...
Overwhelmed by a strong current, the ship is pushed toward 
a large group of rocks that Sinbad recognizes as very danger-
ous. The crystal that makes up the rocks is fragile, but also 
extremely sharp, and touching them would damage the ship’s 
hull. Beneath the crystal layer, the “rocks” are made up of all 
sorts of precious stones, and in each of them lies a real trea-
sure. Even though the main goal is to get out unharmed, there 
might be a Tarì among all the riches.  
Place: A large group of crystal rocks reflecting the sun-
light.
Information: It’s not easy to shatter the crystal without 
dangerous shards flying everywhere. Sinbad can keep con-
trol of the ship, but it will be up to the Hunters to clear the 
way. 
Challenges: Destroy the crystal rocks that block the path 
and recover valuable items from the inner rocks. At least 4 
rocks must be cleared to cross this part of the sea. 
How to get the Tarì:  If the Hunters find at least 5 valu-
ables, the Tarì will also be in the sixth rock.

156
Threat – Crystal Rock
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: I am a fragile, sharp agglomeration of crystal. 
The wrong vibration can make me explode.        
Note: The Storyteller rolls 1 die or chooses from the list to 
complete the Threat Descriptor. 
1: A flock of seagulls nested among the rocks. 
2: Part of me juts underwater dangerously. 
3: My crystal is harder than normal. 
4: I am, in reality, a floating block moving without control.
5: My crystal reflects sunlight much more intensely than nor -
mal. I blind those who approach.
6: I am filled with subtle overgrowths extending in every di-
rection.
Gift - Sensitive to Vibration:  If a crystal is destroyed 
without obtaining a positive Outcome on a Check, the ship 
receives 1 Damage Token. The person who caused the explo-
sion gets 1 Wound and loses 1 Soma. If there are 2 or more 
Damage Tokens on the ship at the end of the Leg, Sinbad will 
not be able to use his Gift - The Sailing Ship, regardless of 
whether he is bored or not, and will go directly to the next 
Leg.  
“B” Legs (Second, Fifth, and Seventh)
Roll 1 die to determine the Leg. Increase the result by 1 if you 
obtain a Leg you have already faced. Alternatively, choose di-
rectly which Leg you prefer to play.
1B Raging Rocs
Note: This Leg can only be played after Leg 3A - The Roc. If 
the Hunters have not yet faced it, consider this a 1A roll.
The story that led us here...
Two huge winged figures appear in the sky, identical to the large 
bird that tried to nest on the ship. The creatures have huge boul-
ders in their talons and seem intent on dropping them on the 
deck. Sinbad begs the Hunters to save the ship before he cau-
tiously flees below deck.  
Place: Sinbad’s ship in the middle of the sea. 
Information: Sinbad has hidden a Roc egg that he secretly 
retrieved from the nest the last time around. The old sailor is 
too greedy to return it, and intends to use the Hunters to get 
rid of the two Rocs. 
Challenges: Deflect the fall of the 4 boulders the Rocs in-
tend to drop on the ship and convince Sinbad to return the 
egg.
How to get the Tarì: When the egg is returned, one of the 
Rocs will drop the coin on the deck of the ship. 
Roc
See Dark Presence on page 13.

157
2B The Elephant Cemetery
The story that led us here...
The ship lands on a small island that looks like an elephant 
graveyard. Another ship has landed on the opposite side, fly-
ing a pirate flag. Sinbad will tell the Hunters that the island is 
home to a community of peaceful elephants and an old Giant 
friend, Rull. The pirates are ivory hunters, and Rull and the 
elephants may be in danger.
Place: A small island full of vegetation and elephant bones. 
Information: The Pirates have captured almost all of 
the elephants and used them as hostages to force Rull to 
surrender. Not content with taking all the ivory from the 
graveyard, they have set up a veritable factory in the Giant’s 
hut, cutting the tusks from the elephants that are still alive. 
Rull has been badly wounded by the Pirates and, even if 
freed, will be unable to help the Hunters. 
Challenges: Defeat the Pirates to free Rull. The Pirate 
Captain is a cunning old buccaneer, and if he understands 
the Hunters’ strength, he will use Rull as a hostage to force 
their surrender. In total, there are twenty Pirates plus the 
Captain on the island. However, there are only eight on 
their ship.
How to get the Tarì:  If Rull is freed, he will donate the 
coin as a thank you. 
Ivory Seeking Pirates
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are ruthless pirates who win every battle 
with our sabers and guns.    
Agenda: Obey the captain’s orders. 
Gift - Pirate Crew: A Pirate increases their OL by 1 when 
the Captain is present. 
Captain of the Ivory Seeking 
Pirates
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an old sea wolf and I have faced all kinds 
of adventures. I am too old to be afraid of anything. 
Agenda: Earn as much as possible and risk as little as pos-
sible. 
Gift - Old Bastard: The Captain is adept at using the sit-

158
uation to his advantage. Once per Exchange, if he suffers 
1 Wound, he can sacrifice a Pirate in his place, placing the 
casualty between himself and the threat at the last second. 
Once per Scene, the Captain can interrupt the effect of a 
Gift that instills fear or restricts the actions of his Pirates 
toward an enemy by telling them how he overcame that 
problem before. 
3B Aladdin of a Thousand 
Wishes
The story that led us here...
The ship arrives at an island ruled by a young Sultan named 
Aladdin. Evil rumors of his deeds are whispered: it is said 
that he came to the throne by usurping the old regent, who 
was loved and well-liked by all. Aladdin seems to have a very 
powerful Djinn at his service, carrying out his every wish 
without fail. In reality, the Sultan has allowed himself to be-
come more and more perverted, because being able to accom-
plish anything he wants, he is plagued by a boredom that he 
cannot satisfy in any way. The inhabitants of the island are 
terrified by his constant and absurd demands, which often 
result in deadly games.  
Place: A large and wealthy city where ships from all over 
the world dock.
Information: The city is kept under control by the Eyes 
of Aladdin, giant eyeballs that suddenly appear on the walls 
of houses. This, too, is the result of the young Sultan’s wish 
to be sure that no one is plotting behind his back. In the 
slums of the city, Jasmina, Aladdin’s young wife and daugh-
ter of the former king, has taken refuge. The girl fled after 
her husband showed the first signs of madness, and she 
tries in every way to help the people of the city. Everyone 
knows that the only way to depose him is to take away the

159
lamp that grants his every wish. Jasmina knows of a secret 
passage that leads into the Sultan’s palace, as it is the same 
one she used to escape.
Challenges: Take the lamp from Aladdin, who lives holed 
up in his palace, protected by a large army of soldiers who 
constantly patrol the city looking for any potential threat.
How to get the Tarì: Once retrieved, the magic lamp will 
turn into the coin. 
Aladdin the Mad Sultan
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am drunk with the power the lamp gives me 
and I demand that the whole world do as I command. I have 
a background as a street thief and know all the tricks of the 
trade.      
Agenda: To find something to kill my terrible boredom.
Gift - Wishing Lamp: Whatever Aladdin wishes for will 
be granted by the Djinn who lives in the lamp. As an action, 
Aladdin can rub the lamp and ask for the following wishes:
1. Eliminate:  (as an action) The target is somehow 
knocked out and suffers all the remaining Wounds, or is 
teleported to a place where they cannot harm Aladdin. The 
target can make a Defense Check to avoid the effect of the 
wish, but will still take 1 Wound even if it succeeds.
2. Create:  (1 time per Scene) Aladdin creates up to 4 
Monsters to do his bidding.
3. Block: (as an action) Aladdin will remove the effect of 
a spell or mind control from a subject. Alternatively, he can 
prevent the use of a Gift that he has already seen in action 
in the Scene.
Aladdin has already made some wishes to make himself 
more powerful, so he can act up to 3 times in an Exchange 
(but not back-to-back).
City Guards
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are skilled soldiers defending the city.      
Agenda: We obey Aladdin’s orders because we fear his 
wrath, but we also want to stop his madness. 
Jasmina the Brave
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the true ruler of the island and would 
give my life for my people. I am charismatic and skilled with 
a scimitar.    
Agenda: Defeat Aladdin and restore the island’s fortunes.  
Monsters Summoned by Aladdin
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC  
(2 Wounds - 4 Monsters)
Descriptor: We are mindless monsters, using claws and 
tentacles to snatch prey.

160
Threat – Eyes of Aladdin
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Magic eyes that keep watch on the whole city. 
Gift - Big Brother:  At the beginning of each Scene, a 
stranger in the city must make a Defense Check against this 
Threat. Roll 1 die and apply the result to all subjects who 
failed.
1-2: Aladdin learns 1 Gift of the observed subject. During 
a confrontation with Aladdin, the learned Gift is automat-
ically considered blocked by the third effect of the Gift - 
Wishing Lamp. 
3-4: Aladdin turns an unsuspecting passerby into a Mon-
ster to attack the foreigners.    
5-6: A lightning bolt springs from the eye, dealing 1 
Wound to the target. A Defense Check can prevent this ef-
fect.
4B The Forty Thieves
The story that led us here...
The ship lands on a large, mostly deserted island with only 
one town. In the harbor, the Hunters meet Baba, a poor man 
begging for help in rescuing his brother, who has been kid-
napped by a band of terrible Thieves. They live in an oasis 
near the city, but no one has ever been able to pinpoint the 
exact location of their lair. Baba knows the magic word to 
access the hideout, having heard his brother speak of it. He 
will lead the Hunters to the oasis, promising them a share of 
the Thieves’ treasure as a reward. 
Place: A large oasis in the desert, full of palm trees and 
waterholes. 
Information: In fact, Baba has already killed most of the 
Thieves with the help of Dirana, a skilled slave girl in his 
service. However, the woman is trapped in the gang’s secret 
lair, and Baba plans to free her and recover the treasure. His 
goal is to get the Hunters to fight the remaining ten Thieves 
and lock himself in their lair, waiting for the survivors of 
the battle to leave. Baba will not tell anyone how to open the 
lair, saying that he cannot fully trust the Hunters without a 
demonstration of their good faith. 
Challenges: Confront the ten remaining Thieves, pre-
venting Baba from entering the secret lair alone and closing 
the door behind him. Dirana is a fearsome fighter and, once 
freed, will face the Hunters to defend her master.
How to get the Tarì:  If Baba fails to lock himself in the 
cave, he will offer the coin to the Hunters for their services, 
begging them to let him live. 
Baba the Poor Conman
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a cunning, poor man who struggles to get 
by. My greed is matched only by my nerve.
Agenda: Use others to eliminate the remaining thieves and 
keep the treasure all to myself.

161
Dirana the Faithful Slave
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fighting slave and fear nothing and no 
one. My kicks can even cut through metal as if it were plain 
paper.
Agenda: Baba saved my life and now I will do the same to 
repay him. 
Gift - Death Machine: As deadly as she is agile, Dirana 
can act 2 times during an Exchange. 
The Thieves
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are looters, skilled with scimitar and sling. 
Our numbers are our strength.
Agenda: Protect our lair and discover the password to enter, 
which only our late leader knew.
Gift - Band of Thieves:  A Thief increases their OL by 1 
as long as the total number of band members is greater than 
the total number of enemies. 
The Oasis 
Descriptor: A large, lush oasis that the Thieves have trans-
formed into the perfect hideout. 
Gift - The Lair:  While fighting inside the Oasis, Thieves 
can benefit from the improvements they have made to the 
place. Once per Exchange, one of these effects will be acti-
vated:
1. Trap: A large coconut falls on enemies, requiring a De-
fense Check against OL Medium 5 to avoid 1 Wound.
2. Shelter: A Thief can literally disappear from sight, re-
appear in another part of the Oasis, and then do their thing. 
No one can stop this movement.
3. Wave: A jet of water engulfs enemies, dragging them 
away. Up to 3 targets in a thirty-foot area must make a De-
fense Check against OL Medium 5 to avoid being tossed to 
the ground. 
5B The Sleeping Man
The story that led us here...
The ship lands in a large port city, where Sinbad meets Hazir, 
an old friend of his. Hazir was a very wealthy merchant, but 
now he has fallen on hard times. The man will explain to 
the Hunters that one day he saw a beggar on the street who 
looked exactly like him, and in order to take a little vacation 
from his work commitments, he suggested an identity swap. 
After a few days off, Hazir was ready to resume his role and 
pay the beggar handsomely, but the beggar, made greedy by 
the possibility of a life of wealth, had Hazir thrown out and 
threatened him to never show his face again. Now the impos-
tor lives under the guise of Hazir, and the poor merchant does 
not know how to regain his identity. 
Place: A large and rich port city full of markets and ships 
from all over the world.
Information: The Imposter resides in Hazir’s palace, just 
outside the harbor, and has enlisted twin warriors who are 
known to be unbeatable when fighting as a pair. The Im-

162
poster has instructed his men to behead Hazir if he even 
dares to approach the palace. Hazir has a moon-shaped 
birthmark on his back that the Imposter does not: this is 
a good way to show who is the original, as Hazir’s four 
daughters are aware of this feature.
Challenges: Defeat the twin fighters and prove that the 
Imposter is not the real Hazir.
How to get the Tarì:  If the Hunters can reach an agree-
ment between Hazir and the Imposter without using force, 
the Imposter will give them the first coin he received as a 
gift, the Tarì. 
Hazir the Poor Merchant
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am an honest merchant who has always 
shown respect for others. My wealth is as great as my good 
heart.    
Agenda: Return to my old life, perhaps without punishing 
the beggar, for I understand the reasons for his selfishness.  
The Imposter
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a poor beggar who pretends to be Hazir. I 
am good at adapting to any situation.
Agenda: To keep the life that was gifted to me. I am not evil 
and therefore I can play the role of the good Hazir well.   
The Four Daughters of Hazir 
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the Daughters of Hazir and we love our 
father because he taught us kindness and humility.  
Hazir’s Guards
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are well-trained in the protection of our 
master.
Agenda: Protect Hazir from all threats / Eliminate the beg-
gar who persecutes our master.

163
Fury and Wrath, the Two Deadly 
Twins 
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are two Djinn fighters who watch each oth-
er’s back. There is no match for our pair fighting technique.      
Agenda: Carry out every order of Hazir.    
Gift - Invincible Duo:  While fighting as a pair, Fury 
and Wrath cannot take Wounds. Any attack or method of 
wounding them will be blocked by one of the two Djinn. 
To avoid this Gift, it is necessary to use an effect that affects 
both Djinn at the same time or to separate them so that 
they cannot support each other. 
6B The Djinn King
The story that led us here...
The Hunters are summoned to the deck of the ship, where 
they will find a mysterious old man smoking a water pipe. 
The old man will ask them to run errands for him, and if they 
do a good job, he will reward them with a golden Tarì. The 
old man exudes a strong aura of power, and the Hunters un-
derstand that the strange figure is hiding his true nature. The 
ship lands on a large island and the old man claps his hands 
in satisfaction: it is time to begin. The Hunters must find nine 
golden columns scattered throughout the city and bring them 
back to him so he can furnish his palace.
Place: A large port city filled with temples and merchant 
districts.
Information: Finding the columns is only a matter of 
time for the Hunters, but they will encounter several people 
who need help. Even if they don’t know it, spending time 
with others will earn the Hunters a positive rating from the 
Djinn King. 
Challenges: Each column requires 1 hour of time and at 
least one Position Check. The list below shows where they 
can be found. Using Gifts and Descriptors for additional in-
formation can speed up the search. For every two columns 
found, roll 1 die to see who needs help, up to a maximum 
of 3 requests. 
The Columns
First: On the pier in the harbor, covered with seaweed and 
shells. / Second: In a dung-covered square used as a hitch-
ing post for horses and camels. / Third: In the inner court-
yard of a merchant’s mansion. / Fourth: In the middle of 
an olive grove, not far from the city walls. / Fifth: In the art 
collection of a wealthy Vizier. / Sixth: In the middle of a 
notorious tavern, as a pillar supporting the roof. / Seventh: 
In the wall of a watchtower. / Eighth: In the bedroom of the 
first daughter of the Sultan. / Ninth: In the hold of Sinbad’s 
ship, stolen and then forgotten by the old sailor years ago.
The Needy
1: A poor farmer has no money to buy seeds for the coming 
season. Without the precious seeds, he will not be able to 
feed his family. 
2: A child just five years old is lost and has wandered clear 
across the city from where he lives. He struggles to speak 
and is frightened, but no one seems to care about him. 
3: A poor sailor was poisoned by some villains because he 
won too much at dice. If he does not get an antidote he will 
die. 
4: A merchant was robbed of a precious gold crown he 
had bought for his daughter’s wedding. Without the crown,

164
promised as a gift to the groom’s mother, the wedding will 
be canceled and the man will have no peace. 
5: A brave slave girl rebelled against her tormentor, who or-
dered her to be hanged in the marketplace as punishment. 
Everyone looks on in horror, but no one dares to intervene. 
6: An old blind man has lost his faithful dog that helps him 
get around town. The animal has been taken by a greedy 
merchant, attracted by its ability to do as it is told.  
How to get the Tarì:  The old man will give the Tarì to 
the Hunters if they help those in need, even at the cost of 
restoring the columns.  
Fourth Leg - The Execution of 
Sherazade
The story that led us here...
The ship lands on the largest island of the archipelago, and 
Sinbad explains to the Hunters that the Sultan himself resides 
in the beautiful palace that towers over the great city. As they 
go ashore, the Hunters see several posters calling the people 
to a great event: the Sultan’s wedding to the young Dinarzad 
and the execution of her evil sister Sherazade (see the Broken 
Tales Core Book, page 200 for details). The event is less than 
an hour away, and Sinbad is concerned. If Sherazade, the 
creator of this story, dies, what will happen to everyone in it?
Note: If Sherazade is an enemy of the Hunters, her execu-
tion will be a trap set to remove her enemies from the game. 
Otherwise, she is truly a prisoner of the Sultan, who has 
forced her to create a Shadow of her dead sister Dinarzad. 
Place: The large town square, where a huge stage was set 
up for the wedding and the execution.
Information: A large crowd of people spills out of each 
street, providing good cover for the Hunters but also slow-
ing their movement. In the crowd are 4 Shadows of Sher -
azade, ready to intervene if anyone tries to stop the event. 
Sherazade is handcuffed to a precious wooden and gold 
chair. Behind her, a masked executioner wields a large 
scimitar. 
Meanwhile, the Sultan stands at center stage, hand in hand 
with Dinarzad, dressed as the most beautiful bride of all.
Challenges: Hunters must stop the execution. Depending 
on their relationship with Sherazade, they will face different 
situations.
Enemy: Sherazade will have the Hunters fight the Sultan’s 
Guards and the executioner to free her, and then she will 
unleash the 4 Shadows against the Hunters.
Ally: Sherazade is clearly in shock and unable to resist the 
Sultan’s psychological subjugation. The memory of what 
happened has led her to create an alternate ending to the 
story in which her sister can live happily with the Sultan 
and Sherazade can sacrifice herself in Dinarzad’s place. 
Breaking the Sultan’s hold on Sherazade will allow her to 
regain control of the Shadows and punish the Sultan once 
again by overcoming, once and for all, the painful memory 
of what she suffered.

165
How to obtain the Tarì: After capturing or freeing Sher-
azade, the ghost of Dinarzad will appear to thank the Hunt-
ers and deliver the Tarì. 
Sherazade (Dark Presence 
Opponent/Allied)
See Core Book, page 200.
Shadows of Sherazade (Dark 
Presence Opponent)
OL Medium 5 - (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We disguise ourselves as helpless citizens, but 
in reality, we are ready to strike with our shadow sabers. 
Agenda: Interfere with and complicate the lives of the Or -
der’s Hunters, preventing them from fulfilling their mission.
