Gift - Shadow Body:  A Shadow is a darker, sadder ver -
sion of the original. They can speak and behave like the 
character they are modelled after, but on a careful inspec-
tion they will appear cold and mechanical. Once per Scene, 
a Shadow can appear or disappear from the darkness by 
moving through walls and other solid obstacles. When it 
spawns, it increases its OL by 1 because of the element of 
surprise. When it suffers the Wound that would kill it, the 
Shadow dissipates until its turn in the Exchange. At that 
point, it will emerge from the darkness as if nothing had 
happened. The Shadow will not reappear if the area the 
Hunters are in is fully illuminated with light sources or 
magic. The only way to truly destroy the Shadow is to ex-
pose it to sunlight.
Sultan’s Guards
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the chosen Guards of the Sultan, and 
with our warrior skills we protect our lord.    
The Executioner
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a ruthless enforcer, and with one stroke of 
my scimitar I can cut a man in two.        
Gift - Art of Death: Wounds inflicted by the Executioner 
are not easily healed and will remain for the next Leg unless 
the Hunter can heal them with an Interlude, Gifts, or by 
using the Gift of Sinbad - The Sailing Ship. 
Epilogue of the Leg:  Whatever the outcome, Sherazade 
is now in the hands of the Hunters. She can be used as a 
Hunter for the next few Legs if the Players wish.

166
Eighth Leg - The Dream Gate
The story that led us here...
When the Hunters reach the end of their journey, where the 
sea seems to fall in an endless cascade, there is a tiny island 
with a large golden gate in the center. Eight Djinn, arms 
crossed, sit in a circle, blocking the way to the gate. The mo-
ment of reckoning has come. Sherazade will explain to the 
Hunters that they must defeat the Djinn to get through, and 
if they lose, the One Who Plots in the Shadows will take them 
all.
Place: The Island of the Dream Gate.
Information: The Djinn are not aggressive and will ask 
the Hunters for a Tarì as a toll. For every coin the Hunters 
hand over, one of the Djinn disappears. The Djinn collect 
the payment, starting with the Eighth and going down to 
the Second. The Djinn who remain without a token try 
to intimidate the Hunters into leaving, unsheathing their 
scimitars to protect the Dream Gate.
Challenges: Defeat the Djinn to gain access to the Dream 
Gate.
Djinn of the Dream Gate
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC  
(3 Wounds - the Guardian and the Servant)  
(1 Wound - All other Djinn)  
(1 Djinn for each undelivered Tarì)
Descriptor: I am a powerful Djinn, my body is pure magic 
and my strength transcends that of wretched humans. I pay 
respect to those who show purity of spirit and courage.          
Gift - Duty: Each Djinn is immune to any effect of mind 
control, deception, or magic that would influence them to 
relinquish their duty.
Gift - Power of the Djinn:  Each Djinn has a different 
power:
First - The Guardian:  If the Djinn receives 1 Wound, 
roll a die; on a roll of 5 or 6, the person who inflicted the 
Wound receives 1 in turn. 
Second - The Warrior:  Once per Scene, even if a De-
fense Check to avoid a Djinn attack succeeds, the target still 
suffers 1 Wound.
Third - The Mage: If an enemy uses a magic-related Gift 
in the presence of the Djinn, they must spend 1 Soma or see 
their spell dissolve without effect.
Fourth - The Servant: If a Djinn takes 1 Wound, it can 
transfer it to the Servant who takes it instead.

167
Fifth - The Wise: Once per Scene, as an extra action, the 
Djinn can block a Gift (except Dark Ego) that an opponent 
has just activated. This Gift cannot be used again for the 
rest of the Scene.
Sixth - The Thief:  After acting, the Djinn can make 1 
Hunter’s piece of Equipment disappear.
Seventh - The Sailor: After acting, the Djinn can move 
to attack a new enemy without being blocked.
Eighth - The Storyteller:  If a Hunter activates their 
Dark Ego, they must suffer a Wound even if they satisfy the 
Activator.
Epilogue – The End of the Journey
If the Hunters defeat the Djinn, they will be able to pass 
through the Gate and return to Broken Europe, Sherazade 
and Sinbad with them. If they lose the battle, the Hunters 
will awaken in a small, dark prison, captured by the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows. The Storyteller will have to de-
cide whether to create a custom Scenario for the Hunters’ 
escape, or to remove them from the Campaign and create 
a new group with which to continue and perhaps find the 
missing companions.

168
Isolde the Singer of 
Lost Love
A Special Scenario inspired by Tristan and Isolde
Once upon a time...
The restless spirit of a young woman killed by injustice reclaims her lost love. Reborn as an emissary of the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows, Isolde, the Singer of Lost Love, stalks the Hunters of the Order, convinced that it was the Order itself that 
helped her rival in love plot the deception that led to the death of her beloved Tristan. But even the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows has forgotten that the power of love can work true miracles and that one of his most trusted agents can become the 
greatest threat.  
Safety keywords: Persecution / Depression / Possession

169
How to use Isolde 
the Singer of Lost 
Love
Isolde the Singer of Lost Love is a special Scenario 
that can be used during the course of a Campaign. Once 
introduced into the game, Isolde will become a recurring 
NPC in Encounters during the Hunter’s travels, holding 
a growing grudge against them. Isolde begins the game 
as an agent of the One Who Plots in the Shadows, but 
she can become a loyal Ally Dark Presence if the Hunters 
show her that they value love.
Isolde may be introduced into the Campaign as an addi-
tion to an Encounter (page 253 of the Core Book) during 
Hunter journeys. Alternatively, Isolde can appear during 
an Encounter in Rome (page 60 of this book). Isolde 
will never appear during Scenarios. 
Isolde the Singer of 
Lost Love 
(Dark Presence Opponent)
Once upon a time, there was a couple united by an impossi-
ble love. His name was Tristan, and he was a distinguished 
knight. Her name was Isolde, and she was the daughter of an 
important Welsh nobleman. They first met by chance when 
Isolde was tending to the wounds Tristan had received from 
killing a terrible monster. An overwhelming bond of love 
formed between them, even though they were both already 
married. For many years there were secret meetings to con-
summate a forbidden love, increasingly tested by the suspi-
cions of their respective spouses. Finally, Tristan’s wife discov-
ered the betrayal. Furious and humiliated, the wife arranged 
a diplomatic voyage to the kingdom where Isolde resided, 
knowing Tristan would be excited to accompany her. The ship 
fell into a planned ambush. Tristan fought bravely but even-
tually perished under the blades of the enemy. Isolde waited a 
long time for her beloved’s arrival. When the messengers sent 
in search of the ship returned, the young lover learned the 
sad truth and fell into a deep depression. She stopped smiling, 
stopped eating, and finally stopped breathing. As she fell into 
her death sleep, she dreamed of her beloved Tristan... When 
she opened her eyes again, the world had changed: she could 
go anywhere she wanted and bend people to her will. She had 
been reborn as a wraith, thanks to the power of the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows. Her savior gave her the chance to take 
revenge on Tristan’s wife, and Isolde didn’t hesitate, stalking 
her until she went mad.

170
OL Medium 5 - (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an invisible spirit born of death and love, 
and I watch the Hunters to judge them. When I manifest my 
power, my body becomes tangible.
Agenda: Spy on Hunters and report their weaknesses to the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows / Test Hunters to understand 
their relationship with love.
Gift - Immortal Soul:  Isolde is an intangible, invis-
ible presence that cannot be seen or harmed in any way. 
Once per Scene, Isolde can possess an NPC by completely 
controlling them. The possession is nearly undetectable, 
though there is an unconscious “attitude” that Isolde adopts 
regardless of who she is possessing. If the possessed NPC 
suffers 1 Wound, Isolde is ejected from their body and 
leaves the Scene. Note: For tips and advice on managing 
this Gift, see page 171.
Gift - Punish Those Who Despise Love:  When the 
rage against the Hunters reaches its peak, Isolde takes hu-
man form, loses her damage immunity, and lashes out at 
the guilty. When this Gift is activated, Tristan, the Knight 
who Conquers Death for Love, rises from the ground, 
ready to assist Isolde. Isolde gains 2 Betrayed Love Tokens, 
which she can add to those she has accumulated from pre-
vious Encounters. After acting in the Exchange, Isolde can 
spend 1 or more Betrayed Love Tokens as an extra action 
to activate the following effects, each of which can only be 
triggered once during the same Exchange. 
1. Blades for Traitors: A blade of light appears to strike 
a target within thirty feet. Avoiding the attack requires a 
Defense Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid 1 Wound. 
2. Exile for the Hater:  Chains of light emerge from the 
ground to immobilize a target, who can make a Defense 
Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid them. A blocked target 
cannot act and must use their entire Exchange action to 
free themselves from the chains.
3. Freedom for the Lover: Isolde and Tristan disappear 
and reappear at a maximum distance of thirty feet from 
where they were. No one can stop this movement.

171
Tristan, the Knight who 
Conquers Death for Love
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a knight devoted to my beloved Isolde 
and have returned from the dead to protect her. 
Agenda: Defend Isolde from those who would harm her.
Gift - Immortal Love: Tristan is immune to all Wounds 
as long as Isolde has at least 1 Wound. The only way to 
eliminate the Knight is to reduce Isolde to 0 Wounds, caus-
ing Tristan’s immediate destruction. When Tristan attacks 
a target that has wounded Isolde, he increases his OL by 1 
and inflicts 2 Wounds. 
•
How to handle Isolde the Singer of 
Lost Love
When Isolde enters the Scene, the Storyteller decides or 
rolls a die to see which NPC she possesses. Using this ad-
vantage, Isolde will deliberately try to create problems, us-
ing any hurt feelings caused by the actions of the NPCs or 
Hunters involved in the Encounter. At the end of the En-
counter, Isolde will evaluate how the Hunters acted based 
on two parameters:
 ◆ If the Hunters take feelings into consideration and help 
those in need even at the expense of their personal 
goals, Isolde will not report them to the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows. 
 ◆ If the Hunters did not care about the feelings of oth-
ers, Isolde will punish them by reporting information 
about them to The One Who Plots in the Shadows, 
which the Storyteller can use in the final confrontation 
of the Campaign or to better prepare a future Assassin 
(page 249 of the Broken Tales Core Book). Isolde re-
ceives Betrayed Love Tokens equal to half the number 
of Hunters directly involved (rounded down). She can 
use the Tokens in the confrontation with the Hunters 
when she activates the Gift - Punish Those Who De-
spise Love. 
Hunters can recognize Isolde’s presence by certain details 
that the spirit includes when she possesses an NPC or ap-
pears as herself. She should have a certain way of speaking 
or an attitude that will be characteristic of any possessed 
NPC, regardless of their background or social status. When 
the Hunters ask questions about the strange behavior, Isol-
de will either play dumb or try to shift the focus to the 
problems she is creating for the Hunters to solve. 
If the Hunters decide to corner the NPC because they real-
ize something is wrong, Isolde will appear suddenly to acti-
vate her Gift - Punish Those Who Despise Love. 
Note: Introducing Isolde during an Encounter may require 
a small amount of planning on the part of the Storyteller; 
we have included a series of tables that can provide useful 
hooks for Isolde’s management.

172
Who is possessed
1-2: An NPC not directly involved in the Encounter.
3-4: One of the Main NPCs involved in the Encounter.
5: A passing Dark Presence where the Encounter takes place. 
6: An Agent of the Order (see page 59) present where the Encounter takes place.
How she tries to test the Hunters 
1: Shows the Hunters the love felt by one of the NPCs in the Encounter.
2: Puts an innocent NPC at risk.
3: Helps an NPC express their feelings.
4: Highlights an injustice in front of everyone. 
5: Reveals to everyone who the Hunters are.
6: Introduces a tense situation, for example, something about a Hunter’s past. 
Attitude she repeats for every possession
1: Very low tone of voice and always speaks gently. 
2: When she hears the word Love, she cannot hold back a tear.
3: When she talks about feelings she always puts her hand over her heart. 
4: When she makes a request, she always ends her sentence with, “Please, in the name of 
l o v e .”
5: She always carries a heart-shaped pendant, a gift from her beloved Tristan. 
6: She speaks with a distinct Welsh accent.
Note: The attitudes in the list are in descending order of difficulty for Hunters. 
Numbers 1 and 2 are attitudes that Players may not pick up on within a few 
Encounters. Attitudes 3 and 4 are easy to identify but only occur in certain 
situations. Attitudes 5 and 6 are highlighted by the Storyteller when the 
Hunters talk to or observe the NPC in front of them.

173
Isolde the Spirit of Love
(Dark Presence Ally)
A spirit that fights for love, ready to punish those who trample 
on the feelings of the innocent. 
OL Medium 5 - (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am an invisible spirit who watches and judges 
those who do not respect love. I can take control of ordinary 
people by entering their bodies and using them to interact with 
the world.                          
Agenda: To help all those who believe in love. 
Gift - Possession: Once per Session, Isolde can take con-
trol of an NPC’s body to interact with the material world. 
Isolde maintains a recurring behavior with each possession, 
as explained on page 172. Isolde does not learn what her 
victim knows. Upon awakening, the victim will be aware of 
having been controlled by a spirit entity, but will not be able 
to describe the experience. The possession ends when the 
NPC suffers 1 Wound or at the end of the Scene following 
the one in which this Gift was activated. 
Gift - Love Triumphs:  At the beginning of the Scenar -
io, Isolde receives 3 Poltergeist Tokens, which she can use to 
intervene in the material world and manipulate objects. A 
Hunter may request Isolde’s help to manipulate an object or 
to gain 1 Additional Success during a Check. The Storytell-
er evaluates the Hunter’s objective, and if it is in accordance 
with Isolde’s Agenda, help is granted. If a Hunter’s actions 
are in blatant conflict with Isolde’s Agenda, the spirit uses 1 
Poltergeist Token to get in the Hunter’s way by moving an 
object or giving the Hunter 1 Penalty. Once per Scene, if the 
Hunter’s actions help an NPC manifest or protect their feel-
ings, Isolde gains 1 Poltergeist Token.
Isolde the Singer of Lost Love in a 
Broken Tales Campaign
By her very nature, Isolde, the Singer of Lost Love, is an ex-
cellent addition to a Broken Tales Campaign, as she brings a 
great deal of variety to make the Encounters and non-Sce-
nario phases more interesting. Isolde is an emissary of the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows, but if the Hunters can con-
vince her of their good intentions, she will become Isolde the 
Spirit of Love, an allied Dark Presence, and will be able to aid 
the Hunters in their endeavors.

174
The King’s 
Awakening
A Scenario inspired by the Arthurian Myth
Once upon a time...
A mysterious island shrouded with mist appeared off  
the coast of England in the North Sea. According to the 
tales of superstitious sailors, the strange island could 
only be the legendary Camelot. The English Crown, 
concerned about the growing unrest among the people, 
sent a ship to understand the nature of this dark place, 
but the expedition was swallowed by the mists and never 
heard from again. The trusted royal advisor Merlin, 
who had a history of working for the good of the Crown, 
returned to London claiming to know the secret of the 
mysterious island. Merlin’s tale prompted the Crown to 
request the Order’s intervention to avert a threat that, 
as the envelope reads, “could not be matched by all the 
armies of the world. ”  
Security keywords: Desecration of graves / Coercion 
through the use of force / Persecution

175
The story that led us here...
Throughout his millennia-long existence, Am-
brose the Sorcerer, known as Old Merlin, has controlled 
the destinies of kingdoms and empires. He has used his 
arcane powers to determine their glorious beginnings 
and miserable ends, with the long list of beneficiaries 
including the legendary Camelot. Merlin manipulat-
ed the ruler Arthur from an early age, prompting the 
pure-hearted young man to unite many peoples under 
his leadership. But in time, each of Arthur’s successes an-
gered Merlin, who grew resentful that, even ignorant of 
the fact that he was merely a puppet, the king could turn 
the Sorcerer’s every trick to the benefit of those most in 
need despite Merlin’s selfish goals.
Blinded by envy, Merlin used his magic to strip Arthur of 
all his Virtues, one by one, gradually transforming a just 
and benevolent king into a bloodthirsty tyrant. Arthur’s 
reign began to unravel as the actions of the now-mad rul-
er incited the populace to rebellion.
One of the many quests undertaken by Percival, a knight 
in the service of Camelot, had led to the discovery of the 
Holy Grail, an artifact that could guarantee eternal life. 
Arthur had drunk from the magic cup, becoming im-
mortal and destined to rule forever. Backed into a corner, 
Merlin realized that control was slipping out of his grasp. 
Invoking the power of magic, he engaged in a battle with 
the king. After a grueling battle that lasted more than a 
hundred days, Merlin succeeded in sending Arthur into 
a deep sleep, plunging all of Camelot into the cold waters 
of the North Sea.
Tired and weak, Merlin took a long rest, leaving the 
world safe from his machinations for several centuries. 
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunter...
Merlin has ignored or forgotten that the one who found 
the Holy Grail, Percival the Seeker, also drank from the cup 
before delivering it to Arthur. By the time Camelot sank, 
Percival was on dry land, immortal in a world unknown to 
him. After hundreds of years of revelry, the Seeker began 
to go mad, until he decided to resurrect his old lord and 
reclaim his “life as a noble knight. ”
Percival threw the now-empty Grail into the water, awak-
ening Arthur and enabling the lord to break Merlin’s spell. 
Camelot then emerged from the water, along with the is-
land and all its people, unaware that they had been frozen 
in time for centuries. 
The awakening of the entire island has brought great up-
heaval: Arthur has given orders to prepare an army for the 
conquest of Europe. The mad king’s idea is to gather all the 
people of Avalon, turn them into undead, and march them 
to the mainland. Guinevere, always an innocent victim of 
Merlin’s machinations, has never accepted her husband’s 
madness and has fled in search of help. Hiding among the 
commoners of Camelot, the queen desperately seeks a way 
to cure her beloved Arthur, who has mobilized the Knights 
of the Round Table to find her and force her back to court.
Merlin, for his part, is determined to leave the dirty work 
in the hands of the Hunters. While the latter will go to great 
lengths to find Virtues to weaken Arthur, the old Sorcer -
er plans to recover and escape with the Virtue of Wisdom. 
Only by returning the quality of wisdom to Arthur can his 
senses be restored, turning him into a just and benevolent 
king to the people once more, perhaps willing to make a 
deal with the Hunters.

176
How to manage the Scenario
This Scenario unfolds as a long exploration of the island of 
Avalon, with the Hunters moving from Zone to Zone, ini-
tially knowing only the location of Camelot Castle, which is 
located in the center of the island. The Storyteller will then 
place the map of the island in the center of the table, show-
ing the Zone that includes the beach where the Hunters’ 
ships have landed.
Y ou can photocopy or print the map of the island on page 
187.
Moving along the map
During the Scenario, Hunters move from one Zone to 
another in an orthogonal way, so diagonal movement is 
prohibited. Each Zone presents situations and possible En-
counters to be dealt with. At the beginning of the Scenario, 
the Hunters are unfamiliar with the island and will learn 
what each Zone contains simply by moving around. How-
ever, interaction with some NPCs may lead the Hunters to 
information about a particular Zone and its contents. The 
Storyteller decides what information will be revealed as the 
Scenario progresses, keeping the narrative consistent and 
updating the map accordingly.
When Hunters call for an Interlude, the usual rules apply, 
with the exception that the NPC Agenda advances only for 
Merlin, as explained on page 179.
The Zones
Each Zone is detailed with a Description and some infor -
mation about what can be found there. The Description 
also includes NPCs and Threats that Hunters may choose 
to face. When Hunters arrive in a Zone, the Storyteller rolls 
on the Encounter Table (page 180) to determine if anyone 
is present. This process adds NPCs with their own Agendas, 
creating a random mix that can lead to fresh situations. The 
Storyteller is free to add NPCs to the Scene as they see fit, 
and is not obligated to introduce them immediately. 
The Storyteller is then free to develop the events as they 
wish. Some of the unique aspects of this Scenario may re-
quire a strong dose of improvisation, as each Zone provides 
a number of clues and potential situations, but does not go 
into too much detail compared to more classic Scenarios. 
Therefore, there are several ways to approach the Scenario:
With prior preparation
The Storyteller describes all the Zones, adds details that 
they find interesting, and completely sets up the Scenario 
before proposing it for the Session. In this way, the explo-
ration can be planned in detail, and locations and NPCs of 
Encounters can be added as needed in the chosen Zones, 
rather than being randomly generated. 
Improvising
The Storyteller does not prepare anything and details things 
on the fly, relying on the information provided by the Zone 
and the Encounter Table to determine which NPCs will be 
present.

1
177
Playing the Scenario with Village 
Rules
Because of its highly exploratory and survivalist nature, 
this Scenario lends itself well to being played using the Vil-
lage rules presented in The Broken Ones. Only a few mod-
ifications are required.
 ◆ The Villagers were part of the first expedition sent by 
the Crown and suffered the unfortunate fate of Cap-
tain Boyce’s crew. Zone 2 - Beach (page 188) becomes 
the initial Zone, while Zone 1 - Landing (page 188) is 
nothing more than an empty beach where the Hunters’ 
ship will land at the end of the Scenario.
 ◆ The Storyteller integrates information from Zone 2 - 
Beach (page 188) and Boyce’s journal to tell the story 
of Glatisant’s attack and the ensuing massacre. Boyce’s 
last wishes will guide the actions of the Villagers, who 
must gather as much information about the island as 
possible and await the arrival of the ship carrying the 
Order’s Hunters.
 ◆ The purpose of the Scenario for the Villagers is to ex-
plore the island and survive for at least 15 days. The 
Storyteller accumulates 1 Day Token to keep track of 
the time that elapses.
 ◆ The other survivors will also settle in Zone 2 - Beach, 
allowing a Player to use a possible Bond in case their 
Villagers die. If the group is far from Zone 2 - Beach 
(page 188), the Storyteller should consider introduc-
ing an Encounter with Survivors (page 180) to re-
plenish the ranks of the Villagers. 
First Scene
Read aloud to the Hunters: After two days of sailing, the 
silhouette of a large island appears before your eyes. Thick 
blankets of fog shroud the coast, making it almost impossible 
to discern its details. All you know is that all traces of the 
previous expedition on behalf of the Crown have been lost. 
“Hunters!” Old Merlin’s voice calls your attention. “We are 
about to enter a land of great and ancient magic that has lain 
dormant at the bottom of the sea for centuries. In Camelot, 
time has stood still, and what you will see is beyond the com-
mon concept of reality as you know it. ” You know that Merlin 
is a sorcerer of great skill, and even the Order seeks his advice 
when their affairs are muddled by magic and spells. He will 
therefore be your guide in this strange land as you attempt to 
defeat the great threat it poses. 
Place: A small uninhabited beach. A long promontory of 
rocks forms a natural jetty to the mainland. 
Time: Early in the morning, a thick blanket of fog makes it 
difficult to see much.
Initial Situation: The crew, led by Captain Bennett, pre-
pares the ship for its stay, while Merlin summons the Hunt-
ers to the beach to instruct them.
Information: Merlin appears willing to dispel the Hunt-
ers’ doubts, but will omit any details that might reveal his 
true intentions. Mainly, he will share with the Hunters what 
he sees fit about the following.
 ◆ In the center of the island stands the Castle of Camelot, 
where a king named Arthur resides. He is a legendary 
hero who became immortal after drinking from the 
cup of the Holy Grail. Long ago, Merlin was able to seal 
Arthur and the entire island in a magical sleep, pre-

178
venting the mad ruler from invading Europe. Arthur’s 
goal is to turn all the island’s inhabitants into the living 
dead and march with this army across the bottom of 
the sea to the mainland.
 ◆ Merlin will not reveal that he was the one who drove 
Arthur mad by taking away his Virtues. He will say, 
however, that Arthur’s old personality is kept in var -
ious objects hidden on the island as part of the spell 
needed to seal the place under the sea. Retrieving them 
and summoning their power in Arthur’s presence 
will gradually bring him to his senses and weaken his 
current tyrannical form. Facing Arthur without the 
Virtues is an impossible challenge, and Merlin rec-
ommends finding at least three of them. From the in-
formation Merlin has gathered, it appears that Arthur 
has no intention of leaving the castle, allowing plenty 
of freedom for the search.
 ◆ Merlin talks to the Hunters about 4 Virtues: Courage, 
Faith, Innocence, and Love. The old wizard deliberately 
omits mentioning Wisdom, as his secret goal is to re-
trieve it and escape from the island.
 ◆ Camelot has been affected by the most powerful magic 
ever cast: it has disrupted space and time, making this 
place unique and sometimes maddening. The Hunters 
must be prepared for anything and keep the inhabi-
tants in the dark about what has happened in the past. 
Many villagers are unaware that they have been frozen 
in time for hundreds of years, and causing panic would 
only make the investigation more difficult.
 ◆ Camelot is a land from a time when monsters and 
magic were not hidden and feared by mankind, and 
the Hunters must remember that.
 ◆ Merlin gives the Hunters a small ocarina. When he 
hears its sound, he will know that they need his help 
and will manifest as soon as possible (see Gift - Wise 
Advice, page 179).

179
Ambrose the Sorcerer known 
as Old Merlin
Inspired by Merlin from the Arthurian Myth 
(Neutral)
Descriptor: I am an ancient sorcerer, my body is weak, but 
my magical power is unmatched.
1 Wound
Opposition Level: Hard 7
Agenda: Pretending to be a wise old man who is always 
ready to help others with the proper advice / Gaining person-
al advantage by manipulating others.
Special Agenda (This Agenda replaces the one in 
the Core Book): Recover the Virtue of Wisdom and flee so 
that Arthur cannot come to his senses and the Hunters are 
forced to eliminate him. 
Gift - Ancient Sorcerer:  Merlin’s power is incredible, 
achieving practically anything he can think of through the 
use of magic. Those who rely on his advice will be able, once 
per Session, to activate the magic power that the wizard has 
infused within them. The magical effects must be decided 
at the time that Merlin blesses the character by defining an 
appropriate Descriptor such as: Taking flight like a bird or 
Having skin as hard as a stone. Whoever carries Merlin’s 
magic can activate its power once, obtaining the Descriptor 
for the entire Scene. Once the Scene resolves the magic is 
drained from the player and cannot be activated again.
Gift - Wise Advice: Merlin will never intervene person-
ally but can appear in a dream or vision to his protégés. 
Once per Scenario, a Hunter can spend 1 Soma to summon 
Merlin’s help. The sorcerer will appear with helpful infor-
mation or to bless the character with one of his spells.
How Merlin Acts in the Scenario
Merlin is the only NPC with an Agenda that advances re-
gardless of the Hunter’s actions. In general, the Storyteller 
must move Merlin to maintain his facade as an ally who 
will manifest when summoned. Merlin will also provide 
information and support to the Hunters as long as they do 
not interfere with his plans. The one thing Merlin always 
leaves out is the location of Zone 13 - Lake (page 198), 
where the Virtue of Wisdom can be retrieved. 
The progression of Merlin’s actions will be as follows:
Scene One: Merlin is in Zone 1 - Landing (page 188), 
briefing the Hunters on the island’s peculiarities.
Within  the  first  two  Interludes: Merlin will be in 
Zone 13 - Lake (page 198) with his servant Mordred to 
retrieve the Virtue of Wisdom (page 194).
By the third Interlude:  Merlin moves along the island 
to get to Zone 15 - Secret Landing (page 199). 
After the third Interlude: Merlin is on the boat in Zone 
15 - Secret Landing (page 199), trying to find a cure for 
the crew members.

180
Encounter Table
When Hunters arrive in a Zone, roll 1 die:
1-3: There are no Encounters, and normal exploration of 
the Zone continues. 
4-6: An Encounter occurs. Roll a die: if it comes up odd, 
it is a Group Encounter; if it comes up even, it is a Single 
Encounter. 
All Encounter profiles are introduced from this page. En-
counters provide additional NPCs that connect with sto-
rylines already present in the Zones. The Storyteller freely 
decides what kind of interaction is created between all the 
elements of the Scene, evaluating the NPCs’ Agendas in re-
lation to the Zone in which they are encountered. In the 
case of a repeated Encounter, the Storyteller must decide if 
it is still interesting and useful for the narrative. If not, the 
previous or next result in the table should be chosen. 
Odd / Group Even / Single
1: Survivors of the First 
Expedition
1: Sir Dagonet the Madman
2: Saxon Marauders 2: The Red Knight 
3: Knights on a Quest 3: The Scarred Knight
4: Villagers 4: Merlin's Shadow
5: Restless Knights 5: Guinevere the Fugitive 
6: Twrch Trwyth 6: Percival the Seeker 
Survivors of the First Expedition
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are Crown agents on the run, terrified by 
the danger of the island.
Agenda: To be able to escape from this cursed island.
Information: At each encounter, 1d6 Survivors will be 
available to talk about an adjacent Zone from which they 
are fleeing. The Storyteller decides what information the 
Survivors have. When talking about the previous expedi-
tion, the Survivors will tell you that as soon as they docked 
on the beach (Zone 2 - Beach page 188), the ship was at-
tacked by a huge creature that they describe as a collection 
of different animals. The crew fled in all directions, scat-
tering across the island as the ship was destroyed. Captain 
Boyce, who was in command of the ship, heroically stayed 
behind to wrestle with the creature so that everyone could 
escape.
Saxon Marauders
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are fierce marauders who have come to 
Avalon to conquer the island. We are experts in group com-
bat and support each other. 
Agenda: Attack and capture anyone we meet along the way.
Gift - Marauders: When attacking an enemy, Marauders 
increase their OL by 1 if they have numerical superiority.

181
Information: There are 2d6 Saxon Marauders per En-
counter. They speak an ancient Germanic language that can 
only be understood with a very specific Gift or Descriptor. 
Upon closer inspection, it becomes clear that the Maraud-
ers are from a bygone era. When a group of Marauders is 
eliminated, the Storyteller sets aside 1 Battle Token: these 
Tokens reduce the danger in Zone 7 - Battlefield (page 
193).
Knights on a Quest
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are knights in the service of Arthur, travel-
ing through Avalon on the orders of our king.
Agenda: Recruit every man and woman for the army that 
will march to conquer the world. No one can refuse to enlist.
Information: 1d6 Knights will attempt to recruit any 
NPC or Hunter present in the Zone, using force if neces-
sary. When a group of Knights is eliminated, the Story-
teller sets aside 1 Empty Seat Token: these Tokens reduce 
the number of Knights present in Zone 8 - Camelot Castle 
(page 193).
Villagers
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are poor villagers who sell vegetables and 
livestock.
Agenda: Travel between island settlements to conduct busi-
ness.
Information: Upon encountering them, the Storyteller 
rolls 1 die to determine which settlement the Villagers are 
from. The table below lists the information they can give 
the Hunters, and in parentheses, who can give the informa-
tion because they are from a particular settlement.

182
1-3: Village of Corrinton 
4-6: Town of Stow
 ◆ King Arthur has called for a general recruitment to 
form an army to invade Europe. Many Villagers are 
afraid to go to battle, but the Knights couldn’t care less 
and take everyone by force. (Corrinton/Stow)
 ◆ A fearsome creature has made its lair on the coast. It is 
a huge monster, hungry for flesh. Knights once hunted 
Twrch Trwyth, particularly tasty wild boars, and used 
their meat to satisfy the monster’s hunger. (Corrinton)
 ◆ Many Knights visit the settlements in search of queen 
Guinevere, who is rumored to have fled the palace 
without telling the king. Arthur is furious, and until 
the queen is found, the war against Europe cannot be-
gin. (Corrinton/Stow)
 ◆ Many people who do not want to go to war seek refuge 
in the town of Stow, which is located in the farthest 
part of the island from Camelot Castle. But it is only a 
matter of time before the Knights arrive there as well. 
(Stow)
Restless Knights
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are the souls of restless knights, resurrected 
at the behest of our king. Our decaying bodies advance cease-
lessly, waiting to march to war. 
Agenda: Kill all humans we come across to strengthen the 
ranks of our king’s army. 
Information: When encountered, the Storyteller rolls 
2d6 to determine the number of Restless Knights. Their 
horde attacks all living creatures, making no distinction be-
tween Hunters and NPCs. If the horde is not destroyed, it 
moves one Zone by attacking those it encounters. The Sto-
ryteller secretly decides which Zone the horde has moved 
to and does not make the Encounter roll when the Hunt-
ers arrive there. Any other damage done by the Peaceless 
Knights is at the discretion of the Storyteller.
Twrch Trwyth 
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a strong and fast boar, I know every cor-
ner of the island and all the secret paths.
Agenda: Find food without getting caught.
Gift - Magic Boar: Whenever the Twrch Trwyth avoids 
an action by someone who is hunting him, he gets 1 Es-
cape Token. As an action, and by spending 2 Escape To-
kens, the Twrch Trwyth can leave the Scene without any-
one being able to stop it. Even when dead, the body of the 
Twrch Trwyth continues to give off an inviting scent and 
glow with an intense magical light. Processing the Twrch 
Trwyth’s flesh can yield 1 Delightful Flesh token. Whoever 
consumes it will recover 1 Wound and 2 Soma.

183
Sir Dagonet the Madman
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor:  I am a crazy, violent knight and I will not hes-
itate to use my sword to get what I want. No one can afford 
to ignore me.    
Agenda: Eliminate any possible threat to my king / Find 
Guinevere, but instead of leading her back to Arthur, I plan 
to kill her and pass off her death as a tragic accident.    
Gift - Don’t Underestimate the Buffoon: Dagonet is 
a true master of foul play. During a battle, Dagonet can at-
tempt a low blow in addition to his action and choose one 
of the following effects:
1. Blade in the Boot:  Dagonet draws it after attacking a 
target and inflicts 1 Additional Wound. Only a Hunter with 
a dueling ability Descriptor can make a Defense Check to 
avoid it.
2. Sand in the Eyes: Activated before a Dagonet attack, 
the target cannot use any of their Gifts during the Exchange.
3. Find a Shield: When Dagonet is attacked, he can avoid 
1 Wound by having another closeby target hit instead. 
Dagonet cannot use the same effect on the same target 
more than once during the Scenario.
Information: Dagonet is a lunatic and will never reveal 
what he knows, even if he is defeated or tortured. However, 
being a true madman, it is possible to make him talk by 
provoking him during combat. Dagonet is on a mission for 
Arthur to find Guinevere, who has escaped from the castle. 
The king has a soft spot for his queen and will not start the 
war until she returns to his side. Dagonet also knows the 
whereabouts of Percival, whom he now considers useless 
to the cause. He has even tried to kill him, but one way or 
another, the knight always comes back to life (Zone 12 - 
Cliff, page 197).
The Red Knight
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an immortal spirit in a knight’s armor, 
and with my skeletal steed I travel all over Avalon in search 
of the one who killed me.
Agenda: Death has clouded my consciousness, and in every 
person I meet I see my hated enemy, Percival. 
Gift - Eternal Vengeance:  When Percival the Seeker 
appears on the Scene, if he is present, the Red Knight will 
attack, ignoring all other risks. If Percival is not present, 
the Red Knight will see the face of the hated opponent in 
the most skilled subject in combat among those present, 
activating this Gift as well. As long as the Red Knight has 
his target in sight, no effect can block or prevent him from 
attacking. In the heat of vengeance, the Red Knight ignores 
all other enemies and reduces his OL against them to Easy 
3. 
Information: The Red Knight is unable to communicate, 
and the only words he utters are vengeful epithets direct-
ed at Percival the Seeker. If the Hunters eliminate the Red 
Knight, he will not appear in Zone 12 - Cliff (page 197).

184
The Scarred Knight
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a former knight of the Round Table, dishon-
orably banished by my king for falling in love with his queen. 
My fighting skills are unparalleled, and my face is completely 
scarred as punishment for my sins.
Agenda: To find my beloved Guinevere and escape Avalon 
with her.    
Information: The Scarred Knight is wandering in search 
of his beloved Guinevere and will make himself available to 
speak to the Hunters. The man will say that his beloved is 
disguised as a common Villager, but can be recognized by the 
feather-shaped brooch he gave her as a token of his love. The 
Scarred Knight will not reveal his face or name, but he prom-
ises incredible riches to Hunters who help him. If a Hunter 
has a Descriptor related to madness or understanding the be-
havior of others, they will clearly understand that the Scarred 
Knight is insane, and an endless obsession burns in his eyes. 
If the Hunters eliminate the Scarred Knight, he will not ap-
pear in Zone 11 - Town of Stow (page 197).
 Merlin’s Shadow
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the shadow of the mighty sorcerer Merlin, 
and with my touch, I can freeze anything. I constantly receive 
visions of what my former master is doing.    
Agenda: Kill the precious Hunters Merlin has sent as a way 
of taking revenge on him for leaving me alone to guard Avalon. 
Gift - Shadow Body:  In semi- or total darkness, Merlin’s 
Shadow is considered OL Hard 7 when defending against an 
attack or magic. The icy touch is capable of destroying living 
creatures. Those who suffer 1 Wound from Merlin’s Shadow 
receive 1 Consumption Token. The Wound tied to the Token 
cannot be healed in any way. During an Interlude, a Hunter 
can use the Recover 1 Wound option to discard 1 Consump-
tion Token instead of healing the Wound. 
Information: Merlin’s Shadow cannot communicate with 
words. When defeated, it drops the Tower Key, which can be 
used in Zone 5 - Merlin’s Tower (page 190).

185
Guinevere the Fugitive
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the queen of Camelot, and my good heart 
is matched only by my beauty.
Agenda: To find a cure for the madness that has taken hold 
of my beloved Arthur. 
Gift - Virtue of Love: Imbued with the power of the Vir-
tue of Love, Guinevere cannot be killed: when she reaches 
0 Wounds, she will fall into a state of apparent death, wak-
ing up fully healed at the end of the Scene. Guinevere will 
give the power of her Virtue to those who show a sincere 
interest in stopping Arthur’s madness: when this happens, 
the Virtue transforms into a heart-shaped pendant. While 
imbued with the power of Virtue, Guinevere cannot be de-
ceived in any way and will see everything for what it really 
is, breaking all magic and deception.
Information: Guinevere poses as a common Villager, 
though her noble features are hard to hide. Guinevere is 
aware that Arthur has gone mad because he has lost his 
Virtues. Indeed, the woman is seeking help and will not 
hesitate to reveal herself to the Hunters if they prove trust-
worthy.

186
Percival the Seeker
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a disgraced knight. My body is immortal, 
but my mind has lost bits of memory over the centuries. 
Agenda: Remember why I am on this strange island. 
Gift - Virtue of Faith: Imbued with the power of the Vir-
tue of Faith, Percival cannot be killed. When he reaches 0 
Wounds, he will fall into a state of apparent death and will 
awake at the end of the Scene fully healed. Percival’s Virtue 
cannot be granted because it is inside the Grail chalice that 
the Knight remembers throwing between the rocks and 
shoals in Zone 12 - Cliff (page 197).
Information: Percival only remembers things when he 
is in his little house on the Cliff: he cycles back to Camelot, 
but on the way he loses his memory and remembers only 
the place he left from. Upon his return, Percival remembers 
his mission and the cycle begins again.
 
The Map of Camelot
Zone 1 - Landing Zone 2 - Beach Zone 3 - Glatisant's 
Lair 
Zone 4 - Village of 
Corrinton
Zone 5 -Merlin’s 
Tower
Zone 6 - Forest Zone 7 - Battlefield Zone 8 - Camelot 
Castle Zone 9 - Fairy Falls Zone 10 - Circle of 
Stones
Zone 11 - Town of 
Stow Zone 12 - Cliff Zone 13 - Lake Zone 14 - Aban-
doned House
Zone 15 - Secret 
Landing

187
The Map of Camelot
N

188
Zone 1 – Landing 
Description: The disembarkation point for the Hunters 
expedition, where all the sailors are guarding the two ships 
that have arrived on the island.    
Information: The sailors are led by Captain Bennett, 
who is always attentive to the Hunter’s needs. The crew is 
unwilling to leave their ship for any reason other than to 
recover survivors from the First Expedition.
Note: In this Zone, you never roll on the Encounter Table.
Zone 2 – Beach 
Description: A large Beach filled with ruins of what were 
once fishermen’s homes. The wreck of the Wavecrest (the 
ship of the First Expedition) is surrounded by the mangled 
bodies of torn sailors.    
Information: Investigating near the wreck, it will be easy 
to notice the footprints of a huge creature, and the damage 
done to the ship also seems to indicate an unequal battle 
between a monster and a ship of unfortunate men. Going 
deeper into the wreck, it is possible to find Captain Boyce’s 
diary. However, there is a risk of falling overboard because 
of the rotten wooden planks.
Threat – Rotten Wreck
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Rotten wood can give way even under light 
weight. 
Gift - Splinters and Scrap: If a Defense Check fails, the 
target falls overboard and suffers 1 Wound, and the Sto-
ryteller gets 1 Wreck Token. When the number of Wreck 
Tokens reaches 3, the ship collapses in on itself, burying 
everything inside.
Hidden among diary pages filled with uninteresting travel 
notes are entries about the island.
Note 1: Boyce followed the creature that destroyed his ship 
and discovered that its lair was east of the beach. 
Note 2: With a group of trusted sailors, Boyce headed to the 
castle that towers in the center of the island in search of help. 
Note 3: The only survivor of the journey to the castle, Boyce 
writes in shaky handwriting, asking God’s forgiveness for his 
sins and for the companions he had to leave behind to escape.
Note 4: A knight in strange red armor roams the beach. Now 
lonely and desperate, Boyce decides to try his best to get his 
attention.

189
Zone 3 – Glatisant’s 
Lair
Description: A wide expanse of rocks and bushes stretch-
es as far as the eye can see. The terrain drops sharply, lead-
ing to what appears to be a chasm. An old and worn sign 
warns, “Steer clear, lair of Glatisant the Beast. ” 
Information: At the bottom of the chasm lies Glatisant, a 
giant flesh-eating monster. When potential prey approach-
es the chasm, the Storyteller rolls a die:
1-3: Glatisant is dormant. If someone descends into the 
lair, a Defense Check is required to prevent the beast from 
waking.    
4-6: Glatisant awakens in hunger. Pretending to be asleep, 
it waits for prey to approach the edge of the chasm, then 
leaps out and devours it. 
Glatisant 
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a giant snake-headed monster with a de-
formed body, the result of the union of more than ten different 
animals. I have an insatiable appetite for flesh.
Agenda: I devour every delicious human I meet, though my 
favorite prey is the tasty Twrch Trwyth.
Gift - Monstrous Abomination:  Glatisant can act 2 
times during an Exchange, but not back to back. Its long 
neck allows it to snatch prey up to thirty feet away, and 
when it moves, it can make great leaps, ignoring enemies 
and terrain in its path. Glatisant is obsessed with the flesh of 
the Twrch Trwyth and, if given the opportunity, will spend 
the entire Exchange devouring it, ignoring even potential 
enemies.
Gift - Virtue of Courage: Glatisant unknowingly carries 
the Virtue of Courage, encased in a sword embedded in his 
right side. Defeating the beast will allow the Hunters to re-
cover the power of Courage. 
Zone 4 – Village of 
Corrinton 
Description: A fishing village buzzing with activity. The 
people are friendly and hospitable, though it is clear from 
their faces that something is troubling the community. 
Information: Chatting with the locals is another way to 
get the information revealed in the Encounter with the Vil-
lagers (page 181). At the explicit request of the Hunters, 
some fishermen will take them by boat along the coast but 
will ask for payment. This arrangement allows the Hunters 
to choose any coastal Zone as their destination (i.e., all ex-
cept 7, 8, and 9). Unless special arrangements are made, the 
fishermen are in a hurry to return to their activities, and 
once they have unloaded the Hunters, they will not wait for 
them to return.

190
Zone 5 – Merlin’s 
Tower 
Description: An old, seemingly abandoned tower stands 
on a headland overlooking the sea. The building appears to 
have no windows, and the only entrance is an old wooden 
door engraved with strange runes. 
Information: If the Hunters have not removed Merlin’s 
Shadow, the Storyteller rolls a die.
1-3: Merlin’s Shadow is not in the Tower, but if someone 
opens the door, it will be there within half an hour to drive 
out any intruders. 
4-6: Merlin’s Shadow is in the Tower, and if anyone forces 
the door open, it will attack without hesitation. 
If the Hunters have not retrieved the Tower Key from Mer-
lin’s Shadow, any attempt to open the door will unleash the 
power of the Haunted Door. 
Threat – Haunted Door
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A creaking old door imbued with all of Merlin’s 
magical power.
Gift - Away with the Harassers:  Attempting to open 
the door without the Tower Key triggers the magical effects 
below. If the Defense Check fails, the Storyteller rolls a die 
to determine the consequences. 
1-2: Loses all remaining Soma.
3-4: Suffers 2 Wounds. 
5-6: Ends with the whole group in another Zone. 
Inside the Tower, the Hunters will find all manner of mag-
ical devilry, as well as clear evidence in books and scrolls 
that the place belongs to a Sorcerer named Merlin. With 
time, the Hunters can uncover Merlin’s diary and, at the 
Storyteller’s discretion, a Treasure of the Order.
The diary is mostly written in an incomprehensible lan-
guage, but there are notes written in red ink that are in 
common English.
Note 1: An overly righteous and beloved king is more danger-
ous than the worst of tyrants, for when raising a hand against 
him an entire people will rise to shield him.
Note 2: Virtue is like a weed, it grows despite all efforts to 
pull it up.
Note 3: It may not be handy, but the bottom of a lake is al-
ways a good place to hide. 
Note 4: White beach, safe harbor.

191
Zone 6 – Forest
Description: An intricate Forest stretches in all directions 
and a light layer of fog that seems to have no regard for the 
weather or the time of day envelops everything. An unnat-
ural silence hangs in the air, broken only by the cries of a 
few animals.
Information: Traversing the Forest without getting lost 
takes time and skill. While exploring the Forest, Hunters 
can find two locations: the Magic Glade and the Green Gar-
den.
Threat – Intricate Maze
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: The great forest of Camelot is a tangle of vege-
tation perpetually shrouded in mist.
In case of Failure, the Storyteller decides which Zone the 
Hunters will land in.
The Magic Glade
Descriptor: A small clearing within a circle formed by an-
cient oaks. Inside, one can clearly feel the magic hovering in 
the air, tickling the skin.
Gift - Place of Peace and Magic:  The Magic Glade is 
a place of peace and serenity. Those who rest in it for a few 
minutes recover 1 Soma. The same subject cannot bene-
fit from this effect more than once per Scene. Invoking an 
Interlude within the Magic Glade does not generate an ad-
vancement in Merlin’s Agenda. 
The Green Garden
Descriptor: A lush field of flowers, in the center of which 
stands a small stone altar engraved with pagan symbols. 
Information: As soon as a subject enters the flowery 
field, a figure covered in ancient armor and foliage stands 
threateningly. The Green Knight will never leave the flower 
field, remaining to defend the altar. The only way to avoid 
the wrath of the Green Knight is to show respect to the an-
cient gods. By eliminating the Green Knight, the Hunters 
can get their hands on the Treasure of the Order, the Ax of 
Retribution (page 192). 
The Green Knight
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the spirit of a knight sworn to protect the 
ancient cults. My armor is old now, but the plants and flow-
ers that have grown on it protect me. With my big ax, I can 
chop off a head with ease. 
Agenda: Protect the altar and allow those who worship the 
old gods to have a place to worship. 
Gift - The Just Punishment:  The Green Knight starts 
each Scene with 3 Retaliation Tokens, each time he suffers 
1 Wound he can spend 1 Token to inflict 1 Wound on who-
ever hit him.

192
The Ax of Retribution
Inspired by the Green Knight of the Arthurian 
Myth
Once upon a time, there was a knight who had made justice 
his calling. He was respected by all the commoners and had 
married a beautiful princess with whom he shared a rich and 
prosperous life. The knight had not always been upright in 
spirit. In the past, he had often fallen into excesses of anger 
and jealousy, even to the point of staining his hands with 
blood. These lower instincts were appeased by a magical ax 
the knight had taken from a fallen enemy. The ax accumulat-
ed all the negative thoughts of the wielder, transforming him 
into a person filled with righteous thoughts. But as is often 
the case, man is not really capable of changing his nature. 
Despite the magical power of the ax, the knight’s thoughts be-
came dark. Convinced that his wife was cheating on him, the 
knight invited a companion to his court and then pretended 
to go on a quest, while actually hiding within the castle walls 
to spy on them. A week passed in which absolutely nothing 
happened, while the princess and her guest chatted amiably 
about the knight’s qualities. Blinded by guilt, the knight came 
to court and told his wife and trusted companion everything. 
He cried tears of remorse, but only the ax, at the height of the 
magical power that gave it life, sensed the lie in the Green 
Knight’s words. To the amazement of all present, the weapon 
came to life and decapitated him with a single blow, unleash-
ing all the anger and envy it had accumulated over the years. 
Ironically, the widow, saddened by her beloved’s untimely de-
parture, found solace in his trusted comrade-in-arms. When 
the mourning was over, they married and began a long and 
successful dynasty. 
Keywords: Trust / Punishment / Retaliation / Sense of 
honor / Beheading / Masterstroke.
Gift - Scales of Justice: The Ax of Retribution protects 
those who wield it from negative thoughts and punishes 
those who behave spitefully. At the beginning of each Sce-
nario, the Ax contains 3 Retaliation Tokens. The wielder 
can spend 1 Token to activate one of the following effects:
1: Get 2 Additional Successes on a Defense Check against 
mind manipulation effects.
2: Once per Scenario, decapitate a target that suffers 1 
Wound from the Ax. This effect immediately reduces any 
remaining Wounds to zero, but only applies to those who 
are truly evil, with the Storyteller having the final say. If the 
target of this effect is not evil, it is the Hunter wielding the 
Ax who takes the Wound in retaliation.
3: Give 1 Additional Success to an ally’s action, but only if 
the goal is to reveal a truth or punish a villain. 
Once per Scene, if the wielder succeeds in changing an 
NPC’s attitude from aggressive to cooperative without re-
sorting to violence, the Ax produces 1 Retaliation Token.

193
Zone 7 – Battlefield 
Description: A vast wasteland littered with corpses. War-
riors in armor bearing the crest of Camelot mingle with the 
bodies of skin-painted barbarians. The stench of death and 
destruction is everywhere, while distinctly Germanic cries 
come from an encampment in the center of the Battlefield.
Information: The remnants of the Saxon army that in-
vaded Camelot are in the camp, ready to celebrate their 
victory over the Camelot army. There are a total of 30 Sax-
on Marauders (page 180), but for every Battle Token the 
Hunters have accumulated, the number drops by 5. Un-
aware that they have landed in a future era not their own, 
the Marauders plan to march on Camelot Castle. Tied to a 
post in the middle of the camp is an old, dying knight. He 
is Uther Pendragon, Arthur’s father, who was at the head of 
the defending army. The Saxons are torturing him, trying to 
extract information. 
Uther Pendragon
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (0 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an experienced knight and a great strate-
gist. I am too old for war now, but that will not stop me from 
saving my kingdom.
Agenda: Protect Camelot and bring Arthur to his senses so 
that he can once again be a worthy ruler of these lands. 
Information: Uther is dying, and even if he is saved, it 
will be clear that his injuries are fatal. Before he breathes 
his last, however, the old man will reveal important details 
to the Hunters.
 ◆ Arthur was always a good ruler, as long as Merlin re-
mained at his side as an advisor. Uther, however, could 
always see envy in the Sorcerer’s eyes, envy for the 
young king that was his protégé. When Merlin sudden-
ly disappeared, Arthur went mad and lost all the traits 
that made him a righteous and benevolent man.
 ◆ In the southeast, past the great Lake, lies an old Aban-
doned House. Arthur spent his childhood there, and 
Uther is convinced that the spirit of all that is good in 
his son’s heart dwells there.
 ◆ There is a secret passage to enter the Camelot Castle. It 
is located inside a large oak tree that has been struck by 
lightning. From there, an underground tunnel leads to 
the hall of the Round Table. 
Zone 8 – Camelot Castle
Description: Camelot Castle appears to be an ancient 
abandoned ruin, with only the cackling of soldiers prepar-
ing for war revealing that it is still inhabited. Knights and 
villagers alike are busy crafting weapons and war machines. 
Information: Arthur is in the hall of the Round Table, 
where he sits with his most trusted Knights. There are thir-
teen seats in total, one is occupied by Arthur, and one is 
empty as it belongs to Merlin. There are then eleven more 
Knights (page 181), but for every Empty Seat Token that 
the Hunters have collected, the number is reduced by one. 
Without his Virtues, Arthur is an invading madman whose 
goal is the conquest of Europe, second only to finding his 
beloved Guinevere. Arthur’s behavior is greatly influenced 
by finding the Virtue of Wisdom (page 194).

194
Arthur
OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the king of Camelot, and my word is law. 
Thanks to the power of the Grail, my body is immortal and I 
possess abilities far beyond those of ordinary men.
Agenda: Bring back my bride, Guinevere, to rule all of Eu-
rope by my side / Prepare the army for battle and make all my 
subjects immortal warriors. 
Gift - The True Power of the Grail:  Arthur is immor-
tal, which means that any Wounds inflicted on him will 
heal immediately. Arthur can act up to 3 times in an Ex-
change, but not back to back.
Gift - Merlin’s Curse: Deprived of the Virtues by Mer-
lin’s magic, Arthur has become a mad and powerful being 
who has abandoned his humanity. Activating the power of 
the Virtues awakens the king’s true soul, making him more 
human. Each Virtue consumed in front of Arthur will have 
the following effect:
Love: Lower his OL by 2.
Innocence: Lower the number of Wounds by 3. 
Faith: He loses his immortality and, if brought to 0 
Wounds, is permanently killed. 
Courage: Acts only 1 time per Exchange. 
Wisdom: He regains his wits and realizes that what he is 
doing is wrong. Arthur’s Agenda changes to: Lead my king-
dom to peace by finding a place in this new world.

195
Zone 9 – Fairy Falls
Description: A small wooded area hides a series of natu-
ral waterfalls. A keen eye might catch a glimpse of female 
figures dancing gracefully on the water.
Information: There are 6 Fairies who will welcome any-
one who arrives with smiles and dancing. The Fairies do 
not hesitate to reveal what they know, engaging guests in 
a long talk.
Fairies
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPCs (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are fairies and we love company and good 
stories. We like humans, even though they tend to age too fast 
by our standards.    
Agenda: Entertain nice people for as long as possible.    
Gift - A Midsummer Night’s Dream: Talking to Fair-
ies for a long time can be fatal for a human, as time passes 
faster in their presence. After a minute of conversation, the 
subject can make a Defense Check to see if they are getting 
old. If successful, the subject can end the conversation. If 
unsuccessful, the subject loses 2 Soma, and if the subject is 
already at 0 Soma, the subject loses 1 Wound. Succeeding 
with a Cost is possible, but the easiest way to interrupt the 
effect of the Gift is to feel pain, so the Cost will always be 
1 Wound. A new Check can be made after each minute of 
conversation. An outside party may attempt to break the ef-
fect of this Gift with words (Position Check) or by inflicting 
1 Wound (Automatic).
Fairy information:
 ◆ They come from the great lake where the house once 
stood, the home of the Lady who cared for all the mag-
ical creatures on the island. The Lady then decided 
to leave, fed up with the many brave young men who 
came to the lake, insistent on gaining her blessing.
 ◆ The Fairies have always stayed to guard the Lake, but 
on the day the Hunters arrived, a grumpy old man who 
clearly looked like a Sorcerer appeared at the lake ac-
companied by a huge knight in armor as black as night. 
The old man frightened off the Fairies, who, peering 
from a distance, clearly saw the two of them walking 
along the shores of the Lake, searching for something.
 ◆ The island contains many illusions that prevent ordi-
nary mortals from discovering certain truths. The Fair-
ies possess a Fairy Crystal that allows those who look 
through it to see the illusions. They are willing to give 
up the Crystal, but only in exchange for something of 
equal value. If the Hunters do not know what to pro-
pose, the Fairies will say that a beautiful treasure is 
guarded by a Knight in the Forest to the east.

196
Scenario Gift - Fairy Crystal
Requirement: Receive the Crystal as a gift from the Fair-
ies. (No need to spend XP)
Thanks to the power of the Crystal, you can see beyond il-
lusions and receive 3 Sight Tokens. Y ou can spend 1 Token 
and look through the Crystal to reveal an illusion, a dis-
guise, or the effect of magic that makes something appear 
different than it really is. When you spend the last Token, 
the Crystal shatters and you lose the effect of this Gift. 
 
Zone 10 – Circle of 
Stones
Description: Huge megalithic stones are arranged in a 
circle at the top of a low hill. The aura of ancient power is 
easily felt even by those unfamiliar with magic. 
Information: Inside the large stones are a series of small-
er stones that replicate the shape of the island. A person 
with a Descriptor related to magic can decipher the code 
of the stones and understand that the Circle is a system for 
magically moving from one place to another. Naming a 
Zone with the correct code will immediately transport ev-
erybody in the Circle there, but travel is only one-way and 
can’t be used to return to the Circle of Stones. Using the 
Fairy Crystal allows one to understand the workings of the 
Circle, even if the person looking at it does not understand 
magic.
Threat – Magic Menhirs
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A circle of ancient stones laid at the dawn of 
time. 
Gift - Magical Journey:  Activating the power of the 
stones is easy, but getting exactly where you want to go is 
much less so. The journey requires a Position Check: with a 
single roll, the person activating the power can take all ob-
jects within the Circle with them. Depending on the result 
of the Check, different effects are achieved.
Failure: Successful journey but the wrong destination. 
The Storyteller chooses the arrival Zone from among those 
adjacent to the original destination. 
Outcome with a Cost: Successful journey but with some 
interference. Each subject loses 1 Equipment decided by 
the Storyteller. The missing Equipment has been transport-
ed in a Zone adjacent to the one of arrival.
Standard Outcome: Successful trip, no problems. 
Outcome with an Increment:  Complimentary pan-
oramic view. Before arriving, travelers can observe the 
destination while lingering safely in the astral dimension 
they have traveled through. The Storyteller will reveal some 
information about the Zone.

197
Zone 11 – Town of Stow  
Description: A small farming town. The people are 
friendly and hospitable, though you can see in their faces 
that the shadow of war hangs over their lives. The streets 
are also filled with outsiders looking for work and a place 
to sleep. 
Information: By chatting with villagers, it is possible to 
obtain the information provided in the Villagers Encounter 
(page 181). 
If the Hunters have not already met Guinevere (page 185), 
the queen will be in Stow, where she has been hired as a 
handmaiden to hide her true identity. When Guinevere re-
alizes that the Hunters are on the island to stop Arthur, she 
will present herself to them and ask for help. 
If the Hunters have not already eliminated the Scarred 
Knight (page 184), he will come to Stow in search of his 
beloved. 
Zone 12 – Cliff 
Description: An impassable overhanging Cliff with the 
sea crashing against it. The Cliff rises on a small promonto-
ry, on the top of which a small house can be seen. 
Information: If the Hunters have not already encoun-
tered Percival the Seeker (page 186), they will find him in 
the house, cooking fish and recuperating.
If the Hunters have not already eliminated the Red Knight 
(page 183), he will appear to confront Percival.
Interacting with the confused Seeker can provide you 
with relevant information and the story of how he threw 
the Holy Grail into the sea to awaken Arthur. Percival will 
lead the Hunters to the Cliff where the current carried the 
cup after the island emerged. The Grail now lies embedded 
ninety feet below. 
Threat – The Sacred Cup Among the 
Rocks
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A steep cliff overlooking the sea that hides the 
cup of the Holy Grail in its rocks. 
Note: Getting to the cup requires moving along the slip-
pery, pointy rocks as the sea crashes relentlessly.    Retriev-
ing the embedded cup requires breaking the rock or find-
ing a way to slide it out.

198
Zone 13 – Lake
Description: A large Lake surrounded by dense vegeta-
tion. There seems to be no human settlement. A few boats 
lie abandoned on the shore, victims of time and weather. 
Information: In this Zone, the initial situation changes 
according to the number of Interludes the Hunters have 
called before their arrival.
Up to Two Interludes: Walking along the shore, Merlin 
can be seen coordinating Mordred’s search for the Virtue. 
The Knight walks until he disappears into the water, only 
to reappear a short time later with some muddy objects. 
Merlin sorts out what his servant has recovered and, with a 
displeased expression, orders him to continue. The Virtue 
of Wisdom is in the form of a Crown. Once retrieved, Mer-
lin instructs Mordred to wait for him at the bottom of the 
lake and then he leaves, abandoning his servant.
Three or more Interludes: Merlin has already rescued 
the Virtue and made his way to the Zone 15 - Secret Land-
ing (page 199). Hovering on the shore and looking into 
the Lake, it’s possible to see a motionless suit of armor un-
derwater. If the Hunters attempt to interact with Mordred, 
he will awaken to eliminate any newcomers.
Mordred the Peaceless
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an undead knight in the service of the evil 
Merlin. I do whatever my master tells me to do, though I only 
wish to return to the grave from which I was torn.    
Agenda (if Merlin is present):  Obey my master’s or-
ders.
Agenda (if Merlin is not present):  Eliminate anyone 
who approaches the waters of the lake, as ordered by my mas-
ter / Be destroyed and return to death, receiving a farewell 
prayer. 
Information: Mordred can be persuaded to surrender in 
exchange for the promise of a burial that will bring peace 
to his soul. If peace is granted, Mordred will reveal Merlin’s 
plans for why he wants the Virtue of Wisdom and the Se-
cret Landing.

199
Zone 14 – Abandoned 
House 
Description: A dilapidated cottage sits in the middle of a 
flat area. Cultivated fields once stretched in every direction, 
but now only ruins and sun-browned earth remain. 
Information: The House appears to be in ruins and long 
abandoned, but with careful investigation, you will notice 
a trapdoor in the floor. Opening the trapdoor will lead you 
to the basement, where old clothes and farming tools are 
piled high. Searching through the junk, the Hunters will 
find a small wooden crucifix that seems untouched by time. 
Once picked up, it will reveal its power as the Virtue of In-
nocence.
Zone 15 – Secret Landing 
Description: On the surface, this place looks like a quiet 
beach. The pale coloring of the sand makes it look almost 
like an expanse of white snow. Merlin’s personal ship waits 
about 30 yards from the shore, but it cannot be seen be-
cause it is hidden by an illusion cast by the Sorcerer.
Information: In this Zone, the initial situation changes 
according to the number of Interludes the Hunters have 
called before their arrival.
Up to Two Interludes:  On the ship, Captain Edgers 
awaits Merlin’s arrival. 
Three Interludes: The situation is the same as in the 
first option, but Merlin has regained the Virtue of Wisdom 
and will arrive at the beach shortly. If the Hunters wait, they 
can catch him off guard.    
Four Interludes: Merlin has returned to the ship and 
preparations for departure are in full swing. Unfortunately, 
part of the crew has contracted a strange disease that makes 
it difficult for Captain Edgers to control the ship. Merlin, 
amidst a thousand curses, does his best to create a cure.
Threat – Illusory Panorama
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A false image that adapts to weather condi-
tions. Small, repetitive details are the only sign that some-
thing is wrong, such as a flock of seagulls flying following a 
cyclic trajectory and clouds that seem stuck in the same po-
sition.

200
A Frozen Reflection 
 A Scenario Inspired by The Snow Queen
Part One – The Two Healers
Safety keywords: Illness / Poisoning / Identity theft
A Frozen Reflection is a special three-part Scenario that tells the story of 
the mysterious con artists Gerda and Kay. All Scenarios are structured as 
a Scene Tree (see page 100 of the Broken Tales Core Book) and are great for 
introductory Sessions or a mini-Campaign. A Storyteller who prefers an 
emergent style of storytelling will still find Events and information useful 
for handling more free-form Hunter action.

201
The Fragments of the 
Mirror of the Other Self
In this Scenario, several NPCs have been affected by the 
Shards of the Mirror of the Other Self, magical Shards 
that can reverse a person’s abilities and dispositions. All 
NPCs affected by the Shard receive the Gift - Power of the 
Shard, which provides a Shard Descriptor and a Shard 
Agenda to replace the original ones until the Mirror 
Shard is removed. To remove a Mirror Shard, you must 
deal with the Threat - Shard of the Mirror of the Other 
Self. 
Threat – Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A tiny shard of mirror capable of emitting 
icy cold.
Gift - The Shard:  Removing the Shard is a complex 
procedure that requires medical skills or the assistance 
of magic. Once removed, the Shard loses all of its power, 
disintegrating into thin air and inflicting 1 Wound on the 
person who removed it. This Wound can only be avoided 
by spending an Increment on a potential Check to re-
move the Shard. 
Miss Snow and Lady Storm in this 
Scenario
When a Hunter is connected to the story on which a Sce-
nario is based, an Exclusive Gift is usually presented that 
can be obtained under certain narrative conditions. This 
is a special case, however, because the Hunter Miss Snow 
and Lady Storm is directly involved in the plot of A Frozen 
Reflection. For this reason, the Storyteller must carefully 
consider whether to introduce this Hunter and how to deal 
with the resulting plot changes. Here are some tips:
 ◆ None of the NPCs who have met Lady Storm will rec-
ognize her while she is in the form of Miss Snow. In-
stead, the Hunter’s identity will be revealed when she 
activates her Dark Ego. All NPCs who contributed in 
any way to Lady Storm’s downfall will fear her and try 
to have as little to do with her as possible.
 ◆ No one knows that Miss Snow is Lady Storm, so there 
will be no qualms or special care in saying the word 
Eternity.
 ◆ During PART THREE - The Winter Castle, if the Ice 
Heart sees Lady Storm in action, it will be willing to 
sacrifice itself to get its mistress back; if Lady Storm 
manifests during the Scene, the Ice Heart will shatter, 
warmed by the love for its queen.

202
Once upon a time…
A terrible epidemic has struck the provinces of Norway, on 
the borders of the Kingdom of Denmark. Frozen Madness is a 
disease that only affects humans and literally drives them in-
sane. Not even the best doctors in the kingdom have been able 
to find a cure for the terrible disease, leaving the families of 
the sick in utter despair. The sudden appearance of the myste-
rious healers Kay and Gerda has brought a ray of hope to the 
fight against the Frozen Madness: after proving that they can 
cure the disease, the two have taken refuge in a remote castle 
in the far north, forcing the families of the sick to undertake 
a long and costly journey to obtain the cure. There are many 
rumors of expeditions that have ended in tragedy, as brigands 
and vicious animals make it seem impossible to reach Gerda 
and Kay without paying handsomely for a good guide. But a 
disease that has only one doctor is a strange thing, especially 
since the dreaded disease appeared shortly before the arrival 
of the two dubious healers. This fact did not go unnoticed 
by the Order, which decided to send a group of Hunters to 
investigate... 
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay are two young scoundrels who have 
always lived from hand to mouth. By a stroke of luck, the 
two have come into possession of many Shards of the Mir-
ror of the Other Self, a powerful magical object capable of 
reversing the character of those injured by one of its Shards. 
With the help of a witch named Flora, Gerda and Kay in-
troduced themselves to the powerful witch Lady Storm and 
managed to strike her with a magical Shard. Storm lost her 
abilities and became a naive and bungling sorceress. Us-
ing Flora’s power, Gerda and Kay forced poor Miss Snow 
(Lady Storm’s new identity) to create a great storm that 
would sweep across Northern Europe, releasing most of the 
Shards from the Mirror of the Other Self to affect as many 
innocent people as possible, spreading the Frozen Mad-
ness. Unable to handle such a powerful atmospheric event, 
Miss Snow was carried away, only to be found by the Order 
with no memory. After the Frozen Madness spread, Gerda 
and Kay used Lady Storm’s magic sleigh to travel to the ma-
jor cities of Norway, presenting themselves as healers of the 
mysterious disease that no one could eradicate. After be-
coming famous, the pair retreated to Lady Storm’s castle. As 
a consequence,    the families of the sick are cruelly forced 
to spend large sums of money on a journey of hope, only 
to find that hope dashed when they are led straight into the 
clutches of Gerda and Kay’s servants.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The Hunters arrive in Bergen, where nobleman Aleksander 
Okland has fallen victim to the dangerous Frozen Madness. 
Fredrika, the nobleman’s daughter, has been trying to cure 
her father, finally turning to the Order when it became clear 
that her beloved parent’s complete change of character was 
not the result of an ordinary illness. Meanwhile, Kay and 
Gerda struck Flora with a Shard, turning the cunning old 
charmer into a naive and clueless old woman. Flora had 
made too many demands in exchange for cooperating with 
the two imposters, so Gerda and Kay immediately took ac-
tion to get her out of the way. Since Flora is quite famous in 
Bergen, where she used to live, the two led the witch back 
to her old home, so that everyone would believe that the 
woman had simply succumbed to dementia.

203
FIRST SCENE – Bergen
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive in Bergen as the 
sun is setting, but what you see is hardly the tranquility of a 
city resting after a long day of activity. Men and women are 
wandering the streets shouting and gesturing outrageously, 
then wriggling away from desperate relatives trying to calm 
them down. As described in the letter from the young Okland 
scion, the Frozen Madness seems to have hit Bergen hard, 
driving many of its inhabitants mad. A young servant runs 
up to you and, after a brief bow, waves for you to follow him 
to the large mansion down the street. 
Place: The Okland family mansion in the city’s wealthiest 
neighborhood.
Time: The sun is setting as the cries of people 
suffering from Frozen Madness echo from the 
streets. 
Initial Situation: Fredrika Okland will intro-
duce herself to the Hunters by inviting them to 
dinner, accompanied by her father Aleksander, 
who suffers from the Frozen Madness. Fredrika 
will ask the Hunters to forgive her father’s excess-
es in light of his condition. 
Information: Fredrika will tell the Hunters 
that three months ago a very heavy snowstorm 
hit Northern Europe, especially the Norwegian 
coast. After the storm, many people began to 
show signs of madness, behaving in ways that 
were the opposite of their usual behavior. Her fa-
ther Aleksander, who had always been a calm and 
jovial man, became hot-tempered and arrogant, 
with outbursts of unnecessary violence. At first, 
no doctor could find a cure. Then two mysteri-
ous healers from the north named Gerda and Kay 
cured some nobles for a hefty fee. As soon as word 
spread of healers who could cure the Frozen Madness, the 
two disappeared, and now to meet them one must contact 
the Black Feathers: two local guides who charge exorbitant 
sums of money to bring desperate people to Gerda and Kay.
During the discussion, this information will also emerge:
 ◆ Near Bergen lives a famous witch named Flora. Many 
have turned to her for healing, but the old woman 
seems to have fallen victim to dementia, or perhaps she 
herself has been struck by the Frozen Madness. Flora is 
famous for her potions, which she distills in the large 
greenhouse where she grows all sorts of herbs. 
 ◆ The only contact with the Black Feathers in town seems 
to be an elderly woman named Lene. The woman was 
1

204
once the head of a criminal organization but 
has retired to private life to enjoy the fruits of 
her misdeeds. Few have the courage to both-
er her without offering something of value in 
return. 
During dinner, Aleksander behaves inappropri-
ately, trying in every way possible to humiliate 
his daughter and question the Hunters’ actions. If 
provoked, Aleksander will not hesitate to use vi-
olence.
Fredrika Okland
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the young scion of the Okland family, I believe in jus-
tice and honesty as my father taught me. 
Agenda: To heal my father and make him the good man who raised me.
Aleksander Okland
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a rich and powerful nobleman, and I use my position to help 
all unfortunate people. I can’t stand the bullying of the weak by the powerful, 
and I strive to change society so that everything is more equal.    
Agenda: Helping others find their way and realize their plans.
