Shard Descriptor: I am a rich and powerful nobleman, and I use my 
position to bend everyone to my command. I can’t stand to be told no, and I 
abuse all the people I consider inferior.
Shard Agenda: Satisfying my thirst for power by crushing those who are 
weaker. 
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of the Other Self re-
verses the Descriptor and Agenda of the person it injured. The holder increas-
es their OL by 1 if they act in accordance with the altered part of their De-
scriptor or Agenda. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters must decide whether to contact Flora or the 
Black Feathers through Lene. Go to SECOND SCENE OPTION A - Flora’s 
Greenhouse or SECOND SCENE OPTION B - Lene’s House.

205
SECOND SCENE OPTION A –  
Flora’s Greenhouse
Read aloud to the Hunters: The home of the famous Flo-
ra is located just outside the city limits, along a large stream 
that runs along the eastern side of Bergen. The witch’s home 
stands out in the middle of the countryside as a colorful 
building flanked by a huge greenhouse. When you reach the 
entrance, there are no lights on in the house, while someone 
in the greenhouse seems to be happily humming a 
childish song.
Place: The House and Greenhouse of the witch 
Flora.
Time: The sun has set and the moonlight softly il-
luminates the Bergen countryside.
Information: Flora has taken up permanent residence 
in the Greenhouse and, in the grip of the Shard effect, be-
witches and imprisons those who visit her, then forgets her 
victims among the plants. Inside the House, the Hunters 
can get their hands on the witch’s diary, in which Flora has 
written some important information about Gerda and Kay. 
In the House:  The House is protected by Flora’s magic, 
and breaking in without her permission will activate her 
defenses. The House is well-maintained and filled with an-
cient magical relics. There are four rooms in total, two of 
which are of interest to Hunters: the Storeroom on the first 
floor and Flora’s Bedroom on the second floor. 
Threat - Haunted House
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A house that emits an aura of ignorant 
well-being, relaxing intruders into sleep. 
Gift - Induced Fatigue:  For each room visited in the 
House, roll one die for each intruder:
1-3: Nothing happens.
4-6: They lose 1 Soma. 
On the second point of Soma lost to this Gift, a Hunter 
must pass a Defense Check to avoid falling into an eight-
hour deep sleep from which they cannot be awakened. For 
an NPC, the effect is triggered when they lose Soma equal 
to the category of their OL: 1 for Easy, 2 for Medium, and 
3 for Hard.
Flora’s Bedroom
Descriptor: A richly decorated room with a large canopy 
bed. Flowers and plants of all kinds grow on the walls, giving 
the impression of being in a small greenhouse. 
Information: The Hunters will quickly notice a small box 
on the wardrobe next to the bed. Inside is Flora’s diary, but 
as you might guess, a witch’s box can’t be opened by just 
anyone.
2a

206
Warden Casket
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fierce Warden Casket, ready to bite any 
thief with my sharp teeth. I am very sensitive to bad smells, 
which makes me sneeze until I vomit my contents. 
Information: Flora’s diary describes Gerda and Kay, two 
criminals who proposed the Frozen Madness scam to Flo-
ra. Flora seems to have agreed mainly to take revenge on an 
old rival of hers, a witch named Lady Storm. The contact 
between Flora and the two criminals was facilitated by a 
woman named Lene, who is identified in the diary as Ger -
da’s mother. Flora reports that she is not too happy about 
involving so many innocent people in this plan, but the 
chance to teach the arrogant and haughty Lady Storm a 
lesson was too good to pass up. Flora does not trust Gerda 
and Kay, but she does not believe that the two criminals can 
pose any real danger to her. 
Storeroom
Descriptor: A small room filled with books, gardening 
tools, and strange vials.
Information: Hidden among the many stacked objects it 
is possible to find a Treasure of the Order. The Storyteller is 
free to decide which one.
The Greenhouse
Descriptor: A large greenhouse filled with vegetation. A 
thick mist of pollen chokes those who walk through the rows 
of plants and flowers with its smell. 
Information:  Flora has taken up residence in the Green-
house, where she repeats the same tasks over and over 
again. Without her careful oversight, many of the plants 
have grown beyond control, and the ecosystem of the place 
has become mixed with the witch’s out-of-control magic. 
Moving inside the Greenhouse activates the Threat - Magic 
Greenhouse. Flora is in the center of it and will not move 
under any circumstances.
Threat - Magic Greenhouse
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A greenhouse filled with strange plants im-
bued with Flora’s magical powers. 
Gift - Wild Plants:  The Greenhouse plants will identify 
anyone other than Flora as a threat. The plants rely on smell 
to detect intruders, so they can be fooled. If a person enters 
the Greenhouse, roll 1 die:
1-2: A carnivorous plant attempts to bite the target to in-
flict 1 Wound. 
3-4: A colored pollen covers the target, which takes 1 
Penalty on all actions while in the Greenhouse. A Defense 
Check is needed to avoid the pollen.
5-6: A large flower emits a sharp hissing sound that stuns 
the target and causes them to lose 1 Soma (1 Wound, in the 
case of an NPC).

207
Flora the Witch
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful witch who has dedicated her 
life to the study of nature-based magic. I wish to be admired 
and recognized for my abilities as a witch.    
Agenda: To gain the respect of others through my powers 
and live a comfortable life. 
Shard Descriptor: I am a clumsy witch who loves 
plants, but unfortunately I have forgotten how to take care 
of my beloved greenhouse. I am humble and don’t care what 
others think of me, because all I want is to speak to the hearts 
of plants. 
Shard Agenda: Take care of my greenhouse and keep 
away any pests that come after me. 
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the 
person it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they 
act in accordance with the altered part of their Descriptor 
or Agenda.    
Gift - Plant Witch:  Flora can manipulate nearby plants 
by animating them, and thanks to this power, she can per-
form up to 2 actions in the Exchange with a range equal to 
the ground covered by the plants themselves. Through the 
plants, Flora can attempt to injure, immobilize, or manip-
ulate a target. 
Information: If the Hunters succeed in removing the 
Shard from Flora’s eye, the old witch will come to her 
senses, but the effects of the Shard will have permanent 
consequences for her. Drained of all magical power, the 
old woman will try to bargain with the Hunters for the in-
formation she possesses in exchange for being exonerated 
of all charges. Flora will then talk about Gerda and Kay’s 
plan, as described in The story that led us here. Flora will 
not know the location of Lady Storm’s castle, as its entrance 
is protected by powerful magic. However, she will tell the 
Hunters that if they continue along the river bordering 
Bergen, they can reach the small White Hospital: a facility 
where Gerda and Kay reside when they leave the castle. To 
get there, Flora will tell the Hunters to look for the Black 
Feather boat in the small river port of Bergen.
End of Scene:  The Hunters decide to meet the Black 
Feathers through Lene. Go to SECOND SCENE OPTION 
B - Lene’s House. If they decide to leave directly instead, go 
to the THIRD SCENE - The Ambush of the Black Feathers.

208
SECOND SCENE OPTION B –  
Lene’s House
Read aloud to the Hunters:  In the slums of Bergen you 
will find a small, seemingly abandoned house. Only the lights 
inside indicate someone’s presence. You feel several eyes on 
you and realize that hooded figures are keeping watch on the 
street. There is no doubt that you have entered the territory 
of a gang...
Place: Lene’s little abandoned house in the slums of Ber-
gen. 
Time: The sun has now set and the only source of illumi-
nation is the many lights coming from the houses in town.
Information: Lene welcomes every visitor to the house 
and offers herself as a contact for the two famous healers. 
For this service, the old woman demands an outrageous 
amount of money that only the truly wealthy can afford. 
Lene’s arguments are especially persuasive because of the 
numerous cutthroats who lurk around her home, ready to 
strike at the slightest sign of trouble.  
Lene the Old Mobster
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am the ruthless queen of Bergen’s under -
world, and I have no qualms about conducting my shady 
business. Everyone in the city fears me, and I use my repu-
tation to intimidate those foolish enough to turn against me. 
Agenda: Making money by taking advantage of people’s 
desperation, taking what they have to offer without really 
leading them to Gerda and Kay. 
Information: When the Hunters confront Lene, the old 
woman will reveal that Gerda and Kay are staying at the 
small White Hospital, a medical facility that can be reached 
by sailing north along the river. Lene will give the Hunters 
a pass that the Black Feathers will recognize as valid for 
boarding their boat to make the journey.
Lene will try hard to hide the fact that she is Gerda’s moth-
er, fearing that this information will get her into even more 
trouble than her daughter’s scheme. When this connection 
is made and Lene is cornered, she will reveal some infor -
mation about her daughter to the Hunters. At this point, 
the Storyteller can decide what to reveal about Gerda by 
consulting her profile on page 223.
Lene’s Cutthroat
OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC 
(1 Wound - 12 Gang Members)
Descriptor: We are ruthless scoundrels and protect our ter-
ritory from any intruder.
Gift - Neighborhood Gang: A Cutthroat increases their 
OL by 1 when they act to wound or capture a target they 
consider an intruder on their territory. 
End of Scene:  The Hunters decide to meet the Black 
Feathers. Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Ambush of the 
Black Feathers. If they decide to meet Flora instead, go to 
SECOND SCENE OPTION A - Flora’s Greenhouse.
2b

209
THIRD SCENE –  The Ambush 
of the Black Feathers
Read aloud to the Hunters: After a night of prepara-
tions, you arrive at the small river port on the eastern side of 
the city, the sun is rising as the port activities have already 
begun. You recognize the small, black boat that has been 
pointed out to you, and as you approach, a young woman 
with clear skin and long raven hair looks at you with suspi-
cious eyes and then nods at you. 
Place: Bergen’s river port. 
Time: It dawns as the city awakens to a new day of work. 
Information: The woman will introduce herself as Kråke 
and ask the Hunters for a pass. If they took one from Lene, 
the boatwoman will wave them aboard. If the Hunters are 
coming on Flora’s instructions, Kråke will instead ask them 
to pay a fare in exchange for her services. 
On the ship, the Hunters will notice a second black-haired 
boatman, Ravn, busy making preparations for departure. 
Regardless of how they got on board, the Black Feath-
ers know the Hunters are unwelcome guests: Lene’s pass 
is marked, a sign the woman leaves specifically to let the 
crewmembers know who should meet a bad end along the 
way because they have not paid enough. If, on the other 
hand, they were sent by Flora, the Black Feathers know that 
the witch has been struck by a Shard and should no longer 
be able to provide this information. Shortly after leaving, 
the Hunters notice a flock of crows that seems to be follow-
ing the boat. Eventually, many birds will rest on the boat, 
and that is when Ravn and Kråke will start their attack. Af-
ter defeating the enemies on the boat, the Hunters will find 
a map to the White Hospital. 
Ravn
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled assassin, and my throwing 
knives can hit a target even in total darkness.    
Agenda: Escort those who have paid the correct amount to 
the White Hospital / Eliminate any potential threat to Gerda 
and Kay’s business.    
Gift - The Raven:  Once per Scene, Ravn can transform 
into a raven and escape by flying away. If he makes a sur -
prise attack, Ravn inflicts 1 Additional Wound.
Kråke
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled assassin, and I can corner any 
opponent with my saber. My swordsmanship is such that I 
can deflect any weapon. 
Agenda: Escort those who have paid the correct amount to 
the White Hospital / Eliminate any potential threat to Gerda 
and Kay’s business.    
Gift - The Rook:  Once per Scene, Kråke can transform 
into a rook and escape by flying away. If she makes a sur -
prise attack, Kråke inflicts 1 Additional Wound. 
3

210
Threat - Murder of Crows
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: We are a great murder of crows and watch over 
our beloved human brothers Ravn and Kråke.
Gift - The Three Feathers:  The Murder of Crows acts in 
the service of Kråke and Ravn to protect them and treat them 
as if they were a Crow. Once per Exchange, both Kråke and 
Ravn can enjoy one of the following effects:
1. Coat of Feathers:  The Murder shields a Black Feather, 
which increases their OL to Hard 7 against an attack.
2. Flight of the Crow:  The Murder lifts the Black Feather 
off the ground and allows it to run over their feathery bodies. 
The Black Feather can move anywhere within a thirty-foot 
radius, and no one can block them. If they make an attack 
after moving, the Black Feather increases their OL by 1.
3. Feather Storm: The Murder encircles a target while it 
performs an action that is not a physical attack. This effect 
can be activated when an opponent acts, but it still counts 
against the limit of one effect per Exchange. The target must 
pass a Defense Check or lose the action.
Epilogue
It takes two days of sailing to get to the White Hospital.
If a Black Feather is captured, they will not agree to talk, but 
the Hunters can use magic or other means to force them to 
reveal the following information:
 ◆ The mission of the Black Feathers is to take the wealthiest 
patients to Os, where the famous White Hospital is lo-
cated, but even that is a cover-up by Gerda and Kay. The 
real goal is to get the patients into the hands of a certain 
Reinsdyr, who will then lead them through the forest to 
be robbed by a group of bandits.
 ◆ The Black Feathers do not know how to get to the cas-
tle where Gerda and Kay live, but they have heard more 
than once from the two fraudsters about witches who 
help them.  
Storyteller’s Note: The Hunters’ journey continues in 
PART TWO - Towards Lady Storm’s Castle. Keep track of the 
fate of the Black Feathers, for they will be recurring enemies 
in the next parts. 
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunter’s 
actions.
If the Hunters use violence to stop Aleksander:  his 
daughter will stand up for him, going so far as to beg the 
Hunters to spare him. If the situation is not resolved, Fredri-
ka will send the Hunters away with less information to end 
the dinner quickly. 
If the Hunters try to destroy Flora’s Greenhouse to 
force her out:  the old witch will use her magic to call for 
help, causing many townspeople to rush to her defense. 
If  the  Hunters  inflict  at  least  one  Wound  on  both 
Black Feathers or corner them: the two will attempt to 
escape by transforming into a rook and a raven and mingling 
with the Murder.

211
Part Two – Towards Lady Storm’s 
Castle
Safety keywords: Illness / Identity theft / Medical fraud
Note: If the Hunters eliminated one of the Black Feath-
ers in the previous Scenario, the other will return to the 
attack in this Scenario to avenge their fallen comrade. A 
new Agenda will be applied to the NPC’s profile, replacing 
the previous one. The Black Feather will always use Murder 
of Crows as in PART ONE - The Two Healers.
Agenda: Eliminate the Hunter responsible for my compan-
ion’s death. 
At the beginning of each Scene, roll 1 die:
1-2: The Black Feather does not intervene in the current 
Scene.
3-4: The Black Feather tries to get in the Hunters’ way by 
acting from a distance, either through the use of the Mur -
der or by providing support to the present NPCs.
5-6: The Black Feather attacks the Hunters by ambushing 
them.  
Once upon a time…
Sailing down the river, the Hunters reach the town of Os, 
home to the famous White Hospital. The town seems to be 
a peaceful place to live, and there is no sign of the Frozen 
Madness.
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay never really stay in Os, but they 
did leave imposters in their place: Magnus and Iselin, two 
young upstarts who managed to get married thanks to Ger-
da and Kay’s intervention. Vegard, Iselin’s old father, did 
not approve of the two lovers’ marriage because of some 
debts that Magnus’ family never paid off. Thanks to a Shard 
that Kay blasted into the man’s eye, Vegard was transformed 
from a grumpy breadwinner into a gentle old man who 
willingly accepted his daughter’s marriage. In exchange for 
their help, Gerda and Kay have the two newlyweds pose 
as the famous healers, sending those in need of a cure to 
Reinsdyr. The thug poses as a guide, but instead, he takes 
the unfortunate people in a carriage into the forest to be 
attacked by Rikke’s gang. Magnus and Iselin are not evil, 
but they agree to deceive people to protect their marriage.

212
The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Magnus and Iselin are in charge of the Hospital’s activi-
ties, trying as much as possible to help those most in need. 
Both have studied to become doctors, but their efforts only 
contribute to Gerda and Kay’s reputation, giving the two 
scoundrels an aura of competence far removed from reality. 
Reinsdyr offers himself as a guide to the rich and needy that 
the two doctors pass on to him, only to lead them into the 
thick of the forest and have them attacked by a group of 
brigands in Gerda and Kay’s service.
FIRST SCENE –  The arrival at Os
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You leave the 
boat in the small port of Os: in front of you you 
see a quiet little town, very different from the cha-
os in which Bergen, hit hard by the Frozen Mad-
ness, finds itself. A large sign clearly points in the 
direction of the famous White Hospital: could it be that the 
strange disease has really been eradicated from this town by 
the two famous healers?
Place: The White Hospital, an austere and clean medical 
facility. 
Time: Early morning as the church bells ring. 
Initial Situation: The Hunters will have no trouble find-
ing the White Hospital and getting an interview with the 
famous healers. Magnus and Iselin are not expecting a visit 
from the Hunters of the Order and naively introduce them-
selves as Gerda and Kay. Under pressure, they do not hesi-
tate to expose the deception of the real Gerda and Kay. The 
following information can be gleaned from the interview 
with the two doctors:
 ◆ Gerda and Kay are two enterprising young people, 
and it is easy to guess from their behavior that they are 
accustomed to the shady dealings of the underworld. 
Kay seems to know about magic, while Gerda shows 
off magical and powerful objects. 
 ◆ Magnus and Iselin’s job is to refer only the wealthiest 
patients to Reinsdyr, whose carriage will take them to 
a mysterious woman a day’s journey from Os. Magnus 
and Iselin do not know what will happen to the pa-
tients. 
 ◆ The village was once protected by a band of nomads 
who took a liking to the locals and began a fruitful 
partnership. After Gerda and Kay passed through, 
young Rikke, who leads the nomads, changed dramati-
cally. Now, she and her companions have taken up ban-
1

213
ditry. Reinsdyr is the only one who knows the 
safe ways through the forest, which is why his 
customers pay him well.
 ◆ Magnus and Iselin are aware that they are doing some-
thing wrong by helping Gerda and Kay, and so they 
serve the poor and needy without asking for anything 
in return, trying to atone for their sins. If only Vegard 
would accept their union, there would be no problem 
and they would stop deceiving everyone.  
When the interview is over, if Iselin has not been coerced 
or threatened by the Hunters, she will say that she has spent 
the last few months studying the Frozen Madness and dis-
covering its magical origins. If the Hunters wish, she will 
teach them how to remove the Mirror Shards from those 
affected. Unfortunately, the process also requires the use of 
magic, which is why Iselin does not dare perform it on pa-
tients herself.
End of Scene: The Hunters decide whether to learn from 
Iselin how to remove the Shard (go to SECOND SCENE 
OPTION A - A Lesson in Medicine) or approach Reinsdyr 
instead (go to SECOND SCENE OPTION B - Reinsdyr the 
Guide.)
SECONDA SCENE OPTION 
A –  A Lesson in Medicine 
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Iselin leads you into her 
study and unrolls a scroll of complex magical formulas, 
saying she found the paper in her father’s great library. The 
inscriptions reveal that the Mirror of the Other Self is an an-
cient and powerful object capable of completely altering the 
souls of those reflected in it. Iselin noticed an ominous glint 
in the eyes of those suffering from Frozen Madness, and the 
symptoms of total behavioral change led her to believe that a 
Shard from the Mirror might be the cause of the illness.
Place: Iselin’s private study.
Time: Late morning.    
Initial Situation: Iselin will help the Hunters decipher 
the scrolls, a task that will take all day. At the end of their 
research, all Hunters who participated will receive the Sce-
nario’s Exclusive Gift - Knowledge of the Mirror of the Oth-
er Self - without spending any XP to acquire it. 
Gift - Knowledge of the Mirror of the Other Self:  
The Hunter receives 2 Additional Successes when facing 
the Threat - Shard of the Mirror of the Other Self. 
End of Scene: Since a whole day has passed and Reinsdyr 
has moved away from the town, the Hunters can only fol-
low his trail. Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Journey.
2a

214
SECOND SCENE OPTION B 
–  Reinsdyr the Guide
Read aloud to the Hunters: Reinsdyr can be found 
in the large central square of Os, his reindeer-drawn 
carriage standing out from the crowd. Reinsdyr is a 
tall man with a thick dark beard and a reindeer skin 
cloak that covers him from head to toe. The headdress 
he wears is also made from a reindeer head, and the 
large antlers above it make the man look almost monstrous.
Place: The central square of Os, filled with caravans and 
merchants. 
Time: In the early hours of the morning, baggers and mer-
chants are already hard at work in the marketplace.    
Initial Situation: Hunters will find Reinsdyr sitting in 
his carriage, waiting for potential customers: the man has 
a surly demeanor, but is willing to talk if money is shown. 
If cornered, he will fight the Hunters to the death, refusing 
to talk while free. If the Hunters manage to subdue him 
somehow, Reinsdyr will reveal the following information:
 ◆ He has an agreement with Gerda and Kay to lead 
wealthy clients to the home of Pernille, an ice witch. 
Pernille and her sister Sinikka were banished by Lady 
Storm for an old betrayal, but the two witches know 
the only way to the secret castle where Gerda and Kay 
have taken refuge.
 ◆ Along the road Reinsdyr travels, a gang of brigands 
has taken up residence. They are led by Rikke, a young 
woman afflicted with the Frozen Madness that has 
turned her into an unscrupulous thief. Rikke’s gang 
robs Reinsdyr’s passengers of all their possessions and 
then lets the coach continue on to Pernille’s house. 
Reinsdyr the Guide
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a very experienced guide and know all 
the secrets of survival in the north. I can take down any en-
emy with ax and pistol without making too much of a fuss.        
Agenda: Escort wealthy Frozen Madness victims to Pernille’s 
house and share the proceeds of the robberies with Rikke.
Gift – Die Hard:  Reinsdyr is resilient and a difficult op-
ponent even for Hunters. The guide can act twice during 
an Exchange, using his ax to hit an enemy in melee combat 
and his gun to hit a target at a distance. When he suffers 
1 Wound, Reinsdyr can immediately make an additional 
attack against the one who wounded him.
End of Scene:  With or without Reinsdyr, the Hunters 
must go to Pernille’s house, go to SCENE THREE - The 
Journey.
2b

215
THIRD SCENE –  The Journey
Note: This Scene can be introduced in a number of ways, 
depending on what the Hunters have already done.
The Hunters learned the cure for the Frozen 
Madness
In this situation, the Hunters missed the meeting with Re-
insdyr, the guide has already left with a wealthy client, and 
the Hunters must travel the road on their own. Here is a list 
of possible contingencies:
 ◆ Rikke and her brigands will ambush the Hunters. Al-
ternatively, the Hunters may arrive while the brigands 
have stopped Reinsdyr’s carriage.
 ◆ The Hunters reach Reinsdyr.
 ◆ The Hunters choose an alternative route, avoiding both 
the brigands and Reinsdyr.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at Pernille’s house, go to 
FOURTH SCENE - The Lapland Woman.
The Hunters hired Reinsdyr for the trip
The Hunters travel “safely” in Reinsdyr’s carriage. Here is a 
list of possible contingencies:
 ◆ Rikke and her brigands will ambush the Hunters.
 ◆ The Hunters convince or force Reinsdyr to avoid brig-
and territory.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at Pernille’s house, go to 
FOURTH SCENE - The Lapland Woman.
The Hunters defeated Reinsdyr in Os.
The Hunters travel in Reinsdyr’s carriage. Here is a list of 
possible contingencies:
 ◆ Rikke and her brigands will ambush them. The Hunt-
ers can try to fool the brigands thanks to Reinsdyr’s 
carriage.
 ◆ The Hunters choose an alternative route while avoiding 
the brigands.
End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at Pernille’s house, go to 
FOURTH SCENE - The Lapland Woman.
Rikke the Nomad
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a northern nomad and have always tried 
to lead my companions on the right path. I am used to the 
hard life of the forest and know how to move and hunt well.
Agenda: To make my nomadic family prosperous.    
Shard Descriptor: I am a nomad from the north, 
trying to accumulate as much wealth as possible in order to 
move to a more hospitable climate. I hate the hard life in these 
latitudes and to survive I exploit my faithful companions.    
Shard Agenda: Gather as much wealth as I can 
through banditry and then run far away, leaving my entire 
clan to their fate. 
Gift - Power of the Shard: The Shard of the Mirror of the 
Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the person 
it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they act in ac-
cordance with the altered part of their Descriptor or Agenda. 
3

216
Nomads
Easy OL 3 - Minor NPC 
(1 Wound - 12 Nomads)
Descriptor: We are nomads from the north and have al-
ways lived by hunting and trading what we find.
Agenda: We want to live in peace, but our loyalty to Rikke 
drives us to follow her in her madness. We would like to find 
a way to cure her and return to our nomadic ways.    
Gift - Nomad Clan:  A Nomad increases their OL by 1 
when they act to defend Rikke from a threat. 
FOURTH SCENE –  The Lap -
land Woman
Read aloud to the Hunters: You arrive at a small house 
in the woods, where the river widens into a small lake. Thick 
smoke pours out of the chimney on the roof into the icy eve-
ning air.    
Place: The house of Pernille, known as The Lapland Wom-
an.
Time: The sun is setting.
Initial Situation: The meeting with Pernille can vary de-
pending on what the Hunters have done so far. The witch is 
only loyal to Gerda and Kay because she is being rewarded 
for her services, and she will not hesitate to betray them if 
the Hunters corner her. Pernille’s approach to dealing with 
the Hunters will vary depending on the possible events pre-
sented at the end of this Scenario.
Pernille The Lapland Woman
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fearsome ice witch who learned the art 
of magic from the famous Lady Storm.
Agenda: Earn as much as possible without risking my life.
Gift - Ice Magic: Pernille can manipulate frost to create 
ice at a range of sixty feet. When she acts, she can choose 
one of the following effects:
1. Ice Rain: The sharp fragments can hit up to 3 targets, 
causing 1 Wound.
4

217
2. Ice Trap: Up to 3 targets are gripped in ice, preventing 
them from moving their legs. To break free, it is necessary 
to pass a Defense Check.
3. Ice Golem:  Summon an ice creature under Pernille’s 
control. 
Ice Golem
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a mighty creature of pure ice and live only 
to serve my creator. 
Information: Pernille is the only one who knows the 
magic formula that opens the portal located in the small 
lake, leading to the house of Sinikka, the Finland Wom-
an. A Hunter who understands magic can also activate the 
portal by studying the passage. If both of these options are 
impractical, Hunters can ask Iselin for help.
Threat –  The Magic Lake Portal
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A magical portal leading close to Lady Storm’s 
castle. Only those who know the magic formula can plunge 
into the lake’s waters and pass through.
Epilogue
Once they plunge into the waters of the lake, the Hunters 
are transported to the home of Sinikka the Finland Wom-
an, a few miles from Lady Storm’s castle. 
Storyteller’s Note: The Hunters’ journey continues with 
PART THREE - The Winter Castle. 
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunt-
er’s actions.
If one of the Black Feathers or Reinsdyr can still 
act after a fight: they will have anticipated the Hunters 
and alerted Pernille to the danger. 
If no one has warned Pernille of the arrival of the 
Hunters: the witch will act as usual and tell the Hunt-
ers that to get to Gerda and Kay’s castle, they must pass 
through a secret passage that only she knows. However, this 
passage is very expensive, and Pernille will ask the Hunters 
for a large sum of money.
If Miss Snow and Lady Storm are among the 
Hunters: Pernille will take refuge in the house and try to 
intimidate the Hunters into leaving. The old witch is afraid 
that Lady Storm will punish her for helping Gerda and Kay.

218
Part Three – The Winter Castle
Safety keywords: Illness / Identity theft  
Once upon a time…
The Hunters emerge from the lake and find themselves in 
front of the house of the Finland Woman. It is a small build-
ing, equal to Pernille’s in every way. Turning their gaze to the 
north, the imposing silhouette of a castle is a dark shadow in 
the middle of a snowstorm.
The story that led us here…
Gerda and Kay retired to Lady Storm’s castle to en-
joy the fruits of their misdeeds. In a fit of boredom, Gerda 
began to study in Lady Storm’s library. The young wom-
an had always had a flair for magic and found great sat-
isfaction in learning the arcane art. Meanwhile, Kay took 
to the good life, using Lady Storm’s magic sleigh to travel 
around Europe, wasting a lot of money on revelry. A week 
ago, however, Gerda attempted a very powerful spell and 
lost control at the climax of the ritual. The castle’s magic 
resonated with the power generated by the unwary Gerda, 
awakening a malevolent consciousness in the structure that 
took the name Ice Heart. The Ice Heart then unleashed a 
powerful blizzard, freezing Kay in an ice shrine, while Ger-
da was struck by a Shard from the Mirror of the Other Self, 
the thing that made her deception possible turning against 
her. The ice reached Sinikka’s house and mercilessly swept 
everything away.
The story at the arrival of the 
Hunters…
The Hunters have arrived at Sinikka’s house, but it will not 
be easy to find out where they really are and how far they 
have traveled thanks to the magic of the lake. The Finland 
Woman’s house is completely covered in a blanket of ice as 
if a blizzard has swept over everything in its fury. In Lady 
Storm’s castle, Gerda desperately tries to save Kay: now that 
her character has been changed by the Shard, the contempt 
she felt for her partner in mischief has become a strong 
love. Unable to thwart the will of the castle, Gerda has taken 
refuge in Lady Storm’s great library in search of a solution.

219
FIRST SCENE –  The Finland 
Woman
Read aloud to the Hunters:  As Pernille indicated, the 
magic lake has led you to the home of Sinikka, her coven 
partner. What puzzles you, however, is the total desolation 
of the dwelling. Everything is covered in a thick layer of ice 
as if a very powerful blizzard has been punishing the place 
for years.    
Place: Sinikka’s completely frozen house.
Time: The sun rises as if the journey across the magic lake 
had taken a whole night.
Initial Situation: Everything is covered in ice. Upon en-
tering the house, the Hunters will see the figure of Sinikka, 
frozen as she was about to turn the ladle in a pot of soup 
above the hearth. It is clear that the witch has been taken 
by surprise. 
Information: Observing the house, it will be easy to see 
that the ice covering it is of magical origin. Sinikka may be 
alive, but melting this ice will require a great deal of effort. If 
the Hunters succeed in freeing Sinikka, the witch will thank 
them and explain that a week ago she sensed the activation 
of powerful magic, summoned by a strong and malevolent 
presence that seemed to emanate from the castle itself. The 
witch did not have time to understand what was happening 
before she was struck by a blizzard. Sinikka is extremely 
weak and will not follow the Hunters into the castle for any 
reason. She has no useful information, other than that since 
Gerda and Kay settled there, only a few people have come 
from the lake to be cured of the Frozen Madness, and none 
have ever returned. 
Threat – Ice Layer
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A thick layer of steel-hard ice. 
End of Scene: The Hunters can head to Lady Storm’s cas-
tle, go to SECOND SCENE - Lady Storm’s Castle.
SECOND SCENE –  Lady Storm’s 
Castle
Read aloud to the Hunters: In front of you is an ancient 
castle that appears to be made entirely of ice. The large en-
trance door is open, and there seems to be no guard at all. As 
you walk across the bridge, which is also frozen, a shiver runs 
down your spine, and you know that the cold is not to blame. 
It may sound crazy, but you feel a gaze directed at you, as if 
the whole structure has a malevolent will of its own.    
Place: Lady Storm’s Castle.
Time: Early morning of a clear-sky day. 
Initial Situation: The castle is open, so it will be easy for 
the Hunters to enter. Each room is completely covered in 
ice, and moving around the structure takes time, consider-
ing there are over two hundred rooms. 
Information: Exploration of the castle is handled by 
Encounter Rooms, each with a different challenge for the 
Hunters. These places represent the will of the Ice Heart 
to eliminate unwanted guests. After each Encounter Room, 
the Hunters will wander through other neutral rooms, 
where they will find information about Lady Storm, the 
true owner of the castle. These rooms are important be-
cause they provide vital clues as to what kind of heat can 
scratch the Ice Heart, as explained on page 224.
1
2

220
Roll or choose what kind of information to give for rooms 
without an encounter.
1: A huge painting depicts the figure of a beautiful woman 
with skin as icy as snow. Her eyes are ice blue and her hair is 
light and flowing. The plaque reads, “Lady Storm, Queen of 
Winter, everything in her is pure frost except her heart. ”
2: A completely frozen old book lies open on a lectern. From 
the few lines that can be made out, it appears to be a treatise 
on ice magic. Among the many pages, the word “friendship” 
is underlined. 
3: An array of gold jewelry and bracelets lies in a completely 
frozen display case. This is jewelry of the highest value, and 
each has a small plaque identifying it as a gift of love from a 
powerful European ruler. 
4: A large painting covers the entire wall, with a beautiful 
woman wrapped in ice in the center and a coven of witches at 
her feet, smiling and offering her gifts in gratitude. 
5: A huge globe is painted on the vaulted ceiling of the room. 
Under the ice covering it, routes can be seen that seem to con-
nect a castle to various parts of the world. A sleigh is depicted 
on the lines of travel, and one of the routes is clearly in the 
shape of a heart.    
6: An ice sculpture stands in the center of a large bedroom. 
The figures of two children are carved in ice, embracing each 
other in an affectionate gesture of friendship.
As soon as the Hunters enter the castle behind them, a wall 
of ice will fall and the windows will turn to frozen slabs. It 
will be clear that there is no way out. 
The following pages contain several Encounter Rooms, and 
you, as the Storyteller, are free to decide which ones you 
want to use. After at least two Encounter Rooms, the Hunt-
ers will find the Special Room - Library, where Gerda 
has locked herself up, and through which it will be possible 
to access the Special Room - Heart of the Castle  and 
end the spell. To get there, Hunters will have to go through 
at least one other Encounter Room.   
Exploration will follow this order:
 ◆ At least 2 Encounter Rooms 
 ◆ Special Room - Library
 ◆ At least 1 more Encounter Room
 ◆ Special Room - Heart of the Castle
A room with information should be inserted between each 
room.

221
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Guard Post 
Descriptor: A large armory with a series of ice statues de-
picting soldiers. 
Information: Walking through the hall will cause the ice 
statues to activate and move to eliminate intruders. There 
are 16 statues in total, activated in groups of 4. The activa-
tion of the statues is determined by the vibrations caused by 
the Hunters walking across the icy floor of the hall. 
Ice Soldier
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a soldier made entirely of ice and I defend 
Lady Storm’s castle. My priority is to always focus on the tar-
gets that make the most noise.
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Col-umns Walk
Descriptor: A very long corridor leads into the heart of 
Lady Storm’s castle. A series of ice pillars divides the corridor, 
and in them, you can see the silhouettes of trapped people.    
Information: The trapped people are mostly treasure 
seekers, but if you look closely, you can see some of the 
Bergen nobles. It will be clear to the Hunters that the sick 
people who made it to the castle are trapped inside the col-
umns. The columns are difficult to melt, and the people 
inside them are dead. Approaching a column without an 
occupant will activate Threat - Columns of Eternal Sleep.
 Threat – Columns  of Eternal Sleep
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A column of magical ice capable of absorbing 
living things and enveloping them in a deadly frost.
Gift - Glacial Embrace: A target that fails a Check when 
the column tries to absorb them is wrapped in ice and takes 
1 Wound. During each action, the encased subject can at-
tempt to free themselves by making a Position Check, suf-
fering 1 Wound if they fail. When they run out of Wounds, 
the target is completely absorbed by the column. An NPC 
automatically suffers 1 Wound with each of its actions un-
til it has consumed all of its Wounds. At the Storyteller’s 
discretion, a particularly skilled NPC may choose to free 
themselves with one of their actions. 
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Me-morial Church
Descriptor: A large Gothic church made entirely of ice. 
Small snowflakes fall from the large vault in the ceiling and 
float gently to the ground.    
Information: The snowflakes are imbued with Lady 
Storm’s power and are capable of erasing the memory of 
those they touch. If a subject does not find a way to protect 
themselves from contact, they will suffer the effect of the 
Threat - Snow of Memory. In the main altar is a large shrine 
with an object inside: this is a Treasure of the Order, which 
the Storyteller must choose at will from those presented 
from page 19. It takes two minutes to sprint through the 
Church.

222
Threat – Snow of Memory
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: Tiny snowflakes that can freeze a person’s 
memories forever until they forget everything. 
Gift - Ice Oblivion:  For each minute a subject is under 
the snowfall, it is touched by a number of flakes equal to 
the result of a die roll. The result determines the effect to be 
applied. If the subject does nothing to protect themselves 
from the snow, they cannot make a Defense Check against 
the Threat. The Storyteller must evaluate, based on protec-
tive measures and the subject’s clothing, whether they are 
more exposed to snowflakes (roll 2 dice and choose the 
higher value) or are more sheltered (roll 2 dice and choose 
the lower value). 
1-2: A severe headache accompanies a cherished memory 
that slowly fades, leaving the target with the perception of 
an event lost forever. The target loses 2 Soma. An NPC re-
duces their OL by 1 for the remainder of the Scenario.
3-4: The face of a loved one appears fleetingly in the tar -
get’s mind before disappearing, leaving them with the feel-
ing of having lost the memory of a loved one. The target 
loses the use of one of their Descriptors (except the Dark 
Ego) until the end of the Scenario. An NPC loses their only 
Descriptor and replaces it with this one: I’ve lost my memo-
ry and have no idea where I am. 
5-6: The target hears a phrase whispered so quietly it is 
unintelligible, leaving them with the perception that a part 
of themselves is lost forever. The target loses the use of one 
Gift for the rest of the Scenario (except Dark Ego). An NPC 
who has no Gifts to lose reduces their OL by one category. If 
they already have OL Easy 3, they suffer 1 Wound instead.  
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Troll 
Workshop
Descriptor: A small room with the remains of a large bro-
ken mirror. Twelve more mirrors are placed in each corner, 
creating a striking play of reflections. 
Information: A Troll stands in the small hall. It is the cre-
ator of the Mirror of the Other Self. After the artifact broke, 
the craftsman went completely insane and now tries to re-
produce the original, failing miserably each time. The Troll 
hates to be interrupted, and any discussion topic other than 
the re-creation of his beloved mirror will incur his wrath. 
Over time, the Troll has blinded himself with splinters from 
the many mirrors he is not satisfied with and, unaware that 
Lady Storm is no longer in the castle, he continues his mad 
work.
Troll Craftsman
OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled craftsman and have served Lady 
Storm for decades, creating stunning ice mirrors for her. I can 
manipulate the reflected images as if they were real.    
Agenda: Rebuilding the Mirror of the Other Self for my 
Lady Storm.    
Special Agenda (Prince Trollo the Innocent): I have 
always wanted an apprentice to pass my art on to, and he 
seems perfect. Whether he wants to or not.    
Gift - The Mirrored Reality:  Although blinded, the 
Troll can see the world around it through the mirrors scat-
tered throughout the room, thanks to the glare of the shards 
in his eyes. The Troll can act against a target by striking at 
a reflected image without the target being able to make a 
Defense Check to avoid it.

223
ENCOUNTER ROOM –  Guest 
Room
Descriptor: A luxurious bedroom. The ice on the walls 
seems to have no effect on the furnishings, and even the tem-
perature seems decidedly milder than in the rest of the castle.    
Information: The room is filled with valuables and com-
forts. Time flows differently here, which is understandable 
since the Hunters will feel extremely rested after a few min-
utes. If they stay in the room for at least an hour, they will 
be able to call an Interlude without the Scenario Agendas 
continuing.
SPECIAL ROOM –  Library
Descriptor: A vast library carved from the ice, with walls 
of books stretching as far as the eye can see. A magical seal, 
pulsing with power, is clearly visible at the library’s entrance. 
Information: The seal is a protective spell that Gerda 
cast to prevent the Ice Heart from reaching her. As Hunters 
wander through the Library, they will hear Gerda mutter -
ing as she conducts her research. The young woman has 
retained the memory of her misdeeds, but now her priority 
is to save Kay, and she will not hesitate to ask the Hunters 
for help. Gerda will give them the following information:
 ◆ The Ice Heart is a core of magical energy that sits atop 
Lady Storm’s throne. Through her research, Gerda dis-
covered that the Heart is a simulacrum of Lady Storm’s 
consciousness that the castle has assimilated over the 
years. 
 ◆ In an old diary hidden in the Library, Gerda has read 
that the only thing Lady Storm fears is heat, which 
means that the Ice Heart can be defeated if it is exposed 
to heat. Gerda has already tried this, but even when she 
brought a flame close to the Heart, nothing happened, 
so Gerda is racking her brain to find out what kind of 
heat the diary is referring to.
 ◆ The Ice Heart has total control of the castle and will 
not allow anyone to leave. Now even the Hunters are 
prisoners like her.
The warmth referred to in the diary, as can be deduced from 
the many scenes depicted in the rooms through which the 
Hunters pass, is that of emotional warmth. However, Ger-
da, who is not a good person, is unable to intuit this truth. 
Gerda the Swindler
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a skilled trickster, my sharp mind matched 
only by my nerve. While studying Lady Storm’s books, I dis-
covered that I can also dabble in magic, and I have no qualms 
about using it to my own advantage.    
Agenda: Living in luxury without working and increasing 
my power to get my hands on more wealth / Supporting Kay 
in his whims, because even though I think he is an idiot, I 
need a trustworthy sidekick who will always do what I say. 
Shard Descriptor: I am a naive girl who struggles 
to express her feelings. Thanks to the magic I have learned, I 
can help others. 
Shard Agenda: Save my beloved Kay from the sad 
fate that befell him / Defeat the Ice Heart.
Gift - Power of the Shard:  The Shard of the Mirror of 
the Other Self reverses the Descriptor and Agenda of the 
person it injured. The holder increases their OL by 1 if they 
act in accordance with the altered part of their Descriptor 
or Agenda.

224
Gift - Magician’s Apprentice: Gerda has learned the 
rudiments of magic and can choose one of the following 
effects as an action. 
1. Create 3 ice darts to hit up to 3 different targets. The same 
subject cannot be the target of more than one dart at a time.
2. Create a blizzard that will knock down all subjects with-
in a sixty-foot radius of Gerda. 
3. Manipulate memory by touching the subject’s forehead: 
Gerda can create a false memory or remove the memory of 
something that happened during the Scene.  
SPECIAL ROOM –  Heart of the 
Castle
Descriptor: Lady Storm’s throne room, where a sphere of 
pure ice floats. The magic in this place is so powerful that 
even ordinary people can feel it.    
Information: Above the great throne hovers the Ice 
Heart, which will greet the Hunters by speaking directly 
to their minds. The Ice Heart will ask the Hunters to turn 
Gerda over to it so it can punish her for what she did to 
Lady Storm. If they cooperate, the Ice Heart will be willing 
to allow the Hunters to leave the castle, delaying their end 
until it unleashes an eternal frost upon the world. 
In the center of the room is an ice pillar with Kay inside, 
trapped in a fleeing position with a terrified expression 
on his face. To free Gerda’s accomplice, you must face the 
Threat – Ice Layer (see page 219).
The Ice Heart
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (6 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a fragment of pure power and live only 
to punish all humans guilty of hurting my Lady Storm. I am 
the castle and within it, all is in my power. I have no human 
malice and speak my mind without a filter. 
Agenda: Punish Gerda for what she did to Lady Storm / 
Punish humanity and freeze it in eternal ice.    
Gift - I Am Winter:  The Ice Heart is a manifestation of 
power and can manipulate anything within the castle. As 
an action, the Heart can inflict 1Wound from cold on any 
subject in the castle without the ability to make a Defense 
Check. The only safe place in the castle is the Library, where 
the Heart’s power cannot reach because of the seal Gerda 
created. A sincere expression of love or friendship in the 
presence of the Ice Heart can immediately shatter and de-
stroy it.

225
Scenario Events
There are 3 Events in this Scenario, though the Storyteller 
should feel free to create new ones in response to the Hunt-
ers’ actions.
If the Hunters succeed in calming the Troll:  the 
Troll will be able to teach them the Scenario’s Exclusive Gift 
- Knowledge of the Mirror of the Other Self. 
If the Hunters remove the Shard from Gerda: now 
a con artist once more, she will try to escape by using her 
magic. 
If the Hunters free Kay:  the scoundrel will try to help 
Gerda, but only if doing so can help him escape. Otherwise, 
he will just try to escape to save himself. 
Epilogue
Eliminating the Ice Heart will cause the castle to collapse, 
while leaving it active will create a threat that the Hunters 
know they will face in the future. If Miss Snow and Lady 
Storm were among the Hunters who participated in the 
playthrough, a choice must be made:
 ◆ Forgetting the Past: Miss Snow’s personality takes 
over, forever relegating Lady Storm to the role of sec-
ond personality. Her Hunter Sheet remains unchanged, 
as the memory of what happened is once again erased, 
and Miss Snow embarks on another search for her past.
 ◆ Remembering the Past: Lady Storm regains control of 
her body, but, feeling indebted to Miss Snow, infuses 
some of her magic to give her life. Her Hunter Sheet is 
replaced with the following one.

Miss Snow
OL Easy 3 - (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am an apprentice ice mage, although I often cause disasters with 
my powers. I am naive and always kind to others. 
 
Gift – Snow Sorceress Apprentice: Miss Snow can use the Gifts of the 
Snow Sorceress and Winter’s Kiss, but when she does, she rolls a die, and 
if she rolls a 4 or higher, a magical disaster (determined by the Storyteller) 
occurs, complicating the situation.
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a powerful ice sorceress and can manipulate the weather. I want 
Snow to learn to be a Hunter, so I limit the use of my powers to solving 
problems for her. 
•  Snow Sorceress: Y ou can use magic to create and manipulate the cold. 
Describe the effects of a spell that has one of the following Keywords as a con-
dition: Ice, Snow, or Frost. Y ou could, for example, Create an Ice Bridge 
to cross a crevasse or Freeze an Enemy to immobilize it. Pay a cost of 1 to 2 
Soma, decided by the Storyteller, to activate the spell. The effects you describe 
last until the end of the Scene. The Soma cost is increased by 1 when environ-
mental conditions are not compatible with cold.
I am impassive and know how to assert my authority, nothing can 
shake my heart. I have a weakness for those who show true love or 
friendship, and it comes naturally to me to help these people, although 
I do not want anyone to notice this weakness of mine. 
•  Winter’s Kiss: In you lies the power of winter. When you give someone 
a chaste kiss, you can choose one of the following effects:
1. Erase one of their memories. It must be the memory of an event in the Scene 
in which you use this effect. Particularly capable Hunters and NPCs may requi-
re a Position Check for the effect to succeed.
2. Make the subject immune to cold for the rest of the Scene. This will prevent 
them from suffering Wounds from frost and make them immune to the effects 
of the Snow Sorceress Gift. 
3. Make an object extremely fragile. 
Y ou can spend 1 Soma when you use this Gift to blow a kiss to the target, thus 
avoiding physical contact.
I have a special insight into understanding people and their intentions, 
and with my magic, I can quickly create help for the weak. I dislike peo-
ple who talk too much and social events.  
•  Dark Ego - Deus Ex Machina:  When all seems lost, use your magic 
to make truth triumph. Reduce by 1 the Soma cost of spells you cast with 
the Gift of the Snow Sorceress when your goal is to help someone. Once per 
Exchange, you can spend 1 Soma to prevent one of your allies from taking 
1 Wound by protecting them with a wall of icy frost. Y ou can use the Gift of 
Winter’s Kiss as a free action once per Exchange.
Activator: Step in and show your power to save a good-hearted person from the 
danger that threatens them.
Equipment
A brooch in the shape of a sleigh / A small knife with an ice blade that never 
melts / A scarf handmade by Miss Snow / Books of magic / A mirror fragment 
with a strange magical aura.
Soma
 5
PX
Lady Storm and 
Miss Snow
Iam a powerful witch who rules over winter and frost. Because of a betrayal, I was robbed of my 
body for a long time and a new personality was created in me: Miss Snow. As much as I hate to admit 
it, it is thanks to Miss Snow that I have regained my full strength, and I intend to help her find her way. Thanks to my magic, I have given her a body, and now we are like twins: identical in appearance, 
but profoundly different in character. Snow has agreed to remain in the ranks of the Order, and until she is able to fend for herself, I will watch over her. 
Special: Miss Snow is an NPC under the complete control of the Storyteller. Lady Storm guards over her on her missions on behalf of the Order.  
Wounds 
1.
2.
3.
Additional 
Wound.

Miss Snow
and Lady Storm

Miss Snow
and Lady Storm
I am an eager sorceress, though I am new to the practice of magic. No matter how hard I try to stay in 
control, I always end up creating a mess. When the Order tracked me down, I had no choice but to become 
a Hunter or face imprisonment. Every night, I have strange dreams in which I am a powerful witch with 
power over the forces of winter. The dreams always end when a dark figure shatters a mirror, and I 
am pierced by the shards. Perhaps by working as a Hunter, I will be able to dispel the darkness that has 
engulfed my mind.
Special: When the Dark Ego Snow Queen is not active, the Hunter is Miss Snow and ignores the crossed-out words in Descriptors, focusing on terms in red. 
When the Dark Ego is active, the Hunter becomes Lady Storm and will focus on the crossed-out parts of the Descriptors, while ignoring the red words.
Descriptors and Gifts
I am a novice  a powerful sorceress with limited power  total control 
over cold and snow. My magic can come with dangerous, unintended 
consequences cause small unwanted effects.
• Snow Sorceress: Y ou can use magic to create and manipulate the 
cold. Describe the effects of a spell that has one of the following Keywords 
as a condition: Ice, Snow, or Frost. Y ou could, for example, Create an Ice 
Bridge to cross a crevasse or Freeze an Enemy to immobilize it. Pay a cost 
of 1 to 3 Soma, decided by the Storyteller, to activate the spell. The effects 
you describe last until the end of the Scene. The Soma cost is increased by 
1 when environmental conditions are not compatible with cold. Y ou can 
decide to lower the cost of the spell by 1 Soma, but this will cause a minor 
magical accident on par with an Outcome with a Cost.
I am good at understanding  dominating people. I have a unique 
ability to comprehend their desires. I tend to distrust even close allies 
trust those I don’t know.
• Winter’s Kiss:  In you lies the power of winter. When you give 
someone a chaste kiss, you can choose one of the following effects:
1. Erase one of their memories. It must be the memory of an event in the 
Scene in which you use this effect. Particularly capable Hunters and 
NPCs may require a Position Check for the effect to succeed.
2. Make the subject immune to cold throughout the rest of the Scene. 
This will prevent them from suffering frost Wounds and allow them to 
ignore the effects of Lady Storm’s snowstorm.
3. Make an object extremely fragile.
Y ou can spend 1 Soma when you use this Gift to blow a kiss to the target, 
thus avoiding physical contact.
Cold and frost have no effect on me are extensions of my soul and I feel 
a great affinity for winter. My heart of pure ice delights is easily touched 
when I see others suffer.
• Dark Ego - Snow Queen:  The tiny fragment of a magic mirror lies in 
your right eye, relegating the real you to oblivion. But when tears warm your 
eyes, or you hear the word that represents your dominion over the cold, Lady 
Storm comes to her senses and woe to those who stand before her.
While you are Lady Storm, you can lower the Soma cost of Snow Sorceress 
by 1 without creating magical accidents. In addition, you can use Winter’s 
Kiss at a distance without spending Soma. If you wish, you can summon a 
snowstorm that will sweep over your location regardless of previous weather 
conditions. Inside the storm, visibility is reduced to about three feet, and all 
involved lower their OL by 1 for each action. While the snowstorm rages, you 
can use the Gift Winter’s Kiss as a free action once per Exchange. This Gift 
ends, and the Hunter becomes Miss Snow once more as soon as you suffer 1 
Wound, or at the end of the Scene.
Activator: Cry over something that genuinely moved you. You hear the word 
“Eternity” spoken by someone who is not aware that it will awaken Lady Storm.
Equipment 
A brooch in the shape of a sleigh / A small knife with a blade made of ice that 
never melts / A large key to a castle whose location you ignore / Spellbooks / A 
mirror fragment with a strange magical aura.

Tremoteus
  Rumpel

Descriptors and Gifts
I have an encyclopedic memory and there is no law that I don’t 
know. I hate to break the rules.
• Examine the Evidence:  Y ou can use each of the following effects 
once per Scene:   
1. For the Record: Y ou can make a note of something a subject has said; 
you will never forget it. Write it down because you can use it when you 
activate your Dark Ego. By spending 1 Soma, the Storyteller will reveal a 
weakness of the subject whose words you have memorized.
2. Consult the Laws: The Storyteller will reveal to you the customs and 
rules of a social group or place you are in. By spending 1 Soma, you 
can create a loophole, known only to you, to the rules that have been 
revealed.
3. The Decisive Proof:  When you examine an object, the Storyteller 
will reveal a quality or feature that few people may notice. By spending 
1 Soma, you will learn who was the last person to own or use what you 
are examining. 
My reputation precedes me, and my word is held in the highest 
regard by anyone with an ounce of honesty. I detest criminals and 
those who act to harm the innocent. Big or small, a crime is still 
a crime.  
• Your Honor: If you stand in front of nobles, lawmen, or leaders in 
general, you can spend 1 Soma to have them recognize your name or 
reputation. If you use your identity as a strength, you gain 1 Additional 
Success. If you give your word in a negotiation, you gain 1 Additional 
Success in a possible Position Check.
I am a keen disputant and have an uncanny ability to detect 
deception. I need hard evidence before I’ll accuse someone of 
dishonesty.
• Dark Ego - Shattered Pact:  Those who break the rules must be 
punished. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success on all Position and Defense 
Checks you make against a subject who’s acting in a way that contradicts 
the statement you recorded using the For the Record effect. If you act 
to harm them, you also inflict 1 Additional Wound. Once per Scene, if 
a subject reveals themselves as they really are, as opposed to how they 
previously presented themselves, or if they break their word, you can 
lecture them on their dishonesty. All your companions gain 1 Additional 
Success on their next action. 
Activator: Make an argument against the guilty by pointing out their 
misdeeds. 
Equipment 
A long lawyer’s robe / A magnifying glass / Documents and deeds from 
over a hundred trials / A fine dueling sword / Property deeds and bank 
deposits / A ring of little value given to me by my first client / A scroll of 
recommendations written by the Pope himself.

Tremoteus 
Rumpel
I  am a skilled lawyer and have always valued justice above all else. I was very successful in life until a rich nobleman 
called me to defend him against a strange deception he had suffered. A young girl (later revealed to be a witch) had 
tricked him into believing that she could turn straw into gold. It took me very little to expose the deception, but what I did 
not expect was the motivation for it. The young woman wanted revenge because the nobleman had caused the death of 
her entire family, reducing them to poverty and starvation by punishingly unjust taxes. So, for the first time in my life, 
I decided to break the covenant imposed on me by my profession and voluntarily lost the case. The Order intervened to 
expose the witch, and I decided to pay for my sin by trading her freedom for my services as a Hunter.

Prince Trollo
  the Innocent

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a young prince, well-versed in the etiquette of the court. I 
am a diligent student, my favorite subjects being history and 
poetry. I am not ashamed of my strange appearance and I hate to 
hide from others.
• Noble Birth: Once per Scene, if you are in a place where your family’s 
authority is recognized, you can gain access to a restricted location, such 
as a dungeon or castle, or a direct audience with an authority figure. 
As a prince, you have learned everything a future ruler needs to know. 
Once per Scene, you gain 2 Additional Successes for an action in which 
you can demonstrate your knowledge of history and poetry, or your skill 
with the sword. 
I learned kindness from my mother and firmness from my father. 
I use these qualities to uphold the good name of my family. I will 
not allow anyone to violate the ideals of justice and courage with 
which I was raised.  
• Good Heart: Y ou have the innocence and strength of a true ruler. 
Y ou gain 1 Additional Success for each Check you make when trying 
to persuade someone to do the right thing. Once per Scenario, if you 
suffer 1 Wound to stand up for what you believe in, you set an example 
for those who help you. Each ally recovers 1 Soma, and a target of your 
choice (Hunter or NPC) immediately gains an additional action they 
must use to try to help you or get you to safety.     
I have strange powers, and my body is stronger and more resilient 
than an ordinary child’s. While I’m not ashamed of my 
appearance, these uncanny abilities mark me as different, and I 
try not to attract attention when I use my power.
• Dark Ego - Oknytt’s Legacy: Y ou appeal to your Dark Ego to gain 
great powers. If it is day, you must spend 1 Soma. If you have no Soma, 
you take 1 Wound. If it is night, there is no cost to activate this Gift, as 
you are a creature of the night. Y ou gain the following powers:
1. Yellow Eyes: See perfectly in total darkness. Once per Scene, you can 
see a target for what they really are, unmasking any disguise they may 
be wearing.    
2. Troll Strength: Once per Scene, you can perform an act of unpre-
cedented strength by lifting or moving a weight equal to ten men. If you 
use your strength to harm someone, you inflict 1 Additional Wound.      
3. Stone Skin: Once per Scene, you can suffer an attack or hazard while 
avoiding any Wounds. Y ou can resist almost everything, including fire, 
falls, and weapons. Only magic can harm you. Y ou must declare the use 
of this effect before any Defense Check. 
Activator: You tap into your heritage in front of someone you have helped 
before and who is unaware of your true nature. 
Equipment
Clothes of fine workmanship / A small wooden crown painted with gold, 
given to me by my father / A splendid short sword / A bag containing 
everything needed to process milk and make butter / A precious bracelet 
engraved with a cradle, a gift from my mother / A well-worn blanket that 
I love and use as a cloak / A bag full of coins and valuables that 
I often lose.

Prince
  Trollo
the Innocent
I am a young prince of the Kingdom of Denmark and grew up in the care of my noble family. My peculiar appearance always attracted 
a lot of attention, which is why my father always preferred to keep me away from the court. But that did not make me sad, because the 
days I spent on the estate with my beloved mother were the best days of my life. I had always suspected that I was different, and this 
truth was revealed to me in the most painful way. One day, my father was visited by the Order’s Hunters, who warned him of dangerous 
monsters who were replacing human babies with their own. Upset by this discovery, my father became convinced that I was an evil 
creature, switched in the cradle while still in swaddling clothes. I escaped with the help of my mother, who, with a kiss on my forehead, 
told me to find my own way and prove myself worthy of the title I bore. I found the Hunters of the Order and asked them to take me with 
them. Now I serve the Papacy, hoping to find the truth behind my birth, to prove who I really am, and to find my place in the world.

Pietrik and the 
Cauldron  Fae

Descriptors and Gifts
I am a rebel who is used to living on the streets. I know my way 
around the slums and how to deal with the lowlifes. Rules are 
made to be broken.
• Lost Childhood: Despite your young age, you have no qualms about 
taking action against enemies. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success in all 
actions that require the use of subterfuge. If you attack a target that is 
unaware of your presence, you inflict 1 Additional Wound.  
I fear nothing and throw myself into any situation. The 
adrenaline of risk drives me to succeed. I get bored easily in 
situations where there is no danger. 
• Fearless: Y ou are immune to any fear or mind control effects that 
prevent you from acting as you wish. If you suffer 1 Wound from an 
Opponent or Threat, you may regain 1 Soma in your next action and 
may consider a Failure as an Outcome with a Cost. 
The Cauldron Fae is always with me, in the form of a small 
floating light. Her magic is still powerful, and I often summon 
her to wreak havoc or play tricks on humans.
• Dark Ego - The Magic of Disbelief:  The Cauldron Fae unleashes 
her magic to enable you to perform amazing actions. As an action, 
you can formulate a magic sentence including the fragment I don’t 
believe and make its effect concrete. For example, “I don’t believe in 
gravity” would allow you to fly, while “I don’t believe the Hunters of 
the Order exist” would allow you to hurt the Hunters in an attempt to 
make them disappear. If your statement has no offensive effect, spend 
1 Soma to gain the power you summoned for the entire Scene. On 
the other hand, if you want the spell to affect a target 
other than yourself, spend 1 Soma 
to inflict a Wound or apply the effect without making a Check. Each 
time you use the Magic of Disbelief, roll a die. On a roll of 5 or 6, the 
Storyteller gains 1 Prank Token. The Storyteller can spend 1 Prank 
Token during a Scene to activate the effect of this Gift (you do not take a 
Wound because the Activator was not normally triggered) and have the 
Cauldron Fae cast a spell to complicate your life or attempt to damage 
something that represents progress and modernity. If the Storyteller has 
no accumulated Prank Tokens, you may choose to ignore all the Soma 
costs to activate the effects of this Gift, but the Storyteller will gain 1 
Prank Token. 
Activator: Without thinking about the possible consequences, throw 
yourself into an action that is impossible for an ordinary person.

A dueling blade / A book of fairytales given to me by Gwendolyn, the only 
person I consider a friend / A necklace with a little bell / A worn pirate flag 
I keep as a trophy / A set of sharp needles.

I am a rebellious young man, guilty of committing acts to disrupt the established order. My anger stems from my 
past when I lost my younger sister, who died from working in large factories run by wealthy industrialists. With the 
help of the Cauldron Fae, a powerful fairy, I tried to destroy the city of London, but the Order defeated us in time. In the 
battle, the powerful fairy joined herself to my soul, and now we share our life force. Captured by the Order, I am forced 
to work as a Hunter, but the day when I take my revenge on adults and society is only postponed.

Loki     
the Deceiver

Descriptors and Gifts
I can disguise myself to assume any identity. I can easily imitate 
men, women, and even fantastical creatures. I have great respect 
for a successful deception and hate to expose it, even if it would 
benefit me.
• Thousand Faces: Once per Scene, you can assume the identity of 
another Hunter. Choose a Hunter’s Sheet that no one is using and use it 
for the rest of the Scene. Y ou have access to all the Gifts and abilities on 
the Sheet, with the exception of the Soma points and Wounds, which you 
will keep from Loki’s Sheet. Even though you have their likeness, you are 
not the Hunter you are impersonating, so you do not know their personal 
history and cannot activate the Special Scenario Gifts associated with 
them. Any effects of the Gifts you activate while impersonating another 
Hunter will end when you return to your true identity or at the end of 
the Scene. If someone discovers who you really are, your masquerade 
ends and you take 1 Wound. If you wish, you can return to your original 
appearance before the end of the Scene. If you discover a deception and 
do nothing to expose it, you gain 1 Soma.
I am a skilled acrobat, I tell stories, perform magic tricks, and put 
my audience in a good mood. I enjoy chaos and adding confusion 
to situations is a way for me to bring balance to the world.
• Trickster: Y ou get 2 Additional Successes if you use your skills as 
an entertainer to perform an action. This includes polite conversation, 
sleight of hand, or performing stunts. Once per Scene, if you help an 
enemy (for example as a Cost), you gain 1 Soma.
I am a master at distorting and unraveling deception. Verbal 
and body language hold no secrets for me. I don’t like to get my 
hands dirty and would rather have someone else do it for me.
• Dark Ego - The Great Deception: The whole world is a lie, and only 
those who are truly aware of it can pull the strings of any story. During 
the course of the Scene, you can activate each of the following effects 
once:
1. Materialize an item that is useful for the situation you are facing and 
that an ally of yours is carrying without knowing it.
2. Switch places with a Minor NPC. The exchange takes place in the 
blink of an eye, and those present will only see where you are now 
and where your target has ended up. Highly skilled NPCs and other 
Hunters are immune to this effect.
3. Make an NPC that you have met before appear in the Scene. This is 
an illusion that will do your bidding and will dissolve as soon as it 
takes 1 Wound or it becomes clear that it is not the real person.
4. Gain 1 Additional Success in a Check you make by exploiting a 
subject’s character flaw.
Activator: Organize a deception by studying it in detail, or expose a 
deception to the people being deceived.
Equipment
Theatrical costume / A snake-shaped flask containing a strong acid / A 
necklace of great value engraved with the word “Brísingamen” / Various 
musical instruments and juggling equipment / A series of sharp daggers 
hidden throughout my clothing / A diary in which are written the 
names of all the men and women whose hearts I have broken.

Loki 
the Deceiver
I am the god of  deception, and I know the truth behind Broken Europe, the Hunters, and the Child 
Savior. I found it so entertaining that I decided to hide these events in my memory and enjoy many 
adventures as a Hunter just to figure it out again. Because let’s face it, no story is exciting without a 
good twist. I live as a Hunter now, taking on many identities and enjoying bringing confusion to the 
world, as befits my role. But don’t tell anyone, it will be our little secret...

The Silent 
Mother

Descriptors and Gifts
I know my way around high society and have amassed great 
wealth through countless marriages. I am known in the 
underworld for my exploits. I hate being accused of being a 
criminal.
• Legacy: Y ou can use all the resources you have accumulated from 
your many marriages of convenience. Y ou can use each of these effects 
once per Scenario:
1. A Family Asset:  Y ou can bring a large property into play, such as 
a mansion or business. Y ou have free access to the property and its 
resources.
2. A Family Friend: Y ou can introduce a Minor NPC who has a debt or 
partnership agreement with your family. The Storyteller will sketch the 
NPC, who will be well-disposed towards you.
3. A Family Treasure: Y ou can bring into play a rare and valuable item 
that you own as a family heirloom. 
I am able to understand the potential of young women and know 
how to guide their steps to develop their true potential. I consider 
each of my protégés a true daughter and would give my life to 
save them.
  
• My Beloved Daughters:  Two young girls you saved from an 
unfortunate fate follow you on your adventures. Y our adopted 
daughters are two Minor NPCs with the following profiles:
OL Medium 5 - (1 Wound)
Decide on their names and stories, and work with the Storyteller to 
create a Descriptor for each of them that describes their abilities. Y our 
adopted daughters cannot have magical abilities or more than a good 
education in their chosen discipline. Y our daughters will do their best to 
serve you at every opportunity. 
Although I appear helpless, I am a capable spy who knows how to 
defend herself in dangerous situations. I never miss an opportunity 
to accumulate wealth and resources.
• Dark Ego - The Caring Mother:  Choose a female NPC or Hunter 
and name her your Pupil. Y our Pupil must be young and, in your 
opinion, deserving of your benevolence. Y ou will always know where 
your Pupil is, and she will gain 1 Additional Success to her actions (or 
increase her OL by 1 in the case of an NPC) if you are present in the 
Scene to comfort her with your words. Y ou get 1 Additional Success if 
you act against someone who endangers your Pupil. If an NPC or Hunter 
uses a Gift to harm your disciple, you can spend 1 Soma to prevent the 
Gift from being activated.  
Activator: Your words inspire a young girl to find her way or protect her 
from injustice.  
Equipment
Noble robes / Family jewelry and wealth / Two trained pigeons you can 
use to send messages / A set of long sewing pins / A long cloak and a mask 
depicting a smiling mother / A sublimely crafted weapon.

The Silent 
Mother
My name is Elizabeth Marâtre, and I am a noblewoman who has been luring young heirs into marriages of convenience 
for more than a decade. I come from a youth of hardship and abuse, inflicted by my stepmother. But the hardships this witch 
inflicted upon me did not bend my soul, instead pushing me further and further toward who I am now. For years, I used my 
loveliness to conjure up marriages with rich and foolish nobles, whose every possession I used to secretly help young people 
in need. Society is not merciful to those who lack the strength to rebel, so I create the right conditions for deserving girls to 
spread their wings and fly from an unjust fate. Unfortunately, after my tenth marriage, I was discovered and the only path left 
to me led to prison. The Order learned of my story and, seeing my abilities, offered me a new mission. I accepted, but only so I 
could continue to watch over the future of so many young girls waiting for a chance to change their lives.

Karasu Kuroi
The Wandering Monk

Descriptors and Gifts
I am an exorcist monk. My body is tempered by a lifetime of asceticism 
and fighting evil spirits. I do not tolerate blasphemy and those who use 
knowledge for personal gain.
• Monk: Y ou can spend 1 Soma to activate a piece of your Spirit Hunter 
Equipment:
1. Pastoral Shakujo:  Banging your staff on the ground can temporarily 
interrupt the effect of a spell or curse. The duration of the interruption (set by 
the Storyteller) can vary from a few moments to the entire Scene, depending 
on the power of the spell.
2. Hauchiwa Fan: With a fan blast, you can make a leap of up to ten meters 
forward or upward. Y ou can use this effect on an ally.
3. Tengu-gui Mask:  Y ou can wear your mask to change your facial fea-
tures, taking on a physical appearance appropriate to the context. Create a 
Descriptor that represents your appearance, but remember that you cannot 
replicate the features of a specific subject. When you activate a Gift or suffer 1 
Wound, you will revert to your original appearance.  
The use of this Gift is limited by the possession of the corresponding 
Equipment: if you lose an item, you cannot use the relevant option until you 
recover it. During an Interlude, you recover any missing Equipment.  
Prayer keeps my mind steadfast and my words are an inspiration to 
those who have faith. I do not tolerate the concepts of faith and 
devotion being questioned. 
• Moral Rigor: Y ou demonstrate righteousness in word and deed. Y ou gain 
1 Additional Success on all Position Checks where you make a commitment 
by giving your word as a guarantee. By spending 1 Soma, you can make 
mental contact with a subject present in the Scene who must accept the link. 
Y ou can mentally communicate for the rest of the Scene.
My greatest secret is that I am like those I fight. My true essence is that 
of a Yokai ghost, which is why I have a talent for unearthing Broken 
Tales. I try to do good deeds in the hope of reincarnating as a human. 
• Dark Ego - You Are What You Fight: When you reveal your true nature, 
your body changes into the form of a humanoid raven. Y ou will gain the 
following abilities:
1. Y ou can move by flying thanks to the large wings on your back. Y ou gain 
1 Additional Success on all Defense Checks while hovering in the air.    
2. Y ou can use your large beak to strike an enemy as an additional action, 
but you cannot communicate by speaking.
3. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success if you act to injure someone who has 
inflicted 1 Wound on you in the current Scene.
Activator: You reveal your true nature to prove that not all spirits are evil.
Equipment 
My prized traveling Shakujō / My prized Hauchiwa fan / A Tengu-gui raven 
mask / A theater mask with a long nose that I use to hide my Asian features / A 
katana that belonged to a famous samurai / A traveling bag containing boiled 
rice and bean paste.
